sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततोऽब्रवीन्मयः पार्थं वासुदेवस्य संनिधौ ।प्राञ्जलिः श्लक्ष्णया वाचा पूजयित्वा पुनः पुनः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato'bravīnmayaḥ pārthaṁ vāsudevasya saṁnidhau ,prāñjaliḥ ślakṣṇayā vācā pūjayitvā punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Maya, with folded hands, addressed Arjuna in the presence of Vāsudeva (Krishna), speaking gentle words after having honored him repeatedly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Maya, with folded hands, addressed Arjuna in the presence of Vāsudeva (Krishna), speaking gentle words after having honored him repeatedly.

sanskrit: अस्माच्च कृष्णात्संक्रुद्धात्पावकाच्च दिधक्षतः ।त्वया त्रातोऽस्मि कौन्तेय ब्रूहि किं करवाणि ते ॥२॥
transliteration: asmācca kṛṣṇātsaṁkruddhātpāvakācca didhakṣataḥ ,tvayā trāto'smi kaunteya brūhi kiṁ karavāṇi te.
english: O Kaunteya (Arjuna), I have been saved by you from this enraged Krishna and from the fire that desired to consume [me]. Tell me, what may I do for you?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 2. O Kaunteya (Arjuna), I have been saved by you from this enraged Krishna and from the fire that desired to consume [me]. Tell me, what may I do for you?

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।कृतमेव त्वया सर्वं स्वस्ति गच्छ महासुर ।प्रीतिमान्भव मे नित्यं प्रीतिमन्तो वयं च ते ॥३॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,kṛtameva tvayā sarvaṁ svasti gaccha mahāsura ,prītimānbhava me nityaṁ prītimanto vayaṁ ca te.
english: Arjuna said: "You have already accomplished everything. Depart in well-being (svasti), great Asura. May you always be pleased with me, and we too are pleased with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 3. Arjuna said: "You have already accomplished everything. Depart in well-being (svasti), great Asura. May you always be pleased with me, and we too are pleased with you."

sanskrit: मय उवाच ।युक्तमेतत्त्वयि विभो यथात्थ पुरुषर्षभ ।प्रीतिपूर्वमहं किंचित्कर्तुमिच्छामि भारत ॥४॥
transliteration: maya uvāca ,yuktametattvayi vibho yathāttha puruṣarṣabha ,prītipūrvamahaṁ kiṁcitkartumicchāmi bhārata.
english: Maya said: "O mighty one (vibho), O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), what you say is indeed proper for you. Nevertheless, O Bhārata (Arjuna), I wish to do something for you out of affection."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 4. Maya said: "O mighty one (vibho), O best among men (puruṣarṣabha), what you say is indeed proper for you. Nevertheless, O Bhārata (Arjuna), I wish to do something for you out of affection."

sanskrit: अहं हि विश्वकर्मा वै दानवानां महाकविः ।सोऽहं वै त्वत्कृते किंचित्कर्तुमिच्छामि पाण्डव ॥५॥
transliteration: ahaṁ hi viśvakarmā vai dānavānāṁ mahākaviḥ ,so'haṁ vai tvatkṛte kiṁcitkartumicchāmi pāṇḍava.
english: Indeed, I am Viśvakarmā, the great artisan among the Dānavas. Therefore, O Pāṇḍava, I certainly wish to do something for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 5. Indeed, I am Viśvakarmā, the great artisan among the Dānavas. Therefore, O Pāṇḍava, I certainly wish to do something for your sake.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।प्राणकृच्छ्राद्विमुक्तं त्वमात्मानं मन्यसे मया ।एवं गते न शक्ष्यामि किंचित्कारयितुं त्वया ॥६॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,prāṇakṛcchrādvimuktaṁ tvamātmānaṁ manyase mayā ,evaṁ gate na śakṣyāmi kiṁcitkārayituṁ tvayā.
english: Arjuna said: "You consider yourself (ātman) liberated from mortal danger by me. Under these circumstances, I will not be able to have anything done by you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 6. Arjuna said: "You consider yourself (ātman) liberated from mortal danger by me. Under these circumstances, I will not be able to have anything done by you."

sanskrit: न चापि तव संकल्पं मोघमिच्छामि दानव ।कृष्णस्य क्रियतां किंचित्तथा प्रतिकृतं मयि ॥७॥
transliteration: na cāpi tava saṁkalpaṁ moghamicchāmi dānava ,kṛṣṇasya kriyatāṁ kiṁcittathā pratikṛtaṁ mayi.
english: Nor, O Dānava, do I wish your resolve (saṃkalpa) to be fruitless. Therefore, let something be done for Kṛṣṇa, as a return for what was done for me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 7. Nor, O Dānava, do I wish your resolve (saṃkalpa) to be fruitless. Therefore, let something be done for Kṛṣṇa, as a return for what was done for me.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।चोदितो वासुदेवस्तु मयेन भरतर्षभ ।मुहूर्तमिव संदध्यौ किमयं चोद्यतामिति ॥८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,codito vāsudevastu mayena bharatarṣabha ,muhūrtamiva saṁdadhyau kimayaṁ codyatāmiti.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O best of Bhāratas, Vāsudeva, urged by Maya, pondered for a moment, 'What indeed should be enjoined upon him?'"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 8. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O best of Bhāratas, Vāsudeva, urged by Maya, pondered for a moment, 'What indeed should be enjoined upon him?'"

sanskrit: चोदयामास तं कृष्णः सभा वै क्रियतामिति ।धर्मराजस्य दैतेय यादृशीमिह मन्यसे ॥९॥
transliteration: codayāmāsa taṁ kṛṣṇaḥ sabhā vai kriyatāmiti ,dharmarājasya daiteya yādṛśīmiha manyase.
english: Krishna urged him, "O son of Diti, let an assembly hall be built for the king of righteousness (dharma)  -  one that you consider fitting here."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 9. Krishna urged him, "O son of Diti, let an assembly hall be built for the king of righteousness (dharma)  -  one that you consider fitting here."

sanskrit: यां कृतां नानुकुर्युस्ते मानवाः प्रेक्ष्य विस्मिताः ।मनुष्यलोके कृत्स्नेऽस्मिंस्तादृशीं कुरु वै सभाम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: yāṁ kṛtāṁ nānukuryuste mānavāḥ prekṣya vismitāḥ ,manuṣyaloke kṛtsne'smiṁstādṛśīṁ kuru vai sabhām.
english: Build such an assembly hall, indeed, in this entire human world, that upon seeing it, people would be astonished and unable to replicate it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 10. Build such an assembly hall, indeed, in this entire human world, that upon seeing it, people would be astonished and unable to replicate it.

sanskrit: यत्र दिव्यानभिप्रायान्पश्येम विहितांस्त्वया ।आसुरान्मानुषांश्चैव तां सभां कुरु वै मय ॥११॥
transliteration: yatra divyānabhiprāyānpaśyema vihitāṁstvayā ,āsurānmānuṣāṁścaiva tāṁ sabhāṁ kuru vai maya.
english: O Maya, construct such an assembly hall where we may behold divine, asuric, and human forms manifested by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 11. O Maya, construct such an assembly hall where we may behold divine, asuric, and human forms manifested by you.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य तु तद्वाक्यं संप्रहृष्टो मयस्तदा ।विमानप्रतिमां चक्रे पाण्डवस्य सभां मुदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya tu tadvākyaṁ saṁprahṛṣṭo mayastadā ,vimānapratimāṁ cakre pāṇḍavasya sabhāṁ mudā.
english: Then, having received that instruction, Maya, greatly delighted, joyfully constructed for the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) an assembly hall resembling a celestial palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 12. Then, having received that instruction, Maya, greatly delighted, joyfully constructed for the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) an assembly hall resembling a celestial palace.

sanskrit: ततः कृष्णश्च पार्थश्च धर्मराजे युधिष्ठिरे ।सर्वमेतद्यथावेद्य दर्शयामासतुर्मयम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛṣṇaśca pārthaśca dharmarāje yudhiṣṭhire ,sarvametadyathāvedya darśayāmāsaturmayam.
english: Then, Krishna and Arjuna, in the presence of King Yudhishthira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), showed all this, as it was known, to Maya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 13. Then, Krishna and Arjuna, in the presence of King Yudhishthira, the upholder of natural law (dharma), showed all this, as it was known, to Maya.

sanskrit: तस्मै युधिष्ठिरः पूजां यथार्हमकरोत्तदा ।स तु तां प्रतिजग्राह मयः सत्कृत्य सत्कृतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasmai yudhiṣṭhiraḥ pūjāṁ yathārhamakarottadā ,sa tu tāṁ pratijagrāha mayaḥ satkṛtya satkṛtaḥ.
english: Then, Yudhishthira offered him (Maya) appropriate honor. And Maya, having been honored, respectfully accepted that honor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 14. Then, Yudhishthira offered him (Maya) appropriate honor. And Maya, having been honored, respectfully accepted that honor.

sanskrit: स पूर्वदेवचरितं तत्र तत्र विशां पते ।कथयामास दैतेयः पाण्डुपुत्रेषु भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa pūrvadevacaritaṁ tatra tatra viśāṁ pate ,kathayāmāsa daiteyaḥ pāṇḍuputreṣu bhārata.
english: O Lord of the people, O descendant of Bharata (Janamejaya), that Daitya (Maya) then narrated various stories about the deeds of ancient divine beings (devas) to the sons of Pandu (the Pandavas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 15. O Lord of the people, O descendant of Bharata (Janamejaya), that Daitya (Maya) then narrated various stories about the deeds of ancient divine beings (devas) to the sons of Pandu (the Pandavas).

sanskrit: स कालं कंचिदाश्वस्य विश्वकर्मा प्रचिन्त्य च ।सभां प्रचक्रमे कर्तुं पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa kālaṁ kaṁcidāśvasya viśvakarmā pracintya ca ,sabhāṁ pracakrame kartuṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: After resting for some time and having reflected (on the design), that master architect (Maya) then began to construct the assembly hall for the great-souled Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 16. After resting for some time and having reflected (on the design), that master architect (Maya) then began to construct the assembly hall for the great-souled Pandavas.

sanskrit: अभिप्रायेण पार्थानां कृष्णस्य च महात्मनः ।पुण्येऽहनि महातेजाः कृतकौतुकमङ्गलः ॥१७॥
transliteration: abhiprāyeṇa pārthānāṁ kṛṣṇasya ca mahātmanaḥ ,puṇye'hani mahātejāḥ kṛtakautukamaṅgalaḥ.
english: On an auspicious day, in accordance with the intentions of the Pārthas and the great-souled (mahātman) Kṛṣṇa, a greatly splendorous one (mahātejāḥ) had performed all auspicious rites (kautukamaṅgala).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 17. On an auspicious day, in accordance with the intentions of the Pārthas and the great-souled (mahātman) Kṛṣṇa, a greatly splendorous one (mahātejāḥ) had performed all auspicious rites (kautukamaṅgala).

sanskrit: तर्पयित्वा द्विजश्रेष्ठान्पायसेन सहस्रशः ।धनं बहुविधं दत्त्वा तेभ्य एव च वीर्यवान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tarpayitvā dvijaśreṣṭhānpāyasena sahasraśaḥ ,dhanaṁ bahuvidhaṁ dattvā tebhya eva ca vīryavān.
english: And that powerful one (vīryavān), having gratified thousands of the best Brahmins (dvija) with rice pudding, also gave them various kinds of wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 18. And that powerful one (vīryavān), having gratified thousands of the best Brahmins (dvija) with rice pudding, also gave them various kinds of wealth.

sanskrit: सर्वर्तुगुणसंपन्नां दिव्यरूपां मनोरमाम् ।दशकिष्कुसहस्रां तां मापयामास सर्वतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sarvartuguṇasaṁpannāṁ divyarūpāṁ manoramām ,daśakiṣkusahasrāṁ tāṁ māpayāmāsa sarvataḥ.
english: He (Maya) caused to be measured on all sides that charming and divinely beautiful structure, endowed with the qualities of all seasons, which was ten thousand kiṣkus (cubits) in measurement.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 1 Verse 19. He (Maya) caused to be measured on all sides that charming and divinely beautiful structure, endowed with the qualities of all seasons, which was ten thousand kiṣkus (cubits) in measurement.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उषित्वा खाण्डवप्रस्थे सुखवासं जनार्दनः ।पार्थैः प्रीतिसमायुक्तैः पूजनार्होऽभिपूजितः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,uṣitvā khāṇḍavaprasthe sukhavāsaṁ janārdanaḥ ,pārthaiḥ prītisamāyuktaiḥ pūjanārho'bhipūjitaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having happily dwelt in Khāṇḍavaprastha, Lord Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), who was worthy of worship, was duly honored by the Pārthas, who were filled with affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having happily dwelt in Khāṇḍavaprastha, Lord Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), who was worthy of worship, was duly honored by the Pārthas, who were filled with affection.

sanskrit: गमनाय मतिं चक्रे पितुर्दर्शनलालसः ।धर्मराजमथामन्त्र्य पृथां च पृथुलोचनः ॥२॥
transliteration: gamanāya matiṁ cakre piturdarśanalālasaḥ ,dharmarājamathāmantrya pṛthāṁ ca pṛthulocanaḥ.
english: The broad-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa), eager to see his father, resolved to depart. Then, after summoning the king of righteousness (dharmarāja) and Pṛthā...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 2. The broad-eyed one (Kṛṣṇa), eager to see his father, resolved to depart. Then, after summoning the king of righteousness (dharmarāja) and Pṛthā...

sanskrit: ववन्दे चरणौ मूर्ध्ना जगद्वन्द्यः पितृष्वसुः ।स तया मूर्ध्न्युपाघ्रातः परिष्वक्तश्च केशवः ॥३॥
transliteration: vavande caraṇau mūrdhnā jagadvandyaḥ pitṛṣvasuḥ ,sa tayā mūrdhnyupāghrātaḥ pariṣvaktaśca keśavaḥ.
english: Keśava, who is revered by the world, bowed his head at the feet of his paternal aunt. She (Pṛthā) then smelled him on the head and embraced him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 3. Keśava, who is revered by the world, bowed his head at the feet of his paternal aunt. She (Pṛthā) then smelled him on the head and embraced him.

sanskrit: ददर्शानन्तरं कृष्णो भगिनीं स्वां महायशाः ।तामुपेत्य हृषीकेशः प्रीत्या बाष्पसमन्वितः ॥४॥
transliteration: dadarśānantaraṁ kṛṣṇo bhaginīṁ svāṁ mahāyaśāḥ ,tāmupetya hṛṣīkeśaḥ prītyā bāṣpasamanvitaḥ.
english: Subsequently, the highly renowned Kṛṣṇa saw his own sister (Subhadrā). Hṛṣīkeśa, approaching her, was overcome with tears due to his affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 4. Subsequently, the highly renowned Kṛṣṇa saw his own sister (Subhadrā). Hṛṣīkeśa, approaching her, was overcome with tears due to his affection.

sanskrit: अर्थ्यं तथ्यं हितं वाक्यं लघु युक्तमनुत्तमम् ।उवाच भगवान्भद्रां सुभद्रां भद्रभाषिणीम् ॥५॥
transliteration: arthyaṁ tathyaṁ hitaṁ vākyaṁ laghu yuktamanuttamam ,uvāca bhagavānbhadrāṁ subhadrāṁ bhadrabhāṣiṇīm.
english: The revered Lord (Kṛṣṇa) spoke words that were meaningful, truthful, beneficial, concise, appropriate, and supreme to the auspicious-speaking Bhadrā Subhadrā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 5. The revered Lord (Kṛṣṇa) spoke words that were meaningful, truthful, beneficial, concise, appropriate, and supreme to the auspicious-speaking Bhadrā Subhadrā.

sanskrit: तया स्वजनगामीनि श्रावितो वचनानि सः ।संपूजितश्चाप्यसकृच्छिरसा चाभिवादितः ॥६॥
transliteration: tayā svajanagāmīni śrāvito vacanāni saḥ ,saṁpūjitaścāpyasakṛcchirasā cābhivāditaḥ.
english: By her (Draupadī), he was made to hear words concerning his kinsmen. He was also repeatedly honored and saluted with a bow of the head.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 6. By her (Draupadī), he was made to hear words concerning his kinsmen. He was also repeatedly honored and saluted with a bow of the head.

sanskrit: तामनुज्ञाप्य वार्ष्णेयः प्रतिनन्द्य च भामिनीम् ।ददर्शानन्तरं कृष्णां धौम्यं चापि जनार्दनः ॥७॥
transliteration: tāmanujñāpya vārṣṇeyaḥ pratinandya ca bhāminīm ,dadarśānantaraṁ kṛṣṇāṁ dhaumyaṁ cāpi janārdanaḥ.
english: Having obtained her (Draupadī's) permission and having respectfully acknowledged the beautiful lady (Draupadī), Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), the Vṛṣṇi scion, subsequently saw Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) and also Dhaumya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 7. Having obtained her (Draupadī's) permission and having respectfully acknowledged the beautiful lady (Draupadī), Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), the Vṛṣṇi scion, subsequently saw Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) and also Dhaumya.

sanskrit: ववन्दे च यथान्यायं धौम्यं पुरुषसत्तमः ।द्रौपदीं सान्त्वयित्वा च आमन्त्र्य च जनार्दनः ॥८॥
transliteration: vavande ca yathānyāyaṁ dhaumyaṁ puruṣasattamaḥ ,draupadīṁ sāntvayitvā ca āmantrya ca janārdanaḥ.
english: And the best among men (Kṛṣṇa) properly saluted Dhaumya. Having consoled Draupadī and bid her farewell, Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa)...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 8. And the best among men (Kṛṣṇa) properly saluted Dhaumya. Having consoled Draupadī and bid her farewell, Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa)...

sanskrit: भ्रातॄनभ्यगमद्धीमान्पार्थेन सहितो बली ।भ्रातृभिः पञ्चभिः कृष्णो वृतः शक्र इवामरैः ॥९॥
transliteration: bhrātṝnabhyagamaddhīmānpārthena sahito balī ,bhrātṛbhiḥ pañcabhiḥ kṛṣṇo vṛtaḥ śakra ivāmaraiḥ.
english: The wise and powerful (Janārdana), accompanied by Pārtha (Arjuna), then approached his brothers. Kṛṣṇa, encircled by the five brothers, shone like Indra (Śakra) surrounded by the immortals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 9. The wise and powerful (Janārdana), accompanied by Pārtha (Arjuna), then approached his brothers. Kṛṣṇa, encircled by the five brothers, shone like Indra (Śakra) surrounded by the immortals.

sanskrit: अर्चयामास देवांश्च द्विजांश्च यदुपुंगवः ।माल्यजप्यनमस्कारैर्गन्धैरुच्चावचैरपि ।स कृत्वा सर्वकार्याणि प्रतस्थे तस्थुषां वरः ॥१०॥
transliteration: arcayāmāsa devāṁśca dvijāṁśca yadupuṁgavaḥ ,mālyajapyanamaskārairgandhairuccāvacairapi ,sa kṛtvā sarvakāryāṇi pratasthe tasthuṣāṁ varaḥ.
english: The foremost among the Yadus worshipped the gods and the Brahmins with garlands, prayers, obeisances, and various kinds of fragrances. Having completed all his duties, he, the best among those who remain steadfast, then departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 10. The foremost among the Yadus worshipped the gods and the Brahmins with garlands, prayers, obeisances, and various kinds of fragrances. Having completed all his duties, he, the best among those who remain steadfast, then departed.

sanskrit: स्वस्ति वाच्यार्हतो विप्रान्दधिपात्रफलाक्षतैः ।वसु प्रदाय च ततः प्रदक्षिणमवर्तत ॥११॥
transliteration: svasti vācyārhato viprāndadhipātraphalākṣataiḥ ,vasu pradāya ca tataḥ pradakṣiṇamavartata.
english: After having the worthy Brahmins pronounce blessings with curds, vessels, fruits, and unbroken rice, and then having given them wealth, he circumambulated them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 11. After having the worthy Brahmins pronounce blessings with curds, vessels, fruits, and unbroken rice, and then having given them wealth, he circumambulated them.

sanskrit: काञ्चनं रथमास्थाय तार्क्ष्यकेतनमाशुगम् ।गदाचक्रासिशार्ङ्गाद्यैरायुधैश्च समन्वितम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: kāñcanaṁ rathamāsthāya tārkṣyaketanamāśugam ,gadācakrāsiśārṅgādyairāyudhaiśca samanvitam.
english: Having mounted his golden chariot, which was swift and bore Garuḍa as its emblem, and which was equipped with weapons such as his mace, discus, sword, and the Śārṅga bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 12. Having mounted his golden chariot, which was swift and bore Garuḍa as its emblem, and which was equipped with weapons such as his mace, discus, sword, and the Śārṅga bow.

sanskrit: तिथावथ च नक्षत्रे मुहूर्ते च गुणान्विते ।प्रययौ पुण्डरीकाक्षः सैन्यसुग्रीववाहनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tithāvatha ca nakṣatre muhūrte ca guṇānvite ,prayayau puṇḍarīkākṣaḥ sainyasugrīvavāhanaḥ.
english: Then, on an auspicious lunar day, constellation, and moment, the lotus-eyed one, whose conveyances included his army and the horse Sugrīva, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 13. Then, on an auspicious lunar day, constellation, and moment, the lotus-eyed one, whose conveyances included his army and the horse Sugrīva, departed.

sanskrit: अन्वारुरोह चाप्येनं प्रेम्णा राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।अपास्य चास्य यन्तारं दारुकं यन्तृसत्तमम् ।अभीषून्संप्रजग्राह स्वयं कुरुपतिस्तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: anvāruroha cāpyenaṁ premṇā rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,apāsya cāsya yantāraṁ dārukaṁ yantṛsattamam ,abhīṣūnsaṁprajagrāha svayaṁ kurupatistadā.
english: Then, King Yudhishthira, the lord of the Kurus, also ascended after him (Krishna) with great affection. Setting aside Krishna's charioteer Daruka, the best of charioteers, he himself took hold of the reins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 14. Then, King Yudhishthira, the lord of the Kurus, also ascended after him (Krishna) with great affection. Setting aside Krishna's charioteer Daruka, the best of charioteers, he himself took hold of the reins.

sanskrit: उपारुह्यार्जुनश्चापि चामरव्यजनं सितम् ।रुक्मदण्डं बृहन्मूर्ध्नि दुधावाभिप्रदक्षिणम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: upāruhyārjunaścāpi cāmaravyajanaṁ sitam ,rukmadaṇḍaṁ bṛhanmūrdhni dudhāvābhipradakṣiṇam.
english: And Arjuna also ascended, then waved the white fly-whisk with a golden handle respectfully clockwise above [Krishna's] head.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 15. And Arjuna also ascended, then waved the white fly-whisk with a golden handle respectfully clockwise above [Krishna's] head.

sanskrit: तथैव भीमसेनोऽपि यमाभ्यां सहितो वशी ।पृष्ठतोऽनुययौ कृष्णमृत्विक्पौरजनैर्वृतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tathaiva bhīmaseno'pi yamābhyāṁ sahito vaśī ,pṛṣṭhato'nuyayau kṛṣṇamṛtvikpaurajanairvṛtaḥ.
english: Similarly, Bhimasena, who was self-controlled, also followed Krishna from behind, accompanied by the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) and surrounded by priests and citizens.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 16. Similarly, Bhimasena, who was self-controlled, also followed Krishna from behind, accompanied by the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva) and surrounded by priests and citizens.

sanskrit: स तथा भ्रातृभिः सार्धं केशवः परवीरहा ।अनुगम्यमानः शुशुभे शिष्यैरिव गुरुः प्रियैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa tathā bhrātṛbhiḥ sārdhaṁ keśavaḥ paravīrahā ,anugamyamānaḥ śuśubhe śiṣyairiva guruḥ priyaiḥ.
english: Thus, Keshava (Krishna), the destroyer of enemy heroes, looked splendid as he was followed by his brothers and, like a beloved teacher (guru) by his dear disciples.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 17. Thus, Keshava (Krishna), the destroyer of enemy heroes, looked splendid as he was followed by his brothers and, like a beloved teacher (guru) by his dear disciples.

sanskrit: पार्थमामन्त्र्य गोविन्दः परिष्वज्य च पीडितम् ।युधिष्ठिरं पूजयित्वा भीमसेनं यमौ तथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: pārthamāmantrya govindaḥ pariṣvajya ca pīḍitam ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ pūjayitvā bhīmasenaṁ yamau tathā.
english: Govinda, after addressing Arjuna (Pārtha) and embracing him (who was still afflicted), and having honored Yudhishthira, Bhimasena, and the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 18. Govinda, after addressing Arjuna (Pārtha) and embracing him (who was still afflicted), and having honored Yudhishthira, Bhimasena, and the two twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) as well.

sanskrit: परिष्वक्तो भृशं ताभ्यां यमाभ्यामभिवादितः ।ततस्तैः संविदं कृत्वा यथावन्मधुसूदनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: pariṣvakto bhṛśaṁ tābhyāṁ yamābhyāmabhivāditaḥ ,tatastaiḥ saṁvidaṁ kṛtvā yathāvanmadhusūdanaḥ.
english: Madhusudana, having been firmly embraced by the two twins and saluted by them, then established a proper understanding with all of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 19. Madhusudana, having been firmly embraced by the two twins and saluted by them, then established a proper understanding with all of them.

sanskrit: निवर्तयित्वा च तदा पाण्डवान्सपदानुगान् ।स्वां पुरीं प्रययौ कृष्णः पुरंदर इवापरः ॥२०॥
transliteration: nivartayitvā ca tadā pāṇḍavānsapadānugān ,svāṁ purīṁ prayayau kṛṣṇaḥ puraṁdara ivāparaḥ.
english: And then, having dismissed the Pandavas with their followers, Krishna departed for his own city, like another Purandara (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 20. And then, having dismissed the Pandavas with their followers, Krishna departed for his own city, like another Purandara (Indra).

sanskrit: लोचनैरनुजग्मुस्ते तमा दृष्टिपथात्तदा ।मनोभिरनुजग्मुस्ते कृष्णं प्रीतिसमन्वयात् ॥२१॥
transliteration: locanairanujagmuste tamā dṛṣṭipathāttadā ,manobhiranujagmuste kṛṣṇaṁ prītisamanvayāt.
english: They followed him with their eyes until he was out of their sight. Then, filled with great affection, they followed Krishna with their minds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 21. They followed him with their eyes until he was out of their sight. Then, filled with great affection, they followed Krishna with their minds.

sanskrit: अतृप्तमनसामेव तेषां केशवदर्शने ।क्षिप्रमन्तर्दधे शौरिश्चक्षुषां प्रियदर्शनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: atṛptamanasāmeva teṣāṁ keśavadarśane ,kṣipramantardadhe śauriścakṣuṣāṁ priyadarśanaḥ.
english: Śauri (Kṛṣṇa), whose sight was so dear to their eyes, quickly disappeared from view, even as their minds remained unsatisfied by merely seeing him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 22. Śauri (Kṛṣṇa), whose sight was so dear to their eyes, quickly disappeared from view, even as their minds remained unsatisfied by merely seeing him.

sanskrit: अकामा इव पार्थास्ते गोविन्दगतमानसाः ।निवृत्योपययुः सर्वे स्वपुरं पुरुषर्षभाः ।स्यन्दनेनाथ कृष्णोऽपि समये द्वारकामगात् ॥२३॥
transliteration: akāmā iva pārthāste govindagatamānasāḥ ,nivṛtyopayayuḥ sarve svapuraṁ puruṣarṣabhāḥ ,syandanenātha kṛṣṇo'pi samaye dvārakāmagāt.
english: Though their minds were completely absorbed in Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), those Pārthas, the foremost of men, returned to their own city as if they had no other desires. Then, Kṛṣṇa also, by chariot, departed for Dvārakā in due course.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 2 Verse 23. Though their minds were completely absorbed in Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), those Pārthas, the foremost of men, returned to their own city as if they had no other desires. Then, Kṛṣṇa also, by chariot, departed for Dvārakā in due course.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अथाब्रवीन्मयः पार्थमर्जुनं जयतां वरम् ।आपृच्छे त्वां गमिष्यामि क्षिप्रमेष्यामि चाप्यहम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,athābravīnmayaḥ pārthamarjunaṁ jayatāṁ varam ,āpṛcche tvāṁ gamiṣyāmi kṣiprameṣyāmi cāpyaham.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Maya spoke to Pārtha Arjuna, the foremost among victors: "I take your leave; I shall depart, and I will also return swiftly."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Maya spoke to Pārtha Arjuna, the foremost among victors: "I take your leave; I shall depart, and I will also return swiftly."

sanskrit: उत्तरेण तु कैलासं मैनाकं पर्वतं प्रति ।यक्ष्यमाणेषु सर्वेषु दानवेषु तदा मया ।कृतं मणिमयं भाण्डं रम्यं बिन्दुसरः प्रति ॥२॥
transliteration: uttareṇa tu kailāsaṁ mainākaṁ parvataṁ prati ,yakṣyamāṇeṣu sarveṣu dānaveṣu tadā mayā ,kṛtaṁ maṇimayaṁ bhāṇḍaṁ ramyaṁ bindusaraḥ prati.
english: Then, when all the Dānavas were about to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) to the north of Mount Kailāsa, towards Mount Maināka, I (Maya) created a beautiful jeweled vessel near the Bindusaras lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 2. Then, when all the Dānavas were about to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña) to the north of Mount Kailāsa, towards Mount Maināka, I (Maya) created a beautiful jeweled vessel near the Bindusaras lake.

sanskrit: सभायां सत्यसंधस्य यदासीद्वृषपर्वणः ।आगमिष्यामि तद्गृह्य यदि तिष्ठति भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: sabhāyāṁ satyasaṁdhasya yadāsīdvṛṣaparvaṇaḥ ,āgamiṣyāmi tadgṛhya yadi tiṣṭhati bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata, I will bring whatever belonged to the truthful Vrishaparvan in his assembly, having taken it, if it still remains there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 3. O descendant of Bharata, I will bring whatever belonged to the truthful Vrishaparvan in his assembly, having taken it, if it still remains there.

sanskrit: ततः सभां करिष्यामि पाण्डवाय यशस्विने ।मनःप्रह्लादिनीं चित्रां सर्वरत्नविभूषिताम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ sabhāṁ kariṣyāmi pāṇḍavāya yaśasvine ,manaḥprahlādinīṁ citrāṁ sarvaratnavibhūṣitām.
english: Thereafter, I will build an assembly hall for the glorious Pandava, one that is delightful to the mind, wonderful, and adorned with all kinds of jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 4. Thereafter, I will build an assembly hall for the glorious Pandava, one that is delightful to the mind, wonderful, and adorned with all kinds of jewels.

sanskrit: अस्ति बिन्दुसरस्येव गदा श्रेष्ठा कुरूद्वह ।निहिता यौवनाश्वेन राज्ञा हत्वा रणे रिपून् ।सुवर्णबिन्दुभिश्चित्रा गुर्वी भारसहा दृढा ॥५॥
transliteration: asti bindusarasyeva gadā śreṣṭhā kurūdvaha ,nihitā yauvanāśvena rājñā hatvā raṇe ripūn ,suvarṇabindubhiścitrā gurvī bhārasahā dṛḍhā.
english: O scion of Kuru, there is an excellent mace (gadā) indeed in Bindusaras, which was placed there by King Yauvanashva after he had killed enemies in battle. It is variegated with golden dots, heavy, capable of enduring great burden, and very strong.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 5. O scion of Kuru, there is an excellent mace (gadā) indeed in Bindusaras, which was placed there by King Yauvanashva after he had killed enemies in battle. It is variegated with golden dots, heavy, capable of enduring great burden, and very strong.

sanskrit: सा वै शतसहस्रस्य संमिता सर्वघातिनी ।अनुरूपा च भीमस्य गाण्डीवं भवतो यथा ॥६॥
transliteration: sā vai śatasahasrasya saṁmitā sarvaghātinī ,anurūpā ca bhīmasya gāṇḍīvaṁ bhavato yathā.
english: Indeed, it is comparable to a hundred thousand (weapons), capable of destroying everything, and it is as suitable for Bhima as the Gaṇḍīva bow is for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 6. Indeed, it is comparable to a hundred thousand (weapons), capable of destroying everything, and it is as suitable for Bhima as the Gaṇḍīva bow is for you.

sanskrit: वारुणश्च महाशङ्खो देवदत्तः सुघोषवान् ।सर्वमेतत्प्रदास्यामि भवते नात्र संशयः ।इत्युक्त्वा सोऽसुरः पार्थं प्रागुदीचीमगाद्दिशम् ॥७॥
transliteration: vāruṇaśca mahāśaṅkho devadattaḥ sughoṣavān ,sarvametatpradāsyāmi bhavate nātra saṁśayaḥ ,ityuktvā so'suraḥ pārthaṁ prāgudīcīmagāddiśam.
english: And the great conch of Varuṇa, named Devadatta, which is beautifully resounding - all of this I will give to you; there is no doubt about it. Having said this to Partha, that asura went towards the northeast direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 7. And the great conch of Varuṇa, named Devadatta, which is beautifully resounding - all of this I will give to you; there is no doubt about it. Having said this to Partha, that asura went towards the northeast direction.

sanskrit: उत्तरेण तु कैलासं मैनाकं पर्वतं प्रति ।हिरण्यशृङ्गो भगवान्महामणिमयो गिरिः ॥८॥
transliteration: uttareṇa tu kailāsaṁ mainākaṁ parvataṁ prati ,hiraṇyaśṛṅgo bhagavānmahāmaṇimayo giriḥ.
english: But to the north, towards Mount Kailasa and Mount Mainaka, is the divine mountain, with golden peaks and composed of great jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 8. But to the north, towards Mount Kailasa and Mount Mainaka, is the divine mountain, with golden peaks and composed of great jewels.

sanskrit: रम्यं बिन्दुसरो नाम यत्र राजा भगीरथः ।दृष्ट्वा भागीरथीं गङ्गामुवास बहुलाः समाः ॥९॥
transliteration: ramyaṁ bindusaro nāma yatra rājā bhagīrathaḥ ,dṛṣṭvā bhāgīrathīṁ gaṅgāmuvāsa bahulāḥ samāḥ.
english: There is a beautiful lake named Bindusara, where King Bhagiratha resided for many years, having seen the Ganga (Bhāgīrathī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 9. There is a beautiful lake named Bindusara, where King Bhagiratha resided for many years, having seen the Ganga (Bhāgīrathī).

sanskrit: यत्रेष्ट्वा सर्वभूतानामीश्वरेण महात्मना ।आहृताः क्रतवो मुख्याः शतं भरतसत्तम ॥१०॥
transliteration: yatreṣṭvā sarvabhūtānāmīśvareṇa mahātmanā ,āhṛtāḥ kratavo mukhyāḥ śataṁ bharatasattama.
english: O best among the Bharatas, it was there that the great-souled Lord (Īśvara), having offered a Vedic ritual (yajña) for all beings, performed a hundred principal rituals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 10. O best among the Bharatas, it was there that the great-souled Lord (Īśvara), having offered a Vedic ritual (yajña) for all beings, performed a hundred principal rituals.

sanskrit: यत्र यूपा मणिमयाश्चित्याश्चापि हिरण्मयाः ।शोभार्थं विहितास्तत्र न तु दृष्टान्ततः कृताः ॥११॥
transliteration: yatra yūpā maṇimayāścityāścāpi hiraṇmayāḥ ,śobhārthaṁ vihitāstatra na tu dṛṣṭāntataḥ kṛtāḥ.
english: Where the sacrificial posts were made of gems and the altars were golden, they were constructed there merely for embellishment, and not to serve as an example (for others to follow).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 11. Where the sacrificial posts were made of gems and the altars were golden, they were constructed there merely for embellishment, and not to serve as an example (for others to follow).

sanskrit: यत्रेष्ट्वा स गतः सिद्धिं सहस्राक्षः शचीपतिः ।यत्र भूतपतिः सृष्ट्वा सर्वलोकान्सनातनः ।उपास्यते तिग्मतेजा वृतो भूतैः सहस्रशः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yatreṣṭvā sa gataḥ siddhiṁ sahasrākṣaḥ śacīpatiḥ ,yatra bhūtapatiḥ sṛṣṭvā sarvalokānsanātanaḥ ,upāsyate tigmatejā vṛto bhūtaiḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: It is there where Indra, the thousand-eyed husband of Śacī, attained perfection (siddhi) by performing a sacrifice. It is also there where the eternal lord of beings, Śiva, of fierce splendor, having created all worlds, is worshipped, surrounded by thousands of elemental beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 12. It is there where Indra, the thousand-eyed husband of Śacī, attained perfection (siddhi) by performing a sacrifice. It is also there where the eternal lord of beings, Śiva, of fierce splendor, having created all worlds, is worshipped, surrounded by thousands of elemental beings.

sanskrit: नरनारायणौ ब्रह्मा यमः स्थाणुश्च पञ्चमः ।उपासते यत्र सत्रं सहस्रयुगपर्यये ॥१३॥
transliteration: naranārāyaṇau brahmā yamaḥ sthāṇuśca pañcamaḥ ,upāsate yatra satraṁ sahasrayugaparyaye.
english: It is there where Nara and Nārāyaṇa, Brahmā, Yama, and Sthāṇu (Śiva) as the fifth, perform a sacrificial session (yajña) at the end of a thousand ages (yuga).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 13. It is there where Nara and Nārāyaṇa, Brahmā, Yama, and Sthāṇu (Śiva) as the fifth, perform a sacrificial session (yajña) at the end of a thousand ages (yuga).

sanskrit: यत्रेष्टं वासुदेवेन सत्रैर्वर्षसहस्रकैः ।श्रद्दधानेन सततं शिष्टसंप्रतिपत्तये ॥१४॥
transliteration: yatreṣṭaṁ vāsudevena satrairvarṣasahasrakaiḥ ,śraddadhānena satataṁ śiṣṭasaṁpratipattaye.
english: Where [Vedic rituals (yajña)] were constantly performed by the faithful Vāsudeva (Krishna), with ritual sessions (satra) lasting thousands of years, for the acceptance (saṃpratipatti) of the disciplined (śiṣṭa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 14. Where [Vedic rituals (yajña)] were constantly performed by the faithful Vāsudeva (Krishna), with ritual sessions (satra) lasting thousands of years, for the acceptance (saṃpratipatti) of the disciplined (śiṣṭa).

sanskrit: सुवर्णमालिनो यूपाश्चित्याश्चाप्यतिभास्वराः ।ददौ यत्र सहस्राणि प्रयुतानि च केशवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: suvarṇamālino yūpāścityāścāpyatibhāsvarāḥ ,dadau yatra sahasrāṇi prayutāni ca keśavaḥ.
english: Where Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) donated thousands and millions of sacrificial posts (yūpa) adorned with gold garlands, along with extremely brilliant piled altars (citi).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 15. Where Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) donated thousands and millions of sacrificial posts (yūpa) adorned with gold garlands, along with extremely brilliant piled altars (citi).

sanskrit: तत्र गत्वा स जग्राह गदां शङ्खं च भारत ।स्फाटिकं च सभाद्रव्यं यदासीद्वृषपर्वणः ।किंकरैः सह रक्षोभिरगृह्णात्सर्वमेव तत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tatra gatvā sa jagrāha gadāṁ śaṅkhaṁ ca bhārata ,sphāṭikaṁ ca sabhādravyaṁ yadāsīdvṛṣaparvaṇaḥ ,kiṁkaraiḥ saha rakṣobhiragṛhṇātsarvameva tat.
english: Having gone there, O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata), he seized a mace and a conch, as well as the crystal materials for the assembly hall that had belonged to Vṛṣaparvan. He took all of that with his attendants and Rākṣasas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 16. Having gone there, O Bhārata (descendant of Bharata), he seized a mace and a conch, as well as the crystal materials for the assembly hall that had belonged to Vṛṣaparvan. He took all of that with his attendants and Rākṣasas.

sanskrit: तदाहृत्य तु तां चक्रे सोऽसुरोऽप्रतिमां सभाम् ।विश्रुतां त्रिषु लोकेषु दिव्यां मणिमयीं शुभाम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tadāhṛtya tu tāṁ cakre so'suro'pratimāṁ sabhām ,viśrutāṁ triṣu lokeṣu divyāṁ maṇimayīṁ śubhām.
english: Then, having brought those things, that Asura (demon) created an incomparable assembly hall (sabhā), famous throughout the three worlds, divine, adorned with jewels, and auspicious.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 17. Then, having brought those things, that Asura (demon) created an incomparable assembly hall (sabhā), famous throughout the three worlds, divine, adorned with jewels, and auspicious.

sanskrit: गदां च भीमसेनाय प्रवरां प्रददौ तदा ।देवदत्तं च पार्थाय ददौ शङ्खमनुत्तमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: gadāṁ ca bhīmasenāya pravarāṁ pradadau tadā ,devadattaṁ ca pārthāya dadau śaṅkhamanuttamam.
english: Then he gave an excellent mace to Bhimasena, and he gave the superb conch named Devadatta to Partha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 18. Then he gave an excellent mace to Bhimasena, and he gave the superb conch named Devadatta to Partha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: सभा तु सा महाराज शातकुम्भमयद्रुमा ।दश किष्कुसहस्राणि समन्तादायताभवत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: sabhā tu sā mahārāja śātakumbhamayadrumā ,daśa kiṣkusahasrāṇi samantādāyatābhavat.
english: Indeed, O great king, that assembly hall, adorned with trees made of pure gold, was ten thousand kiṣkus in extent on all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 19. Indeed, O great king, that assembly hall, adorned with trees made of pure gold, was ten thousand kiṣkus in extent on all sides.

sanskrit: यथा वह्नेर्यथार्कस्य सोमस्य च यथैव सा ।भ्राजमाना तथा दिव्या बभार परमं वपुः ॥२०॥
transliteration: yathā vahneryathārkasya somasya ca yathaiva sā ,bhrājamānā tathā divyā babhāra paramaṁ vapuḥ.
english: Just as fire, the sun, and indeed the moon shine, so too that divine assembly hall, resplendent, possessed a supreme beauty.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 20. Just as fire, the sun, and indeed the moon shine, so too that divine assembly hall, resplendent, possessed a supreme beauty.

sanskrit: प्रतिघ्नतीव प्रभया प्रभामर्कस्य भास्वराम् ।प्रबभौ ज्वलमानेव दिव्या दिव्येन वर्चसा ॥२१॥
transliteration: pratighnatīva prabhayā prabhāmarkasya bhāsvarām ,prababhau jvalamāneva divyā divyena varcasā.
english: It shone brilliantly, a divine presence, as if obstructing the dazzling splendor of the sun with its own radiance, blazing with a divine luster.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 21. It shone brilliantly, a divine presence, as if obstructing the dazzling splendor of the sun with its own radiance, blazing with a divine luster.

sanskrit: नगमेघप्रतीकाशा दिवमावृत्य विष्ठिता ।आयता विपुला श्लक्ष्णा विपाप्मा विगतक्लमा ॥२२॥
transliteration: nagameghapratīkāśā divamāvṛtya viṣṭhitā ,āyatā vipulā ślakṣṇā vipāpmā vigataklamā.
english: Resembling mountains and clouds, it stood encompassing the sky; it was extensive, broad, smooth, free from impurity, and devoid of weariness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 22. Resembling mountains and clouds, it stood encompassing the sky; it was extensive, broad, smooth, free from impurity, and devoid of weariness.

sanskrit: उत्तमद्रव्यसंपन्ना मणिप्राकारमालिनी ।बहुरत्ना बहुधना सुकृता विश्वकर्मणा ॥२३॥
transliteration: uttamadravyasaṁpannā maṇiprākāramālinī ,bahuratnā bahudhanā sukṛtā viśvakarmaṇā.
english: It was endowed with excellent materials, adorned with a garland of gem-walls, possessed many jewels and much wealth, and was expertly crafted by Viśvakarman.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 23. It was endowed with excellent materials, adorned with a garland of gem-walls, possessed many jewels and much wealth, and was expertly crafted by Viśvakarman.

sanskrit: न दाशार्ही सुधर्मा वा ब्रह्मणो वापि तादृशी ।आसीद्रूपेण संपन्ना यां चक्रेऽप्रतिमां मयः ॥२४॥
transliteration: na dāśārhī sudharmā vā brahmaṇo vāpi tādṛśī ,āsīdrūpeṇa saṁpannā yāṁ cakre'pratimāṁ mayaḥ.
english: Neither the assembly hall of the Dāśārhas, nor the Sudharmā assembly hall of the gods, nor even the one belonging to Brahmā, was so splendidly endowed with beauty as the incomparable hall that Maya constructed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 24. Neither the assembly hall of the Dāśārhas, nor the Sudharmā assembly hall of the gods, nor even the one belonging to Brahmā, was so splendidly endowed with beauty as the incomparable hall that Maya constructed.

sanskrit: तां स्म तत्र मयेनोक्ता रक्षन्ति च वहन्ति च ।सभामष्टौ सहस्राणि किंकरा नाम राक्षसाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tāṁ sma tatra mayenoktā rakṣanti ca vahanti ca ,sabhāmaṣṭau sahasrāṇi kiṁkarā nāma rākṣasāḥ.
english: Indeed, eight thousand rākṣasas named Kiṅkaras, appointed by Maya, would protect and carry that assembly hall there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 25. Indeed, eight thousand rākṣasas named Kiṅkaras, appointed by Maya, would protect and carry that assembly hall there.

sanskrit: अन्तरिक्षचरा घोरा महाकाया महाबलाः ।रक्ताक्षाः पिङ्गलाक्षाश्च शुक्तिकर्णाः प्रहारिणः ॥२६॥
transliteration: antarikṣacarā ghorā mahākāyā mahābalāḥ ,raktākṣāḥ piṅgalākṣāśca śuktikarṇāḥ prahāriṇaḥ.
english: They were sky-roaming, formidable, with massive bodies and immense strength; they had red eyes, and tawny eyes, shell-like ears, and were prone to striking.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 26. They were sky-roaming, formidable, with massive bodies and immense strength; they had red eyes, and tawny eyes, shell-like ears, and were prone to striking.

sanskrit: तस्यां सभायां नलिनीं चकाराप्रतिमां मयः ।वैडूर्यपत्रविततां मणिनालमयाम्बुजाम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ sabhāyāṁ nalinīṁ cakārāpratimāṁ mayaḥ ,vaiḍūryapatravitatāṁ maṇinālamayāmbujām.
english: In that assembly hall, Maya created an unparalleled lotus pond. Its leaves were spread with cat's eye gems, and its lotuses had stalks made of jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 27. In that assembly hall, Maya created an unparalleled lotus pond. Its leaves were spread with cat's eye gems, and its lotuses had stalks made of jewels.

sanskrit: पद्मसौगन्धिकवतीं नानाद्विजगणायुताम् ।पुष्पितैः पङ्कजैश्चित्रां कूर्ममत्स्यैश्च शोभिताम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: padmasaugandhikavatīṁ nānādvijagaṇāyutām ,puṣpitaiḥ paṅkajaiścitrāṁ kūrmamatsyaiśca śobhitām.
english: It was filled with fragrant lotuses and frequented by flocks of diverse birds. It appeared charming with its blossomed lotuses and was graced by tortoises and fish.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 28. It was filled with fragrant lotuses and frequented by flocks of diverse birds. It appeared charming with its blossomed lotuses and was graced by tortoises and fish.

sanskrit: सूपतीर्थामकलुषां सर्वर्तुसलिलां शुभाम् ।मारुतेनैव चोद्धूतैर्मुक्ताबिन्दुभिराचिताम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: sūpatīrthāmakaluṣāṁ sarvartusalilāṁ śubhām ,mārutenaiva coddhūtairmuktābindubhirācitām.
english: It was furnished with excellent bathing places, clear, pure, and filled with water in all seasons. It was auspicious and covered with pearl-like drops stirred up by the wind itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 29. It was furnished with excellent bathing places, clear, pure, and filled with water in all seasons. It was auspicious and covered with pearl-like drops stirred up by the wind itself.

sanskrit: मणिरत्नचितां तां तु केचिदभ्येत्य पार्थिवाः ।दृष्ट्वापि नाभ्यजानन्त तेऽज्ञानात्प्रपतन्त्युत ॥३०॥
transliteration: maṇiratnacitāṁ tāṁ tu kecidabhyetya pārthivāḥ ,dṛṣṭvāpi nābhyajānanta te'jñānātprapatantyuta.
english: Indeed, some kings, even after approaching and seeing that (assembly hall) adorned with jewels and gems, did not recognize it. Certainly, due to ignorance, they fall into degradation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 30. Indeed, some kings, even after approaching and seeing that (assembly hall) adorned with jewels and gems, did not recognize it. Certainly, due to ignorance, they fall into degradation.

sanskrit: तां सभामभितो नित्यं पुष्पवन्तो महाद्रुमाः ।आसन्नानाविधा नीलाः शीतच्छाया मनोरमाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: tāṁ sabhāmabhito nityaṁ puṣpavanto mahādrumāḥ ,āsannānāvidhā nīlāḥ śītacchāyā manoramāḥ.
english: All around that assembly hall, there were always great trees, full of flowers, of various kinds, dark-colored, cool-shaded, and delightful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 31. All around that assembly hall, there were always great trees, full of flowers, of various kinds, dark-colored, cool-shaded, and delightful.

sanskrit: काननानि सुगन्धीनि पुष्करिण्यश्च सर्वशः ।हंसकारण्डवयुताश्चक्रवाकोपशोभिताः ॥३२॥
transliteration: kānanāni sugandhīni puṣkariṇyaśca sarvaśaḥ ,haṁsakāraṇḍavayutāścakravākopaśobhitāḥ.
english: And everywhere there were fragrant groves and lotus ponds, frequented by swans and ducks, and graced by cakravāka birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 32. And everywhere there were fragrant groves and lotus ponds, frequented by swans and ducks, and graced by cakravāka birds.

sanskrit: जलजानां च माल्यानां स्थलजानां च सर्वशः ।मारुतो गन्धमादाय पाण्डवान्स्म निषेवते ॥३३॥
transliteration: jalajānāṁ ca mālyānāṁ sthalajānāṁ ca sarvaśaḥ ,māruto gandhamādāya pāṇḍavānsma niṣevate.
english: The wind, collecting the fragrance from all kinds of garlands, both those originating from water and those from land, would attend to the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 33. The wind, collecting the fragrance from all kinds of garlands, both those originating from water and those from land, would attend to the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: ईदृशीं तां सभां कृत्वा मासैः परिचतुर्दशैः ।निष्ठितां धर्मराजाय मयो राज्ञे न्यवेदयत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: īdṛśīṁ tāṁ sabhāṁ kṛtvā māsaiḥ paricaturdaśaiḥ ,niṣṭhitāṁ dharmarājāya mayo rājñe nyavedayat.
english: Having constructed and completed that sort of assembly hall in approximately fourteen months, Maya presented it to King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 3 Verse 34. Having constructed and completed that sort of assembly hall in approximately fourteen months, Maya presented it to King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः प्रवेशनं चक्रे तस्यां राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।अयुतं भोजयामास ब्राह्मणानां नराधिपः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ praveśanaṁ cakre tasyāṁ rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,ayutaṁ bhojayāmāsa brāhmaṇānāṁ narādhipaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, the ruler of men, entered that hall and fed ten thousand Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira, the ruler of men, entered that hall and fed ten thousand Brahmins.

sanskrit: घृतपायसेन मधुना भक्ष्यैर्मूलफलैस्तथा ।अहतैश्चैव वासोभिर्माल्यैरुच्चावचैरपि ॥२॥
transliteration: ghṛtapāyasena madhunā bhakṣyairmūlaphalaistathā ,ahataiścaiva vāsobhirmālyairuccāvacairapi.
english: With rice pudding cooked in ghee, honey, various edible roots and fruits, and also with unworn garments and diverse garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 2. With rice pudding cooked in ghee, honey, various edible roots and fruits, and also with unworn garments and diverse garlands.

sanskrit: ददौ तेभ्यः सहस्राणि गवां प्रत्येकशः प्रभुः ।पुण्याहघोषस्तत्रासीद्दिवस्पृगिव भारत ॥३॥
transliteration: dadau tebhyaḥ sahasrāṇi gavāṁ pratyekaśaḥ prabhuḥ ,puṇyāhaghoṣastatrāsīddivaspṛgiva bhārata.
english: The lord (prabhu) gave them thousands of cows, each one. There, a sound of auspicious proclamations (puṇyāhaghoṣa) arose, reaching as if to the heavens, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 3. The lord (prabhu) gave them thousands of cows, each one. There, a sound of auspicious proclamations (puṇyāhaghoṣa) arose, reaching as if to the heavens, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: वादित्रैर्विविधैर्गीतैर्गन्धैरुच्चावचैरपि ।पूजयित्वा कुरुश्रेष्ठो दैवतानि निवेश्य च ॥४॥
transliteration: vāditrairvividhairgītairgandhairuccāvacairapi ,pūjayitvā kuruśreṣṭho daivatāni niveśya ca.
english: With various musical instruments, songs, and diverse fragrances, the best of the Kurus (Kuruśreṣṭha), having worshipped the deities and placed them, also...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 4. With various musical instruments, songs, and diverse fragrances, the best of the Kurus (Kuruśreṣṭha), having worshipped the deities and placed them, also...

sanskrit: तत्र मल्ला नटा झल्लाः सूता वैतालिकास्तथा ।उपतस्थुर्महात्मानं सप्तरात्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tatra mallā naṭā jhallāḥ sūtā vaitālikāstathā ,upatasthurmahātmānaṁ saptarātraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: There, for seven nights, wrestlers, actors, acrobats, bards, and eulogists attended the noble (mahātmā) Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 5. There, for seven nights, wrestlers, actors, acrobats, bards, and eulogists attended the noble (mahātmā) Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: तथा स कृत्वा पूजां तां भ्रातृभिः सह पाण्डवः ।तस्यां सभायां रम्यायां रेमे शक्रो यथा दिवि ॥६॥
transliteration: tathā sa kṛtvā pūjāṁ tāṁ bhrātṛbhiḥ saha pāṇḍavaḥ ,tasyāṁ sabhāyāṁ ramyāyāṁ reme śakro yathā divi.
english: Having thus offered that honor (pūjā) with his brothers, the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) enjoyed himself in that beautiful assembly hall just as Indra delights in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 6. Having thus offered that honor (pūjā) with his brothers, the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira) enjoyed himself in that beautiful assembly hall just as Indra delights in heaven.

sanskrit: सभायामृषयस्तस्यां पाण्डवैः सह आसते ।आसां चक्रुर्नरेन्द्राश्च नानादेशसमागताः ॥७॥
transliteration: sabhāyāmṛṣayastasyāṁ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha āsate ,āsāṁ cakrurnarendrāśca nānādeśasamāgatāḥ.
english: In that assembly hall, sages sat with the Pāṇḍavas. And kings, who had arrived from various countries, also took their seats.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 7. In that assembly hall, sages sat with the Pāṇḍavas. And kings, who had arrived from various countries, also took their seats.

sanskrit: असितो देवलः सत्यः सर्पमाली महाशिराः ।अर्वावसुः सुमित्रश्च मैत्रेयः शुनको बलिः ॥८॥
transliteration: asito devalaḥ satyaḥ sarpamālī mahāśirāḥ ,arvāvasuḥ sumitraśca maitreyaḥ śunako baliḥ.
english: Asita, Devala, Satya, Sarpa-māli, Mahāśirā, Arvāvasu, Sumitra, Maitreya, Śunaka, and Bali.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 8. Asita, Devala, Satya, Sarpa-māli, Mahāśirā, Arvāvasu, Sumitra, Maitreya, Śunaka, and Bali.

sanskrit: बको दाल्भ्यः स्थूलशिराः कृष्णद्वैपायनः शुकः ।सुमन्तुर्जैमिनिः पैलो व्यासशिष्यास्तथा वयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: bako dālbhyaḥ sthūlaśirāḥ kṛṣṇadvaipāyanaḥ śukaḥ ,sumanturjaiminiḥ pailo vyāsaśiṣyāstathā vayam.
english: Baka Dālbhya, Sthūlaśirā, Kṛṣṇadvaipāyana, Śuka, Sumantu, Jaimini, Paila; we are also disciples of Vyāsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 9. Baka Dālbhya, Sthūlaśirā, Kṛṣṇadvaipāyana, Śuka, Sumantu, Jaimini, Paila; we are also disciples of Vyāsa.

sanskrit: तित्तिरिर्याज्ञवल्क्यश्च ससुतो लोमहर्षणः ।अप्सुहोम्यश्च धौम्यश्च आणीमाण्डव्यकौशिकौ ॥१०॥
transliteration: tittiriryājñavalkyaśca sasuto lomaharṣaṇaḥ ,apsuhomyaśca dhaumyaśca āṇīmāṇḍavyakauśikau.
english: Tittiri, Yājñavalkya, and Loma-harṣaṇa with his son; also Apsuhomya, Dhaumya, Āṇī, Māṇḍavya, and Kauśika.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 10. Tittiri, Yājñavalkya, and Loma-harṣaṇa with his son; also Apsuhomya, Dhaumya, Āṇī, Māṇḍavya, and Kauśika.

sanskrit: दामोष्णीषस्त्रैवणिश्च पर्णादो घटजानुकः ।मौञ्जायनो वायुभक्षः पाराशर्यश्च सारिकौ ॥११॥
transliteration: dāmoṣṇīṣastraivaṇiśca parṇādo ghaṭajānukaḥ ,mauñjāyano vāyubhakṣaḥ pārāśaryaśca sārikau.
english: Dāmoshṇīṣa, Traivaṇi, Parṇāda, Ghaṭajānuka, Mauñjāyana, Vāyubhakṣa, Pārāśarya, and the two Sārikas (are mentioned).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 11. Dāmoshṇīṣa, Traivaṇi, Parṇāda, Ghaṭajānuka, Mauñjāyana, Vāyubhakṣa, Pārāśarya, and the two Sārikas (are mentioned).

sanskrit: बलवाकः शिनीवाकः सुत्यपालः कृतश्रमः ।जातूकर्णः शिखावांश्च सुबलः पारिजातकः ॥१२॥
transliteration: balavākaḥ śinīvākaḥ sutyapālaḥ kṛtaśramaḥ ,jātūkarṇaḥ śikhāvāṁśca subalaḥ pārijātakaḥ.
english: Balavāka, Śinīvāka, Sutyapāla, Kṛtaśrama, Jātūkarṇa, Śikhāvan, Subala, and Pārijātaka (are mentioned).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 12. Balavāka, Śinīvāka, Sutyapāla, Kṛtaśrama, Jātūkarṇa, Śikhāvan, Subala, and Pārijātaka (are mentioned).

sanskrit: पर्वतश्च महाभागो मार्कण्डेयस्तथा मुनिः ।पवित्रपाणिः सावर्णिर्भालुकिर्गालवस्तथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: parvataśca mahābhāgo mārkaṇḍeyastathā muniḥ ,pavitrapāṇiḥ sāvarṇirbhālukirgālavastathā.
english: Parvata, who is greatly fortunate (mahābhāga), and Mārkaṇḍeya, the sage (muni); likewise, Pavitrapāṇi, Sāvarṇi, Bhāluki, and Gālava (are present).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 13. Parvata, who is greatly fortunate (mahābhāga), and Mārkaṇḍeya, the sage (muni); likewise, Pavitrapāṇi, Sāvarṇi, Bhāluki, and Gālava (are present).

sanskrit: जङ्घाबन्धुश्च रैभ्यश्च कोपवेगश्रवा भृगुः ।हरिबभ्रुश्च कौण्डिन्यो बभ्रुमाली सनातनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: jaṅghābandhuśca raibhyaśca kopavegaśravā bhṛguḥ ,haribabhruśca kauṇḍinyo babhrumālī sanātanaḥ.
english: There were also Jaṅghābandhu, Raibhya, Kopavegaśravā, Bhṛgu, Haribabhru, Kauṇḍinya, Babhrumālī, and Sanātana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 14. There were also Jaṅghābandhu, Raibhya, Kopavegaśravā, Bhṛgu, Haribabhru, Kauṇḍinya, Babhrumālī, and Sanātana.

sanskrit: कक्षीवानौशिजश्चैव नाचिकेतोऽथ गौतमः ।पैङ्गो वराहः शुनकः शाण्डिल्यश्च महातपाः ।कर्करो वेणुजङ्घश्च कलापः कठ एव च ॥१५॥
transliteration: kakṣīvānauśijaścaiva nāciketo'tha gautamaḥ ,paiṅgo varāhaḥ śunakaḥ śāṇḍilyaśca mahātapāḥ ,karkaro veṇujaṅghaśca kalāpaḥ kaṭha eva ca.
english: Kakṣīvān, Auśija, and Nāciketa, then Gautama; Paiṅga, Varāha, Śunaka, and Śāṇḍilya, the one of great austerity (tapas); Karkara, Veṇujaṅgha, and Kalāpa, and Kaṭha as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 15. Kakṣīvān, Auśija, and Nāciketa, then Gautama; Paiṅga, Varāha, Śunaka, and Śāṇḍilya, the one of great austerity (tapas); Karkara, Veṇujaṅgha, and Kalāpa, and Kaṭha as well.

sanskrit: मुनयो धर्मसहिता धृतात्मानो जितेन्द्रियाः ।एते चान्ये च बहवो वेदवेदाङ्गपारगाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: munayo dharmasahitā dhṛtātmāno jitendriyāḥ ,ete cānye ca bahavo vedavedāṅgapāragāḥ.
english: These sages, accompanied by (natural) law (dharma), with firm self-control (ātman), and having conquered their senses, along with many other masters of the Vedas and Vedāṅgas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 16. These sages, accompanied by (natural) law (dharma), with firm self-control (ātman), and having conquered their senses, along with many other masters of the Vedas and Vedāṅgas.

sanskrit: उपासते महात्मानं सभायामृषिसत्तमाः ।कथयन्तः कथाः पुण्या धर्मज्ञाः शुचयोऽमलाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: upāsate mahātmānaṁ sabhāyāmṛṣisattamāḥ ,kathayantaḥ kathāḥ puṇyā dharmajñāḥ śucayo'malāḥ.
english: The most excellent sages, who are knowers of natural law (dharma), pure, and spotless, attend upon the noble one (mahātman) in the assembly, narrating sacred stories.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 17. The most excellent sages, who are knowers of natural law (dharma), pure, and spotless, attend upon the noble one (mahātman) in the assembly, narrating sacred stories.

sanskrit: तथैव क्षत्रियश्रेष्ठा धर्मराजमुपासते ।श्रीमान्महात्मा धर्मात्मा मुञ्जकेतुर्विवर्धनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathaiva kṣatriyaśreṣṭhā dharmarājamupāsate ,śrīmānmahātmā dharmātmā muñjaketurvivardhanaḥ.
english: Similarly, the finest warriors (kṣatriya) attend upon Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), including the glorious (śrīmān), noble (mahātman), and righteous (dharmātman) Muñjaketu, the promoter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 18. Similarly, the finest warriors (kṣatriya) attend upon Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), including the glorious (śrīmān), noble (mahātman), and righteous (dharmātman) Muñjaketu, the promoter.

sanskrit: संग्रामजिद्दुर्मुखश्च उग्रसेनश्च वीर्यवान् ।कक्षसेनः क्षितिपतिः क्षेमकश्चापराजितः ।काम्बोजराजः कमलः कम्पनश्च महाबलः ॥१९॥
transliteration: saṁgrāmajiddurmukhaśca ugrasenaśca vīryavān ,kakṣasenaḥ kṣitipatiḥ kṣemakaścāparājitaḥ ,kāmbojarājaḥ kamalaḥ kampanaśca mahābalaḥ.
english: Saṃgrāmajit and Durmukha, and the valorous Ugrasena; Kakṣasena, the king, and the unconquered Kṣemaka; Kamala, the king of Kambojas, and the mighty Kampana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 19. Saṃgrāmajit and Durmukha, and the valorous Ugrasena; Kakṣasena, the king, and the unconquered Kṣemaka; Kamala, the king of Kambojas, and the mighty Kampana.

sanskrit: सततं कम्पयामास यवनानेक एव यः ।यथासुरान्कालकेयान्देवो वज्रधरस्तथा ॥२०॥
transliteration: satataṁ kampayāmāsa yavanāneka eva yaḥ ,yathāsurānkālakeyāndevo vajradharastathā.
english: He alone constantly made the Yavanas tremble, just as the thunderbolt-wielding deity [Indra] made the Kālakeyā asuras tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 20. He alone constantly made the Yavanas tremble, just as the thunderbolt-wielding deity [Indra] made the Kālakeyā asuras tremble.

sanskrit: जटासुरो मद्रकान्तश्च राजा कुन्तिः कुणिन्दश्च किरातराजः ।तथाङ्गवङ्गौ सह पुण्ड्रकेण पाण्ड्योड्रराजौ सह चान्ध्रकेण ॥२१॥
transliteration: jaṭāsuro madrakāntaśca rājā; kuntiḥ kuṇindaśca kirātarājaḥ ,tathāṅgavaṅgau saha puṇḍrakeṇa; pāṇḍyoḍrarājau saha cāndhrakeṇa.
english: Jaṭāsura, Madrakānta, King Kunti, Kuṇinda, and the Kirāta king were present. Also among them were Aṅga and Vaṅga with Puṇḍraka, and the kings of Pāṇḍya and Oḍra with Āndhraka.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 21. Jaṭāsura, Madrakānta, King Kunti, Kuṇinda, and the Kirāta king were present. Also among them were Aṅga and Vaṅga with Puṇḍraka, and the kings of Pāṇḍya and Oḍra with Āndhraka.

sanskrit: किरातराजः सुमना यवनाधिपतिस्तथा ।चाणूरो देवरातश्च भोजो भीमरथश्च यः ॥२२॥
transliteration: kirātarājaḥ sumanā yavanādhipatistathā ,cāṇūro devarātaśca bhojo bhīmarathaśca yaḥ.
english: The Kirāta king Sumanā, and the lord of the Yavanas, were also present. As were Cāṇūra, Devarāta, Bhoja, and Bhīmaratha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 22. The Kirāta king Sumanā, and the lord of the Yavanas, were also present. As were Cāṇūra, Devarāta, Bhoja, and Bhīmaratha.

sanskrit: श्रुतायुधश्च कालिङ्गो जयत्सेनश्च मागधः ।सुशर्मा चेकितानश्च सुरथोऽमित्रकर्षणः ॥२३॥
transliteration: śrutāyudhaśca kāliṅgo jayatsenaśca māgadhaḥ ,suśarmā cekitānaśca suratho'mitrakarṣaṇaḥ.
english: Śrutāyudha and the king of Kaliṅga, Jayatsena and the king of Magadha, Suśarmā and Cekitāna, and Suratha, the tormentor of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 23. Śrutāyudha and the king of Kaliṅga, Jayatsena and the king of Magadha, Suśarmā and Cekitāna, and Suratha, the tormentor of enemies.

sanskrit: केतुमान्वसुदानश्च वैदेहोऽथ कृतक्षणः ।सुधर्मा चानिरुद्धश्च श्रुतायुश्च महाबलः ॥२४॥
transliteration: ketumānvasudānaśca vaideho'tha kṛtakṣaṇaḥ ,sudharmā cāniruddhaśca śrutāyuśca mahābalaḥ.
english: Ketumān, Vasudāna, and the king of Videha, then Kṛtakṣaṇa; also Sudharmā, Aniruddha, and Śrutāyu, the very mighty one.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 24. Ketumān, Vasudāna, and the king of Videha, then Kṛtakṣaṇa; also Sudharmā, Aniruddha, and Śrutāyu, the very mighty one.

sanskrit: अनूपराजो दुर्धर्षः क्षेमजिच्च सुदक्षिणः ।शिशुपालः सहसुतः करूषाधिपतिस्तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: anūparājo durdharṣaḥ kṣemajicca sudakṣiṇaḥ ,śiśupālaḥ sahasutaḥ karūṣādhipatistathā.
english: The king of Anūpa, Durdharṣa, Kṣemajit, and Sudakṣiṇa; also Śiśupāla with his sons, and the lord of Karūṣa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 25. The king of Anūpa, Durdharṣa, Kṣemajit, and Sudakṣiṇa; also Śiśupāla with his sons, and the lord of Karūṣa.

sanskrit: वृष्णीनां चैव दुर्धर्षाः कुमारा देवरूपिणः ।आहुको विपृथुश्चैव गदः सारण एव च ॥२६॥
transliteration: vṛṣṇīnāṁ caiva durdharṣāḥ kumārā devarūpiṇaḥ ,āhuko vipṛthuścaiva gadaḥ sāraṇa eva ca.
english: Among the Vrishnis were formidable princes, god-like in form: Ahuka, Vipṛthu, Gada, and Sāraṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 26. Among the Vrishnis were formidable princes, god-like in form: Ahuka, Vipṛthu, Gada, and Sāraṇa.

sanskrit: अक्रूरः कृतवर्मा च सात्यकिश्च शिनेः सुतः ।भीष्मकोऽथाहृतिश्चैव द्युमत्सेनश्च वीर्यवान् ।केकयाश्च महेष्वासा यज्ञसेनश्च सौमकिः ॥२७॥
transliteration: akrūraḥ kṛtavarmā ca sātyakiśca śineḥ sutaḥ ,bhīṣmako'thāhṛtiścaiva dyumatsenaśca vīryavān ,kekayāśca maheṣvāsā yajñasenaśca saumakiḥ.
english: Akrūra, Kṛtavarmā, and Sātyaki (the son of Śini), as well as Bhīṣmaka, Āhṛti, and the valorous Dyumatsena, were present. Also among them were the Kekayas, who were great archers, and Yajñasena, the son of Soma.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 27. Akrūra, Kṛtavarmā, and Sātyaki (the son of Śini), as well as Bhīṣmaka, Āhṛti, and the valorous Dyumatsena, were present. Also among them were the Kekayas, who were great archers, and Yajñasena, the son of Soma.

sanskrit: अर्जुनं चापि संश्रित्य राजपुत्रा महाबलाः ।अशिक्षन्त धनुर्वेदं रौरवाजिनवाससः ॥२८॥
transliteration: arjunaṁ cāpi saṁśritya rājaputrā mahābalāḥ ,aśikṣanta dhanurvedaṁ rauravājinavāsasaḥ.
english: Moreover, the mighty princes, clad in deer skin garments, learned the science of archery, relying on Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 28. Moreover, the mighty princes, clad in deer skin garments, learned the science of archery, relying on Arjuna.

sanskrit: तत्रैव शिक्षिता राजन्कुमारा वृष्णिनन्दनाः ।रौक्मिणेयश्च साम्बश्च युयुधानश्च सात्यकिः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tatraiva śikṣitā rājankumārā vṛṣṇinandanāḥ ,raukmiṇeyaśca sāmbaśca yuyudhānaśca sātyakiḥ.
english: O king, there itself were trained the Vṛṣṇi princes: Pradyumna (Raukmiṇeya), Samba, Yuyudhana, and Satyaki.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 29. O king, there itself were trained the Vṛṣṇi princes: Pradyumna (Raukmiṇeya), Samba, Yuyudhana, and Satyaki.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो राजानः पृथिवीपते ।धनंजयसखा चात्र नित्यमास्ते स्म तुम्बुरुः ॥३०॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo rājānaḥ pṛthivīpate ,dhanaṁjayasakhā cātra nityamāste sma tumburuḥ.
english: And O lord of the earth, these and many other kings were present there. And here, Tumburu, the friend of Arjuna (Dhanañjaya), always used to stay.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 30. And O lord of the earth, these and many other kings were present there. And here, Tumburu, the friend of Arjuna (Dhanañjaya), always used to stay.

sanskrit: चित्रसेनः सहामात्यो गन्धर्वाप्सरसस्तथा ।गीतवादित्रकुशलाः शम्यातालविशारदाः ॥३१॥
transliteration: citrasenaḥ sahāmātyo gandharvāpsarasastathā ,gītavāditrakuśalāḥ śamyātālaviśāradāḥ.
english: Chitrasena, accompanied by his ministers, and similarly Gandharvas and Apsaras, who were skilled in singing and playing musical instruments, and experts in rhythm and time-keeping, were also present.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 31. Chitrasena, accompanied by his ministers, and similarly Gandharvas and Apsaras, who were skilled in singing and playing musical instruments, and experts in rhythm and time-keeping, were also present.

sanskrit: प्रमाणेऽथ लयस्थाने किंनराः कृतनिश्रमाः ।संचोदितास्तुम्बुरुणा गन्धर्वाः सहिता जगुः ॥३२॥
transliteration: pramāṇe'tha layasthāne kiṁnarāḥ kṛtaniśramāḥ ,saṁcoditāstumburuṇā gandharvāḥ sahitā jaguḥ.
english: Then, the Kinnaras, having perfected their skill in rhythm and tempo, were prompted by Tumburu, and the Gandharvas sang together.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 32. Then, the Kinnaras, having perfected their skill in rhythm and tempo, were prompted by Tumburu, and the Gandharvas sang together.

sanskrit: गायन्ति दिव्यतानैस्ते यथान्यायं मनस्विनः ।पाण्डुपुत्रानृषींश्चैव रमयन्त उपासते ॥३३॥
transliteration: gāyanti divyatānaiste yathānyāyaṁ manasvinaḥ ,pāṇḍuputrānṛṣīṁścaiva ramayanta upāsate.
english: Those high-minded (manasvin) beings sing divine melodies in perfect accordance with the rules, delighting the sons of Pāṇḍu and the sages as they attend upon them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 33. Those high-minded (manasvin) beings sing divine melodies in perfect accordance with the rules, delighting the sons of Pāṇḍu and the sages as they attend upon them.

sanskrit: तस्यां सभायामासीनाः सुव्रताः सत्यसंगराः ।दिवीव देवा ब्रह्माणं युधिष्ठिरमुपासते ॥३४॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ sabhāyāmāsīnāḥ suvratāḥ satyasaṁgarāḥ ,divīva devā brahmāṇaṁ yudhiṣṭhiramupāsate.
english: In that assembly hall, those who are firm in excellent vows (suvrata) and truthful in their engagements (satyasaṅgara) are seated. They attend upon Yudhiṣṭhira just as the gods attend upon Brahmā in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 4 Verse 34. In that assembly hall, those who are firm in excellent vows (suvrata) and truthful in their engagements (satyasaṅgara) are seated. They attend upon Yudhiṣṭhira just as the gods attend upon Brahmā in heaven.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा तत्रोपविष्टेषु पाण्डवेषु महात्मसु ।महत्सु चोपविष्टेषु गन्धर्वेषु च भारत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā tatropaviṣṭeṣu pāṇḍaveṣu mahātmasu ,mahatsu copaviṣṭeṣu gandharveṣu ca bhārata.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), at that time, as the great-souled Pāṇḍavas and also the great Gandharvas were seated there...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O descendant of Bharata (bhārata), at that time, as the great-souled Pāṇḍavas and also the great Gandharvas were seated there...

sanskrit: लोकाननुचरन्सर्वानागमत्तां सभामृषिः ।नारदः सुमहातेजा ऋषिभिः सहितस्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: lokānanucaransarvānāgamattāṁ sabhāmṛṣiḥ ,nāradaḥ sumahātejā ṛṣibhiḥ sahitastadā.
english: Then, the sage Nārada, of immense splendor, traversing all the worlds, arrived at that assembly, accompanied by other sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 2. Then, the sage Nārada, of immense splendor, traversing all the worlds, arrived at that assembly, accompanied by other sages.

sanskrit: पारिजातेन राजेन्द्र रैवतेन च धीमता ।सुमुखेन च सौम्येन देवर्षिरमितद्युतिः ।सभास्थान्पाण्डवान्द्रष्टुं प्रीयमाणो मनोजवः ॥३॥
transliteration: pārijātena rājendra raivatena ca dhīmatā ,sumukhena ca saumyena devarṣiramitadyutiḥ ,sabhāsthānpāṇḍavāndraṣṭuṁ prīyamāṇo manojavaḥ.
english: O king of kings (rājendra), the divine sage (devarṣi) Nārada, who possessed immeasurable splendor (amitadyuti) and was swift as thought (manojavāḥ), arrived, accompanied by Pārijāta, the intelligent Raivata, and the gentle Sumukha, delighted to behold the Pāṇḍavas present in the assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 3. O king of kings (rājendra), the divine sage (devarṣi) Nārada, who possessed immeasurable splendor (amitadyuti) and was swift as thought (manojavāḥ), arrived, accompanied by Pārijāta, the intelligent Raivata, and the gentle Sumukha, delighted to behold the Pāṇḍavas present in the assembly.

sanskrit: तमागतमृषिं दृष्ट्वा नारदं सर्वधर्मवित् ।सहसा पाण्डवश्रेष्ठः प्रत्युत्थायानुजैः सह ।अभ्यवादयत प्रीत्या विनयावनतस्तदा ॥४॥
transliteration: tamāgatamṛṣiṁ dṛṣṭvā nāradaṁ sarvadharmavit ,sahasā pāṇḍavaśreṣṭhaḥ pratyutthāyānujaiḥ saha ,abhyavādayata prītyā vinayāvanatastadā.
english: Seeing the arrived sage Nārada, the best of the Pāṇḍavas, who was conversant with all principles of natural law (dharma), immediately rose with his younger brothers. Then, humble and bowing with devotion, he greeted him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 4. Seeing the arrived sage Nārada, the best of the Pāṇḍavas, who was conversant with all principles of natural law (dharma), immediately rose with his younger brothers. Then, humble and bowing with devotion, he greeted him.

sanskrit: तदर्हमासनं तस्मै संप्रदाय यथाविधि ।अर्चयामास रत्नैश्च सर्वकामैश्च धर्मवित् ॥५॥
transliteration: tadarhamāsanaṁ tasmai saṁpradāya yathāvidhi ,arcayāmāsa ratnaiśca sarvakāmaiśca dharmavit.
english: The one conversant with natural law (dharma), having duly offered him a seat worthy of him, worshipped him with jewels and with all desired objects.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 5. The one conversant with natural law (dharma), having duly offered him a seat worthy of him, worshipped him with jewels and with all desired objects.

sanskrit: सोऽर्चितः पाण्डवैः सर्वैर्महर्षिर्वेदपारगः ।धर्मकामार्थसंयुक्तं पप्रच्छेदं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥६॥
transliteration: so'rcitaḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ sarvairmaharṣirvedapāragaḥ ,dharmakāmārthasaṁyuktaṁ papracchedaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: The great sage (maharṣi), who was conversant with all the Vedas and had been worshipped by all the Pāṇḍavas, then asked Yudhiṣṭhira about matters related to natural law (dharma), legitimate desire (kāma), and material prosperity (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 6. The great sage (maharṣi), who was conversant with all the Vedas and had been worshipped by all the Pāṇḍavas, then asked Yudhiṣṭhira about matters related to natural law (dharma), legitimate desire (kāma), and material prosperity (artha).

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।कच्चिदर्थाश्च कल्पन्ते धर्मे च रमते मनः ।सुखानि चानुभूयन्ते मनश्च न विहन्यते ॥७॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,kaccidarthāśca kalpante dharme ca ramate manaḥ ,sukhāni cānubhūyante manaśca na vihanyate.
english: Nārada said: Do your material pursuits (artha) flourish? Does your mind (manas) find joy in adhering to natural law (dharma)? Are you experiencing happiness? And is your mind not troubled?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 7. Nārada said: Do your material pursuits (artha) flourish? Does your mind (manas) find joy in adhering to natural law (dharma)? Are you experiencing happiness? And is your mind not troubled?

sanskrit: कच्चिदाचरितां पूर्वैर्नरदेव पितामहैः ।वर्तसे वृत्तिमक्षीणां धर्मार्थसहितां नृषु ॥८॥
transliteration: kaccidācaritāṁ pūrvairnaradeva pitāmahaiḥ ,vartase vṛttimakṣīṇāṁ dharmārthasahitāṁ nṛṣu.
english: O king, I hope you are upholding among your people that undiminished conduct (vṛtti) which was observed by your forefathers, and which encompasses both natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 8. O king, I hope you are upholding among your people that undiminished conduct (vṛtti) which was observed by your forefathers, and which encompasses both natural law (dharma) and material prosperity (artha).

sanskrit: कच्चिदर्थेन वा धर्मं धर्मेणार्थमथापि वा ।उभौ वा प्रीतिसारेण न कामेन प्रबाधसे ॥९॥
transliteration: kaccidarthena vā dharmaṁ dharmeṇārthamathāpi vā ,ubhau vā prītisāreṇa na kāmena prabādhase.
english: I hope you do not hinder natural law (dharma) for the sake of material pursuits (artha), nor material pursuits for the sake of natural law (dharma). Do you not obstruct both out of mere desire (kāma), but rather through a core of affection?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 9. I hope you do not hinder natural law (dharma) for the sake of material pursuits (artha), nor material pursuits for the sake of natural law (dharma). Do you not obstruct both out of mere desire (kāma), but rather through a core of affection?

sanskrit: कच्चिदर्थं च धर्मं च कामं च जयतां वर ।विभज्य काले कालज्ञ सदा वरद सेवसे ॥१०॥
transliteration: kaccidarthaṁ ca dharmaṁ ca kāmaṁ ca jayatāṁ vara ,vibhajya kāle kālajña sadā varada sevase.
english: O best among conquerors, O knower of timing, O bestower of boons, I hope you always pursue material prosperity, righteous conduct (dharma), and pleasure, distributing them appropriately at the right time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 10. O best among conquerors, O knower of timing, O bestower of boons, I hope you always pursue material prosperity, righteous conduct (dharma), and pleasure, distributing them appropriately at the right time.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्राजगुणैः षड्भिः सप्तोपायांस्तथानघ ।बलाबलं तथा सम्यक्चतुर्दश परीक्षसे ॥११॥
transliteration: kaccidrājaguṇaiḥ ṣaḍbhiḥ saptopāyāṁstathānagha ,balābalaṁ tathā samyakcaturdaśa parīkṣase.
english: O faultless one, I hope you thoroughly examine the six strategic measures, the seven expedients (upāyāḥ), and the relative strengths and weaknesses, as well as the fourteen (points of investigation).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 11. O faultless one, I hope you thoroughly examine the six strategic measures, the seven expedients (upāyāḥ), and the relative strengths and weaknesses, as well as the fourteen (points of investigation).

sanskrit: कच्चिदात्मानमन्वीक्ष्य परांश्च जयतां वर ।तथा संधाय कर्माणि अष्टौ भारत सेवसे ॥१२॥
transliteration: kaccidātmānamanvīkṣya parāṁśca jayatāṁ vara ,tathā saṁdhāya karmāṇi aṣṭau bhārata sevase.
english: O best among conquerors, O descendant of Bharata, I hope that, having fully investigated yourself (ātman) and others, you properly manage the eight types of activities (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 12. O best among conquerors, O descendant of Bharata, I hope that, having fully investigated yourself (ātman) and others, you properly manage the eight types of activities (karma).

sanskrit: कच्चित्प्रकृतयः षट्ते न लुप्ता भरतर्षभ ।आढ्यास्तथाव्यसनिनः स्वनुरक्ताश्च सर्वशः ॥१३॥
transliteration: kaccitprakṛtayaḥ ṣaṭte na luptā bharatarṣabha ,āḍhyāstathāvyasaninaḥ svanuraktāśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: O best of Bharatas, I hope that your six essential constituents (prakṛti) are not neglected, and that they are prosperous, free from vices, and completely loyal in all respects.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 13. O best of Bharatas, I hope that your six essential constituents (prakṛti) are not neglected, and that they are prosperous, free from vices, and completely loyal in all respects.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न तर्कैर्दूतैर्वा ये चाप्यपरिशङ्किताः ।त्वत्तो वा तव वामात्यैर्भिद्यते जातु मन्त्रितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: kaccinna tarkairdūtairvā ye cāpyapariśaṅkitāḥ ,tvatto vā tava vāmātyairbhidyate jātu mantritam.
english: I hope that your secret counsel is never revealed by conjectures, or by messengers, or even by unsuspecting individuals, nor by yourself, nor by your ministers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 14. I hope that your secret counsel is never revealed by conjectures, or by messengers, or even by unsuspecting individuals, nor by yourself, nor by your ministers.

sanskrit: कच्चित्संधिं यथाकालं विग्रहं चोपसेवसे ।कच्चिद्वृत्तिमुदासीने मध्यमे चानुवर्तसे ॥१५॥
transliteration: kaccitsaṁdhiṁ yathākālaṁ vigrahaṁ copasevase ,kaccidvṛttimudāsīne madhyame cānuvartase.
english: I hope that you appropriately employ policies of peace (sandhi) and war (vigraha). And I hope that you maintain the correct conduct (vṛtti) towards neutral (udāsīna) and middle (madhyama) states.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 15. I hope that you appropriately employ policies of peace (sandhi) and war (vigraha). And I hope that you maintain the correct conduct (vṛtti) towards neutral (udāsīna) and middle (madhyama) states.

sanskrit: कच्चिदात्मसमा बुद्ध्या शुचयो जीवितक्षमाः ।कुलीनाश्चानुरक्ताश्च कृतास्ते वीर मन्त्रिणः ॥१६॥
transliteration: kaccidātmasamā buddhyā śucayo jīvitakṣamāḥ ,kulīnāścānuraktāśca kṛtāste vīra mantriṇaḥ.
english: O hero, I hope that your ministers are those who are equal to you in intellect, pure, capable of safeguarding your life, of noble lineage, and loyal.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 16. O hero, I hope that your ministers are those who are equal to you in intellect, pure, capable of safeguarding your life, of noble lineage, and loyal.

sanskrit: विजयो मन्त्रमूलो हि राज्ञां भवति भारत ।सुसंवृतो मन्त्रधनैरमात्यैः शास्त्रकोविदैः ॥१७॥
transliteration: vijayo mantramūlo hi rājñāṁ bhavati bhārata ,susaṁvṛto mantradhanairamātyaiḥ śāstrakovidaiḥ.
english: Indeed, O Bhārata, the victory of kings truly has its foundation in counsel, and it is well-protected by ministers who possess abundant counsel and are expert in the scriptures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 17. Indeed, O Bhārata, the victory of kings truly has its foundation in counsel, and it is well-protected by ministers who possess abundant counsel and are expert in the scriptures.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्निद्रावशं नैषि कच्चित्काले विबुध्यसे ।कच्चिच्चापररात्रेषु चिन्तयस्यर्थमर्थवित् ॥१८॥
transliteration: kaccinnidrāvaśaṁ naiṣi kaccitkāle vibudhyase ,kacciccāpararātreṣu cintayasyarthamarthavit.
english: I hope you do not succumb to sleep, and that you awaken at the proper time. And I hope that, being well-versed in matters of state, you reflect upon important affairs in the latter part of the night.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 18. I hope you do not succumb to sleep, and that you awaken at the proper time. And I hope that, being well-versed in matters of state, you reflect upon important affairs in the latter part of the night.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्मन्त्रयसे नैकः कच्चिन्न बहुभिः सह ।कच्चित्ते मन्त्रितो मन्त्रो न राष्ट्रमनुधावति ॥१९॥
transliteration: kaccinmantrayase naikaḥ kaccinna bahubhiḥ saha ,kaccitte mantrito mantro na rāṣṭramanudhāvati.
english: I hope you do not deliberate alone, nor do you consult with many. I trust that the counsel you have deliberated upon does not become public knowledge throughout the kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 19. I hope you do not deliberate alone, nor do you consult with many. I trust that the counsel you have deliberated upon does not become public knowledge throughout the kingdom.

sanskrit: कच्चिदर्थान्विनिश्चित्य लघुमूलान्महोदयान् ।क्षिप्रमारभसे कर्तुं न विघ्नयसि तादृशान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: kaccidarthānviniścitya laghumūlānmahodayān ,kṣipramārabhase kartuṁ na vighnayasi tādṛśān.
english: I hope that, having thoroughly ascertained those affairs that require little initial investment but yield great results, you quickly begin to implement them, and do not obstruct such endeavors.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 20. I hope that, having thoroughly ascertained those affairs that require little initial investment but yield great results, you quickly begin to implement them, and do not obstruct such endeavors.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न सर्वे कर्मान्ताः परोक्षास्ते विशङ्किताः ।सर्वे वा पुनरुत्सृष्टाः संसृष्टं ह्यत्र कारणम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: kaccinna sarve karmāntāḥ parokṣāste viśaṅkitāḥ ,sarve vā punarutsṛṣṭāḥ saṁsṛṣṭaṁ hyatra kāraṇam.
english: I trust that not all your enterprises are kept hidden and consequently viewed with suspicion. Nor are all of them simply abandoned. For, in this matter, a mixed approach is indeed the proper strategy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 21. I trust that not all your enterprises are kept hidden and consequently viewed with suspicion. Nor are all of them simply abandoned. For, in this matter, a mixed approach is indeed the proper strategy.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्राजन्कृतान्येव कृतप्रायाणि वा पुनः ।विदुस्ते वीर कर्माणि नानवाप्तानि कानिचित् ॥२२॥
transliteration: kaccidrājankṛtānyeva kṛtaprāyāṇi vā punaḥ ,viduste vīra karmāṇi nānavāptāni kānicit.
english: O King, O hero, I hope that they are aware of all your undertakings (karma), both those that are completed and those that are nearly completed, and that none remain unaccomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 22. O King, O hero, I hope that they are aware of all your undertakings (karma), both those that are completed and those that are nearly completed, and that none remain unaccomplished.

sanskrit: कच्चित्कारणिकाः सर्वे सर्वशास्त्रेषु कोविदाः ।कारयन्ति कुमारांश्च योधमुख्यांश्च सर्वशः ॥२३॥
transliteration: kaccitkāraṇikāḥ sarve sarvaśāstreṣu kovidāḥ ,kārayanti kumārāṁśca yodhamukhyāṁśca sarvaśaḥ.
