english: I hope that all your experts, who are proficient in all branches of knowledge (śāstra), effectively instruct the princes and chief warriors in every respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 23. I hope that all your experts, who are proficient in all branches of knowledge (śāstra), effectively instruct the princes and chief warriors in every respect.

sanskrit: कच्चित्सहस्रैर्मूर्खाणामेकं क्रीणासि पण्डितम् ।पण्डितो ह्यर्थकृच्छ्रेषु कुर्यान्निःश्रेयसं परम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: kaccitsahasrairmūrkhāṇāmekaṁ krīṇāsi paṇḍitam ,paṇḍito hyarthakṛcchreṣu kuryānniḥśreyasaṁ param.
english: I hope that you would acquire one wise person (paṇḍita) in exchange for a thousand fools. Indeed, a wise person (paṇḍita) can achieve the supreme benefit (niḥśreyasa) in times of financial or circumstantial difficulty.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 24. I hope that you would acquire one wise person (paṇḍita) in exchange for a thousand fools. Indeed, a wise person (paṇḍita) can achieve the supreme benefit (niḥśreyasa) in times of financial or circumstantial difficulty.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्दुर्गाणि सर्वाणि धनधान्यायुधोदकैः ।यन्त्रैश्च परिपूर्णानि तथा शिल्पिधनुर्धरैः ॥२५॥
transliteration: kacciddurgāṇi sarvāṇi dhanadhānyāyudhodakaiḥ ,yantraiśca paripūrṇāni tathā śilpidhanurdharaiḥ.
english: I hope that all your forts are well-supplied with wealth, grain, weapons, and water, and also with instruments, and similarly, fully manned by artisans and archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 25. I hope that all your forts are well-supplied with wealth, grain, weapons, and water, and also with instruments, and similarly, fully manned by artisans and archers.

sanskrit: एकोऽप्यमात्यो मेधावी शूरो दान्तो विचक्षणः ।राजानं राजपुत्रं वा प्रापयेन्महतीं श्रियम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: eko'pyamātyo medhāvī śūro dānto vicakṣaṇaḥ ,rājānaṁ rājaputraṁ vā prāpayenmahatīṁ śriyam.
english: Even a single minister who is intelligent, brave, self-controlled, and discerning can bring great prosperity to a king or a prince.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 26. Even a single minister who is intelligent, brave, self-controlled, and discerning can bring great prosperity to a king or a prince.

sanskrit: कच्चिदष्टादशान्येषु स्वपक्षे दश पञ्च च ।त्रिभिस्त्रिभिरविज्ञातैर्वेत्सि तीर्थानि चारकैः ॥२७॥
transliteration: kaccidaṣṭādaśānyeṣu svapakṣe daśa pañca ca ,tribhistribhiravijñātairvetsi tīrthāni cārakaiḥ.
english: I hope that, by means of three distinct groups of unknown agents (cārakaiḥ), you are keeping informed about the eighteen key departments (tīrthāni) in the enemy's kingdom and the fifteen in your own administration.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 27. I hope that, by means of three distinct groups of unknown agents (cārakaiḥ), you are keeping informed about the eighteen key departments (tīrthāni) in the enemy's kingdom and the fifteen in your own administration.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्द्विषामविदितः प्रतियत्तश्च सर्वदा ।नित्ययुक्तो रिपून्सर्वान्वीक्षसे रिपुसूदन ॥२८॥
transliteration: kacciddviṣāmaviditaḥ pratiyattaśca sarvadā ,nityayukto ripūnsarvānvīkṣase ripusūdana.
english: O slayer of enemies, I hope that you are always vigilant and prepared, discreetly observing all your enemies without their knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 28. O slayer of enemies, I hope that you are always vigilant and prepared, discreetly observing all your enemies without their knowledge.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्विनयसंपन्नः कुलपुत्रो बहुश्रुतः ।अनसूयुरनुप्रष्टा सत्कृतस्ते पुरोहितः ॥२९॥
transliteration: kaccidvinayasaṁpannaḥ kulaputro bahuśrutaḥ ,anasūyuranupraṣṭā satkṛtaste purohitaḥ.
english: I hope that your chief priest is a man of noble birth, endowed with discipline, highly learned, free from malice, and one who asks appropriate questions, and that he is duly honored by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 29. I hope that your chief priest is a man of noble birth, endowed with discipline, highly learned, free from malice, and one who asks appropriate questions, and that he is duly honored by you.

sanskrit: कच्चिदग्निषु ते युक्तो विधिज्ञो मतिमानृजुः ।हुतं च होष्यमाणं च काले वेदयते सदा ॥३०॥
transliteration: kaccidagniṣu te yukto vidhijño matimānṛjuḥ ,hutaṁ ca hoṣyamāṇaṁ ca kāle vedayate sadā.
english: I hope that your priest, who is diligent with the sacred fires, knowledgeable in rituals, intelligent, and honest, always informs you at the proper time about the oblations offered and those to be offered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 30. I hope that your priest, who is diligent with the sacred fires, knowledgeable in rituals, intelligent, and honest, always informs you at the proper time about the oblations offered and those to be offered.

sanskrit: कच्चिदङ्गेषु निष्णातो ज्योतिषां प्रतिपादकः ।उत्पातेषु च सर्वेषु दैवज्ञः कुशलस्तव ॥३१॥
transliteration: kaccidaṅgeṣu niṣṇāto jyotiṣāṁ pratipādakaḥ ,utpāteṣu ca sarveṣu daivajñaḥ kuśalastava.
english: Is your astrologer (daivajña) skilled in the auxiliary sciences (aṅga), an expounder of astrology (jyotiṣa), and proficient in all portents?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 31. Is your astrologer (daivajña) skilled in the auxiliary sciences (aṅga), an expounder of astrology (jyotiṣa), and proficient in all portents?

sanskrit: कच्चिन्मुख्या महत्स्वेव मध्यमेषु च मध्यमाः ।जघन्याश्च जघन्येषु भृत्याः कर्मसु योजिताः ॥३२॥
transliteration: kaccinmukhyā mahatsveva madhyameṣu ca madhyamāḥ ,jaghanyāśca jaghanyeṣu bhṛtyāḥ karmasu yojitāḥ.
english: Are your principal servants employed only in important tasks, the average ones in medium tasks, and the lowest in inferior tasks?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 32. Are your principal servants employed only in important tasks, the average ones in medium tasks, and the lowest in inferior tasks?

sanskrit: अमात्यानुपधातीतान्पितृपैतामहाञ्शुचीन् ।श्रेष्ठाञ्श्रेष्ठेषु कच्चित्त्वं नियोजयसि कर्मसु ॥३३॥
transliteration: amātyānupadhātītānpitṛpaitāmahāñśucīn ,śreṣṭhāñśreṣṭheṣu kaccittvaṁ niyojayasi karmasu.
english: Do you appoint ministers who have passed the test of integrity (upadhā), are hereditary, honest, and excellent, to the most important tasks?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 33. Do you appoint ministers who have passed the test of integrity (upadhā), are hereditary, honest, and excellent, to the most important tasks?

sanskrit: कच्चिन्नोग्रेण दण्डेन भृशमुद्वेजितप्रजाः ।राष्ट्रं तवानुशासन्ति मन्त्रिणो भरतर्षभ ॥३४॥
transliteration: kaccinnogreṇa daṇḍena bhṛśamudvejitaprajāḥ ,rāṣṭraṁ tavānuśāsanti mantriṇo bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among Bharatas, I hope your ministers are not governing your state by severely distressing the subjects through harsh punishment.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 34. O best among Bharatas, I hope your ministers are not governing your state by severely distressing the subjects through harsh punishment.

sanskrit: कच्चित्त्वां नावजानन्ति याजकाः पतितं यथा ।उग्रप्रतिग्रहीतारं कामयानमिव स्त्रियः ॥३५॥
transliteration: kaccittvāṁ nāvajānanti yājakāḥ patitaṁ yathā ,ugrapratigrahītāraṁ kāmayānamiva striyaḥ.
english: I hope that priests do not disrespect you as if you were an outcast. And I hope women do not disregard you as they would a man excessively greedy in receiving gifts, or one who is driven by lust.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 35. I hope that priests do not disrespect you as if you were an outcast. And I hope women do not disregard you as they would a man excessively greedy in receiving gifts, or one who is driven by lust.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्धृष्टश्च शूरश्च मतिमान्धृतिमाञ्शुचिः ।कुलीनश्चानुरक्तश्च दक्षः सेनापतिस्तव ॥३६॥
transliteration: kacciddhṛṣṭaśca śūraśca matimāndhṛtimāñśuciḥ ,kulīnaścānuraktaśca dakṣaḥ senāpatistava.
english: I hope your commander-in-chief is bold, brave, intelligent, resolute, pure, of noble birth, loyal, and capable.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 36. I hope your commander-in-chief is bold, brave, intelligent, resolute, pure, of noble birth, loyal, and capable.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्बलस्य ते मुख्याः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ।दृष्टापदाना विक्रान्तास्त्वया सत्कृत्य मानिताः ॥३७॥
transliteration: kaccidbalasya te mukhyāḥ sarve yuddhaviśāradāḥ ,dṛṣṭāpadānā vikrāntāstvayā satkṛtya mānitāḥ.
english: I hope that all the principal chiefs of your army, who are skilled in warfare, whose heroic deeds have been witnessed, and who are valiant, are honored by you with due respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 37. I hope that all the principal chiefs of your army, who are skilled in warfare, whose heroic deeds have been witnessed, and who are valiant, are honored by you with due respect.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्बलस्य भक्तं च वेतनं च यथोचितम् ।संप्राप्तकालं दातव्यं ददासि न विकर्षसि ॥३८॥
transliteration: kaccidbalasya bhaktaṁ ca vetanaṁ ca yathocitam ,saṁprāptakālaṁ dātavyaṁ dadāsi na vikarṣasi.
english: I hope you give to the army its food and wages, which are proper and due at the right time, and that you do not delay them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 38. I hope you give to the army its food and wages, which are proper and due at the right time, and that you do not delay them.

sanskrit: कालातिक्रमणाद्ध्येते भक्तवेतनयोर्भृताः ।भर्तुः कुप्यन्ति दौर्गत्यात्सोऽनर्थः सुमहान्स्मृतः ॥३९॥
transliteration: kālātikramaṇāddhyete bhaktavetanayorbhṛtāḥ ,bhartuḥ kupyanti daurgatyātso'narthaḥ sumahānsmṛtaḥ.
english: Indeed, these employees, due to the delay in their food and wages, become angry with their master because of their financial distress. That is considered a very great calamity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 39. Indeed, these employees, due to the delay in their food and wages, become angry with their master because of their financial distress. That is considered a very great calamity.

sanskrit: कच्चित्सर्वेऽनुरक्तास्त्वां कुलपुत्राः प्रधानतः ।कच्चित्प्राणांस्तवार्थेषु संत्यजन्ति सदा युधि ॥४०॥
transliteration: kaccitsarve'nuraktāstvāṁ kulaputrāḥ pradhānataḥ ,kaccitprāṇāṁstavārtheṣu saṁtyajanti sadā yudhi.
english: Are all the principal noblemen loyal to you? And do they always sacrifice their lives in battle for your cause?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 40. Are all the principal noblemen loyal to you? And do they always sacrifice their lives in battle for your cause?

sanskrit: कच्चिन्नैको बहूनर्थान्सर्वशः सांपरायिकान् ।अनुशास्सि यथाकामं कामात्मा शासनातिगः ॥४१॥
transliteration: kaccinnaiko bahūnarthānsarvaśaḥ sāṁparāyikān ,anuśāssi yathākāmaṁ kāmātmā śāsanātigaḥ.
english: I hope you do not, driven by personal desire (kāma) and transgressing the established rules of governance, unilaterally manage many strategic affairs entirely as you please.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 41. I hope you do not, driven by personal desire (kāma) and transgressing the established rules of governance, unilaterally manage many strategic affairs entirely as you please.

sanskrit: कच्चित्पुरुषकारेण पुरुषः कर्म शोभयन् ।लभते मानमधिकं भूयो वा भक्तवेतनम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: kaccitpuruṣakāreṇa puruṣaḥ karma śobhayan ,labhate mānamadhikaṁ bhūyo vā bhaktavetanam.
english: Is it true that a person, by his own individual exertion (puruṣakāra), while excelling in his work, obtains greater honor or additional wages for his devotion?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 42. Is it true that a person, by his own individual exertion (puruṣakāra), while excelling in his work, obtains greater honor or additional wages for his devotion?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्विद्याविनीतांश्च नराञ्ज्ञानविशारदान् ।यथार्हं गुणतश्चैव दानेनाभ्यवपद्यसे ॥४३॥
transliteration: kaccidvidyāvinītāṁśca narāñjñānaviśāradān ,yathārhaṁ guṇataścaiva dānenābhyavapadyase.
english: I trust that you honor, with fitting gifts (dāna), those men who are cultured through learning and skilled in wisdom, according to their merits and qualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 43. I trust that you honor, with fitting gifts (dāna), those men who are cultured through learning and skilled in wisdom, according to their merits and qualities.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्दारान्मनुष्याणां तवार्थे मृत्युमेयुषाम् ।व्यसनं चाभ्युपेतानां बिभर्षि भरतर्षभ ॥४४॥
transliteration: kacciddārānmanuṣyāṇāṁ tavārthe mṛtyumeyuṣām ,vyasanaṁ cābhyupetānāṁ bibharṣi bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, I trust that you support the wives of those men who have died for your sake and of those who have fallen into misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 44. O best of the Bhāratas, I trust that you support the wives of those men who have died for your sake and of those who have fallen into misfortune.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्भयादुपनतं क्लीबं वा रिपुमागतम् ।युद्धे वा विजितं पार्थ पुत्रवत्परिरक्षसि ॥४५॥
transliteration: kaccidbhayādupanataṁ klībaṁ vā ripumāgatam ,yuddhe vā vijitaṁ pārtha putravatparirakṣasi.
english: O Pārtha, I trust that you protect like a son an enemy who has approached you out of fear and is helpless, or one who has been defeated in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 45. O Pārtha, I trust that you protect like a son an enemy who has approached you out of fear and is helpless, or one who has been defeated in battle.

sanskrit: कच्चित्त्वमेव सर्वस्याः पृथिव्याः पृथिवीपते ।समश्च नाभिशङ्क्यश्च यथा माता यथा पिता ॥४६॥
transliteration: kaccittvameva sarvasyāḥ pṛthivyāḥ pṛthivīpate ,samaśca nābhiśaṅkyaśca yathā mātā yathā pitā.
english: O protector of the earth, I hope that you are indeed impartial to all the earth and above reproach, just like a mother and a father.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 46. O protector of the earth, I hope that you are indeed impartial to all the earth and above reproach, just like a mother and a father.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्व्यसनिनं शत्रुं निशम्य भरतर्षभ ।अभियासि जवेनैव समीक्ष्य त्रिविधं बलम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: kaccidvyasaninaṁ śatruṁ niśamya bharatarṣabha ,abhiyāsi javenaiva samīkṣya trividhaṁ balam.
english: O best of the Bharatas, I hope that upon learning of an enemy in distress, you attack swiftly, having thoroughly assessed your threefold army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 47. O best of the Bharatas, I hope that upon learning of an enemy in distress, you attack swiftly, having thoroughly assessed your threefold army.

sanskrit: पार्ष्णिमूलं च विज्ञाय व्यवसायं पराजयम् ।बलस्य च महाराज दत्त्वा वेतनमग्रतः ॥४८॥
transliteration: pārṣṇimūlaṁ ca vijñāya vyavasāyaṁ parājayam ,balasya ca mahārāja dattvā vetanamagrataḥ.
english: And, O great king, having ascertained the enemy's rear base (pārṣṇimūla), their objective (vyavasāya), and their vulnerability to defeat (parājaya); and having paid the army's wages in advance...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 48. And, O great king, having ascertained the enemy's rear base (pārṣṇimūla), their objective (vyavasāya), and their vulnerability to defeat (parājaya); and having paid the army's wages in advance...

sanskrit: कच्चिच्च बलमुख्येभ्यः परराष्ट्रे परंतप ।उपच्छन्नानि रत्नानि प्रयच्छसि यथार्हतः ॥४९॥
transliteration: kaccicca balamukhyebhyaḥ pararāṣṭre paraṁtapa ,upacchannāni ratnāni prayacchasi yathārhataḥ.
english: O tormentor of enemies, do you ensure that the concealed treasures obtained from foreign lands are distributed among the army chiefs according to their deservingness?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 49. O tormentor of enemies, do you ensure that the concealed treasures obtained from foreign lands are distributed among the army chiefs according to their deservingness?

sanskrit: कच्चिदात्मानमेवाग्रे विजित्य विजितेन्द्रियः ।पराञ्जिगीषसे पार्थ प्रमत्तानजितेन्द्रियान् ॥५०॥
transliteration: kaccidātmānamevāgre vijitya vijitendriyaḥ ,parāñjigīṣase pārtha pramattānajitendriyān.
english: O Pārtha, after first conquering your own self (ātman) and having mastered your senses, do you then desire to conquer those heedless enemies who have not mastered their senses?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 50. O Pārtha, after first conquering your own self (ātman) and having mastered your senses, do you then desire to conquer those heedless enemies who have not mastered their senses?

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते यास्यतः शत्रून्पूर्वं यान्ति स्वनुष्ठिताः ।साम दानं च भेदश्च दण्डश्च विधिवद्गुणाः ॥५१॥
transliteration: kaccitte yāsyataḥ śatrūnpūrvaṁ yānti svanuṣṭhitāḥ ,sāma dānaṁ ca bhedaśca daṇḍaśca vidhivadguṇāḥ.
english: I hope that, as you prepare to confront your enemies, your well-implemented policies - conciliation, strategic gifts, creating division, and the use of force - are first applied according to established rules.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 51. I hope that, as you prepare to confront your enemies, your well-implemented policies - conciliation, strategic gifts, creating division, and the use of force - are first applied according to established rules.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्मूलं दृढं कृत्वा यात्रां यासि विशां पते ।तांश्च विक्रमसे जेतुं जित्वा च परिरक्षसि ॥५२॥
transliteration: kaccinmūlaṁ dṛḍhaṁ kṛtvā yātrāṁ yāsi viśāṁ pate ,tāṁśca vikramase jetuṁ jitvā ca parirakṣasi.
english: O lord of the people, I trust that after firmly securing your base, you undertake your expeditions. Do you then exert yourself to conquer them (your enemies), and having conquered them, do you also protect them?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 52. O lord of the people, I trust that after firmly securing your base, you undertake your expeditions. Do you then exert yourself to conquer them (your enemies), and having conquered them, do you also protect them?

sanskrit: कच्चिदष्टाङ्गसंयुक्ता चतुर्विधबला चमूः ।बलमुख्यैः सुनीता ते द्विषतां प्रतिबाधनी ॥५३॥
transliteration: kaccidaṣṭāṅgasaṁyuktā caturvidhabalā camūḥ ,balamukhyaiḥ sunītā te dviṣatāṁ pratibādhanī.
english: I hope that your army is equipped with the eight constituents (of statecraft) and comprises four types of forces. Is it well-led by your chief officers and capable of repelling your enemies?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 53. I hope that your army is equipped with the eight constituents (of statecraft) and comprises four types of forces. Is it well-led by your chief officers and capable of repelling your enemies?

sanskrit: कच्चिल्लवं च मुष्टिं च परराष्ट्रे परंतप ।अविहाय महाराज विहंसि समरे रिपून् ॥५४॥
transliteration: kaccillavaṁ ca muṣṭiṁ ca pararāṣṭre paraṁtapa ,avihāya mahārāja vihaṁsi samare ripūn.
english: O tormentor of enemies, O great king, I trust that in enemy territory, you do not abandon even a small part or a handful (of land or resources), and that you utterly defeat your foes in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 54. O tormentor of enemies, O great king, I trust that in enemy territory, you do not abandon even a small part or a handful (of land or resources), and that you utterly defeat your foes in battle.

sanskrit: कच्चित्स्वपरराष्ट्रेषु बहवोऽधिकृतास्तव ।अर्थान्समनुतिष्ठन्ति रक्षन्ति च परस्परम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: kaccitsvapararāṣṭreṣu bahavo'dhikṛtāstava ,arthānsamanutiṣṭhanti rakṣanti ca parasparam.
english: I hope that many of your appointed officials, both in your own kingdom and in others, are properly managing affairs and mutually protecting each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 55. I hope that many of your appointed officials, both in your own kingdom and in others, are properly managing affairs and mutually protecting each other.

sanskrit: कच्चिदभ्यवहार्याणि गात्रसंस्पर्शकानि च ।घ्रेयाणि च महाराज रक्षन्त्यनुमतास्तव ॥५६॥
transliteration: kaccidabhyavahāryāṇi gātrasaṁsparśakāni ca ,ghreyāṇi ca mahārāja rakṣantyanumatāstava.
english: O great king, I hope that your authorized individuals are protecting your provisions of food and drink, pleasant bodily comforts, and fragrances.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 56. O great king, I hope that your authorized individuals are protecting your provisions of food and drink, pleasant bodily comforts, and fragrances.

sanskrit: कच्चित्कोशं च कोष्ठं च वाहनं द्वारमायुधम् ।आयश्च कृतकल्याणैस्तव भक्तैरनुष्ठितः ॥५७॥
transliteration: kaccitkośaṁ ca koṣṭhaṁ ca vāhanaṁ dvāramāyudham ,āyaśca kṛtakalyāṇaistava bhaktairanuṣṭhitaḥ.
english: I hope that your treasury, granary, vehicles, gates, and armory are properly maintained, and that your income is managed by your devoted and benevolent followers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 57. I hope that your treasury, granary, vehicles, gates, and armory are properly maintained, and that your income is managed by your devoted and benevolent followers.

sanskrit: कच्चिदाभ्यन्तरेभ्यश्च बाह्येभ्यश्च विशां पते ।रक्षस्यात्मानमेवाग्रे तांश्च स्वेभ्यो मिथश्च तान् ॥५८॥
transliteration: kaccidābhyantarebhyaśca bāhyebhyaśca viśāṁ pate ,rakṣasyātmānamevāgre tāṁśca svebhyo mithaśca tān.
english: O lord of the people, do you first protect your own self (ātman) from both internal and external dangers? And do you also protect your subjects from external threats, from mutual strife among themselves, and from dangers posed by your own people (officials/kinsmen)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 58. O lord of the people, do you first protect your own self (ātman) from both internal and external dangers? And do you also protect your subjects from external threats, from mutual strife among themselves, and from dangers posed by your own people (officials/kinsmen)?

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न पाने द्यूते वा क्रीडासु प्रमदासु च ।प्रतिजानन्ति पूर्वाह्णे व्ययं व्यसनजं तव ॥५९॥
transliteration: kaccinna pāne dyūte vā krīḍāsu pramadāsu ca ,pratijānanti pūrvāhṇe vyayaṁ vyasanajaṁ tava.
english: I hope that (your officials) do not acknowledge, already in the forenoon, your expenditures arising from vices like drinking, gambling, amusements, and women.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 59. I hope that (your officials) do not acknowledge, already in the forenoon, your expenditures arising from vices like drinking, gambling, amusements, and women.

sanskrit: कच्चिदायस्य चार्धेन चतुर्भागेन वा पुनः ।पादभागैस्त्रिभिर्वापि व्ययः संशोध्यते तव ॥६०॥
transliteration: kaccidāyasya cārdhena caturbhāgena vā punaḥ ,pādabhāgaistribhirvāpi vyayaḥ saṁśodhyate tava.
english: Hopefully, is your expenditure settled (within) half, or a fourth, or even three-quarters of your income (āya)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 60. Hopefully, is your expenditure settled (within) half, or a fourth, or even three-quarters of your income (āya)?

sanskrit: कच्चिज्ज्ञातीन्गुरून्वृद्धान्वणिजः शिल्पिनः श्रितान् ।अभीक्ष्णमनुगृह्णासि धनधान्येन दुर्गतान् ॥६१॥
transliteration: kaccijjñātīngurūnvṛddhānvaṇijaḥ śilpinaḥ śritān ,abhīkṣṇamanugṛhṇāsi dhanadhānyena durgatān.
english: I hope that you constantly assist your distressed relatives, spiritual teachers (guru), elders, merchants, artisans, and dependents with wealth and grain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 61. I hope that you constantly assist your distressed relatives, spiritual teachers (guru), elders, merchants, artisans, and dependents with wealth and grain.

sanskrit: कच्चिदायव्यये युक्ताः सर्वे गणकलेखकाः ।अनुतिष्ठन्ति पूर्वाह्णे नित्यमायव्ययं तव ॥६२॥
transliteration: kaccidāyavyaye yuktāḥ sarve gaṇakalekhakāḥ ,anutiṣṭhanti pūrvāhṇe nityamāyavyayaṁ tava.
english: I hope that all your appointed accountants and scribes diligently manage your income and expenditure accounts every forenoon.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 62. I hope that all your appointed accountants and scribes diligently manage your income and expenditure accounts every forenoon.

sanskrit: कच्चिदर्थेषु संप्रौढान्हितकामाननुप्रियान् ।नापकर्षसि कर्मभ्यः पूर्वमप्राप्य किल्बिषम् ॥६३॥
transliteration: kaccidartheṣu saṁprauḍhānhitakāmānanupriyān ,nāpakarṣasi karmabhyaḥ pūrvamaprāpya kilbiṣam.
english: I hope that you do not dismiss from their posts those who are highly competent in state affairs, who wish you well, and are devoted, without first ascertaining their offense.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 63. I hope that you do not dismiss from their posts those who are highly competent in state affairs, who wish you well, and are devoted, without first ascertaining their offense.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्विदित्वा पुरुषानुत्तमाधममध्यमान् ।त्वं कर्मस्वनुरूपेषु नियोजयसि भारत ॥६४॥
transliteration: kaccidviditvā puruṣānuttamādhamamadhyamān ,tvaṁ karmasvanurūpeṣu niyojayasi bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, having ascertained the superior, middling, and inferior individuals (puruṣa), I trust you appoint them to suitable tasks (karma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 64. O Bhārata, having ascertained the superior, middling, and inferior individuals (puruṣa), I trust you appoint them to suitable tasks (karma)?

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न लुब्धाश्चौरा वा वैरिणो वा विशां पते ।अप्राप्तव्यवहारा वा तव कर्मस्वनुष्ठिताः ॥६५॥
transliteration: kaccinna lubdhāścaurā vā vairiṇo vā viśāṁ pate ,aprāptavyavahārā vā tava karmasvanuṣṭhitāḥ.
english: O Lord of the people, I hope that greedy individuals, thieves, enemies, or those who lack experience in proper conduct are not appointed to your tasks (karma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 65. O Lord of the people, I hope that greedy individuals, thieves, enemies, or those who lack experience in proper conduct are not appointed to your tasks (karma)?

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न लुब्धैश्चौरैर्वा कुमारैः स्त्रीबलेन वा ।त्वया वा पीड्यते राष्ट्रं कच्चित्पुष्टाः कृषीवलाः ॥६६॥
transliteration: kaccinna lubdhaiścaurairvā kumāraiḥ strībalena vā ,tvayā vā pīḍyate rāṣṭraṁ kaccitpuṣṭāḥ kṛṣīvalāḥ.
english: I trust that the kingdom is not oppressed by greedy individuals, thieves, princes, or women (strībala), or even by you? And I trust that the farmers (kṛṣīvala) are well-nourished and prosperous?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 66. I trust that the kingdom is not oppressed by greedy individuals, thieves, princes, or women (strībala), or even by you? And I trust that the farmers (kṛṣīvala) are well-nourished and prosperous?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्राष्ट्रे तडागानि पूर्णानि च महान्ति च ।भागशो विनिविष्टानि न कृषिर्देवमातृका ॥६७॥
transliteration: kaccidrāṣṭre taḍāgāni pūrṇāni ca mahānti ca ,bhāgaśo viniviṣṭāni na kṛṣirdevamātṛkā.
english: Is it true that in the kingdom, there are large and full tanks, properly distributed in various parts, so that agriculture is not dependent solely on rain (devamātṛkā)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 67. Is it true that in the kingdom, there are large and full tanks, properly distributed in various parts, so that agriculture is not dependent solely on rain (devamātṛkā)?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्बीजं च भक्तं च कर्षकायावसीदते ।प्रतिकं च शतं वृद्ध्या ददास्यृणमनुग्रहम् ॥६८॥
transliteration: kaccidbījaṁ ca bhaktaṁ ca karṣakāyāvasīdate ,pratikaṁ ca śataṁ vṛddhyā dadāsyṛṇamanugraham.
english: Is it true that seeds and provisions are not lacking for the farmers? Do you grant them loans (ṛṇa) at one percent interest (vṛddhi) as a favor (anugraha)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 68. Is it true that seeds and provisions are not lacking for the farmers? Do you grant them loans (ṛṇa) at one percent interest (vṛddhi) as a favor (anugraha)?

sanskrit: कच्चित्स्वनुष्ठिता तात वार्त्ता ते साधुभिर्जनैः ।वार्त्तायां संश्रितस्तात लोकोऽयं सुखमेधते ॥६९॥
transliteration: kaccitsvanuṣṭhitā tāta vārttā te sādhubhirjanaiḥ ,vārttāyāṁ saṁśritastāta loko'yaṁ sukhamedhate.
english: My dear son, is your (people's) economic activity (vārttā) well-managed by honest individuals? For, dear son, by depending on that economic activity (vārttā), these people prosper happily.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 69. My dear son, is your (people's) economic activity (vārttā) well-managed by honest individuals? For, dear son, by depending on that economic activity (vārttā), these people prosper happily.

sanskrit: कच्चिच्छुचिकृतः प्राज्ञाः पञ्च पञ्च स्वनुष्ठिताः ।क्षेमं कुर्वन्ति संहत्य राजञ्जनपदे तव ॥७०॥
transliteration: kaccicchucikṛtaḥ prājñāḥ pañca pañca svanuṣṭhitāḥ ,kṣemaṁ kurvanti saṁhatya rājañjanapade tava.
english: O King, I hope that the wise and pure (officials), arranged in two groups of five, who are well-established, work together to ensure the security and welfare of your country.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 70. O King, I hope that the wise and pure (officials), arranged in two groups of five, who are well-established, work together to ensure the security and welfare of your country.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्नगरगुप्त्यर्थं ग्रामा नगरवत्कृताः ।ग्रामवच्च कृता रक्षा ते च सर्वे तदर्पणाः ॥७१॥
transliteration: kaccinnagaraguptyarthaṁ grāmā nagaravatkṛtāḥ ,grāmavacca kṛtā rakṣā te ca sarve tadarpaṇāḥ.
english: I hope that for the protection of the cities, villages have been fortified like cities, and that protection (for the villages) is also established in a village-like manner, and that all these (measures or forces) are dedicated to that purpose.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 71. I hope that for the protection of the cities, villages have been fortified like cities, and that protection (for the villages) is also established in a village-like manner, and that all these (measures or forces) are dedicated to that purpose.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्बलेनानुगताः समानि विषमाणि च ।पुराणचौराः साध्यक्षाश्चरन्ति विषये तव ॥७२॥
transliteration: kaccidbalenānugatāḥ samāni viṣamāṇi ca ,purāṇacaurāḥ sādhyakṣāścaranti viṣaye tava.
english: I hope that experienced thieves, accompanied by their own forces and with their leaders, do not roam through your territory, both in plains and difficult terrains.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 72. I hope that experienced thieves, accompanied by their own forces and with their leaders, do not roam through your territory, both in plains and difficult terrains.

sanskrit: कच्चित्स्त्रियः सान्त्वयसि कच्चित्ताश्च सुरक्षिताः ।कच्चिन्न श्रद्दधास्यासां कच्चिद्गुह्यं न भाषसे ॥७३॥
transliteration: kaccitstriyaḥ sāntvayasi kaccittāśca surakṣitāḥ ,kaccinna śraddadhāsyāsāṁ kaccidguhyaṁ na bhāṣase.
english: Do you console the women? Are they well-protected? Do you not place trust (śraddhā) in them [with sensitive information]? And do you not disclose secrets to them?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 73. Do you console the women? Are they well-protected? Do you not place trust (śraddhā) in them [with sensitive information]? And do you not disclose secrets to them?

sanskrit: कच्चिच्चारान्निशि श्रुत्वा तत्कार्यमनुचिन्त्य च ।प्रियाण्यनुभवञ्शेषे विदित्वाभ्यन्तरं जनम् ॥७४॥
transliteration: kacciccārānniśi śrutvā tatkāryamanucintya ca ,priyāṇyanubhavañśeṣe viditvābhyantaraṁ janam.
english: After listening to your spies at night and contemplating those matters, do you then rest peacefully and enjoy pleasantries, having truly understood the people within your inner circle?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 74. After listening to your spies at night and contemplating those matters, do you then rest peacefully and enjoy pleasantries, having truly understood the people within your inner circle?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्द्वौ प्रथमौ यामौ रात्र्यां सुप्त्वा विशां पते ।संचिन्तयसि धर्मार्थौ याम उत्थाय पश्चिमे ॥७५॥
transliteration: kacciddvau prathamau yāmau rātryāṁ suptvā viśāṁ pate ,saṁcintayasi dharmārthau yāma utthāya paścime.
english: O lord of the people, having slept during the first two watches of the night, do you then, rising in the last watch, reflect upon righteousness (dharma) and material prosperity (artha)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 75. O lord of the people, having slept during the first two watches of the night, do you then, rising in the last watch, reflect upon righteousness (dharma) and material prosperity (artha)?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्दर्शयसे नित्यं मनुष्यान्समलंकृतान् ।उत्थाय काले कालज्ञः सह पाण्डव मन्त्रिभिः ॥७६॥
transliteration: kacciddarśayase nityaṁ manuṣyānsamalaṁkṛtān ,utthāya kāle kālajñaḥ saha pāṇḍava mantribhiḥ.
english: O Pāṇḍava, you who know the opportune time, do you always present yourself, having risen at the proper hour along with your ministers, before people who are well-adorned?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 76. O Pāṇḍava, you who know the opportune time, do you always present yourself, having risen at the proper hour along with your ministers, before people who are well-adorned?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्रक्ताम्बरधराः खड्गहस्ताः स्वलंकृताः ।अभितस्त्वामुपासन्ते रक्षणार्थमरिंदम ॥७७॥
transliteration: kaccidraktāmbaradharāḥ khaḍgahastāḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,abhitastvāmupāsante rakṣaṇārthamariṁdama.
english: O subduer of enemies, do well-adorned men, clad in red garments and bearing swords, attend upon you from all sides for your protection?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 77. O subduer of enemies, do well-adorned men, clad in red garments and bearing swords, attend upon you from all sides for your protection?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्दण्ड्येषु यमवत्पूज्येषु च विशां पते ।परीक्ष्य वर्तसे सम्यगप्रियेषु प्रियेषु च ॥७८॥
transliteration: kacciddaṇḍyeṣu yamavatpūjyeṣu ca viśāṁ pate ,parīkṣya vartase samyagapriyeṣu priyeṣu ca.
english: O lord of the people, do you, after careful examination, act justly towards those deserving punishment (like Yama), and also towards those worthy of honor, and impartially towards friends and enemies alike?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 78. O lord of the people, do you, after careful examination, act justly towards those deserving punishment (like Yama), and also towards those worthy of honor, and impartially towards friends and enemies alike?

sanskrit: कच्चिच्छारीरमाबाधमौषधैर्नियमेन वा ।मानसं वृद्धसेवाभिः सदा पार्थापकर्षसि ॥७९॥
transliteration: kaccicchārīramābādhamauṣadhairniyamena vā ,mānasaṁ vṛddhasevābhiḥ sadā pārthāpakarṣasi.
english: O Pārtha, I hope you always dispel bodily ailments with medicines or disciplined practices, and mental distress by serving elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 79. O Pārtha, I hope you always dispel bodily ailments with medicines or disciplined practices, and mental distress by serving elders.

sanskrit: कच्चिद्वैद्याश्चिकित्सायामष्टाङ्गायां विशारदाः ।सुहृदश्चानुरक्ताश्च शरीरे ते हिताः सदा ॥८०॥
transliteration: kaccidvaidyāścikitsāyāmaṣṭāṅgāyāṁ viśāradāḥ ,suhṛdaścānuraktāśca śarīre te hitāḥ sadā.
english: I hope that physicians skilled in the eight branches of medical treatment, as well as friends who are good-hearted and devoted, are always beneficial for your physical well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 80. I hope that physicians skilled in the eight branches of medical treatment, as well as friends who are good-hearted and devoted, are always beneficial for your physical well-being.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न मानान्मोहाद्वा कामाद्वापि विशां पते ।अर्थिप्रत्यर्थिनः प्राप्तानपास्यसि कथंचन ॥८१॥
transliteration: kaccinna mānānmohādvā kāmādvāpi viśāṁ pate ,arthipratyarthinaḥ prāptānapāsyasi kathaṁcana.
english: O lord of the people, I hope you never, by any means, turn away petitioners and respondents who have approached you, whether due to pride, delusion, or even desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 81. O lord of the people, I hope you never, by any means, turn away petitioners and respondents who have approached you, whether due to pride, delusion, or even desire.

sanskrit: कच्चिन्न लोभान्मोहाद्वा विश्रम्भात्प्रणयेन वा ।आश्रितानां मनुष्याणां वृत्तिं त्वं संरुणत्सि च ॥८२॥
transliteration: kaccinna lobhānmohādvā viśrambhātpraṇayena vā ,āśritānāṁ manuṣyāṇāṁ vṛttiṁ tvaṁ saṁruṇatsi ca.
english: I hope that you do not, out of greed, or delusion, or excessive trust, or even affection, obstruct the livelihood of people who have sought your refuge.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 82. I hope that you do not, out of greed, or delusion, or excessive trust, or even affection, obstruct the livelihood of people who have sought your refuge.

sanskrit: कच्चित्पौरा न सहिता ये च ते राष्ट्रवासिनः ।त्वया सह विरुध्यन्ते परैः क्रीताः कथंचन ॥८३॥
transliteration: kaccitpaurā na sahitā ye ca te rāṣṭravāsinaḥ ,tvayā saha virudhyante paraiḥ krītāḥ kathaṁcana.
english: I hope that your city dwellers and those who reside in your kingdom are not disunited, or perhaps, having been corrupted by enemies, somehow oppose you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 83. I hope that your city dwellers and those who reside in your kingdom are not disunited, or perhaps, having been corrupted by enemies, somehow oppose you.

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते दुर्बलः शत्रुर्बलेनोपनिपीडितः ।मन्त्रेण बलवान्कश्चिदुभाभ्यां वा युधिष्ठिर ॥८४॥
transliteration: kaccitte durbalaḥ śatrurbalenopanipīḍitaḥ ,mantreṇa balavānkaścidubhābhyāṁ vā yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, I hope that your weak enemy is suppressed by military force, and that any powerful enemy is subdued by wise counsel, or by both.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 84. O Yudhiṣṭhira, I hope that your weak enemy is suppressed by military force, and that any powerful enemy is subdued by wise counsel, or by both.

sanskrit: कच्चित्सर्वेऽनुरक्तास्त्वां भूमिपालाः प्रधानतः ।कच्चित्प्राणांस्त्वदर्थेषु संत्यजन्ति त्वया हृताः ॥८५॥
transliteration: kaccitsarve'nuraktāstvāṁ bhūmipālāḥ pradhānataḥ ,kaccitprāṇāṁstvadartheṣu saṁtyajanti tvayā hṛtāḥ.
english: Are all the kings, especially the principal ones, devoted to you? Do those whose hearts are captivated by you willingly sacrifice their lives for your cause?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 85. Are all the kings, especially the principal ones, devoted to you? Do those whose hearts are captivated by you willingly sacrifice their lives for your cause?

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते सर्वविद्यासु गुणतोऽर्चा प्रवर्तते ।ब्राह्मणानां च साधूनां तव निःश्रेयसे शुभा ॥८६॥
transliteration: kaccitte sarvavidyāsu guṇato'rcā pravartate ,brāhmaṇānāṁ ca sādhūnāṁ tava niḥśreyase śubhā.
english: Is it true that, in all branches of knowledge, honor is shown to you according to your virtues? And is auspicious honor given to Brahmins and virtuous people, leading to your ultimate well-being (niḥśreyasa)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 86. Is it true that, in all branches of knowledge, honor is shown to you according to your virtues? And is auspicious honor given to Brahmins and virtuous people, leading to your ultimate well-being (niḥśreyasa)?

sanskrit: कच्चिद्धर्मे त्रयीमूले पूर्वैराचरिते जनैः ।वर्तमानस्तथा कर्तुं तस्मिन्कर्मणि वर्तसे ॥८७॥
transliteration: kacciddharme trayīmūle pūrvairācarite janaiḥ ,vartamānastathā kartuṁ tasminkarmaṇi vartase.
english: Are you, while performing your duties (karma), upholding the (natural law) dharma that is rooted in the three Vedas and was practiced by your ancestors?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 87. Are you, while performing your duties (karma), upholding the (natural law) dharma that is rooted in the three Vedas and was practiced by your ancestors?

sanskrit: कच्चित्तव गृहेऽन्नानि स्वादून्यश्नन्ति वै द्विजाः ।गुणवन्ति गुणोपेतास्तवाध्यक्षं सदक्षिणम् ॥८८॥
transliteration: kaccittava gṛhe'nnāni svādūnyaśnanti vai dvijāḥ ,guṇavanti guṇopetāstavādhyakṣaṁ sadakṣiṇam.
english: I hope that in your house, the virtuous Brahmins (dvija) partake of delicious and wholesome foods, offered in your presence and accompanied by appropriate gifts.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 88. I hope that in your house, the virtuous Brahmins (dvija) partake of delicious and wholesome foods, offered in your presence and accompanied by appropriate gifts.

sanskrit: कच्चित्क्रतूनेकचित्तो वाजपेयांश्च सर्वशः ।पुण्डरीकांश्च कार्त्स्न्येन यतसे कर्तुमात्मवान् ॥८९॥
transliteration: kaccitkratūnekacitto vājapeyāṁśca sarvaśaḥ ,puṇḍarīkāṁśca kārtsnyena yatase kartumātmavān.
english: I hope that you, being self-controlled (ātman) and single-minded, strive to perform all Vājapeya and Puṇḍarīka Vedic rituals (kratu) completely.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 89. I hope that you, being self-controlled (ātman) and single-minded, strive to perform all Vājapeya and Puṇḍarīka Vedic rituals (kratu) completely.

sanskrit: कच्चिज्ज्ञातीन्गुरून्वृद्धान्दैवतांस्तापसानपि ।चैत्यांश्च वृक्षान्कल्याणान्ब्राह्मणांश्च नमस्यसि ॥९०॥
transliteration: kaccijjñātīngurūnvṛddhāndaivatāṁstāpasānapi ,caityāṁśca vṛkṣānkalyāṇānbrāhmaṇāṁśca namasyasi.
english: I hope you revere your relatives, teachers (guru), elders, deities, and ascetics, as well as sacred shrines (caitya), auspicious trees, and Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 90. I hope you revere your relatives, teachers (guru), elders, deities, and ascetics, as well as sacred shrines (caitya), auspicious trees, and Brahmins.

sanskrit: कच्चिदेषा च ते बुद्धिर्वृत्तिरेषा च तेऽनघ ।आयुष्या च यशस्या च धर्मकामार्थदर्शिनी ॥९१॥
transliteration: kaccideṣā ca te buddhirvṛttireṣā ca te'nagha ,āyuṣyā ca yaśasyā ca dharmakāmārthadarśinī.
english: O sinless one, I trust that this intellect and this way of life you possess are conducive to longevity and renown, and are discerning of righteousness (dharma), legitimate desires (kāma), and material prosperity (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 91. O sinless one, I trust that this intellect and this way of life you possess are conducive to longevity and renown, and are discerning of righteousness (dharma), legitimate desires (kāma), and material prosperity (artha).

sanskrit: एतया वर्तमानस्य बुद्ध्या राष्ट्रं न सीदति ।विजित्य च महीं राजा सोऽत्यन्तं सुखमेधते ॥९२॥
transliteration: etayā vartamānasya buddhyā rāṣṭraṁ na sīdati ,vijitya ca mahīṁ rājā so'tyantaṁ sukhamedhate.
english: When one acts with such understanding (buddhi), the kingdom does not falter. And having conquered the earth, that king (rājā) attains supreme happiness and flourishes exceedingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 92. When one acts with such understanding (buddhi), the kingdom does not falter. And having conquered the earth, that king (rājā) attains supreme happiness and flourishes exceedingly.

sanskrit: कच्चिदार्यो विशुद्धात्मा क्षारितश्चौरकर्मणि ।अदृष्टशास्त्रकुशलैर्न लोभाद्वध्यते शुचिः ॥९३॥
transliteration: kaccidāryo viśuddhātmā kṣāritaścaurakarmaṇi ,adṛṣṭaśāstrakuśalairna lobhādvadhyate śuciḥ.
english: I hope that a noble (ārya), pure (śuci), and pure-souled (ātman) person, even if accused of a crime of theft, is not unjustly executed out of greed by those unskilled in discerning the law (śāstra) and its principles.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 93. I hope that a noble (ārya), pure (śuci), and pure-souled (ātman) person, even if accused of a crime of theft, is not unjustly executed out of greed by those unskilled in discerning the law (śāstra) and its principles.

sanskrit: पृष्टो गृहीतस्तत्कारी तज्ज्ञैर्दृष्टः सकारणः ।कच्चिन्न मुच्यते स्तेनो द्रव्यलोभान्नरर्षभ ॥९४॥
transliteration: pṛṣṭo gṛhītastatkārī tajjñairdṛṣṭaḥ sakāraṇaḥ ,kaccinna mucyate steno dravyalobhānnararṣabha.
english: O best among men, when a thief, driven by greed for wealth, has been interrogated, apprehended, confirmed as the perpetrator, and proven guilty with evidence by those who know the case, surely he is not released, is he?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 94. O best among men, when a thief, driven by greed for wealth, has been interrogated, apprehended, confirmed as the perpetrator, and proven guilty with evidence by those who know the case, surely he is not released, is he?

sanskrit: व्युत्पन्ने कच्चिदाढ्यस्य दरिद्रस्य च भारत ।अर्थान्न मिथ्या पश्यन्ति तवामात्या हृता धनैः ॥९५॥
transliteration: vyutpanne kaccidāḍhyasya daridrasya ca bhārata ,arthānna mithyā paśyanti tavāmātyā hṛtā dhanaiḥ.
english: O Bhārata, I hope that your ministers, who have been swayed by wealth, do not judge the cases of the rich and the poor falsely, even when the situation is clearly understood.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 95. O Bhārata, I hope that your ministers, who have been swayed by wealth, do not judge the cases of the rich and the poor falsely, even when the situation is clearly understood.

sanskrit: नास्तिक्यमनृतं क्रोधं प्रमादं दीर्घसूत्रताम् ।अदर्शनं ज्ञानवतामालस्यं क्षिप्तचित्तताम् ॥९६॥
transliteration: nāstikyamanṛtaṁ krodhaṁ pramādaṁ dīrghasūtratām ,adarśanaṁ jñānavatāmālasyaṁ kṣiptacittatām.
english: Disbelief, untruthfulness, anger, carelessness, procrastination, ignoring those who are knowledgeable, laziness, and mental agitation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 96. Disbelief, untruthfulness, anger, carelessness, procrastination, ignoring those who are knowledgeable, laziness, and mental agitation.

sanskrit: एकचिन्तनमर्थानामनर्थज्ञैश्च चिन्तनम् ।निश्चितानामनारम्भं मन्त्रस्यापरिरक्षणम् ॥९७॥
transliteration: ekacintanamarthānāmanarthajñaiśca cintanam ,niścitānāmanārambhaṁ mantrasyāparirakṣaṇam.
english: Considering affairs with a singular focus; seeking counsel from those who do not understand what is detrimental; not commencing what has been definitively decided; and not protecting (maintaining the secrecy of) one's plans (mantra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 97. Considering affairs with a singular focus; seeking counsel from those who do not understand what is detrimental; not commencing what has been definitively decided; and not protecting (maintaining the secrecy of) one's plans (mantra).

sanskrit: मङ्गल्यस्याप्रयोगं च प्रसङ्गं विषयेषु च ।कच्चित्त्वं वर्जयस्येतान्राजदोषांश्चतुर्दश ॥९८॥
transliteration: maṅgalyasyāprayogaṁ ca prasaṅgaṁ viṣayeṣu ca ,kaccittvaṁ varjayasyetānrājadoṣāṁścaturdaśa.
english: Are you indeed avoiding the neglect of auspicious practices, and also excessive indulgence in sensual pleasures? Are you avoiding these fourteen royal faults (rāja-doṣa)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 98. Are you indeed avoiding the neglect of auspicious practices, and also excessive indulgence in sensual pleasures? Are you avoiding these fourteen royal faults (rāja-doṣa)?

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते सफला वेदाः कच्चित्ते सफलं धनम् ।कच्चित्ते सफला दाराः कच्चित्ते सफलं श्रुतम् ॥९९॥
transliteration: kaccitte saphalā vedāḥ kaccitte saphalaṁ dhanam ,kaccitte saphalā dārāḥ kaccitte saphalaṁ śrutam.
english: Are your knowledge of the Vedas yielding good results? Is your wealth proving fruitful? Are your wives successful in their roles? And is your sacred learning (śrutam) bearing fruit?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 99. Are your knowledge of the Vedas yielding good results? Is your wealth proving fruitful? Are your wives successful in their roles? And is your sacred learning (śrutam) bearing fruit?

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कथं वै सफला वेदाः कथं वै सफलं धनम् ।कथं वै सफला दाराः कथं वै सफलं श्रुतम् ॥१००॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kathaṁ vai saphalā vedāḥ kathaṁ vai saphalaṁ dhanam ,kathaṁ vai saphalā dārāḥ kathaṁ vai saphalaṁ śrutam.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira asked: What makes the Vedas fruitful? What makes wealth fruitful? What makes wives fruitful? And what makes learning fruitful?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 100. Yudhiṣṭhira asked: What makes the Vedas fruitful? What makes wealth fruitful? What makes wives fruitful? And what makes learning fruitful?

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।अग्निहोत्रफला वेदा दत्तभुक्तफलं धनम् ।रतिपुत्रफला दाराः शीलवृत्तफलं श्रुतम् ॥१०१॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,agnihotraphalā vedā dattabhuktaphalaṁ dhanam ,ratiputraphalā dārāḥ śīlavṛttaphalaṁ śrutam.
english: Nārada said: The Vedas are fruitful when performed with the fire ritual (agnihotra). Wealth is fruitful when given away and enjoyed. Wives are fruitful through conjugal pleasure and progeny. Learning is fruitful through good character and ethical conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 101. Nārada said: The Vedas are fruitful when performed with the fire ritual (agnihotra). Wealth is fruitful when given away and enjoyed. Wives are fruitful through conjugal pleasure and progeny. Learning is fruitful through good character and ethical conduct.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतदाख्याय स मुनिर्नारदः सुमहातपाः ।पप्रच्छानन्तरमिदं धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥१०२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etadākhyāya sa munirnāradaḥ sumahātapāḥ ,papracchānantaramidaṁ dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having narrated this, that greatly ascetic (tapas) sage Nārada immediately asked this of Yudhiṣṭhira, who possessed a righteous (dharma) soul (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 102. Vaiśampāyana said: Having narrated this, that greatly ascetic (tapas) sage Nārada immediately asked this of Yudhiṣṭhira, who possessed a righteous (dharma) soul (ātman).

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।कच्चिदभ्यागता दूराद्वणिजो लाभकारणात् ।यथोक्तमवहार्यन्ते शुल्कं शुल्कोपजीविभिः ॥१०३॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,kaccidabhyāgatā dūrādvaṇijo lābhakāraṇāt ,yathoktamavahāryante śulkaṁ śulkopajīvibhiḥ.
english: Nārada said, "I hope that the merchants who have come from afar for the purpose of profit are made to pay the prescribed duty by the tax collectors."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 103. Nārada said, "I hope that the merchants who have come from afar for the purpose of profit are made to pay the prescribed duty by the tax collectors."

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते पुरुषा राजन्पुरे राष्ट्रे च मानिताः ।उपानयन्ति पण्यानि उपधाभिरवञ्चिताः ॥१०४॥
transliteration: kaccitte puruṣā rājanpure rāṣṭre ca mānitāḥ ,upānayanti paṇyāni upadhābhiravañcitāḥ.
english: O King, I hope that your honored men in the city and kingdom, who are not deceived by any trickery, bring in merchandise.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 104. O King, I hope that your honored men in the city and kingdom, who are not deceived by any trickery, bring in merchandise.

sanskrit: कच्चिच्छृणोषि वृद्धानां धर्मार्थसहिता गिरः ।नित्यमर्थविदां तात तथा धर्मानुदर्शिनाम् ॥१०५॥
transliteration: kaccicchṛṇoṣi vṛddhānāṁ dharmārthasahitā giraḥ ,nityamarthavidāṁ tāta tathā dharmānudarśinām.
english: O dear one, I hope you constantly listen to the counsel of the elders who are knowledgeable in prosperity and perceive natural law (dharma), counsel that pertains to natural law (dharma) and prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 105. O dear one, I hope you constantly listen to the counsel of the elders who are knowledgeable in prosperity and perceive natural law (dharma), counsel that pertains to natural law (dharma) and prosperity.

sanskrit: कच्चित्ते कृषितन्त्रेषु गोषु पुष्पफलेषु च ।धर्मार्थं च द्विजातिभ्यो दीयते मधुसर्पिषी ॥१०६॥
transliteration: kaccitte kṛṣitantreṣu goṣu puṣpaphaleṣu ca ,dharmārthaṁ ca dvijātibhyo dīyate madhusarpiṣī.
english: I hope that you regularly provide honey and ghee to the twice-born (dvijāti) for the sake of righteousness (dharma), especially concerning matters of agriculture, cows, flowers, and fruits.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 106. I hope that you regularly provide honey and ghee to the twice-born (dvijāti) for the sake of righteousness (dharma), especially concerning matters of agriculture, cows, flowers, and fruits.

sanskrit: द्रव्योपकरणं कच्चित्सर्वदा सर्वशिल्पिनाम् ।चातुर्मास्यावरं सम्यङ्नियतं संप्रयच्छसि ॥१०७॥
transliteration: dravyopakaraṇaṁ kaccitsarvadā sarvaśilpinām ,cāturmāsyāvaraṁ samyaṅniyataṁ saṁprayacchasi.
english: I hope that you always provide all artisans with materials and tools for a period of not less than four months, properly and regularly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 107. I hope that you always provide all artisans with materials and tools for a period of not less than four months, properly and regularly.

sanskrit: कच्चित्कृतं विजानीषे कर्तारं च प्रशंससि ।सतां मध्ये महाराज सत्करोषि च पूजयन् ॥१०८॥
transliteration: kaccitkṛtaṁ vijānīṣe kartāraṁ ca praśaṁsasi ,satāṁ madhye mahārāja satkaroṣi ca pūjayan.
english: O great king (mahārāja), I hope that you acknowledge good deeds, praise the doer, and honor them respectfully among worthy people (sat).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 108. O great king (mahārāja), I hope that you acknowledge good deeds, praise the doer, and honor them respectfully among worthy people (sat).

sanskrit: कच्चित्सूत्राणि सर्वाणि गृह्णासि भरतर्षभ ।हस्तिसूत्राश्वसूत्राणि रथसूत्राणि चाभिभो ॥१०९॥
transliteration: kaccitsūtrāṇi sarvāṇi gṛhṇāsi bharatarṣabha ,hastisūtrāśvasūtrāṇi rathasūtrāṇi cābhibho.
english: O best among the Bharatas, O lord, I hope you master all the sciences, including those pertaining to elephants, horses, and chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 109. O best among the Bharatas, O lord, I hope you master all the sciences, including those pertaining to elephants, horses, and chariots.

sanskrit: कच्चिदभ्यस्यते शश्वद्गृहे ते भरतर्षभ ।धनुर्वेदस्य सूत्रं च यन्त्रसूत्रं च नागरम् ॥११०॥
transliteration: kaccidabhyasyate śaśvadgṛhe te bharatarṣabha ,dhanurvedasya sūtraṁ ca yantrasūtraṁ ca nāgaram.
english: O best among the Bharatas, I hope that the science of archery (dhanurveda), the science of mechanisms, and civic sciences are constantly practiced in your home.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 110. O best among the Bharatas, I hope that the science of archery (dhanurveda), the science of mechanisms, and civic sciences are constantly practiced in your home.

sanskrit: कच्चिदस्त्राणि सर्वाणि ब्रह्मदण्डश्च तेऽनघ ।विषयोगाश्च ते सर्वे विदिताः शत्रुनाशनाः ॥१११॥
transliteration: kaccidastrāṇi sarvāṇi brahmadaṇḍaśca te'nagha ,viṣayogāśca te sarve viditāḥ śatrunāśanāḥ.
english: O sinless one, I hope that all weapons, the divine staff (brahmadaṇḍa), and all your enemy-destroying applications of poison are known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 111. O sinless one, I hope that all weapons, the divine staff (brahmadaṇḍa), and all your enemy-destroying applications of poison are known to you.

sanskrit: कच्चिदग्निभयाच्चैव सर्पव्यालभयात्तथा ।रोगरक्षोभयाच्चैव राष्ट्रं स्वं परिरक्षसि ॥११२॥
transliteration: kaccidagnibhayāccaiva sarpavyālabhayāttathā ,rogarakṣobhayāccaiva rāṣṭraṁ svaṁ parirakṣasi.
english: Do you surely protect your own kingdom from dangers like fire, and similarly from the fear of snakes and wild animals, and furthermore, from disease and rākṣasas?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 112. Do you surely protect your own kingdom from dangers like fire, and similarly from the fear of snakes and wild animals, and furthermore, from disease and rākṣasas?

sanskrit: कच्चिदन्धांश्च मूकांश्च पङ्गून्व्यङ्गानबान्धवान् ।पितेव पासि धर्मज्ञ तथा प्रव्रजितानपि ॥११३॥
transliteration: kaccidandhāṁśca mūkāṁśca paṅgūnvyaṅgānabāndhavān ,piteva pāsi dharmajña tathā pravrajitānapi.
english: O knower of natural law (dharma), do you surely protect, like a father, the blind, the mute, the lame, the deformed, and those without relatives, and also the renunciants?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 113. O knower of natural law (dharma), do you surely protect, like a father, the blind, the mute, the lame, the deformed, and those without relatives, and also the renunciants?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एताः कुरूणामृषभो महात्मा श्रुत्वा गिरो ब्राह्मणसत्तमस्य ।प्रणम्य पादावभिवाद्य हृष्टो राजाब्रवीन्नारदं देवरूपम् ॥११४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etāḥ kurūṇāmṛṣabho mahātmā; śrutvā giro brāhmaṇasattamasya ,praṇamya pādāvabhivādya hṛṣṭo; rājābravīnnāradaṁ devarūpam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: The great-souled King, the chief of the Kurus, was delighted upon hearing these words of the best among Brahmins. He then bowed down at his feet, offered his greetings, and spoke to Nārada, who appeared divine.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 114. Vaiśampāyana said: The great-souled King, the chief of the Kurus, was delighted upon hearing these words of the best among Brahmins. He then bowed down at his feet, offered his greetings, and spoke to Nārada, who appeared divine.

sanskrit: एवं करिष्यामि यथा त्वयोक्तं प्रज्ञा हि मे भूय एवाभिवृद्धा ।उक्त्वा तथा चैव चकार राजा लेभे महीं सागरमेखलां च ॥११५॥
transliteration: evaṁ kariṣyāmi yathā tvayoktaṁ; prajñā hi me bhūya evābhivṛddhā ,uktvā tathā caiva cakāra rājā; lebhe mahīṁ sāgaramekhalāṁ ca.
english: I will certainly do as you have instructed, for my understanding (prajñā) has indeed greatly increased. Having said this, the king acted accordingly and obtained the entire earth, which is girdled by the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 115. I will certainly do as you have instructed, for my understanding (prajñā) has indeed greatly increased. Having said this, the king acted accordingly and obtained the entire earth, which is girdled by the ocean.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।एवं यो वर्तते राजा चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य रक्षणे ।स विहृत्येह सुसुखी शक्रस्यैति सलोकताम् ॥११६॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,evaṁ yo vartate rājā cāturvarṇyasya rakṣaṇe ,sa vihṛtyeha susukhī śakrasyaiti salokatām.
english: Nārada said: The king who conducts himself in this manner, dedicated to the protection of the four social classes (cāturvarṇya), lives very happily in this world and then attains the same celestial abode as Indra (Śakra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 5 Verse 116. Nārada said: The king who conducts himself in this manner, dedicated to the protection of the four social classes (cāturvarṇya), lives very happily in this world and then attains the same celestial abode as Indra (Śakra).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।संपूज्याथाभ्यनुज्ञातो महर्षेर्वचनात्परम् ।प्रत्युवाचानुपूर्व्येण धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,saṁpūjyāthābhyanujñāto maharṣervacanātparam ,pratyuvācānupūrvyeṇa dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having paid due respect and then received permission, after the great sage (maharṣi) had concluded his words, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), replied in due order.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having paid due respect and then received permission, after the great sage (maharṣi) had concluded his words, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), replied in due order.

sanskrit: भगवन्न्याय्यमाहैतं यथावद्धर्मनिश्चयम् ।यथाशक्ति यथान्यायं क्रियतेऽयं विधिर्मया ॥२॥
transliteration: bhagavannyāyyamāhaitaṁ yathāvaddharmaniścayam ,yathāśakti yathānyāyaṁ kriyate'yaṁ vidhirmayā.
english: O Lord, this determination of natural law (dharma) has been declared appropriately. This injunction is being performed by me according to my capacity and according to what is just.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 2. O Lord, this determination of natural law (dharma) has been declared appropriately. This injunction is being performed by me according to my capacity and according to what is just.

sanskrit: राजभिर्यद्यथा कार्यं पुरा तत्तन्न संशयः ।यथान्यायोपनीतार्थं कृतं हेतुमदर्थवत् ॥३॥
transliteration: rājabhiryadyathā kāryaṁ purā tattanna saṁśayaḥ ,yathānyāyopanītārthaṁ kṛtaṁ hetumadarthavat.
english: Whatever had to be done by kings in the past, there is no doubt that it was indeed done. It was performed with its purpose established according to justice, and it was both reasonable and meaningful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 3. Whatever had to be done by kings in the past, there is no doubt that it was indeed done. It was performed with its purpose established according to justice, and it was both reasonable and meaningful.

sanskrit: वयं तु सत्पथं तेषां यातुमिच्छामहे प्रभो ।न तु शक्यं तथा गन्तुं यथा तैर्नियतात्मभिः ॥४॥
transliteration: vayaṁ tu satpathaṁ teṣāṁ yātumicchāmahe prabho ,na tu śakyaṁ tathā gantuṁ yathā tairniyatātmabhiḥ.
english: But O Lord, we indeed wish to follow their righteous path. However, it is not possible for us to proceed in the same manner as those with controlled selves (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 4. But O Lord, we indeed wish to follow their righteous path. However, it is not possible for us to proceed in the same manner as those with controlled selves (ātman).

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा स धर्मात्मा वाक्यं तदभिपूज्य च ।मुहूर्तात्प्राप्तकालं च दृष्ट्वा लोकचरं मुनिम् ॥५॥
transliteration: evamuktvā sa dharmātmā vākyaṁ tadabhipūjya ca ,muhūrtātprāptakālaṁ ca dṛṣṭvā lokacaraṁ munim.
english: Having spoken thus, that righteous individual (dharmātmā), after honoring that statement, and perceiving the sage (muni) who traverses the worlds and had arrived at an opportune moment within a short time...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 5. Having spoken thus, that righteous individual (dharmātmā), after honoring that statement, and perceiving the sage (muni) who traverses the worlds and had arrived at an opportune moment within a short time...

sanskrit: नारदं स्वस्थमासीनमुपासीनो युधिष्ठिरः ।अपृच्छत्पाण्डवस्तत्र राजमध्ये महामतिः ॥६॥
transliteration: nāradaṁ svasthamāsīnamupāsīno yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,apṛcchatpāṇḍavastatra rājamadhye mahāmatiḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, the great-minded (mahāmatiḥ) Pāṇḍava, attended upon Nārada, who was comfortably seated, and then questioned him there amidst the kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 6. Yudhiṣṭhira, the great-minded (mahāmatiḥ) Pāṇḍava, attended upon Nārada, who was comfortably seated, and then questioned him there amidst the kings.

sanskrit: भवान्संचरते लोकान्सदा नानाविधान्बहून् ।ब्रह्मणा निर्मितान्पूर्वं प्रेक्षमाणो मनोजवः ॥७॥
transliteration: bhavānsaṁcarate lokānsadā nānāvidhānbahūn ,brahmaṇā nirmitānpūrvaṁ prekṣamāṇo manojavaḥ.
english: Your honor (bhavān) always travels through many diverse worlds, which were created long ago by Brahmā, observing them with a mind swift as thought (manojavḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 7. Your honor (bhavān) always travels through many diverse worlds, which were created long ago by Brahmā, observing them with a mind swift as thought (manojavḥ).

sanskrit: ईदृशी भवता काचिद्दृष्टपूर्वा सभा क्वचित् ।इतो वा श्रेयसी ब्रह्मंस्तन्ममाचक्ष्व पृच्छतः ॥८॥
transliteration: īdṛśī bhavatā kāciddṛṣṭapūrvā sabhā kvacit ,ito vā śreyasī brahmaṁstanmamācakṣva pṛcchataḥ.
english: O Brahmin, have you ever seen such an assembly (sabhā) before, or one superior to this, anywhere? Please tell me, as I am asking.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 8. O Brahmin, have you ever seen such an assembly (sabhā) before, or one superior to this, anywhere? Please tell me, as I am asking.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा नारदस्तस्य धर्मराजस्य भाषितम् ।पाण्डवं प्रत्युवाचेदं स्मयन्मधुरया गिरा ॥९॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā nāradastasya dharmarājasya bhāṣitam ,pāṇḍavaṁ pratyuvācedaṁ smayanmadhurayā girā.
english: Having heard that speech from Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), Nārada, smiling, spoke these words to the Pāṇḍava in a sweet voice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 9. Having heard that speech from Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), Nārada, smiling, spoke these words to the Pāṇḍava in a sweet voice.

sanskrit: मानुषेषु न मे तात दृष्टपूर्वा न च श्रुता ।सभा मणिमयी राजन्यथेयं तव भारत ॥१०॥
transliteration: mānuṣeṣu na me tāta dṛṣṭapūrvā na ca śrutā ,sabhā maṇimayī rājanyatheyaṁ tava bhārata.
english: O dear one, O King, O Bhārata, among humans, I have neither seen nor heard of a jeweled assembly (sabhā) like this one of yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 10. O dear one, O King, O Bhārata, among humans, I have neither seen nor heard of a jeweled assembly (sabhā) like this one of yours.

sanskrit: सभां तु पितृराजस्य वरुणस्य च धीमतः ।कथयिष्ये तथेन्द्रस्य कैलासनिलयस्य च ॥११॥
transliteration: sabhāṁ tu pitṛrājasya varuṇasya ca dhīmataḥ ,kathayiṣye tathendrasya kailāsanilayasya ca.
english: Indeed, I will describe the assembly of Yama, and of the wise Varuna, and similarly that of Indra, and of Kubera, who resides on Kailasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 11. Indeed, I will describe the assembly of Yama, and of the wise Varuna, and similarly that of Indra, and of Kubera, who resides on Kailasa.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मणश्च सभां दिव्यां कथयिष्ये गतक्लमाम् ।यदि ते श्रवणे बुद्धिर्वर्तते भरतर्षभ ॥१२॥
transliteration: brahmaṇaśca sabhāṁ divyāṁ kathayiṣye gataklamām ,yadi te śravaṇe buddhirvartate bharatarṣabha.
english: And I will describe the divine assembly of Brahma, which is beyond weariness, if you have an inclination (buddhi) to listen, O best of Bharatas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 12. And I will describe the divine assembly of Brahma, which is beyond weariness, if you have an inclination (buddhi) to listen, O best of Bharatas.

sanskrit: नारदेनैवमुक्तस्तु धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।प्राञ्जलिर्भ्रातृभिः सार्धं तैश्च सर्वैर्नृपैर्वृतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: nāradenaivamuktastu dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,prāñjalirbhrātṛbhiḥ sārdhaṁ taiśca sarvairnṛpairvṛtaḥ.
english: Indeed, when he was thus addressed by Narada, King Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma), stood with folded hands, accompanied by his brothers and surrounded by all those kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 13. Indeed, when he was thus addressed by Narada, King Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharma), stood with folded hands, accompanied by his brothers and surrounded by all those kings.

sanskrit: नारदं प्रत्युवाचेदं धर्मराजो महामनाः ।सभाः कथय ताः सर्वाः श्रोतुमिच्छामहे वयम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: nāradaṁ pratyuvācedaṁ dharmarājo mahāmanāḥ ,sabhāḥ kathaya tāḥ sarvāḥ śrotumicchāmahe vayam.
english: The great-minded King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja) replied to Narada, "Please describe all those assemblies (sabhāḥ) to us; we wish to hear about them."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 14. The great-minded King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja) replied to Narada, "Please describe all those assemblies (sabhāḥ) to us; we wish to hear about them."

sanskrit: किंद्रव्यास्ताः सभा ब्रह्मन्किंविस्ताराः किमायताः ।पितामहं च के तस्यां सभायां पर्युपासते ॥१५॥
transliteration: kiṁdravyāstāḥ sabhā brahmankiṁvistārāḥ kimāyatāḥ ,pitāmahaṁ ca ke tasyāṁ sabhāyāṁ paryupāsate.
english: O brahmana, of what substance are those assemblies (sabhāḥ)? What is their width and what is their length? And who attends to Grandfather Brahma in that assembly?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 15. O brahmana, of what substance are those assemblies (sabhāḥ)? What is their width and what is their length? And who attends to Grandfather Brahma in that assembly?

sanskrit: वासवं देवराजं च यमं वैवस्वतं च के ।वरुणं च कुबेरं च सभायां पर्युपासते ॥१६॥
transliteration: vāsavaṁ devarājaṁ ca yamaṁ vaivasvataṁ ca ke ,varuṇaṁ ca kuberaṁ ca sabhāyāṁ paryupāsate.
english: And who attends to Vasava (Indra), the king of the gods, and to Yama, the son of Vivasvat; and to Varuna and Kubera, in their assembly?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 16. And who attends to Vasava (Indra), the king of the gods, and to Yama, the son of Vivasvat; and to Varuna and Kubera, in their assembly?

sanskrit: एतत्सर्वं यथातत्त्वं देवर्षे वदतस्तव ।श्रोतुमिच्छाम सहिताः परं कौतूहलं हि नः ॥१७॥
transliteration: etatsarvaṁ yathātattvaṁ devarṣe vadatastava ,śrotumicchāma sahitāḥ paraṁ kautūhalaṁ hi naḥ.
english: O divine sage, as you speak, we, assembled together, wish to hear all this as it truly is, for indeed, our curiosity is immense.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 17. O divine sage, as you speak, we, assembled together, wish to hear all this as it truly is, for indeed, our curiosity is immense.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः पाण्डवेन नारदः प्रत्युवाच तम् ।क्रमेण राजन्दिव्यास्ताः श्रूयन्तामिह नः सभाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ pāṇḍavena nāradaḥ pratyuvāca tam ,krameṇa rājandivyāstāḥ śrūyantāmiha naḥ sabhāḥ.
english: Thus addressed by the Pāṇḍava, Nārada replied to him, 'O King, let those divine assemblies (sabhāḥ) be heard by us here, in due order.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 6 Verse 18. Thus addressed by the Pāṇḍava, Nārada replied to him, 'O King, let those divine assemblies (sabhāḥ) be heard by us here, in due order.'

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।शक्रस्य तु सभा दिव्या भास्वरा कर्मभिर्जिता ।स्वयं शक्रेण कौरव्य निर्मितार्कसमप्रभा ॥१॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,śakrasya tu sabhā divyā bhāsvarā karmabhirjitā ,svayaṁ śakreṇa kauravya nirmitārkasamaprabhā.
english: Nārada said, 'O scion of Kuru, the divine and splendid assembly (sabhā) of Indra (Śakra), acquired through his valorous deeds (karma), was built by Śakra himself, shining with a brilliance equal to the sun.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 1. Nārada said, 'O scion of Kuru, the divine and splendid assembly (sabhā) of Indra (Śakra), acquired through his valorous deeds (karma), was built by Śakra himself, shining with a brilliance equal to the sun.'

sanskrit: विस्तीर्णा योजनशतं शतमध्यर्धमायता ।वैहायसी कामगमा पञ्चयोजनमुच्छ्रिता ॥२॥
transliteration: vistīrṇā yojanaśataṁ śatamadhyardhamāyatā ,vaihāyasī kāmagamā pañcayojanamucchritā.
english: It was a hundred yojanas wide and one hundred and fifty yojanas long. It moved through the sky at will and was five yojanas high.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 2. It was a hundred yojanas wide and one hundred and fifty yojanas long. It moved through the sky at will and was five yojanas high.

sanskrit: जराशोकक्लमापेता निरातङ्का शिवा शुभा ।वेश्मासनवती रम्या दिव्यपादपशोभिता ॥३॥
transliteration: jarāśokaklamāpetā nirātaṅkā śivā śubhā ,veśmāsanavatī ramyā divyapādapaśobhitā.
english: It was free from old age, sorrow, and weariness, secure, auspicious, and splendid. It possessed palaces and seats, was delightful, and adorned with divine trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 3. It was free from old age, sorrow, and weariness, secure, auspicious, and splendid. It possessed palaces and seats, was delightful, and adorned with divine trees.

sanskrit: तस्यां देवेश्वरः पार्थ सभायां परमासने ।आस्ते शच्या महेन्द्राण्या श्रिया लक्ष्म्या च भारत ॥४॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ deveśvaraḥ pārtha sabhāyāṁ paramāsane ,āste śacyā mahendrāṇyā śriyā lakṣmyā ca bhārata.
english: O Pārtha, in that assembly hall, the lord of the gods sits on the supreme seat. He is accompanied by Śacī, Mahendrāṇī, Śrī, and Lakṣmī, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 4. O Pārtha, in that assembly hall, the lord of the gods sits on the supreme seat. He is accompanied by Śacī, Mahendrāṇī, Śrī, and Lakṣmī, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: बिभ्रद्वपुरनिर्देश्यं किरीटी लोहिताङ्गदः ।विरजोम्बरश्चित्रमाल्यो ह्रीकीर्तिद्युतिभिः सह ॥५॥
transliteration: bibhradvapuranirdeśyaṁ kirīṭī lohitāṅgadaḥ ,virajombaraścitramālyo hrīkīrtidyutibhiḥ saha.
english: Bearing an indescribable form, crowned, adorned with red armlets, wearing stainless garments and a variegated garland, he is accompanied by modesty, fame, and splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 5. Bearing an indescribable form, crowned, adorned with red armlets, wearing stainless garments and a variegated garland, he is accompanied by modesty, fame, and splendor.

sanskrit: तस्यामुपासते नित्यं महात्मानं शतक्रतुम् ।मरुतः सर्वतो राजन्सर्वे च गृहमेधिनः ।सिद्धा देवर्षयश्चैव साध्या देवगणास्तथा ॥६॥
transliteration: tasyāmupāsate nityaṁ mahātmānaṁ śatakratum ,marutaḥ sarvato rājansarve ca gṛhamedhinaḥ ,siddhā devarṣayaścaiva sādhyā devagaṇāstathā.
english: O King, in her (or, there), the Maruts, and all householders (gṛhamedhinaḥ), the Siddhas, the divine sages (devarṣayaḥ), the Sadhyas, and likewise the hosts of gods, constantly worship the great soul (mahātman), Śatakratu (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 6. O King, in her (or, there), the Maruts, and all householders (gṛhamedhinaḥ), the Siddhas, the divine sages (devarṣayaḥ), the Sadhyas, and likewise the hosts of gods, constantly worship the great soul (mahātman), Śatakratu (Indra).

sanskrit: एते सानुचराः सर्वे दिव्यरूपाः स्वलंकृताः ।उपासते महात्मानं देवराजमरिंदमम् ॥७॥
transliteration: ete sānucarāḥ sarve divyarūpāḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ ,upāsate mahātmānaṁ devarājamariṁdamam.
english: All of these, accompanied by their followers, having divine forms and well-adorned, worship the great soul (mahātman), the king of gods (devarāja), the vanquisher of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 7. All of these, accompanied by their followers, having divine forms and well-adorned, worship the great soul (mahātman), the king of gods (devarāja), the vanquisher of enemies.

sanskrit: तथा देवर्षयः सर्वे पार्थ शक्रमुपासते ।अमला धूतपाप्मानो दीप्यमाना इवाग्नयः ।तेजस्विनः सोमयुजो विपापा विगतक्लमाः ॥८॥
transliteration: tathā devarṣayaḥ sarve pārtha śakramupāsate ,amalā dhūtapāpmāno dīpyamānā ivāgnayaḥ ,tejasvinaḥ somayujo vipāpā vigataklamāḥ.
english: O Pārtha, all the divine sages similarly worship Indra. They are pure, cleansed of their sins, and shine like fires. They are glorious, associated with the Soma (yajña), free from sin, and devoid of weariness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 8. O Pārtha, all the divine sages similarly worship Indra. They are pure, cleansed of their sins, and shine like fires. They are glorious, associated with the Soma (yajña), free from sin, and devoid of weariness.

sanskrit: पराशरः पर्वतश्च तथा सावर्णिगालवौ ।शङ्खश्च लिखितश्चैव तथा गौरशिरा मुनिः ॥९॥
transliteration: parāśaraḥ parvataśca tathā sāvarṇigālavau ,śaṅkhaśca likhitaścaiva tathā gauraśirā muniḥ.
english: Parāśara, Parvata, and also Sāvarṇi and Gālava; and Śaṅkha, and Likhita as well; and similarly, the sage Gauraśirā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 9. Parāśara, Parvata, and also Sāvarṇi and Gālava; and Śaṅkha, and Likhita as well; and similarly, the sage Gauraśirā.

sanskrit: दुर्वासाश्च दीर्घतपा याज्ञवल्क्योऽथ भालुकिः ।उद्दालकः श्वेतकेतुस्तथा शाट्यायनः प्रभुः ॥१०॥
transliteration: durvāsāśca dīrghatapā yājñavalkyo'tha bhālukiḥ ,uddālakaḥ śvetaketustathā śāṭyāyanaḥ prabhuḥ.
english: And Durvāsas, the sage of profound asceticism (tapas); then Yājñavalkya, Bhāluki, Uddālaka, and Śvetaketu; and also the venerable Śāṭyāyana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 10. And Durvāsas, the sage of profound asceticism (tapas); then Yājñavalkya, Bhāluki, Uddālaka, and Śvetaketu; and also the venerable Śāṭyāyana.

sanskrit: हविष्मांश्च गविष्ठश्च हरिश्चन्द्रश्च पार्थिवः ।हृद्यश्चोदरशाण्डिल्यः पाराशर्यः कृषीवलः ॥११॥
transliteration: haviṣmāṁśca gaviṣṭhaśca hariścandraśca pārthivaḥ ,hṛdyaścodaraśāṇḍilyaḥ pārāśaryaḥ kṛṣīvalaḥ.
english: Haviṣmān, Gaviṣṭha, and King Hariścandra, along with Hṛdya, Udaraśāṇḍilya, Pārāśarya, and Kṛṣīvala.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 11. Haviṣmān, Gaviṣṭha, and King Hariścandra, along with Hṛdya, Udaraśāṇḍilya, Pārāśarya, and Kṛṣīvala.

sanskrit: वातस्कन्धो विशाखश्च विधाता काल एव च ।अनन्तदन्तस्त्वष्टा च विश्वकर्मा च तुम्बुरुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vātaskandho viśākhaśca vidhātā kāla eva ca ,anantadantastvaṣṭā ca viśvakarmā ca tumburuḥ.
english: Vātaskandha, Viśākha, Vidhātā, and Kāla (Time), as well as Anantadanta, Tvaṣṭā, Viśvakarmā, and Tumburu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 12. Vātaskandha, Viśākha, Vidhātā, and Kāla (Time), as well as Anantadanta, Tvaṣṭā, Viśvakarmā, and Tumburu.

sanskrit: अयोनिजा योनिजाश्च वायुभक्षा हुताशिनः ।ईशानं सर्वलोकस्य वज्रिणं समुपासते ॥१३॥
transliteration: ayonijā yonijāśca vāyubhakṣā hutāśinaḥ ,īśānaṁ sarvalokasya vajriṇaṁ samupāsate.
english: Both those who are not born from a womb and those who are, as well as those who subsist on air and those who consume offerings, all worship the ruler (īśāna) of all worlds, the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 13. Both those who are not born from a womb and those who are, as well as those who subsist on air and those who consume offerings, all worship the ruler (īśāna) of all worlds, the wielder of the thunderbolt (vajrī).

sanskrit: सहदेवः सुनीथश्च वाल्मीकिश्च महातपाः ।समीकः सत्यवांश्चैव प्रचेताः सत्यसंगरः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sahadevaḥ sunīthaśca vālmīkiśca mahātapāḥ ,samīkaḥ satyavāṁścaiva pracetāḥ satyasaṁgaraḥ.
english: Sahadeva, Sunītha, and Vālmīki, the great ascetic (tapas); Samīka, Satyavān, and Pracetas, the one true to his vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 14. Sahadeva, Sunītha, and Vālmīki, the great ascetic (tapas); Samīka, Satyavān, and Pracetas, the one true to his vows.

sanskrit: मेधातिथिर्वामदेवः पुलस्त्यः पुलहः क्रतुः ।मरुत्तश्च मरीचिश्च स्थाणुश्चात्रिर्महातपाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: medhātithirvāmadevaḥ pulastyaḥ pulahaḥ kratuḥ ,maruttaśca marīciśca sthāṇuścātrirmahātapāḥ.
english: Medhātithi, Vāmadeva, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu; and Marutta, and Marīci, and Sthāṇu, and Atri, the great ascetic (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 15. Medhātithi, Vāmadeva, Pulastya, Pulaha, Kratu; and Marutta, and Marīci, and Sthāṇu, and Atri, the great ascetic (tapas).

sanskrit: कक्षीवान्गौतमस्तार्क्ष्यस्तथा वैश्वानरो मुनिः ।मुनिः कालकवृक्षीय आश्राव्योऽथ हिरण्यदः ।संवर्तो देवहव्यश्च विष्वक्सेनश्च वीर्यवान् ॥१६॥
transliteration: kakṣīvāngautamastārkṣyastathā vaiśvānaro muniḥ ,muniḥ kālakavṛkṣīya āśrāvyo'tha hiraṇyadaḥ ,saṁvarto devahavyaśca viṣvaksenaśca vīryavān.
english: Kakṣīvān, Gautama, Tārkṣya, and also Vaiśvānara the sage (muni); the sage (muni) Kālakavṛkṣīya, Āśrāvya, and Hiraṇyada; Saṃvarta, Devahavya, and Viṣvaksena the powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 16. Kakṣīvān, Gautama, Tārkṣya, and also Vaiśvānara the sage (muni); the sage (muni) Kālakavṛkṣīya, Āśrāvya, and Hiraṇyada; Saṃvarta, Devahavya, and Viṣvaksena the powerful.

sanskrit: दिव्या आपस्तथौषध्यः श्रद्धा मेधा सरस्वती ।अर्थो धर्मश्च कामश्च विद्युतश्चापि पाण्डव ॥१७॥
transliteration: divyā āpastathauṣadhyaḥ śraddhā medhā sarasvatī ,artho dharmaśca kāmaśca vidyutaścāpi pāṇḍava.
english: O Pāṇḍava, (these include) the divine waters, medicinal herbs, faith (śraddhā), intellect, Sarasvatī, wealth, righteousness (dharma), desire, and also lightnings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 17. O Pāṇḍava, (these include) the divine waters, medicinal herbs, faith (śraddhā), intellect, Sarasvatī, wealth, righteousness (dharma), desire, and also lightnings.

sanskrit: जलवाहास्तथा मेघा वायवः स्तनयित्नवः ।प्राची दिग्यज्ञवाहाश्च पावकाः सप्तविंशतिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: jalavāhāstathā meghā vāyavaḥ stanayitnavaḥ ,prācī digyajñavāhāśca pāvakāḥ saptaviṁśatiḥ.
english: And similarly, the water-carrying clouds, other clouds, the winds, the thunder-clouds, the Eastern direction, the carriers of offerings (yajña), and the twenty-seven purifiers (pāvakāḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 18. And similarly, the water-carrying clouds, other clouds, the winds, the thunder-clouds, the Eastern direction, the carriers of offerings (yajña), and the twenty-seven purifiers (pāvakāḥ).

sanskrit: अग्नीषोमौ तथेन्द्राग्नी मित्रोऽथ सवितार्यमा ।भगो विश्वे च साध्याश्च शुक्रो मन्थी च भारत ॥१९॥
transliteration: agnīṣomau tathendrāgnī mitro'tha savitāryamā ,bhago viśve ca sādhyāśca śukro manthī ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, (these include) Agni and Soma, and similarly, Indra and Agni. Moreover, Mitra, Savitṛ, Aryaman, Bhaga, the Viśvedevas, and the Sādhyas, and also Śukra and Manthī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 19. O Bhārata, (these include) Agni and Soma, and similarly, Indra and Agni. Moreover, Mitra, Savitṛ, Aryaman, Bhaga, the Viśvedevas, and the Sādhyas, and also Śukra and Manthī.
