sanskrit: यज्ञाश्च दक्षिणाश्चैव ग्रहाः स्तोभाश्च सर्वशः ।यज्ञवाहाश्च ये मन्त्राः सर्वे तत्र समासते ॥२०॥
transliteration: yajñāśca dakṣiṇāścaiva grahāḥ stobhāśca sarvaśaḥ ,yajñavāhāśca ye mantrāḥ sarve tatra samāsate.
english: All sacrifices (yajñas) and ritual fees (dakṣiṇās), as well as the planets (grahās) and praises (stobhās) of all kinds, along with those mantras that convey the sacrifices, are all assembled there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 20. All sacrifices (yajñas) and ritual fees (dakṣiṇās), as well as the planets (grahās) and praises (stobhās) of all kinds, along with those mantras that convey the sacrifices, are all assembled there.

sanskrit: तथैवाप्सरसो राजन्गन्धर्वाश्च मनोरमाः ।नृत्यवादित्रगीतैश्च हास्यैश्च विविधैरपि ।रमयन्ति स्म नृपते देवराजं शतक्रतुम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathaivāpsaraso rājangandharvāśca manoramāḥ ,nṛtyavāditragītaiśca hāsyaiśca vividhairapi ,ramayanti sma nṛpate devarājaṁ śatakratum.
english: Similarly, O King, charming Apsaras and Gandharvas, with dances, instrumental music, and songs, and also with various kinds of humor, used to delight Śatakratu, the king of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 21. Similarly, O King, charming Apsaras and Gandharvas, with dances, instrumental music, and songs, and also with various kinds of humor, used to delight Śatakratu, the king of the gods.

sanskrit: स्तुतिभिर्मङ्गलैश्चैव स्तुवन्तः कर्मभिस्तथा ।विक्रमैश्च महात्मानं बलवृत्रनिषूदनम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: stutibhirmaṅgalaiścaiva stuvantaḥ karmabhistathā ,vikramaiśca mahātmānaṁ balavṛtraniṣūdanam.
english: Praising the great-souled one (mahātman) and the annihilator of Bala and Vṛtra, they did so with hymns, auspicious utterances, noble deeds (karma), and valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 22. Praising the great-souled one (mahātman) and the annihilator of Bala and Vṛtra, they did so with hymns, auspicious utterances, noble deeds (karma), and valor.

sanskrit: ब्रह्मराजर्षयः सर्वे सर्वे देवर्षयस्तथा ।विमानैर्विविधैर्दिव्यैर्भ्राजमानैरिवाग्निभिः ॥२३॥
transliteration: brahmarājarṣayaḥ sarve sarve devarṣayastathā ,vimānairvividhairdivyairbhrājamānairivāgnibhiḥ.
english: All the Brāhmaṇa-sages and royal sages, and similarly all the divine sages, were present, radiant like fires, in their various divine celestial chariots.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 23. All the Brāhmaṇa-sages and royal sages, and similarly all the divine sages, were present, radiant like fires, in their various divine celestial chariots.

sanskrit: स्रग्विणो भूषिताश्चान्ये यान्ति चायान्ति चापरे ।बृहस्पतिश्च शुक्रश्च तस्यामाययतुः सह ॥२४॥
transliteration: sragviṇo bhūṣitāścānye yānti cāyānti cāpare ,bṛhaspatiśca śukraśca tasyāmāyayatuḥ saha.
english: Others, adorned and garlanded, were coming and going. Bṛhaspati and Śukra also arrived there together.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 24. Others, adorned and garlanded, were coming and going. Bṛhaspati and Śukra also arrived there together.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो यतात्मानो यतव्रताः ।विमानैश्चन्द्रसंकाशैः सोमवत्प्रियदर्शनाः ।ब्रह्मणो वचनाद्राजन्भृगुः सप्तर्षयस्तथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo yatātmāno yatavratāḥ ,vimānaiścandrasaṁkāśaiḥ somavatpriyadarśanāḥ ,brahmaṇo vacanādrājanbhṛguḥ saptarṣayastathā.
english: And these, along with many other individuals who had disciplined their inner being (ātman) and strictly observed their vows, delightful to behold like Soma, arrived in celestial chariots that shone like the moon. O King, Bhṛgu and the seven sages (Saptaṛṣis) also came by the command of Brahmā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 25. And these, along with many other individuals who had disciplined their inner being (ātman) and strictly observed their vows, delightful to behold like Soma, arrived in celestial chariots that shone like the moon. O King, Bhṛgu and the seven sages (Saptaṛṣis) also came by the command of Brahmā.

sanskrit: एषा सभा मया राजन्दृष्टा पुष्करमालिनी ।शतक्रतोर्महाराज याम्यां शृणु ममानघ ॥२६॥
transliteration: eṣā sabhā mayā rājandṛṣṭā puṣkaramālinī ,śatakratormahārāja yāmyāṁ śṛṇu mamānagha.
english: O King, I have seen this assembly hall adorned with lotuses. O great king, performer of a hundred sacrifices, O sinless one, listen to me about Yama's (assembly hall).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 7 Verse 26. O King, I have seen this assembly hall adorned with lotuses. O great king, performer of a hundred sacrifices, O sinless one, listen to me about Yama's (assembly hall).

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।कथयिष्ये सभां दिव्यां युधिष्ठिर निबोध ताम् ।वैवस्वतस्य यामर्थे विश्वकर्मा चकार ह ॥१॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,kathayiṣye sabhāṁ divyāṁ yudhiṣṭhira nibodha tām ,vaivasvatasya yāmarthe viśvakarmā cakāra ha.
english: Nārada said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, I will describe the divine assembly hall; listen carefully to that, which Viśvakarmā indeed built for Vaivasvata (Yama).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 1. Nārada said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, I will describe the divine assembly hall; listen carefully to that, which Viśvakarmā indeed built for Vaivasvata (Yama).'

sanskrit: तैजसी सा सभा राजन्बभूव शतयोजना ।विस्तारायामसंपन्ना भूयसी चापि पाण्डव ॥२॥
transliteration: taijasī sā sabhā rājanbabhūva śatayojanā ,vistārāyāmasaṁpannā bhūyasī cāpi pāṇḍava.
english: O King, that luminous assembly hall measured a hundred yojanas. And, O Pāṇḍava, it was endowed with immense breadth and length.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 2. O King, that luminous assembly hall measured a hundred yojanas. And, O Pāṇḍava, it was endowed with immense breadth and length.

sanskrit: अर्कप्रकाशा भ्राजिष्णुः सर्वतः कामचारिणी ।नैवातिशीता नात्युष्णा मनसश्च प्रहर्षिणी ॥३॥
transliteration: arkaprakāśā bhrājiṣṇuḥ sarvataḥ kāmacāriṇī ,naivātiśītā nātyuṣṇā manasaśca praharṣiṇī.
english: It is as radiant as the sun, glittering brightly, and moving freely everywhere. It is neither too cold nor too hot, and it delights the mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 3. It is as radiant as the sun, glittering brightly, and moving freely everywhere. It is neither too cold nor too hot, and it delights the mind.

sanskrit: न शोको न जरा तस्यां क्षुत्पिपासे न चाप्रियम् ।न च दैन्यं क्लमो वापि प्रतिकूलं न चाप्युत ॥४॥
transliteration: na śoko na jarā tasyāṁ kṣutpipāse na cāpriyam ,na ca dainyaṁ klamo vāpi pratikūlaṁ na cāpyuta.
english: In it, there is no sorrow, no old age, no hunger or thirst, nor any unpleasantness. Neither is there misery, nor fatigue, nor even anything adverse.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 4. In it, there is no sorrow, no old age, no hunger or thirst, nor any unpleasantness. Neither is there misery, nor fatigue, nor even anything adverse.

sanskrit: सर्वे कामाः स्थितास्तस्यां ये दिव्या ये च मानुषाः ।रसवच्च प्रभूतं च भक्ष्यभोज्यमरिंदम ॥५॥
transliteration: sarve kāmāḥ sthitāstasyāṁ ye divyā ye ca mānuṣāḥ ,rasavacca prabhūtaṁ ca bhakṣyabhojyamariṁdama.
english: O subduer of enemies (ariṃdama), all desires - both divine and human - are present in it. And there is also delicious and abundant food and drink.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 5. O subduer of enemies (ariṃdama), all desires - both divine and human - are present in it. And there is also delicious and abundant food and drink.

sanskrit: पुण्यगन्धाः स्रजस्तत्र नित्यपुष्पफलद्रुमाः ।रसवन्ति च तोयानि शीतान्युष्णानि चैव ह ॥६॥
transliteration: puṇyagandhāḥ srajastatra nityapuṣpaphaladrumāḥ ,rasavanti ca toyāni śītānyuṣṇāni caiva ha.
english: There are fragrant garlands, and trees that perpetually bear flowers and fruits. Also present are waters, both cold and hot, that are tasteful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 6. There are fragrant garlands, and trees that perpetually bear flowers and fruits. Also present are waters, both cold and hot, that are tasteful.

sanskrit: तस्यां राजर्षयः पुण्यास्तथा ब्रह्मर्षयोऽमलाः ।यमं वैवस्वतं तात प्रहृष्टाः पर्युपासते ॥७॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ rājarṣayaḥ puṇyāstathā brahmarṣayo'malāḥ ,yamaṁ vaivasvataṁ tāta prahṛṣṭāḥ paryupāsate.
english: O dear one, in that very place, pure royal sages and spotless brahmin sages joyfully worship Yama, the son of Vivasvat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 7. O dear one, in that very place, pure royal sages and spotless brahmin sages joyfully worship Yama, the son of Vivasvat.

sanskrit: ययातिर्नहुषः पूरुर्मान्धाता सोमको नृगः ।त्रसदस्युश्च तुरयः कृतवीर्यः श्रुतश्रवाः ॥८॥
transliteration: yayātirnahuṣaḥ pūrurmāndhātā somako nṛgaḥ ,trasadasyuśca turayaḥ kṛtavīryaḥ śrutaśravāḥ.
english: (Among them are) Yayāti, Nahuṣa, Pūru, Māndhātā, Somaka, Nṛga, and Trasadasyu, Turaya, Kṛtavīrya, and Śrutaśravāḥ.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 8. (Among them are) Yayāti, Nahuṣa, Pūru, Māndhātā, Somaka, Nṛga, and Trasadasyu, Turaya, Kṛtavīrya, and Śrutaśravāḥ.

sanskrit: अरिप्रणुत्सुसिंहश्च कृतवेगः कृतिर्निमिः ।प्रतर्दनः शिबिर्मत्स्यः पृथ्वक्षोऽथ बृहद्रथः ॥९॥
transliteration: aripraṇutsusiṁhaśca kṛtavegaḥ kṛtirnimiḥ ,pratardanaḥ śibirmatsyaḥ pṛthvakṣo'tha bṛhadrathaḥ.
english: Aripranut, Susimha, Krtavēga, Krti, Nimi, Pratardana, Shibi, Matsya, Pṛthvakṣa, and then Bṛhadratha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 9. Aripranut, Susimha, Krtavēga, Krti, Nimi, Pratardana, Shibi, Matsya, Pṛthvakṣa, and then Bṛhadratha.

sanskrit: ऐडो मरुत्तः कुशिकः सांकाश्यः सांकृतिर्भवः ।चतुरश्वः सदश्वोर्मिः कार्तवीर्यश्च पार्थिवः ॥१०॥
transliteration: aiḍo maruttaḥ kuśikaḥ sāṁkāśyaḥ sāṁkṛtirbhavaḥ ,caturaśvaḥ sadaśvormiḥ kārtavīryaśca pārthivaḥ.
english: Aiḍa, Marutta, Kuśika, Sāṃkāśya, Sāṃkṛti, Bhava, Caturāśva, Sadaśvormi, Kārtavīrya, and Pārthiva.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 10. Aiḍa, Marutta, Kuśika, Sāṃkāśya, Sāṃkṛti, Bhava, Caturāśva, Sadaśvormi, Kārtavīrya, and Pārthiva.

sanskrit: भरतस्तथा सुरथः सुनीथो नैषधो नलः ।दिवोदासोऽथ सुमना अम्बरीषो भगीरथः ॥११॥
transliteration: bharatastathā surathaḥ sunītho naiṣadho nalaḥ ,divodāso'tha sumanā ambarīṣo bhagīrathaḥ.
english: Bharata, similarly Suratha, Sunītha, Naiṣadha, Nala, Divodāsa, then Sumana, Ambarīṣa, and Bhagīratha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 11. Bharata, similarly Suratha, Sunītha, Naiṣadha, Nala, Divodāsa, then Sumana, Ambarīṣa, and Bhagīratha.

sanskrit: व्यश्वः सदश्वो वध्र्यश्वः पञ्चहस्तः पृथुश्रवाः ।रुषद्गुर्वृषसेनश्च क्षुपश्च सुमहाबलः ॥१२॥
transliteration: vyaśvaḥ sadaśvo vadhryaśvaḥ pañcahastaḥ pṛthuśravāḥ ,ruṣadgurvṛṣasenaśca kṣupaśca sumahābalaḥ.
english: Vyāśva, Sadaśva, Vadhryaśva, Pañcahasta, Pṛthuśravāḥ, Ruṣadgu, Vṛṣasena, Kṣupa, and the very mighty Sumahābala.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 12. Vyāśva, Sadaśva, Vadhryaśva, Pañcahasta, Pṛthuśravāḥ, Ruṣadgu, Vṛṣasena, Kṣupa, and the very mighty Sumahābala.

sanskrit: रुषदश्वो वसुमनाः पुरुकुत्सो ध्वजी रथी ।आर्ष्टिषेणो दिलीपश्च महात्मा चाप्युशीनरः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ruṣadaśvo vasumanāḥ purukutso dhvajī rathī ,ārṣṭiṣeṇo dilīpaśca mahātmā cāpyuśīnaraḥ.
english: Ruṣadaśva, Vasumanāḥ, Purukutsa, Dhvajī, Rathī, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Dilīpa, and also the great-souled (mahātmā) Uśīnara.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 13. Ruṣadaśva, Vasumanāḥ, Purukutsa, Dhvajī, Rathī, Ārṣṭiṣeṇa, Dilīpa, and also the great-souled (mahātmā) Uśīnara.

sanskrit: औशीनरः पुण्डरीकः शर्यातिः शरभः शुचिः ।अङ्गोऽरिष्टश्च वेनश्च दुःषन्तः संजयो जयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: auśīnaraḥ puṇḍarīkaḥ śaryātiḥ śarabhaḥ śuciḥ ,aṅgo'riṣṭaśca venaśca duḥṣantaḥ saṁjayo jayaḥ.
english: Auśīnara, Puṇḍarīka, Śaryāti, Śarabha, Śuci, Aṅga, Ariṣṭa, Vena, Duḥṣanta, Saṃjaya, and Jaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 14. Auśīnara, Puṇḍarīka, Śaryāti, Śarabha, Śuci, Aṅga, Ariṣṭa, Vena, Duḥṣanta, Saṃjaya, and Jaya.

sanskrit: भाङ्गास्वरिः सुनीथश्च निषधोऽथ त्विषीरथः ।करंधमो बाह्लिकश्च सुद्युम्नो बलवान्मधुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhāṅgāsvariḥ sunīthaśca niṣadho'tha tviṣīrathaḥ ,karaṁdhamo bāhlikaśca sudyumno balavānmadhuḥ.
english: Bhāṅgāsvara, Sunītha, and Niṣadha, then Tviṣīratha; Karaṃdhama, Bāhlika, Sudyumna, Balavān, and Madhu are mentioned.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 15. Bhāṅgāsvara, Sunītha, and Niṣadha, then Tviṣīratha; Karaṃdhama, Bāhlika, Sudyumna, Balavān, and Madhu are mentioned.

sanskrit: कपोतरोमा तृणकः सहदेवार्जुनौ तथा ।रामो दाशरथिश्चैव लक्ष्मणोऽथ प्रतर्दनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: kapotaromā tṛṇakaḥ sahadevārjunau tathā ,rāmo dāśarathiścaiva lakṣmaṇo'tha pratardanaḥ.
english: Kapotaroma, Tṛṇaka, Sahadeva and Arjuna, and also Rāma, Dāśarathi, and Lakṣmaṇa, then Pratardana are mentioned.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 16. Kapotaroma, Tṛṇaka, Sahadeva and Arjuna, and also Rāma, Dāśarathi, and Lakṣmaṇa, then Pratardana are mentioned.

sanskrit: अलर्कः कक्षसेनश्च गयो गौराश्व एव च ।जामदग्न्योऽथ रामोऽत्र नाभागसगरौ तथा ॥१७॥
transliteration: alarkaḥ kakṣasenaśca gayo gaurāśva eva ca ,jāmadagnyo'tha rāmo'tra nābhāgasagarau tathā.
english: Alarka, and Kakṣasena, Gaya, and also Gaurāśva. Then Jāmadagnya and Rāma are mentioned here, as well as Nābhāga and Sagara.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 17. Alarka, and Kakṣasena, Gaya, and also Gaurāśva. Then Jāmadagnya and Rāma are mentioned here, as well as Nābhāga and Sagara.

sanskrit: भूरिद्युम्नो महाश्वश्च पृथ्वश्वो जनकस्तथा ।वैन्यो राजा वारिषेणः पुरुजो जनमेजयः ॥१८॥
transliteration: bhūridyumno mahāśvaśca pṛthvaśvo janakastathā ,vainyo rājā vāriṣeṇaḥ purujo janamejayaḥ.
english: Bhūridyumna, Mahāśva, Pṛthvaśva, and Janaka; Vainya, King Vāriṣeṇa, Puruja, and Janamejaya (are mentioned here).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 18. Bhūridyumna, Mahāśva, Pṛthvaśva, and Janaka; Vainya, King Vāriṣeṇa, Puruja, and Janamejaya (are mentioned here).

sanskrit: ब्रह्मदत्तस्त्रिगर्तश्च राजोपरिचरस्तथा ।इन्द्रद्युम्नो भीमजानुर्गयः पृष्ठो नयोऽनघः ॥१९॥
transliteration: brahmadattastrigartaśca rājoparicarastathā ,indradyumno bhīmajānurgayaḥ pṛṣṭho nayo'naghaḥ.
english: Brahmadatta, Trigarta, and King Uparicara; Indradyumna, Bhīmajānu, Gaya, Pṛṣṭha, Naya, and Anagha (are also listed).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 19. Brahmadatta, Trigarta, and King Uparicara; Indradyumna, Bhīmajānu, Gaya, Pṛṣṭha, Naya, and Anagha (are also listed).

sanskrit: पद्मोऽथ मुचुकुन्दश्च भूरिद्युम्नः प्रसेनजित् ।अरिष्टनेमिः प्रद्युम्नः पृथगश्वोऽजकस्तथा ॥२०॥
transliteration: padmo'tha mucukundaśca bhūridyumnaḥ prasenajit ,ariṣṭanemiḥ pradyumnaḥ pṛthagaśvo'jakastathā.
english: Padma, and then Mucukunda; Bhūridyumna, Prasenajit; Ariṣṭanemi, Pradyumna, Pṛthagaśva, and Ajaka (are also enumerated here).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 20. Padma, and then Mucukunda; Bhūridyumna, Prasenajit; Ariṣṭanemi, Pradyumna, Pṛthagaśva, and Ajaka (are also enumerated here).

sanskrit: शतं मत्स्या नृपतयः शतं नीपाः शतं हयाः ।धृतराष्ट्राश्चैकशतमशीतिर्जनमेजयाः ॥२१॥
transliteration: śataṁ matsyā nṛpatayaḥ śataṁ nīpāḥ śataṁ hayāḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭrāścaikaśatamaśītirjanamejayāḥ.
english: There were a hundred Matsya kings, a hundred Nipas, a hundred Hayas, a hundred Dhṛtarāṣṭras, and eighty Janamejayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 21. There were a hundred Matsya kings, a hundred Nipas, a hundred Hayas, a hundred Dhṛtarāṣṭras, and eighty Janamejayas.

sanskrit: शतं च ब्रह्मदत्तानामीरिणां वैरिणां शतम् ।शंतनुश्चैव राजर्षिः पाण्डुश्चैव पिता तव ॥२२॥
transliteration: śataṁ ca brahmadattānāmīriṇāṁ vairiṇāṁ śatam ,śaṁtanuścaiva rājarṣiḥ pāṇḍuścaiva pitā tava.
english: And there were a hundred Brahmasdattas, a hundred Īriṇas, and a hundred Vairinas. Also, the royal sage Śantanu and your father Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 22. And there were a hundred Brahmasdattas, a hundred Īriṇas, and a hundred Vairinas. Also, the royal sage Śantanu and your father Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: उशद्गवः शतरथो देवराजो जयद्रथः ।वृषादर्भिश्च राजर्षिर्धाम्ना सह समन्त्रिणा ॥२३॥
transliteration: uśadgavaḥ śataratho devarājo jayadrathaḥ ,vṛṣādarbhiśca rājarṣirdhāmnā saha samantriṇā.
english: Uśadgava, Śataratha, Devarāja, Jayadratha, and Vṛṣādarbhi, the royal sage, accompanied by Dhāman along with his minister.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 23. Uśadgava, Śataratha, Devarāja, Jayadratha, and Vṛṣādarbhi, the royal sage, accompanied by Dhāman along with his minister.

sanskrit: अथापरे सहस्राणि ये गताः शशबिन्दवः ।इष्ट्वाश्वमेधैर्बहुभिर्महद्भिर्भूरिदक्षिणैः ॥२४॥
transliteration: athāpare sahasrāṇi ye gatāḥ śaśabindavaḥ ,iṣṭvāśvamedhairbahubhirmahadbhirbhūridakṣiṇaiḥ.
english: Then, thousands of others, like Śaśabindu, attained [greatness] after performing many magnificent horse (Aśvamedha) Vedic rituals (yajña) that involved lavish donations.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 24. Then, thousands of others, like Śaśabindu, attained [greatness] after performing many magnificent horse (Aśvamedha) Vedic rituals (yajña) that involved lavish donations.

sanskrit: एते राजर्षयः पुण्याः कीर्तिमन्तो बहुश्रुताः ।तस्यां सभायां राजर्षे वैवस्वतमुपासते ॥२५॥
transliteration: ete rājarṣayaḥ puṇyāḥ kīrtimanto bahuśrutāḥ ,tasyāṁ sabhāyāṁ rājarṣe vaivasvatamupāsate.
english: O royal sage, these meritorious, renowned, and highly learned royal sages (rājarṣi) attend upon Yama, the son of Vivasvān, in that assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 25. O royal sage, these meritorious, renowned, and highly learned royal sages (rājarṣi) attend upon Yama, the son of Vivasvān, in that assembly.

sanskrit: अगस्त्योऽथ मतङ्गश्च कालो मृत्युस्तथैव च ।यज्वानश्चैव सिद्धाश्च ये च योगशरीरिणः ॥२६॥
transliteration: agastyo'tha mataṅgaśca kālo mṛtyustathaiva ca ,yajvānaścaiva siddhāśca ye ca yogaśarīriṇaḥ.
english: Agastya, Mataṅga, Kāla (Time), and Mṛtyu (Death) are there, as are sacrificers (yajvan), perfected beings (siddha), and those who possess bodies created through the power of yoga (yoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 26. Agastya, Mataṅga, Kāla (Time), and Mṛtyu (Death) are there, as are sacrificers (yajvan), perfected beings (siddha), and those who possess bodies created through the power of yoga (yoga).

sanskrit: अग्निष्वात्ताश्च पितरः फेनपाश्चोष्मपाश्च ये ।स्वधावन्तो बर्हिषदो मूर्तिमन्तस्तथापरे ॥२७॥
transliteration: agniṣvāttāśca pitaraḥ phenapāścoṣmapāśca ye ,svadhāvanto barhiṣado mūrtimantastathāpare.
english: There are ancestors (Pitṛs) such as the Agniṣvāttas, the foam-drinkers, and the vapor-drinkers. Similarly, there are others who partake of the svadhā offerings and those who sit on the sacrificial grass; all these are embodied.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 27. There are ancestors (Pitṛs) such as the Agniṣvāttas, the foam-drinkers, and the vapor-drinkers. Similarly, there are others who partake of the svadhā offerings and those who sit on the sacrificial grass; all these are embodied.

sanskrit: कालचक्रं च साक्षाच्च भगवान्हव्यवाहनः ।नरा दुष्कृतकर्माणो दक्षिणायनमृत्यवः ॥२८॥
transliteration: kālacakraṁ ca sākṣācca bhagavānhavyavāhanaḥ ,narā duṣkṛtakarmāṇo dakṣiṇāyanamṛtyavaḥ.
english: The wheel of time (kālacakra) and the divine lord Agni, who directly carries oblations, are present. Additionally, wicked men who perform evil deeds (karma) meet their deaths during the southern course of the sun (dakṣiṇāyana).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 28. The wheel of time (kālacakra) and the divine lord Agni, who directly carries oblations, are present. Additionally, wicked men who perform evil deeds (karma) meet their deaths during the southern course of the sun (dakṣiṇāyana).

sanskrit: कालस्य नयने युक्ता यमस्य पुरुषाश्च ये ।तस्यां शिंशपपालाशास्तथा काशकुशादयः ।उपासते धर्मराजं मूर्तिमन्तो निरामयाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: kālasya nayane yuktā yamasya puruṣāśca ye ,tasyāṁ śiṁśapapālāśāstathā kāśakuśādayaḥ ,upāsate dharmarājaṁ mūrtimanto nirāmayāḥ.
english: And those who are engaged in the governance of time, along with the servants of Yama, and similarly, the śiṃśapā and palāśa trees, as well as kāśa grass, kuśa grass, and others  -  all these, being embodied and free from illness, serve Dharmarāja (Yama, the king of natural law).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 29. And those who are engaged in the governance of time, along with the servants of Yama, and similarly, the śiṃśapā and palāśa trees, as well as kāśa grass, kuśa grass, and others  -  all these, being embodied and free from illness, serve Dharmarāja (Yama, the king of natural law).

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवः पितृराजसभासदः ।अशक्याः परिसंख्यातुं नामभिः कर्मभिस्तथा ॥३०॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavaḥ pitṛrājasabhāsadaḥ ,aśakyāḥ parisaṁkhyātuṁ nāmabhiḥ karmabhistathā.
english: These and many other members of the assembly of Yama, the King of the Ancestors, are impossible to fully enumerate by their names or by their actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 30. These and many other members of the assembly of Yama, the King of the Ancestors, are impossible to fully enumerate by their names or by their actions (karma).

sanskrit: असंबाधा हि सा पार्थ रम्या कामगमा सभा ।दीर्घकालं तपस्तप्त्वा निर्मिता विश्वकर्मणा ॥३१॥
transliteration: asaṁbādhā hi sā pārtha ramyā kāmagamā sabhā ,dīrghakālaṁ tapastaptvā nirmitā viśvakarmaṇā.
english: Indeed, O Pārtha, that unobstructed, beautiful assembly hall, which can go anywhere at will, was constructed by Viśvakarma after he had performed arduous austerities (tapas) for a long time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 31. Indeed, O Pārtha, that unobstructed, beautiful assembly hall, which can go anywhere at will, was constructed by Viśvakarma after he had performed arduous austerities (tapas) for a long time.

sanskrit: प्रभासन्ती ज्वलन्तीव तेजसा स्वेन भारत ।तामुग्रतपसो यान्ति सुव्रताः सत्यवादिनः ॥३२॥
transliteration: prabhāsantī jvalantīva tejasā svena bhārata ,tāmugratapaso yānti suvratāḥ satyavādinaḥ.
english: Shining and as if blazing with its own brilliance, O Bhārata, to that assembly go those who have performed severe austerities (tapas), who keep good vows, and who speak the truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 32. Shining and as if blazing with its own brilliance, O Bhārata, to that assembly go those who have performed severe austerities (tapas), who keep good vows, and who speak the truth.

sanskrit: शान्ताः संन्यासिनः सिद्धाः पूताः पुण्येन कर्मणा ।सर्वे भास्वरदेहाश्च सर्वे च विरजोम्बराः ॥३३॥
transliteration: śāntāḥ saṁnyāsinaḥ siddhāḥ pūtāḥ puṇyena karmaṇā ,sarve bhāsvaradehāśca sarve ca virajombarāḥ.
english: The serene renunciates (saṃnyāsinaḥ) and perfected beings (siddhāḥ), purified by their virtuous actions (karma), all possessed radiant bodies and wore spotless garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 33. The serene renunciates (saṃnyāsinaḥ) and perfected beings (siddhāḥ), purified by their virtuous actions (karma), all possessed radiant bodies and wore spotless garments.

sanskrit: चित्राङ्गदाश्चित्रमाल्याः सर्वे ज्वलितकुण्डलाः ।सुकृतैः कर्मभिः पुण्यैः परिबर्हैर्विभूषिताः ॥३४॥
transliteration: citrāṅgadāścitramālyāḥ sarve jvalitakuṇḍalāḥ ,sukṛtaiḥ karmabhiḥ puṇyaiḥ paribarhairvibhūṣitāḥ.
english: All of them wore colorful armlets, variegated garlands, and shining earrings. They were adorned by their good and meritorious deeds (karma), which served as their ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 34. All of them wore colorful armlets, variegated garlands, and shining earrings. They were adorned by their good and meritorious deeds (karma), which served as their ornaments.

sanskrit: गन्धर्वाश्च महात्मानः शतशश्चाप्सरोगणाः ।वादित्रं नृत्तगीतं च हास्यं लास्यं च सर्वशः ॥३५॥
transliteration: gandharvāśca mahātmānaḥ śataśaścāpsarogaṇāḥ ,vāditraṁ nṛttagītaṁ ca hāsyaṁ lāsyaṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: There were also hundreds of Gandharvas (gandharvāḥ), great-souled beings, and hosts of Apsaras. Everywhere there was music from instruments, dance and song, laughter, and graceful dancing.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 35. There were also hundreds of Gandharvas (gandharvāḥ), great-souled beings, and hosts of Apsaras. Everywhere there was music from instruments, dance and song, laughter, and graceful dancing.

sanskrit: पुण्याश्च गन्धाः शब्दाश्च तस्यां पार्थ समन्ततः ।दिव्यानि माल्यानि च तामुपतिष्ठन्ति सर्वशः ॥३६॥
transliteration: puṇyāśca gandhāḥ śabdāśca tasyāṁ pārtha samantataḥ ,divyāni mālyāni ca tāmupatiṣṭhanti sarvaśaḥ.
english: O Partha, auspicious fragrances and sounds are present everywhere in that assembly. Furthermore, divine garlands attend to it from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 36. O Partha, auspicious fragrances and sounds are present everywhere in that assembly. Furthermore, divine garlands attend to it from all directions.

sanskrit: शतं शतसहस्राणि धर्मिणां तं प्रजेश्वरम् ।उपासते महात्मानं रूपयुक्ता मनस्विनः ॥३७॥
transliteration: śataṁ śatasahasrāṇi dharmiṇāṁ taṁ prajeśvaram ,upāsate mahātmānaṁ rūpayuktā manasvinaḥ.
english: Ten million individuals devoted to natural law (dharma), endowed with beautiful forms and noble minds, worship that great-souled (mahātman) lord of creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 37. Ten million individuals devoted to natural law (dharma), endowed with beautiful forms and noble minds, worship that great-souled (mahātman) lord of creatures.

sanskrit: ईदृशी सा सभा राजन्पितृराज्ञो महात्मनः ।वरुणस्यापि वक्ष्यामि सभां पुष्करमालिनीम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: īdṛśī sā sabhā rājanpitṛrājño mahātmanaḥ ,varuṇasyāpi vakṣyāmi sabhāṁ puṣkaramālinīm.
english: O King, that is the nature of the assembly belonging to the great-souled (mahātman) king of the ancestors. I will now also describe the assembly of Varuna, which is adorned with lotus garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 8 Verse 38. O King, that is the nature of the assembly belonging to the great-souled (mahātman) king of the ancestors. I will now also describe the assembly of Varuna, which is adorned with lotus garlands.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।युधिष्ठिर सभा दिव्या वरुणस्य सितप्रभा ।प्रमाणेन यथा याम्या शुभप्राकारतोरणा ॥१॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhira sabhā divyā varuṇasya sitaprabhā ,pramāṇena yathā yāmyā śubhaprākāratoraṇā.
english: Nārada said: "O Yudhiṣṭhira, Varuṇa's divine assembly hall, shining white, is equal in measure to that of Yama and possesses auspicious walls and gateways."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 1. Nārada said: "O Yudhiṣṭhira, Varuṇa's divine assembly hall, shining white, is equal in measure to that of Yama and possesses auspicious walls and gateways."

sanskrit: अन्तःसलिलमास्थाय विहिता विश्वकर्मणा ।दिव्यरत्नमयैर्वृक्षैः फलपुष्पप्रदैर्युता ॥२॥
transliteration: antaḥsalilamāsthāya vihitā viśvakarmaṇā ,divyaratnamayairvṛkṣaiḥ phalapuṣpapradairyutā.
english: It was constructed by Viśvakarmā, situated within the water, and adorned with trees made of divine jewels that provide fruits and flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 2. It was constructed by Viśvakarmā, situated within the water, and adorned with trees made of divine jewels that provide fruits and flowers.

sanskrit: नीलपीतासितश्यामैः सितैर्लोहितकैरपि ।अवतानैस्तथा गुल्मैः पुष्पमञ्जरिधारिभिः ॥३॥
transliteration: nīlapītāsitaśyāmaiḥ sitairlohitakairapi ,avatānaistathā gulmaiḥ puṣpamañjaridhāribhiḥ.
english: It is also adorned with blue, yellow, black, dark, white, and red widespread creepers and bushes, all bearing clusters of flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 3. It is also adorned with blue, yellow, black, dark, white, and red widespread creepers and bushes, all bearing clusters of flowers.

sanskrit: तथा शकुनयस्तस्यां नानारूपा मृदुस्वराः ।अनिर्देश्या वपुष्मन्तः शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥४॥
transliteration: tathā śakunayastasyāṁ nānārūpā mṛdusvarāḥ ,anirdeśyā vapuṣmantaḥ śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: Similarly, in that assembly hall, there were hundreds and thousands of birds of various forms, with soft voices, indescribably beautiful, and possessing splendid bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 4. Similarly, in that assembly hall, there were hundreds and thousands of birds of various forms, with soft voices, indescribably beautiful, and possessing splendid bodies.

sanskrit: सा सभा सुखसंस्पर्शा न शीता न च घर्मदा ।वेश्मासनवती रम्या सिता वरुणपालिता ॥५॥
transliteration: sā sabhā sukhasaṁsparśā na śītā na ca gharmadā ,veśmāsanavatī ramyā sitā varuṇapālitā.
english: That assembly hall was pleasant to the touch, neither cold nor oppressive with heat. It was charming, white, filled with dwellings and seats, and protected by Varuṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 5. That assembly hall was pleasant to the touch, neither cold nor oppressive with heat. It was charming, white, filled with dwellings and seats, and protected by Varuṇa.

sanskrit: यस्यामास्ते स वरुणो वारुण्या सह भारत ।दिव्यरत्नाम्बरधरो भूषणैरुपशोभितः ॥६॥
transliteration: yasyāmāste sa varuṇo vāruṇyā saha bhārata ,divyaratnāmbaradharo bhūṣaṇairupaśobhitaḥ.
english: In that (hall), O Bhārata, Varuṇa himself sits with Varuṇī, wearing divine jewels and garments, and splendidly adorned with ornaments.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 6. In that (hall), O Bhārata, Varuṇa himself sits with Varuṇī, wearing divine jewels and garments, and splendidly adorned with ornaments.

sanskrit: स्रग्विणो भूषिताश्चापि दिव्यमाल्यानुकर्षिणः ।आदित्यास्तत्र वरुणं जलेश्वरमुपासते ॥७॥
transliteration: sragviṇo bhūṣitāścāpi divyamālyānukarṣiṇaḥ ,ādityāstatra varuṇaṁ jaleśvaramupāsate.
english: There, the Adityas, garlanded, adorned, and bearing divine garlands, worship Varuna, the lord of waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 7. There, the Adityas, garlanded, adorned, and bearing divine garlands, worship Varuna, the lord of waters.

sanskrit: वासुकिस्तक्षकश्चैव नागश्चैरावतस्तथा ।कृष्णश्च लोहितश्चैव पद्मश्चित्रश्च वीर्यवान् ॥८॥
transliteration: vāsukistakṣakaścaiva nāgaścairāvatastathā ,kṛṣṇaśca lohitaścaiva padmaścitraśca vīryavān.
english: Vasuki, Takshaka, and also the naga Airavata, as well as Krishna, Lohita, Padma, and the powerful Chitra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 8. Vasuki, Takshaka, and also the naga Airavata, as well as Krishna, Lohita, Padma, and the powerful Chitra.

sanskrit: कम्बलाश्वतरौ नागौ धृतराष्ट्रबलाहकौ ।मणिमान्कुण्डलधरः कर्कोटकधनंजयौ ॥९॥
transliteration: kambalāśvatarau nāgau dhṛtarāṣṭrabalāhakau ,maṇimānkuṇḍaladharaḥ karkoṭakadhanaṁjayau.
english: The two nagas Kambala and Ashvatara; Dhritarashtra and Balahaka; Maniman, Kundaladhara; and Karkotaka and Dhananjaya (are also present).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 9. The two nagas Kambala and Ashvatara; Dhritarashtra and Balahaka; Maniman, Kundaladhara; and Karkotaka and Dhananjaya (are also present).

sanskrit: प्रह्लादो मूषिकादश्च तथैव जनमेजयः ।पताकिनो मण्डलिनः फणवन्तश्च सर्वशः ॥१०॥
transliteration: prahlādo mūṣikādaśca tathaiva janamejayaḥ ,patākino maṇḍalinaḥ phaṇavantaśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Prahlāda, Mūṣikāda, and likewise Janamejaya – all of them are present, distinguished by their banners, their coiled bodies, and their hoods.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 10. Prahlāda, Mūṣikāda, and likewise Janamejaya – all of them are present, distinguished by their banners, their coiled bodies, and their hoods.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवः सर्पास्तस्यां युधिष्ठिर ।उपासते महात्मानं वरुणं विगतक्लमाः ॥११॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavaḥ sarpāstasyāṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,upāsate mahātmānaṁ varuṇaṁ vigataklamāḥ.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, these and many other serpents, free from weariness, attend upon the great-souled Varuṇa there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 11. O Yudhiṣṭhira, these and many other serpents, free from weariness, attend upon the great-souled Varuṇa there.

sanskrit: बलिर्वैरोचनो राजा नरकः पृथिवींजयः ।प्रह्लादो विप्रचित्तिश्च कालखञ्जाश्च सर्वशः ॥१२॥
transliteration: balirvairocano rājā narakaḥ pṛthivīṁjayaḥ ,prahlādo vipracittiśca kālakhañjāśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: King Bali Vairochana, Naraka, Pṛthivīṃjaya, Prahlāda, and Vipracitti, along with all the Kālakhañjas, are present.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 12. King Bali Vairochana, Naraka, Pṛthivīṃjaya, Prahlāda, and Vipracitti, along with all the Kālakhañjas, are present.

sanskrit: सुहनुर्दुर्मुखः शङ्खः सुमनाः सुमतिः स्वनः ।घटोदरो महापार्श्वः क्रथनः पिठरस्तथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: suhanurdurmukhaḥ śaṅkhaḥ sumanāḥ sumatiḥ svanaḥ ,ghaṭodaro mahāpārśvaḥ krathanaḥ piṭharastathā.
english: Suhanu, Durmukha, Śaṅkha, Sumanas, Sumati, Svana, Ghaṭodara, Mahāpārśva, Krathana, and Piṭhara.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 13. Suhanu, Durmukha, Śaṅkha, Sumanas, Sumati, Svana, Ghaṭodara, Mahāpārśva, Krathana, and Piṭhara.

sanskrit: विश्वरूपः सुरूपश्च विरूपोऽथ महाशिराः ।दशग्रीवश्च वाली च मेघवासा दशावरः ॥१४॥
transliteration: viśvarūpaḥ surūpaśca virūpo'tha mahāśirāḥ ,daśagrīvaśca vālī ca meghavāsā daśāvaraḥ.
english: Viśvarūpa, Surūpa, Virūpa, Mahāśiras, Daśagrīva, Vālī, Meghavāsa, and Daśāvara.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 14. Viśvarūpa, Surūpa, Virūpa, Mahāśiras, Daśagrīva, Vālī, Meghavāsa, and Daśāvara.

sanskrit: कैटभो विटटूतश्च संह्रादश्चेन्द्रतापनः ।दैत्यदानवसंघाश्च सर्वे रुचिरकुण्डलाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: kaiṭabho viṭaṭūtaśca saṁhrādaścendratāpanaḥ ,daityadānavasaṁghāśca sarve rucirakuṇḍalāḥ.
english: Kaiṭabha, Viṭatūta, Saṃhrāda, and Indratāpana; also, all the hosts of Daityas and Dānavas, wearing splendid earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 15. Kaiṭabha, Viṭatūta, Saṃhrāda, and Indratāpana; also, all the hosts of Daityas and Dānavas, wearing splendid earrings.

sanskrit: स्रग्विणो मौलिनः सर्वे तथा दिव्यपरिच्छदाः ।सर्वे लब्धवराः शूराः सर्वे विगतमृत्यवः ॥१६॥
transliteration: sragviṇo maulinaḥ sarve tathā divyaparicchadāḥ ,sarve labdhavarāḥ śūrāḥ sarve vigatamṛtyavaḥ.
english: All of them are adorned with garlands and crowns, dressed in divine attire, have received boons, are valiant, and have conquered death.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 16. All of them are adorned with garlands and crowns, dressed in divine attire, have received boons, are valiant, and have conquered death.

sanskrit: ते तस्यां वरुणं देवं धर्मपाशस्थिताः सदा ।उपासते महात्मानं सर्वे सुचरितव्रताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: te tasyāṁ varuṇaṁ devaṁ dharmapāśasthitāḥ sadā ,upāsate mahātmānaṁ sarve sucaritavratāḥ.
english: In that (realm), all of them, whose observances (vrata) are well-performed and who always abide by the natural law (dharma), worship the magnificent god Varuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 17. In that (realm), all of them, whose observances (vrata) are well-performed and who always abide by the natural law (dharma), worship the magnificent god Varuna.

sanskrit: तथा समुद्राश्चत्वारो नदी भागीरथी च या ।कालिन्दी विदिशा वेण्णा नर्मदा वेगवाहिनी ॥१८॥
transliteration: tathā samudrāścatvāro nadī bhāgīrathī ca yā ,kālindī vidiśā veṇṇā narmadā vegavāhinī.
english: Similarly, the four oceans, and the river Bhagirathi (Ganges), as well as the Kalindi (Yamuna), Vidisha, Venna, and the swift-flowing Narmada (are present).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 18. Similarly, the four oceans, and the river Bhagirathi (Ganges), as well as the Kalindi (Yamuna), Vidisha, Venna, and the swift-flowing Narmada (are present).

sanskrit: विपाशा च शतद्रुश्च चन्द्रभागा सरस्वती ।इरावती वितस्ता च सिन्धुर्देवनदस्तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: vipāśā ca śatadruśca candrabhāgā sarasvatī ,irāvatī vitastā ca sindhurdevanadastathā.
english: The Vipasha, the Shatadru, the Chandrabhaga, the Sarasvati, the Iravati, the Vitasta, and the Sindhu, as well as the Devanada (the divine river).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 19. The Vipasha, the Shatadru, the Chandrabhaga, the Sarasvati, the Iravati, the Vitasta, and the Sindhu, as well as the Devanada (the divine river).

sanskrit: गोदावरी कृष्णवेण्णा कावेरी च सरिद्वरा ।एताश्चान्याश्च सरितस्तीर्थानि च सरांसि च ॥२०॥
transliteration: godāvarī kṛṣṇaveṇṇā kāverī ca saridvarā ,etāścānyāśca saritastīrthāni ca sarāṁsi ca.
english: The Godavari, the Krishnavenna, and the Kaveri - preeminent among rivers - these and other rivers, as well as sacred bathing places and lakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 20. The Godavari, the Krishnavenna, and the Kaveri - preeminent among rivers - these and other rivers, as well as sacred bathing places and lakes.

sanskrit: कूपाश्च सप्रस्रवणा देहवन्तो युधिष्ठिर ।पल्वलानि तडागानि देहवन्त्यथ भारत ॥२१॥
transliteration: kūpāśca saprasravaṇā dehavanto yudhiṣṭhira ,palvalāni taḍāgāni dehavantyatha bhārata.
english: O Yudhishthira, wells and springs are embodied, and O Bharata, small ponds and large tanks also are embodied.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 21. O Yudhishthira, wells and springs are embodied, and O Bharata, small ponds and large tanks also are embodied.

sanskrit: दिशस्तथा मही चैव तथा सर्वे महीधराः ।उपासते महात्मानं सर्वे जलचरास्तथा ॥२२॥
transliteration: diśastathā mahī caiva tathā sarve mahīdharāḥ ,upāsate mahātmānaṁ sarve jalacarāstathā.
english: The directions, and also the Earth itself, and all the mountains, attend upon the great soul (mahātman). Similarly, all aquatic creatures do so as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 22. The directions, and also the Earth itself, and all the mountains, attend upon the great soul (mahātman). Similarly, all aquatic creatures do so as well.

sanskrit: गीतवादित्रवन्तश्च गन्धर्वाप्सरसां गणाः ।स्तुवन्तो वरुणं तस्यां सर्व एव समासते ॥२३॥
transliteration: gītavāditravantaśca gandharvāpsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ ,stuvanto varuṇaṁ tasyāṁ sarva eva samāsate.
english: Groups of Gandharvas and Apsaras, with their songs and musical instruments, all praising Varuna, are present there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 23. Groups of Gandharvas and Apsaras, with their songs and musical instruments, all praising Varuna, are present there.

sanskrit: महीधरा रत्नवन्तो रसा येषु प्रतिष्ठिताः ।सर्वे विग्रहवन्तस्ते तमीश्वरमुपासते ॥२४॥
transliteration: mahīdharā ratnavanto rasā yeṣu pratiṣṭhitāḥ ,sarve vigrahavantaste tamīśvaramupāsate.
english: The jewel-bearing mountains, within which the earth's vital essences are situated, all of them, possessing a physical form, worship that supreme Lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 24. The jewel-bearing mountains, within which the earth's vital essences are situated, all of them, possessing a physical form, worship that supreme Lord.

sanskrit: एषा मया संपतता वारुणी भरतर्षभ ।दृष्टपूर्वा सभा रम्या कुबेरस्य सभां शृणु ॥२५॥
transliteration: eṣā mayā saṁpatatā vāruṇī bharatarṣabha ,dṛṣṭapūrvā sabhā ramyā kuberasya sabhāṁ śṛṇu.
english: O bull among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), I have seen this beautiful assembly hall of Varuṇa while flying. Now, hear about Kubera's assembly hall.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 9 Verse 25. O bull among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), I have seen this beautiful assembly hall of Varuṇa while flying. Now, hear about Kubera's assembly hall.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।सभा वैश्रवणी राजञ्शतयोजनमायता ।विस्तीर्णा सप्ततिश्चैव योजनानि सितप्रभा ॥१॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,sabhā vaiśravaṇī rājañśatayojanamāyatā ,vistīrṇā saptatiścaiva yojanāni sitaprabhā.
english: Nārada said: 'O King, Vaiśravaṇa's (Kubera's) assembly hall is one hundred yojanas long and indeed seventy yojanas wide, shining with a white radiance.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 1. Nārada said: 'O King, Vaiśravaṇa's (Kubera's) assembly hall is one hundred yojanas long and indeed seventy yojanas wide, shining with a white radiance.'

sanskrit: तपसा निर्मिता राजन्स्वयं वैश्रवणेन सा ।शशिप्रभा खेचरीणां कैलासशिखरोपमा ॥२॥
transliteration: tapasā nirmitā rājansvayaṁ vaiśravaṇena sā ,śaśiprabhā khecarīṇāṁ kailāsaśikharopamā.
english: O King, that hall was constructed by Vaiśravaṇa himself through ascetic practice (tapas). It has the radiance of the moon and, for the sky-wanderers, it is like a peak of Mount Kailāsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 2. O King, that hall was constructed by Vaiśravaṇa himself through ascetic practice (tapas). It has the radiance of the moon and, for the sky-wanderers, it is like a peak of Mount Kailāsa.

sanskrit: गुह्यकैरुह्यमाना सा खे विषक्तेव दृश्यते ।दिव्या हेममयैरुच्चैः पादपैरुपशोभिता ॥३॥
transliteration: guhyakairuhyamānā sā khe viṣakteva dṛśyate ,divyā hemamayairuccaiḥ pādapairupaśobhitā.
english: That divine hall, being carried by the Guhyakas, appears suspended in the sky as if attached to it. It is splendidly adorned with tall, golden trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 3. That divine hall, being carried by the Guhyakas, appears suspended in the sky as if attached to it. It is splendidly adorned with tall, golden trees.

sanskrit: रश्मिवती भास्वरा च दिव्यगन्धा मनोरमा ।सिताभ्रशिखराकारा प्लवमानेव दृश्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: raśmivatī bhāsvarā ca divyagandhā manoramā ,sitābhraśikharākārā plavamāneva dṛśyate.
english: It appears radiant, brilliant, divinely fragrant, and charming, resembling the peaks of white clouds, as if floating.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 4. It appears radiant, brilliant, divinely fragrant, and charming, resembling the peaks of white clouds, as if floating.

sanskrit: तस्यां वैश्रवणो राजा विचित्राभरणाम्बरः ।स्त्रीसहस्रावृतः श्रीमानास्ते ज्वलितकुण्डलः ॥५॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ vaiśravaṇo rājā vicitrābharaṇāmbaraḥ ,strīsahasrāvṛtaḥ śrīmānāste jvalitakuṇḍalaḥ.
english: In that place, King Kubera (Vaiśravaṇa), splendid and adorned with various ornaments and garments, sits, surrounded by a thousand women and wearing gleaming earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 5. In that place, King Kubera (Vaiśravaṇa), splendid and adorned with various ornaments and garments, sits, surrounded by a thousand women and wearing gleaming earrings.

sanskrit: दिवाकरनिभे पुण्ये दिव्यास्तरणसंवृते ।दिव्यपादोपधाने च निषण्णः परमासने ॥६॥
transliteration: divākaranibhe puṇye divyāstaraṇasaṁvṛte ,divyapādopadhāne ca niṣaṇṇaḥ paramāsane.
english: He (Kubera) is seated on a supreme throne (āsana), which is sacred, radiant like the sun, covered with divine carpets, and furnished with divine footrests.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 6. He (Kubera) is seated on a supreme throne (āsana), which is sacred, radiant like the sun, covered with divine carpets, and furnished with divine footrests.

sanskrit: मन्दाराणामुदाराणां वनानि सुरभीणि च ।सौगन्धिकानां चादाय गन्धान्गन्धवहः शुचिः ॥७॥
transliteration: mandārāṇāmudārāṇāṁ vanāni surabhīṇi ca ,saugandhikānāṁ cādāya gandhāngandhavahaḥ śuciḥ.
english: The pure wind (gandhavaha), gathering the fragrances from the magnificent forests of Mandara trees and from the fragrant water lilies,
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 7. The pure wind (gandhavaha), gathering the fragrances from the magnificent forests of Mandara trees and from the fragrant water lilies,

sanskrit: नलिन्याश्चालकाख्यायाश्चन्दनानां वनस्य च ।मनोहृदयसंह्लादी वायुस्तमुपसेवते ॥८॥
transliteration: nalinyāścālakākhyāyāścandanānāṁ vanasya ca ,manohṛdayasaṁhlādī vāyustamupasevate.
english: And the wind (vāyu), which is delightful to the mind and heart, serves him (Kubera), having gathered fragrances also from the lotus pond (nalinī), from the one named Alakā, and from the sandalwood forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 8. And the wind (vāyu), which is delightful to the mind and heart, serves him (Kubera), having gathered fragrances also from the lotus pond (nalinī), from the one named Alakā, and from the sandalwood forest.

sanskrit: तत्र देवाः सगन्धर्वा गणैरप्सरसां वृताः ।दिव्यतानेन गीतानि गान्ति दिव्यानि भारत ॥९॥
transliteration: tatra devāḥ sagandharvā gaṇairapsarasāṁ vṛtāḥ ,divyatānena gītāni gānti divyāni bhārata.
english: There, gods along with Gandharvas, surrounded by groups of Apsaras, sing divine songs with divine melodies, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 9. There, gods along with Gandharvas, surrounded by groups of Apsaras, sing divine songs with divine melodies, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: मिश्रकेशी च रम्भा च चित्रसेना शुचिस्मिता ।चारुनेत्रा घृताची च मेनका पुञ्जिकस्थला ॥१०॥
transliteration: miśrakeśī ca rambhā ca citrasenā śucismitā ,cārunetrā ghṛtācī ca menakā puñjikasthalā.
english: Miśrakeśī, and Rambhā, and Citrasenā, Śucismitā, Cārunetrā, Ghṛtācī, and Menakā, Puñjikasthalā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 10. Miśrakeśī, and Rambhā, and Citrasenā, Śucismitā, Cārunetrā, Ghṛtācī, and Menakā, Puñjikasthalā.

sanskrit: विश्वाची सहजन्या च प्रम्लोचा उर्वशी इरा ।वर्गा च सौरभेयी च समीची बुद्बुदा लता ॥११॥
transliteration: viśvācī sahajanyā ca pramlocā urvaśī irā ,vargā ca saurabheyī ca samīcī budbudā latā.
english: Viśvācī, Sahajanyā, and Pramlocā, Urvaśī, Irā, Vargā, and Saurabheyī, and Samīcī, Budbudā, Latā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 11. Viśvācī, Sahajanyā, and Pramlocā, Urvaśī, Irā, Vargā, and Saurabheyī, and Samīcī, Budbudā, Latā.

sanskrit: एताः सहस्रशश्चान्या नृत्तगीतविशारदाः ।उपतिष्ठन्ति धनदं पाण्डवाप्सरसां गणाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: etāḥ sahasraśaścānyā nṛttagītaviśāradāḥ ,upatiṣṭhanti dhanadaṁ pāṇḍavāpsarasāṁ gaṇāḥ.
english: These, and thousands of other groups of Apsaras, O Pāṇḍava, skilled in dance and song, attend upon Kubera (dhanada).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 12. These, and thousands of other groups of Apsaras, O Pāṇḍava, skilled in dance and song, attend upon Kubera (dhanada).

sanskrit: अनिशं दिव्यवादित्रैर्नृत्तैर्गीतैश्च सा सभा ।अशून्या रुचिरा भाति गन्धर्वाप्सरसां गणैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: aniśaṁ divyavāditrairnṛttairgītaiśca sā sabhā ,aśūnyā rucirā bhāti gandharvāpsarasāṁ gaṇaiḥ.
english: That assembly hall constantly shines, beautiful and full with divine musical instruments, dances, songs, and groups of Gandharvas and Apsaras.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 13. That assembly hall constantly shines, beautiful and full with divine musical instruments, dances, songs, and groups of Gandharvas and Apsaras.

sanskrit: किंनरा नाम गन्धर्वा नरा नाम तथापरे ।मणिभद्रोऽथ धनदः श्वेतभद्रश्च गुह्यकः ॥१४॥
transliteration: kiṁnarā nāma gandharvā narā nāma tathāpare ,maṇibhadro'tha dhanadaḥ śvetabhadraśca guhyakaḥ.
english: Among them are the Kinnaras, a type of Gandharva, and others known as Naras. Also present are Maṇibhadra, then Dhanada, and Śvetabhadra, who is a Guhyaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 14. Among them are the Kinnaras, a type of Gandharva, and others known as Naras. Also present are Maṇibhadra, then Dhanada, and Śvetabhadra, who is a Guhyaka.

sanskrit: कशेरको गण्डकण्डुः प्रद्योतश्च महाबलः ।कुस्तुम्बुरुः पिशाचश्च गजकर्णो विशालकः ॥१५॥
transliteration: kaśerako gaṇḍakaṇḍuḥ pradyotaśca mahābalaḥ ,kustumburuḥ piśācaśca gajakarṇo viśālakaḥ.
english: Kaśeraka, Gaṇḍakaṇḍu, and Pradyota, the mighty one (mahābala). Kustumburu, and Piśāca, Gajakarṇa, and Viśālaka are also present.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 15. Kaśeraka, Gaṇḍakaṇḍu, and Pradyota, the mighty one (mahābala). Kustumburu, and Piśāca, Gajakarṇa, and Viśālaka are also present.

sanskrit: वराहकर्णः सान्द्रोष्ठः फलभक्षः फलोदकः ।अङ्गचूडः शिखावर्तो हेमनेत्रो विभीषणः ॥१६॥
transliteration: varāhakarṇaḥ sāndroṣṭhaḥ phalabhakṣaḥ phalodakaḥ ,aṅgacūḍaḥ śikhāvarto hemanetro vibhīṣaṇaḥ.
english: Varāhakarṇa, Sāndroṣṭha, Phalabhakṣa, and Phalodaka. Aṅgacūḍa, Śikhāvarta, Hemanetra, and Vibhīṣaṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 16. Varāhakarṇa, Sāndroṣṭha, Phalabhakṣa, and Phalodaka. Aṅgacūḍa, Śikhāvarta, Hemanetra, and Vibhīṣaṇa.

sanskrit: पुष्पाननः पिङ्गलकः शोणितोदः प्रवालकः ।वृक्षवास्यनिकेतश्च चीरवासाश्च भारत ॥१७॥
transliteration: puṣpānanaḥ piṅgalakaḥ śoṇitodaḥ pravālakaḥ ,vṛkṣavāsyaniketaśca cīravāsāśca bhārata.
english: Puṣpānana, Piṅgalaka, Śoṇitoda, and Pravālaka; also Vṛkṣavāsiniketa and Cīravāsas, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 17. Puṣpānana, Piṅgalaka, Śoṇitoda, and Pravālaka; also Vṛkṣavāsiniketa and Cīravāsas, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो यक्षाः शतसहस्रशः ।सदा भगवती च श्रीस्तथैव नलकूबरः ॥१८॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo yakṣāḥ śatasahasraśaḥ ,sadā bhagavatī ca śrīstathaiva nalakūbaraḥ.
english: These, along with many hundreds of thousands of other Yakṣas, are present. Also always present are the venerable (bhagavatī) Śrī and Nalakūbara.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 18. These, along with many hundreds of thousands of other Yakṣas, are present. Also always present are the venerable (bhagavatī) Śrī and Nalakūbara.

sanskrit: अहं च बहुशस्तस्यां भवन्त्यन्ये च मद्विधाः ।आचार्याश्चाभवंस्तत्र तथा देवर्षयोऽपरे ॥१९॥
transliteration: ahaṁ ca bahuśastasyāṁ bhavantyanye ca madvidhāḥ ,ācāryāścābhavaṁstatra tathā devarṣayo'pare.
english: I, and many others like me, frequently reside in that assembly. Similarly, other preceptors and divine sages were also present there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 19. I, and many others like me, frequently reside in that assembly. Similarly, other preceptors and divine sages were also present there.

sanskrit: भगवान्भूतसंघैश्च वृतः शतसहस्रशः ।उमापतिः पशुपतिः शूलधृग्भगनेत्रहा ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhagavānbhūtasaṁghaiśca vṛtaḥ śatasahasraśaḥ ,umāpatiḥ paśupatiḥ śūladhṛgbhaganetrahā.
english: The revered Lord, the husband of Umā (Umāpati), the Lord of beings (Paśupati), the trident-bearer, and the destroyer of Bhaga's eye, was surrounded by hundreds of thousands of hosts of spirits (bhūta).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 20. The revered Lord, the husband of Umā (Umāpati), the Lord of beings (Paśupati), the trident-bearer, and the destroyer of Bhaga's eye, was surrounded by hundreds of thousands of hosts of spirits (bhūta).

sanskrit: त्र्यम्बको राजशार्दूल देवी च विगतक्लमा ।वामनैर्विकटैः कुब्जैः क्षतजाक्षैर्मनोजवैः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tryambako rājaśārdūla devī ca vigataklamā ,vāmanairvikaṭaiḥ kubjaiḥ kṣatajākṣairmanojavaiḥ.
english: O tiger among kings, there was also the three-eyed Lord (Treyambaka), and the goddess (Devī) free from fatigue. [He was accompanied] by dwarfs, by hideous and hunchbacked beings, by those with bloodshot eyes, and by those swift as thought.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 21. O tiger among kings, there was also the three-eyed Lord (Treyambaka), and the goddess (Devī) free from fatigue. [He was accompanied] by dwarfs, by hideous and hunchbacked beings, by those with bloodshot eyes, and by those swift as thought.

sanskrit: मांसमेदोवसाहारैरुग्रश्रवणदर्शनैः ।नानाप्रहरणैर्घोरैर्वातैरिव महाजवैः ।वृतः सखायमन्वास्ते सदैव धनदं नृप ॥२२॥
transliteration: māṁsamedovasāhārairugraśravaṇadarśanaiḥ ,nānāpraharaṇairghorairvātairiva mahājavaiḥ ,vṛtaḥ sakhāyamanvāste sadaiva dhanadaṁ nṛpa.
english: O king, surrounded by formidable beings whose diet consists of flesh, fat, and marrow, whose hearing and sight are dreadful, who wield various weapons, and who are as swift as mighty winds, he (Shiva) always sits near his friend Kubera, the giver of wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 22. O king, surrounded by formidable beings whose diet consists of flesh, fat, and marrow, whose hearing and sight are dreadful, who wield various weapons, and who are as swift as mighty winds, he (Shiva) always sits near his friend Kubera, the giver of wealth.

sanskrit: सा सभा तादृशी राजन्मया दृष्टान्तरिक्षगा ।पितामहसभां राजन्कथयिष्ये गतक्लमाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: sā sabhā tādṛśī rājanmayā dṛṣṭāntarikṣagā ,pitāmahasabhāṁ rājankathayiṣye gataklamām.
english: O king, that assembly (of Shiva), such as it was, moving in space, was seen by me. O king, I shall now narrate [the description of] the assembly of the Grand-Sire (Brahmā), which is free from fatigue.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 10 Verse 23. O king, that assembly (of Shiva), such as it was, moving in space, was seen by me. O king, I shall now narrate [the description of] the assembly of the Grand-Sire (Brahmā), which is free from fatigue.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।पुरा देवयुगे राजन्नादित्यो भगवान्दिवः ।आगच्छन्मानुषं लोकं दिदृक्षुर्विगतक्लमः ॥१॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,purā devayuge rājannādityo bhagavāndivaḥ ,āgacchanmānuṣaṁ lokaṁ didṛkṣurvigataklamaḥ.
english: Nārada said: O King, long ago, in a divine age, the revered Sun god descended from heaven to the human world, desiring to see it and free from weariness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 1. Nārada said: O King, long ago, in a divine age, the revered Sun god descended from heaven to the human world, desiring to see it and free from weariness.

sanskrit: चरन्मानुषरूपेण सभां दृष्ट्वा स्वयंभुवः ।सभामकथयन्मह्यं ब्राह्मीं तत्त्वेन पाण्डव ॥२॥
transliteration: caranmānuṣarūpeṇa sabhāṁ dṛṣṭvā svayaṁbhuvaḥ ,sabhāmakathayanmahyaṁ brāhmīṁ tattvena pāṇḍava.
english: O Pāṇḍava, wandering in a human form, he saw the assembly of the self-born Brahmā. Then he truly described that divine assembly to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 2. O Pāṇḍava, wandering in a human form, he saw the assembly of the self-born Brahmā. Then he truly described that divine assembly to me.

sanskrit: अप्रमेयप्रभां दिव्यां मानसीं भरतर्षभ ।अनिर्देश्यां प्रभावेन सर्वभूतमनोरमाम् ॥३॥
transliteration: aprameyaprabhāṁ divyāṁ mānasīṁ bharatarṣabha ,anirdeśyāṁ prabhāvena sarvabhūtamanoramām.
english: O best among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), that assembly had immeasurable splendor, was divine, and was created by mind (mānasī). It was indescribable in its power and charming to all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 3. O best among the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), that assembly had immeasurable splendor, was divine, and was created by mind (mānasī). It was indescribable in its power and charming to all beings.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा गुणानहं तस्याः सभायाः पाण्डुनन्दन ।दर्शनेप्सुस्तथा राजन्नादित्यमहमब्रुवम् ॥४॥
transliteration: śrutvā guṇānahaṁ tasyāḥ sabhāyāḥ pāṇḍunandana ,darśanepsustathā rājannādityamahamabruvam.
english: O son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍunandana), O King, after hearing the qualities of that assembly, I, desiring to see it, then spoke to the Sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 4. O son of Pāṇḍu (pāṇḍunandana), O King, after hearing the qualities of that assembly, I, desiring to see it, then spoke to the Sun.

sanskrit: भगवन्द्रष्टुमिच्छामि पितामहसभामहम् ।येन सा तपसा शक्या कर्मणा वापि गोपते ॥५॥
transliteration: bhagavandraṣṭumicchāmi pitāmahasabhāmaham ,yena sā tapasā śakyā karmaṇā vāpi gopate.
english: O revered one, I wish to see the assembly of the Grandfather (Brahmā). O Protector, by what (means), whether by asceticism (tapas) or by action (karma), can it be seen (or achieved)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 5. O revered one, I wish to see the assembly of the Grandfather (Brahmā). O Protector, by what (means), whether by asceticism (tapas) or by action (karma), can it be seen (or achieved)?

sanskrit: औषधैर्वा तथा युक्तैरुत वा मायया यया ।तन्ममाचक्ष्व भगवन्पश्येयं तां सभां कथम् ॥६॥
transliteration: auṣadhairvā tathā yuktairuta vā māyayā yayā ,tanmamācakṣva bhagavanpaśyeyaṁ tāṁ sabhāṁ katham.
english: O revered one, tell me how it was made - whether by properly combined medicines, or by some illusion (māyā). How may I see that assembly hall?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 6. O revered one, tell me how it was made - whether by properly combined medicines, or by some illusion (māyā). How may I see that assembly hall?

sanskrit: ततः स भगवान्सूर्यो मामुपादाय वीर्यवान् ।अगच्छत्तां सभां ब्राह्मीं विपापां विगतक्लमाम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa bhagavānsūryo māmupādāya vīryavān ,agacchattāṁ sabhāṁ brāhmīṁ vipāpāṁ vigataklamām.
english: Then, that revered and mighty Sun (Sūrya), taking me, went to that divine (brāhmī) assembly hall, which was free from impurities and weariness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 7. Then, that revered and mighty Sun (Sūrya), taking me, went to that divine (brāhmī) assembly hall, which was free from impurities and weariness.

sanskrit: एवंरूपेति सा शक्या न निर्देष्टुं जनाधिप ।क्षणेन हि बिभर्त्यन्यदनिर्देश्यं वपुस्तथा ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁrūpeti sā śakyā na nirdeṣṭuṁ janādhipa ,kṣaṇena hi bibhartyanyadanirdeśyaṁ vapustathā.
english: O king, it is not possible to describe it by saying, 'it is of such a form,' for in an instant it assumes another, equally indescribable, body.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 8. O king, it is not possible to describe it by saying, 'it is of such a form,' for in an instant it assumes another, equally indescribable, body.

sanskrit: न वेद परिमाणं वा संस्थानं वापि भारत ।न च रूपं मया तादृग्दृष्टपूर्वं कदाचन ॥९॥
transliteration: na veda parimāṇaṁ vā saṁsthānaṁ vāpi bhārata ,na ca rūpaṁ mayā tādṛgdṛṣṭapūrvaṁ kadācana.
english: O descendant of Bharata, no one knows its measure or its structure, nor have I ever seen such a form before.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 9. O descendant of Bharata, no one knows its measure or its structure, nor have I ever seen such a form before.

sanskrit: सुसुखा सा सभा राजन्न शीता न च घर्मदा ।न क्षुत्पिपासे न ग्लानिं प्राप्य तां प्राप्नुवन्त्युत ॥१०॥
transliteration: susukhā sā sabhā rājanna śītā na ca gharmadā ,na kṣutpipāse na glāniṁ prāpya tāṁ prāpnuvantyuta.
english: O king, that assembly hall is very pleasant; it is neither cold nor does it cause heat. Moreover, those who reach it do not experience hunger, thirst, or weariness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 10. O king, that assembly hall is very pleasant; it is neither cold nor does it cause heat. Moreover, those who reach it do not experience hunger, thirst, or weariness.

sanskrit: नानारूपैरिव कृता सुविचित्रैः सुभास्वरैः ।स्तम्भैर्न च धृता सा तु शाश्वती न च सा क्षरा ॥११॥
transliteration: nānārūpairiva kṛtā suvicitraiḥ subhāsvaraiḥ ,stambhairna ca dhṛtā sā tu śāśvatī na ca sā kṣarā.
english: That assembly hall appears as if made with various, very wonderful, and exceedingly radiant forms, yet it is not supported by pillars. Indeed, it is eternal, and it is not perishable.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 11. That assembly hall appears as if made with various, very wonderful, and exceedingly radiant forms, yet it is not supported by pillars. Indeed, it is eternal, and it is not perishable.

sanskrit: अति चन्द्रं च सूर्यं च शिखिनं च स्वयंप्रभा ।दीप्यते नाकपृष्ठस्था भासयन्तीव भास्करम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: ati candraṁ ca sūryaṁ ca śikhinaṁ ca svayaṁprabhā ,dīpyate nākapṛṣṭhasthā bhāsayantīva bhāskaram.
english: Self-luminous (svayamprabhā), it shines beyond the moon, the sun, and fire. Situated in the celestial sphere, it illuminates even the sun (bhāskara), as it were.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 12. Self-luminous (svayamprabhā), it shines beyond the moon, the sun, and fire. Situated in the celestial sphere, it illuminates even the sun (bhāskara), as it were.

sanskrit: तस्यां स भगवानास्ते विदधद्देवमायया ।स्वयमेकोऽनिशं राजँल्लोकाँल्लोकपितामहः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ sa bhagavānāste vidadhaddevamāyayā ,svayameko'niśaṁ rājaँllokāँllokapitāmahaḥ.
english: O king, in that assembly hall, the worshipful (bhagavān) grandfather of the worlds (lokapitāmahaḥ) himself, alone and constantly, sits performing (vidadhat) [his functions] through his divine creative power (māyā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 13. O king, in that assembly hall, the worshipful (bhagavān) grandfather of the worlds (lokapitāmahaḥ) himself, alone and constantly, sits performing (vidadhat) [his functions] through his divine creative power (māyā).

sanskrit: उपतिष्ठन्ति चाप्येनं प्रजानां पतयः प्रभुम् ।दक्षः प्रचेताः पुलहो मरीचिः कश्यपस्तथा ॥१४॥
transliteration: upatiṣṭhanti cāpyenaṁ prajānāṁ patayaḥ prabhum ,dakṣaḥ pracetāḥ pulaho marīciḥ kaśyapastathā.
english: And the lords of beings (prajā), such as Dakṣa, Pracetas, Pulaha, Marīci, and Kaśyapa, attend upon this Lord.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 14. And the lords of beings (prajā), such as Dakṣa, Pracetas, Pulaha, Marīci, and Kaśyapa, attend upon this Lord.

sanskrit: भृगुरत्रिर्वसिष्ठश्च गौतमश्च तथाङ्गिराः ।मनोऽन्तरिक्षं विद्याश्च वायुस्तेजो जलं मही ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhṛguratrirvasiṣṭhaśca gautamaśca tathāṅgirāḥ ,mano'ntarikṣaṁ vidyāśca vāyustejo jalaṁ mahī.
english: Bhṛgu, Atri, Vasiṣṭha, Gautama, and Aṅgirā; the mind (manas), space, knowledges, wind, light, water, and earth (mahī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 15. Bhṛgu, Atri, Vasiṣṭha, Gautama, and Aṅgirā; the mind (manas), space, knowledges, wind, light, water, and earth (mahī).

sanskrit: शब्दः स्पर्शस्तथा रूपं रसो गन्धश्च भारत ।प्रकृतिश्च विकारश्च यच्चान्यत्कारणं भुवः ॥१६॥
transliteration: śabdaḥ sparśastathā rūpaṁ raso gandhaśca bhārata ,prakṛtiśca vikāraśca yaccānyatkāraṇaṁ bhuvaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, sound, touch, form, taste, and smell, along with primordial nature (prakṛti) and its modifications, and whatever other causes of the earth (bhū) there may be.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 16. O Bhārata, sound, touch, form, taste, and smell, along with primordial nature (prakṛti) and its modifications, and whatever other causes of the earth (bhū) there may be.

sanskrit: चन्द्रमाः सह नक्षत्रैरादित्यश्च गभस्तिमान् ।वायवः क्रतवश्चैव संकल्पः प्राण एव च ॥१७॥
transliteration: candramāḥ saha nakṣatrairādityaśca gabhastimān ,vāyavaḥ kratavaścaiva saṁkalpaḥ prāṇa eva ca.
english: The moon with its constellations, and the radiant sun; the winds, and the rituals (kratu); and indeed, resolve (saṃkalpa) and vital breath (prāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 17. The moon with its constellations, and the radiant sun; the winds, and the rituals (kratu); and indeed, resolve (saṃkalpa) and vital breath (prāṇa).

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवः स्वयंभुवमुपस्थिताः ।अर्थो धर्मश्च कामश्च हर्षो द्वेषस्तपो दमः ॥१८॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavaḥ svayaṁbhuvamupasthitāḥ ,artho dharmaśca kāmaśca harṣo dveṣastapo damaḥ.
english: Many of these and other beings, along with material prosperity, natural law (dharma), desire, joy, hatred, asceticism (tapas), and self-control, approached the Self-existent (Brahmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 18. Many of these and other beings, along with material prosperity, natural law (dharma), desire, joy, hatred, asceticism (tapas), and self-control, approached the Self-existent (Brahmā).

sanskrit: आयान्ति तस्यां सहिता गन्धर्वाप्सरसस्तथा ।विंशतिः सप्त चैवान्ये लोकपालाश्च सर्वशः ॥१९॥
transliteration: āyānti tasyāṁ sahitā gandharvāpsarasastathā ,viṁśatiḥ sapta caivānye lokapālāśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: The Gandharvas and Apsarases arrive there together. Also, twenty-seven others, and all the world-protectors (lokapālas), entirely.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 19. The Gandharvas and Apsarases arrive there together. Also, twenty-seven others, and all the world-protectors (lokapālas), entirely.

sanskrit: शुक्रो बृहस्पतिश्चैव बुधोऽङ्गारक एव च ।शनैश्चरश्च राहुश्च ग्रहाः सर्वे तथैव च ॥२०॥
transliteration: śukro bṛhaspatiścaiva budho'ṅgāraka eva ca ,śanaiścaraśca rāhuśca grahāḥ sarve tathaiva ca.
english: Venus (Śukra), Jupiter (Bṛhaspati), Mercury (Budha), and Mars (Aṅgāraka); and also Saturn (Śanaiścara) and Rahu (Rāhu) – indeed all these planets too.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 20. Venus (Śukra), Jupiter (Bṛhaspati), Mercury (Budha), and Mars (Aṅgāraka); and also Saturn (Śanaiścara) and Rahu (Rāhu) – indeed all these planets too.

sanskrit: मन्त्रो रथंतरश्चैव हरिमान्वसुमानपि ।आदित्याः साधिराजानो नानाद्वंद्वैरुदाहृताः ॥२१॥
transliteration: mantro rathaṁtaraścaiva harimānvasumānapi ,ādityāḥ sādhirājāno nānādvaṁdvairudāhṛtāḥ.
english: The sacred chant (mantra) Rathantara, and also Harimān and Vasumān (Sāmans); and the Adityas, along with their rulers, are mentioned through various dualities.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 21. The sacred chant (mantra) Rathantara, and also Harimān and Vasumān (Sāmans); and the Adityas, along with their rulers, are mentioned through various dualities.

sanskrit: मरुतो विश्वकर्मा च वसवश्चैव भारत ।तथा पितृगणाः सर्वे सर्वाणि च हवींष्यथ ॥२२॥
transliteration: maruto viśvakarmā ca vasavaścaiva bhārata ,tathā pitṛgaṇāḥ sarve sarvāṇi ca havīṁṣyatha.
english: O Bhārata, the Maruts, Viśvakarmā, and the Vasus; similarly, all the groups of ancestors (pitṛs) and indeed all the offerings (havis).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 22. O Bhārata, the Maruts, Viśvakarmā, and the Vasus; similarly, all the groups of ancestors (pitṛs) and indeed all the offerings (havis).

sanskrit: ऋग्वेदः सामवेदश्च यजुर्वेदश्च पाण्डव ।अथर्ववेदश्च तथा पर्वाणि च विशां पते ॥२३॥
transliteration: ṛgvedaḥ sāmavedaśca yajurvedaśca pāṇḍava ,atharvavedaśca tathā parvāṇi ca viśāṁ pate.
english: O Pāṇḍava, the Ṛgveda, the Sāmaveda, and the Yajurveda; and similarly the Atharvaveda; and O lord of the people, the sections (parvan)...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 23. O Pāṇḍava, the Ṛgveda, the Sāmaveda, and the Yajurveda; and similarly the Atharvaveda; and O lord of the people, the sections (parvan)...

sanskrit: इतिहासोपवेदाश्च वेदाङ्गानि च सर्वशः ।ग्रहा यज्ञाश्च सोमश्च दैवतानि च सर्वशः ॥२४॥
transliteration: itihāsopavedāśca vedāṅgāni ca sarvaśaḥ ,grahā yajñāśca somaśca daivatāni ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: And the epics (itihāsa) and subsidiary Vedas (upaveda), and all the auxiliaries of the Vedas (vedāṅga) in every respect; the planets (graha), and the sacrificial rites (yajña), and Soma, and all the deities in every respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 24. And the epics (itihāsa) and subsidiary Vedas (upaveda), and all the auxiliaries of the Vedas (vedāṅga) in every respect; the planets (graha), and the sacrificial rites (yajña), and Soma, and all the deities in every respect.

sanskrit: सावित्री दुर्गतरणी वाणी सप्तविधा तथा ।मेधा धृतिः श्रुतिश्चैव प्रज्ञा बुद्धिर्यशः क्षमा ॥२५॥
transliteration: sāvitrī durgataraṇī vāṇī saptavidhā tathā ,medhā dhṛtiḥ śrutiścaiva prajñā buddhiryaśaḥ kṣamā.
english: Sāvitrī, the traverser of difficulties, and the sevenfold form of speech (vāṇī); similarly, wisdom (medhā), steadfastness (dhṛti), and indeed sacred tradition (śruti), insight (prajñā), intellect (buddhi), fame, and forgiveness (kṣamā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 25. Sāvitrī, the traverser of difficulties, and the sevenfold form of speech (vāṇī); similarly, wisdom (medhā), steadfastness (dhṛti), and indeed sacred tradition (śruti), insight (prajñā), intellect (buddhi), fame, and forgiveness (kṣamā).

sanskrit: सामानि स्तुतिशस्त्राणि गाथाश्च विविधास्तथा ।भाष्याणि तर्कयुक्तानि देहवन्ति विशां पते ॥२६॥
transliteration: sāmāni stutiśastrāṇi gāthāśca vividhāstathā ,bhāṣyāṇi tarkayuktāni dehavanti viśāṁ pate.
english: O Lord of the people, the Sāma hymns, the praises and invocations, and various songs, as well as the logical commentaries, all exist in embodied forms.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 26. O Lord of the people, the Sāma hymns, the praises and invocations, and various songs, as well as the logical commentaries, all exist in embodied forms.

sanskrit: क्षणा लवा मुहूर्ताश्च दिवा रात्रिस्तथैव च ।अर्धमासाश्च मासाश्च ऋतवः षट्च भारत ॥२७॥
transliteration: kṣaṇā lavā muhūrtāśca divā rātristathaiva ca ,ardhamāsāśca māsāśca ṛtavaḥ ṣaṭca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, moments, fractions of moments, and muhūrtas, as well as day and night, similarly half-months and months, and the six seasons, are all parts of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 27. O Bhārata, moments, fractions of moments, and muhūrtas, as well as day and night, similarly half-months and months, and the six seasons, are all parts of time.

sanskrit: संवत्सराः पञ्चयुगमहोरात्राश्चतुर्विधाः ।कालचक्रं च यद्दिव्यं नित्यमक्षयमव्ययम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: saṁvatsarāḥ pañcayugamahorātrāścaturvidhāḥ ,kālacakraṁ ca yaddivyaṁ nityamakṣayamavyayam.
english: The years, and the four kinds of days and nights that constitute a five-year yuga, and that divine wheel of time (kālacakra) which is eternal, undecaying, and imperishable.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 28. The years, and the four kinds of days and nights that constitute a five-year yuga, and that divine wheel of time (kālacakra) which is eternal, undecaying, and imperishable.

sanskrit: अदितिर्दितिर्दनुश्चैव सुरसा विनता इरा ।कालका सुरभिर्देवी सरमा चाथ गौतमी ॥२९॥
transliteration: aditirditirdanuścaiva surasā vinatā irā ,kālakā surabhirdevī saramā cātha gautamī.
english: Aditi, Diti, and Danu; likewise Surasā, Vinatā, Irā, Kālakā, the goddess Surabhi, then Saramā, and Gautamī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 29. Aditi, Diti, and Danu; likewise Surasā, Vinatā, Irā, Kālakā, the goddess Surabhi, then Saramā, and Gautamī.

sanskrit: आदित्या वसवो रुद्रा मरुतश्चाश्विनावपि ।विश्वेदेवाश्च साध्याश्च पितरश्च मनोजवाः ॥३०॥
transliteration: ādityā vasavo rudrā marutaścāśvināvapi ,viśvedevāśca sādhyāśca pitaraśca manojavāḥ.
english: The Ādityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, the Maruts, and also the Aśvinīs; likewise, the Viśvedevas, the Sādhyas, and the Pitṛs, who are swift as thought, [are present].
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 30. The Ādityas, the Vasus, the Rudras, the Maruts, and also the Aśvinīs; likewise, the Viśvedevas, the Sādhyas, and the Pitṛs, who are swift as thought, [are present].

sanskrit: राक्षसाश्च पिशाचाश्च दानवा गुह्यकास्तथा ।सुपर्णनागपशवः पितामहमुपासते ॥३१॥
transliteration: rākṣasāśca piśācāśca dānavā guhyakāstathā ,suparṇanāgapaśavaḥ pitāmahamupāsate.
english: The Rākṣasas, the Piśācas, the Dānavas, and the Guhyakas; likewise, the Suparṇas (Garudas), Nāgas (serpents), and animals - all worship the grandfather (Brahmā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 31. The Rākṣasas, the Piśācas, the Dānavas, and the Guhyakas; likewise, the Suparṇas (Garudas), Nāgas (serpents), and animals - all worship the grandfather (Brahmā).

sanskrit: देवो नारायणस्तस्यां तथा देवर्षयश्च ये ।ऋषयो वालखिल्याश्च योनिजायोनिजास्तथा ॥३२॥
transliteration: devo nārāyaṇastasyāṁ tathā devarṣayaśca ye ,ṛṣayo vālakhilyāśca yonijāyonijāstathā.
english: The divine Nārāyaṇa is in her (that entity/place), and those who are divine sages (devarṣis), and also the Vālakhilya sages; similarly, those born from an origin (yonijā) and those not born from an origin (ayonijā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 32. The divine Nārāyaṇa is in her (that entity/place), and those who are divine sages (devarṣis), and also the Vālakhilya sages; similarly, those born from an origin (yonijā) and those not born from an origin (ayonijā).

sanskrit: यच्च किंचित्त्रिलोकेऽस्मिन्दृश्यते स्थाणुजङ्गमम् ।सर्वं तस्यां मया दृष्टं तद्विद्धि मनुजाधिप ॥३३॥
transliteration: yacca kiṁcittriloke'smindṛśyate sthāṇujaṅgamam ,sarvaṁ tasyāṁ mayā dṛṣṭaṁ tadviddhi manujādhipa.
english: And whatever is seen in these three worlds (triloka), both immobile and mobile  -  all of that has been seen by me in her (that entity/place). Know this, O lord of men (manujādhipa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 33. And whatever is seen in these three worlds (triloka), both immobile and mobile  -  all of that has been seen by me in her (that entity/place). Know this, O lord of men (manujādhipa).

sanskrit: अष्टाशीतिसहस्राणि यतीनामूर्ध्वरेतसाम् ।प्रजावतां च पञ्चाशदृषीणामपि पाण्डव ॥३४॥
transliteration: aṣṭāśītisahasrāṇi yatīnāmūrdhvaretasām ,prajāvatāṁ ca pañcāśadṛṣīṇāmapi pāṇḍava.
english: O Pāṇḍava, there were eighty-eight thousand ascetics (yati) who observed celibacy, and also fifty sages (ṛṣi) who had progeny.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 34. O Pāṇḍava, there were eighty-eight thousand ascetics (yati) who observed celibacy, and also fifty sages (ṛṣi) who had progeny.

sanskrit: ते स्म तत्र यथाकामं दृष्ट्वा सर्वे दिवौकसः ।प्रणम्य शिरसा तस्मै प्रतियान्ति यथागतम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: te sma tatra yathākāmaṁ dṛṣṭvā sarve divaukasaḥ ,praṇamya śirasā tasmai pratiyānti yathāgatam.
english: All those celestial beings (divaukasaḥ), having seen him there, would bow their heads to him as they wished and then return the way they had come.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 35. All those celestial beings (divaukasaḥ), having seen him there, would bow their heads to him as they wished and then return the way they had come.

sanskrit: अतिथीनागतान्देवान्दैत्यान्नागान्मुनींस्तथा ।यक्षान्सुपर्णान्कालेयान्गन्धर्वाप्सरसस्तथा ॥३६॥
transliteration: atithīnāgatāndevāndaityānnāgānmunīṁstathā ,yakṣānsuparṇānkāleyāngandharvāpsarasastathā.
english: (Brahmā receives) guests (atithi) who have arrived, gods, Daitas, Nāgas, sages (muni), and likewise Yakṣas, Suparṇas, Kāleyas, and Gandharvas and Apsarases.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 36. (Brahmā receives) guests (atithi) who have arrived, gods, Daitas, Nāgas, sages (muni), and likewise Yakṣas, Suparṇas, Kāleyas, and Gandharvas and Apsarases.

sanskrit: महाभागानमितधीर्ब्रह्मा लोकपितामहः ।दयावान्सर्वभूतेषु यथार्हं प्रतिपद्यते ॥३७॥
transliteration: mahābhāgānamitadhīrbrahmā lokapitāmahaḥ ,dayāvānsarvabhūteṣu yathārhaṁ pratipadyate.
english: Brahmā, the grandfather of the worlds (lokapitāmaha), possessed of immeasurable intellect, and compassionate towards all beings, receives and treats those greatly revered ones (mahābhāgān) appropriately.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 37. Brahmā, the grandfather of the worlds (lokapitāmaha), possessed of immeasurable intellect, and compassionate towards all beings, receives and treats those greatly revered ones (mahābhāgān) appropriately.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य च विश्वात्मा स्वयंभूरमितप्रभः ।सान्त्वमानार्थसंभोगैर्युनक्ति मनुजाधिप ॥३८॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya ca viśvātmā svayaṁbhūramitaprabhaḥ ,sāntvamānārthasaṁbhogairyunakti manujādhipa.
english: O lord of men (manujādhipa), the Self-Existent (svayambhū), the all-pervading Spirit (viśvātmā), of immeasurable splendor (amitaprabha), having accepted them, engages them with soothing words (sāntva), honors (māna), wealth (artha), and enjoyments (saṃbhoga).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 38. O lord of men (manujādhipa), the Self-Existent (svayambhū), the all-pervading Spirit (viśvātmā), of immeasurable splendor (amitaprabha), having accepted them, engages them with soothing words (sāntva), honors (māna), wealth (artha), and enjoyments (saṃbhoga).

sanskrit: तथा तैरुपयातैश्च प्रतियातैश्च भारत ।आकुला सा सभा तात भवति स्म सुखप्रदा ॥३९॥
transliteration: tathā tairupayātaiśca pratiyātaiśca bhārata ,ākulā sā sabhā tāta bhavati sma sukhapradā.
english: Thus, O Bhārata (bhārata), O dear one (tāta), that assembly (sabhā) was bustling (ākulā) with those arriving (upayāta) and those departing (pratiyāta), and it was indeed comfort-giving (sukhapradā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 39. Thus, O Bhārata (bhārata), O dear one (tāta), that assembly (sabhā) was bustling (ākulā) with those arriving (upayāta) and those departing (pratiyāta), and it was indeed comfort-giving (sukhapradā).

sanskrit: सर्वतेजोमयी दिव्या ब्रह्मर्षिगणसेविता ।ब्राह्म्या श्रिया दीप्यमाना शुशुभे विगतक्लमा ॥४०॥
transliteration: sarvatejomayī divyā brahmarṣigaṇasevitā ,brāhmyā śriyā dīpyamānā śuśubhe vigataklamā.
english: That divine (divyā) assembly, full of all splendor (sarvatejomayī), served by hosts of Brāhmaṇa sages (brahmarṣigaṇasevitā), shone brightly (dīpyamānā) with a divine (brāhmyā) luster (śriyā), appearing tireless (vigataklamā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 40. That divine (divyā) assembly, full of all splendor (sarvatejomayī), served by hosts of Brāhmaṇa sages (brahmarṣigaṇasevitā), shone brightly (dīpyamānā) with a divine (brāhmyā) luster (śriyā), appearing tireless (vigataklamā).

sanskrit: सा सभा तादृशी दृष्टा सर्वलोकेषु दुर्लभा ।सभेयं राजशार्दूल मनुष्येषु यथा तव ॥४१॥
transliteration: sā sabhā tādṛśī dṛṣṭā sarvalokeṣu durlabhā ,sabheyaṁ rājaśārdūla manuṣyeṣu yathā tava.
english: That assembly (sabhā), as it was seen, was of such a kind (tādṛśī) that it is rare (durlabhā) in all the worlds (sarvalokeṣu). O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), this assembly (sabhā iyam) among humans (manuṣyeṣu) is just like yours (tava).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 41. That assembly (sabhā), as it was seen, was of such a kind (tādṛśī) that it is rare (durlabhā) in all the worlds (sarvalokeṣu). O tiger among kings (rājaśārdūla), this assembly (sabhā iyam) among humans (manuṣyeṣu) is just like yours (tava).

sanskrit: एता मया दृष्टपूर्वाः सभा देवेषु पाण्डव ।तवेयं मानुषे लोके सर्वश्रेष्ठतमा सभा ॥४२॥
transliteration: etā mayā dṛṣṭapūrvāḥ sabhā deveṣu pāṇḍava ,taveyaṁ mānuṣe loke sarvaśreṣṭhatamā sabhā.
english: O Pāṇḍava, I have seen such assemblies before among the gods. This assembly of yours in the human world is the most excellent of all.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 42. O Pāṇḍava, I have seen such assemblies before among the gods. This assembly of yours in the human world is the most excellent of all.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।प्रायशो राजलोकस्ते कथितो वदतां वर ।वैवस्वतसभायां तु यथा वदसि वै प्रभो ॥४३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,prāyaśo rājalokaste kathito vadatāṁ vara ,vaivasvatasabhāyāṁ tu yathā vadasi vai prabho.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O best among speakers, you have mostly described the assemblies of kings. But regarding the assembly of Vaivasvata (Yama), O lord, you speak truly.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 43. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O best among speakers, you have mostly described the assemblies of kings. But regarding the assembly of Vaivasvata (Yama), O lord, you speak truly.'

sanskrit: वरुणस्य सभायां तु नागास्ते कथिता विभो ।दैत्येन्द्राश्चैव भूयिष्ठाः सरितः सागरास्तथा ॥४४॥
transliteration: varuṇasya sabhāyāṁ tu nāgāste kathitā vibho ,daityendrāścaiva bhūyiṣṭhāḥ saritaḥ sāgarāstathā.
english: But in Varuṇa's assembly, O powerful one, the Nāgas were described by you, and also most of the chief Daityas, along with rivers and oceans.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 44. But in Varuṇa's assembly, O powerful one, the Nāgas were described by you, and also most of the chief Daityas, along with rivers and oceans.

sanskrit: तथा धनपतेर्यक्षा गुह्यका राक्षसास्तथा ।गन्धर्वाप्सरसश्चैव भगवांश्च वृषध्वजः ॥४५॥
transliteration: tathā dhanapateryakṣā guhyakā rākṣasāstathā ,gandharvāpsarasaścaiva bhagavāṁśca vṛṣadhvajaḥ.
english: Similarly, the Yakṣas of the god of wealth (Dhanapati), Guhyakas, and Rākṣasas (were described). And also Gandharvas and Apsarases, and the worshipful bull-bannered one (Śiva).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 45. Similarly, the Yakṣas of the god of wealth (Dhanapati), Guhyakas, and Rākṣasas (were described). And also Gandharvas and Apsarases, and the worshipful bull-bannered one (Śiva).

sanskrit: पितामहसभायां तु कथितास्ते महर्षयः ।सर्वदेवनिकायाश्च सर्वशास्त्राणि चैव हि ॥४६॥
transliteration: pitāmahasabhāyāṁ tu kathitāste maharṣayaḥ ,sarvadevanikāyāśca sarvaśāstrāṇi caiva hi.
english: Indeed, in the assembly of Brahmā, those great sages were mentioned, along with all groups of gods and all scriptures.
