search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 46. Indeed, in the assembly of Brahmā, those great sages were mentioned, along with all groups of gods and all scriptures.

sanskrit: शतक्रतुसभायां तु देवाः संकीर्तिता मुने ।उद्देशतश्च गन्धर्वा विविधाश्च महर्षयः ॥४७॥
transliteration: śatakratusabhāyāṁ tu devāḥ saṁkīrtitā mune ,uddeśataśca gandharvā vividhāśca maharṣayaḥ.
english: O sage, in the assembly of Śatakratu (Indra), the gods were enumerated, and various Gandharvas and great sages were also mentioned in summary.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 47. O sage, in the assembly of Śatakratu (Indra), the gods were enumerated, and various Gandharvas and great sages were also mentioned in summary.

sanskrit: एक एव तु राजर्षिर्हरिश्चन्द्रो महामुने ।कथितस्ते सभानित्यो देवेन्द्रस्य महात्मनः ॥४८॥
transliteration: eka eva tu rājarṣirhariścandro mahāmune ,kathitaste sabhānityo devendrasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: O great sage, Hariścandra was indeed the only royal sage mentioned to you as a constant member of the assembly of the great-souled lord of gods (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 48. O great sage, Hariścandra was indeed the only royal sage mentioned to you as a constant member of the assembly of the great-souled lord of gods (Indra).

sanskrit: किं कर्म तेनाचरितं तपो वा नियतव्रतम् ।येनासौ सह शक्रेण स्पर्धते स्म महायशाः ॥४९॥
transliteration: kiṁ karma tenācaritaṁ tapo vā niyatavratam ,yenāsau saha śakreṇa spardhate sma mahāyaśāḥ.
english: What action (karma) or austerity (tapas) with fixed vows did he perform, by which that greatly renowned one could rival Indra?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 49. What action (karma) or austerity (tapas) with fixed vows did he perform, by which that greatly renowned one could rival Indra?

sanskrit: पितृलोकगतश्चापि त्वया विप्र पिता मम ।दृष्टः पाण्डुर्महाभागः कथं चासि समागतः ॥५०॥
transliteration: pitṛlokagataścāpi tvayā vipra pitā mama ,dṛṣṭaḥ pāṇḍurmahābhāgaḥ kathaṁ cāsi samāgataḥ.
english: O Brahmin, my father Pāṇḍu, the highly fortunate one, who has gone to the realm of ancestors, was also seen by you. And how did you arrive?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 50. O Brahmin, my father Pāṇḍu, the highly fortunate one, who has gone to the realm of ancestors, was also seen by you. And how did you arrive?

sanskrit: किमुक्तवांश्च भगवन्नेतदिच्छामि वेदितुम् ।त्वत्तः श्रोतुमहं सर्वं परं कौतूहलं हि मे ॥५१॥
transliteration: kimuktavāṁśca bhagavannetadicchāmi veditum ,tvattaḥ śrotumahaṁ sarvaṁ paraṁ kautūhalaṁ hi me.
english: And what did he say, O reverent one (bhagavan)? I wish to know this. I want to hear everything from you, for indeed, I have great curiosity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 51. And what did he say, O reverent one (bhagavan)? I wish to know this. I want to hear everything from you, for indeed, I have great curiosity.

sanskrit: नारद उवाच ।यन्मां पृच्छसि राजेन्द्र हरिश्चन्द्रं प्रति प्रभो ।तत्तेऽहं संप्रवक्ष्यामि माहात्म्यं तस्य धीमतः ॥५२॥
transliteration: nārada uvāca ,yanmāṁ pṛcchasi rājendra hariścandraṁ prati prabho ,tatte'haṁ saṁpravakṣyāmi māhātmyaṁ tasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Nārada said: 'O King of kings (rājendra), O Lord, regarding what you ask me about Hariśchandra, I will indeed fully explain to you the greatness of that wise one.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 52. Nārada said: 'O King of kings (rājendra), O Lord, regarding what you ask me about Hariśchandra, I will indeed fully explain to you the greatness of that wise one.'

sanskrit: स राजा बलवानासीत्सम्राट्सर्वमहीक्षिताम् ।तस्य सर्वे महीपालाः शासनावनताः स्थिताः ॥५३॥
transliteration: sa rājā balavānāsītsamrāṭsarvamahīkṣitām ,tasya sarve mahīpālāḥ śāsanāvanatāḥ sthitāḥ.
english: That king was powerful, an emperor among all earth-rulers. All the kings were subservient to his command.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 53. That king was powerful, an emperor among all earth-rulers. All the kings were subservient to his command.

sanskrit: तेनैकं रथमास्थाय जैत्रं हेमविभूषितम् ।शस्त्रप्रतापेन जिता द्वीपाः सप्त नरेश्वर ॥५४॥
transliteration: tenaikaṁ rathamāsthāya jaitraṁ hemavibhūṣitam ,śastrapratāpena jitā dvīpāḥ sapta nareśvara.
english: O King (nara-īśvara), having mounted a single victorious chariot adorned with gold, he conquered the seven islands by the might of his weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 54. O King (nara-īśvara), having mounted a single victorious chariot adorned with gold, he conquered the seven islands by the might of his weapons.

sanskrit: स विजित्य महीं सर्वां सशैलवनकाननाम् ।आजहार महाराज राजसूयं महाक्रतुम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: sa vijitya mahīṁ sarvāṁ saśailavanakānanām ,ājahāra mahārāja rājasūyaṁ mahākratum.
english: O great king, having completely conquered the entire earth, including its mountains, forests, and groves, he then performed the magnificent Rājasūya (yajña) ritual.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 55. O great king, having completely conquered the entire earth, including its mountains, forests, and groves, he then performed the magnificent Rājasūya (yajña) ritual.

sanskrit: तस्य सर्वे महीपाला धनान्याजह्रुराज्ञया ।द्विजानां परिवेष्टारस्तस्मिन्यज्ञे च तेऽभवन् ॥५६॥
transliteration: tasya sarve mahīpālā dhanānyājahrurājñayā ,dvijānāṁ pariveṣṭārastasminyajñe ca te'bhavan.
english: All the kings (mahīpāla), by his command, brought him their wealth. And in that (yajña) sacrifice, they became the attendants of the twice-born (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 56. All the kings (mahīpāla), by his command, brought him their wealth. And in that (yajña) sacrifice, they became the attendants of the twice-born (dvija).

sanskrit: प्रादाच्च द्रविणं प्रीत्या याजकानां नरेश्वरः ।यथोक्तं तत्र तैस्तस्मिंस्ततः पञ्चगुणाधिकम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: prādācca draviṇaṁ prītyā yājakānāṁ nareśvaraḥ ,yathoktaṁ tatra taistasmiṁstataḥ pañcaguṇādhikam.
english: And the king (nareśvara), affectionately, gave wealth to the priests (yājaka), five times more than what was prescribed by them for that occasion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 57. And the king (nareśvara), affectionately, gave wealth to the priests (yājaka), five times more than what was prescribed by them for that occasion.

sanskrit: अतर्पयच्च विविधैर्वसुभिर्ब्राह्मणांस्तथा ।प्रासर्पकाले संप्राप्ते नानादिग्भ्यः समागतान् ॥५८॥
transliteration: atarpayacca vividhairvasubhirbrāhmaṇāṁstathā ,prāsarpakāle saṁprāpte nānādigbhyaḥ samāgatān.
english: And he satisfied the Brahmins, who had arrived from various directions, with diverse riches at the appointed time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 58. And he satisfied the Brahmins, who had arrived from various directions, with diverse riches at the appointed time.

sanskrit: भक्ष्यैर्भोज्यैश्च विविधैर्यथाकामपुरस्कृतैः ।रत्नौघतर्पितैस्तुष्टैर्द्विजैश्च समुदाहृतम् ।तेजस्वी च यशस्वी च नृपेभ्योऽभ्यधिकोऽभवत् ॥५९॥
transliteration: bhakṣyairbhojyaiśca vividhairyathākāmapuraskṛtaiḥ ,ratnaughatarpitaistuṣṭairdvijaiśca samudāhṛtam ,tejasvī ca yaśasvī ca nṛpebhyo'bhyadhiko'bhavat.
english: And with various edibles and eatables, offered as desired, and with the twice-born (dvija) priests satisfied and contented by heaps of jewels, it was declared [by them]: 'He became brilliant and famous, superior to other kings.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 59. And with various edibles and eatables, offered as desired, and with the twice-born (dvija) priests satisfied and contented by heaps of jewels, it was declared [by them]: 'He became brilliant and famous, superior to other kings.'

sanskrit: एतस्मात्कारणात्पार्थ हरिश्चन्द्रो विराजते ।तेभ्यो राजसहस्रेभ्यस्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥६०॥
transliteration: etasmātkāraṇātpārtha hariścandro virājate ,tebhyo rājasahasrebhyastadviddhi bharatarṣabha.
english: For this reason, O Pārtha, Harishchandra shines forth. Know this, O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), that he excels over thousands of other kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 60. For this reason, O Pārtha, Harishchandra shines forth. Know this, O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), that he excels over thousands of other kings.

sanskrit: समाप्य च हरिश्चन्द्रो महायज्ञं प्रतापवान् ।अभिषिक्तः स शुशुभे साम्राज्येन नराधिप ॥६१॥
transliteration: samāpya ca hariścandro mahāyajñaṁ pratāpavān ,abhiṣiktaḥ sa śuśubhe sāmrājyena narādhipa.
english: O ruler of men, the glorious Hariścandra, having completed the great Vedic ritual (yajña), was then consecrated and shone splendidly with his imperial sovereignty.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 61. O ruler of men, the glorious Hariścandra, having completed the great Vedic ritual (yajña), was then consecrated and shone splendidly with his imperial sovereignty.

sanskrit: ये चान्येऽपि महीपाला राजसूयं महाक्रतुम् ।यजन्ते ते महेन्द्रेण मोदन्ते सह भारत ॥६२॥
transliteration: ye cānye'pi mahīpālā rājasūyaṁ mahākratum ,yajante te mahendreṇa modante saha bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, those other kings who also perform the great Rājasūya (yajña), they rejoice together with Mahendra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 62. O Bhārata, those other kings who also perform the great Rājasūya (yajña), they rejoice together with Mahendra.

sanskrit: ये चापि निधनं प्राप्ताः संग्रामेष्वपलायिनः ।ते तत्सदः समासाद्य मोदन्ते भरतर्षभ ॥६३॥
transliteration: ye cāpi nidhanaṁ prāptāḥ saṁgrāmeṣvapalāyinaḥ ,te tatsadaḥ samāsādya modante bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bhāratas, those who, never fleeing in battles, attained death, they also reach that abode and rejoice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 63. O best of the Bhāratas, those who, never fleeing in battles, attained death, they also reach that abode and rejoice.

sanskrit: तपसा ये च तीव्रेण त्यजन्तीह कलेवरम् ।तेऽपि तत्स्थानमासाद्य श्रीमन्तो भान्ति नित्यशः ॥६४॥
transliteration: tapasā ye ca tīvreṇa tyajantīha kalevaram ,te'pi tatsthānamāsādya śrīmanto bhānti nityaśaḥ.
english: And those who, through severe austerity (tapas), abandon their body in this world, they too attain that abode and shine eternally with glory.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 64. And those who, through severe austerity (tapas), abandon their body in this world, they too attain that abode and shine eternally with glory.

sanskrit: पिता च त्वाह कौन्तेय पाण्डुः कौरवनन्दनः ।हरिश्चन्द्रे श्रियं दृष्ट्वा नृपतौ जातविस्मयः ॥६५॥
transliteration: pitā ca tvāha kaunteya pāṇḍuḥ kauravanandanaḥ ,hariścandre śriyaṁ dṛṣṭvā nṛpatau jātavismayaḥ.
english: And your father Pāṇḍu, O son of Kunti, the delight of the Kurus, said to you, having become astonished after seeing the glory in King Hariścandra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 65. And your father Pāṇḍu, O son of Kunti, the delight of the Kurus, said to you, having become astonished after seeing the glory in King Hariścandra.

sanskrit: समर्थोऽसि महीं जेतुं भ्रातरस्ते वशे स्थिताः ।राजसूयं क्रतुश्रेष्ठमाहरस्वेति भारत ॥६६॥
transliteration: samartho'si mahīṁ jetuṁ bhrātaraste vaśe sthitāḥ ,rājasūyaṁ kratuśreṣṭhamāharasveti bhārata.
english: You are capable of conquering the earth, and your brothers are under your command. Therefore, O descendant of Bharata, perform the Rājasūya (yajña), the foremost of rituals!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 66. You are capable of conquering the earth, and your brothers are under your command. Therefore, O descendant of Bharata, perform the Rājasūya (yajña), the foremost of rituals!

sanskrit: तस्य त्वं पुरुषव्याघ्र संकल्पं कुरु पाण्डव ।गन्तारस्ते महेन्द्रस्य पूर्वैः सह सलोकताम् ॥६७॥
transliteration: tasya tvaṁ puruṣavyāghra saṁkalpaṁ kuru pāṇḍava ,gantāraste mahendrasya pūrvaiḥ saha salokatām.
english: O Pāṇḍava, O tiger among men, you must make a firm resolve for that [sacrifice]. Your ancestors, along with you, will attain the same realm as the great Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 67. O Pāṇḍava, O tiger among men, you must make a firm resolve for that [sacrifice]. Your ancestors, along with you, will attain the same realm as the great Indra.

sanskrit: बहुविघ्नश्च नृपते क्रतुरेष स्मृतो महान् ।छिद्राण्यत्र हि वाञ्छन्ति यज्ञघ्ना ब्रह्मराक्षसाः ॥६८॥
transliteration: bahuvighnaśca nṛpate kratureṣa smṛto mahān ,chidrāṇyatra hi vāñchanti yajñaghnā brahmarākṣasāḥ.
english: O king, this great Vedic ritual (yajña) is known to have many obstacles. Indeed, the Brahma-Rakshasas, who are destroyers of Vedic rituals (yajñas), seek out weaknesses in it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 68. O king, this great Vedic ritual (yajña) is known to have many obstacles. Indeed, the Brahma-Rakshasas, who are destroyers of Vedic rituals (yajñas), seek out weaknesses in it.

sanskrit: युद्धं च पृष्ठगमनं पृथिवीक्षयकारकम् ।किंचिदेव निमित्तं च भवत्यत्र क्षयावहम् ॥६९॥
transliteration: yuddhaṁ ca pṛṣṭhagamanaṁ pṛthivīkṣayakārakam ,kiṁcideva nimittaṁ ca bhavatyatra kṣayāvaham.
english: War and retreat are both causes of the destruction of the world. And in this matter, even a minor reason can become a cause of ruin.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 69. War and retreat are both causes of the destruction of the world. And in this matter, even a minor reason can become a cause of ruin.

sanskrit: एतत्संचिन्त्य राजेन्द्र यत्क्षमं तत्समाचर ।अप्रमत्तोत्थितो नित्यं चातुर्वर्ण्यस्य रक्षणे ।भव एधस्व मोदस्व दानैस्तर्पय च द्विजान् ॥७०॥
transliteration: etatsaṁcintya rājendra yatkṣamaṁ tatsamācara ,apramattotthito nityaṁ cāturvarṇyasya rakṣaṇe ,bhava edhasva modasva dānaistarpaya ca dvijān.
english: O great king, having thoroughly considered this, always act in a manner that is appropriate by being vigilant and active in the protection of the four social classes. Prosper, rejoice, and satisfy the twice-born (dvija) with gifts.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 70. O great king, having thoroughly considered this, always act in a manner that is appropriate by being vigilant and active in the protection of the four social classes. Prosper, rejoice, and satisfy the twice-born (dvija) with gifts.

sanskrit: एतत्ते विस्तरेणोक्तं यन्मां त्वं परिपृच्छसि ।आपृच्छे त्वां गमिष्यामि दाशार्हनगरीं प्रति ॥७१॥
transliteration: etatte vistareṇoktaṁ yanmāṁ tvaṁ paripṛcchasi ,āpṛcche tvāṁ gamiṣyāmi dāśārhanagarīṁ prati.
english: I have explained to you in detail everything that you asked me. Now I bid you farewell, as I will depart for the city of Dāśārha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 71. I have explained to you in detail everything that you asked me. Now I bid you farewell, as I will depart for the city of Dāśārha.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमाख्याय पार्थेभ्यो नारदो जनमेजय ।जगाम तैर्वृतो राजन्नृषिभिर्यैः समागतः ॥७२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamākhyāya pārthebhyo nārado janamejaya ,jagāma tairvṛto rājannṛṣibhiryaiḥ samāgataḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O Janamejaya, after thus narrating to the Pārthas, Nārada departed, O King, accompanied by the very sages with whom he had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 72. Vaiśampāyana said: O Janamejaya, after thus narrating to the Pārthas, Nārada departed, O King, accompanied by the very sages with whom he had arrived.

sanskrit: गते तु नारदे पार्थो भ्रातृभिः सह कौरव ।राजसूयं क्रतुश्रेष्ठं चिन्तयामास भारत ॥७३॥
transliteration: gate tu nārade pārtho bhrātṛbhiḥ saha kaurava ,rājasūyaṁ kratuśreṣṭhaṁ cintayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O descendant of Kuru, O Bharata, when Narada had departed, Partha (Yudhishthira), along with his brothers, began to ponder the Rajasūya, that supreme Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 11 Verse 73. O descendant of Kuru, O Bharata, when Narada had departed, Partha (Yudhishthira), along with his brothers, began to ponder the Rajasūya, that supreme Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ऋषेस्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा निशश्वास युधिष्ठिरः ।चिन्तयन्राजसूयाप्तिं न लेभे शर्म भारत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ṛṣestadvacanaṁ śrutvā niśaśvāsa yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,cintayanrājasūyāptiṁ na lebhe śarma bhārata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard those words of the sage, Yudhishthira sighed. O Bhārata, pondering the attainment of the Rājasūya (yajña), he found no comfort.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard those words of the sage, Yudhishthira sighed. O Bhārata, pondering the attainment of the Rājasūya (yajña), he found no comfort.

sanskrit: राजर्षीणां हि तं श्रुत्वा महिमानं महात्मनाम् ।यज्वनां कर्मभिः पुण्यैर्लोकप्राप्तिं समीक्ष्य च ॥२॥
transliteration: rājarṣīṇāṁ hi taṁ śrutvā mahimānaṁ mahātmanām ,yajvanāṁ karmabhiḥ puṇyairlokaprāptiṁ samīkṣya ca.
english: Indeed, having heard of the glory of those great-souled royal sages, and having observed the attainment of superior realms through the meritorious deeds (karma) of those who perform sacrifices...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 2. Indeed, having heard of the glory of those great-souled royal sages, and having observed the attainment of superior realms through the meritorious deeds (karma) of those who perform sacrifices...

sanskrit: हरिश्चन्द्रं च राजर्षिं रोचमानं विशेषतः ।यज्वानं यज्ञमाहर्तुं राजसूयमियेष सः ॥३॥
transliteration: hariścandraṁ ca rājarṣiṁ rocamānaṁ viśeṣataḥ ,yajvānaṁ yajñamāhartuṁ rājasūyamiyeṣa saḥ.
english: He (Yudhishthira) desired to undertake the Rajasūya Vedic ritual (yajña), wishing to become a performer of Vedic rituals (yajñas) and, especially, to shine forth like King Harishchandra, the royal sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 3. He (Yudhishthira) desired to undertake the Rajasūya Vedic ritual (yajña), wishing to become a performer of Vedic rituals (yajñas) and, especially, to shine forth like King Harishchandra, the royal sage.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्ततः सर्वानर्चयित्वा सभासदः ।प्रत्यर्चितश्च तैः सर्वैर्यज्ञायैव मनो दधे ॥४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastataḥ sarvānarcayitvā sabhāsadaḥ ,pratyarcitaśca taiḥ sarvairyajñāyaiva mano dadhe.
english: Then Yudhishthira, after honoring all the assembly members and being honored in return by all of them, set his mind exclusively on the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 4. Then Yudhishthira, after honoring all the assembly members and being honored in return by all of them, set his mind exclusively on the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: स राजसूयं राजेन्द्र कुरूणामृषभः क्रतुम् ।आहर्तुं प्रवणं चक्रे मनः संचिन्त्य सोऽसकृत् ॥५॥
transliteration: sa rājasūyaṁ rājendra kurūṇāmṛṣabhaḥ kratum ,āhartuṁ pravaṇaṁ cakre manaḥ saṁcintya so'sakṛt.
english: That foremost among the Kurus, the king of kings, repeatedly considered it and then set his mind on performing the Rājasūya (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 5. That foremost among the Kurus, the king of kings, repeatedly considered it and then set his mind on performing the Rājasūya (yajña).

sanskrit: भूयश्चाद्भुतवीर्यौजा धर्ममेवानुपालयन् ।किं हितं सर्वलोकानां भवेदिति मनो दधे ॥६॥
transliteration: bhūyaścādbhutavīryaujā dharmamevānupālayan ,kiṁ hitaṁ sarvalokānāṁ bhavediti mano dadhe.
english: And again, he, possessing wondrous valor and energy, upholding only the natural law (dharma), considered, 'What would be beneficial for all people?' and thus resolved.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 6. And again, he, possessing wondrous valor and energy, upholding only the natural law (dharma), considered, 'What would be beneficial for all people?' and thus resolved.

sanskrit: अनुगृह्णन्प्रजाः सर्वाः सर्वधर्मविदां वरः ।अविशेषेण सर्वेषां हितं चक्रे युधिष्ठिरः ॥७॥
transliteration: anugṛhṇanprajāḥ sarvāḥ sarvadharmavidāṁ varaḥ ,aviśeṣeṇa sarveṣāṁ hitaṁ cakre yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among those who know all aspects of natural law (dharma), showing favor to all subjects, worked for the benefit of everyone without discrimination.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 7. Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost among those who know all aspects of natural law (dharma), showing favor to all subjects, worked for the benefit of everyone without discrimination.

sanskrit: एवं गते ततस्तस्मिन्पितरीवाश्वसञ्जनाः ।न तस्य विद्यते द्वेष्टा ततोऽस्याजातशत्रुता ॥८॥
transliteration: evaṁ gate tatastasminpitarīvāśvasañjanāḥ ,na tasya vidyate dveṣṭā tato'syājātaśatrutā.
english: As things progressed in this manner, people placed their trust in him as they would in a father. He had no enemies, and this is why he was known as Ajātaśatru (he who has no born enemies).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 8. As things progressed in this manner, people placed their trust in him as they would in a father. He had no enemies, and this is why he was known as Ajātaśatru (he who has no born enemies).

sanskrit: स मन्त्रिणः समानाय्य भ्रातॄंश्च वदतां वरः ।राजसूयं प्रति तदा पुनः पुनरपृच्छत ॥९॥
transliteration: sa mantriṇaḥ samānāyya bhrātṝṁśca vadatāṁ varaḥ ,rājasūyaṁ prati tadā punaḥ punarapṛcchata.
english: Then, that best among speakers, having summoned his ministers and brothers, repeatedly inquired about the Rājasūya (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 9. Then, that best among speakers, having summoned his ministers and brothers, repeatedly inquired about the Rājasūya (yajña).

sanskrit: ते पृच्छ्यमानाः सहिता वचोऽर्थ्यं मन्त्रिणस्तदा ।युधिष्ठिरं महाप्राज्ञं यियक्षुमिदमब्रुवन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: te pṛcchyamānāḥ sahitā vaco'rthyaṁ mantriṇastadā ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ mahāprājñaṁ yiyakṣumidamabruvan.
english: Then, those ministers, who were gathered and being questioned, together spoke these significant words to the very wise Yudhishthira, who desired to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 10. Then, those ministers, who were gathered and being questioned, together spoke these significant words to the very wise Yudhishthira, who desired to perform a Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: येनाभिषिक्तो नृपतिर्वारुणं गुणमृच्छति ।तेन राजापि सन्कृत्स्नं सम्राड्गुणमभीप्सति ॥११॥
transliteration: yenābhiṣikto nṛpatirvāruṇaṁ guṇamṛcchati ,tena rājāpi sankṛtsnaṁ samrāḍguṇamabhīpsati.
english: A king, when consecrated, attains the quality of Varuna. Therefore, even a king, by virtue of being a king, desires to fully achieve the quality of an emperor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 11. A king, when consecrated, attains the quality of Varuna. Therefore, even a king, by virtue of being a king, desires to fully achieve the quality of an emperor.

sanskrit: तस्य सम्राड्गुणार्हस्य भवतः कुरुनन्दन ।राजसूयस्य समयं मन्यन्ते सुहृदस्तव ॥१२॥
transliteration: tasya samrāḍguṇārhasya bhavataḥ kurunandana ,rājasūyasya samayaṁ manyante suhṛdastava.
english: O delight of the Kurus, your well-wishers deem this the opportune moment for the Rājasūya (yajña) for you, who are indeed worthy of the qualities of an emperor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 12. O delight of the Kurus, your well-wishers deem this the opportune moment for the Rājasūya (yajña) for you, who are indeed worthy of the qualities of an emperor.

sanskrit: तस्य यज्ञस्य समयः स्वाधीनः क्षत्रसंपदा ।साम्ना षडग्नयो यस्मिंश्चीयन्ते संशितव्रतैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasya yajñasya samayaḥ svādhīnaḥ kṣatrasaṁpadā ,sāmnā ṣaḍagnayo yasmiṁścīyante saṁśitavrataiḥ.
english: The time for that Vedic ritual (yajña) is under the control of royal power and prosperity. In this ritual, six fires are ritually arranged with Sāman chants by those who are firm in their vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 13. The time for that Vedic ritual (yajña) is under the control of royal power and prosperity. In this ritual, six fires are ritually arranged with Sāman chants by those who are firm in their vows.

sanskrit: दर्वीहोमानुपादाय सर्वान्यः प्राप्नुते क्रतून् ।अभिषेकं च यज्ञान्ते सर्वजित्तेन चोच्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: darvīhomānupādāya sarvānyaḥ prāpnute kratūn ,abhiṣekaṁ ca yajñānte sarvajittena cocyate.
english: He who, by performing the Darvī-homas, accomplishes all other Vedic rituals (kratu) and also receives the consecration at the conclusion of the Vedic ritual (yajña), is for that reason called "All-conquering" (sarvajit).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 14. He who, by performing the Darvī-homas, accomplishes all other Vedic rituals (kratu) and also receives the consecration at the conclusion of the Vedic ritual (yajña), is for that reason called "All-conquering" (sarvajit).

sanskrit: समर्थोऽसि महाबाहो सर्वे ते वशगा वयम् ।अविचार्य महाराज राजसूये मनः कुरु ॥१५॥
transliteration: samartho'si mahābāho sarve te vaśagā vayam ,avicārya mahārāja rājasūye manaḥ kuru.
english: O mighty-armed one (Mahābāho), you are certainly capable! We are all at your command. Therefore, O great king (Mahārāja), without any hesitation, set your mind upon the Rājasūya (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 15. O mighty-armed one (Mahābāho), you are certainly capable! We are all at your command. Therefore, O great king (Mahārāja), without any hesitation, set your mind upon the Rājasūya (yajña).

sanskrit: इत्येवं सुहृदः सर्वे पृथक्च सह चाब्रुवन् ।स धर्म्यं पाण्डवस्तेषां वचः श्रुत्वा विशां पते ।धृष्टमिष्टं वरिष्ठं च जग्राह मनसारिहा ॥१६॥
transliteration: ityevaṁ suhṛdaḥ sarve pṛthakca saha cābruvan ,sa dharmyaṁ pāṇḍavasteṣāṁ vacaḥ śrutvā viśāṁ pate ,dhṛṣṭamiṣṭaṁ variṣṭhaṁ ca jagrāha manasārihā.
english: Thus, all his friends spoke, both individually and collectively. The Pāṇḍava (Yudhishthira), that lord of the people and annihilator of enemies, having heard their counsel, which was righteous (dharmya), bold, desirable, and excellent, accepted it mentally.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 16. Thus, all his friends spoke, both individually and collectively. The Pāṇḍava (Yudhishthira), that lord of the people and annihilator of enemies, having heard their counsel, which was righteous (dharmya), bold, desirable, and excellent, accepted it mentally.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा सुहृद्वचस्तच्च जानंश्चाप्यात्मनः क्षमम् ।पुनः पुनर्मनो दध्रे राजसूयाय भारत ॥१७॥
transliteration: śrutvā suhṛdvacastacca jānaṁścāpyātmanaḥ kṣamam ,punaḥ punarmano dadhre rājasūyāya bhārata.
english: Having heard that friendly advice, and also knowing it to be appropriate for himself (ātman), he repeatedly resolved his mind upon the Rājasūya (ritual), O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 17. Having heard that friendly advice, and also knowing it to be appropriate for himself (ātman), he repeatedly resolved his mind upon the Rājasūya (ritual), O Bhārata.

sanskrit: स भ्रातृभिः पुनर्धीमानृत्विग्भिश्च महात्मभिः ।धौम्यद्वैपायनाद्यैश्च मन्त्रयामास मन्त्रिभिः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa bhrātṛbhiḥ punardhīmānṛtvigbhiśca mahātmabhiḥ ,dhaumyadvaipāyanādyaiśca mantrayāmāsa mantribhiḥ.
english: That wise man (dhīmān) again consulted with his brothers, and with the magnanimous priests (ṛtvij), and also with Dhaumya, Dvaipāyana, and other counselors.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 18. That wise man (dhīmān) again consulted with his brothers, and with the magnanimous priests (ṛtvij), and also with Dhaumya, Dvaipāyana, and other counselors.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।इयं या राजसूयस्य सम्राडर्हस्य सुक्रतोः ।श्रद्दधानस्य वदतः स्पृहा मे सा कथं भवेत् ॥१९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,iyaṁ yā rājasūyasya samrāḍarhasya sukratoḥ ,śraddadhānasya vadataḥ spṛhā me sā kathaṁ bhavet.
english: Yudhishthira said: How can I possibly entertain the desire for this Rājasūya (Vedic ritual), this great ritual worthy of an emperor, about which one speaks with such deep faith (śraddhā)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 19. Yudhishthira said: How can I possibly entertain the desire for this Rājasūya (Vedic ritual), this great ritual worthy of an emperor, about which one speaks with such deep faith (śraddhā)?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तास्तु ते तेन राज्ञा राजीवलोचन ।इदमूचुर्वचः काले धर्मात्मानं युधिष्ठिरम् ।अर्हस्त्वमसि धर्मज्ञ राजसूयं महाक्रतुम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktāstu te tena rājñā rājīvalocana ,idamūcurvacaḥ kāle dharmātmānaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,arhastvamasi dharmajña rājasūyaṁ mahākratum.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O lotus-eyed one, when they were thus addressed by that king, they spoke these words at that time to Yudhishthira, the virtuous soul: 'O knower of natural law (dharma), you are indeed worthy of the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual), the great ritual!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 20. Vaiśampāyana said: O lotus-eyed one, when they were thus addressed by that king, they spoke these words at that time to Yudhishthira, the virtuous soul: 'O knower of natural law (dharma), you are indeed worthy of the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual), the great ritual!'

sanskrit: अथैवमुक्ते नृपतावृत्विग्भिरृषिभिस्तथा ।मन्त्रिणो भ्रातरश्चास्य तद्वचः प्रत्यपूजयन् ॥२१॥
transliteration: athaivamukte nṛpatāvṛtvigbhirṛṣibhistathā ,mantriṇo bhrātaraścāsya tadvacaḥ pratyapūjayan.
english: Then, after the king was addressed in this manner by the priests and sages, his ministers and brothers approved of those words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 21. Then, after the king was addressed in this manner by the priests and sages, his ministers and brothers approved of those words.

sanskrit: स तु राजा महाप्राज्ञः पुनरेवात्मनात्मवान् ।भूयो विममृशे पार्थो लोकानां हितकाम्यया ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa tu rājā mahāprājñaḥ punarevātmanātmavān ,bhūyo vimamṛśe pārtho lokānāṁ hitakāmyayā.
english: But that greatly wise king, personally endowed with self-possession (ātman), again reflected, O Pārtha, desiring the welfare of the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 22. But that greatly wise king, personally endowed with self-possession (ātman), again reflected, O Pārtha, desiring the welfare of the people.

sanskrit: सामर्थ्ययोगं संप्रेक्ष्य देशकालौ व्ययागमौ ।विमृश्य सम्यक्च धिया कुर्वन्प्राज्ञो न सीदति ॥२३॥
transliteration: sāmarthyayogaṁ saṁprekṣya deśakālau vyayāgamau ,vimṛśya samyakca dhiyā kurvanprājño na sīdati.
english: A wise person (prājña), having thoroughly observed the combination of capabilities, considering also the place, time, expenditures, and incomes, and having properly deliberated with their intellect, does not fail when acting.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 23. A wise person (prājña), having thoroughly observed the combination of capabilities, considering also the place, time, expenditures, and incomes, and having properly deliberated with their intellect, does not fail when acting.

sanskrit: न हि यज्ञसमारम्भः केवलात्मविपत्तये ।भवतीति समाज्ञाय यत्नतः कार्यमुद्वहन् ॥२४॥
transliteration: na hi yajñasamārambhaḥ kevalātmavipattaye ,bhavatīti samājñāya yatnataḥ kāryamudvahan.
english: Indeed, the commencement of a Vedic ritual (yajña) is not merely for one's self-destruction. Having fully understood this, one should diligently carry out the task with effort.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 24. Indeed, the commencement of a Vedic ritual (yajña) is not merely for one's self-destruction. Having fully understood this, one should diligently carry out the task with effort.

sanskrit: स निश्चयार्थं कार्यस्य कृष्णमेव जनार्दनम् ।सर्वलोकात्परं मत्वा जगाम मनसा हरिम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa niścayārthaṁ kāryasya kṛṣṇameva janārdanam ,sarvalokātparaṁ matvā jagāma manasā harim.
english: For the purpose of making a firm decision regarding the matter, he mentally approached Hari, Krishna (Janārdana) himself, considering him superior to all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 25. For the purpose of making a firm decision regarding the matter, he mentally approached Hari, Krishna (Janārdana) himself, considering him superior to all beings.

sanskrit: अप्रमेयं महाबाहुं कामाज्जातमजं नृषु ।पाण्डवस्तर्कयामास कर्मभिर्देवसंमितैः ॥२६॥
transliteration: aprameyaṁ mahābāhuṁ kāmājjātamajaṁ nṛṣu ,pāṇḍavastarkayāmāsa karmabhirdevasaṁmitaiḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍava reasoned about him - the immeasurable, mighty-armed one, the unborn who is nevertheless born among humans out of his own desire - through his deeds that were comparable to those of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 26. The Pāṇḍava reasoned about him - the immeasurable, mighty-armed one, the unborn who is nevertheless born among humans out of his own desire - through his deeds that were comparable to those of the gods.

sanskrit: नास्य किंचिदविज्ञातं नास्य किंचिदकर्मजम् ।न स किंचिन्न विषहेदिति कृष्णममन्यत ॥२७॥
transliteration: nāsya kiṁcidavijñātaṁ nāsya kiṁcidakarmajam ,na sa kiṁcinna viṣahediti kṛṣṇamamanyata.
english: He thought of Krishna: 'Nothing is unknown to him; there is nothing that is not born from his (karma) action; and there is nothing he would not endure.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 27. He thought of Krishna: 'Nothing is unknown to him; there is nothing that is not born from his (karma) action; and there is nothing he would not endure.'

sanskrit: स तु तां नैष्ठिकीं बुद्धिं कृत्वा पार्थो युधिष्ठिरः ।गुरुवद्भूतगुरवे प्राहिणोद्दूतमञ्जसा ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa tu tāṁ naiṣṭhikīṁ buddhiṁ kṛtvā pārtho yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,guruvadbhūtagurave prāhiṇoddūtamañjasā.
english: But he, Pārtha Yudhiṣṭhira, having formed that resolute understanding, quickly sent a messenger to the preceptor of all beings (guru) as if to his own teacher (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 28. But he, Pārtha Yudhiṣṭhira, having formed that resolute understanding, quickly sent a messenger to the preceptor of all beings (guru) as if to his own teacher (guru).

sanskrit: शीघ्रगेन रथेनाशु स दूतः प्राप्य यादवान् ।द्वारकावासिनं कृष्णं द्वारवत्यां समासदत् ॥२९॥
transliteration: śīghragena rathenāśu sa dūtaḥ prāpya yādavān ,dvārakāvāsinaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ dvāravatyāṁ samāsadat.
english: That messenger, traveling swiftly by chariot, quickly reached the Yadus. He then found Kṛṣṇa, who resided in Dvārakā, in the city of Dvāravatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 29. That messenger, traveling swiftly by chariot, quickly reached the Yadus. He then found Kṛṣṇa, who resided in Dvārakā, in the city of Dvāravatī.

sanskrit: दर्शनाकाङ्क्षिणं पार्थं दर्शनाकाङ्क्षयाच्युतः ।इन्द्रसेनेन सहित इन्द्रप्रस्थं ययौ तदा ॥३०॥
transliteration: darśanākāṅkṣiṇaṁ pārthaṁ darśanākāṅkṣayācyutaḥ ,indrasenena sahita indraprasthaṁ yayau tadā.
english: Then, Acyuta, eager to see Pārtha (who was also eager to see him), went to Indraprastha accompanied by Indrasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 30. Then, Acyuta, eager to see Pārtha (who was also eager to see him), went to Indraprastha accompanied by Indrasena.

sanskrit: व्यतीत्य विविधान्देशांस्त्वरावान्क्षिप्रवाहनः ।इन्द्रप्रस्थगतं पार्थमभ्यगच्छज्जनार्दनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: vyatītya vividhāndeśāṁstvarāvānkṣipravāhanaḥ ,indraprasthagataṁ pārthamabhyagacchajjanārdanaḥ.
english: Janārdana, being swift and traveling in a speedy vehicle, crossed various regions and approached Pārtha, who was then in Indraprastha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 31. Janārdana, being swift and traveling in a speedy vehicle, crossed various regions and approached Pārtha, who was then in Indraprastha.

sanskrit: स गृहे भ्रातृवद्भ्रात्रा धर्मराजेन पूजितः ।भीमेन च ततोऽपश्यत्स्वसारं प्रीतिमान्पितुः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa gṛhe bhrātṛvadbhrātrā dharmarājena pūjitaḥ ,bhīmena ca tato'paśyatsvasāraṁ prītimānpituḥ.
english: Being honored in the house by his brother, the king of (natural law) dharma, Yudhiṣṭhira, and also by Bhīma, he then saw his father's sister, filled with affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 32. Being honored in the house by his brother, the king of (natural law) dharma, Yudhiṣṭhira, and also by Bhīma, he then saw his father's sister, filled with affection.

sanskrit: प्रीतः प्रियेण सुहृदा रेमे स सहितस्तदा ।अर्जुनेन यमाभ्यां च गुरुवत्पर्युपस्थितः ॥३३॥
transliteration: prītaḥ priyeṇa suhṛdā reme sa sahitastadā ,arjunena yamābhyāṁ ca guruvatparyupasthitaḥ.
english: Then, pleased, he enjoyed himself in the company of his dear friend, while Arjuna and the two Yamas attended upon him as they would a preceptor (guru).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 33. Then, pleased, he enjoyed himself in the company of his dear friend, while Arjuna and the two Yamas attended upon him as they would a preceptor (guru).

sanskrit: तं विश्रान्तं शुभे देशे क्षणिनं कल्यमच्युतम् ।धर्मराजः समागम्य ज्ञापयत्स्वं प्रयोजनम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: taṁ viśrāntaṁ śubhe deśe kṣaṇinaṁ kalyamacyutam ,dharmarājaḥ samāgamya jñāpayatsvaṁ prayojanam.
english: Then Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), having approached Acyuta (Krishna) who was rested, at leisure, and healthy in an auspicious place, informed him of his own purpose.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 34. Then Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), having approached Acyuta (Krishna) who was rested, at leisure, and healthy in an auspicious place, informed him of his own purpose.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।प्रार्थितो राजसूयो मे न चासौ केवलेप्सया ।प्राप्यते येन तत्ते ह विदितं कृष्ण सर्वशः ॥३५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,prārthito rājasūyo me na cāsau kevalepsayā ,prāpyate yena tatte ha viditaṁ kṛṣṇa sarvaśaḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: This Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) has been desired by me, yet it is not attained by mere wishing. Indeed, O Krishna, you know all aspects of how it is achieved.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 35. Yudhishthira said: This Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) has been desired by me, yet it is not attained by mere wishing. Indeed, O Krishna, you know all aspects of how it is achieved.

sanskrit: यस्मिन्सर्वं संभवति यश्च सर्वत्र पूज्यते ।यश्च सर्वेश्वरो राजा राजसूयं स विन्दति ॥३६॥
transliteration: yasminsarvaṁ saṁbhavati yaśca sarvatra pūjyate ,yaśca sarveśvaro rājā rājasūyaṁ sa vindati.
english: He in whom everything originates, and who is honored everywhere, and who is the king, the lord of all, he attains the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 36. He in whom everything originates, and who is honored everywhere, and who is the king, the lord of all, he attains the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual).

sanskrit: तं राजसूयं सुहृदः कार्यमाहुः समेत्य मे ।तत्र मे निश्चिततमं तव कृष्ण गिरा भवेत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: taṁ rājasūyaṁ suhṛdaḥ kāryamāhuḥ sametya me ,tatra me niścitatamaṁ tava kṛṣṇa girā bhavet.
english: My friends, having gathered, have advised me that this Rājasūya (royal consecration) is a duty to be performed. Regarding that matter, my most definite conclusion will be reached through your words, O Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 37. My friends, having gathered, have advised me that this Rājasūya (royal consecration) is a duty to be performed. Regarding that matter, my most definite conclusion will be reached through your words, O Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: केचिद्धि सौहृदादेव दोषं न परिचक्षते ।अर्थहेतोस्तथैवान्ये प्रियमेव वदन्त्युत ॥३८॥
transliteration: keciddhi sauhṛdādeva doṣaṁ na paricakṣate ,arthahetostathaivānye priyameva vadantyuta.
english: Indeed, some individuals do not point out (my) faults simply out of affection (souhṛda). Similarly, others, motivated by personal gain, speak only what is pleasing (priya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 38. Indeed, some individuals do not point out (my) faults simply out of affection (souhṛda). Similarly, others, motivated by personal gain, speak only what is pleasing (priya).

sanskrit: प्रियमेव परीप्सन्ते केचिदात्मनि यद्धितम् ।एवंप्रायाश्च दृश्यन्ते जनवादाः प्रयोजने ॥३९॥
transliteration: priyameva parīpsante kecidātmani yaddhitam ,evaṁprāyāśca dṛśyante janavādāḥ prayojane.
english: Some individuals only desire what is beneficial to themselves (ātman) and what is pleasing. Similarly, in practical matters, public opinions (janavāda) of this nature are observed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 39. Some individuals only desire what is beneficial to themselves (ātman) and what is pleasing. Similarly, in practical matters, public opinions (janavāda) of this nature are observed.

sanskrit: त्वं तु हेतूनतीत्यैतान्कामक्रोधौ व्यतीत्य च ।परमं नः क्षमं लोके यथावद्वक्तुमर्हसि ॥४०॥
transliteration: tvaṁ tu hetūnatītyaitānkāmakrodhau vyatītya ca ,paramaṁ naḥ kṣamaṁ loke yathāvadvaktumarhasi.
english: But you, having risen above these (biased) motivations, and having transcended both desire (kāma) and anger (krodha), should properly speak to us what is supremely suitable in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 12 Verse 40. But you, having risen above these (biased) motivations, and having transcended both desire (kāma) and anger (krodha), should properly speak to us what is supremely suitable in the world.

sanskrit: श्रीकृष्ण उवाच ।सर्वैर्गुणैर्महाराज राजसूयं त्वमर्हसि ।जानतस्त्वेव ते सर्वं किंचिद्वक्ष्यामि भारत ॥१॥
transliteration: śrīkṛṣṇa uvāca ,sarvairguṇairmahārāja rājasūyaṁ tvamarhasi ,jānatastveva te sarvaṁ kiṁcidvakṣyāmi bhārata.
english: Śrī Kṛṣṇa said: O great king, by all your virtues, you are indeed worthy of the Rājasūya sacrifice. Even though you already know everything, O descendant of Bharata, I will explain a few things to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 1. Śrī Kṛṣṇa said: O great king, by all your virtues, you are indeed worthy of the Rājasūya sacrifice. Even though you already know everything, O descendant of Bharata, I will explain a few things to you.

sanskrit: जामदग्न्येन रामेण क्षत्रं यदवशेषितम् ।तस्मादवरजं लोके यदिदं क्षत्रसंज्ञितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: jāmadagnyena rāmeṇa kṣatraṁ yadavaśeṣitam ,tasmādavarajaṁ loke yadidaṁ kṣatrasaṁjñitam.
english: Whatever warrior class (kṣatra) was spared by Rāma, the son of Jamadagni, from that stock descended what is now called the warrior class (kṣatra) in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 2. Whatever warrior class (kṣatra) was spared by Rāma, the son of Jamadagni, from that stock descended what is now called the warrior class (kṣatra) in this world.

sanskrit: कृतोऽयं कुलसंकल्पः क्षत्रियैर्वसुधाधिप ।निदेशवाग्भिस्तत्ते ह विदितं भरतर्षभ ॥३॥
transliteration: kṛto'yaṁ kulasaṁkalpaḥ kṣatriyairvasudhādhipa ,nideśavāgbhistatte ha viditaṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: O ruler of the earth, this resolution (saṃkalpa) concerning the lineage has been made by the kṣatriyas. This is indeed known to you, O best of Bharatas, through these words of instruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 3. O ruler of the earth, this resolution (saṃkalpa) concerning the lineage has been made by the kṣatriyas. This is indeed known to you, O best of Bharatas, through these words of instruction.

sanskrit: ऐलस्येक्ष्वाकुवंशस्य प्रकृतिं परिचक्षते ।राजानः श्रेणिबद्धाश्च ततोऽन्ये क्षत्रिया भुवि ॥४॥
transliteration: ailasyekṣvākuvaṁśasya prakṛtiṁ paricakṣate ,rājānaḥ śreṇibaddhāśca tato'nye kṣatriyā bhuvi.
english: They declare the Aila and Ikṣvāku dynasties to be the foundational stock (prakṛti). All other kings and kṣatriyas on Earth are then arranged in ranks according to these (lineages).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 4. They declare the Aila and Ikṣvāku dynasties to be the foundational stock (prakṛti). All other kings and kṣatriyas on Earth are then arranged in ranks according to these (lineages).

sanskrit: ऐलवंश्यास्तु ये राजंस्तथैवेक्ष्वाकवो नृपाः ।तानि चैकशतं विद्धि कुलानि भरतर्षभ ॥५॥
transliteration: ailavaṁśyāstu ye rājaṁstathaivekṣvākavo nṛpāḥ ,tāni caikaśataṁ viddhi kulāni bharatarṣabha.
english: O King, O best among the Bharatas, know that those kings who are of the Aila lineage, and similarly the Ikshvakus, comprise these one hundred and one clans.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 5. O King, O best among the Bharatas, know that those kings who are of the Aila lineage, and similarly the Ikshvakus, comprise these one hundred and one clans.

sanskrit: ययातेस्त्वेव भोजानां विस्तरोऽतिगुणो महान् ।भजते च महाराज विस्तरः स चतुर्दिशम् ॥६॥
transliteration: yayātestveva bhojānāṁ vistaro'tiguṇo mahān ,bhajate ca mahārāja vistaraḥ sa caturdiśam.
english: O great king, indeed, the lineage of Yayati, [which comprises] the Bhojas, is very extensive and possessed of excellent qualities. That widespread lineage extends in all four directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 6. O great king, indeed, the lineage of Yayati, [which comprises] the Bhojas, is very extensive and possessed of excellent qualities. That widespread lineage extends in all four directions.

sanskrit: तेषां तथैव तां लक्ष्मीं सर्वक्षत्रमुपासते ।सोऽवनीं मध्यमां भुक्त्वा मिथोभेदेष्वमन्यत ॥७॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ tathaiva tāṁ lakṣmīṁ sarvakṣatramupāsate ,so'vanīṁ madhyamāṁ bhuktvā mithobhedeṣvamanyata.
english: Similarly, all the warrior class (kṣatra) attended upon their glory and prosperity (lakṣmī). Having ruled the central earth (avanī), he then engaged in mutual divisions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 7. Similarly, all the warrior class (kṣatra) attended upon their glory and prosperity (lakṣmī). Having ruled the central earth (avanī), he then engaged in mutual divisions.

sanskrit: चतुर्युस्त्वपरो राजा यस्मिन्नेकशतोऽभवत् ।स साम्राज्यं जरासंधः प्राप्तो भवति योनितः ॥८॥
transliteration: caturyustvaparo rājā yasminnekaśato'bhavat ,sa sāmrājyaṁ jarāsaṁdhaḥ prāpto bhavati yonitaḥ.
english: There was another king, Caturyu, in whose reign there were one hundred and one [clans]. That Jarasandha obtained the empire by birthright (yonitaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 8. There was another king, Caturyu, in whose reign there were one hundred and one [clans]. That Jarasandha obtained the empire by birthright (yonitaḥ).

sanskrit: तं स राजा महाप्राज्ञ संश्रित्य किल सर्वशः ।राजन्सेनापतिर्जातः शिशुपालः प्रतापवान् ॥९॥
transliteration: taṁ sa rājā mahāprājña saṁśritya kila sarvaśaḥ ,rājansenāpatirjātaḥ śiśupālaḥ pratāpavān.
english: Indeed, having completely sought refuge with him, that greatly wise King Śiśupāla became a mighty commander-in-chief, O King.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 9. Indeed, having completely sought refuge with him, that greatly wise King Śiśupāla became a mighty commander-in-chief, O King.

sanskrit: तमेव च महाराज शिष्यवत्समुपस्थितः ।वक्रः करूषाधिपतिर्मायायोधी महाबलः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tameva ca mahārāja śiṣyavatsamupasthitaḥ ,vakraḥ karūṣādhipatirmāyāyodhī mahābalaḥ.
english: And that Vakra, the ruler of Karūṣa, who fought with illusion and possessed great strength, also attended upon him like a disciple, O great King.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 10. And that Vakra, the ruler of Karūṣa, who fought with illusion and possessed great strength, also attended upon him like a disciple, O great King.

sanskrit: अपरौ च महावीर्यौ महात्मानौ समाश्रितौ ।जरासंधं महावीर्यं तौ हंसडिभकावुभौ ॥११॥
transliteration: aparau ca mahāvīryau mahātmānau samāśritau ,jarāsaṁdhaṁ mahāvīryaṁ tau haṁsaḍibhakāvubhau.
english: Also, those two others, Haṃsa and Ḍimbaka, both exceedingly mighty and magnanimous, sought refuge with the immensely powerful Jarāsandha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 11. Also, those two others, Haṃsa and Ḍimbaka, both exceedingly mighty and magnanimous, sought refuge with the immensely powerful Jarāsandha.

sanskrit: दन्तवक्रः करूषश्च कलभो मेघवाहनः ।मूर्ध्ना दिव्यं मणिं बिभ्रद्यं तं भूतमणिं विदुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: dantavakraḥ karūṣaśca kalabho meghavāhanaḥ ,mūrdhnā divyaṁ maṇiṁ bibhradyaṁ taṁ bhūtamaṇiṁ viduḥ.
english: Dantavakra, Karūṣa, Kalabha, and Meghavāhana, the latter bearing on his head a divine gem which they know as the 'Bhūtamaṇi'.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 12. Dantavakra, Karūṣa, Kalabha, and Meghavāhana, the latter bearing on his head a divine gem which they know as the 'Bhūtamaṇi'.

sanskrit: मुरं च नरकं चैव शास्ति यो यवनाधिपौ ।अपर्यन्तबलो राजा प्रतीच्यां वरुणो यथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: muraṁ ca narakaṁ caiva śāsti yo yavanādhipau ,aparyantabalo rājā pratīcyāṁ varuṇo yathā.
english: He who governs Murā and Naraka, the two Yavana chiefs, is a king of boundless strength, just like Varuṇa in the western direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 13. He who governs Murā and Naraka, the two Yavana chiefs, is a king of boundless strength, just like Varuṇa in the western direction.

sanskrit: भगदत्तो महाराज वृद्धस्तव पितुः सखा ।स वाचा प्रणतस्तस्य कर्मणा चैव भारत ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhagadatto mahārāja vṛddhastava pituḥ sakhā ,sa vācā praṇatastasya karmaṇā caiva bhārata.
english: O great king, Bhagadatta is an elder and a friend of your father. He is devoted to him (Duryodhana) by both word and deed (karma), O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 14. O great king, Bhagadatta is an elder and a friend of your father. He is devoted to him (Duryodhana) by both word and deed (karma), O Bhārata.

sanskrit: स्नेहबद्धस्तु पितृवन्मनसा भक्तिमांस्त्वयि ।प्रतीच्यां दक्षिणं चान्तं पृथिव्याः पाति यो नृपः ॥१५॥
transliteration: snehabaddhastu pitṛvanmanasā bhaktimāṁstvayi ,pratīcyāṁ dakṣiṇaṁ cāntaṁ pṛthivyāḥ pāti yo nṛpaḥ.
english: Though bound by affection and like a father, he is devoted (bhakti) to you in his heart. He is the king who protects the western and southern regions of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 15. Though bound by affection and like a father, he is devoted (bhakti) to you in his heart. He is the king who protects the western and southern regions of the earth.

sanskrit: मातुलो भवतः शूरः पुरुजित्कुन्तिवर्धनः ।स ते संनतिमानेकः स्नेहतः शत्रुतापनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: mātulo bhavataḥ śūraḥ purujitkuntivardhanaḥ ,sa te saṁnatimānekaḥ snehataḥ śatrutāpanaḥ.
english: Your valorous maternal uncle, Purujit, who augments Kunti's glory, is uniquely respectful towards you due to his affection, and he is a tormentor of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 16. Your valorous maternal uncle, Purujit, who augments Kunti's glory, is uniquely respectful towards you due to his affection, and he is a tormentor of enemies.

sanskrit: जरासंधं गतस्त्वेवं पुरा यो न मया हतः ।पुरुषोत्तमविज्ञातो योऽसौ चेदिषु दुर्मतिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdhaṁ gatastvevaṁ purā yo na mayā hataḥ ,puruṣottamavijñāto yo'sau cediṣu durmatiḥ.
english: That evil-minded one among the Cedis, who was formerly not killed by me when he went to Jarāsandha, is now known as the supreme person (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 17. That evil-minded one among the Cedis, who was formerly not killed by me when he went to Jarāsandha, is now known as the supreme person (puruṣa).

sanskrit: आत्मानं प्रतिजानाति लोकेऽस्मिन्पुरुषोत्तमम् ।आदत्ते सततं मोहाद्यः स चिह्नं च मामकम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ātmānaṁ pratijānāti loke'sminpuruṣottamam ,ādatte satataṁ mohādyaḥ sa cihnaṁ ca māmakam.
english: In this world, he who declares himself to be the supreme person (puruṣa) and, out of delusion, constantly assumes my emblem as well.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 18. In this world, he who declares himself to be the supreme person (puruṣa) and, out of delusion, constantly assumes my emblem as well.

sanskrit: वङ्गपुण्ड्रकिरातेषु राजा बलसमन्वितः ।पौण्ड्रको वासुदेवेति योऽसौ लोकेषु विश्रुतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: vaṅgapuṇḍrakirāteṣu rājā balasamanvitaḥ ,pauṇḍrako vāsudeveti yo'sau lokeṣu viśrutaḥ.
english: This Puṇḍraka, a mighty king among the Vaṅgas, Puṇḍras, and Kirātas, is the one who is renowned in the world as 'Vāsudeva'.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 19. This Puṇḍraka, a mighty king among the Vaṅgas, Puṇḍras, and Kirātas, is the one who is renowned in the world as 'Vāsudeva'.

sanskrit: चतुर्युः स महाराज भोज इन्द्रसखो बली ।विद्याबलाद्यो व्यजयत्पाण्ड्यक्रथककैशिकान् ॥२०॥
transliteration: caturyuḥ sa mahārāja bhoja indrasakho balī ,vidyābalādyo vyajayatpāṇḍyakrathakakaiśikān.
english: He, O great king, is Bhoja, the powerful friend of Indra, who, with his four-fold might, conquered the Pāṇḍyas, Krathakas, and Kaiśikas by the strength of his knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 20. He, O great king, is Bhoja, the powerful friend of Indra, who, with his four-fold might, conquered the Pāṇḍyas, Krathakas, and Kaiśikas by the strength of his knowledge.

sanskrit: भ्राता यस्याहृतिः शूरो जामदग्न्यसमो युधि ।स भक्तो मागधं राजा भीष्मकः परवीरहा ॥२१॥
transliteration: bhrātā yasyāhṛtiḥ śūro jāmadagnyasamo yudhi ,sa bhakto māgadhaṁ rājā bhīṣmakaḥ paravīrahā.
english: King Bhīṣmaka, the slayer of enemy heroes, whose brother is valiant and whose might (āhṛti) in battle is equal to that of Paraśurāma (Jāmadagnya), is a follower of the Magadhan king (Jarāsandha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 21. King Bhīṣmaka, the slayer of enemy heroes, whose brother is valiant and whose might (āhṛti) in battle is equal to that of Paraśurāma (Jāmadagnya), is a follower of the Magadhan king (Jarāsandha).

sanskrit: प्रियाण्याचरतः प्रह्वान्सदा संबन्धिनः सतः ।भजतो न भजत्यस्मानप्रियेषु व्यवस्थितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: priyāṇyācarataḥ prahvānsadā saṁbandhinaḥ sataḥ ,bhajato na bhajatyasmānapriyeṣu vyavasthitaḥ.
english: Even though we perform favors, are always kinsmen (saṃbandhin), are good, and are devoted (bhakti), he, being engaged in disagreeable matters, does not favor us, who are humble (prahva).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 22. Even though we perform favors, are always kinsmen (saṃbandhin), are good, and are devoted (bhakti), he, being engaged in disagreeable matters, does not favor us, who are humble (prahva).

sanskrit: न कुलं न बलं राजन्नभिजानंस्तथात्मनः ।पश्यमानो यशो दीप्तं जरासंधमुपाश्रितः ॥२३॥
transliteration: na kulaṁ na balaṁ rājannabhijānaṁstathātmanaḥ ,paśyamāno yaśo dīptaṁ jarāsaṁdhamupāśritaḥ.
english: O King, not recognizing his own lineage (kula), nor his own strength (bala), nor his true nature (ātman), and seeing the brilliant fame (yaśas) of Jarāsandha, he sought refuge with him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 23. O King, not recognizing his own lineage (kula), nor his own strength (bala), nor his true nature (ātman), and seeing the brilliant fame (yaśas) of Jarāsandha, he sought refuge with him.

sanskrit: उदीच्यभोजाश्च तथा कुलान्यष्टादशाभिभो ।जरासंधभयादेव प्रतीचीं दिशमाश्रिताः ॥२४॥
transliteration: udīcyabhojāśca tathā kulānyaṣṭādaśābhibho ,jarāsaṁdhabhayādeva pratīcīṁ diśamāśritāḥ.
english: And also, O Lord (abhibho), the Northern Bhojas (udīcyabhoja) and eighteen other tribes, out of fear of Jarāsandha, resorted to the western direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 24. And also, O Lord (abhibho), the Northern Bhojas (udīcyabhoja) and eighteen other tribes, out of fear of Jarāsandha, resorted to the western direction.

sanskrit: शूरसेना भद्रकारा बोधाः शाल्वाः पटच्चराः ।सुस्थराश्च सुकुट्टाश्च कुणिन्दाः कुन्तिभिः सह ॥२५॥
transliteration: śūrasenā bhadrakārā bodhāḥ śālvāḥ paṭaccarāḥ ,sustharāśca sukuṭṭāśca kuṇindāḥ kuntibhiḥ saha.
english: The Śūrasenas, Bhadrakāras, Bodhas, Śālvas, Paṭaccaras, Sustharas, and Sukuṭṭas, along with the Kuṇindas and Kuntis (people).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 25. The Śūrasenas, Bhadrakāras, Bodhas, Śālvas, Paṭaccaras, Sustharas, and Sukuṭṭas, along with the Kuṇindas and Kuntis (people).

sanskrit: शाल्वेयानां च राजानः सोदर्यानुचरैः सह ।दक्षिणा ये च पाञ्चालाः पूर्वाः कुन्तिषु कोशलाः ॥२६॥
transliteration: śālveyānāṁ ca rājānaḥ sodaryānucaraiḥ saha ,dakṣiṇā ye ca pāñcālāḥ pūrvāḥ kuntiṣu kośalāḥ.
english: And the kings of the Śālveyas, along with their brothers and followers, (as well as) the southern Pañcālas and the eastern Kosalas residing among the Kuntis (people).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 26. And the kings of the Śālveyas, along with their brothers and followers, (as well as) the southern Pañcālas and the eastern Kosalas residing among the Kuntis (people).

sanskrit: तथोत्तरां दिशं चापि परित्यज्य भयार्दिताः ।मत्स्याः संन्यस्तपादाश्च दक्षिणां दिशमाश्रिताः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tathottarāṁ diśaṁ cāpi parityajya bhayārditāḥ ,matsyāḥ saṁnyastapādāśca dakṣiṇāṁ diśamāśritāḥ.
english: Thus, afflicted by fear, the Matsyas, having abandoned the northern region and having settled elsewhere, also took refuge in the southern region.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 27. Thus, afflicted by fear, the Matsyas, having abandoned the northern region and having settled elsewhere, also took refuge in the southern region.

sanskrit: तथैव सर्वपाञ्चाला जरासंधभयार्दिताः ।स्वराष्ट्रं संपरित्यज्य विद्रुताः सर्वतोदिशम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: tathaiva sarvapāñcālā jarāsaṁdhabhayārditāḥ ,svarāṣṭraṁ saṁparityajya vidrutāḥ sarvatodiśam.
english: In the same way, all the Pañcālas, afflicted by fear of Jarāsaṃdha, completely abandoned their own kingdom and scattered in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 28. In the same way, all the Pañcālas, afflicted by fear of Jarāsaṃdha, completely abandoned their own kingdom and scattered in all directions.

sanskrit: कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य कंसो निर्मथ्य बान्धवान् ।बार्हद्रथसुते देव्यावुपागच्छद्वृथामतिः ॥२९॥
transliteration: kasyacittvatha kālasya kaṁso nirmathya bāndhavān ,bārhadrathasute devyāvupāgacchadvṛthāmatiḥ.
english: Then, after some time, Kaṃsa, having subjugated his relatives, approached the two daughters of Bṛhadratha, as his intellect was misguided.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 29. Then, after some time, Kaṃsa, having subjugated his relatives, approached the two daughters of Bṛhadratha, as his intellect was misguided.

sanskrit: अस्तिः प्राप्तिश्च नाम्ना ते सहदेवानुजेऽबले ।बलेन तेन स ज्ञातीनभिभूय वृथामतिः ॥३०॥
transliteration: astiḥ prāptiśca nāmnā te sahadevānuje'bale ,balena tena sa jñātīnabhibhūya vṛthāmatiḥ.
english: They were Asti and Prāpti by name, the two powerless younger sisters of Sahadeva. With that strength, he (Kaṃsa), being of misguided intellect, overpowered his kinsmen.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 30. They were Asti and Prāpti by name, the two powerless younger sisters of Sahadeva. With that strength, he (Kaṃsa), being of misguided intellect, overpowered his kinsmen.

sanskrit: श्रैष्ठ्यं प्राप्तः स तस्यासीदतीवापनयो महान् ।भोजराजन्यवृद्धैस्तु पीड्यमानैर्दुरात्मना ॥३१॥
transliteration: śraiṣṭhyaṁ prāptaḥ sa tasyāsīdatīvāpanayo mahān ,bhojarājanyavṛddhaistu pīḍyamānairdurātmanā.
english: Having attained supremacy, he became a very great source of misconduct for her. Indeed, he was a wicked person, tormenting the elders of the Bhoja and other royal families.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 31. Having attained supremacy, he became a very great source of misconduct for her. Indeed, he was a wicked person, tormenting the elders of the Bhoja and other royal families.

sanskrit: ज्ञातित्राणमभीप्सद्भिरस्मत्संभावना कृता ।दत्त्वाक्रूराय सुतनुं तामाहुकसुतां तदा ॥३२॥
transliteration: jñātitrāṇamabhīpsadbhirasmatsaṁbhāvanā kṛtā ,dattvākrūrāya sutanuṁ tāmāhukasutāṁ tadā.
english: Wishing for the protection of kinsmen, an appeal was made to us. Therefore, giving that fair-limbed daughter of Āhuka to Akrūra then...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 32. Wishing for the protection of kinsmen, an appeal was made to us. Therefore, giving that fair-limbed daughter of Āhuka to Akrūra then...

sanskrit: संकर्षणद्वितीयेन ज्ञातिकार्यं मया कृतम् ।हतौ कंससुनामानौ मया रामेण चाप्युत ॥३३॥
transliteration: saṁkarṣaṇadvitīyena jñātikāryaṁ mayā kṛtam ,hatau kaṁsasunāmānau mayā rāmeṇa cāpyuta.
english: I, accompanied by Saṅkarṣaṇa, have fulfilled the duty (dharma) towards our kinsmen. Kaṃsa and Sunāman were killed by me, and indeed also by Rāma.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 33. I, accompanied by Saṅkarṣaṇa, have fulfilled the duty (dharma) towards our kinsmen. Kaṃsa and Sunāman were killed by me, and indeed also by Rāma.

sanskrit: भये तु समुपक्रान्ते जरासंधे समुद्यते ।मन्त्रोऽयं मन्त्रितो राजन्कुलैरष्टादशावरैः ॥३४॥
transliteration: bhaye tu samupakrānte jarāsaṁdhe samudyate ,mantro'yaṁ mantrito rājankulairaṣṭādaśāvaraiḥ.
english: But when fear approached and Jarāsaṃdha rose in readiness for battle, O King, this counsel (mantra) was deliberated upon by the eighteen clans.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 34. But when fear approached and Jarāsaṃdha rose in readiness for battle, O King, this counsel (mantra) was deliberated upon by the eighteen clans.

sanskrit: अनारमन्तो निघ्नन्तो महास्त्रैः शतघातिभिः ।न हन्याम वयं तस्य त्रिभिर्वर्षशतैर्बलम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: anāramanto nighnanto mahāstraiḥ śataghātibhiḥ ,na hanyāma vayaṁ tasya tribhirvarṣaśatairbalam.
english: Even if we were to continuously engage in battle, striking with powerful weapons that can slay hundreds, we would still not be able to destroy his army (balam) even in three hundred years.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 35. Even if we were to continuously engage in battle, striking with powerful weapons that can slay hundreds, we would still not be able to destroy his army (balam) even in three hundred years.

sanskrit: तस्य ह्यमरसंकाशौ बलेन बलिनां वरौ ।नामभ्यां हंसडिभकावित्यास्तां योधसत्तमौ ॥३६॥
transliteration: tasya hyamarasaṁkāśau balena balināṁ varau ,nāmabhyāṁ haṁsaḍibhakāvityāstāṁ yodhasattamau.
english: Indeed, among his (Jarāsaṃdha's) men, there were two excellent warriors (yodhasattamau) named Haṃsa and Ḍibhaka, who were like gods and foremost in strength among the powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 36. Indeed, among his (Jarāsaṃdha's) men, there were two excellent warriors (yodhasattamau) named Haṃsa and Ḍibhaka, who were like gods and foremost in strength among the powerful.

sanskrit: तावुभौ सहितौ वीरौ जरासंधश्च वीर्यवान् ।त्रयस्त्रयाणां लोकानां पर्याप्ता इति मे मतिः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tāvubhau sahitau vīrau jarāsaṁdhaśca vīryavān ,trayastrayāṇāṁ lokānāṁ paryāptā iti me matiḥ.
english: Those two heroes, united, along with the powerful Jarasandha - these three, I believe, are formidable enough to contend with the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 37. Those two heroes, united, along with the powerful Jarasandha - these three, I believe, are formidable enough to contend with the three worlds.

sanskrit: न हि केवलमस्माकं यावन्तोऽन्ये च पार्थिवाः ।तथैव तेषामासीच्च बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतां वर ॥३८॥
transliteration: na hi kevalamasmākaṁ yāvanto'nye ca pārthivāḥ ,tathaiva teṣāmāsīcca buddhirbuddhimatāṁ vara.
english: It was not only our opinion, but also the opinion of many other kings, O best among the intelligent (buddhimatām vara).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 38. It was not only our opinion, but also the opinion of many other kings, O best among the intelligent (buddhimatām vara).

sanskrit: अथ हंस इति ख्यातः कश्चिदासीन्महान्नृपः ।स चान्यैः सहितो राजन्संग्रामेऽष्टादशावरैः ॥३९॥
transliteration: atha haṁsa iti khyātaḥ kaścidāsīnmahānnṛpaḥ ,sa cānyaiḥ sahito rājansaṁgrāme'ṣṭādaśāvaraiḥ.
english: Now, there was a great king renowned as Haṃsa. He, O King, along with other (kings), with eighteen (allies) or fewer, in battle...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 39. Now, there was a great king renowned as Haṃsa. He, O King, along with other (kings), with eighteen (allies) or fewer, in battle...

sanskrit: हतो हंस इति प्रोक्तमथ केनापि भारत ।तच्छ्रुत्वा डिभको राजन्यमुनाम्भस्यमज्जत ॥४०॥
transliteration: hato haṁsa iti proktamatha kenāpi bhārata ,tacchrutvā ḍibhako rājanyamunāmbhasyamajjata.
english: Someone then said, 'Haṃsa is killed,' O Bhārata. Hearing that, Diṃbhaka, O King, drowned himself in the waters of the Yamuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 40. Someone then said, 'Haṃsa is killed,' O Bhārata. Hearing that, Diṃbhaka, O King, drowned himself in the waters of the Yamuna.

sanskrit: विना हंसेन लोकेऽस्मिन्नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे ।इत्येतां मतिमास्थाय डिभको निधनं गतः ॥४१॥
transliteration: vinā haṁsena loke'sminnāhaṁ jīvitumutsahe ,ityetāṁ matimāsthāya ḍibhako nidhanaṁ gataḥ.
english: “Without Haṃsa, I cannot bear to live in this world.” Having adopted this resolve, Diṃbhaka died.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 41. “Without Haṃsa, I cannot bear to live in this world.” Having adopted this resolve, Diṃbhaka died.

sanskrit: तथा तु डिभकं श्रुत्वा हंसः परपुरंजयः ।प्रपेदे यमुनामेव सोऽपि तस्यां न्यमज्जत ॥४२॥
transliteration: tathā tu ḍibhakaṁ śrutvā haṁsaḥ parapuraṁjayaḥ ,prapede yamunāmeva so'pi tasyāṁ nyamajjata.
english: Indeed, having heard about Diṃbhaka, Haṃsa, the conqueror of enemy cities, himself went to the Yamunā, and he also drowned in it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 42. Indeed, having heard about Diṃbhaka, Haṃsa, the conqueror of enemy cities, himself went to the Yamunā, and he also drowned in it.

sanskrit: तौ स राजा जरासंधः श्रुत्वाप्सु निधनं गतौ ।स्वपुरं शूरसेनानां प्रययौ भरतर्षभ ॥४३॥
transliteration: tau sa rājā jarāsaṁdhaḥ śrutvāpsu nidhanaṁ gatau ,svapuraṁ śūrasenānāṁ prayayau bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, that king Jarāsaṃdha, having heard that those two (Haṃsa and Diṃbhaka) had died in the waters, returned to his own city, the city of the Śūrasenas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 43. O best of Bharatas, that king Jarāsaṃdha, having heard that those two (Haṃsa and Diṃbhaka) had died in the waters, returned to his own city, the city of the Śūrasenas.

sanskrit: ततो वयममित्रघ्न तस्मिन्प्रतिगते नृपे ।पुनरानन्दिताः सर्वे मथुरायां वसामहे ॥४४॥
transliteration: tato vayamamitraghna tasminpratigate nṛpe ,punarānanditāḥ sarve mathurāyāṁ vasāmahe.
english: O slayer of enemies, then, after that king had departed, all of us, gladdened once again, reside in Mathurā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 44. O slayer of enemies, then, after that king had departed, all of us, gladdened once again, reside in Mathurā.

sanskrit: यदा त्वभ्येत्य पितरं सा वै राजीवलोचना ।कंसभार्या जरासंधं दुहिता मागधं नृपम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: yadā tvabhyetya pitaraṁ sā vai rājīvalocanā ,kaṁsabhāryā jarāsaṁdhaṁ duhitā māgadhaṁ nṛpam.
english: When, however, she, the lotus-eyed one, Kamsa's wife and Jarasandha's daughter, approached her father, King Jarasandha of Magadha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 45. When, however, she, the lotus-eyed one, Kamsa's wife and Jarasandha's daughter, approached her father, King Jarasandha of Magadha.

sanskrit: चोदयत्येव राजेन्द्र पतिव्यसनदुःखिता ।पतिघ्नं मे जहीत्येवं पुनः पुनररिंदम ॥४६॥
transliteration: codayatyeva rājendra pativyasanaduḥkhitā ,patighnaṁ me jahītyevaṁ punaḥ punarariṁdama.
english: Distressed by the calamity of her husband, she repeatedly urges, 'Slay my husband's killer!' thus, O King of kings, O destroyer of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 46. Distressed by the calamity of her husband, she repeatedly urges, 'Slay my husband's killer!' thus, O King of kings, O destroyer of enemies.

sanskrit: ततो वयं महाराज तं मन्त्रं पूर्वमन्त्रितम् ।संस्मरन्तो विमनसो व्यपयाता नराधिप ॥४७॥
transliteration: tato vayaṁ mahārāja taṁ mantraṁ pūrvamantritam ,saṁsmaranto vimanaso vyapayātā narādhipa.
english: Then, O great king, O lord of men, remembering that previously discussed counsel (mantra), we became disheartened and departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 47. Then, O great king, O lord of men, remembering that previously discussed counsel (mantra), we became disheartened and departed.

sanskrit: पृथक्त्वेन द्रुता राजन्संक्षिप्य महतीं श्रियम् ।प्रपतामो भयात्तस्य सधनज्ञातिबान्धवाः ॥४८॥
transliteration: pṛthaktvena drutā rājansaṁkṣipya mahatīṁ śriyam ,prapatāmo bhayāttasya sadhanajñātibāndhavāḥ.
english: Scattered individually, O king, having abandoned our great prosperity (śrī), we fled out of fear of him, along with our wealth, kinsmen, and relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 48. Scattered individually, O king, having abandoned our great prosperity (śrī), we fled out of fear of him, along with our wealth, kinsmen, and relatives.

sanskrit: इति संचिन्त्य सर्वे स्म प्रतीचीं दिशमाश्रिताः ।कुशस्थलीं पुरीं रम्यां रैवतेनोपशोभिताम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: iti saṁcintya sarve sma pratīcīṁ diśamāśritāḥ ,kuśasthalīṁ purīṁ ramyāṁ raivatenopaśobhitām.
english: Having thoroughly considered this, all of us resorted to the western direction, settling in the charming city of Kuśasthalī, which was adorned by Mount Raivata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 49. Having thoroughly considered this, all of us resorted to the western direction, settling in the charming city of Kuśasthalī, which was adorned by Mount Raivata.

sanskrit: पुनर्निवेशनं तस्यां कृतवन्तो वयं नृप ।तथैव दुर्गसंस्कारं देवैरपि दुरासदम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: punarniveśanaṁ tasyāṁ kṛtavanto vayaṁ nṛpa ,tathaiva durgasaṁskāraṁ devairapi durāsadam.
english: O King, we re-established our settlement in that city and similarly constructed fortifications (saṃskāra) that were impregnable even for the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 50. O King, we re-established our settlement in that city and similarly constructed fortifications (saṃskāra) that were impregnable even for the gods.

sanskrit: स्त्रियोऽपि यस्यां युध्येयुः किं पुनर्वृष्णिपुंगवाः ।तस्यां वयममित्रघ्न निवसामोऽकुतोभयाः ॥५१॥
transliteration: striyo'pi yasyāṁ yudhyeyuḥ kiṁ punarvṛṣṇipuṁgavāḥ ,tasyāṁ vayamamitraghna nivasāmo'kutobhayāḥ.
english: In that city, even women would be capable of fighting, let alone the foremost warriors among the Vṛṣṇis! O slayer of enemies, we dwell there, fearless from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 51. In that city, even women would be capable of fighting, let alone the foremost warriors among the Vṛṣṇis! O slayer of enemies, we dwell there, fearless from all directions.

sanskrit: आलोक्य गिरिमुख्यं तं माधवीतीर्थमेव च ।माधवाः कुरुशार्दूल परां मुदमवाप्नुवन् ॥५२॥
transliteration: ālokya girimukhyaṁ taṁ mādhavītīrthameva ca ,mādhavāḥ kuruśārdūla parāṁ mudamavāpnuvan.
english: Having seen that chief mountain (Mount Raivata) and also the sacred bathing place of Mādhava (mādhavītīrtha), the Vṛṣṇis (Mādhavas), O tiger among the Kurus, attained supreme joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 52. Having seen that chief mountain (Mount Raivata) and also the sacred bathing place of Mādhava (mādhavītīrtha), the Vṛṣṇis (Mādhavas), O tiger among the Kurus, attained supreme joy.

sanskrit: एवं वयं जरासंधादादितः कृतकिल्बिषाः ।सामर्थ्यवन्तः संबन्धाद्भवन्तं समुपाश्रिताः ॥५३॥
transliteration: evaṁ vayaṁ jarāsaṁdhādāditaḥ kṛtakilbiṣāḥ ,sāmarthyavantaḥ saṁbandhādbhavantaṁ samupāśritāḥ.
english: Thus, we, having committed offenses against Jarāsandha from the very beginning, have, being powerful through our connection, sought refuge in you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 53. Thus, we, having committed offenses against Jarāsandha from the very beginning, have, being powerful through our connection, sought refuge in you.

sanskrit: त्रियोजनायतं सद्म त्रिस्कन्धं योजनादधि ।योजनान्ते शतद्वारं विक्रमक्रमतोरणम् ।अष्टादशावरैर्नद्धं क्षत्रियैर्युद्धदुर्मदैः ॥५४॥
transliteration: triyojanāyataṁ sadma triskandhaṁ yojanādadhi ,yojanānte śatadvāraṁ vikramakramatoraṇam ,aṣṭādaśāvarairnaddhaṁ kṣatriyairyuddhadurmadaiḥ.
english: The abode (sadma) is three yojanas long, three-storied, and more than a yojana tall. It has a hundred gates at a distance of one yojana, with archways marking valorous steps. It is fortified by no fewer than eighteen warriors (kṣatriyas) who are fiercely proud in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 54. The abode (sadma) is three yojanas long, three-storied, and more than a yojana tall. It has a hundred gates at a distance of one yojana, with archways marking valorous steps. It is fortified by no fewer than eighteen warriors (kṣatriyas) who are fiercely proud in battle.

sanskrit: अष्टादश सहस्राणि व्रातानां सन्ति नः कुले ।आहुकस्य शतं पुत्रा एकैकस्त्रिशतावरः ॥५५॥
transliteration: aṣṭādaśa sahasrāṇi vrātānāṁ santi naḥ kule ,āhukasya śataṁ putrā ekaikastriśatāvaraḥ.
english: There are eighteen thousand troops in our clan. Āhuka has a hundred sons, each of whom commands no fewer than three hundred (warriors).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 55. There are eighteen thousand troops in our clan. Āhuka has a hundred sons, each of whom commands no fewer than three hundred (warriors).

sanskrit: चारुदेष्णः सह भ्रात्रा चक्रदेवोऽथ सात्यकिः ।अहं च रौहिणेयश्च साम्बः शौरिसमो युधि ॥५६॥
transliteration: cārudeṣṇaḥ saha bhrātrā cakradevo'tha sātyakiḥ ,ahaṁ ca rauhiṇeyaśca sāmbaḥ śaurisamo yudhi.
english: Cārudeṣṇa with his brother, Cakradeva, and Sātyaki; and I, along with Rauhiṇeya (Balarāma) and Sāmba, are equal to Śauri (Kṛṣṇa) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 56. Cārudeṣṇa with his brother, Cakradeva, and Sātyaki; and I, along with Rauhiṇeya (Balarāma) and Sāmba, are equal to Śauri (Kṛṣṇa) in battle.

sanskrit: एवमेते रथाः सप्त राजन्नन्यान्निबोध मे ।कृतवर्मा अनाधृष्टिः समीकः समितिंजयः ॥५७॥
transliteration: evamete rathāḥ sapta rājannanyānnibodha me ,kṛtavarmā anādhṛṣṭiḥ samīkaḥ samitiṁjayaḥ.
english: O King, I have thus described these seven charioteers. Now, learn from me about others: Kṛtavarmā, Anādhṛṣṭi, Samīka, and Samitiṃjaya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 57. O King, I have thus described these seven charioteers. Now, learn from me about others: Kṛtavarmā, Anādhṛṣṭi, Samīka, and Samitiṃjaya.

sanskrit: कह्वः शङ्कुर्निदान्तश्च सप्तैवैते महारथाः ।पुत्रौ चान्धकभोजस्य वृद्धो राजा च ते दश ॥५८॥
transliteration: kahvaḥ śaṅkurnidāntaśca saptaivaite mahārathāḥ ,putrau cāndhakabhojasya vṛddho rājā ca te daśa.
english: Kahva, Śaṅku, and Nidānta - these seven are indeed great charioteers. And the two sons of Andhaka-Bhoja, along with the old king, make a total of ten.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 58. Kahva, Śaṅku, and Nidānta - these seven are indeed great charioteers. And the two sons of Andhaka-Bhoja, along with the old king, make a total of ten.

sanskrit: लोकसंहनना वीरा वीर्यवन्तो महाबलाः ।स्मरन्तो मध्यमं देशं वृष्णिमध्ये गतव्यथाः ॥५९॥
transliteration: lokasaṁhananā vīrā vīryavanto mahābalāḥ ,smaranto madhyamaṁ deśaṁ vṛṣṇimadhye gatavyathāḥ.
english: These heroes, who are the strength of the world, are valiant and immensely powerful. Residing among the Vṛṣṇis, they are free from distress and ever mindful of the central region.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 59. These heroes, who are the strength of the world, are valiant and immensely powerful. Residing among the Vṛṣṇis, they are free from distress and ever mindful of the central region.

sanskrit: स त्वं सम्राड्गुणैर्युक्तः सदा भरतसत्तम ।क्षत्रे सम्राजमात्मानं कर्तुमर्हसि भारत ॥६०॥
transliteration: sa tvaṁ samrāḍguṇairyuktaḥ sadā bharatasattama ,kṣatre samrājamātmānaṁ kartumarhasi bhārata.
english: O best among the Bharatas, you are indeed that sovereign (samrāṭ), always endowed with [noble] qualities. O Bhārata, you ought to establish yourself as an emperor (samrāṭ) in the realm of kingship (kṣatra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 60. O best among the Bharatas, you are indeed that sovereign (samrāṭ), always endowed with [noble] qualities. O Bhārata, you ought to establish yourself as an emperor (samrāṭ) in the realm of kingship (kṣatra).

sanskrit: न तु शक्यं जरासंधे जीवमाने महाबले ।राजसूयस्त्वया प्राप्तुमेषा राजन्मतिर्मम ॥६१॥
transliteration: na tu śakyaṁ jarāsaṁdhe jīvamāne mahābale ,rājasūyastvayā prāptumeṣā rājanmatirmama.
english: O King, it is not possible for you to obtain the royal consecration (rājasūya) while the mighty Jarāsandha is alive. This is my opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 61. O King, it is not possible for you to obtain the royal consecration (rājasūya) while the mighty Jarāsandha is alive. This is my opinion.

sanskrit: तेन रुद्धा हि राजानः सर्वे जित्वा गिरिव्रजे ।कन्दरायां गिरीन्द्रस्य सिंहेनेव महाद्विपाः ॥६२॥
transliteration: tena ruddhā hi rājānaḥ sarve jitvā girivraje ,kandarāyāṁ girīndrasya siṁheneva mahādvipāḥ.
english: Indeed, all those kings, having been conquered by him in Girivraja, are imprisoned in a mountain cave, just as mighty elephants are (imprisoned) by a lion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 62. Indeed, all those kings, having been conquered by him in Girivraja, are imprisoned in a mountain cave, just as mighty elephants are (imprisoned) by a lion.

sanskrit: सोऽपि राजा जरासंधो यियक्षुर्वसुधाधिपैः ।आराध्य हि महादेवं निर्जितास्तेन पार्थिवाः ॥६३॥
transliteration: so'pi rājā jarāsaṁdho yiyakṣurvasudhādhipaiḥ ,ārādhya hi mahādevaṁ nirjitāstena pārthivāḥ.
english: That King Jarāsandha, intending to offer rulers as a Vedic ritual (yajña), indeed conquered those kings after propitiating Mahādeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 63. That King Jarāsandha, intending to offer rulers as a Vedic ritual (yajña), indeed conquered those kings after propitiating Mahādeva.

sanskrit: स हि निर्जित्य निर्जित्य पार्थिवान्पृतनागतान् ।पुरमानीय बद्ध्वा च चकार पुरुषव्रजम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: sa hi nirjitya nirjitya pārthivānpṛtanāgatān ,puramānīya baddhvā ca cakāra puruṣavrajam.
english: Indeed, after repeatedly conquering the kings who advanced with their armies, he brought them to his city, bound them, and created a multitude of human captives.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 64. Indeed, after repeatedly conquering the kings who advanced with their armies, he brought them to his city, bound them, and created a multitude of human captives.

sanskrit: वयं चैव महाराज जरासंधभयात्तदा ।मथुरां संपरित्यज्य गता द्वारवतीं पुरीम् ॥६५॥
transliteration: vayaṁ caiva mahārāja jarāsaṁdhabhayāttadā ,mathurāṁ saṁparityajya gatā dvāravatīṁ purīm.
english: And indeed, O great king, at that time, out of fear of Jarāsaṃdha, we completely abandoned Mathurā and went to the city of Dvāravatī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 65. And indeed, O great king, at that time, out of fear of Jarāsaṃdha, we completely abandoned Mathurā and went to the city of Dvāravatī.

sanskrit: यदि त्वेनं महाराज यज्ञं प्राप्तुमिहेच्छसि ।यतस्व तेषां मोक्षाय जरासंधवधाय च ॥६६॥
transliteration: yadi tvenaṁ mahārāja yajñaṁ prāptumihecchasi ,yatasva teṣāṁ mokṣāya jarāsaṁdhavadhāya ca.
english: O great king, if you truly desire to perform this (Vedic ritual) (yajña) here, then strive for the liberation (mokṣa) of those (kings) and for the killing of Jarāsaṃdha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 66. O great king, if you truly desire to perform this (Vedic ritual) (yajña) here, then strive for the liberation (mokṣa) of those (kings) and for the killing of Jarāsaṃdha.

sanskrit: समारम्भो हि शक्योऽयं नान्यथा कुरुनन्दन ।राजसूयस्य कार्त्स्न्येन कर्तुं मतिमतां वर ॥६७॥
transliteration: samārambho hi śakyo'yaṁ nānyathā kurunandana ,rājasūyasya kārtsnyena kartuṁ matimatāṁ vara.
english: Indeed, this undertaking, O delight of the Kurus, is possible only in this way, and not otherwise. It is the best way for the wise to perform the Rājasūya sacrifice completely.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 67. Indeed, this undertaking, O delight of the Kurus, is possible only in this way, and not otherwise. It is the best way for the wise to perform the Rājasūya sacrifice completely.
