sanskrit: इत्येषा मे मती राजन्यथा वा मन्यसेऽनघ ।एवं गते ममाचक्ष्व स्वयं निश्चित्य हेतुभिः ॥६८॥
transliteration: ityeṣā me matī rājanyathā vā manyase'nagha ,evaṁ gate mamācakṣva svayaṁ niścitya hetubhiḥ.
english: This is my opinion, O king. Or, O sinless one, whatever you yourself consider fitting, after you have thus personally ascertained with reasons, please tell me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 13 Verse 68. This is my opinion, O king. Or, O sinless one, whatever you yourself consider fitting, after you have thus personally ascertained with reasons, please tell me.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।उक्तं त्वया बुद्धिमता यन्नान्यो वक्तुमर्हति ।संशयानां हि निर्मोक्ता त्वन्नान्यो विद्यते भुवि ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,uktaṁ tvayā buddhimatā yannānyo vaktumarhati ,saṁśayānāṁ hi nirmoktā tvannānyo vidyate bhuvi.
english: Yudhishthira said: 'What you, the wise one, have spoken, no one else is capable of uttering. Indeed, there is no one else on earth like you who can dispel doubts.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said: 'What you, the wise one, have spoken, no one else is capable of uttering. Indeed, there is no one else on earth like you who can dispel doubts.'

sanskrit: गृहे गृहे हि राजानः स्वस्य स्वस्य प्रियंकराः ।न च साम्राज्यमाप्तास्ते सम्राट्शब्दो हि कृत्स्नभाक् ॥२॥
transliteration: gṛhe gṛhe hi rājānaḥ svasya svasya priyaṁkarāḥ ,na ca sāmrājyamāptāste samrāṭśabdo hi kṛtsnabhāk.
english: Indeed, there are kings in every house, each pursuing their own interests. However, they do not achieve true sovereignty, for the title 'emperor' (samrāṭ) belongs only to one who possesses the entirety of the realm.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 2. Indeed, there are kings in every house, each pursuing their own interests. However, they do not achieve true sovereignty, for the title 'emperor' (samrāṭ) belongs only to one who possesses the entirety of the realm.

sanskrit: कथं परानुभावज्ञः स्वं प्रशंसितुमर्हति ।परेण समवेतस्तु यः प्रशस्तः स पूज्यते ॥३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ parānubhāvajñaḥ svaṁ praśaṁsitumarhati ,pareṇa samavetastu yaḥ praśastaḥ sa pūjyate.
english: How can one who understands the power and qualities of others praise oneself? However, a person praised by another is truly revered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 3. How can one who understands the power and qualities of others praise oneself? However, a person praised by another is truly revered.

sanskrit: विशाला बहुला भूमिर्बहुरत्नसमाचिता ।दूरं गत्वा विजानाति श्रेयो वृष्णिकुलोद्वह ॥४॥
transliteration: viśālā bahulā bhūmirbahuratnasamācitā ,dūraṁ gatvā vijānāti śreyo vṛṣṇikulodvaha.
english: O upholder of the Vrishni family, one truly comprehends the value of this vast and expansive earth, abundant with many jewels, only by traveling widely.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 4. O upholder of the Vrishni family, one truly comprehends the value of this vast and expansive earth, abundant with many jewels, only by traveling widely.

sanskrit: शममेव परं मन्ये न तु मोक्षाद्भवेच्छमः ।आरम्भे पारमेष्ठ्यं तु न प्राप्यमिति मे मतिः ॥५॥
transliteration: śamameva paraṁ manye na tu mokṣādbhavecchamaḥ ,ārambhe pārameṣṭhyaṁ tu na prāpyamiti me matiḥ.
english: I consider tranquility (śama) to be supreme, but tranquility (śama) does not arise from liberation (mokṣa). However, my opinion is that the highest state (pārameṣṭhya) is not attainable at the very beginning.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 5. I consider tranquility (śama) to be supreme, but tranquility (śama) does not arise from liberation (mokṣa). However, my opinion is that the highest state (pārameṣṭhya) is not attainable at the very beginning.

sanskrit: एवमेवाभिजानन्ति कुले जाता मनस्विनः ।कश्चित्कदाचिदेतेषां भवेच्छ्रेष्ठो जनार्दन ॥६॥
transliteration: evamevābhijānanti kule jātā manasvinaḥ ,kaścitkadācideteṣāṁ bhavecchreṣṭho janārdana.
english: High-minded individuals (manasvin) born in noble families understand things in this very way. However, O Janārdana, it is possible that occasionally someone among them might become the most excellent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 6. High-minded individuals (manasvin) born in noble families understand things in this very way. However, O Janārdana, it is possible that occasionally someone among them might become the most excellent.

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।अनारम्भपरो राजा वल्मीक इव सीदति ।दुर्बलश्चानुपायेन बलिनं योऽधितिष्ठति ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,anārambhaparo rājā valmīka iva sīdati ,durbalaścānupāyena balinaṁ yo'dhitiṣṭhati.
english: Bhīma said: "A king who avoids initiating actions decays like an anthill. Similarly, a weak person who, without proper means, tries to confront a strong opponent (will also fail)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 7. Bhīma said: "A king who avoids initiating actions decays like an anthill. Similarly, a weak person who, without proper means, tries to confront a strong opponent (will also fail)."

sanskrit: अतन्द्रितस्तु प्रायेण दुर्बलो बलिनं रिपुम् ।जयेत्सम्यङ्नयो राजन्नीत्यार्थानात्मनो हितान् ॥८॥
transliteration: atandritastu prāyeṇa durbalo balinaṁ ripum ,jayetsamyaṅnayo rājannītyārthānātmano hitān.
english: However, a diligent person, even if generally weak, can often conquer a strong enemy through sound policy. O King, one secures one's own beneficial objectives (artha) by means of policy (nīti).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 8. However, a diligent person, even if generally weak, can often conquer a strong enemy through sound policy. O King, one secures one's own beneficial objectives (artha) by means of policy (nīti).

sanskrit: कृष्णे नयो मयि बलं जयः पार्थे धनंजये ।मागधं साधयिष्यामो वयं त्रय इवाग्नयः ॥९॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇe nayo mayi balaṁ jayaḥ pārthe dhanaṁjaye ,māgadhaṁ sādhayiṣyāmo vayaṁ traya ivāgnayaḥ.
english: Krishna (Kṛṣṇa) possesses the strategy, I have the strength, and victory belongs to Partha (Pārtha), Dhananjaya. We three, like the (three) sacred fires, will subdue the Magadhan king (Māgadha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 9. Krishna (Kṛṣṇa) possesses the strategy, I have the strength, and victory belongs to Partha (Pārtha), Dhananjaya. We three, like the (three) sacred fires, will subdue the Magadhan king (Māgadha).

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।आदत्तेऽर्थपरो बालो नानुबन्धमवेक्षते ।तस्मादरिं न मृष्यन्ति बालमर्थपरायणम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,ādatte'rthaparo bālo nānubandhamavekṣate ,tasmādariṁ na mṛṣyanti bālamarthaparāyaṇam.
english: Krishna (Kṛṣṇa) said: An immature person (bāla) who is solely focused on personal gain does not consider the consequences. Therefore, people do not tolerate an enemy who is immature and exclusively intent on self-interest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 10. Krishna (Kṛṣṇa) said: An immature person (bāla) who is solely focused on personal gain does not consider the consequences. Therefore, people do not tolerate an enemy who is immature and exclusively intent on self-interest.

sanskrit: हित्वा करान्यौवनाश्वः पालनाच्च भगीरथः ।कार्तवीर्यस्तपोयोगाद्बलात्तु भरतो विभुः ।ऋद्ध्या मरुत्तस्तान्पञ्च सम्राज इति शुश्रुमः ॥११॥
transliteration: hitvā karānyauvanāśvaḥ pālanācca bhagīrathaḥ ,kārtavīryastapoyogādbalāttu bharato vibhuḥ ,ṛddhyā maruttastānpañca samrāja iti śuśrumaḥ.
english: We have heard that Yauvanāśva relinquished taxes; Bhagīratha (achieved greatness) through protection; Kārtavīrya through the power of asceticism (tapas) and spiritual discipline (yoga); Bharata, the mighty ruler (vibhu), through strength; and Marutta through prosperity. Thus, these five were known as emperors.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 11. We have heard that Yauvanāśva relinquished taxes; Bhagīratha (achieved greatness) through protection; Kārtavīrya through the power of asceticism (tapas) and spiritual discipline (yoga); Bharata, the mighty ruler (vibhu), through strength; and Marutta through prosperity. Thus, these five were known as emperors.

sanskrit: निग्राह्यलक्षणं प्राप्तो धर्मार्थनयलक्षणैः ।बार्हद्रथो जरासंधस्तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥१२॥
transliteration: nigrāhyalakṣaṇaṁ prāpto dharmārthanayalakṣaṇaiḥ ,bārhadratho jarāsaṁdhastadviddhi bharatarṣabha.
english: Jarasandha (Jarasandha), the son of Bṛhadratha (Bārhadratha), has attained the qualities marking him as one who should be suppressed, according to the principles of righteousness (dharma), material prosperity (artha), and policy (naya). O best of the Bharatas, know this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 12. Jarasandha (Jarasandha), the son of Bṛhadratha (Bārhadratha), has attained the qualities marking him as one who should be suppressed, according to the principles of righteousness (dharma), material prosperity (artha), and policy (naya). O best of the Bharatas, know this.

sanskrit: न चैनमनुरुध्यन्ते कुलान्येकशतं नृपाः ।तस्मादेतद्बलादेव साम्राज्यं कुरुतेऽद्य सः ॥१३॥
transliteration: na cainamanurudhyante kulānyekaśataṁ nṛpāḥ ,tasmādetadbalādeva sāmrājyaṁ kurute'dya saḥ.
english: And one hundred and one royal lineages do not obey him. Therefore, he now establishes his empire solely by his own strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 13. And one hundred and one royal lineages do not obey him. Therefore, he now establishes his empire solely by his own strength.

sanskrit: रत्नभाजो हि राजानो जरासंधमुपासते ।न च तुष्यति तेनापि बाल्यादनयमास्थितः ॥१४॥
transliteration: ratnabhājo hi rājāno jarāsaṁdhamupāsate ,na ca tuṣyati tenāpi bālyādanayamāsthitaḥ.
english: Indeed, kings who offer valuable possessions attend upon Jarāsandha. Yet, he, having resorted to improper conduct since childhood, is not satisfied even by that.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 14. Indeed, kings who offer valuable possessions attend upon Jarāsandha. Yet, he, having resorted to improper conduct since childhood, is not satisfied even by that.

sanskrit: मूर्धाभिषिक्तं नृपतिं प्रधानपुरुषं बलात् ।आदत्ते न च नो दृष्टोऽभागः पुरुषतः क्वचित् ॥१५॥
transliteration: mūrdhābhiṣiktaṁ nṛpatiṁ pradhānapuruṣaṁ balāt ,ādatte na ca no dṛṣṭo'bhāgaḥ puruṣataḥ kvacit.
english: He forcibly seizes a consecrated king, a principal leader. And we have never seen any person deprived of his share/kingdom by him anywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 15. He forcibly seizes a consecrated king, a principal leader. And we have never seen any person deprived of his share/kingdom by him anywhere.

sanskrit: एवं सर्वान्वशे चक्रे जरासंधः शतावरान् ।तं दुर्बलतरो राजा कथं पार्थ उपैष्यति ॥१६॥
transliteration: evaṁ sarvānvaśe cakre jarāsaṁdhaḥ śatāvarān ,taṁ durbalataro rājā kathaṁ pārtha upaiṣyati.
english: Thus, Jarāsandha brought all, numbering at least one hundred, under his control. O Pārtha, how will a weaker king approach him?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 16. Thus, Jarāsandha brought all, numbering at least one hundred, under his control. O Pārtha, how will a weaker king approach him?

sanskrit: प्रोक्षितानां प्रमृष्टानां राज्ञां पशुपतेर्गृहे ।पशूनामिव का प्रीतिर्जीविते भरतर्षभ ॥१७॥
transliteration: prokṣitānāṁ pramṛṣṭānāṁ rājñāṁ paśupatergṛhe ,paśūnāmiva kā prītirjīvite bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among the Bharatas, what joy can there be in life for kings who are consecrated and purified in the house of Paśupati, just like sacrificial animals?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 17. O best among the Bharatas, what joy can there be in life for kings who are consecrated and purified in the house of Paśupati, just like sacrificial animals?

sanskrit: क्षत्रियः शस्त्रमरणो यदा भवति सत्कृतः ।ननु स्म मागधं सर्वे प्रतिबाधेम यद्वयम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: kṣatriyaḥ śastramaraṇo yadā bhavati satkṛtaḥ ,nanu sma māgadhaṁ sarve pratibādhema yadvayam.
english: When a kṣatriya becomes honored by dying with a weapon, then surely all of us should resist the king of Magadha, since we are united.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 18. When a kṣatriya becomes honored by dying with a weapon, then surely all of us should resist the king of Magadha, since we are united.

sanskrit: षडशीतिः समानीताः शेषा राजंश्चतुर्दश ।जरासंधेन राजानस्ततः क्रूरं प्रपत्स्यते ॥१९॥
transliteration: ṣaḍaśītiḥ samānītāḥ śeṣā rājaṁścaturdaśa ,jarāsaṁdhena rājānastataḥ krūraṁ prapatsyate.
english: Eighty-six kings have been brought; O king, fourteen remain. Then Jarasandha will perpetrate a cruel act.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 19. Eighty-six kings have been brought; O king, fourteen remain. Then Jarasandha will perpetrate a cruel act.

sanskrit: प्राप्नुयात्स यशो दीप्तं तत्र यो विघ्नमाचरेत् ।जयेद्यश्च जरासंधं स सम्राण्नियतं भवेत् ॥२०॥
transliteration: prāpnuyātsa yaśo dīptaṁ tatra yo vighnamācaret ,jayedyaśca jarāsaṁdhaṁ sa samrāṇniyataṁ bhavet.
english: One who creates an obstacle there will achieve brilliant fame. And he who conquers Jarasandha will certainly become an emperor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 14 Verse 20. One who creates an obstacle there will achieve brilliant fame. And he who conquers Jarasandha will certainly become an emperor.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।सम्राड्गुणमभीप्सन्वै युष्मान्स्वार्थपरायणः ।कथं प्रहिणुयां भीमं बलात्केवलसाहसात् ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,samrāḍguṇamabhīpsanvai yuṣmānsvārthaparāyaṇaḥ ,kathaṁ prahiṇuyāṁ bhīmaṁ balātkevalasāhasāt.
english: Yudhishthira said: "As one who desires the qualities of an emperor (samrāṭ) and is devoted to his own interests, how can I send Bhima by force, based merely on recklessness (sāhasa)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said: "As one who desires the qualities of an emperor (samrāṭ) and is devoted to his own interests, how can I send Bhima by force, based merely on recklessness (sāhasa)?"

sanskrit: भीमार्जुनावुभौ नेत्रे मनो मन्ये जनार्दनम् ।मनश्चक्षुर्विहीनस्य कीदृशं जीवितं भवेत् ॥२॥
transliteration: bhīmārjunāvubhau netre mano manye janārdanam ,manaścakṣurvihīnasya kīdṛśaṁ jīvitaṁ bhavet.
english: Bhima and Arjuna are both my two eyes, and I consider Janardana (Krishna) to be my mind. What sort of life would a person have who is deprived of both mind and eyes?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 2. Bhima and Arjuna are both my two eyes, and I consider Janardana (Krishna) to be my mind. What sort of life would a person have who is deprived of both mind and eyes?

sanskrit: जरासंधबलं प्राप्य दुष्पारं भीमविक्रमम् ।श्रमो हि वः पराजय्यात्किमु तत्र विचेष्टितम् ॥३॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdhabalaṁ prāpya duṣpāraṁ bhīmavikramam ,śramo hi vaḥ parājayyātkimu tatra viceṣṭitam.
english: After encountering Jarasandha's army, which is difficult to overcome and possesses terrible prowess, any effort (śrama) on your part would indeed lead to defeat. What can possibly be attempted there?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 3. After encountering Jarasandha's army, which is difficult to overcome and possesses terrible prowess, any effort (śrama) on your part would indeed lead to defeat. What can possibly be attempted there?

sanskrit: अस्मिन्नर्थान्तरे युक्तमनर्थः प्रतिपद्यते ।यथाहं विमृशाम्येकस्तत्तावच्छ्रूयतां मम ॥४॥
transliteration: asminnarthāntare yuktamanarthaḥ pratipadyate ,yathāhaṁ vimṛśāmyekastattāvacchrūyatāṁ mama.
english: In this alternative situation, an appropriate misfortune (anartha) will befall us. Therefore, as I ponder this alone, let my words be heard for now.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 4. In this alternative situation, an appropriate misfortune (anartha) will befall us. Therefore, as I ponder this alone, let my words be heard for now.

sanskrit: संन्यासं रोचये साधु कार्यस्यास्य जनार्दन ।प्रतिहन्ति मनो मेऽद्य राजसूयो दुरासदः ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁnyāsaṁ rocaye sādhu kāryasyāsya janārdana ,pratihanti mano me'dya rājasūyo durāsadaḥ.
english: O Janārdana, I would truly prefer renunciation regarding this task. Today, the formidable Rājasūya sacrifice obstructs my mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 5. O Janārdana, I would truly prefer renunciation regarding this task. Today, the formidable Rājasūya sacrifice obstructs my mind.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।पार्थः प्राप्य धनुःश्रेष्ठमक्षय्यौ च महेषुधी ।रथं ध्वजं सभां चैव युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pārthaḥ prāpya dhanuḥśreṣṭhamakṣayyau ca maheṣudhī ,rathaṁ dhvajaṁ sabhāṁ caiva yudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Pārtha (Arjuna), having obtained the excellent bow, the two inexhaustible great quivers, the chariot, the banner, and the assembly hall, spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 6. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Pārtha (Arjuna), having obtained the excellent bow, the two inexhaustible great quivers, the chariot, the banner, and the assembly hall, spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: धनुरस्त्रं शरा वीर्यं पक्षो भूमिर्यशो बलम् ।प्राप्तमेतन्मया राजन्दुष्प्रापं यदभीप्सितम् ॥७॥
transliteration: dhanurastraṁ śarā vīryaṁ pakṣo bhūmiryaśo balam ,prāptametanmayā rājanduṣprāpaṁ yadabhīpsitam.
english: Bow, weapons, arrows, valor, allies, land, fame, and strength - all these, O King, which were desired and difficult to obtain, have been acquired by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 7. Bow, weapons, arrows, valor, allies, land, fame, and strength - all these, O King, which were desired and difficult to obtain, have been acquired by me.

sanskrit: कुले जन्म प्रशंसन्ति वैद्याः साधु सुनिष्ठिताः ।बलेन सदृशं नास्ति वीर्यं तु मम रोचते ॥८॥
transliteration: kule janma praśaṁsanti vaidyāḥ sādhu suniṣṭhitāḥ ,balena sadṛśaṁ nāsti vīryaṁ tu mama rocate.
english: Well-versed scholars truly praise birth in a noble family. There is nothing comparable to strength, but it is valor that pleases me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 8. Well-versed scholars truly praise birth in a noble family. There is nothing comparable to strength, but it is valor that pleases me.

sanskrit: कृतवीर्यकुले जातो निर्वीर्यः किं करिष्यति ।क्षत्रियः सर्वशो राजन्यस्य वृत्तिः पराजये ॥९॥
transliteration: kṛtavīryakule jāto nirvīryaḥ kiṁ kariṣyati ,kṣatriyaḥ sarvaśo rājanyasya vṛttiḥ parājaye.
english: What will a powerless man, born in the Kṛtavīrya family, accomplish? Such a kṣatriya, whose profession, in every respect, leads to the defeat of a kingly person (rājanya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 9. What will a powerless man, born in the Kṛtavīrya family, accomplish? Such a kṣatriya, whose profession, in every respect, leads to the defeat of a kingly person (rājanya).

sanskrit: सर्वैरपि गुणैर्हीनो वीर्यवान्हि तरेद्रिपून् ।सर्वैरपि गुणैर्युक्तो निर्वीर्यः किं करिष्यति ॥१०॥
transliteration: sarvairapi guṇairhīno vīryavānhi taredripūn ,sarvairapi guṇairyukto nirvīryaḥ kiṁ kariṣyati.
english: Indeed, a powerful (vīryavān) person, even if devoid of all qualities, can overcome enemies. What will a powerless (nirvīrya) person, endowed with all qualities, accomplish?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 10. Indeed, a powerful (vīryavān) person, even if devoid of all qualities, can overcome enemies. What will a powerless (nirvīrya) person, endowed with all qualities, accomplish?

sanskrit: द्रव्यभूता गुणाः सर्वे तिष्ठन्ति हि पराक्रमे ।जयस्य हेतुः सिद्धिर्हि कर्म दैवं च संश्रितम् ॥११॥
transliteration: dravyabhūtā guṇāḥ sarve tiṣṭhanti hi parākrame ,jayasya hetuḥ siddhirhi karma daivaṁ ca saṁśritam.
english: All qualities, having taken the form of substances, indeed reside in valor. For the cause of victory and its accomplishment depends on both action (karma) and destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 11. All qualities, having taken the form of substances, indeed reside in valor. For the cause of victory and its accomplishment depends on both action (karma) and destiny.

sanskrit: संयुक्तो हि बलैः कश्चित्प्रमादान्नोपयुज्यते ।तेन द्वारेण शत्रुभ्यः क्षीयते सबलो रिपुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: saṁyukto hi balaiḥ kaścitpramādānnopayujyate ,tena dvāreṇa śatrubhyaḥ kṣīyate sabalo ripuḥ.
english: Indeed, if someone, though equipped with forces, is not utilized due to negligence, then through that very weakness, a powerful foe (referring to the negligent person who becomes a liability) is destroyed for the benefit of the (actual) enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 12. Indeed, if someone, though equipped with forces, is not utilized due to negligence, then through that very weakness, a powerful foe (referring to the negligent person who becomes a liability) is destroyed for the benefit of the (actual) enemies.

sanskrit: दैन्यं यथाबलवति तथा मोहो बलान्विते ।तावुभौ नाशकौ हेतू राज्ञा त्याज्यौ जयार्थिना ॥१३॥
transliteration: dainyaṁ yathābalavati tathā moho balānvite ,tāvubhau nāśakau hetū rājñā tyājyau jayārthinā.
english: Just as dejection occurs in the powerful, similarly, delusion occurs in the strong. Both of these are destructive causes and must be abandoned by a king who desires victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 13. Just as dejection occurs in the powerful, similarly, delusion occurs in the strong. Both of these are destructive causes and must be abandoned by a king who desires victory.

sanskrit: जरासंधविनाशं च राज्ञां च परिमोक्षणम् ।यदि कुर्याम यज्ञार्थं किं ततः परमं भवेत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdhavināśaṁ ca rājñāṁ ca parimokṣaṇam ,yadi kuryāma yajñārthaṁ kiṁ tataḥ paramaṁ bhavet.
english: If we were to accomplish the destruction of Jarāsandha and the liberation of kings for the purpose of a ritual (yajña), what could be greater than that?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 14. If we were to accomplish the destruction of Jarāsandha and the liberation of kings for the purpose of a ritual (yajña), what could be greater than that?

sanskrit: अनारम्भे तु नियतो भवेदगुणनिश्चयः ।गुणान्निःसंशयाद्राजन्नैर्गुण्यं मन्यसे कथम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: anārambhe tu niyato bhavedaguṇaniścayaḥ ,guṇānniḥsaṁśayādrājannairguṇyaṁ manyase katham.
english: However, in non-action, there would certainly be a definite absence of positive results. O King, when (the potential for) merits is beyond doubt, how can you consider it as leading to demerit?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 15. However, in non-action, there would certainly be a definite absence of positive results. O King, when (the potential for) merits is beyond doubt, how can you consider it as leading to demerit?

sanskrit: काषायं सुलभं पश्चान्मुनीनां शममिच्छताम् ।साम्राज्यं तु तवेच्छन्तो वयं योत्स्यामहे परैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: kāṣāyaṁ sulabhaṁ paścānmunīnāṁ śamamicchatām ,sāmrājyaṁ tu tavecchanto vayaṁ yotsyāmahe paraiḥ.
english: A saffron robe is easily obtained later by sages desiring tranquility. But we, desiring your imperial rule, shall fight against enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 15 Verse 16. A saffron robe is easily obtained later by sages desiring tranquility. But we, desiring your imperial rule, shall fight against enemies.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।जातस्य भारते वंशे तथा कुन्त्याः सुतस्य च ।या वै युक्ता मतिः सेयमर्जुनेन प्रदर्शिता ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,jātasya bhārate vaṁśe tathā kuntyāḥ sutasya ca ,yā vai yuktā matiḥ seyamarjunena pradarśitā.
english: Vāsudeva said: This resolve (mati) demonstrated by Arjuna is indeed appropriate for one born in the Bhārata lineage and for the son of Kuntī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 1. Vāsudeva said: This resolve (mati) demonstrated by Arjuna is indeed appropriate for one born in the Bhārata lineage and for the son of Kuntī.

sanskrit: न मृत्योः समयं विद्म रात्रौ वा यदि वा दिवा ।न चापि कंचिदमरमयुद्धेनापि शुश्रुमः ॥२॥
transliteration: na mṛtyoḥ samayaṁ vidma rātrau vā yadi vā divā ,na cāpi kaṁcidamaramayuddhenāpi śuśrumaḥ.
english: We do not know the time of death, be it night or day. Nor have we heard of anyone becoming immortal (amaram) without engaging in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 2. We do not know the time of death, be it night or day. Nor have we heard of anyone becoming immortal (amaram) without engaging in battle.

sanskrit: एतावदेव पुरुषैः कार्यं हृदयतोषणम् ।नयेन विधिदृष्टेन यदुपक्रमते परान् ॥३॥
transliteration: etāvadeva puruṣaiḥ kāryaṁ hṛdayatoṣaṇam ,nayena vidhidṛṣṭena yadupakramate parān.
english: This much alone is the action (kāryam) for men that brings satisfaction to the heart: when one assails enemies (parān) with a policy (naya) that is in accordance with the law (vidhidṛṣṭena).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 3. This much alone is the action (kāryam) for men that brings satisfaction to the heart: when one assails enemies (parān) with a policy (naya) that is in accordance with the law (vidhidṛṣṭena).

sanskrit: सुनयस्यानपायस्य संयुगे परमः क्रमः ।संशयो जायते साम्ये साम्यं च न भवेद्द्वयोः ॥४॥
transliteration: sunayasyānapāyasya saṁyuge paramaḥ kramaḥ ,saṁśayo jāyate sāmye sāmyaṁ ca na bhaveddvayoḥ.
english: The supreme method (krama) in battle (saṃyuge) comes from a good (sunaya) and unfailing (anapāya) policy. Doubt (saṃśaya) arises from parity (sāmye), and there should be no equality (sāmyam) between two.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 4. The supreme method (krama) in battle (saṃyuge) comes from a good (sunaya) and unfailing (anapāya) policy. Doubt (saṃśaya) arises from parity (sāmye), and there should be no equality (sāmyam) between two.

sanskrit: ते वयं नयमास्थाय शत्रुदेहसमीपगाः ।कथमन्तं न गच्छेम वृक्षस्येव नदीरयाः ।पररन्ध्रे पराक्रान्ताः स्वरन्ध्रावरणे स्थिताः ॥५॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ nayamāsthāya śatrudehasamīpagāḥ ,kathamantaṁ na gacchema vṛkṣasyeva nadīrayāḥ ,pararandhre parākrāntāḥ svarandhrāvaraṇe sthitāḥ.
english: Having adopted a sound policy and being close to the enemy's stronghold, how can we not bring about their destruction, just as powerful river currents can uproot a tree? We must act decisively against their vulnerabilities while maintaining a position that covers our own.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 5. Having adopted a sound policy and being close to the enemy's stronghold, how can we not bring about their destruction, just as powerful river currents can uproot a tree? We must act decisively against their vulnerabilities while maintaining a position that covers our own.

sanskrit: व्यूढानीकैरनुबलैर्नोपेयाद्बलवत्तरम् ।इति बुद्धिमतां नीतिस्तन्ममापीह रोचते ॥६॥
transliteration: vyūḍhānīkairanubalairnopeyādbalavattaram ,iti buddhimatāṁ nītistanmamāpīha rocate.
english: One should not engage with a more powerful foe, even if one has arrayed armies and auxiliary forces. This is the maxim (nīti) of the discerning, and it is also pleasing to me here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 6. One should not engage with a more powerful foe, even if one has arrayed armies and auxiliary forces. This is the maxim (nīti) of the discerning, and it is also pleasing to me here.

sanskrit: अनवद्या ह्यसंबुद्धाः प्रविष्टाः शत्रुसद्म तत् ।शत्रुदेहमुपाक्रम्य तं कामं प्राप्नुयामहे ॥७॥
transliteration: anavadyā hyasaṁbuddhāḥ praviṣṭāḥ śatrusadma tat ,śatrudehamupākramya taṁ kāmaṁ prāpnuyāmahe.
english: Indeed, being faultless and undetected, having entered that enemy's dwelling, we shall achieve our desired objective by assaulting their very core (position).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 7. Indeed, being faultless and undetected, having entered that enemy's dwelling, we shall achieve our desired objective by assaulting their very core (position).

sanskrit: एको ह्येव श्रियं नित्यं बिभर्ति पुरुषर्षभ ।अन्तरात्मेव भूतानां तत्क्षये वै बलक्षयः ॥८॥
transliteration: eko hyeva śriyaṁ nityaṁ bibharti puruṣarṣabha ,antarātmeva bhūtānāṁ tatkṣaye vai balakṣayaḥ.
english: Indeed, only one individual perpetually sustains prosperity, O best of men. Just as the inner self (ātman) pervades and sustains all beings, so too, upon that individual's downfall, there is certainly a decline of power.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 8. Indeed, only one individual perpetually sustains prosperity, O best of men. Just as the inner self (ātman) pervades and sustains all beings, so too, upon that individual's downfall, there is certainly a decline of power.

sanskrit: अथ चेत्तं निहत्याजौ शेषेणाभिसमागताः ।प्राप्नुयाम ततः स्वर्गं ज्ञातित्राणपरायणाः ॥९॥
transliteration: atha cettaṁ nihatyājau śeṣeṇābhisamāgatāḥ ,prāpnuyāma tataḥ svargaṁ jñātitrāṇaparāyaṇāḥ.
english: And if, intent on saving our kinsmen, we were to kill him in battle and unite with the remaining (allies), we would then attain heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 9. And if, intent on saving our kinsmen, we were to kill him in battle and unite with the remaining (allies), we would then attain heaven.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कृष्ण कोऽयं जरासंधः किंवीर्यः किंपराक्रमः ।यस्त्वां स्पृष्ट्वाग्निसदृशं न दग्धः शलभो यथा ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kṛṣṇa ko'yaṁ jarāsaṁdhaḥ kiṁvīryaḥ kiṁparākramaḥ ,yastvāṁ spṛṣṭvāgnisadṛśaṁ na dagdhaḥ śalabho yathā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O Kṛṣṇa, who is this Jarāsandha? What is his valor, and what is his prowess, that he, having touched you, who are like fire, was not burned, unlike a moth (which would be incinerated)?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 10. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O Kṛṣṇa, who is this Jarāsandha? What is his valor, and what is his prowess, that he, having touched you, who are like fire, was not burned, unlike a moth (which would be incinerated)?'

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।शृणु राजञ्जरासंधो यद्वीर्यो यत्पराक्रमः ।यथा चोपेक्षितोऽस्माभिर्बहुशः कृतविप्रियः ॥११॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,śṛṇu rājañjarāsaṁdho yadvīryo yatparākramaḥ ,yathā copekṣito'smābhirbahuśaḥ kṛtavipriyaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa said: 'Listen, O King, I shall tell you what Jarāsandha's valor and prowess are, and how he, despite repeatedly causing us displeasure, has been tolerated by us.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 11. Kṛṣṇa said: 'Listen, O King, I shall tell you what Jarāsandha's valor and prowess are, and how he, despite repeatedly causing us displeasure, has been tolerated by us.'

sanskrit: अक्षौहिणीनां तिसृणामासीत्समरदर्पितः ।राजा बृहद्रथो नाम मगधाधिपतिः पतिः ॥१२॥
transliteration: akṣauhiṇīnāṁ tisṛṇāmāsītsamaradarpitaḥ ,rājā bṛhadratho nāma magadhādhipatiḥ patiḥ.
english: There was a king named Bṛhadratha, the ruler of Magadha and master of three akṣauhiṇī divisions, who was arrogant in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 12. There was a king named Bṛhadratha, the ruler of Magadha and master of three akṣauhiṇī divisions, who was arrogant in battle.

sanskrit: रूपवान्वीर्यसंपन्नः श्रीमानतुलविक्रमः ।नित्यं दीक्षाकृशतनुः शतक्रतुरिवापरः ॥१३॥
transliteration: rūpavānvīryasaṁpannaḥ śrīmānatulavikramaḥ ,nityaṁ dīkṣākṛśatanuḥ śatakraturivāparaḥ.
english: He was handsome, endowed with valor, glorious, and of unequaled prowess. His body was always emaciated by sacred vows (dīkṣā), like another Indra (śatakratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 13. He was handsome, endowed with valor, glorious, and of unequaled prowess. His body was always emaciated by sacred vows (dīkṣā), like another Indra (śatakratu).

sanskrit: तेजसा सूर्यसदृशः क्षमया पृथिवीसमः ।यमान्तकसमः कोपे श्रिया वैश्रवणोपमः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tejasā sūryasadṛśaḥ kṣamayā pṛthivīsamaḥ ,yamāntakasamaḥ kope śriyā vaiśravaṇopamaḥ.
english: By his brilliance, he was like the sun; by his patience, he was equal to the earth. In anger, he was like Yama (Death) himself; and by his splendor (or wealth), he was comparable to Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 14. By his brilliance, he was like the sun; by his patience, he was equal to the earth. In anger, he was like Yama (Death) himself; and by his splendor (or wealth), he was comparable to Vaiśravaṇa (Kubera).

sanskrit: तस्याभिजनसंयुक्तैर्गुणैर्भरतसत्तम ।व्याप्तेयं पृथिवी सर्वा सूर्यस्येव गभस्तिभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasyābhijanasaṁyuktairguṇairbharatasattama ,vyāpteyaṁ pṛthivī sarvā sūryasyeva gabhastibhiḥ.
english: O best of the Bharatas, this entire earth is pervaded by his noble and excellent qualities, just as it is by the sun's rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 15. O best of the Bharatas, this entire earth is pervaded by his noble and excellent qualities, just as it is by the sun's rays.

sanskrit: स काशिराजस्य सुते यमजे भरतर्षभ ।उपयेमे महावीर्यो रूपद्रविणसंमते ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa kāśirājasya sute yamaje bharatarṣabha ,upayeme mahāvīryo rūpadraviṇasaṁmate.
english: O bull among the Bharatas, that exceedingly mighty one married the two twin daughters of the King of Kashi, who were renowned for their beauty and wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 16. O bull among the Bharatas, that exceedingly mighty one married the two twin daughters of the King of Kashi, who were renowned for their beauty and wealth.

sanskrit: तयोश्चकार समयं मिथः स पुरुषर्षभः ।नातिवर्तिष्य इत्येवं पत्नीभ्यां संनिधौ तदा ॥१७॥
transliteration: tayoścakāra samayaṁ mithaḥ sa puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,nātivartiṣya ityevaṁ patnībhyāṁ saṁnidhau tadā.
english: Then, that best among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ) made a mutual agreement in the presence of his two wives, declaring, 'I will not transgress [this pact].'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 17. Then, that best among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ) made a mutual agreement in the presence of his two wives, declaring, 'I will not transgress [this pact].'

sanskrit: स ताभ्यां शुशुभे राजा पत्नीभ्यां मनुजाधिप ।प्रियाभ्यामनुरूपाभ्यां करेणुभ्यामिव द्विपः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa tābhyāṁ śuśubhe rājā patnībhyāṁ manujādhipa ,priyābhyāmanurūpābhyāṁ kareṇubhyāmiva dvipaḥ.
english: That king, the lord of men (manujādhipa), appeared splendid with his two beloved and well-suited wives, just as an elephant (dvipaḥ) shines with two female elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 18. That king, the lord of men (manujādhipa), appeared splendid with his two beloved and well-suited wives, just as an elephant (dvipaḥ) shines with two female elephants.

sanskrit: तयोर्मध्यगतश्चापि रराज वसुधाधिपः ।गङ्गायमुनयोर्मध्ये मूर्तिमानिव सागरः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tayormadhyagataścāpi rarāja vasudhādhipaḥ ,gaṅgāyamunayormadhye mūrtimāniva sāgaraḥ.
english: And the lord of the earth (vasudhādhipaḥ), situated between those two (wives), appeared splendid, like the ocean (sāgaraḥ) personified, situated between the Ganga and Yamuna rivers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 19. And the lord of the earth (vasudhādhipaḥ), situated between those two (wives), appeared splendid, like the ocean (sāgaraḥ) personified, situated between the Ganga and Yamuna rivers.

sanskrit: विषयेषु निमग्नस्य तस्य यौवनमत्यगात् ।न च वंशकरः पुत्रस्तस्याजायत कश्चन ॥२०॥
transliteration: viṣayeṣu nimagnasya tasya yauvanamatyagāt ,na ca vaṁśakaraḥ putrastasyājāyata kaścana.
english: While he was deeply absorbed in worldly pleasures, his youth passed away. And no son, capable of continuing his lineage, was born to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 20. While he was deeply absorbed in worldly pleasures, his youth passed away. And no son, capable of continuing his lineage, was born to him.

sanskrit: मङ्गलैर्बहुभिर्होमैः पुत्रकामाभिरिष्टिभिः ।नाससाद नृपश्रेष्ठः पुत्रं कुलविवर्धनम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: maṅgalairbahubhirhomaiḥ putrakāmābhiriṣṭibhiḥ ,nāsasāda nṛpaśreṣṭhaḥ putraṁ kulavivardhanam.
english: Despite many auspicious rites, Vedic fire rituals (homa), and other Vedic rituals (iṣṭi) performed with the desire for a son, the best of kings did not obtain a son who would expand his lineage.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 21. Despite many auspicious rites, Vedic fire rituals (homa), and other Vedic rituals (iṣṭi) performed with the desire for a son, the best of kings did not obtain a son who would expand his lineage.

sanskrit: अथ काक्षीवतः पुत्रं गौतमस्य महात्मनः ।शुश्राव तपसि श्रान्तमुदारं चण्डकौशिकम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: atha kākṣīvataḥ putraṁ gautamasya mahātmanaḥ ,śuśrāva tapasi śrāntamudāraṁ caṇḍakauśikam.
english: Then he heard about the noble Caṇḍakauśika, who was the son of Kakṣīvat and of the great-souled Gautama, and who was exhausted from his ascetic practices (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 22. Then he heard about the noble Caṇḍakauśika, who was the son of Kakṣīvat and of the great-souled Gautama, and who was exhausted from his ascetic practices (tapas).

sanskrit: यदृच्छयागतं तं तु वृक्षमूलमुपाश्रितम् ।पत्नीभ्यां सहितो राजा सर्वरत्नैरतोषयत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: yadṛcchayāgataṁ taṁ tu vṛkṣamūlamupāśritam ,patnībhyāṁ sahito rājā sarvaratnairatoṣayat.
english: The king, accompanied by his two wives, appeased him - who had arrived by chance and was resting at the foot of a tree - with all kinds of jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 23. The king, accompanied by his two wives, appeased him - who had arrived by chance and was resting at the foot of a tree - with all kinds of jewels.

sanskrit: तमब्रवीत्सत्यधृतिः सत्यवागृषिसत्तमः ।परितुष्टोऽस्मि ते राजन्वरं वरय सुव्रत ॥२४॥
transliteration: tamabravītsatyadhṛtiḥ satyavāgṛṣisattamaḥ ,parituṣṭo'smi te rājanvaraṁ varaya suvrata.
english: The best of sages (ṛṣisattamaḥ), who was firm in truth (satyadhṛtiḥ) and a speaker of truth (satyavāk), said to him: 'O King, O virtuous one, I am completely satisfied with you. Ask for a boon.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 24. The best of sages (ṛṣisattamaḥ), who was firm in truth (satyadhṛtiḥ) and a speaker of truth (satyavāk), said to him: 'O King, O virtuous one, I am completely satisfied with you. Ask for a boon.'

sanskrit: ततः सभार्यः प्रणतस्तमुवाच बृहद्रथः ।पुत्रदर्शननैराश्याद्बाष्पगद्गदया गिरा ॥२५॥
transliteration: tataḥ sabhāryaḥ praṇatastamuvāca bṛhadrathaḥ ,putradarśananairāśyādbāṣpagadgadayā girā.
english: Then Brihadratha, accompanied by his wife and having bowed, spoke to him with a voice choked with tears, due to his despair over not having a son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 25. Then Brihadratha, accompanied by his wife and having bowed, spoke to him with a voice choked with tears, due to his despair over not having a son.

sanskrit: बृहद्रथ उवाच ।भगवन्राज्यमुत्सृज्य प्रस्थितस्य तपोवनम् ।किं वरेणाल्पभाग्यस्य किं राज्येनाप्रजस्य मे ॥२६॥
transliteration: bṛhadratha uvāca ,bhagavanrājyamutsṛjya prasthitasya tapovanam ,kiṁ vareṇālpabhāgyasya kiṁ rājyenāprajasya me.
english: Brihadratha said: 'O Venerable One (bhagavan), having abandoned my kingdom and departed for the hermitage (tapovanam), what good is a boon for me, an unfortunate person? What good is a kingdom for me, who is childless?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 26. Brihadratha said: 'O Venerable One (bhagavan), having abandoned my kingdom and departed for the hermitage (tapovanam), what good is a boon for me, an unfortunate person? What good is a kingdom for me, who is childless?'

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा मुनिर्ध्यानमगमत्क्षुभितेन्द्रियः ।तस्यैव चाम्रवृक्षस्य छायायां समुपाविशत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,etacchrutvā munirdhyānamagamatkṣubhitendriyaḥ ,tasyaiva cāmravṛkṣasya chāyāyāṁ samupāviśat.
english: Krishna said: 'Having heard this, the sage, with his senses agitated, entered into meditation (dhyāna). And he sat down in the shade of that very mango tree.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 27. Krishna said: 'Having heard this, the sage, with his senses agitated, entered into meditation (dhyāna). And he sat down in the shade of that very mango tree.'

sanskrit: तस्योपविष्टस्य मुनेरुत्सङ्गे निपपात ह ।अवातमशुकादष्टमेकमाम्रफलं किल ॥२८॥
transliteration: tasyopaviṣṭasya munerutsaṅge nipapāta ha ,avātamaśukādaṣṭamekamāmraphalaṁ kila.
english: And indeed, one mango fruit, neither fallen by the wind nor bitten by a parrot, fell into the lap of that seated sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 28. And indeed, one mango fruit, neither fallen by the wind nor bitten by a parrot, fell into the lap of that seated sage.

sanskrit: तत्प्रगृह्य मुनिश्रेष्ठो हृदयेनाभिमन्त्र्य च ।राज्ञे ददावप्रतिमं पुत्रसंप्राप्तिकारकम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tatpragṛhya muniśreṣṭho hṛdayenābhimantrya ca ,rājñe dadāvapratimaṁ putrasaṁprāptikārakam.
english: Having taken that (fruit), the foremost sage, after consecrating it with his heart, gave the king that incomparable item which would ensure the attainment of sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 29. Having taken that (fruit), the foremost sage, after consecrating it with his heart, gave the king that incomparable item which would ensure the attainment of sons.

sanskrit: उवाच च महाप्राज्ञस्तं राजानं महामुनिः ।गच्छ राजन्कृतार्थोऽसि निवर्त मनुजाधिप ॥३०॥
transliteration: uvāca ca mahāprājñastaṁ rājānaṁ mahāmuniḥ ,gaccha rājankṛtārtho'si nivarta manujādhipa.
english: And the great sage, who was exceedingly wise, spoke to that king, saying: 'Go, O king! You have achieved your purpose. Return, O lord of men!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 30. And the great sage, who was exceedingly wise, spoke to that king, saying: 'Go, O king! You have achieved your purpose. Return, O lord of men!'

sanskrit: यथासमयमाज्ञाय तदा स नृपसत्तमः ।द्वाभ्यामेकं फलं प्रादात्पत्नीभ्यां भरतर्षभ ॥३१॥
transliteration: yathāsamayamājñāya tadā sa nṛpasattamaḥ ,dvābhyāmekaṁ phalaṁ prādātpatnībhyāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Then, having understood what was appropriate, that best of kings gave one fruit to his two wives, O bull among the Bhāratas!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 31. Then, having understood what was appropriate, that best of kings gave one fruit to his two wives, O bull among the Bhāratas!

sanskrit: ते तदाम्रं द्विधा कृत्वा भक्षयामासतुः शुभे ।भावित्वादपि चार्थस्य सत्यवाक्यात्तथा मुनेः ॥३२॥
transliteration: te tadāmraṁ dvidhā kṛtvā bhakṣayāmāsatuḥ śubhe ,bhāvitvādapi cārthasya satyavākyāttathā muneḥ.
english: Those two (queens), having divided that mango fruit into two, ate it, O auspicious one! They did so both because of the certainty of the matter and because of the truthful words of the sage.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 32. Those two (queens), having divided that mango fruit into two, ate it, O auspicious one! They did so both because of the certainty of the matter and because of the truthful words of the sage.

sanskrit: तयोः समभवद्गर्भः फलप्राशनसंभवः ।ते च दृष्ट्वा नरपतिः परां मुदमवाप ह ॥३३॥
transliteration: tayoḥ samabhavadgarbhaḥ phalaprāśanasaṁbhavaḥ ,te ca dṛṣṭvā narapatiḥ parāṁ mudamavāpa ha.
english: To the two queens, an embryo was conceived, which was born from the eating of the fruit. And upon seeing them, the king indeed experienced great joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 33. To the two queens, an embryo was conceived, which was born from the eating of the fruit. And upon seeing them, the king indeed experienced great joy.

sanskrit: अथ काले महाप्राज्ञ यथासमयमागते ।प्रजायेतामुभे राजञ्शरीरशकले तदा ॥३४॥
transliteration: atha kāle mahāprājña yathāsamayamāgate ,prajāyetāmubhe rājañśarīraśakale tadā.
english: Then, O great wise one, when the proper time had arrived, O king, both pieces of a body were born.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 34. Then, O great wise one, when the proper time had arrived, O king, both pieces of a body were born.

sanskrit: एकाक्षिबाहुचरणे अर्धोदरमुखस्फिजे ।दृष्ट्वा शरीरशकले प्रवेपाते उभे भृशम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: ekākṣibāhucaraṇe ardhodaramukhasphije ,dṛṣṭvā śarīraśakale pravepāte ubhe bhṛśam.
english: Having seen the two body pieces, each with one eye, one arm, and one leg, and with half a belly, half a face, and half a buttock, both (queens) trembled violently.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 35. Having seen the two body pieces, each with one eye, one arm, and one leg, and with half a belly, half a face, and half a buttock, both (queens) trembled violently.

sanskrit: उद्विग्ने सह संमन्त्र्य ते भगिन्यौ तदाबले ।सजीवे प्राणिशकले तत्यजाते सुदुःखिते ॥३६॥
transliteration: udvigne saha saṁmantrya te bhaginyau tadābale ,sajīve prāṇiśakale tatyajāte suduḥkhite.
english: Distressed and deeply saddened, those two sisters, who were then powerless, consulted together and abandoned the two living fragments of the creature.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 36. Distressed and deeply saddened, those two sisters, who were then powerless, consulted together and abandoned the two living fragments of the creature.

sanskrit: तयोर्धात्र्यौ सुसंवीते कृत्वा ते गर्भसंप्लवे ।निर्गम्यान्तःपुरद्वारात्समुत्सृज्याशु जग्मतुः ॥३७॥
transliteration: tayordhātryau susaṁvīte kṛtvā te garbhasaṁplave ,nirgamyāntaḥpuradvārātsamutsṛjyāśu jagmatuḥ.
english: The two wet-nurses, after thoroughly covering those two parts of the embryo, exited the palace gate, quickly abandoned them, and then departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 37. The two wet-nurses, after thoroughly covering those two parts of the embryo, exited the palace gate, quickly abandoned them, and then departed.

sanskrit: ते चतुष्पथनिक्षिप्ते जरा नामाथ राक्षसी ।जग्राह मनुजव्याघ्र मांसशोणितभोजना ॥३८॥
transliteration: te catuṣpathanikṣipte jarā nāmātha rākṣasī ,jagrāha manujavyāghra māṁsaśoṇitabhojanā.
english: O tiger among men, then a female demon named Jarā, who subsisted on flesh and blood, picked up those two parts that had been discarded at a crossroads.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 38. O tiger among men, then a female demon named Jarā, who subsisted on flesh and blood, picked up those two parts that had been discarded at a crossroads.

sanskrit: कर्तुकामा सुखवहे शकले सा तु राक्षसी ।संघट्टयामास तदा विधानबलचोदिता ॥३९॥
transliteration: kartukāmā sukhavahe śakale sā tu rākṣasī ,saṁghaṭṭayāmāsa tadā vidhānabalacoditā.
english: But that demoness, wishing to make those two auspicious pieces fit together comfortably, then joined them, prompted by the power of (vidhāna) destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 39. But that demoness, wishing to make those two auspicious pieces fit together comfortably, then joined them, prompted by the power of (vidhāna) destiny.

sanskrit: ते समानीतमात्रे तु शकले पुरुषर्षभ ।एकमूर्तिकृते वीरः कुमारः समपद्यत ॥४०॥
transliteration: te samānītamātre tu śakale puruṣarṣabha ,ekamūrtikṛte vīraḥ kumāraḥ samapadyata.
english: O bull among men, as soon as those two pieces were joined together and made into a single form, a valiant boy appeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 40. O bull among men, as soon as those two pieces were joined together and made into a single form, a valiant boy appeared.

sanskrit: ततः सा राक्षसी राजन्विस्मयोत्फुल्ललोचना ।न शशाक समुद्वोढुं वज्रसारमयं शिशुम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: tataḥ sā rākṣasī rājanvismayotphullalocanā ,na śaśāka samudvoḍhuṁ vajrasāramayaṁ śiśum.
english: Then, O King, that female demon, with her eyes wide open in astonishment, was unable to lift the child, who was as hard and strong as a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 41. Then, O King, that female demon, with her eyes wide open in astonishment, was unable to lift the child, who was as hard and strong as a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: बालस्ताम्रतलं मुष्टिं कृत्वा चास्ये निधाय सः ।प्राक्रोशदतिसंरम्भात्सतोय इव तोयदः ॥४२॥
transliteration: bālastāmratalaṁ muṣṭiṁ kṛtvā cāsye nidhāya saḥ ,prākrośadatisaṁrambhātsatoya iva toyadaḥ.
english: The child, having made a fist with his red palm and placed it in his mouth, cried out with great fury, just like a rain cloud filled with water.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 42. The child, having made a fist with his red palm and placed it in his mouth, cried out with great fury, just like a rain cloud filled with water.

sanskrit: तेन शब्देन संभ्रान्तः सहसान्तःपुरे जनः ।निर्जगाम नरव्याघ्र राज्ञा सह परंतप ॥४३॥
transliteration: tena śabdena saṁbhrāntaḥ sahasāntaḥpure janaḥ ,nirjagāma naravyāghra rājñā saha paraṁtapa.
english: O tiger among men, O tormentor of foes, the people in the inner chambers were suddenly startled by that sound and emerged along with the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 43. O tiger among men, O tormentor of foes, the people in the inner chambers were suddenly startled by that sound and emerged along with the king.

sanskrit: ते चाबले परिग्लाने पयःपूर्णपयोधरे ।निराशे पुत्रलाभाय सहसैवाभ्यगच्छताम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: te cābale pariglāne payaḥpūrṇapayodhare ,nirāśe putralābhāya sahasaivābhyagacchatām.
english: And those two weak, exhausted women, with breasts full of milk and despairing of a son's birth, suddenly approached.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 44. And those two weak, exhausted women, with breasts full of milk and despairing of a son's birth, suddenly approached.

sanskrit: अथ दृष्ट्वा तथाभूते राजानं चेष्टसंततिम् ।तं च बालं सुबलिनं चिन्तयामास राक्षसी ॥४५॥
transliteration: atha dṛṣṭvā tathābhūte rājānaṁ ceṣṭasaṁtatim ,taṁ ca bālaṁ subalinaṁ cintayāmāsa rākṣasī.
english: Then, upon seeing the king in such a state, yearning for offspring, and also the very strong child, the female demon pondered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 45. Then, upon seeing the king in such a state, yearning for offspring, and also the very strong child, the female demon pondered.

sanskrit: नार्हामि विषये राज्ञो वसन्ती पुत्रगृद्धिनः ।बालं पुत्रमुपादातुं मेघलेखेव भास्करम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: nārhāmi viṣaye rājño vasantī putragṛddhinaḥ ,bālaṁ putramupādātuṁ meghalekheva bhāskaram.
english: Dwelling in the territory of the king, who yearns for a son, I should not take this child away, just as a line of clouds should not obscure the sun.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 46. Dwelling in the territory of the king, who yearns for a son, I should not take this child away, just as a line of clouds should not obscure the sun.

sanskrit: सा कृत्वा मानुषं रूपमुवाच मनुजाधिपम् ।बृहद्रथ सुतस्तेऽयं मद्दत्तः प्रतिगृह्यताम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: sā kṛtvā mānuṣaṁ rūpamuvāca manujādhipam ,bṛhadratha sutaste'yaṁ maddattaḥ pratigṛhyatām.
english: Assuming a human form, she addressed the king, saying, "O Bṛhadratha, this is your son, given by me; please accept him."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 47. Assuming a human form, she addressed the king, saying, "O Bṛhadratha, this is your son, given by me; please accept him."

sanskrit: तव पत्नीद्वये जातो द्विजातिवरशासनात् ।धात्रीजनपरित्यक्तो मयायं परिरक्षितः ॥४८॥
transliteration: tava patnīdvaye jāto dvijātivaraśāsanāt ,dhātrījanaparityakto mayāyaṁ parirakṣitaḥ.
english: This child was born from your two wives due to the command of the excellent Brahmin, and though abandoned by the wet nurses, he was protected by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 48. This child was born from your two wives due to the command of the excellent Brahmin, and though abandoned by the wet nurses, he was protected by me.

sanskrit: ततस्ते भरतश्रेष्ठ काशिराजसुते शुभे ।तं बालमभिपत्याशु प्रस्नवैरभिषिञ्चताम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: tataste bharataśreṣṭha kāśirājasute śubhe ,taṁ bālamabhipatyāśu prasnavairabhiṣiñcatām.
english: Then, O best among the Bharatas, let those two auspicious daughters of the King of Kāśi quickly approach that child and anoint him with streams (of fluid).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 49. Then, O best among the Bharatas, let those two auspicious daughters of the King of Kāśi quickly approach that child and anoint him with streams (of fluid).

sanskrit: ततः स राजा संहृष्टः सर्वं तदुपलभ्य च ।अपृच्छन्नवहेमाभां राक्षसीं तामराक्षसीम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa rājā saṁhṛṣṭaḥ sarvaṁ tadupalabhya ca ,apṛcchannavahemābhāṁ rākṣasīṁ tāmarākṣasīm.
english: Then that king, greatly delighted and having understood all that, questioned that female demon who had the luster of new gold and who appeared non-demonic.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 50. Then that king, greatly delighted and having understood all that, questioned that female demon who had the luster of new gold and who appeared non-demonic.

sanskrit: का त्वं कमलगर्भाभे मम पुत्रप्रदायिनी ।कामया ब्रूहि कल्याणि देवता प्रतिभासि मे ॥५१॥
transliteration: kā tvaṁ kamalagarbhābhe mama putrapradāyinī ,kāmayā brūhi kalyāṇi devatā pratibhāsi me.
english: Who are you, O one with a form like the interior of a lotus, who has granted me a son? Tell me willingly, O auspicious one, for you appear to me as a deity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 16 Verse 51. Who are you, O one with a form like the interior of a lotus, who has granted me a son? Tell me willingly, O auspicious one, for you appear to me as a deity.

sanskrit: राक्षस्युवाच ।जरा नामास्मि भद्रं ते राक्षसी कामरूपिणी ।तव वेश्मनि राजेन्द्र पूजिता न्यवसं सुखम् ॥१॥
transliteration: rākṣasyuvāca ,jarā nāmāsmi bhadraṁ te rākṣasī kāmarūpiṇī ,tava veśmani rājendra pūjitā nyavasaṁ sukham.
english: The female demon replied: "I am named Jarā, blessings upon you. I am a female demon capable of assuming any form at will. O King of kings, I dwelled happily in your house, having been worshipped."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 1. The female demon replied: "I am named Jarā, blessings upon you. I am a female demon capable of assuming any form at will. O King of kings, I dwelled happily in your house, having been worshipped."

sanskrit: साहं प्रत्युपकारार्थं चिन्तयन्त्यनिशं नृप ।तवेमे पुत्रशकले दृष्टवत्यस्मि धार्मिक ॥२॥
transliteration: sāhaṁ pratyupakārārthaṁ cintayantyaniśaṁ nṛpa ,taveme putraśakale dṛṣṭavatyasmi dhārmika.
english: Therefore, O King, O righteous one, as I was constantly pondering how to repay your kindness, I saw these two pieces of your son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 2. Therefore, O King, O righteous one, as I was constantly pondering how to repay your kindness, I saw these two pieces of your son.

sanskrit: संश्लेषिते मया दैवात्कुमारः समपद्यत ।तव भाग्यैर्महाराज हेतुमात्रमहं त्विह ॥३॥
transliteration: saṁśleṣite mayā daivātkumāraḥ samapadyata ,tava bhāgyairmahārāja hetumātramahaṁ tviha.
english: When these pieces were joined by me through destiny (daiva), a boy was born. O Great King, it was by your good fortune (bhāgya) that this happened; I was merely an instrument in this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 3. When these pieces were joined by me through destiny (daiva), a boy was born. O Great King, it was by your good fortune (bhāgya) that this happened; I was merely an instrument in this.

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा तु सा राजंस्तत्रैवान्तरधीयत ।स गृह्य च कुमारं तं प्राविशत्स्वगृहं नृपः ॥४॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,evamuktvā tu sā rājaṁstatraivāntaradhīyata ,sa gṛhya ca kumāraṁ taṁ prāviśatsvagṛhaṁ nṛpaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa said: 'Having spoken thus, O King, she vanished right there. And the king, taking that boy, entered his own home.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 4. Kṛṣṇa said: 'Having spoken thus, O King, she vanished right there. And the king, taking that boy, entered his own home.'

sanskrit: तस्य बालस्य यत्कृत्यं तच्चकार नृपस्तदा ।आज्ञापयच्च राक्षस्या मागधेषु महोत्सवम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tasya bālasya yatkṛtyaṁ taccakāra nṛpastadā ,ājñāpayacca rākṣasyā māgadheṣu mahotsavam.
english: Then the king performed the necessary rites for that child. And he ordered a great festival (mahotsava) in Magadha in honor of the demoness (rākṣasī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 5. Then the king performed the necessary rites for that child. And he ordered a great festival (mahotsava) in Magadha in honor of the demoness (rākṣasī).

sanskrit: तस्य नामाकरोत्तत्र प्रजापतिसमः पिता ।जरया संधितो यस्माज्जरासंधस्ततोऽभवत् ॥६॥
transliteration: tasya nāmākarottatra prajāpatisamaḥ pitā ,jarayā saṁdhito yasmājjarāsaṁdhastato'bhavat.
english: His father, who was like Prajāpati, gave him a name there. Because he was joined together by Jarā, he therefore became Jarāsandha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 6. His father, who was like Prajāpati, gave him a name there. Because he was joined together by Jarā, he therefore became Jarāsandha.

sanskrit: सोऽवर्धत महातेजा मगधाधिपतेः सुतः ।प्रमाणबलसंपन्नो हुताहुतिरिवानलः ॥७॥
transliteration: so'vardhata mahātejā magadhādhipateḥ sutaḥ ,pramāṇabalasaṁpanno hutāhutirivānalaḥ.
english: He, the mighty son of the king of Magadha, grew, endowed with immense strength, just like a fire (anala) nourished by offerings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 7. He, the mighty son of the king of Magadha, grew, endowed with immense strength, just like a fire (anala) nourished by offerings.

sanskrit: कस्यचित्त्वथ कालस्य पुनरेव महातपाः ।मगधानुपचक्राम भगवांश्चण्डकौशिकः ॥८॥
transliteration: kasyacittvatha kālasya punareva mahātapāḥ ,magadhānupacakrāma bhagavāṁścaṇḍakauśikaḥ.
english: Now, after some time, the venerable (bhagavān) great ascetic (mahātapāḥ) Chaṇḍakauśika again approached the Magadha kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 8. Now, after some time, the venerable (bhagavān) great ascetic (mahātapāḥ) Chaṇḍakauśika again approached the Magadha kingdom.

sanskrit: तस्यागमनसंहृष्टः सामात्यः सपुरःसरः ।सभार्यः सह पुत्रेण निर्जगाम बृहद्रथः ॥९॥
transliteration: tasyāgamanasaṁhṛṣṭaḥ sāmātyaḥ sapuraḥsaraḥ ,sabhāryaḥ saha putreṇa nirjagāma bṛhadrathaḥ.
english: King Bṛhadratha, overjoyed (saṃhṛṣṭa) by his (the sage's) arrival, came out accompanied by his ministers, chief officers, wife, and son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 9. King Bṛhadratha, overjoyed (saṃhṛṣṭa) by his (the sage's) arrival, came out accompanied by his ministers, chief officers, wife, and son.

sanskrit: पाद्यार्घ्याचमनीयैस्तमर्चयामास भारत ।स नृपो राज्यसहितं पुत्रं चास्मै न्यवेदयत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: pādyārghyācamanīyaistamarcayāmāsa bhārata ,sa nṛpo rājyasahitaṁ putraṁ cāsmai nyavedayat.
english: O Bhārata, he worshipped him with offerings of water for the feet (pādya), respectful oblations (arghya), and water for sipping (ācamanīya). That king also presented to him his son along with his kingdom.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 10. O Bhārata, he worshipped him with offerings of water for the feet (pādya), respectful oblations (arghya), and water for sipping (ācamanīya). That king also presented to him his son along with his kingdom.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य तु तां पूजां पार्थिवाद्भगवानृषिः ।उवाच मागधं राजन्प्रहृष्टेनान्तरात्मना ॥११॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya tu tāṁ pūjāṁ pārthivādbhagavānṛṣiḥ ,uvāca māgadhaṁ rājanprahṛṣṭenāntarātmanā.
english: Having accepted that worship from the king, the divine sage (bhagavān ṛṣi) addressed the king of Magadha, "O king," with a greatly delighted inner self (antarātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 11. Having accepted that worship from the king, the divine sage (bhagavān ṛṣi) addressed the king of Magadha, "O king," with a greatly delighted inner self (antarātman).

sanskrit: सर्वमेतन्मया राजन्विज्ञातं ज्ञानचक्षुषा ।पुत्रस्तु शृणु राजेन्द्र यादृशोऽयं भविष्यति ॥१२॥
transliteration: sarvametanmayā rājanvijñātaṁ jñānacakṣuṣā ,putrastu śṛṇu rājendra yādṛśo'yaṁ bhaviṣyati.
english: "O king, all this has been understood by me through the eye of knowledge (jñānacakṣu). But, O best of kings, listen to what kind of son this one will be."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 12. "O king, all this has been understood by me through the eye of knowledge (jñānacakṣu). But, O best of kings, listen to what kind of son this one will be."

sanskrit: अस्य वीर्यवतो वीर्यं नानुयास्यन्ति पार्थिवाः ।देवैरपि विसृष्टानि शस्त्राण्यस्य महीपते ।न रुजं जनयिष्यन्ति गिरेरिव नदीरयाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: asya vīryavato vīryaṁ nānuyāsyanti pārthivāḥ ,devairapi visṛṣṭāni śastrāṇyasya mahīpate ,na rujaṁ janayiṣyanti gireriva nadīrayāḥ.
english: Other kings will not be able to match the prowess of this valiant one. Even weapons discharged by the gods will not inflict injury upon this lord of the earth, just as river currents cannot harm a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 13. Other kings will not be able to match the prowess of this valiant one. Even weapons discharged by the gods will not inflict injury upon this lord of the earth, just as river currents cannot harm a mountain.

sanskrit: सर्वमूर्धाभिषिक्तानामेष मूर्ध्नि ज्वलिष्यति ।सर्वेषां निष्प्रभकरो ज्योतिषामिव भास्करः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sarvamūrdhābhiṣiktānāmeṣa mūrdhni jvaliṣyati ,sarveṣāṁ niṣprabhakaro jyotiṣāmiva bhāskaraḥ.
english: This one will shine at the forefront of all anointed rulers, dimming the splendor of all others, just as the sun outshines the other celestial bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 14. This one will shine at the forefront of all anointed rulers, dimming the splendor of all others, just as the sun outshines the other celestial bodies.

sanskrit: एनमासाद्य राजानः समृद्धबलवाहनाः ।विनाशमुपयास्यन्ति शलभा इव पावकम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: enamāsādya rājānaḥ samṛddhabalavāhanāḥ ,vināśamupayāsyanti śalabhā iva pāvakam.
english: Upon encountering him, kings with flourishing armies and conveyances will meet their destruction, just like moths rushing into a fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 15. Upon encountering him, kings with flourishing armies and conveyances will meet their destruction, just like moths rushing into a fire.

sanskrit: एष श्रियं समुदितां सर्वराज्ञां ग्रहीष्यति ।वर्षास्विवोद्धतजला नदीर्नदनदीपतिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: eṣa śriyaṁ samuditāṁ sarvarājñāṁ grahīṣyati ,varṣāsvivoddhatajalā nadīrnadanadīpatiḥ.
english: This one will seize the abundant prosperity of all kings, just as the lord of rivers (the ocean) receives the rivers swollen with water during the rainy season.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 16. This one will seize the abundant prosperity of all kings, just as the lord of rivers (the ocean) receives the rivers swollen with water during the rainy season.

sanskrit: एष धारयिता सम्यक्चातुर्वर्ण्यं महाबलः ।शुभाशुभमिव स्फीता सर्वसस्यधरा धरा ॥१७॥
transliteration: eṣa dhārayitā samyakcāturvarṇyaṁ mahābalaḥ ,śubhāśubhamiva sphītā sarvasasyadharā dharā.
english: This greatly powerful one will properly uphold the system of four social classes (cāturvarṇyam), just as the abundant earth, bearing all crops, sustains both the auspicious and the inauspicious.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 17. This greatly powerful one will properly uphold the system of four social classes (cāturvarṇyam), just as the abundant earth, bearing all crops, sustains both the auspicious and the inauspicious.

sanskrit: अस्याज्ञावशगाः सर्वे भविष्यन्ति नराधिपाः ।सर्वभूतात्मभूतस्य वायोरिव शरीरिणः ॥१८॥
transliteration: asyājñāvaśagāḥ sarve bhaviṣyanti narādhipāḥ ,sarvabhūtātmabhūtasya vāyoriva śarīriṇaḥ.
english: All kings will become subservient to his command, just as embodied beings are subject to the all-pervading wind (vāyu), which has become the subtle essence (ātman) of all creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 18. All kings will become subservient to his command, just as embodied beings are subject to the all-pervading wind (vāyu), which has become the subtle essence (ātman) of all creatures.

sanskrit: एष रुद्रं महादेवं त्रिपुरान्तकरं हरम् ।सर्वलोकेष्वतिबलः स्वयं द्रक्ष्यति मागधः ॥१९॥
transliteration: eṣa rudraṁ mahādevaṁ tripurāntakaraṁ haram ,sarvalokeṣvatibalaḥ svayaṁ drakṣyati māgadhaḥ.
english: This Magadha king (Jarasandha) himself, exceedingly powerful in all worlds, will behold Rudra, the great god (mahādeva), Hara, the destroyer of Tripura.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 19. This Magadha king (Jarasandha) himself, exceedingly powerful in all worlds, will behold Rudra, the great god (mahādeva), Hara, the destroyer of Tripura.

sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवन्नेव मुनिः स्वकार्यार्थं विचिन्तयन् ।विसर्जयामास नृपं बृहद्रथमथारिहन् ॥२०॥
transliteration: evaṁ bruvanneva muniḥ svakāryārthaṁ vicintayan ,visarjayāmāsa nṛpaṁ bṛhadrathamathārihan.
english: Thus speaking, the sage, contemplating his own objective, then dismissed King Brihadratha, O destroyer of enemies (arihan).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 20. Thus speaking, the sage, contemplating his own objective, then dismissed King Brihadratha, O destroyer of enemies (arihan).

sanskrit: प्रविश्य नगरं चैव ज्ञातिसंबन्धिभिर्वृतः ।अभिषिच्य जरासंधं मगधाधिपतिस्तदा ।बृहद्रथो नरपतिः परां निर्वृतिमाययौ ॥२१॥
transliteration: praviśya nagaraṁ caiva jñātisaṁbandhibhirvṛtaḥ ,abhiṣicya jarāsaṁdhaṁ magadhādhipatistadā ,bṛhadratho narapatiḥ parāṁ nirvṛtimāyayau.
english: Having entered the city and being surrounded by kinsmen and relatives, King Brihadratha, the lord of Magadha, then coronated Jarasandha and attained supreme contentment (nirvṛti).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 21. Having entered the city and being surrounded by kinsmen and relatives, King Brihadratha, the lord of Magadha, then coronated Jarasandha and attained supreme contentment (nirvṛti).

sanskrit: अभिषिक्ते जरासंधे तदा राजा बृहद्रथः ।पत्नीद्वयेनानुगतस्तपोवनरतोऽभवत् ॥२२॥
transliteration: abhiṣikte jarāsaṁdhe tadā rājā bṛhadrathaḥ ,patnīdvayenānugatastapovanarato'bhavat.
english: After Jarāsandha was consecrated, King Bṛhadratha, accompanied by his two wives, became dedicated to the hermitage.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 22. After Jarāsandha was consecrated, King Bṛhadratha, accompanied by his two wives, became dedicated to the hermitage.

sanskrit: तपोवनस्थे पितरि मातृभ्यां सह भारत ।जरासंधः स्ववीर्येण पार्थिवानकरोद्वशे ॥२३॥
transliteration: tapovanasthe pitari mātṛbhyāṁ saha bhārata ,jarāsaṁdhaḥ svavīryeṇa pārthivānakarodvaśe.
english: O Bhārata, while his father was residing in the hermitage with his two mothers, Jarāsandha brought kings under his control by his own might.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 23. O Bhārata, while his father was residing in the hermitage with his two mothers, Jarāsandha brought kings under his control by his own might.

sanskrit: अथ दीर्घस्य कालस्य तपोवनगतो नृपः ।सभार्यः स्वर्गमगमत्तपस्तप्त्वा बृहद्रथः ॥२४॥
transliteration: atha dīrghasya kālasya tapovanagato nṛpaḥ ,sabhāryaḥ svargamagamattapastaptvā bṛhadrathaḥ.
english: Then, after a long time, King Bṛhadratha, who had gone to the hermitage, attained heaven with his wife, having performed severe austerities (tapas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 24. Then, after a long time, King Bṛhadratha, who had gone to the hermitage, attained heaven with his wife, having performed severe austerities (tapas).

sanskrit: तस्यास्तां हंसडिभकावशस्त्रनिधनावुभौ ।मन्त्रे मतिमतां श्रेष्ठौ युद्धशास्त्रविशारदौ ॥२५॥
transliteration: tasyāstāṁ haṁsaḍibhakāvaśastranidhanāvubhau ,mantre matimatāṁ śreṣṭhau yuddhaśāstraviśāradau.
english: He (Jarāsandha) had two (ministers/generals), Haṃsa and Ḍibhaka, who were destined not to die by weapons. Both were foremost among the wise in counsel (mantra) and highly skilled in the science of warfare.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 25. He (Jarāsandha) had two (ministers/generals), Haṃsa and Ḍibhaka, who were destined not to die by weapons. Both were foremost among the wise in counsel (mantra) and highly skilled in the science of warfare.

sanskrit: यौ तौ मया ते कथितौ पूर्वमेव महाबलौ ।त्रयस्त्रयाणां लोकानां पर्याप्ता इति मे मतिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: yau tau mayā te kathitau pūrvameva mahābalau ,trayastrayāṇāṁ lokānāṁ paryāptā iti me matiḥ.
english: Those two exceedingly mighty ones, about whom I have already informed you, are, in my opinion, sufficient for the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 26. Those two exceedingly mighty ones, about whom I have already informed you, are, in my opinion, sufficient for the three worlds.

sanskrit: एवमेष तदा वीर बलिभिः कुकुरान्धकैः ।वृष्णिभिश्च महाराज नीतिहेतोरुपेक्षितः ॥२७॥
transliteration: evameṣa tadā vīra balibhiḥ kukurāndhakaiḥ ,vṛṣṇibhiśca mahārāja nītihetorupekṣitaḥ.
english: Thus, O hero, O great king, this person was then disregarded by the mighty Kukuras, Andhakas, and Vrishnis for the sake of political expediency.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 17 Verse 27. Thus, O hero, O great king, this person was then disregarded by the mighty Kukuras, Andhakas, and Vrishnis for the sake of political expediency.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।पतितौ हंसडिभकौ कंसामात्यौ निपातितौ ।जरासंधस्य निधने कालोऽयं समुपागतः ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,patitau haṁsaḍibhakau kaṁsāmātyau nipātitau ,jarāsaṁdhasya nidhane kālo'yaṁ samupāgataḥ.
english: Vasudeva said: Hamsa and Dimbhaka, Kamsa's two ministers, have been struck down. Now, the time for Jarasandha's destruction has arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 1. Vasudeva said: Hamsa and Dimbhaka, Kamsa's two ministers, have been struck down. Now, the time for Jarasandha's destruction has arrived.

sanskrit: न स शक्यो रणे जेतुं सर्वैरपि सुरासुरैः ।प्राणयुद्धेन जेतव्यः स इत्युपलभामहे ॥२॥
transliteration: na sa śakyo raṇe jetuṁ sarvairapi surāsuraiḥ ,prāṇayuddhena jetavyaḥ sa ityupalabhāmahe.
english: He cannot be conquered in battle, not even by all the gods and asuras. We understand that he must be overcome through a fight to the death.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 2. He cannot be conquered in battle, not even by all the gods and asuras. We understand that he must be overcome through a fight to the death.

sanskrit: मयि नीतिर्बलं भीमे रक्षिता चावयोर्जुनः ।साधयिष्याम तं राजन्वयं त्रय इवाग्नयः ॥३॥
transliteration: mayi nītirbalaṁ bhīme rakṣitā cāvayorjunaḥ ,sādhayiṣyāma taṁ rājanvayaṁ traya ivāgnayaḥ.
english: O King, with strategy (nīti) residing in me, strength (bala) in Bhima, and protection (rakṣitā) provided by Arjuna among us two, we three, like the (three sacred) fires, will accomplish (our goal against) him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 3. O King, with strategy (nīti) residing in me, strength (bala) in Bhima, and protection (rakṣitā) provided by Arjuna among us two, we three, like the (three sacred) fires, will accomplish (our goal against) him.

sanskrit: त्रिभिरासादितोऽस्माभिर्विजने स नराधिपः ।न संदेहो यथा युद्धमेकेनाभ्युपयास्यति ॥४॥
transliteration: tribhirāsādito'smābhirvijane sa narādhipaḥ ,na saṁdeho yathā yuddhamekenābhyupayāsyati.
english: When that king (Jarāsandha) is cornered by the three of us in a secluded place, there is no doubt that he will engage in battle with only one (of us).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 4. When that king (Jarāsandha) is cornered by the three of us in a secluded place, there is no doubt that he will engage in battle with only one (of us).

sanskrit: अवमानाच्च लोकस्य व्यायतत्वाच्च धर्षितः ।भीमसेनेन युद्धाय ध्रुवमभ्युपयास्यति ॥५॥
transliteration: avamānācca lokasya vyāyatatvācca dharṣitaḥ ,bhīmasenena yuddhāya dhruvamabhyupayāsyati.
english: And because of the disrespect from the people and his own extensive power, having been challenged by Bhimasena, he (Jarāsandha) will certainly engage in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 5. And because of the disrespect from the people and his own extensive power, having been challenged by Bhimasena, he (Jarāsandha) will certainly engage in battle.

sanskrit: अलं तस्य महाबाहुर्भीमसेनो महाबलः ।लोकस्य समुदीर्णस्य निधनायान्तको यथा ॥६॥
transliteration: alaṁ tasya mahābāhurbhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,lokasya samudīrṇasya nidhanāyāntako yathā.
english: That mighty-armed (mahābāhu) and extremely powerful (mahābala) Bhimasena alone is sufficient for his destruction, just as Yama (antaka) is for the destruction of a rebellious world (loka).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 6. That mighty-armed (mahābāhu) and extremely powerful (mahābala) Bhimasena alone is sufficient for his destruction, just as Yama (antaka) is for the destruction of a rebellious world (loka).

sanskrit: यदि ते हृदयं वेत्ति यदि ते प्रत्ययो मयि ।भीमसेनार्जुनौ शीघ्रं न्यासभूतौ प्रयच्छ मे ॥७॥
transliteration: yadi te hṛdayaṁ vetti yadi te pratyayo mayi ,bhīmasenārjunau śīghraṁ nyāsabhūtau prayaccha me.
english: If your heart truly understands and if you have faith (śraddhā) in me, then quickly hand over Bhimasena and Arjuna to me, treating them as a sacred trust.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 7. If your heart truly understands and if you have faith (śraddhā) in me, then quickly hand over Bhimasena and Arjuna to me, treating them as a sacred trust.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तो भगवता प्रत्युवाच युधिष्ठिरः ।भीमपार्थौ समालोक्य संप्रहृष्टमुखौ स्थितौ ॥८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamukto bhagavatā pratyuvāca yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhīmapārthau samālokya saṁprahṛṣṭamukhau sthitau.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Addressed in this manner by the Lord (Bhagavat), Yudhiṣṭhira responded after seeing Bhima and Arjuna standing there with radiant, joyful faces.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 8. Vaiśampāyana said: Addressed in this manner by the Lord (Bhagavat), Yudhiṣṭhira responded after seeing Bhima and Arjuna standing there with radiant, joyful faces.

sanskrit: अच्युताच्युत मा मैवं व्याहरामित्रकर्षण ।पाण्डवानां भवान्नाथो भवन्तं चाश्रिता वयम् ॥९॥
transliteration: acyutācyuta mā maivaṁ vyāharāmitrakarṣaṇa ,pāṇḍavānāṁ bhavānnātho bhavantaṁ cāśritā vayam.
english: "O Acyuta, Acyuta! Do not speak in such a way, O destroyer of foes! You are indeed the protector of the Pāṇḍavas, and we have taken refuge in you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 9. "O Acyuta, Acyuta! Do not speak in such a way, O destroyer of foes! You are indeed the protector of the Pāṇḍavas, and we have taken refuge in you."

sanskrit: यथा वदसि गोविन्द सर्वं तदुपपद्यते ।न हि त्वमग्रतस्तेषां येषां लक्ष्मीः पराङ्मुखी ॥१०॥
transliteration: yathā vadasi govinda sarvaṁ tadupapadyate ,na hi tvamagratasteṣāṁ yeṣāṁ lakṣmīḥ parāṅmukhī.
english: Whatever you say, O Govinda, is entirely appropriate. Indeed, you would not be favorable to those from whom prosperity (lakṣmī) has turned its face away.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 10. Whatever you say, O Govinda, is entirely appropriate. Indeed, you would not be favorable to those from whom prosperity (lakṣmī) has turned its face away.

sanskrit: निहतश्च जरासंधो मोक्षिताश्च महीक्षितः ।राजसूयश्च मे लब्धो निदेशे तव तिष्ठतः ॥११॥
transliteration: nihataśca jarāsaṁdho mokṣitāśca mahīkṣitaḥ ,rājasūyaśca me labdho nideśe tava tiṣṭhataḥ.
english: Jarāsaṃdha has been killed, and the kings have been liberated (mokṣa). Also, the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) has been obtained by me, while I remain under your command.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 11. Jarāsaṃdha has been killed, and the kings have been liberated (mokṣa). Also, the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) has been obtained by me, while I remain under your command.

sanskrit: क्षिप्रकारिन्यथा त्वेतत्कार्यं समुपपद्यते ।मम कार्यं जगत्कार्यं तथा कुरु नरोत्तम ॥१२॥
transliteration: kṣiprakārinyathā tvetatkāryaṁ samupapadyate ,mama kāryaṁ jagatkāryaṁ tathā kuru narottama.
english: O swift doer, just as this task is achieved, so too accomplish my task, which is indeed the world's task, O best among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 12. O swift doer, just as this task is achieved, so too accomplish my task, which is indeed the world's task, O best among men.

sanskrit: त्रिभिर्भवद्भिर्हि विना नाहं जीवितुमुत्सहे ।धर्मकामार्थरहितो रोगार्त इव दुर्गतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: tribhirbhavadbhirhi vinā nāhaṁ jīvitumutsahe ,dharmakāmārtharahito rogārta iva durgataḥ.
english: Indeed, without the three of you, I cannot bear to live, like a distressed person (durgata) afflicted by disease, devoid of rectitude (dharma), legitimate desires (kāma), and prosperity (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 13. Indeed, without the three of you, I cannot bear to live, like a distressed person (durgata) afflicted by disease, devoid of rectitude (dharma), legitimate desires (kāma), and prosperity (artha).

sanskrit: न शौरिणा विना पार्थो न शौरिः पाण्डवं विना ।नाजेयोऽस्त्यनयोर्लोके कृष्णयोरिति मे मतिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: na śauriṇā vinā pārtho na śauriḥ pāṇḍavaṁ vinā ,nājeyo'styanayorloke kṛṣṇayoriti me matiḥ.
english: Pārtha (Arjuna) cannot exist without Śauri (Krishna), nor can Śauri (Krishna) exist without Pārtha (Arjuna). In this world, there is no one unconquerable by these two Krishnas (referring to both Krishna and Arjuna) – this is my firm opinion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 14. Pārtha (Arjuna) cannot exist without Śauri (Krishna), nor can Śauri (Krishna) exist without Pārtha (Arjuna). In this world, there is no one unconquerable by these two Krishnas (referring to both Krishna and Arjuna) – this is my firm opinion.

sanskrit: अयं च बलिनां श्रेष्ठः श्रीमानपि वृकोदरः ।युवाभ्यां सहितो वीरः किं न कुर्यान्महायशाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ayaṁ ca balināṁ śreṣṭhaḥ śrīmānapi vṛkodaraḥ ,yuvābhyāṁ sahito vīraḥ kiṁ na kuryānmahāyaśāḥ.
english: And what could this hero, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who is the foremost among the mighty, glorious, and greatly renowned, not accomplish when accompanied by both of you?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 15. And what could this hero, Vṛkodara (Bhīma), who is the foremost among the mighty, glorious, and greatly renowned, not accomplish when accompanied by both of you?

sanskrit: सुप्रणीतो बलौघो हि कुरुते कार्यमुत्तमम् ।अन्धं जडं बलं प्राहुः प्रणेतव्यं विचक्षणैः ॥१६॥
transliteration: supraṇīto balaugho hi kurute kāryamuttamam ,andhaṁ jaḍaṁ balaṁ prāhuḥ praṇetavyaṁ vicakṣaṇaiḥ.
english: Indeed, a well-directed military force accomplishes an excellent task. People declare raw power to be blind and inert; it must be guided by the discerning.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 16. Indeed, a well-directed military force accomplishes an excellent task. People declare raw power to be blind and inert; it must be guided by the discerning.

sanskrit: यतो हि निम्नं भवति नयन्तीह ततो जलम् ।यतश्छिद्रं ततश्चापि नयन्ते धीधना बलम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yato hi nimnaṁ bhavati nayantīha tato jalam ,yataśchidraṁ tataścāpi nayante dhīdhanā balam.
english: For indeed, people lead water to wherever there is a low-lying area. Similarly, the intelligent guide power to wherever there is a weak point.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 17. For indeed, people lead water to wherever there is a low-lying area. Similarly, the intelligent guide power to wherever there is a weak point.

sanskrit: तस्मान्नयविधानज्ञं पुरुषं लोकविश्रुतम् ।वयमाश्रित्य गोविन्दं यतामः कार्यसिद्धये ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasmānnayavidhānajñaṁ puruṣaṁ lokaviśrutam ,vayamāśritya govindaṁ yatāmaḥ kāryasiddhaye.
english: Therefore, relying on Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), who is a person renowned throughout the world and skilled in the principles of policy, we shall strive for the accomplishment of our task.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 18. Therefore, relying on Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), who is a person renowned throughout the world and skilled in the principles of policy, we shall strive for the accomplishment of our task.

sanskrit: एवं प्रज्ञानयबलं क्रियोपायसमन्वितम् ।पुरस्कुर्वीत कार्येषु कृष्ण कार्यार्थसिद्धये ॥१९॥
transliteration: evaṁ prajñānayabalaṁ kriyopāyasamanvitam ,puraskurvīta kāryeṣu kṛṣṇa kāryārthasiddhaye.
