english: Thus, O Krishna, for the successful accomplishment of objectives in undertakings, one should employ the strength derived from intellect and strategy, combined with effective actions and means.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 19. Thus, O Krishna, for the successful accomplishment of objectives in undertakings, one should employ the strength derived from intellect and strategy, combined with effective actions and means.

sanskrit: एवमेव यदुश्रेष्ठं पार्थः कार्यार्थसिद्धये ।अर्जुनः कृष्णमन्वेतु भीमोऽन्वेतु धनंजयम् ।नयो जयो बलं चैव विक्रमे सिद्धिमेष्यति ॥२०॥
transliteration: evameva yaduśreṣṭhaṁ pārthaḥ kāryārthasiddhaye ,arjunaḥ kṛṣṇamanvetu bhīmo'nvetu dhanaṁjayam ,nayo jayo balaṁ caiva vikrame siddhimeṣyati.
english: Just so, O best of the Yadus, Pārtha (Arjuna) should follow Krishna for the attainment of objectives. Bhīma should follow Dhanañjaya (Arjuna). Indeed, strategy, victory, and strength will achieve success through valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 20. Just so, O best of the Yadus, Pārtha (Arjuna) should follow Krishna for the attainment of objectives. Bhīma should follow Dhanañjaya (Arjuna). Indeed, strategy, victory, and strength will achieve success through valor.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तास्ततः सर्वे भ्रातरो विपुलौजसः ।वार्ष्णेयः पाण्डवेयौ च प्रतस्थुर्मागधं प्रति ॥२१॥
transliteration: evamuktāstataḥ sarve bhrātaro vipulaujasaḥ ,vārṣṇeyaḥ pāṇḍaveyau ca pratasthurmāgadhaṁ prati.
english: Thus addressed, all those brothers of great might, that is, Vārṣṇeya (Krishna) and the two sons of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna and Bhīma), then set out towards Māgadha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 21. Thus addressed, all those brothers of great might, that is, Vārṣṇeya (Krishna) and the two sons of Pāṇḍu (Arjuna and Bhīma), then set out towards Māgadha.

sanskrit: वर्चस्विनां ब्राह्मणानां स्नातकानां परिच्छदान् ।आच्छाद्य सुहृदां वाक्यैर्मनोज्ञैरभिनन्दिताः ॥२२॥
transliteration: varcasvināṁ brāhmaṇānāṁ snātakānāṁ paricchadān ,ācchādya suhṛdāṁ vākyairmanojñairabhinanditāḥ.
english: Having disguised themselves in the garments of brilliant Brahmins (snātakas), they were welcomed and encouraged by the pleasing words of their friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 22. Having disguised themselves in the garments of brilliant Brahmins (snātakas), they were welcomed and encouraged by the pleasing words of their friends.

sanskrit: अमर्षादभितप्तानां ज्ञात्यर्थं मुख्यवाससाम् ।रविसोमाग्निवपुषां भीममासीत्तदा वपुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: amarṣādabhitaptānāṁ jñātyarthaṁ mukhyavāsasām ,ravisomāgnivapuṣāṁ bhīmamāsīttadā vapuḥ.
english: Then, the forms of those who were tormented by indignation for the sake of their relatives, wearing their principal garments, and whose bodies resembled the sun, moon, and fire, became fearsome.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 23. Then, the forms of those who were tormented by indignation for the sake of their relatives, wearing their principal garments, and whose bodies resembled the sun, moon, and fire, became fearsome.

sanskrit: हतं मेने जरासंधं दृष्ट्वा भीमपुरोगमौ ।एककार्यसमुद्युक्तौ कृष्णौ युद्धेऽपराजितौ ॥२४॥
transliteration: hataṁ mene jarāsaṁdhaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmapurogamau ,ekakāryasamudyuktau kṛṣṇau yuddhe'parājitau.
english: Seeing Jarāsaṃdha as slain, he considered the two (Bhīma and Kṛṣṇa), who were led by Bhīma, fully engaged in a single task, and invincible in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 24. Seeing Jarāsaṃdha as slain, he considered the two (Bhīma and Kṛṣṇa), who were led by Bhīma, fully engaged in a single task, and invincible in battle.

sanskrit: ईशौ हि तौ महात्मानौ सर्वकार्यप्रवर्तने ।धर्मार्थकामकार्याणां कार्याणामिव निग्रहे ॥२५॥
transliteration: īśau hi tau mahātmānau sarvakāryapravartane ,dharmārthakāmakāryāṇāṁ kāryāṇāmiva nigrahe.
english: Indeed, those two great-souled beings (mahātmānau) are capable lords in initiating all undertakings, just as they are in the restraint of actions related to natural law (dharma), material prosperity (artha), and sensual pleasure (kāma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 25. Indeed, those two great-souled beings (mahātmānau) are capable lords in initiating all undertakings, just as they are in the restraint of actions related to natural law (dharma), material prosperity (artha), and sensual pleasure (kāma).

sanskrit: कुरुभ्यः प्रस्थितास्ते तु मध्येन कुरुजाङ्गलम् ।रम्यं पद्मसरो गत्वा कालकूटमतीत्य च ॥२६॥
transliteration: kurubhyaḥ prasthitāste tu madhyena kurujāṅgalam ,ramyaṁ padmasaro gatvā kālakūṭamatītya ca.
english: But having departed from the Kuru lands, they passed through the Kuru-Jāṅgala region; having reached the delightful Lotus Lake, they then crossed over Kālakūṭa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 26. But having departed from the Kuru lands, they passed through the Kuru-Jāṅgala region; having reached the delightful Lotus Lake, they then crossed over Kālakūṭa.

sanskrit: गण्डकीयां तथा शोणं सदानीरां तथैव च ।एकपर्वतके नद्यः क्रमेणैत्य व्रजन्ति ते ॥२७॥
transliteration: gaṇḍakīyāṁ tathā śoṇaṁ sadānīrāṁ tathaiva ca ,ekaparvatake nadyaḥ krameṇaitya vrajanti te.
english: They (the travelers) arrived at the Gaṇḍakī, as well as the Śoṇa and Sadānīrā rivers. These rivers, originating from a single mountain, flow forth successively.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 27. They (the travelers) arrived at the Gaṇḍakī, as well as the Śoṇa and Sadānīrā rivers. These rivers, originating from a single mountain, flow forth successively.

sanskrit: संतीर्य सरयूं रम्यां दृष्ट्वा पूर्वांश्च कोसलान् ।अतीत्य जग्मुर्मिथिलां मालां चर्मण्वतीं नदीम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: saṁtīrya sarayūṁ ramyāṁ dṛṣṭvā pūrvāṁśca kosalān ,atītya jagmurmithilāṁ mālāṁ carmaṇvatīṁ nadīm.
english: Having crossed the beautiful Sarayū river and seen the eastern Kośala region, they passed beyond the Mālā and Carmaṇvatī rivers and proceeded to Mithilā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 28. Having crossed the beautiful Sarayū river and seen the eastern Kośala region, they passed beyond the Mālā and Carmaṇvatī rivers and proceeded to Mithilā.

sanskrit: उत्तीर्य गङ्गां शोणं च सर्वे ते प्राङ्मुखास्त्रयः ।कुरवोरश्छदं जग्मुर्मागधं क्षेत्रमच्युताः ॥२९॥
transliteration: uttīrya gaṅgāṁ śoṇaṁ ca sarve te prāṅmukhāstrayaḥ ,kuravoraśchadaṁ jagmurmāgadhaṁ kṣetramacyutāḥ.
english: All three of them, facing east, having crossed the Gaṅgā and Śoṇa rivers, proceeded undeterred to the Magadhan region (kṣetra) known as Kuravoraśchada.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 29. All three of them, facing east, having crossed the Gaṅgā and Śoṇa rivers, proceeded undeterred to the Magadhan region (kṣetra) known as Kuravoraśchada.

sanskrit: ते शश्वद्गोधनाकीर्णमम्बुमन्तं शुभद्रुमम् ।गोरथं गिरिमासाद्य ददृशुर्मागधं पुरम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: te śaśvadgodhanākīrṇamambumantaṁ śubhadrumam ,gorathaṁ girimāsādya dadṛśurmāgadhaṁ puram.
english: Having reached the Goratha mountain, which was always teeming with cattle, rich in water, and adorned with beautiful trees, they then saw the Magadhan city.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 18 Verse 30. Having reached the Goratha mountain, which was always teeming with cattle, rich in water, and adorned with beautiful trees, they then saw the Magadhan city.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।एष पार्थ महान्स्वादुः पशुमान्नित्यमम्बुमान् ।निरामयः सुवेश्माढ्यो निवेशो मागधः शुभः ॥१॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,eṣa pārtha mahānsvāduḥ paśumānnityamambumān ,nirāmayaḥ suveśmāḍhyo niveśo māgadhaḥ śubhaḥ.
english: Vāsudeva said: "O Pārtha, this Magadhan settlement is great, delightful, always rich in cattle and water. It is free from disease, abundant in good houses, and auspicious."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 1. Vāsudeva said: "O Pārtha, this Magadhan settlement is great, delightful, always rich in cattle and water. It is free from disease, abundant in good houses, and auspicious."

sanskrit: वैहारो विपुलः शैलो वराहो वृषभस्तथा ।तथैवर्षिगिरिस्तात शुभाश्चैत्यकपञ्चमाः ॥२॥
transliteration: vaihāro vipulaḥ śailo varāho vṛṣabhastathā ,tathaivarṣigiristāta śubhāścaityakapañcamāḥ.
english: O dear one, the vast Vaihāra mountain, and also Varāha, Vṛṣabha, and Ṛṣigiri, are auspicious, with Caityaka being the fifth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 2. O dear one, the vast Vaihāra mountain, and also Varāha, Vṛṣabha, and Ṛṣigiri, are auspicious, with Caityaka being the fifth.

sanskrit: एते पञ्च महाशृङ्गाः पर्वताः शीतलद्रुमाः ।रक्षन्तीवाभिसंहत्य संहताङ्गा गिरिव्रजम् ॥३॥
transliteration: ete pañca mahāśṛṅgāḥ parvatāḥ śītaladrumāḥ ,rakṣantīvābhisaṁhatya saṁhatāṅgā girivrajam.
english: These five great-peaked mountains, covered with cool trees, seem to protect the city of Girivraja, standing united with their compact parts.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 3. These five great-peaked mountains, covered with cool trees, seem to protect the city of Girivraja, standing united with their compact parts.

sanskrit: पुष्पवेष्टितशाखाग्रैर्गन्धवद्भिर्मनोरमैः ।निगूढा इव लोध्राणां वनैः कामिजनप्रियैः ॥४॥
transliteration: puṣpaveṣṭitaśākhāgrairgandhavadbhirmanoramaiḥ ,nigūḍhā iva lodhrāṇāṁ vanaiḥ kāmijanapriyaiḥ.
english: They appear as if hidden by charming and fragrant forests of Lodhra trees, whose branch-tips are encircled by flowers and which are dear to lovers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 4. They appear as if hidden by charming and fragrant forests of Lodhra trees, whose branch-tips are encircled by flowers and which are dear to lovers.

sanskrit: शूद्रायां गौतमो यत्र महात्मा संशितव्रतः ।औशीनर्यामजनयत्काक्षीवादीन्सुतानृषिः ॥५॥
transliteration: śūdrāyāṁ gautamo yatra mahātmā saṁśitavrataḥ ,auśīnaryāmajanayatkākṣīvādīnsutānṛṣiḥ.
english: There, the great-souled (mahātmā) sage (ṛṣi) Gautama, who possessed firm vows, begot sons like Kākṣīvān from a śūdra woman and from Auśīnarī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 5. There, the great-souled (mahātmā) sage (ṛṣi) Gautama, who possessed firm vows, begot sons like Kākṣīvān from a śūdra woman and from Auśīnarī.

sanskrit: गौतमः क्षयणादस्मादथासौ तत्र वेश्मनि ।भजते मागधं वंशं स नृपाणामनुग्रहात् ॥६॥
transliteration: gautamaḥ kṣayaṇādasmādathāsau tatra veśmani ,bhajate māgadhaṁ vaṁśaṁ sa nṛpāṇāmanugrahāt.
english: Following this decline of Gautama's lineage, his descendant then adopted the Magadha dynasty in that dwelling, through the favor of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 6. Following this decline of Gautama's lineage, his descendant then adopted the Magadha dynasty in that dwelling, through the favor of kings.

sanskrit: अङ्गवङ्गादयश्चैव राजानः सुमहाबलाः ।गौतमक्षयमभ्येत्य रमन्ते स्म पुरार्जुन ॥७॥
transliteration: aṅgavaṅgādayaścaiva rājānaḥ sumahābalāḥ ,gautamakṣayamabhyetya ramante sma purārjuna.
english: O Arjuna, kings such as Aṅga, Vaṅga, and others, who were indeed very mighty, used to enjoy themselves after reaching Gautama's dwelling in ancient times.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 7. O Arjuna, kings such as Aṅga, Vaṅga, and others, who were indeed very mighty, used to enjoy themselves after reaching Gautama's dwelling in ancient times.

sanskrit: वनराजीस्तु पश्येमाः प्रियालानां मनोरमाः ।लोध्राणां च शुभाः पार्थ गौतमौकःसमीपजाः ॥८॥
transliteration: vanarājīstu paśyemāḥ priyālānāṁ manoramāḥ ,lodhrāṇāṁ ca śubhāḥ pārtha gautamaukaḥsamīpajāḥ.
english: But behold, O Arjuna, these charming forest groves of priyāla trees, and these beautiful ones of lodhra trees, which have grown near Gautama's dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 8. But behold, O Arjuna, these charming forest groves of priyāla trees, and these beautiful ones of lodhra trees, which have grown near Gautama's dwelling.

sanskrit: अर्बुदः शक्रवापी च पन्नगौ शत्रुतापनौ ।स्वस्तिकस्यालयश्चात्र मणिनागस्य चोत्तमः ॥९॥
transliteration: arbudaḥ śakravāpī ca pannagau śatrutāpanau ,svastikasyālayaścātra maṇināgasya cottamaḥ.
english: Here are Arbuda and Śakravāpī, the two snakes who torment their enemies, and also the excellent abode of Svastika and Maṇināga.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 9. Here are Arbuda and Śakravāpī, the two snakes who torment their enemies, and also the excellent abode of Svastika and Maṇināga.

sanskrit: अपरिहार्या मेघानां मागधेयं मणेः कृते ।कौशिको मणिमांश्चैव ववृधाते ह्यनुग्रहम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: aparihāryā meghānāṁ māgadheyaṁ maṇeḥ kṛte ,kauśiko maṇimāṁścaiva vavṛdhāte hyanugraham.
english: For the sake of the gem, the Magadhan share (of tribute) is unavoidable for the clouds (or cloud-like kings). Indeed, Kauśika and Maṇimān both fostered favor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 10. For the sake of the gem, the Magadhan share (of tribute) is unavoidable for the clouds (or cloud-like kings). Indeed, Kauśika and Maṇimān both fostered favor.

sanskrit: अर्थसिद्धिं त्वनपगां जरासंधोऽभिमन्यते ।वयमासादने तस्य दर्पमद्य निहन्म हि ॥११॥
transliteration: arthasiddhiṁ tvanapagāṁ jarāsaṁdho'bhimanyate ,vayamāsādane tasya darpamadya nihanma hi.
english: Jarasandha, however, considers his achievement of purpose (arthasiddhi) to be unfailing. Indeed, upon confronting him, we shall now destroy his pride.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 11. Jarasandha, however, considers his achievement of purpose (arthasiddhi) to be unfailing. Indeed, upon confronting him, we shall now destroy his pride.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा ततः सर्वे भ्रातरो विपुलौजसः ।वार्ष्णेयः पाण्डवेयौ च प्रतस्थुर्मागधं पुरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā tataḥ sarve bhrātaro vipulaujasaḥ ,vārṣṇeyaḥ pāṇḍaveyau ca pratasthurmāgadhaṁ puram.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, all the brothers, endowed with great energy, along with the Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) and the two Pāṇḍava brothers (Bhīma and Arjuna), then set out for the city of Magadha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 12. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, all the brothers, endowed with great energy, along with the Vārṣṇeya (Kṛṣṇa) and the two Pāṇḍava brothers (Bhīma and Arjuna), then set out for the city of Magadha.

sanskrit: तुष्टपुष्टजनोपेतं चातुर्वर्ण्यजनाकुलम् ।स्फीतोत्सवमनाधृष्यमासेदुश्च गिरिव्रजम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tuṣṭapuṣṭajanopetaṁ cāturvarṇyajanākulam ,sphītotsavamanādhṛṣyamāseduśca girivrajam.
english: And they approached Girivraja, a city filled with content and prosperous people, teeming with inhabitants from all four social orders, abundant in festivals, and impregnable.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 13. And they approached Girivraja, a city filled with content and prosperous people, teeming with inhabitants from all four social orders, abundant in festivals, and impregnable.

sanskrit: तेऽथ द्वारमनासाद्य पुरस्य गिरिमुच्छ्रितम् ।बार्हद्रथैः पूज्यमानं तथा नगरवासिभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: te'tha dvāramanāsādya purasya girimucchritam ,bārhadrathaiḥ pūjyamānaṁ tathā nagaravāsibhiḥ.
english: Then, without reaching the city's gate, they saw the lofty mountain being worshipped by the Bārhadratas and also by the city-dwellers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 14. Then, without reaching the city's gate, they saw the lofty mountain being worshipped by the Bārhadratas and also by the city-dwellers.

sanskrit: यत्र माषादमृषभमाससाद बृहद्रथः ।तं हत्वा माषनालाश्च तिस्रो भेरीरकारयत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: yatra māṣādamṛṣabhamāsasāda bṛhadrathaḥ ,taṁ hatvā māṣanālāśca tisro bherīrakārayat.
english: It was here that Bṛhadratha acquired 'Amṛṣabha' from Māṣa; having killed Māṣa, he then caused Māṣa's entrails to be processed and three drums (bherī) to be made.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 15. It was here that Bṛhadratha acquired 'Amṛṣabha' from Māṣa; having killed Māṣa, he then caused Māṣa's entrails to be processed and three drums (bherī) to be made.

sanskrit: आनह्य चर्मणा तेन स्थापयामास स्वे पुरे ।यत्र ताः प्राणदन्भेर्यो दिव्यपुष्पावचूर्णिताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ānahya carmaṇā tena sthāpayāmāsa sve pure ,yatra tāḥ prāṇadanbheryo divyapuṣpāvacūrṇitāḥ.
english: Having fitted them with that hide, he installed them in his own city. There, those drums (bherī), strewn with divine flowers, would reverberate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 16. Having fitted them with that hide, he installed them in his own city. There, those drums (bherī), strewn with divine flowers, would reverberate.

sanskrit: मागधानां सुरुचिरं चैत्यकान्तं समाद्रवन् ।शिरसीव जिघांसन्तो जरासंधजिघांसवः ॥१७॥
transliteration: māgadhānāṁ suruciraṁ caityakāntaṁ samādravan ,śirasīva jighāṁsanto jarāsaṁdhajighāṁsavaḥ.
english: Those who desired to kill Jarāsandha rushed towards the very beautiful and charming shrine (caitya) of the Magadhas, as if intending to strike its very head.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 17. Those who desired to kill Jarāsandha rushed towards the very beautiful and charming shrine (caitya) of the Magadhas, as if intending to strike its very head.

sanskrit: स्थिरं सुविपुलं शृङ्गं सुमहान्तं पुरातनम् ।अर्चितं माल्यदामैश्च सततं सुप्रतिष्ठितम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: sthiraṁ suvipulaṁ śṛṅgaṁ sumahāntaṁ purātanam ,arcitaṁ mālyadāmaiśca satataṁ supratiṣṭhitam.
english: It was a stable, very vast, and enormous summit, ancient, constantly worshipped with garlands, and very well-established.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 18. It was a stable, very vast, and enormous summit, ancient, constantly worshipped with garlands, and very well-established.

sanskrit: विपुलैर्बाहुभिर्वीरास्तेऽभिहत्याभ्यपातयन् ।ततस्ते मागधं दृष्ट्वा पुरं प्रविविशुस्तदा ॥१९॥
transliteration: vipulairbāhubhirvīrāste'bhihatyābhyapātayan ,tataste māgadhaṁ dṛṣṭvā puraṁ praviviśustadā.
english: Those heroes, striking it with their mighty arms, brought it down. Then, having seen the Magadha city, they entered it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 19. Those heroes, striking it with their mighty arms, brought it down. Then, having seen the Magadha city, they entered it.

sanskrit: एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु जरासंधं समर्चयन् ।पर्यग्नि कुर्वंश्च नृपं द्विरदस्थं पुरोहिताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: etasminneva kāle tu jarāsaṁdhaṁ samarcayan ,paryagni kurvaṁśca nṛpaṁ dviradasthaṁ purohitāḥ.
english: Indeed, at this very time, the priests were worshipping King Jarāsandha, who was mounted on an elephant, and performing rites around the sacred fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 20. Indeed, at this very time, the priests were worshipping King Jarāsandha, who was mounted on an elephant, and performing rites around the sacred fire.

sanskrit: स्नातकव्रतिनस्ते तु बाहुशस्त्रा निरायुधाः ।युयुत्सवः प्रविविशुर्जरासंधेन भारत ॥२१॥
transliteration: snātakavratinaste tu bāhuśastrā nirāyudhāḥ ,yuyutsavaḥ praviviśurjarāsaṁdhena bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, those who had completed their student vows entered. Although they were unarmed, their powerful arms served as their weapons, and they were eager to fight Jarāsaṃdha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 21. O Bhārata, those who had completed their student vows entered. Although they were unarmed, their powerful arms served as their weapons, and they were eager to fight Jarāsaṃdha.

sanskrit: भक्ष्यमाल्यापणानां च ददृशुः श्रियमुत्तमाम् ।स्फीतां सर्वगुणोपेतां सर्वकामसमृद्धिनीम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: bhakṣyamālyāpaṇānāṁ ca dadṛśuḥ śriyamuttamām ,sphītāṁ sarvaguṇopetāṁ sarvakāmasamṛddhinīm.
english: And they beheld the exquisite splendor of the food stalls and flower shops, which was flourishing, endowed with every good quality, and fulfilling all desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 22. And they beheld the exquisite splendor of the food stalls and flower shops, which was flourishing, endowed with every good quality, and fulfilling all desires.

sanskrit: तां तु दृष्ट्वा समृद्धिं ते वीथ्यां तस्यां नरोत्तमाः ।राजमार्गेण गच्छन्तः कृष्णभीमधनंजयाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tāṁ tu dṛṣṭvā samṛddhiṁ te vīthyāṁ tasyāṁ narottamāḥ ,rājamārgeṇa gacchantaḥ kṛṣṇabhīmadhanaṁjayāḥ.
english: But having observed that prosperity on that thoroughfare, Kṛṣṇa, Bhīma, and Arjuna, those foremost among men, were proceeding along the royal road.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 23. But having observed that prosperity on that thoroughfare, Kṛṣṇa, Bhīma, and Arjuna, those foremost among men, were proceeding along the royal road.

sanskrit: बलाद्गृहीत्वा माल्यानि मालाकारान्महाबलाः ।विरागवसनाः सर्वे स्रग्विणो मृष्टकुण्डलाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: balādgṛhītvā mālyāni mālākārānmahābalāḥ ,virāgavasanāḥ sarve sragviṇo mṛṣṭakuṇḍalāḥ.
english: All the mighty ones, having forcibly seized garlands from the garland-makers, were dressed in faded garments, adorned with garlands, and wore shining earrings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 24. All the mighty ones, having forcibly seized garlands from the garland-makers, were dressed in faded garments, adorned with garlands, and wore shining earrings.

sanskrit: निवेशनमथाजग्मुर्जरासंधस्य धीमतः ।गोवासमिव वीक्षन्तः सिंहा हैमवता यथा ॥२५॥
transliteration: niveśanamathājagmurjarāsaṁdhasya dhīmataḥ ,govāsamiva vīkṣantaḥ siṁhā haimavatā yathā.
english: Then, they approached the dwelling of the intelligent Jarāsandha, looking upon it as if they were Himalayan lions viewing a mere cattle pen.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 25. Then, they approached the dwelling of the intelligent Jarāsandha, looking upon it as if they were Himalayan lions viewing a mere cattle pen.

sanskrit: शैलस्तम्भनिभास्तेषां चन्दनागुरुभूषिताः ।अशोभन्त महाराज बाहवो बाहुशालिनाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: śailastambhanibhāsteṣāṁ candanāgurubhūṣitāḥ ,aśobhanta mahārāja bāhavo bāhuśālinām.
english: O great king, their arms, resembling stone pillars and adorned with sandalwood paste and agarwood, shone brightly – the arms of those mighty-armed heroes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 26. O great king, their arms, resembling stone pillars and adorned with sandalwood paste and agarwood, shone brightly – the arms of those mighty-armed heroes.

sanskrit: तान्दृष्ट्वा द्विरदप्रख्याञ्शालस्कन्धानिवोद्गतान् ।व्यूढोरस्कान्मागधानां विस्मयः समजायत ॥२७॥
transliteration: tāndṛṣṭvā dviradaprakhyāñśālaskandhānivodgatān ,vyūḍhoraskānmāgadhānāṁ vismayaḥ samajāyata.
english: Having seen them  -  elephant-like, with broad shoulders like the massive trunks of śāla trees, and prominently built chests  -  astonishment arose among the people of Magadha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 27. Having seen them  -  elephant-like, with broad shoulders like the massive trunks of śāla trees, and prominently built chests  -  astonishment arose among the people of Magadha.

sanskrit: ते त्वतीत्य जनाकीर्णास्तिस्रः कक्ष्या नरर्षभाः ।अहंकारेण राजानमुपतस्थुर्महाबलाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: te tvatītya janākīrṇāstisraḥ kakṣyā nararṣabhāḥ ,ahaṁkāreṇa rājānamupatasthurmahābalāḥ.
english: But those mighty heroes, the best among men (nararṣabhāḥ), having passed through the three enclosures crowded with people with bold confidence (ahaṅkāra), then approached the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 28. But those mighty heroes, the best among men (nararṣabhāḥ), having passed through the three enclosures crowded with people with bold confidence (ahaṅkāra), then approached the king.

sanskrit: तान्पाद्यमधुपर्कार्हान्मानार्हान्सत्कृतिं गतान् ।प्रत्युत्थाय जरासंध उपतस्थे यथाविधि ॥२९॥
transliteration: tānpādyamadhuparkārhānmānārhānsatkṛtiṁ gatān ,pratyutthāya jarāsaṁdha upatasthe yathāvidhi.
english: Jarāsandha rose to greet them, who were worthy of receiving water for their feet, the honey-mixture offering, and honor. He attended to these honored guests according to the established custom.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 29. Jarāsandha rose to greet them, who were worthy of receiving water for their feet, the honey-mixture offering, and honor. He attended to these honored guests according to the established custom.

sanskrit: उवाच चैतान्राजासौ स्वागतं वोऽस्त्विति प्रभुः ।तस्य ह्येतद्व्रतं राजन्बभूव भुवि विश्रुतम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: uvāca caitānrājāsau svāgataṁ vo'stviti prabhuḥ ,tasya hyetadvrataṁ rājanbabhūva bhuvi viśrutam.
english: And that mighty King (Jarāsandha) said to them, "Welcome to you!" Indeed, O King, this sacred observance (vrata) of his was well-known on earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 30. And that mighty King (Jarāsandha) said to them, "Welcome to you!" Indeed, O King, this sacred observance (vrata) of his was well-known on earth.

sanskrit: स्नातकान्ब्राह्मणान्प्राप्ताञ्श्रुत्वा स समितिंजयः ।अप्यर्धरात्रे नृपतिः प्रत्युद्गच्छति भारत ॥३१॥
transliteration: snātakānbrāhmaṇānprāptāñśrutvā sa samitiṁjayaḥ ,apyardharātre nṛpatiḥ pratyudgacchati bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, having heard that Snātaka Brahmins had arrived, that King Jarāsandha, the conqueror in assemblies, would go to meet them even at midnight.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 31. O Bhārata, having heard that Snātaka Brahmins had arrived, that King Jarāsandha, the conqueror in assemblies, would go to meet them even at midnight.

sanskrit: तांस्त्वपूर्वेण वेषेण दृष्ट्वा नृपतिसत्तमः ।उपतस्थे जरासंधो विस्मितश्चाभवत्तदा ॥३२॥
transliteration: tāṁstvapūrveṇa veṣeṇa dṛṣṭvā nṛpatisattamaḥ ,upatasthe jarāsaṁdho vismitaścābhavattadā.
english: But having seen them in an unprecedented disguise, Jarāsandha, the best of kings, attended to them and then became astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 32. But having seen them in an unprecedented disguise, Jarāsandha, the best of kings, attended to them and then became astonished.

sanskrit: ते तु दृष्ट्वैव राजानं जरासंधं नरर्षभाः ।इदमूचुरमित्रघ्नाः सर्वे भरतसत्तम ॥३३॥
transliteration: te tu dṛṣṭvaiva rājānaṁ jarāsaṁdhaṁ nararṣabhāḥ ,idamūcuramitraghnāḥ sarve bharatasattama.
english: O best of Bharatas, all those best among men, the slayers of enemies, indeed, having just seen King Jarāsandha, said this:
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 33. O best of Bharatas, all those best among men, the slayers of enemies, indeed, having just seen King Jarāsandha, said this:

sanskrit: स्वस्त्यस्तु कुशलं राजन्निति सर्वे व्यवस्थिताः ।तं नृपं नृपशार्दूल विप्रैक्षन्त परस्परम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: svastyastu kuśalaṁ rājanniti sarve vyavasthitāḥ ,taṁ nṛpaṁ nṛpaśārdūla vipraikṣanta parasparam.
english: O tiger among kings, all of them stood poised and said, 'May there be well-being and welfare, O King!' Then they looked at that king and at each other.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 34. O tiger among kings, all of them stood poised and said, 'May there be well-being and welfare, O King!' Then they looked at that king and at each other.

sanskrit: तानब्रवीज्जरासंधस्तदा यादवपाण्डवान् ।आस्यतामिति राजेन्द्र ब्राह्मणच्छद्मसंवृतान् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tānabravījjarāsaṁdhastadā yādavapāṇḍavān ,āsyatāmiti rājendra brāhmaṇacchadmasaṁvṛtān.
english: O supreme ruler, Jarāsandha then said to those Yadavas and Pāṇḍavas, who were disguised as Brahmins, 'Please sit down.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 35. O supreme ruler, Jarāsandha then said to those Yadavas and Pāṇḍavas, who were disguised as Brahmins, 'Please sit down.'

sanskrit: अथोपविविशुः सर्वे त्रयस्ते पुरुषर्षभाः ।संप्रदीप्तास्त्रयो लक्ष्म्या महाध्वर इवाग्नयः ॥३६॥
transliteration: athopaviviśuḥ sarve trayaste puruṣarṣabhāḥ ,saṁpradīptāstrayo lakṣmyā mahādhvara ivāgnayaḥ.
english: Then, all three of those foremost among men sat down, gleaming brilliantly with splendor (lakṣmī), like the three fires (agnayaḥ) in a great Vedic ritual (mahādhvara).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 36. Then, all three of those foremost among men sat down, gleaming brilliantly with splendor (lakṣmī), like the three fires (agnayaḥ) in a great Vedic ritual (mahādhvara).

sanskrit: तानुवाच जरासंधः सत्यसंधो नराधिपः ।विगर्हमाणः कौरव्य वेषग्रहणकारणात् ॥३७॥
transliteration: tānuvāca jarāsaṁdhaḥ satyasaṁdho narādhipaḥ ,vigarhamāṇaḥ kauravya veṣagrahaṇakāraṇāt.
english: Jarasandha, the king of men who was steadfast in his promise, spoke to them, reproaching them, O son of Kuru, on account of their having assumed a disguise.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 37. Jarasandha, the king of men who was steadfast in his promise, spoke to them, reproaching them, O son of Kuru, on account of their having assumed a disguise.

sanskrit: न स्नातकव्रता विप्रा बहिर्माल्यानुलेपनाः ।भवन्तीति नृलोकेऽस्मिन्विदितं मम सर्वशः ॥३८॥
transliteration: na snātakavratā viprā bahirmālyānulepanāḥ ,bhavantīti nṛloke'sminviditaṁ mama sarvaśaḥ.
english: Brahmins (vipra) who observe the vows of a Vedic graduate (snātaka) are not externally adorned with garlands and ointments. This fact is entirely known to me in this human world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 38. Brahmins (vipra) who observe the vows of a Vedic graduate (snātaka) are not externally adorned with garlands and ointments. This fact is entirely known to me in this human world.

sanskrit: ते यूयं पुष्पवन्तश्च भुजैर्ज्याघातलक्षणैः ।बिभ्रतः क्षात्रमोजश्च ब्राह्मण्यं प्रतिजानथ ॥३९॥
transliteration: te yūyaṁ puṣpavantaśca bhujairjyāghātalakṣaṇaiḥ ,bibhrataḥ kṣātramojaśca brāhmaṇyaṁ pratijānatha.
english: And you all, adorned with flowers, whose arms bear the distinctive marks of bowstring scars, possessing martial (kṣātra) strength, still declare yourselves to be Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 39. And you all, adorned with flowers, whose arms bear the distinctive marks of bowstring scars, possessing martial (kṣātra) strength, still declare yourselves to be Brahmins.

sanskrit: एवं विरागवसना बहिर्माल्यानुलेपनाः ।सत्यं वदत के यूयं सत्यं राजसु शोभते ॥४०॥
transliteration: evaṁ virāgavasanā bahirmālyānulepanāḥ ,satyaṁ vadata ke yūyaṁ satyaṁ rājasu śobhate.
english: Therefore, wearing plain, unadorned garments and being devoid of external garlands and ointments, tell me truly, who are you? Indeed, truth is fitting for kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 40. Therefore, wearing plain, unadorned garments and being devoid of external garlands and ointments, tell me truly, who are you? Indeed, truth is fitting for kings.

sanskrit: चैत्यकं च गिरेः शृङ्गं भित्त्वा किमिव सद्म नः ।अद्वारेण प्रविष्टाः स्थ निर्भया राजकिल्बिषात् ॥४१॥
transliteration: caityakaṁ ca gireḥ śṛṅgaṁ bhittvā kimiva sadma naḥ ,advāreṇa praviṣṭāḥ stha nirbhayā rājakilbiṣāt.
english: Having broken through the mountain's peak, what sort of dwelling is this of ours? You have entered without a door, completely fearless of any royal transgression.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 41. Having broken through the mountain's peak, what sort of dwelling is this of ours? You have entered without a door, completely fearless of any royal transgression.

sanskrit: कर्म चैतद्विलिङ्गस्य किं वाद्य प्रसमीक्षितम् ।वदध्वं वाचि वीर्यं च ब्राह्मणस्य विशेषतः ॥४२॥
transliteration: karma caitadviliṅgasya kiṁ vādya prasamīkṣitam ,vadadhvaṁ vāci vīryaṁ ca brāhmaṇasya viśeṣataḥ.
english: What action by this disguised person has been observed or considered today? Speak! And [consider] the power in speech, especially that of a brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 42. What action by this disguised person has been observed or considered today? Speak! And [consider] the power in speech, especially that of a brahmin.

sanskrit: एवं च मामुपस्थाय कस्माच्च विधिनार्हणाम् ।प्रणीतां नो न गृह्णीत कार्यं किं चास्मदागमे ॥४३॥
transliteration: evaṁ ca māmupasthāya kasmācca vidhinārhaṇām ,praṇītāṁ no na gṛhṇīta kāryaṁ kiṁ cāsmadāgame.
english: Having thus approached me, why do you not accept the respectful offering presented by us according to custom? And what is your purpose for coming to us?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 43. Having thus approached me, why do you not accept the respectful offering presented by us according to custom? And what is your purpose for coming to us?

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्ततः कृष्णः प्रत्युवाच महामनाः ।स्निग्धगम्भीरया वाचा वाक्यं वाक्यविशारदः ॥४४॥
transliteration: evamuktastataḥ kṛṣṇaḥ pratyuvāca mahāmanāḥ ,snigdhagambhīrayā vācā vākyaṁ vākyaviśāradaḥ.
english: Thus addressed, the magnanimous Kṛṣṇa, an expert in speech, then replied with words in a gentle and profound voice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 44. Thus addressed, the magnanimous Kṛṣṇa, an expert in speech, then replied with words in a gentle and profound voice.

sanskrit: स्नातकव्रतिनो राजन्ब्राह्मणाः क्षत्रिया विशः ।विशेषनियमाश्चैषामविशेषाश्च सन्त्युत ॥४५॥
transliteration: snātakavratino rājanbrāhmaṇāḥ kṣatriyā viśaḥ ,viśeṣaniyamāścaiṣāmaviśeṣāśca santyuta.
english: O King, for Brahmins, Kshatriyas, and Vaishyas who have completed their Vedic studies and undertaken the graduate's vow, there are indeed both specific rules and general rules.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 45. O King, for Brahmins, Kshatriyas, and Vaishyas who have completed their Vedic studies and undertaken the graduate's vow, there are indeed both specific rules and general rules.

sanskrit: विशेषवांश्च सततं क्षत्रियः श्रियमर्छति ।पुष्पवत्सु ध्रुवा श्रीश्च पुष्पवन्तस्ततो वयम् ॥४६॥
transliteration: viśeṣavāṁśca satataṁ kṣatriyaḥ śriyamarchati ,puṣpavatsu dhruvā śrīśca puṣpavantastato vayam.
english: And a specially distinguished kshatriya (kṣatriya) always attains prosperity (śrī). Prosperity (śrī) is enduring among the prosperous, and therefore, we are also prosperous.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 46. And a specially distinguished kshatriya (kṣatriya) always attains prosperity (śrī). Prosperity (śrī) is enduring among the prosperous, and therefore, we are also prosperous.

sanskrit: क्षत्रियो बाहुवीर्यस्तु न तथा वाक्यवीर्यवान् ।अप्रगल्भं वचस्तस्य तस्माद्बार्हद्रथे स्मृतम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: kṣatriyo bāhuvīryastu na tathā vākyavīryavān ,apragalbhaṁ vacastasya tasmādbārhadrathe smṛtam.
english: A kshatriya (kṣatriya) is indeed strong through his arms, but not so much through his words. His speech is not eloquent, therefore, this is remembered concerning Bṛhadratha's son (Jarāsandha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 47. A kshatriya (kṣatriya) is indeed strong through his arms, but not so much through his words. His speech is not eloquent, therefore, this is remembered concerning Bṛhadratha's son (Jarāsandha).

sanskrit: स्ववीर्यं क्षत्रियाणां च बाह्वोर्धाता न्यवेशयत् ।तद्दिदृक्षसि चेद्राजन्द्रष्टास्यद्य न संशयः ॥४८॥
transliteration: svavīryaṁ kṣatriyāṇāṁ ca bāhvordhātā nyaveśayat ,taddidṛkṣasi cedrājandraṣṭāsyadya na saṁśayaḥ.
english: And the Creator (dhātā) placed the intrinsic strength (svavīrya) of the kshatriyas (kṣatriya) in their arms. If, O King, you wish to see that, you shall certainly see it today; there is no doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 48. And the Creator (dhātā) placed the intrinsic strength (svavīrya) of the kshatriyas (kṣatriya) in their arms. If, O King, you wish to see that, you shall certainly see it today; there is no doubt.

sanskrit: अद्वारेण रिपोर्गेहं द्वारेण सुहृदो गृहम् ।प्रविशन्ति सदा सन्तो द्वारं नो वर्जितं ततः ॥४९॥
transliteration: advāreṇa riporgehaṁ dvāreṇa suhṛdo gṛham ,praviśanti sadā santo dvāraṁ no varjitaṁ tataḥ.
english: Righteous individuals (santaḥ) always enter an enemy's dwelling through unauthorized means, while they enter a friend's home respectfully through the proper entrance. Consequently, no entrance is ever closed to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 49. Righteous individuals (santaḥ) always enter an enemy's dwelling through unauthorized means, while they enter a friend's home respectfully through the proper entrance. Consequently, no entrance is ever closed to them.

sanskrit: कार्यवन्तो गृहानेत्य शत्रुतो नार्हणां वयम् ।प्रतिगृह्णीम तद्विद्धि एतन्नः शाश्वतं व्रतम् ॥५०॥
transliteration: kāryavanto gṛhānetya śatruto nārhaṇāṁ vayam ,pratigṛhṇīma tadviddhi etannaḥ śāśvataṁ vratam.
english: We do not accept hospitality or honor from an enemy, even if they come to our homes with some purpose. Understand this; it is our eternal vow (vrata).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 19 Verse 50. We do not accept hospitality or honor from an enemy, even if they come to our homes with some purpose. Understand this; it is our eternal vow (vrata).

sanskrit: जरासंध उवाच ।न स्मरेयं कदा वैरं कृतं युष्माभिरित्युत ।चिन्तयंश्च न पश्यामि भवतां प्रति वैकृतम् ॥१॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdha uvāca ,na smareyaṁ kadā vairaṁ kṛtaṁ yuṣmābhirityuta ,cintayaṁśca na paśyāmi bhavatāṁ prati vaikṛtam.
english: Jarāsaṃdha said: 'I cannot remember any hostility initiated by you. Even after careful consideration, I perceive no malicious act on your part.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 1. Jarāsaṃdha said: 'I cannot remember any hostility initiated by you. Even after careful consideration, I perceive no malicious act on your part.'

sanskrit: वैकृते चासति कथं मन्यध्वं मामनागसम् ।अरिं विब्रूत तद्विप्राः सतां समय एष हि ॥२॥
transliteration: vaikṛte cāsati kathaṁ manyadhvaṁ māmanāgasam ,ariṁ vibrūta tadviprāḥ satāṁ samaya eṣa hi.
english: And since no malicious act (vaikṛta) exists on my part, how can you consider me faultless? Therefore, O learned ones (viprāḥ), declare me an enemy, because this is truly the code of conduct (samaya) for virtuous individuals (satām).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 2. And since no malicious act (vaikṛta) exists on my part, how can you consider me faultless? Therefore, O learned ones (viprāḥ), declare me an enemy, because this is truly the code of conduct (samaya) for virtuous individuals (satām).

sanskrit: अथ धर्मोपघाताद्धि मनः समुपतप्यते ।योऽनागसि प्रसृजति क्षत्रियोऽपि न संशयः ॥३॥
transliteration: atha dharmopaghātāddhi manaḥ samupatapyate ,yo'nāgasi prasṛjati kṣatriyo'pi na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Now, certainly, the mind is greatly tormented by the violation of natural law (dharma). There is no doubt that even a warrior (kṣatriya) who harms an innocent person experiences such anguish.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 3. Now, certainly, the mind is greatly tormented by the violation of natural law (dharma). There is no doubt that even a warrior (kṣatriya) who harms an innocent person experiences such anguish.

sanskrit: अतोऽन्यथाचरँल्लोके धर्मज्ञः सन्महाव्रतः ।वृजिनां गतिमाप्नोति श्रेयसोऽप्युपहन्ति च ॥४॥
transliteration: ato'nyathācaraँlloke dharmajñaḥ sanmahāvrataḥ ,vṛjināṁ gatimāpnoti śreyaso'pyupahanti ca.
english: Therefore, anyone in this world who acts contrary to natural law (dharma), even while being knowledgeable of duty (dharma-jña) and having great vows (mahāvrata), attains the destiny of the wicked and also undermines their own well-being.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 4. Therefore, anyone in this world who acts contrary to natural law (dharma), even while being knowledgeable of duty (dharma-jña) and having great vows (mahāvrata), attains the destiny of the wicked and also undermines their own well-being.

sanskrit: त्रैलोक्ये क्षत्रधर्माद्धि श्रेयांसं साधुचारिणाम् ।अनागसं प्रजानानाः प्रमादादिव जल्पथ ॥५॥
transliteration: trailokye kṣatradharmāddhi śreyāṁsaṁ sādhucāriṇām ,anāgasaṁ prajānānāḥ pramādādiva jalpatha.
english: Indeed, you speak as if from carelessness, you who know the innocent (anāgasam) and the virtuous (sādhucāriṇām), claiming that something is superior (śreyāṃsam) to the warrior's (kṣatra) natural law (dharma) in the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 5. Indeed, you speak as if from carelessness, you who know the innocent (anāgasam) and the virtuous (sādhucāriṇām), claiming that something is superior (śreyāṃsam) to the warrior's (kṣatra) natural law (dharma) in the three worlds.

sanskrit: वासुदेव उवाच ।कुलकार्यं महाराज कश्चिदेकः कुलोद्वहः ।वहते तन्नियोगाद्वै वयमभ्युत्थितास्त्रयः ॥६॥
transliteration: vāsudeva uvāca ,kulakāryaṁ mahārāja kaścidekaḥ kulodvahaḥ ,vahate tanniyogādvai vayamabhyutthitāstrayaḥ.
english: Vāsudeva said: 'O Great King, it is indeed one upholder of the lineage (kulodvaha) who carries out the family's duty. By his directive, we three have risen to action.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 6. Vāsudeva said: 'O Great King, it is indeed one upholder of the lineage (kulodvaha) who carries out the family's duty. By his directive, we three have risen to action.'

sanskrit: त्वया चोपहृता राजन्क्षत्रिया लोकवासिनः ।तदागः क्रूरमुत्पाद्य मन्यसे किं त्वनागसम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tvayā copahṛtā rājankṣatriyā lokavāsinaḥ ,tadāgaḥ krūramutpādya manyase kiṁ tvanāgasam.
english: O King, you have killed the kshatriyas who inhabit the world. Having committed such a cruel offense, do you then consider yourself to be innocent?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 7. O King, you have killed the kshatriyas who inhabit the world. Having committed such a cruel offense, do you then consider yourself to be innocent?

sanskrit: राजा राज्ञः कथं साधून्हिंस्यान्नृपतिसत्तम ।तद्राज्ञः संनिगृह्य त्वं रुद्रायोपजिहीर्षसि ॥८॥
transliteration: rājā rājñaḥ kathaṁ sādhūnhiṁsyānnṛpatisattama ,tadrājñaḥ saṁnigṛhya tvaṁ rudrāyopajihīrṣasi.
english: O best of kings, how can a king harm righteous people (sādhūn), who are themselves kings? Having seized those kings, you intend to offer them as a sacrifice to Rudra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 8. O best of kings, how can a king harm righteous people (sādhūn), who are themselves kings? Having seized those kings, you intend to offer them as a sacrifice to Rudra.

sanskrit: अस्मांस्तदेनो गच्छेत त्वया बार्हद्रथे कृतम् ।वयं हि शक्ता धर्मस्य रक्षणे धर्मचारिणः ॥९॥
transliteration: asmāṁstadeno gaccheta tvayā bārhadrathe kṛtam ,vayaṁ hi śaktā dharmasya rakṣaṇe dharmacāriṇaḥ.
english: O son of Bṛhadratha, that sin (enas) committed by you would fall upon us. Indeed, we who uphold the natural law (dharma) are capable of its protection.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 9. O son of Bṛhadratha, that sin (enas) committed by you would fall upon us. Indeed, we who uphold the natural law (dharma) are capable of its protection.

sanskrit: मनुष्याणां समालम्भो न च दृष्टः कदाचन ।स कथं मानुषैर्देवं यष्टुमिच्छसि शंकरम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: manuṣyāṇāṁ samālambho na ca dṛṣṭaḥ kadācana ,sa kathaṁ mānuṣairdevaṁ yaṣṭumicchasi śaṁkaram.
english: The Vedic ritual (yajña) or slaughter of humans has never been observed. How then do you wish to offer the god Shankara a sacrifice with humans?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 10. The Vedic ritual (yajña) or slaughter of humans has never been observed. How then do you wish to offer the god Shankara a sacrifice with humans?

sanskrit: सवर्णो हि सवर्णानां पशुसंज्ञां करिष्यति ।कोऽन्य एवं यथा हि त्वं जरासंध वृथामतिः ॥११॥
transliteration: savarṇo hi savarṇānāṁ paśusaṁjñāṁ kariṣyati ,ko'nya evaṁ yathā hi tvaṁ jarāsaṁdha vṛthāmatiḥ.
english: Indeed, a person of the same social class will treat others of that same class as mere animals. Who else but you, O Jarāsaṃdha, with your misguided intellect, would act in such a way?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 11. Indeed, a person of the same social class will treat others of that same class as mere animals. Who else but you, O Jarāsaṃdha, with your misguided intellect, would act in such a way?

sanskrit: ते त्वां ज्ञातिक्षयकरं वयमार्तानुसारिणः ।ज्ञातिवृद्धिनिमित्तार्थं विनियन्तुमिहागताः ॥१२॥
transliteration: te tvāṁ jñātikṣayakaraṁ vayamārtānusāriṇaḥ ,jñātivṛddhinimittārthaṁ viniyantumihāgatāḥ.
english: We, who sympathize with the distressed, have come here to restrain you, the destroyer of kinsmen, for the sake of our kinsmen's prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 12. We, who sympathize with the distressed, have come here to restrain you, the destroyer of kinsmen, for the sake of our kinsmen's prosperity.

sanskrit: नास्ति लोके पुमानन्यः क्षत्रियेष्विति चैव यत् ।मन्यसे स च ते राजन्सुमहान्बुद्धिविप्लवः ॥१३॥
transliteration: nāsti loke pumānanyaḥ kṣatriyeṣviti caiva yat ,manyase sa ca te rājansumahānbuddhiviplavaḥ.
english: And that belief of yours, O King, that there is no other man among the kṣatriyas (warriors) capable of challenging you, is indeed a very great perversion of your intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 13. And that belief of yours, O King, that there is no other man among the kṣatriyas (warriors) capable of challenging you, is indeed a very great perversion of your intellect.

sanskrit: को हि जानन्नभिजनमात्मनः क्षत्रियो नृप ।नाविशेत्स्वर्गमतुलं रणानन्तरमव्ययम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: ko hi jānannabhijanamātmanaḥ kṣatriyo nṛpa ,nāviśetsvargamatulaṁ raṇānantaramavyayam.
english: Indeed, O King, what kṣatriya (warrior), knowing his own (ātman) noble lineage, would not attain the unequalled and imperishable heaven immediately after battle?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 14. Indeed, O King, what kṣatriya (warrior), knowing his own (ātman) noble lineage, would not attain the unequalled and imperishable heaven immediately after battle?

sanskrit: स्वर्गं ह्येव समास्थाय रणयज्ञेषु दीक्षिताः ।यजन्ते क्षत्रिया लोकांस्तद्विद्धि मगधाधिप ॥१५॥
transliteration: svargaṁ hyeva samāsthāya raṇayajñeṣu dīkṣitāḥ ,yajante kṣatriyā lokāṁstadviddhi magadhādhipa.
english: O ruler of Magadha, know this: kṣatriyas, who are consecrated (dīkṣita) for the Vedic rituals (yajña) of war and have their minds firmly set on attaining heaven, indeed achieve (celestial) worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 15. O ruler of Magadha, know this: kṣatriyas, who are consecrated (dīkṣita) for the Vedic rituals (yajña) of war and have their minds firmly set on attaining heaven, indeed achieve (celestial) worlds.

sanskrit: स्वर्गयोनिर्जयो राजन्स्वर्गयोनिर्महद्यशः ।स्वर्गयोनिस्तपो युद्धे मार्गः सोऽव्यभिचारवान् ॥१६॥
transliteration: svargayonirjayo rājansvargayonirmahadyaśaḥ ,svargayonistapo yuddhe mārgaḥ so'vyabhicāravān.
english: O King, victory leads to heaven, great fame leads to heaven, and the discipline (tapas) in battle also leads to heaven. That is the unfailing path.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 16. O King, victory leads to heaven, great fame leads to heaven, and the discipline (tapas) in battle also leads to heaven. That is the unfailing path.

sanskrit: एष ह्यैन्द्रो वैजयन्तो गुणो नित्यं समाहितः ।येनासुरान्पराजित्य जगत्पाति शतक्रतुः ॥१७॥
transliteration: eṣa hyaindro vaijayanto guṇo nityaṁ samāhitaḥ ,yenāsurānparājitya jagatpāti śatakratuḥ.
english: Indeed, this is the divine, victorious quality, always possessed. By means of it, Indra (śatakratu), having conquered the asuras, protects the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 17. Indeed, this is the divine, victorious quality, always possessed. By means of it, Indra (śatakratu), having conquered the asuras, protects the world.

sanskrit: स्वर्गमास्थाय कस्य स्याद्विग्रहित्वं यथा तव ।मागधैर्विपुलैः सैन्यैर्बाहुल्यबलदर्पितैः ॥१८॥
transliteration: svargamāsthāya kasya syādvigrahitvaṁ yathā tava ,māgadhairvipulaiḥ sainyairbāhulyabaladarpitaiḥ.
english: Having fixed your goal on heaven, whose animosity could compare to yours? [Yours, supported] by vast Magadhan armies, arrogant due to their abundant strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 18. Having fixed your goal on heaven, whose animosity could compare to yours? [Yours, supported] by vast Magadhan armies, arrogant due to their abundant strength.

sanskrit: मावमंस्थाः परान्राजन्नास्ति वीर्यं नरे नरे ।समं तेजस्त्वया चैव केवलं मनुजेश्वर ॥१९॥
transliteration: māvamaṁsthāḥ parānrājannāsti vīryaṁ nare nare ,samaṁ tejastvayā caiva kevalaṁ manujeśvara.
english: O King, do not disrespect others! Not every person possesses equal strength (vīrya), and power (tejas) resides in you alone, O lord of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 19. O King, do not disrespect others! Not every person possesses equal strength (vīrya), and power (tejas) resides in you alone, O lord of men.

sanskrit: यावदेव न संबुद्धं तावदेव भवेत्तव ।विषह्यमेतदस्माकमतो राजन्ब्रवीमि ते ॥२०॥
transliteration: yāvadeva na saṁbuddhaṁ tāvadeva bhavettava ,viṣahyametadasmākamato rājanbravīmi te.
english: As long as this is not properly understood, so long will it remain your concern. This much is tolerable for us; therefore, O King, I speak to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 20. As long as this is not properly understood, so long will it remain your concern. This much is tolerable for us; therefore, O King, I speak to you.

sanskrit: जहि त्वं सदृशेष्वेव मानं दर्पं च मागध ।मा गमः ससुतामात्यः सबलश्च यमक्षयम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: jahi tvaṁ sadṛśeṣveva mānaṁ darpaṁ ca māgadha ,mā gamaḥ sasutāmātyaḥ sabalaśca yamakṣayam.
english: O King of Magadha, give up your pride (māna) and arrogance (darpa) towards those who are your equals! Do not go, along with your sons, ministers, and army, to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 21. O King of Magadha, give up your pride (māna) and arrogance (darpa) towards those who are your equals! Do not go, along with your sons, ministers, and army, to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: दम्भोद्भवः कार्तवीर्य उत्तरश्च बृहद्रथः ।श्रेयसो ह्यवमन्येह विनेशुः सबला नृपाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: dambhodbhavaḥ kārtavīrya uttaraśca bṛhadrathaḥ ,śreyaso hyavamanyeha vineśuḥ sabalā nṛpāḥ.
english: Dambhodbhava, Kārtavīrya, Uttara, and Bṛhadratha – these kings, indeed, having disrespected superior individuals, perished here along with their armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 22. Dambhodbhava, Kārtavīrya, Uttara, and Bṛhadratha – these kings, indeed, having disrespected superior individuals, perished here along with their armies.

sanskrit: मुमुक्षमाणास्त्वत्तश्च न वयं ब्राह्मणब्रुवाः ।शौरिरस्मि हृषीकेशो नृवीरौ पाण्डवाविमौ ॥२३॥
transliteration: mumukṣamāṇāstvattaśca na vayaṁ brāhmaṇabruvāḥ ,śaurirasmi hṛṣīkeśo nṛvīrau pāṇḍavāvimau.
english: We are not Brahmins merely calling ourselves such, desiring liberation (mokṣa) from you. I am Hṛṣīkeśa, the son of Śūra, and these two Pāṇḍavas are heroes among men.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 23. We are not Brahmins merely calling ourselves such, desiring liberation (mokṣa) from you. I am Hṛṣīkeśa, the son of Śūra, and these two Pāṇḍavas are heroes among men.

sanskrit: त्वामाह्वयामहे राजन्स्थिरो युध्यस्व मागध ।मुञ्च वा नृपतीन्सर्वान्मा गमस्त्वं यमक्षयम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tvāmāhvayāmahe rājansthiro yudhyasva māgadha ,muñca vā nṛpatīnsarvānmā gamastvaṁ yamakṣayam.
english: O King of Magadha, we challenge you! Fight steadfastly, or else release all the kings, so that you do not go to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 24. O King of Magadha, we challenge you! Fight steadfastly, or else release all the kings, so that you do not go to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: जरासंध उवाच ।नाजितान्वै नरपतीनहमादद्मि कांश्चन ।जितः कः पर्यवस्थाता कोऽत्र यो न मया जितः ॥२५॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdha uvāca ,nājitānvai narapatīnahamādadmi kāṁścana ,jitaḥ kaḥ paryavasthātā ko'tra yo na mayā jitaḥ.
english: Jarāsaṃdha said, "Indeed, I do not take any kings who are not (first) conquered. Who, once conquered, remains as an opponent? Who is there in this world whom I have not conquered?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 25. Jarāsaṃdha said, "Indeed, I do not take any kings who are not (first) conquered. Who, once conquered, remains as an opponent? Who is there in this world whom I have not conquered?"

sanskrit: क्षत्रियस्यैतदेवाहुर्धर्म्यं कृष्णोपजीवनम् ।विक्रम्य वशमानीय कामतो यत्समाचरेत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: kṣatriyasyaitadevāhurdharmyaṁ kṛṣṇopajīvanam ,vikramya vaśamānīya kāmato yatsamācaret.
english: They say that for a kṣatriya, this alone is the righteous (dharmya) way of life for a warrior: to act as one desires after displaying valor and bringing others under control.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 26. They say that for a kṣatriya, this alone is the righteous (dharmya) way of life for a warrior: to act as one desires after displaying valor and bringing others under control.

sanskrit: देवतार्थमुपाकृत्य राज्ञः कृष्ण कथं भयात् ।अहमद्य विमुञ्चेयं क्षात्रं व्रतमनुस्मरन् ॥२७॥
transliteration: devatārthamupākṛtya rājñaḥ kṛṣṇa kathaṁ bhayāt ,ahamadya vimuñceyaṁ kṣātraṁ vratamanusmaran.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, having consecrated the king for the sake of the gods (devatā), how can I, remembering my warrior's (kṣātra) vow (vrata), abandon it today out of fear?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 27. O Kṛṣṇa, having consecrated the king for the sake of the gods (devatā), how can I, remembering my warrior's (kṣātra) vow (vrata), abandon it today out of fear?

sanskrit: सैन्यं सैन्येन व्यूढेन एक एकेन वा पुनः ।द्वाभ्यां त्रिभिर्वा योत्स्येऽहं युगपत्पृथगेव वा ॥२८॥
transliteration: sainyaṁ sainyena vyūḍhena eka ekena vā punaḥ ,dvābhyāṁ tribhirvā yotsye'haṁ yugapatpṛthageva vā.
english: I will fight an army against an arrayed army, or one-on-one; or again, by two (against two), or by three (against three), either simultaneously or separately.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 28. I will fight an army against an arrayed army, or one-on-one; or again, by two (against two), or by three (against three), either simultaneously or separately.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा जरासंधः सहदेवाभिषेचनम् ।आज्ञापयत्तदा राजा युयुत्सुर्भीमकर्मभिः ॥२९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā jarāsaṁdhaḥ sahadevābhiṣecanam ,ājñāpayattadā rājā yuyutsurbhīmakarmabhiḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, King Jarāsaṃdha, eager for battle (yuyutsu) and known for terrible deeds, then commanded the anointing (abhiṣecana) of Sahadeva (for sacrifice).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 29. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, King Jarāsaṃdha, eager for battle (yuyutsu) and known for terrible deeds, then commanded the anointing (abhiṣecana) of Sahadeva (for sacrifice).

sanskrit: स तु सेनापती राजा सस्मार भरतर्षभ ।कौशिकं चित्रसेनं च तस्मिन्युद्ध उपस्थिते ॥३०॥
transliteration: sa tu senāpatī rājā sasmāra bharatarṣabha ,kauśikaṁ citrasenaṁ ca tasminyuddha upasthite.
english: But that king (Jarāsaṃdha), O best of Bharatas, remembered (sasmāra) his two commanders (senāpati), Kauśika and Citrasena, when that battle (yuddha) was imminent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 30. But that king (Jarāsaṃdha), O best of Bharatas, remembered (sasmāra) his two commanders (senāpati), Kauśika and Citrasena, when that battle (yuddha) was imminent.

sanskrit: ययोस्ते नामनी लोके हंसेति डिभकेति च ।पूर्वं संकथिते पुम्भिर्नृलोके लोकसत्कृते ॥३१॥
transliteration: yayoste nāmanī loke haṁseti ḍibhaketi ca ,pūrvaṁ saṁkathite pumbhirnṛloke lokasatkṛte.
english: In the world, their two names, Haṃsa and Ḍibhaka, were formerly spoken of by men in the human realm, which is honored by all people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 31. In the world, their two names, Haṃsa and Ḍibhaka, were formerly spoken of by men in the human realm, which is honored by all people.

sanskrit: तं तु राजन्विभुः शौरी राजानं बलिनां वरम् ।स्मृत्वा पुरुषशार्दूल शार्दूलसमविक्रमम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: taṁ tu rājanvibhuḥ śaurī rājānaṁ balināṁ varam ,smṛtvā puruṣaśārdūla śārdūlasamavikramam.
english: But, O King, O tiger among men, the powerful Kṛṣṇa (Śaurī), having remembered that king - the best among the strong, whose might was like that of a tiger - ...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 32. But, O King, O tiger among men, the powerful Kṛṣṇa (Śaurī), having remembered that king - the best among the strong, whose might was like that of a tiger - ...

sanskrit: सत्यसंधो जरासंधं भुवि भीमपराक्रमम् ।भागमन्यस्य निर्दिष्टं वध्यं भूमिभृदच्युतः ॥३३॥
transliteration: satyasaṁdho jarāsaṁdhaṁ bhuvi bhīmaparākramam ,bhāgamanyasya nirdiṣṭaṁ vadhyaṁ bhūmibhṛdacyutaḥ.
english: The truthful (satyasaṃdha), infallible (acyuta) protector of the earth (bhūmibhṛt) [remembered] Jarāsaṅdha, who possessed terrible valor on earth, and who was designated as the killable portion of another.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 33. The truthful (satyasaṃdha), infallible (acyuta) protector of the earth (bhūmibhṛt) [remembered] Jarāsaṅdha, who possessed terrible valor on earth, and who was designated as the killable portion of another.

sanskrit: नात्मनात्मवतां मुख्य इयेष मधुसूदनः ।ब्रह्मणोऽऽज्ञां पुरस्कृत्य हन्तुं हलधरानुजः ॥३४॥
transliteration: nātmanātmavatāṁ mukhya iyeṣa madhusūdanaḥ ,brahmaṇo''jñāṁ puraskṛtya hantuṁ haladharānujaḥ.
english: The foremost among those possessing self-control (ātman), Kṛṣṇa (Madhusūdana), the younger brother of Baladeva (Haladharānuja), did not desire to kill [Jarāsaṅdha] himself, having honored Brahmā's command.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 20 Verse 34. The foremost among those possessing self-control (ātman), Kṛṣṇa (Madhusūdana), the younger brother of Baladeva (Haladharānuja), did not desire to kill [Jarāsaṅdha] himself, having honored Brahmā's command.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततस्तं निश्चितात्मानं युद्धाय यदुनन्दनः ।उवाच वाग्मी राजानं जरासंधमधोक्षजः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tatastaṁ niścitātmānaṁ yuddhāya yadunandanaḥ ,uvāca vāgmī rājānaṁ jarāsaṁdhamadhokṣajaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the eloquent Krishna (yadunandana), Adhokṣaja, addressed King Jarasandha, who was resolute in his mind (ātman) for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, the eloquent Krishna (yadunandana), Adhokṣaja, addressed King Jarasandha, who was resolute in his mind (ātman) for battle.

sanskrit: त्रयाणां केन ते राजन्योद्धुं वितरते मनः ।अस्मदन्यतमेनेह सज्जीभवतु को युधि ॥२॥
transliteration: trayāṇāṁ kena te rājanyoddhuṁ vitarate manaḥ ,asmadanyatameneha sajjībhavatu ko yudhi.
english: O King, among the three of us, with whom does your mind incline to fight? Let that one among us be ready for battle here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 2. O King, among the three of us, with whom does your mind incline to fight? Let that one among us be ready for battle here.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स कृष्णेन युद्धं वव्रे महाद्युतिः ।जरासंधस्ततो राजन्भीमसेनेन मागधः ॥३॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa kṛṣṇena yuddhaṁ vavre mahādyutiḥ ,jarāsaṁdhastato rājanbhīmasenena māgadhaḥ.
english: O King, thus addressed by Krishna, the greatly radiant Jarasandha, the king of Magadha, then chose battle with Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 3. O King, thus addressed by Krishna, the greatly radiant Jarasandha, the king of Magadha, then chose battle with Bhimasena.

sanskrit: धारयन्नगदान्मुख्यान्निर्वृतीर्वेदनानि च ।उपतस्थे जरासंधं युयुत्सुं वै पुरोहितः ॥४॥
transliteration: dhārayannagadānmukhyānnirvṛtīrvedanāni ca ,upatasthe jarāsaṁdhaṁ yuyutsuṁ vai purohitaḥ.
english: The priest, carrying important medicines (agada) and healing remedies (nirvṛti), indeed approached Jarasandha, who was eager for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 4. The priest, carrying important medicines (agada) and healing remedies (nirvṛti), indeed approached Jarasandha, who was eager for battle.

sanskrit: कृतस्वस्त्ययनो विद्वान्ब्राह्मणेन यशस्विना ।समनह्यज्जरासंधः क्षत्रधर्ममनुव्रतः ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛtasvastyayano vidvānbrāhmaṇena yaśasvinā ,samanahyajjarāsaṁdhaḥ kṣatradharmamanuvrataḥ.
english: After a renowned and learned brahmin had performed the propitiatory rites for him, Jarāsandha, who adhered to the code of conduct for warriors (kṣatra-dharma), prepared himself for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 5. After a renowned and learned brahmin had performed the propitiatory rites for him, Jarāsandha, who adhered to the code of conduct for warriors (kṣatra-dharma), prepared himself for battle.

sanskrit: अवमुच्य किरीटं स केशान्समनुमृज्य च ।उदतिष्ठज्जरासंधो वेलातिग इवार्णवः ॥६॥
transliteration: avamucya kirīṭaṁ sa keśānsamanumṛjya ca ,udatiṣṭhajjarāsaṁdho velātiga ivārṇavaḥ.
english: He (Jarāsandha), taking off his crown and smoothing his hair, stood up like an ocean overflowing its bounds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 6. He (Jarāsandha), taking off his crown and smoothing his hair, stood up like an ocean overflowing its bounds.

sanskrit: उवाच मतिमान्राजा भीमं भीमपराक्रमम् ।भीम योत्स्ये त्वया सार्धं श्रेयसा निर्जितं वरम् ॥७॥
transliteration: uvāca matimānrājā bhīmaṁ bhīmaparākramam ,bhīma yotsye tvayā sārdhaṁ śreyasā nirjitaṁ varam.
english: The wise king (Jarāsandha) addressed Bhīma, who possessed formidable valor: 'O Bhīma, I will fight with you. Indeed, the excellent (among warriors) should be defeated by a superior one.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 7. The wise king (Jarāsandha) addressed Bhīma, who possessed formidable valor: 'O Bhīma, I will fight with you. Indeed, the excellent (among warriors) should be defeated by a superior one.'

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा जरासंधो भीमसेनमरिंदमः ।प्रत्युद्ययौ महातेजाः शक्रं बलिरिवासुरः ॥८॥
transliteration: evamuktvā jarāsaṁdho bhīmasenamariṁdamaḥ ,pratyudyayau mahātejāḥ śakraṁ balirivāsuraḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, Jarāsandha, the subduer of enemies and a man of great energy, advanced against Bhīmasena, just as the demon (asura) Bali advanced against Śakra (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 8. Having spoken thus, Jarāsandha, the subduer of enemies and a man of great energy, advanced against Bhīmasena, just as the demon (asura) Bali advanced against Śakra (Indra).

sanskrit: ततः संमन्त्र्य कृष्णेन कृतस्वस्त्ययनो बली ।भीमसेनो जरासंधमाससाद युयुत्सया ॥९॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁmantrya kṛṣṇena kṛtasvastyayano balī ,bhīmaseno jarāsaṁdhamāsasāda yuyutsayā.
english: Then, having consulted with Kṛṣṇa and having performed the propitiatory rites (svastyayana), the mighty Bhīmasena approached Jarāsaṃdha with the desire to fight.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 9. Then, having consulted with Kṛṣṇa and having performed the propitiatory rites (svastyayana), the mighty Bhīmasena approached Jarāsaṃdha with the desire to fight.

sanskrit: ततस्तौ नरशार्दूलौ बाहुशस्त्रौ समीयतुः ।वीरौ परमसंहृष्टावन्योन्यजयकाङ्क्षिणौ ॥१०॥
transliteration: tatastau naraśārdūlau bāhuśastrau samīyatuḥ ,vīrau paramasaṁhṛṣṭāvanyonyajayakāṅkṣiṇau.
english: Then those two tiger-like men, whose weapons were their arms, approached each other. They were heroes, extremely delighted, and desirous of conquering one another.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 10. Then those two tiger-like men, whose weapons were their arms, approached each other. They were heroes, extremely delighted, and desirous of conquering one another.

sanskrit: तयोरथ भुजाघातान्निग्रहप्रग्रहात्तथा ।आसीत्सुभीमसंह्रादो वज्रपर्वतयोरिव ॥११॥
transliteration: tayoratha bhujāghātānnigrahapragrahāttathā ,āsītsubhīmasaṁhrādo vajraparvatayoriva.
english: Then, from the blows of their arms and similarly from their grasping and releasing, there arose a terribly fierce roar, like that of crashing thunderbolts and mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 11. Then, from the blows of their arms and similarly from their grasping and releasing, there arose a terribly fierce roar, like that of crashing thunderbolts and mountains.

sanskrit: उभौ परमसंहृष्टौ बलेनातिबलावुभौ ।अन्योन्यस्यान्तरं प्रेप्सू परस्परजयैषिणौ ॥१२॥
transliteration: ubhau paramasaṁhṛṣṭau balenātibalāvubhau ,anyonyasyāntaraṁ prepsū parasparajayaiṣiṇau.
english: Both were extremely delighted and both were exceedingly powerful due to their strength. They were both seeking an opening in each other, and both desired mutual victory.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 12. Both were extremely delighted and both were exceedingly powerful due to their strength. They were both seeking an opening in each other, and both desired mutual victory.

sanskrit: तद्भीममुत्सार्य जनं युद्धमासीदुपह्वरे ।बलिनोः संयुगे राजन्वृत्रवासवयोरिव ॥१३॥
transliteration: tadbhīmamutsārya janaṁ yuddhamāsīdupahvare ,balinoḥ saṁyuge rājanvṛtravāsavayoriva.
english: O King, having driven away that terrified crowd, the battle ensued in close quarters, just like the combat between the powerful Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 13. O King, having driven away that terrified crowd, the battle ensued in close quarters, just like the combat between the powerful Vṛtra and Vāsava (Indra).

sanskrit: प्रकर्षणाकर्षणाभ्यामभ्याकर्षविकर्षणैः ।आकर्षेतां तथान्योन्यं जानुभिश्चाभिजघ्नतुः ॥१४॥
transliteration: prakarṣaṇākarṣaṇābhyāmabhyākarṣavikarṣaṇaiḥ ,ākarṣetāṁ tathānyonyaṁ jānubhiścābhijaghnatuḥ.
english: Through repeated pulling and drawing, and through dragging and pushing apart, they pulled each other back and forth, and they also struck each other with their knees.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 14. Through repeated pulling and drawing, and through dragging and pushing apart, they pulled each other back and forth, and they also struck each other with their knees.

sanskrit: ततः शब्देन महता भर्त्सयन्तौ परस्परम् ।पाषाणसंघातनिभैः प्रहारैरभिजघ्नतुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ śabdena mahatā bhartsayantau parasparam ,pāṣāṇasaṁghātanibhaiḥ prahārairabhijaghnatuḥ.
english: Then, threatening each other with a great roar, they struck each other with blows that resembled masses of rock.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 15. Then, threatening each other with a great roar, they struck each other with blows that resembled masses of rock.

sanskrit: व्यूढोरस्कौ दीर्घभुजौ नियुद्धकुशलावुभौ ।बाहुभिः समसज्जेतामायसैः परिघैरिव ॥१६॥
transliteration: vyūḍhoraskau dīrghabhujau niyuddhakuśalāvubhau ,bāhubhiḥ samasajjetāmāyasaiḥ parighairiva.
english: Both of them, having broad chests, long arms, and being skilled in wrestling, engaged each other with their arms as if with iron maces.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 16. Both of them, having broad chests, long arms, and being skilled in wrestling, engaged each other with their arms as if with iron maces.

sanskrit: कार्त्तिकस्य तु मासस्य प्रवृत्तं प्रथमेऽहनि ।अनारतं दिवारात्रमविश्रान्तमवर्तत ॥१७॥
transliteration: kārttikasya tu māsasya pravṛttaṁ prathame'hani ,anārataṁ divārātramaviśrāntamavartata.
english: It commenced on the first day of the month of Kārttika and continued incessantly, day and night, without rest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 17. It commenced on the first day of the month of Kārttika and continued incessantly, day and night, without rest.

sanskrit: तद्वृत्तं तु त्रयोदश्यां समवेतं महात्मनोः ।चतुर्दश्यां निशायां तु निवृत्तो मागधः क्लमात् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tadvṛttaṁ tu trayodaśyāṁ samavetaṁ mahātmanoḥ ,caturdaśyāṁ niśāyāṁ tu nivṛtto māgadhaḥ klamāt.
english: That encounter of the two great souls occurred on the thirteenth day. But on the fourteenth night, the king of Magadha (Jarāsandha) withdrew due to exhaustion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 18. That encounter of the two great souls occurred on the thirteenth day. But on the fourteenth night, the king of Magadha (Jarāsandha) withdrew due to exhaustion.

sanskrit: तं राजानं तथा क्लान्तं दृष्ट्वा राजञ्जनार्दनः ।उवाच भीमकर्माणं भीमं संबोधयन्निव ॥१९॥
transliteration: taṁ rājānaṁ tathā klāntaṁ dṛṣṭvā rājañjanārdanaḥ ,uvāca bhīmakarmāṇaṁ bhīmaṁ saṁbodhayanniva.
english: O king (Yudhiṣṭhira), Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), seeing that king (Jarāsandha) so exhausted, spoke to Bhīma, the one of formidable deeds, as if offering counsel.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 19. O king (Yudhiṣṭhira), Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), seeing that king (Jarāsandha) so exhausted, spoke to Bhīma, the one of formidable deeds, as if offering counsel.

sanskrit: क्लान्तः शत्रुर्न कौन्तेय लभ्यः पीडयितुं रणे ।पीड्यमानो हि कार्त्स्न्येन जह्याज्जीवितमात्मनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: klāntaḥ śatrurna kaunteya labhyaḥ pīḍayituṁ raṇe ,pīḍyamāno hi kārtsnyena jahyājjīvitamātmanaḥ.
english: O son of Kuntī (Bhīma), an exhausted enemy should not be oppressed in battle. For if one is completely tormented, he would indeed give up his own life (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 20. O son of Kuntī (Bhīma), an exhausted enemy should not be oppressed in battle. For if one is completely tormented, he would indeed give up his own life (ātman).

sanskrit: तस्मात्ते नैव कौन्तेय पीडनीयो नराधिपः ।सममेतेन युध्यस्व बाहुभ्यां भरतर्षभ ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasmātte naiva kaunteya pīḍanīyo narādhipaḥ ,samametena yudhyasva bāhubhyāṁ bharatarṣabha.
english: Therefore, O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), that king should certainly not be harassed by you. O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), fight equally with him using your arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 21. Therefore, O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), that king should certainly not be harassed by you. O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), fight equally with him using your arms.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स कृष्णेन पाण्डवः परवीरहा ।जरासंधस्य तद्रन्ध्रं ज्ञात्वा चक्रे मतिं वधे ॥२२॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa kṛṣṇena pāṇḍavaḥ paravīrahā ,jarāsaṁdhasya tadrandhraṁ jñātvā cakre matiṁ vadhe.
english: Thus addressed by Kṛṣṇa, that Pāṇḍava, the slayer of enemy heroes, understanding Jarāsandha's weakness, resolved to kill him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 22. Thus addressed by Kṛṣṇa, that Pāṇḍava, the slayer of enemy heroes, understanding Jarāsandha's weakness, resolved to kill him.

sanskrit: ततस्तमजितं जेतुं जरासंधं वृकोदरः ।संरभ्य बलिनां मुख्यो जग्राह कुरुनन्दनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: tatastamajitaṁ jetuṁ jarāsaṁdhaṁ vṛkodaraḥ ,saṁrabhya balināṁ mukhyo jagrāha kurunandanaḥ.
english: Then, to conquer that invincible Jarāsandha, Vṛkodara, the chief among the strong and the delight of the Kurus (kurunandanaḥ), became enraged and seized him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 21 Verse 23. Then, to conquer that invincible Jarāsandha, Vṛkodara, the chief among the strong and the delight of the Kurus (kurunandanaḥ), became enraged and seized him.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।भीमसेनस्ततः कृष्णमुवाच यदुनन्दनम् ।बुद्धिमास्थाय विपुलां जरासंधजिघांसया ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,bhīmasenastataḥ kṛṣṇamuvāca yadunandanam ,buddhimāsthāya vipulāṁ jarāsaṁdhajighāṁsayā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then Bhīmasena, having adopted a great resolve with the desire to kill Jarāsandha, spoke to Kṛṣṇa, the delight of the Yadus (yadunandanam).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then Bhīmasena, having adopted a great resolve with the desire to kill Jarāsandha, spoke to Kṛṣṇa, the delight of the Yadus (yadunandanam).

sanskrit: नायं पापो मया कृष्ण युक्तः स्यादनुरोधितुम् ।प्राणेन यदुशार्दूल बद्धवङ्क्षणवाससा ॥२॥
transliteration: nāyaṁ pāpo mayā kṛṣṇa yuktaḥ syādanurodhitum ,prāṇena yaduśārdūla baddhavaṅkṣaṇavāsasā.
english: O Kṛṣṇa, O tiger among the Yadus, this wicked man, with his garment tied around his loins, should not be requested by me even at the cost of my life (prāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 2. O Kṛṣṇa, O tiger among the Yadus, this wicked man, with his garment tied around his loins, should not be requested by me even at the cost of my life (prāṇa).

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्ततः कृष्णः प्रत्युवाच वृकोदरम् ।त्वरयन्पुरुषव्याघ्रो जरासंधवधेप्सया ॥३॥
transliteration: evamuktastataḥ kṛṣṇaḥ pratyuvāca vṛkodaram ,tvarayanpuruṣavyāghro jarāsaṁdhavadhepsayā.
english: Having been addressed thus, Kṛṣṇa, that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), then replied to Vṛkodara (Bhīma), urging him on with the desire for Jarāsandha's slaying.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 3. Having been addressed thus, Kṛṣṇa, that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), then replied to Vṛkodara (Bhīma), urging him on with the desire for Jarāsandha's slaying.

sanskrit: यत्ते दैवं परं सत्त्वं यच्च ते मातरिश्वनः ।बलं भीम जरासंधे दर्शयाशु तदद्य नः ॥४॥
transliteration: yatte daivaṁ paraṁ sattvaṁ yacca te mātariśvanaḥ ,balaṁ bhīma jarāsaṁdhe darśayāśu tadadya naḥ.
english: O Bhīma, quickly display to us today against Jarāsandha that supreme divine strength (sattva) which you possess, and also the strength (bala) that belongs to Mātariśvan (Vāyu), your father.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 4. O Bhīma, quickly display to us today against Jarāsandha that supreme divine strength (sattva) which you possess, and also the strength (bala) that belongs to Mātariśvan (Vāyu), your father.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तस्तदा भीमो जरासंधमरिंदमः ।उत्क्षिप्य भ्रामयद्राजन्बलवन्तं महाबलः ॥५॥
transliteration: evamuktastadā bhīmo jarāsaṁdhamariṁdamaḥ ,utkṣipya bhrāmayadrājanbalavantaṁ mahābalaḥ.
english: O King, Bhīma, the subduer of enemies (ariṃdama) and immensely powerful (mahābala), having thus been addressed, then lifted up the mighty Jarāsandha and began to whirl him around.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 5. O King, Bhīma, the subduer of enemies (ariṃdama) and immensely powerful (mahābala), having thus been addressed, then lifted up the mighty Jarāsandha and began to whirl him around.

sanskrit: भ्रामयित्वा शतगुणं भुजाभ्यां भरतर्षभ ।बभञ्ज पृष्ठे संक्षिप्य निष्पिष्य विननाद च ॥६॥
transliteration: bhrāmayitvā śataguṇaṁ bhujābhyāṁ bharatarṣabha ,babhañja pṛṣṭhe saṁkṣipya niṣpiṣya vinanāda ca.
english: O best of Bharatas, having whirled him around a hundred times with his arms, he then bent and crushed his back, and roared.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 6. O best of Bharatas, having whirled him around a hundred times with his arms, he then bent and crushed his back, and roared.

sanskrit: तस्य निष्पिष्यमाणस्य पाण्डवस्य च गर्जतः ।अभवत्तुमुलो नादः सर्वप्राणिभयंकरः ॥७॥
transliteration: tasya niṣpiṣyamāṇasya pāṇḍavasya ca garjataḥ ,abhavattumulo nādaḥ sarvaprāṇibhayaṁkaraḥ.
english: While he (Jarāsandha) was being crushed and the Pandava (Bhīmasena) was roaring, there arose a tumultuous sound, terrifying to all creatures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 7. While he (Jarāsandha) was being crushed and the Pandava (Bhīmasena) was roaring, there arose a tumultuous sound, terrifying to all creatures.

sanskrit: वित्रेसुर्मागधाः सर्वे स्त्रीणां गर्भाश्च सुस्रुवुः ।भीमसेनस्य नादेन जरासंधस्य चैव ह ॥८॥
transliteration: vitresurmāgadhāḥ sarve strīṇāṁ garbhāśca susruvuḥ ,bhīmasenasya nādena jarāsaṁdhasya caiva ha.
english: All the people of Magadha trembled, and the pregnancies of the women miscarried, indeed, due to the roar of Bhīmasena and (the dying cries) of Jarāsandha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 8. All the people of Magadha trembled, and the pregnancies of the women miscarried, indeed, due to the roar of Bhīmasena and (the dying cries) of Jarāsandha.

sanskrit: किं नु स्विद्धिमवान्भिन्नः किं नु स्विद्दीर्यते मही ।इति स्म मागधा जज्ञुर्भीमसेनस्य निस्वनात् ॥९॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu sviddhimavānbhinnaḥ kiṁ nu sviddīryate mahī ,iti sma māgadhā jajñurbhīmasenasya nisvanāt.
english: The people of Magadha wondered, 'Has the Himālayan mountain indeed split apart? Is the earth indeed rending?'  -  hearing Bhīmasena's roar.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 9. The people of Magadha wondered, 'Has the Himālayan mountain indeed split apart? Is the earth indeed rending?'  -  hearing Bhīmasena's roar.

sanskrit: ततो राजकुलद्वारि प्रसुप्तमिव तं नृपम् ।रात्रौ परासुमुत्सृज्य निश्चक्रमुररिंदमाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato rājakuladvāri prasuptamiva taṁ nṛpam ,rātrau parāsumutsṛjya niścakramurariṁdamāḥ.
english: Then, at night, the subduers of foes departed, having left that king, who seemed merely asleep but was actually lifeless, at the gate of the royal palace.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 10. Then, at night, the subduers of foes departed, having left that king, who seemed merely asleep but was actually lifeless, at the gate of the royal palace.

sanskrit: जरासंधरथं कृष्णो योजयित्वा पताकिनम् ।आरोप्य भ्रातरौ चैव मोक्षयामास बान्धवान् ॥११॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdharathaṁ kṛṣṇo yojayitvā patākinam ,āropya bhrātarau caiva mokṣayāmāsa bāndhavān.
english: Krishna, having yoked Jarāsaṃdha's banner-adorned chariot and mounted the two brothers (Bhima and Arjuna) upon it, then liberated the imprisoned relatives.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 11. Krishna, having yoked Jarāsaṃdha's banner-adorned chariot and mounted the two brothers (Bhima and Arjuna) upon it, then liberated the imprisoned relatives.

sanskrit: ते वै रत्नभुजं कृष्णं रत्नार्हं पृथिवीश्वराः ।राजानश्चक्रुरासाद्य मोक्षिता महतो भयात् ॥१२॥
transliteration: te vai ratnabhujaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ ratnārhaṁ pṛthivīśvarāḥ ,rājānaścakrurāsādya mokṣitā mahato bhayāt.
english: Indeed, the kings of the earth, having been liberated (mokṣa) from great fear, approached Krishna – who had acquired precious jewels and was worthy of such royal emblems – and appointed him as their king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 12. Indeed, the kings of the earth, having been liberated (mokṣa) from great fear, approached Krishna – who had acquired precious jewels and was worthy of such royal emblems – and appointed him as their king.

sanskrit: अक्षतः शस्त्रसंपन्नो जितारिः सह राजभिः ।रथमास्थाय तं दिव्यं निर्जगाम गिरिव्रजात् ॥१३॥
transliteration: akṣataḥ śastrasaṁpanno jitāriḥ saha rājabhiḥ ,rathamāsthāya taṁ divyaṁ nirjagāma girivrajāt.
english: Uninjured, fully armed, and a vanquisher of foes, he (Krishna), along with the kings, mounted that divine chariot and departed from Girivraja.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 13. Uninjured, fully armed, and a vanquisher of foes, he (Krishna), along with the kings, mounted that divine chariot and departed from Girivraja.

sanskrit: यः स सोदर्यवान्नाम द्वियोधः कृष्णसारथिः ।अभ्यासघाती संदृश्यो दुर्जयः सर्वराजभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: yaḥ sa sodaryavānnāma dviyodhaḥ kṛṣṇasārathiḥ ,abhyāsaghātī saṁdṛśyo durjayaḥ sarvarājabhiḥ.
english: That famous warrior, who is endowed with brothers, fights like two men, and has Krishna as his charioteer, striking down opponents, is visibly unconquerable by all kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 14. That famous warrior, who is endowed with brothers, fights like two men, and has Krishna as his charioteer, striking down opponents, is visibly unconquerable by all kings.

sanskrit: भीमार्जुनाभ्यां योधाभ्यामास्थितः कृष्णसारथिः ।शुशुभे रथवर्योऽसौ दुर्जयः सर्वधन्विभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhīmārjunābhyāṁ yodhābhyāmāsthitaḥ kṛṣṇasārathiḥ ,śuśubhe rathavaryo'sau durjayaḥ sarvadhanvibhiḥ.
english: That excellent chariot, occupied by the two warriors Bhima and Arjuna, and having Krishna as its charioteer, shone splendidly, unconquerable by all archers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 15. That excellent chariot, occupied by the two warriors Bhima and Arjuna, and having Krishna as its charioteer, shone splendidly, unconquerable by all archers.

sanskrit: शक्रविष्णू हि संग्रामे चेरतुस्तारकामये ।रथेन तेन तं कृष्ण उपारुह्य ययौ तदा ॥१६॥
transliteration: śakraviṣṇū hi saṁgrāme ceratustārakāmaye ,rathena tena taṁ kṛṣṇa upāruhya yayau tadā.
english: Indeed, Indra and Vishnu (śakra-viṣṇū) fought in the Tārakā-maya war. Then, Krishna (kṛṣṇa), ascending with Arjuna in that very chariot, went forth.
