search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 16. Indeed, Indra and Vishnu (śakra-viṣṇū) fought in the Tārakā-maya war. Then, Krishna (kṛṣṇa), ascending with Arjuna in that very chariot, went forth.

sanskrit: तप्तचामीकराभेण किङ्किणीजालमालिना ।मेघनिर्घोषनादेन जैत्रेणामित्रघातिना ॥१७॥
transliteration: taptacāmīkarābheṇa kiṅkiṇījālamālinā ,meghanirghoṣanādena jaitreṇāmitraghātinā.
english: It was shining like molten gold, adorned with networks of small bells, sounding like the roar of clouds, victorious, and destructive to enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 17. It was shining like molten gold, adorned with networks of small bells, sounding like the roar of clouds, victorious, and destructive to enemies.

sanskrit: येन शक्रो दानवानां जघान नवतीर्नव ।तं प्राप्य समहृष्यन्त रथं ते पुरुषर्षभाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yena śakro dānavānāṁ jaghāna navatīrnava ,taṁ prāpya samahṛṣyanta rathaṁ te puruṣarṣabhāḥ.
english: Those foremost among men greatly rejoiced upon obtaining that chariot, by which Indra had once slain ninety Dānavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 18. Those foremost among men greatly rejoiced upon obtaining that chariot, by which Indra had once slain ninety Dānavas.

sanskrit: ततः कृष्णं महाबाहुं भ्रातृभ्यां सहितं तदा ।रथस्थं मागधा दृष्ट्वा समपद्यन्त विस्मिताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛṣṇaṁ mahābāhuṁ bhrātṛbhyāṁ sahitaṁ tadā ,rathasthaṁ māgadhā dṛṣṭvā samapadyanta vismitāḥ.
english: Then, upon seeing the mighty-armed Krishna, who was accompanied by his two brothers and standing on the chariot, the people of Magadha became utterly astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 19. Then, upon seeing the mighty-armed Krishna, who was accompanied by his two brothers and standing on the chariot, the people of Magadha became utterly astonished.

sanskrit: हयैर्दिव्यैः समायुक्तो रथो वायुसमो जवे ।अधिष्ठितः स शुशुभे कृष्णेनातीव भारत ॥२०॥
transliteration: hayairdivyaiḥ samāyukto ratho vāyusamo jave ,adhiṣṭhitaḥ sa śuśubhe kṛṣṇenātīva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, that chariot, yoked with divine horses and as swift as the wind, shone with immense splendor when Krishna took his place upon it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 20. O Bhārata, that chariot, yoked with divine horses and as swift as the wind, shone with immense splendor when Krishna took his place upon it.

sanskrit: असङ्गी देवविहितस्तस्मिन्रथवरे ध्वजः ।योजनाद्ददृशे श्रीमानिन्द्रायुधसमप्रभः ॥२१॥
transliteration: asaṅgī devavihitastasminrathavare dhvajaḥ ,yojanāddadṛśe śrīmānindrāyudhasamaprabhaḥ.
english: On that excellent chariot, a glorious banner, divinely established and unobstructed, shone with the splendor of a rainbow (Indra's bow), visible from a distance of a Yojana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 21. On that excellent chariot, a glorious banner, divinely established and unobstructed, shone with the splendor of a rainbow (Indra's bow), visible from a distance of a Yojana.

sanskrit: चिन्तयामास कृष्णोऽथ गरुत्मन्तं स चाभ्ययात् ।क्षणे तस्मिन्स तेनासीच्चैत्ययूप इवोच्छ्रितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: cintayāmāsa kṛṣṇo'tha garutmantaṁ sa cābhyayāt ,kṣaṇe tasminsa tenāsīccaityayūpa ivocchritaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa then thought of Garuḍa, and Garuḍa immediately approached. In that very moment, he was elevated by Kṛṣṇa, like a monumental sacrificial post (caityayūpa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 22. Kṛṣṇa then thought of Garuḍa, and Garuḍa immediately approached. In that very moment, he was elevated by Kṛṣṇa, like a monumental sacrificial post (caityayūpa).

sanskrit: व्यादितास्यैर्महानादैः सह भूतैर्ध्वजालयैः ।तस्थौ रथवरे तस्मिन्गरुत्मान्पन्नगाशनः ॥२३॥
transliteration: vyāditāsyairmahānādaiḥ saha bhūtairdhvajālayaiḥ ,tasthau rathavare tasmingarutmānpannagāśanaḥ.
english: Garuḍa, the devourer of serpents (pannagāśana), stood on that excellent chariot. He was accompanied by attendants (bhūta) with gaping mouths and mighty roars, and by the inhabitants of his standard.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 23. Garuḍa, the devourer of serpents (pannagāśana), stood on that excellent chariot. He was accompanied by attendants (bhūta) with gaping mouths and mighty roars, and by the inhabitants of his standard.

sanskrit: दुर्निरीक्ष्यो हि भूतानां तेजसाभ्यधिकं बभौ ।आदित्य इव मध्याह्ने सहस्रकिरणावृतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: durnirīkṣyo hi bhūtānāṁ tejasābhyadhikaṁ babhau ,āditya iva madhyāhne sahasrakiraṇāvṛtaḥ.
english: Indeed, he shone with exceeding splendor, difficult for (all) beings (bhūta) to behold, just like the sun (āditya) at midday, enveloped in a thousand rays.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 24. Indeed, he shone with exceeding splendor, difficult for (all) beings (bhūta) to behold, just like the sun (āditya) at midday, enveloped in a thousand rays.

sanskrit: न स सज्जति वृक्षेषु शस्त्रैश्चापि न रिष्यते ।दिव्यो ध्वजवरो राजन्दृश्यते देवमानुषैः ॥२५॥
transliteration: na sa sajjati vṛkṣeṣu śastraiścāpi na riṣyate ,divyo dhvajavaro rājandṛśyate devamānuṣaiḥ.
english: O king (rājan), that divine, excellent standard (dhvajavara) does not get entangled in trees and is also not harmed by weapons. It is beheld by both gods and humans.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 25. O king (rājan), that divine, excellent standard (dhvajavara) does not get entangled in trees and is also not harmed by weapons. It is beheld by both gods and humans.

sanskrit: तमास्थाय रथं दिव्यं पर्जन्यसमनिस्वनम् ।निर्ययौ पुरुषव्याघ्रः पाण्डवाभ्यां सहाच्युतः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tamāsthāya rathaṁ divyaṁ parjanyasamanisvanam ,niryayau puruṣavyāghraḥ pāṇḍavābhyāṁ sahācyutaḥ.
english: Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa), that foremost among men, mounted that divine chariot, which rumbled like a thundercloud, and departed with the two Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 26. Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa), that foremost among men, mounted that divine chariot, which rumbled like a thundercloud, and departed with the two Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: यं लेभे वासवाद्राजा वसुस्तस्माद्बृहद्रथः ।बृहद्रथात्क्रमेणैव प्राप्तो बार्हद्रथं नृपम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: yaṁ lebhe vāsavādrājā vasustasmādbṛhadrathaḥ ,bṛhadrathātkrameṇaiva prāpto bārhadrathaṁ nṛpam.
english: King Vasu obtained that (chariot) from Vāsava (Indra). From him (Vasu), Bṛhadratha received it. And from Bṛhadratha, in due succession, King Bārhadrathes (Jarāsandha) obtained it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 27. King Vasu obtained that (chariot) from Vāsava (Indra). From him (Vasu), Bṛhadratha received it. And from Bṛhadratha, in due succession, King Bārhadrathes (Jarāsandha) obtained it.

sanskrit: स निर्ययौ महाबाहुः पुण्डरीकेक्षणस्ततः ।गिरिव्रजाद्बहिस्तस्थौ समे देशे महायशाः ॥२८॥
transliteration: sa niryayau mahābāhuḥ puṇḍarīkekṣaṇastataḥ ,girivrajādbahistasthau same deśe mahāyaśāḥ.
english: Then, the mighty-armed, lotus-eyed and highly renowned king (Jarāsandha) departed. He stood outside Girivraja on a level ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 28. Then, the mighty-armed, lotus-eyed and highly renowned king (Jarāsandha) departed. He stood outside Girivraja on a level ground.

sanskrit: तत्रैनं नागराः सर्वे सत्कारेणाभ्ययुस्तदा ।ब्राह्मणप्रमुखा राजन्विधिदृष्टेन कर्मणा ॥२९॥
transliteration: tatrainaṁ nāgarāḥ sarve satkāreṇābhyayustadā ,brāhmaṇapramukhā rājanvidhidṛṣṭena karmaṇā.
english: O King, then all the citizens there, led by Brahmins, approached him (Jarāsandha) with honors and with actions prescribed by sacred law.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 29. O King, then all the citizens there, led by Brahmins, approached him (Jarāsandha) with honors and with actions prescribed by sacred law.

sanskrit: बन्धनाद्विप्रमुक्ताश्च राजानो मधुसूदनम् ।पूजयामासुरूचुश्च सान्त्वपूर्वमिदं वचः ॥३०॥
transliteration: bandhanādvipramuktāśca rājāno madhusūdanam ,pūjayāmāsurūcuśca sāntvapūrvamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: And the kings, freed from their captivity, worshipped Madhusūdana and spoke these conciliatory words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 30. And the kings, freed from their captivity, worshipped Madhusūdana and spoke these conciliatory words.

sanskrit: नैतच्चित्रं महाबाहो त्वयि देवकिनन्दन ।भीमार्जुनबलोपेते धर्मस्य परिपालनम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: naitaccitraṁ mahābāho tvayi devakinandana ,bhīmārjunabalopete dharmasya paripālanam.
english: O mighty-armed one, son of Devakī, this upholding of natural law (dharma) is not surprising in you, who are endowed with the strength of Bhīma and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 31. O mighty-armed one, son of Devakī, this upholding of natural law (dharma) is not surprising in you, who are endowed with the strength of Bhīma and Arjuna.

sanskrit: जरासंधह्रदे घोरे दुःखपङ्के निमज्जताम् ।राज्ञां समभ्युद्धरणं यदिदं कृतमद्य ते ॥३२॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdhahrade ghore duḥkhapaṅke nimajjatām ,rājñāṁ samabhyuddharaṇaṁ yadidaṁ kṛtamadya te.
english: Today, you have accomplished this rescue of the kings who were immersed in the dreadful lake of Jarāsandha, a quagmire of suffering.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 32. Today, you have accomplished this rescue of the kings who were immersed in the dreadful lake of Jarāsandha, a quagmire of suffering.

sanskrit: विष्णो समवसन्नानां गिरिदुर्गे सुदारुणे ।दिष्ट्या मोक्षाद्यशो दीप्तमाप्तं ते पुरुषोत्तम ॥३३॥
transliteration: viṣṇo samavasannānāṁ giridurge sudāruṇe ,diṣṭyā mokṣādyaśo dīptamāptaṁ te puruṣottama.
english: O Viṣṇu, O supreme cosmic person (puruṣa)! Fortunately, you have achieved brilliant fame from the liberation (mokṣa) of those afflicted in that extremely formidable mountain fortress.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 33. O Viṣṇu, O supreme cosmic person (puruṣa)! Fortunately, you have achieved brilliant fame from the liberation (mokṣa) of those afflicted in that extremely formidable mountain fortress.

sanskrit: किं कुर्मः पुरुषव्याघ्र ब्रवीहि पुरुषर्षभ ।कृतमित्येव तज्ज्ञेयं नृपैर्यद्यपि दुष्करम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: kiṁ kurmaḥ puruṣavyāghra bravīhi puruṣarṣabha ,kṛtamityeva tajjñeyaṁ nṛpairyadyapi duṣkaram.
english: O foremost of men, O mightiest of men, tell us what we should do. Whatever is to be done must be considered as already accomplished, even if it is difficult for kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 34. O foremost of men, O mightiest of men, tell us what we should do. Whatever is to be done must be considered as already accomplished, even if it is difficult for kings.

sanskrit: तानुवाच हृषीकेशः समाश्वास्य महामनाः ।युधिष्ठिरो राजसूयं क्रतुमाहर्तुमिच्छति ॥३५॥
transliteration: tānuvāca hṛṣīkeśaḥ samāśvāsya mahāmanāḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiro rājasūyaṁ kratumāhartumicchati.
english: The magnanimous Hṛṣīkeśa, having comforted them, spoke: 'Yudhiṣṭhira desires to perform the Rājasūya (rājasūya) Vedic ritual.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 35. The magnanimous Hṛṣīkeśa, having comforted them, spoke: 'Yudhiṣṭhira desires to perform the Rājasūya (rājasūya) Vedic ritual.'

sanskrit: तस्य धर्मप्रवृत्तस्य पार्थिवत्वं चिकीर्षतः ।सर्वैर्भवद्भिर्यज्ञार्थे साहाय्यं दीयतामिति ॥३६॥
transliteration: tasya dharmapravṛttasya pārthivatvaṁ cikīrṣataḥ ,sarvairbhavadbhiryajñārthe sāhāyyaṁ dīyatāmiti.
english: Therefore, for Yudhishthira, who is committed to natural law (dharma) and desires to achieve universal kingship, let assistance be given by all of you for the purpose of the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 36. Therefore, for Yudhishthira, who is committed to natural law (dharma) and desires to achieve universal kingship, let assistance be given by all of you for the purpose of the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: ततः प्रतीतमनसस्ते नृपा भरतर्षभ ।तथेत्येवाब्रुवन्सर्वे प्रतिजज्ञुश्च तां गिरम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ pratītamanasaste nṛpā bharatarṣabha ,tathetyevābruvansarve pratijajñuśca tāṁ giram.
english: Then, O foremost of the Bharatas, those kings, with contented minds, all said, 'So be it,' and pledged to abide by that command.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 37. Then, O foremost of the Bharatas, those kings, with contented minds, all said, 'So be it,' and pledged to abide by that command.

sanskrit: रत्नभाजं च दाशार्हं चक्रुस्ते पृथिवीश्वराः ।कृच्छ्राज्जग्राह गोविन्दस्तेषां तदनुकम्पया ॥३८॥
transliteration: ratnabhājaṁ ca dāśārhaṁ cakruste pṛthivīśvarāḥ ,kṛcchrājjagrāha govindasteṣāṁ tadanukampayā.
english: Those kings made Daśārha (Kṛṣṇa) a recipient of tribute in the form of jewels. Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), with compassion, accepted that from them, though with difficulty.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 38. Those kings made Daśārha (Kṛṣṇa) a recipient of tribute in the form of jewels. Govinda (Kṛṣṇa), with compassion, accepted that from them, though with difficulty.

sanskrit: जरासंधात्मजश्चैव सहदेवो महारथः ।निर्ययौ सजनामात्यः पुरस्कृत्य पुरोहितम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: jarāsaṁdhātmajaścaiva sahadevo mahārathaḥ ,niryayau sajanāmātyaḥ puraskṛtya purohitam.
english: Indeed, Sahadeva, the son of Jarāsandha and a great warrior, came forth with his people and ministers, having placed his priest at the forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 39. Indeed, Sahadeva, the son of Jarāsandha and a great warrior, came forth with his people and ministers, having placed his priest at the forefront.

sanskrit: स नीचैः प्रश्रितो भूत्वा बहुरत्नपुरोगमः ।सहदेवो नृणां देवं वासुदेवमुपस्थितः ॥४०॥
transliteration: sa nīcaiḥ praśrito bhūtvā bahuratnapurogamaḥ ,sahadevo nṛṇāṁ devaṁ vāsudevamupasthitaḥ.
english: Having become humble and modest, Sahadeva, accompanied by many jewels as tribute, approached Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the deity of men.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 40. Having become humble and modest, Sahadeva, accompanied by many jewels as tribute, approached Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), the deity of men.

sanskrit: भयार्ताय ततस्तस्मै कृष्णो दत्त्वाभयं तदा ।अभ्यषिञ्चत तत्रैव जरासंधात्मजं तदा ॥४१॥
transliteration: bhayārtāya tatastasmai kṛṣṇo dattvābhayaṁ tadā ,abhyaṣiñcata tatraiva jarāsaṁdhātmajaṁ tadā.
english: Then Kṛṣṇa, having given assurance of safety (abhaya) to that fearful man, immediately consecrated Jarāsandha's son right there at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 41. Then Kṛṣṇa, having given assurance of safety (abhaya) to that fearful man, immediately consecrated Jarāsandha's son right there at that time.

sanskrit: गत्वैकत्वं च कृष्णेन पार्थाभ्यां चैव सत्कृतः ।विवेश राजा मतिमान्पुनर्बार्हद्रथं पुरम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: gatvaikatvaṁ ca kṛṣṇena pārthābhyāṁ caiva satkṛtaḥ ,viveśa rājā matimānpunarbārhadrathaṁ puram.
english: The intelligent king, having achieved unity (with them) and having been honored by Krishna and the two sons of Pṛthā (Arjuna and Bhima), again entered the city of Jarasandha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 42. The intelligent king, having achieved unity (with them) and having been honored by Krishna and the two sons of Pṛthā (Arjuna and Bhima), again entered the city of Jarasandha.

sanskrit: कृष्णस्तु सह पार्थाभ्यां श्रिया परमया ज्वलन् ।रत्नान्यादाय भूरीणि प्रययौ पुष्करेक्षणः ॥४३॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇastu saha pārthābhyāṁ śriyā paramayā jvalan ,ratnānyādāya bhūrīṇi prayayau puṣkarekṣaṇaḥ.
english: But Krishna, the lotus-eyed one, radiant with supreme splendor along with the two sons of Pṛthā (Arjuna and Bhima), having taken numerous jewels, departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 43. But Krishna, the lotus-eyed one, radiant with supreme splendor along with the two sons of Pṛthā (Arjuna and Bhima), having taken numerous jewels, departed.

sanskrit: इन्द्रप्रस्थमुपागम्य पाण्डवाभ्यां सहाच्युतः ।समेत्य धर्मराजानं प्रीयमाणोऽभ्यभाषत ॥४४॥
transliteration: indraprasthamupāgamya pāṇḍavābhyāṁ sahācyutaḥ ,sametya dharmarājānaṁ prīyamāṇo'bhyabhāṣata.
english: Achyuta (Krishna), having reached Indraprastha along with the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna and Bhima), and having met the king of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), spoke to him joyfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 44. Achyuta (Krishna), having reached Indraprastha along with the Pāṇḍavas (Arjuna and Bhima), and having met the king of dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira), spoke to him joyfully.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या भीमेन बलवाञ्जरासंधो निपातितः ।राजानो मोक्षिताश्चेमे बन्धनान्नृपसत्तम ॥४५॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā bhīmena balavāñjarāsaṁdho nipātitaḥ ,rājāno mokṣitāśceme bandhanānnṛpasattama.
english: O best of kings, by good fortune, the mighty Jarasandha was struck down by Bhima, and these kings were liberated from captivity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 45. O best of kings, by good fortune, the mighty Jarasandha was struck down by Bhima, and these kings were liberated from captivity.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या कुशलिनौ चेमौ भीमसेनधनंजयौ ।पुनः स्वनगरं प्राप्तावक्षताविति भारत ॥४६॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā kuśalinau cemau bhīmasenadhanaṁjayau ,punaḥ svanagaraṁ prāptāvakṣatāviti bhārata.
english: Fortunately, these two, Bhimasena and Dhananjaya, have safely returned to their own city, unharmed, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 46. Fortunately, these two, Bhimasena and Dhananjaya, have safely returned to their own city, unharmed, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरः कृष्णं पूजयित्वा यथार्हतः ।भीमसेनार्जुनौ चैव प्रहृष्टः परिषस्वजे ॥४७॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiraḥ kṛṣṇaṁ pūjayitvā yathārhataḥ ,bhīmasenārjunau caiva prahṛṣṭaḥ pariṣasvaje.
english: Then Yudhiṣṭhira, having honored Kṛṣṇa appropriately, joyfully embraced Bhimasena and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 47. Then Yudhiṣṭhira, having honored Kṛṣṇa appropriately, joyfully embraced Bhimasena and Arjuna.

sanskrit: ततः क्षीणे जरासंधे भ्रातृभ्यां विहितं जयम् ।अजातशत्रुरासाद्य मुमुदे भ्रातृभिः सह ॥४८॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṣīṇe jarāsaṁdhe bhrātṛbhyāṁ vihitaṁ jayam ,ajātaśatrurāsādya mumude bhrātṛbhiḥ saha.
english: Then, with Jarāsaṃdha vanquished, Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), having attained the victory accomplished by his two brothers, rejoiced along with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 48. Then, with Jarāsaṃdha vanquished, Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), having attained the victory accomplished by his two brothers, rejoiced along with his brothers.

sanskrit: यथावयः समागम्य राजभिस्तैश्च पाण्डवः ।सत्कृत्य पूजयित्वा च विससर्ज नराधिपान् ॥४९॥
transliteration: yathāvayaḥ samāgamya rājabhistaiśca pāṇḍavaḥ ,satkṛtya pūjayitvā ca visasarja narādhipān.
english: And the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), having met with those kings according to their seniority, and having respectfully honored and worshipped them, then dismissed the rulers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 49. And the Pāṇḍava (Yudhiṣṭhira), having met with those kings according to their seniority, and having respectfully honored and worshipped them, then dismissed the rulers.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिराभ्यनुज्ञातास्ते नृपा हृष्टमानसाः ।जग्मुः स्वदेशांस्त्वरिता यानैरुच्चावचैस्ततः ॥५०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirābhyanujñātāste nṛpā hṛṣṭamānasāḥ ,jagmuḥ svadeśāṁstvaritā yānairuccāvacaistataḥ.
english: Then, those kings, their hearts joyful, having received permission from Yudhiṣṭhira, swiftly departed for their own countries in various vehicles.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 50. Then, those kings, their hearts joyful, having received permission from Yudhiṣṭhira, swiftly departed for their own countries in various vehicles.

sanskrit: एवं पुरुषशार्दूलो महाबुद्धिर्जनार्दनः ।पाण्डवैर्घातयामास जरासंधमरिं तदा ॥५१॥
transliteration: evaṁ puruṣaśārdūlo mahābuddhirjanārdanaḥ ,pāṇḍavairghātayāmāsa jarāsaṁdhamariṁ tadā.
english: In this way, Janārdana, that great-minded foremost among men (puruṣa), then had his enemy Jarāsaṃdha killed by the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 51. In this way, Janārdana, that great-minded foremost among men (puruṣa), then had his enemy Jarāsaṃdha killed by the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: घातयित्वा जरासंधं बुद्धिपूर्वमरिंदमः ।धर्मराजमनुज्ञाप्य पृथां कृष्णां च भारत ॥५२॥
transliteration: ghātayitvā jarāsaṁdhaṁ buddhipūrvamariṁdamaḥ ,dharmarājamanujñāpya pṛthāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ ca bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, having deliberately caused the enemy Jarāsaṃdha to be killed, the subduer of foes (Kṛṣṇa) then took leave of Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), Pṛthā (Kuntī), and Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 52. O Bhārata, having deliberately caused the enemy Jarāsaṃdha to be killed, the subduer of foes (Kṛṣṇa) then took leave of Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), Pṛthā (Kuntī), and Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī).

sanskrit: सुभद्रां भीमसेनं च फल्गुनं यमजौ तथा ।धौम्यमामन्त्रयित्वा च प्रययौ स्वां पुरीं प्रति ॥५३॥
transliteration: subhadrāṁ bhīmasenaṁ ca phalgunaṁ yamajau tathā ,dhaumyamāmantrayitvā ca prayayau svāṁ purīṁ prati.
english: ...and having also taken leave of Subhadrā, Bhīmasena, Phalguna (Arjuna), and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Dhaumya, he (Kṛṣṇa) departed for his own city.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 53. ...and having also taken leave of Subhadrā, Bhīmasena, Phalguna (Arjuna), and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva), and Dhaumya, he (Kṛṣṇa) departed for his own city.

sanskrit: तेनैव रथमुख्येन तरुणादित्यवर्चसा ।धर्मराजविसृष्टेन दिव्येनानादयन्दिशः ॥५४॥
transliteration: tenaiva rathamukhyena taruṇādityavarcasā ,dharmarājavisṛṣṭena divyenānādayandiśaḥ.
english: With that very chief chariot, divine, brilliant as the rising sun, and dispatched by the King of natural law (dharma - Yama), they made the directions reverberate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 54. With that very chief chariot, divine, brilliant as the rising sun, and dispatched by the King of natural law (dharma - Yama), they made the directions reverberate.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरमुखाः पाण्डवा भरतर्षभ ।प्रदक्षिणमकुर्वन्त कृष्णमक्लिष्टकारिणम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiramukhāḥ pāṇḍavā bharatarṣabha ,pradakṣiṇamakurvanta kṛṣṇamakliṣṭakāriṇam.
english: Then, O best of Bharatas, the Pandavas, led by Yudhishthira, performed a respectful circumambulation of Krishna, who accomplishes deeds effortlessly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 55. Then, O best of Bharatas, the Pandavas, led by Yudhishthira, performed a respectful circumambulation of Krishna, who accomplishes deeds effortlessly.

sanskrit: ततो गते भगवति कृष्णे देवकिनन्दने ।जयं लब्ध्वा सुविपुलं राज्ञामभयदास्तदा ॥५६॥
transliteration: tato gate bhagavati kṛṣṇe devakinandane ,jayaṁ labdhvā suvipulaṁ rājñāmabhayadāstadā.
english: Then, when the revered Krishna, son of Devaki, had departed, they, having gained a very great victory, at that time gave fearlessness (protection) to kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 56. Then, when the revered Krishna, son of Devaki, had departed, they, having gained a very great victory, at that time gave fearlessness (protection) to kings.

sanskrit: संवर्धितौजसो भूयः कर्मणा तेन भारत ।द्रौपद्याः पाण्डवा राजन्परां प्रीतिमवर्धयन् ॥५७॥
transliteration: saṁvardhitaujaso bhūyaḥ karmaṇā tena bhārata ,draupadyāḥ pāṇḍavā rājanparāṁ prītimavardhayan.
english: O Bharata, O King, the Pandavas, having thereby further increased their vigor by that deed, greatly enhanced Draupadi's joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 57. O Bharata, O King, the Pandavas, having thereby further increased their vigor by that deed, greatly enhanced Draupadi's joy.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्काले तु यद्युक्तं धर्मकामार्थसंहितम् ।तद्राजा धर्मतश्चक्रे राज्यपालनकीर्तिमान् ॥५८॥
transliteration: tasminkāle tu yadyuktaṁ dharmakāmārthasaṁhitam ,tadrājā dharmataścakre rājyapālanakīrtimān.
english: The king, renowned for governing his kingdom, then acted righteously (dharmataḥ) in whatever manner was appropriate and consistent with natural law (dharma), legitimate desires (kāma), and material well-being (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 22 Verse 58. The king, renowned for governing his kingdom, then acted righteously (dharmataḥ) in whatever manner was appropriate and consistent with natural law (dharma), legitimate desires (kāma), and material well-being (artha).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।पार्थः प्राप्य धनुःश्रेष्ठमक्षय्यौ च महेषुधी ।रथं ध्वजं सभां चैव युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pārthaḥ prāpya dhanuḥśreṣṭhamakṣayyau ca maheṣudhī ,rathaṁ dhvajaṁ sabhāṁ caiva yudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having received the supreme bow, the two inexhaustible great quivers, the chariot, the banner, and the assembly hall, Pārtha (Arjuna) then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having received the supreme bow, the two inexhaustible great quivers, the chariot, the banner, and the assembly hall, Pārtha (Arjuna) then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: धनुरस्त्रं शरा वीर्यं पक्षो भूमिर्यशो बलम् ।प्राप्तमेतन्मया राजन्दुष्प्रापं यदभीप्सितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: dhanurastraṁ śarā vīryaṁ pakṣo bhūmiryaśo balam ,prāptametanmayā rājanduṣprāpaṁ yadabhīpsitam.
english: O King, I have now obtained the bow, the projectile weapons, the arrows, valor, allies, territory, fame, and military strength - all that was desired and difficult to acquire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 2. O King, I have now obtained the bow, the projectile weapons, the arrows, valor, allies, territory, fame, and military strength - all that was desired and difficult to acquire.

sanskrit: तत्र कृत्यमहं मन्ये कोशस्यास्य विवर्धनम् ।करमाहारयिष्यामि राज्ञः सर्वान्नृपोत्तम ॥३॥
transliteration: tatra kṛtyamahaṁ manye kośasyāsya vivardhanam ,karamāhārayiṣyāmi rājñaḥ sarvānnṛpottama.
english: O best of kings, regarding this, I consider the augmentation of this treasury to be the proper course of action. I will levy taxes from all kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 3. O best of kings, regarding this, I consider the augmentation of this treasury to be the proper course of action. I will levy taxes from all kings.

sanskrit: विजयाय प्रयास्यामि दिशं धनदरक्षिताम् ।तिथावथ मुहूर्ते च नक्षत्रे च तथा शिवे ॥४॥
transliteration: vijayāya prayāsyāmi diśaṁ dhanadarakṣitām ,tithāvatha muhūrte ca nakṣatre ca tathā śive.
english: I will set out for victory towards the region protected by Kubera, choosing an auspicious lunar day, a favorable moment, and a propitious constellation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 4. I will set out for victory towards the region protected by Kubera, choosing an auspicious lunar day, a favorable moment, and a propitious constellation.

sanskrit: धनंजयवचः श्रुत्वा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।स्निग्धगम्भीरनादिन्या तं गिरा प्रत्यभाषत ॥५॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayavacaḥ śrutvā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,snigdhagambhīranādinyā taṁ girā pratyabhāṣata.
english: Having heard Dhanaṃjaya's (Arjuna's) words, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king who embodies righteousness (dharma), replied to him with soft and deep-sounding speech.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 5. Having heard Dhanaṃjaya's (Arjuna's) words, Yudhiṣṭhira, the king who embodies righteousness (dharma), replied to him with soft and deep-sounding speech.

sanskrit: स्वस्ति वाच्यार्हतो विप्रान्प्रयाहि भरतर्षभ ।दुर्हृदामप्रहर्षाय सुहृदां नन्दनाय च ।विजयस्ते ध्रुवं पार्थ प्रियं काममवाप्नुहि ॥६॥
transliteration: svasti vācyārhato viprānprayāhi bharatarṣabha ,durhṛdāmapraharṣāya suhṛdāṁ nandanāya ca ,vijayaste dhruvaṁ pārtha priyaṁ kāmamavāpnuhi.
english: O best of the Bharatas, go forth after having the revered Brahmins pronounce blessings for your welfare. May it bring sorrow to your enemies and joy to your friends. O Pārtha (Arjuna), victory is surely yours; may you attain your cherished desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 6. O best of the Bharatas, go forth after having the revered Brahmins pronounce blessings for your welfare. May it bring sorrow to your enemies and joy to your friends. O Pārtha (Arjuna), victory is surely yours; may you attain your cherished desire.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तः प्रययौ पार्थः सैन्येन महता वृतः ।अग्निदत्तेन दिव्येन रथेनाद्भुतकर्मणा ॥७॥
transliteration: ityuktaḥ prayayau pārthaḥ sainyena mahatā vṛtaḥ ,agnidattena divyena rathenādbhutakarmaṇā.
english: Having been thus addressed, Pārtha (Arjuna) departed, surrounded by a vast army, in his divine chariot, Agnidatta, renowned for its wondrous feats.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 7. Having been thus addressed, Pārtha (Arjuna) departed, surrounded by a vast army, in his divine chariot, Agnidatta, renowned for its wondrous feats.

sanskrit: तथैव भीमसेनोऽपि यमौ च पुरुषर्षभौ ।ससैन्याः प्रययुः सर्वे धर्मराजाभिपूजिताः ॥८॥
transliteration: tathaiva bhīmaseno'pi yamau ca puruṣarṣabhau ,sasainyāḥ prayayuḥ sarve dharmarājābhipūjitāḥ.
english: In the same way, Bhimasena and the two foremost men, the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), all of them honored by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja), departed with their armies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 8. In the same way, Bhimasena and the two foremost men, the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), all of them honored by King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja), departed with their armies.

sanskrit: दिशं धनपतेरिष्टामजयत्पाकशासनिः ।भीमसेनस्तथा प्राचीं सहदेवस्तु दक्षिणाम् ॥९॥
transliteration: diśaṁ dhanapateriṣṭāmajayatpākaśāsaniḥ ,bhīmasenastathā prācīṁ sahadevastu dakṣiṇām.
english: Arjuna (pākaśāsani), the son of Indra, conquered the direction favored by Kubera, the lord of wealth (the North). Similarly, Bhimasena conquered the eastern direction, and Sahadeva conquered the southern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 9. Arjuna (pākaśāsani), the son of Indra, conquered the direction favored by Kubera, the lord of wealth (the North). Similarly, Bhimasena conquered the eastern direction, and Sahadeva conquered the southern direction.

sanskrit: प्रतीचीं नकुलो राजन्दिशं व्यजयदस्त्रवित् ।खाण्डवप्रस्थमध्यास्ते धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ॥१०॥
transliteration: pratīcīṁ nakulo rājandiśaṁ vyajayadastravit ,khāṇḍavaprasthamadhyāste dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: O King, Nakula, skilled in weapons, conquered the western direction. King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja) resides in Khāṇḍavaprastha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 10. O King, Nakula, skilled in weapons, conquered the western direction. King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja) resides in Khāṇḍavaprastha.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।दिशामभिजयं ब्रह्मन्विस्तरेणानुकीर्तय ।न हि तृप्यामि पूर्वेषां शृण्वानश्चरितं महत् ॥११॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,diśāmabhijayaṁ brahmanvistareṇānukīrtaya ,na hi tṛpyāmi pūrveṣāṁ śṛṇvānaścaritaṁ mahat.
english: Janamejaya said: 'O Brahmin, please describe in detail the conquest of the directions. I am indeed not satisfied by hearing the great deeds of my ancestors.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 11. Janamejaya said: 'O Brahmin, please describe in detail the conquest of the directions. I am indeed not satisfied by hearing the great deeds of my ancestors.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।धनंजयस्य वक्ष्यामि विजयं पूर्वमेव ते ।यौगपद्येन पार्थैर्हि विजितेयं वसुंधरा ॥१२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dhanaṁjayasya vakṣyāmi vijayaṁ pūrvameva te ,yaugapadyena pārthairhi vijiteyaṁ vasuṁdharā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "First, I will narrate to you the victory of Dhanañjaya. Indeed, this entire earth was conquered simultaneously by the Pāṇḍavas."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 12. Vaiśampāyana said: "First, I will narrate to you the victory of Dhanañjaya. Indeed, this entire earth was conquered simultaneously by the Pāṇḍavas."

sanskrit: पूर्वं कुणिन्दविषये वशे चक्रे महीपतीन् ।धनंजयो महाबाहुर्नातितीव्रेण कर्मणा ॥१३॥
transliteration: pūrvaṁ kuṇindaviṣaye vaśe cakre mahīpatīn ,dhanaṁjayo mahābāhurnātitīvreṇa karmaṇā.
english: Formerly, in the region of Kuṇinda, the mighty-armed Dhanañjaya brought kings under his control through actions (karma) that were not exceedingly harsh.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 13. Formerly, in the region of Kuṇinda, the mighty-armed Dhanañjaya brought kings under his control through actions (karma) that were not exceedingly harsh.

sanskrit: आनर्तान्कालकूटांश्च कुणिन्दांश्च विजित्य सः ।सुमण्डलं पापजितं कृतवाननुसैनिकम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: ānartānkālakūṭāṁśca kuṇindāṁśca vijitya saḥ ,sumaṇḍalaṁ pāpajitaṁ kṛtavānanusainikam.
english: Having conquered the Ānartas, the Kālakuṭas, and the Kuṇindas, he then formed Sumaṇḍala, which was (already) conquered by Pāpa, into a subordinate military contingent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 14. Having conquered the Ānartas, the Kālakuṭas, and the Kuṇindas, he then formed Sumaṇḍala, which was (already) conquered by Pāpa, into a subordinate military contingent.

sanskrit: स तेन सहितो राजन्सव्यसाची परंतपः ।विजिग्ये सकलं द्वीपं प्रतिविन्ध्यं च पार्थिवम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: sa tena sahito rājansavyasācī paraṁtapaḥ ,vijigye sakalaṁ dvīpaṁ prativindhyaṁ ca pārthivam.
english: O King, that Savyasācin (Arjuna), the tormentor of foes, accompanied by those forces, conquered the entire island and King Prativindhya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 15. O King, that Savyasācin (Arjuna), the tormentor of foes, accompanied by those forces, conquered the entire island and King Prativindhya.

sanskrit: सकलद्वीपवासांश्च सप्तद्वीपे च ये नृपाः ।अर्जुनस्य च सैन्यानां विग्रहस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥१६॥
transliteration: sakaladvīpavāsāṁśca saptadvīpe ca ye nṛpāḥ ,arjunasya ca sainyānāṁ vigrahastumulo'bhavat.
english: A fierce battle took place between Arjuna's armies and the inhabitants of all islands, as well as the kings on the seven continents.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 16. A fierce battle took place between Arjuna's armies and the inhabitants of all islands, as well as the kings on the seven continents.

sanskrit: स तानपि महेष्वासो विजित्य भरतर्षभ ।तैरेव सहितः सर्वैः प्राग्ज्योतिषमुपाद्रवत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa tānapi maheṣvāso vijitya bharatarṣabha ,taireva sahitaḥ sarvaiḥ prāgjyotiṣamupādravat.
english: O best among the Bharatas, that great archer (Arjuna), having conquered all of them, then proceeded to Prāgjyotiṣa, accompanied by all of those (allies).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 17. O best among the Bharatas, that great archer (Arjuna), having conquered all of them, then proceeded to Prāgjyotiṣa, accompanied by all of those (allies).

sanskrit: तत्र राजा महानासीद्भगदत्तो विशां पते ।तेनासीत्सुमहद्युद्धं पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatra rājā mahānāsīdbhagadatto viśāṁ pate ,tenāsītsumahadyuddhaṁ pāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, there was a great king named Bhagadatta there. With him, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), who was great in spirit (ātman), fought a very fierce battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 18. O lord of the people, there was a great king named Bhagadatta there. With him, the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna), who was great in spirit (ātman), fought a very fierce battle.

sanskrit: स किरातैश्च चीनैश्च वृतः प्राग्ज्योतिषोऽभवत् ।अन्यैश्च बहुभिर्योधैः सागरानूपवासिभिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: sa kirātaiśca cīnaiśca vṛtaḥ prāgjyotiṣo'bhavat ,anyaiśca bahubhiryodhaiḥ sāgarānūpavāsibhiḥ.
english: The king of Prāgjyotiṣa (Bhagadatta) was surrounded by Kirātas and Cīnas, as well as by many other warriors dwelling in the regions near the sea.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 19. The king of Prāgjyotiṣa (Bhagadatta) was surrounded by Kirātas and Cīnas, as well as by many other warriors dwelling in the regions near the sea.

sanskrit: ततः स दिवसानष्टौ योधयित्वा धनंजयम् ।प्रहसन्नब्रवीद्राजा संग्रामे विगतक्लमः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa divasānaṣṭau yodhayitvā dhanaṁjayam ,prahasannabravīdrājā saṁgrāme vigataklamaḥ.
english: Then, after fighting Dhananjaya for eight days, that king, who was tireless in battle, spoke with a smile.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 20. Then, after fighting Dhananjaya for eight days, that king, who was tireless in battle, spoke with a smile.

sanskrit: उपपन्नं महाबाहो त्वयि पाण्डवनन्दन ।पाकशासनदायादे वीर्यमाहवशोभिनि ॥२१॥
transliteration: upapannaṁ mahābāho tvayi pāṇḍavanandana ,pākaśāsanadāyāde vīryamāhavaśobhini.
english: O mighty-armed one, O delight of the Pandavas, O heir of Indra (pākaśāsana), this valor, which shines brightly in battle, is truly befitting of you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 21. O mighty-armed one, O delight of the Pandavas, O heir of Indra (pākaśāsana), this valor, which shines brightly in battle, is truly befitting of you.

sanskrit: अहं सखा सुरेन्द्रस्य शक्रादनवमो रणे ।न च शक्नोमि ते तात स्थातुं प्रमुखतो युधि ॥२२॥
transliteration: ahaṁ sakhā surendrasya śakrādanavamo raṇe ,na ca śaknomi te tāta sthātuṁ pramukhato yudhi.
english: I am a friend of Indra (surendra), not inferior to Indra (śakra) himself in battle. Yet, O dear one, I am unable to stand before you in combat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 22. I am a friend of Indra (surendra), not inferior to Indra (śakra) himself in battle. Yet, O dear one, I am unable to stand before you in combat.

sanskrit: किमीप्सितं पाण्डवेय ब्रूहि किं करवाणि ते ।यद्वक्ष्यसि महाबाहो तत्करिष्यामि पुत्रक ॥२३॥
transliteration: kimīpsitaṁ pāṇḍaveya brūhi kiṁ karavāṇi te ,yadvakṣyasi mahābāho tatkariṣyāmi putraka.
english: O Pandava, what is your desire? Tell me what I should do for you. Whatever you command, O mighty-armed one, I will do that, my dear son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 23. O Pandava, what is your desire? Tell me what I should do for you. Whatever you command, O mighty-armed one, I will do that, my dear son.

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।कुरूणामृषभो राजा धर्मपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।तस्य पार्थिवतामीप्से करस्तस्मै प्रदीयताम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,kurūṇāmṛṣabho rājā dharmaputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,tasya pārthivatāmīpse karastasmai pradīyatām.
english: Arjuna said: "King Yudhishthira, the son of Dharma (natural law) and the foremost among the Kurus - I desire his sovereignty. Let tribute be given to him."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 24. Arjuna said: "King Yudhishthira, the son of Dharma (natural law) and the foremost among the Kurus - I desire his sovereignty. Let tribute be given to him."

sanskrit: भवान्पितृसखा चैव प्रीयमाणो मयापि च ।ततो नाज्ञापयामि त्वां प्रीतिपूर्वं प्रदीयताम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: bhavānpitṛsakhā caiva prīyamāṇo mayāpi ca ,tato nājñāpayāmi tvāṁ prītipūrvaṁ pradīyatām.
english: "You are indeed a friend of my father, and you are also well-pleased with me. Therefore, I do not command you; rather, let it be given with affection."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 25. "You are indeed a friend of my father, and you are also well-pleased with me. Therefore, I do not command you; rather, let it be given with affection."

sanskrit: भगदत्त उवाच ।कुन्तीमातर्यथा मे त्वं तथा राजा युधिष्ठिरः ।सर्वमेतत्करिष्यामि किं चान्यत्करवाणि ते ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhagadatta uvāca ,kuntīmātaryathā me tvaṁ tathā rājā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,sarvametatkariṣyāmi kiṁ cānyatkaravāṇi te.
english: Bhagadatta said: "O son of Kunti, just as you are to me, so is King Yudhishthira. I will perform all this; what else shall I do for you?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 23 Verse 26. Bhagadatta said: "O son of Kunti, just as you are to me, so is King Yudhishthira. I will perform all this; what else shall I do for you?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तं विजित्य महाबाहुः कुन्तीपुत्रो धनंजयः ।प्रययावुत्तरां तस्माद्दिशं धनदपालिताम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,taṁ vijitya mahābāhuḥ kuntīputro dhanaṁjayaḥ ,prayayāvuttarāṁ tasmāddiśaṁ dhanadapālitām.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Having conquered him, the mighty-armed Dhananjaya, the son of Kunti, then proceeded northward from that place, towards the region protected by Dhanada."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: "Having conquered him, the mighty-armed Dhananjaya, the son of Kunti, then proceeded northward from that place, towards the region protected by Dhanada."

sanskrit: अन्तर्गिरिं च कौन्तेयस्तथैव च बहिर्गिरिम् ।तथोपरिगिरिं चैव विजिग्ये पुरुषर्षभः ॥२॥
transliteration: antargiriṁ ca kaunteyastathaiva ca bahirgirim ,tathoparigiriṁ caiva vijigye puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: The great hero (puruṣarṣabhaḥ) Kaunteya (Arjuna) conquered the region within the mountains (antargirim), and likewise the region outside the mountains (bahirgirim), and also the region above the mountains (uparigirim).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 2. The great hero (puruṣarṣabhaḥ) Kaunteya (Arjuna) conquered the region within the mountains (antargirim), and likewise the region outside the mountains (bahirgirim), and also the region above the mountains (uparigirim).

sanskrit: विजित्य पर्वतान्सर्वान्ये च तत्र नराधिपाः ।तान्वशे स्थापयित्वा स रत्नान्यादाय सर्वशः ॥३॥
transliteration: vijitya parvatānsarvānye ca tatra narādhipāḥ ,tānvaśe sthāpayitvā sa ratnānyādāya sarvaśaḥ.
english: Having conquered all the mountains and also the kings who resided there, and having established them under his control, he seized all the jewels and treasures completely.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 3. Having conquered all the mountains and also the kings who resided there, and having established them under his control, he seized all the jewels and treasures completely.

sanskrit: तैरेव सहितः सर्वैरनुरज्य च तान्नृपान् ।कुलूतवासिनं राजन्बृहन्तमुपजग्मिवान् ॥४॥
transliteration: taireva sahitaḥ sarvairanurajya ca tānnṛpān ,kulūtavāsinaṁ rājanbṛhantamupajagmivān.
english: And, O King, accompanied by all of them (the subdued kings) and having appeased those rulers, he approached Brihanta, the resident of Kulūta.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 4. And, O King, accompanied by all of them (the subdued kings) and having appeased those rulers, he approached Brihanta, the resident of Kulūta.

sanskrit: मृदङ्गवरनादेन रथनेमिस्वनेन च ।हस्तिनां च निनादेन कम्पयन्वसुधामिमाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: mṛdaṅgavaranādena rathanemisvanena ca ,hastināṁ ca ninādena kampayanvasudhāmimām.
english: Causing this earth to tremble with the excellent sound of drums, with the rumble of chariot wheels, and with the trumpeting of elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 5. Causing this earth to tremble with the excellent sound of drums, with the rumble of chariot wheels, and with the trumpeting of elephants.

sanskrit: ततो बृहन्तस्तरुणो बलेन चतुरङ्गिणा ।निष्क्रम्य नगरात्तस्माद्योधयामास पाण्डवम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tato bṛhantastaruṇo balena caturaṅgiṇā ,niṣkramya nagarāttasmādyodhayāmāsa pāṇḍavam.
english: Then, young Brihanta, having emerged from that city with a four-fold army, fought the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 6. Then, young Brihanta, having emerged from that city with a four-fold army, fought the Pāṇḍava (Arjuna).

sanskrit: सुमहान्संनिपातोऽभूद्धनंजयबृहन्तयोः ।न शशाक बृहन्तस्तु सोढुं पाण्डवविक्रमम् ॥७॥
transliteration: sumahānsaṁnipāto'bhūddhanaṁjayabṛhantayoḥ ,na śaśāka bṛhantastu soḍhuṁ pāṇḍavavikramam.
english: A very great clash occurred between Dhananjaya and Brihanta. However, Brihanta was unable to endure the Pāṇḍava's valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 7. A very great clash occurred between Dhananjaya and Brihanta. However, Brihanta was unable to endure the Pāṇḍava's valor.

sanskrit: सोऽविषह्यतमं ज्ञात्वा कौन्तेयं पर्वतेश्वरः ।उपावर्तत दुर्मेधा रत्नान्यादाय सर्वशः ॥८॥
transliteration: so'viṣahyatamaṁ jñātvā kaunteyaṁ parvateśvaraḥ ,upāvartata durmedhā ratnānyādāya sarvaśaḥ.
english: Having recognized Kaunteya (Arjuna) as utterly irresistible, that foolish lord of the mountains (Brihanta) retreated, taking all the jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 8. Having recognized Kaunteya (Arjuna) as utterly irresistible, that foolish lord of the mountains (Brihanta) retreated, taking all the jewels.

sanskrit: स तद्राज्यमवस्थाप्य कुलूतसहितो ययौ ।सेनाबिन्दुमथो राजन्राज्यादाशु समाक्षिपत् ॥९॥
transliteration: sa tadrājyamavasthāpya kulūtasahito yayau ,senābindumatho rājanrājyādāśu samākṣipat.
english: Having established that kingdom, he (Arjuna), accompanied by Kulūta, departed. Then, O king, he swiftly expelled Senābindu from his realm.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 9. Having established that kingdom, he (Arjuna), accompanied by Kulūta, departed. Then, O king, he swiftly expelled Senābindu from his realm.

sanskrit: मोदापुरं वामदेवं सुदामानं सुसंकुलम् ।कुलूतानुत्तरांश्चैव तांश्च राज्ञः समानयत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: modāpuraṁ vāmadevaṁ sudāmānaṁ susaṁkulam ,kulūtānuttarāṁścaiva tāṁśca rājñaḥ samānayat.
english: He assembled Modapura, Vamadeva, Sudama, the well-populated (susaṃkula) region, the Kulutas and also the Northerners, and those kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 10. He assembled Modapura, Vamadeva, Sudama, the well-populated (susaṃkula) region, the Kulutas and also the Northerners, and those kings.

sanskrit: तत्रस्थः पुरुषैरेव धर्मराजस्य शासनात् ।व्यजयद्धनंजयो राजन्देशान्पञ्च प्रमाणतः ॥११॥
transliteration: tatrasthaḥ puruṣaireva dharmarājasya śāsanāt ,vyajayaddhanaṁjayo rājandeśānpañca pramāṇataḥ.
english: Remaining there, O King, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) with his very own men conquered exactly five regions by the command of King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 11. Remaining there, O King, Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna) with his very own men conquered exactly five regions by the command of King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharma-rāja).

sanskrit: स दिवःप्रस्थमासाद्य सेनाबिन्दोः पुरं महत् ।बलेन चतुरङ्गेण निवेशमकरोत्प्रभुः ॥१२॥
transliteration: sa divaḥprasthamāsādya senābindoḥ puraṁ mahat ,balena caturaṅgeṇa niveśamakarotprabhuḥ.
english: Having reached Divapratha and the great city of Senābindu, that powerful one made an encampment with his four-divisioned army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 12. Having reached Divapratha and the great city of Senābindu, that powerful one made an encampment with his four-divisioned army.

sanskrit: स तैः परिवृतः सर्वैर्विष्वगश्वं नराधिपम् ।अभ्यगच्छन्महातेजाः पौरवं पुरुषर्षभः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sa taiḥ parivṛtaḥ sarvairviṣvagaśvaṁ narādhipam ,abhyagacchanmahātejāḥ pauravaṁ puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: He (Arjuna), the bull among men (puruṣarṣabha) and greatly powerful, surrounded by all of them, approached Viṣvagaśva, the Paurava king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 13. He (Arjuna), the bull among men (puruṣarṣabha) and greatly powerful, surrounded by all of them, approached Viṣvagaśva, the Paurava king.

sanskrit: विजित्य चाहवे शूरान्पार्वतीयान्महारथान् ।ध्वजिन्या व्यजयद्राजन्पुरं पौरवरक्षितम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: vijitya cāhave śūrānpārvatīyānmahārathān ,dhvajinyā vyajayadrājanpuraṁ pauravarakṣitam.
english: O King, having conquered the brave and mighty mountain charioteers in battle, he then, with his army, vanquished the city which was protected by the Pauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 14. O King, having conquered the brave and mighty mountain charioteers in battle, he then, with his army, vanquished the city which was protected by the Pauravas.

sanskrit: पौरवं तु विनिर्जित्य दस्यून्पर्वतवासिनः ।गणानुत्सवसंकेतानजयत्सप्त पाण्डवः ॥१५॥
transliteration: pauravaṁ tu vinirjitya dasyūnparvatavāsinaḥ ,gaṇānutsavasaṁketānajayatsapta pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Moreover, the Pāṇḍava, having thoroughly conquered the Paurava region and the mountain-dwelling bandits, also vanquished seven tribal republics (gaṇas) whose assemblies were known for their festive nature.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 15. Moreover, the Pāṇḍava, having thoroughly conquered the Paurava region and the mountain-dwelling bandits, also vanquished seven tribal republics (gaṇas) whose assemblies were known for their festive nature.

sanskrit: ततः काश्मीरकान्वीरान्क्षत्रियान्क्षत्रियर्षभः ।व्यजयल्लोहितं चैव मण्डलैर्दशभिः सह ॥१६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kāśmīrakānvīrānkṣatriyānkṣatriyarṣabhaḥ ,vyajayallohitaṁ caiva maṇḍalairdaśabhiḥ saha.
english: Thereafter, the chief among Kṣatriyas conquered the valiant Kṣatriyas of Kashmir, and indeed, also the Lohita territory along with its ten districts.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 16. Thereafter, the chief among Kṣatriyas conquered the valiant Kṣatriyas of Kashmir, and indeed, also the Lohita territory along with its ten districts.

sanskrit: ततस्त्रिगर्तान्कौन्तेयो दार्वान्कोकनदाश्च ये ।क्षत्रिया बहवो राजन्नुपावर्तन्त सर्वशः ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatastrigartānkaunteyo dārvānkokanadāśca ye ,kṣatriyā bahavo rājannupāvartanta sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, O King, Kaunteya (Arjuna conquered) the Trigartas, the Dārvas, and those Kokanadas; many Kṣatriyas, indeed, all of them, submitted completely.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 17. Then, O King, Kaunteya (Arjuna conquered) the Trigartas, the Dārvas, and those Kokanadas; many Kṣatriyas, indeed, all of them, submitted completely.

sanskrit: अभिसारीं ततो रम्यां विजिग्ये कुरुनन्दनः ।उरशावासिनं चैव रोचमानं रणेऽजयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: abhisārīṁ tato ramyāṁ vijigye kurunandanaḥ ,uraśāvāsinaṁ caiva rocamānaṁ raṇe'jayat.
english: Then, Arjuna, the delight of the Kurus, conquered the charming region of Abhisārī. He also defeated Rocamāna, who resided in Urashā, in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 18. Then, Arjuna, the delight of the Kurus, conquered the charming region of Abhisārī. He also defeated Rocamāna, who resided in Urashā, in battle.

sanskrit: ततः सिंहपुरं रम्यं चित्रायुधसुरक्षितम् ।प्रामथद्बलमास्थाय पाकशासनिराहवे ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ siṁhapuraṁ ramyaṁ citrāyudhasurakṣitam ,prāmathadbalamāsthāya pākaśāsanirāhave.
english: Then, resorting to his army (bala), Arjuna, the son of Indra (Pākaśāsana), crushed the delightful city of Siṃhapura, which was well-guarded by Citrāyudha, in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 19. Then, resorting to his army (bala), Arjuna, the son of Indra (Pākaśāsana), crushed the delightful city of Siṃhapura, which was well-guarded by Citrāyudha, in battle.

sanskrit: ततः सुह्मांश्च चोलांश्च किरीटी पाण्डवर्षभः ।सहितः सर्वसैन्येन प्रामथत्कुरुनन्दनः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ suhmāṁśca colāṁśca kirīṭī pāṇḍavarṣabhaḥ ,sahitaḥ sarvasainyena prāmathatkurunandanaḥ.
english: Then, Arjuna, the wearer of the diadem (kirīṭī) and chief among the Pāṇḍavas, along with his entire army, crushed both the Suhmās and the Colas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 20. Then, Arjuna, the wearer of the diadem (kirīṭī) and chief among the Pāṇḍavas, along with his entire army, crushed both the Suhmās and the Colas.

sanskrit: ततः परमविक्रान्तो बाह्लीकान्कुरुनन्दनः ।महता परिमर्देन वशे चक्रे दुरासदान् ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ paramavikrānto bāhlīkānkurunandanaḥ ,mahatā parimardena vaśe cakre durāsadān.
english: Then, the supremely mighty Arjuna, the delight of the Kurus, brought the Bāhlīkas, who were difficult to approach, under his control through a great suppression.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 21. Then, the supremely mighty Arjuna, the delight of the Kurus, brought the Bāhlīkas, who were difficult to approach, under his control through a great suppression.

sanskrit: गृहीत्वा तु बलं सारं फल्गु चोत्सृज्य पाण्डवः ।दरदान्सह काम्बोजैरजयत्पाकशासनिः ॥२२॥
transliteration: gṛhītvā tu balaṁ sāraṁ phalgu cotsṛjya pāṇḍavaḥ ,daradānsaha kāmbojairajayatpākaśāsaniḥ.
english: Having taken the main part of his forces and discarded the weak, Arjuna, the son of Indra (pākaśāsaniḥ), conquered the Daradas along with the Kambojas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 22. Having taken the main part of his forces and discarded the weak, Arjuna, the son of Indra (pākaśāsaniḥ), conquered the Daradas along with the Kambojas.

sanskrit: प्रागुत्तरां दिशं ये च वसन्त्याश्रित्य दस्यवः ।निवसन्ति वने ये च तान्सर्वानजयत्प्रभुः ॥२३॥
transliteration: prāguttarāṁ diśaṁ ye ca vasantyāśritya dasyavaḥ ,nivasanti vane ye ca tānsarvānajayatprabhuḥ.
english: The lord (prabhuḥ) conquered all those Dasyus who inhabit the north-eastern region, and also those who reside in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 23. The lord (prabhuḥ) conquered all those Dasyus who inhabit the north-eastern region, and also those who reside in the forest.

sanskrit: लोहान्परमकाम्बोजानृषिकानुत्तरानपि ।सहितांस्तान्महाराज व्यजयत्पाकशासनिः ॥२४॥
transliteration: lohānparamakāmbojānṛṣikānuttarānapi ,sahitāṁstānmahārāja vyajayatpākaśāsaniḥ.
english: O great king (mahārāja), Arjuna, the son of Indra (pākaśāsaniḥ), conquered all those united Lohas, the foremost Kambojas, and also the Northern Rishikas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 24. O great king (mahārāja), Arjuna, the son of Indra (pākaśāsaniḥ), conquered all those united Lohas, the foremost Kambojas, and also the Northern Rishikas.

sanskrit: ऋषिकेषु तु संग्रामो बभूवातिभयंकरः ।तारकामयसंकाशः परमर्षिकपार्थयोः ॥२५॥
transliteration: ṛṣikeṣu tu saṁgrāmo babhūvātibhayaṁkaraḥ ,tārakāmayasaṁkāśaḥ paramarṣikapārthayoḥ.
english: Indeed, a most terrifying battle (saṃgrāmaḥ) occurred among the Rishikas, resembling the war of Tarakamaya, between the chief of the Rishikas and Pārtha (Arjuna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 25. Indeed, a most terrifying battle (saṃgrāmaḥ) occurred among the Rishikas, resembling the war of Tarakamaya, between the chief of the Rishikas and Pārtha (Arjuna).

sanskrit: स विजित्य ततो राजन्नृषिकान्रणमूर्धनि ।शुकोदरसमप्रख्यान्हयानष्टौ समानयत् ।मयूरसदृशानन्यानुभयानेव चापरान् ॥२६॥
transliteration: sa vijitya tato rājannṛṣikānraṇamūrdhani ,śukodarasamaprakhyānhayānaṣṭau samānayat ,mayūrasadṛśānanyānubhayāneva cāparān.
english: O King, having then conquered the Nṛṣikas on the battlefield, he brought back eight horses that resembled the color of a parrot's belly. He also brought other horses that resembled peacocks, and still others that were of both types.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 26. O King, having then conquered the Nṛṣikas on the battlefield, he brought back eight horses that resembled the color of a parrot's belly. He also brought other horses that resembled peacocks, and still others that were of both types.

sanskrit: स विनिर्जित्य संग्रामे हिमवन्तं सनिष्कुटम् ।श्वेतपर्वतमासाद्य न्यवसत्पुरुषर्षभः ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa vinirjitya saṁgrāme himavantaṁ saniṣkuṭam ,śvetaparvatamāsādya nyavasatpuruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Having completely conquered the Himalayas along with its groves in battle, that best among men then reached the White Mountain and dwelled there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 24 Verse 27. Having completely conquered the Himalayas along with its groves in battle, that best among men then reached the White Mountain and dwelled there.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स श्वेतपर्वतं वीरः समतिक्रम्य भारत ।देशं किंपुरुषावासं द्रुमपुत्रेण रक्षितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa śvetaparvataṁ vīraḥ samatikramya bhārata ,deśaṁ kiṁpuruṣāvāsaṁ drumaputreṇa rakṣitam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O Bhārata, that hero, having crossed the White Mountain, then came to the region inhabited by the Kiṃpuruṣas, which was protected by Drumaputra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: O Bhārata, that hero, having crossed the White Mountain, then came to the region inhabited by the Kiṃpuruṣas, which was protected by Drumaputra.

sanskrit: महता संनिपातेन क्षत्रियान्तकरेण ह ।व्यजयत्पाण्डवश्रेष्ठः करे चैव न्यवेशयत् ॥२॥
transliteration: mahatā saṁnipātena kṣatriyāntakareṇa ha ,vyajayatpāṇḍavaśreṣṭhaḥ kare caiva nyaveśayat.
english: With a great, indeed destructive, battle against kṣatriyas, the best of the Pāṇḍavas conquered them and brought them under his control.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 2. With a great, indeed destructive, battle against kṣatriyas, the best of the Pāṇḍavas conquered them and brought them under his control.

sanskrit: तं जित्वा हाटकं नाम देशं गुह्यकरक्षितम् ।पाकशासनिरव्यग्रः सहसैन्यः समासदत् ॥३॥
transliteration: taṁ jitvā hāṭakaṁ nāma deśaṁ guhyakarakṣitam ,pākaśāsaniravyagraḥ sahasainyaḥ samāsadat.
english: Having conquered that region named Hāṭaka, which was protected by Guhyakas, Arjuna (Pākaśāsani) then fearlessly approached it with his army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 3. Having conquered that region named Hāṭaka, which was protected by Guhyakas, Arjuna (Pākaśāsani) then fearlessly approached it with his army.

sanskrit: तांस्तु सान्त्वेन निर्जित्य मानसं सर उत्तमम् ।ऋषिकुल्याश्च ताः सर्वा ददर्श कुरुनन्दनः ॥४॥
transliteration: tāṁstu sāntvena nirjitya mānasaṁ sara uttamam ,ṛṣikulyāśca tāḥ sarvā dadarśa kurunandanaḥ.
english: But having subdued those (Guhyakas) with conciliatory words, Arjuna (Kuru-nandana) then saw the excellent Mānasa lake and all those hermitages of sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 4. But having subdued those (Guhyakas) with conciliatory words, Arjuna (Kuru-nandana) then saw the excellent Mānasa lake and all those hermitages of sages.

sanskrit: सरो मानसमासाद्य हाटकानभितः प्रभुः ।गन्धर्वरक्षितं देशं व्यजयत्पाण्डवस्ततः ॥५॥
transliteration: saro mānasamāsādya hāṭakānabhitaḥ prabhuḥ ,gandharvarakṣitaṁ deśaṁ vyajayatpāṇḍavastataḥ.
english: Having reached the Mānasa lake, the lord Arjuna (Pāṇḍava) then conquered the region around Hāṭaka, which was protected by Gandharvas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 5. Having reached the Mānasa lake, the lord Arjuna (Pāṇḍava) then conquered the region around Hāṭaka, which was protected by Gandharvas.

sanskrit: तत्र तित्तिरिकल्माषान्मण्डूकाक्षान्हयोत्तमान् ।लेभे स करमत्यन्तं गन्धर्वनगरात्तदा ॥६॥
transliteration: tatra tittirikalmāṣānmaṇḍūkākṣānhayottamān ,lebhe sa karamatyantaṁ gandharvanagarāttadā.
english: There, from the Gandharva city, he (Arjuna) then received an immense tribute consisting of excellent horses, including those speckled like partridges and those with eyes like frogs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 6. There, from the Gandharva city, he (Arjuna) then received an immense tribute consisting of excellent horses, including those speckled like partridges and those with eyes like frogs.

sanskrit: उत्तरं हरिवर्षं तु समासाद्य स पाण्डवः ।इयेष जेतुं तं देशं पाकशासननन्दनः ॥७॥
transliteration: uttaraṁ harivarṣaṁ tu samāsādya sa pāṇḍavaḥ ,iyeṣa jetuṁ taṁ deśaṁ pākaśāsananandanaḥ.
english: He, that son of the controller of Pāka (Indra), the descendant of Pāṇḍu, having reached the northern region of Harivarṣa, desired to conquer that country.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 7. He, that son of the controller of Pāka (Indra), the descendant of Pāṇḍu, having reached the northern region of Harivarṣa, desired to conquer that country.

sanskrit: तत एनं महाकाया महावीर्या महाबलाः ।द्वारपालाः समासाद्य हृष्टा वचनमब्रुवन् ॥८॥
transliteration: tata enaṁ mahākāyā mahāvīryā mahābalāḥ ,dvārapālāḥ samāsādya hṛṣṭā vacanamabruvan.
english: Then, the gatekeepers (dvārapālāḥ), who were immensely bodied, greatly valorous, and exceedingly powerful, approached him and joyfully spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 8. Then, the gatekeepers (dvārapālāḥ), who were immensely bodied, greatly valorous, and exceedingly powerful, approached him and joyfully spoke these words.

sanskrit: पार्थ नेदं त्वया शक्यं पुरं जेतुं कथंचन ।उपावर्तस्व कल्याण पर्याप्तमिदमच्युत ॥९॥
transliteration: pārtha nedaṁ tvayā śakyaṁ puraṁ jetuṁ kathaṁcana ,upāvartasva kalyāṇa paryāptamidamacyuta.
english: O Pārtha, this city cannot be conquered by you in any way. O blessed one, turn back; O infallible one, this is enough.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 9. O Pārtha, this city cannot be conquered by you in any way. O blessed one, turn back; O infallible one, this is enough.

sanskrit: इदं पुरं यः प्रविशेद्ध्रुवं स न भवेन्नरः ।प्रीयामहे त्वया वीर पर्याप्तो विजयस्तव ॥१०॥
transliteration: idaṁ puraṁ yaḥ praviśeddhruvaṁ sa na bhavennaraḥ ,prīyāmahe tvayā vīra paryāpto vijayastava.
english: Any man who enters this city will certainly not remain a man. O hero, we are pleased with you; your victory is sufficient.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 10. Any man who enters this city will certainly not remain a man. O hero, we are pleased with you; your victory is sufficient.

sanskrit: न चापि किंचिज्जेतव्यमर्जुनात्र प्रदृश्यते ।उत्तराः कुरवो ह्येते नात्र युद्धं प्रवर्तते ॥११॥
transliteration: na cāpi kiṁcijjetavyamarjunātra pradṛśyate ,uttarāḥ kuravo hyete nātra yuddhaṁ pravartate.
english: Arjuna, nothing at all appears here that needs to be conquered. These are indeed the Northern Kurus, and no battle is waged here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 11. Arjuna, nothing at all appears here that needs to be conquered. These are indeed the Northern Kurus, and no battle is waged here.

sanskrit: प्रविष्टश्चापि कौन्तेय नेह द्रक्ष्यसि किंचन ।न हि मानुषदेहेन शक्यमत्राभिवीक्षितुम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: praviṣṭaścāpi kaunteya neha drakṣyasi kiṁcana ,na hi mānuṣadehena śakyamatrābhivīkṣitum.
english: And even if you enter, O son of Kunti, you will not see anything here. Indeed, it is not possible to observe this place with a human body.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 12. And even if you enter, O son of Kunti, you will not see anything here. Indeed, it is not possible to observe this place with a human body.

sanskrit: अथेह पुरुषव्याघ्र किंचिदन्यच्चिकीर्षसि ।तद्ब्रवीहि करिष्यामो वचनात्तव भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: atheha puruṣavyāghra kiṁcidanyaccikīrṣasi ,tadbravīhi kariṣyāmo vacanāttava bhārata.
english: Now, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), if you wish to do anything else here, then tell us, O descendant of bharata (bhārata); we will perform it according to your word.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 13. Now, O tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), if you wish to do anything else here, then tell us, O descendant of bharata (bhārata); we will perform it according to your word.

sanskrit: ततस्तानब्रवीद्राजन्नर्जुनः पाकशासनिः ।पार्थिवत्वं चिकीर्षामि धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tatastānabravīdrājannarjunaḥ pākaśāsaniḥ ,pārthivatvaṁ cikīrṣāmi dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Then, O king, Arjuna, the son of indra (pākaśāsani), spoke to them: 'I desire the sovereignty of the wise King of Righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 14. Then, O king, Arjuna, the son of indra (pākaśāsani), spoke to them: 'I desire the sovereignty of the wise King of Righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira.'

sanskrit: न प्रवेक्ष्यामि वो देशं बाध्यत्वं यदि मानुषैः ।युधिष्ठिराय यत्किंचित्करवन्नः प्रदीयताम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: na pravekṣyāmi vo deśaṁ bādhyatvaṁ yadi mānuṣaiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhirāya yatkiṁcitkaravannaḥ pradīyatām.
english: I will not enter your territory if your people cause trouble or offer resistance. Whatever tribute (kara) is due for Yudhiṣṭhira, let that be given to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 15. I will not enter your territory if your people cause trouble or offer resistance. Whatever tribute (kara) is due for Yudhiṣṭhira, let that be given to us.

sanskrit: ततो दिव्यानि वस्त्राणि दिव्यान्याभरणानि च ।मोकाजिनानि दिव्यानि तस्मै ते प्रददुः करम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato divyāni vastrāṇi divyānyābharaṇāni ca ,mokājināni divyāni tasmai te pradaduḥ karam.
english: Then they gave him splendid garments, magnificent ornaments, and excellent mokā (deer) skins as tribute (kara).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 16. Then they gave him splendid garments, magnificent ornaments, and excellent mokā (deer) skins as tribute (kara).

sanskrit: एवं स पुरुषव्याघ्रो विजिग्ये दिशमुत्तराम् ।संग्रामान्सुबहून्कृत्वा क्षत्रियैर्दस्युभिस्तथा ॥१७॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa puruṣavyāghro vijigye diśamuttarām ,saṁgrāmānsubahūnkṛtvā kṣatriyairdasyubhistathā.
english: Thus, that lion among men (Arjuna) conquered the northern direction, having fought many battles with both kṣatriyas (warriors) and dacoits.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 17. Thus, that lion among men (Arjuna) conquered the northern direction, having fought many battles with both kṣatriyas (warriors) and dacoits.

sanskrit: स विनिर्जित्य राज्ञस्तान्करे च विनिवेश्य ह ।धनान्यादाय सर्वेभ्यो रत्नानि विविधानि च ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa vinirjitya rājñastānkare ca viniveśya ha ,dhanānyādāya sarvebhyo ratnāni vividhāni ca.
english: Having completely conquered those kings and brought them under tribute (kara), he (Arjuna) indeed took wealth and various jewels from all of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 18. Having completely conquered those kings and brought them under tribute (kara), he (Arjuna) indeed took wealth and various jewels from all of them.

sanskrit: हयांस्तित्तिरिकल्माषाञ्शुकपत्रनिभानपि ।मयूरसदृशांश्चान्यान्सर्वाननिलरंहसः ॥१९॥
transliteration: hayāṁstittirikalmāṣāñśukapatranibhānapi ,mayūrasadṛśāṁścānyānsarvānanilaraṁhasaḥ.
english: Horses, some spotted like partridges, others resembling parrot feathers, and still others similar to peacocks, all possessing the speed of the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 19. Horses, some spotted like partridges, others resembling parrot feathers, and still others similar to peacocks, all possessing the speed of the wind.

sanskrit: वृतः सुमहता राजन्बलेन चतुरङ्गिणा ।आजगाम पुनर्वीरः शक्रप्रस्थं पुरोत्तमम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: vṛtaḥ sumahatā rājanbalena caturaṅgiṇā ,ājagāma punarvīraḥ śakraprasthaṁ purottamam.
english: O King, the hero, accompanied by a very large four-division army, returned to Śakraprastha, the finest of cities.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 25 Verse 20. O King, the hero, accompanied by a very large four-division army, returned to Śakraprastha, the finest of cities.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतस्मिन्नेव काले तु भीमसेनोऽपि वीर्यवान् ।धर्मराजमनुज्ञाप्य ययौ प्राचीं दिशं प्रति ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etasminneva kāle tu bhīmaseno'pi vīryavān ,dharmarājamanujñāpya yayau prācīṁ diśaṁ prati.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: At this very time, the mighty Bhīmasena also, having obtained permission from the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), departed towards the eastern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: At this very time, the mighty Bhīmasena also, having obtained permission from the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), departed towards the eastern direction.

sanskrit: महता बलचक्रेण परराष्ट्रावमर्दिना ।वृतो भरतशार्दूलो द्विषच्छोकविवर्धनः ॥२॥
transliteration: mahatā balacakreṇa pararāṣṭrāvamardinā ,vṛto bharataśārdūlo dviṣacchokavivardhanaḥ.
english: The tiger among the Bhāratas, accompanied by a mighty army capable of crushing hostile kingdoms, amplified the grief of his adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 2. The tiger among the Bhāratas, accompanied by a mighty army capable of crushing hostile kingdoms, amplified the grief of his adversaries.

sanskrit: स गत्वा राजशार्दूलः पाञ्चालानां पुरं महत् ।पाञ्चालान्विविधोपायैः सान्त्वयामास पाण्डवः ॥३॥
transliteration: sa gatvā rājaśārdūlaḥ pāñcālānāṁ puraṁ mahat ,pāñcālānvividhopāyaiḥ sāntvayāmāsa pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍava (Bhīma), who was a lion among kings, went to the great city of the Pañcālas and pacified them using various strategies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 3. The Pāṇḍava (Bhīma), who was a lion among kings, went to the great city of the Pañcālas and pacified them using various strategies.

sanskrit: ततः स गण्डकीं शूरो विदेहांश्च नरर्षभः ।विजित्याल्पेन कालेन दशार्णानगमत्प्रभुः ॥४॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa gaṇḍakīṁ śūro videhāṁśca nararṣabhaḥ ,vijityālpena kālena daśārṇānagamatprabhuḥ.
english: Then, that valiant lord, the best among men, having conquered the Gaṇḍakī region and the Videhas in a short time, proceeded to the Daśārṇas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 4. Then, that valiant lord, the best among men, having conquered the Gaṇḍakī region and the Videhas in a short time, proceeded to the Daśārṇas.

sanskrit: तत्र दाशार्णको राजा सुधर्मा लोमहर्षणम् ।कृतवान्कर्म भीमेन महद्युद्धं निरायुधम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tatra dāśārṇako rājā sudharmā lomaharṣaṇam ,kṛtavānkarma bhīmena mahadyuddhaṁ nirāyudham.
english: There, Sudharman, the king of Daśārṇa, engaged in a great and thrilling unarmed battle (karma) with Bhīma.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 5. There, Sudharman, the king of Daśārṇa, engaged in a great and thrilling unarmed battle (karma) with Bhīma.

sanskrit: भीमसेनस्तु तद्दृष्ट्वा तस्य कर्म परंतपः ।अधिसेनापतिं चक्रे सुधर्माणं महाबलम् ॥६॥
transliteration: bhīmasenastu taddṛṣṭvā tasya karma paraṁtapaḥ ,adhisenāpatiṁ cakre sudharmāṇaṁ mahābalam.
english: But Bhīmasena, the tormentor of his foes, upon witnessing that deed (karma) of his, appointed the mighty Sudharman as his chief commander.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 6. But Bhīmasena, the tormentor of his foes, upon witnessing that deed (karma) of his, appointed the mighty Sudharman as his chief commander.

sanskrit: ततः प्राचीं दिशं भीमो ययौ भीमपराक्रमः ।सैन्येन महता राजन्कम्पयन्निव मेदिनीम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ prācīṁ diśaṁ bhīmo yayau bhīmaparākramaḥ ,sainyena mahatā rājankampayanniva medinīm.
english: O King, then Bhima, possessing terrible prowess, marched towards the eastern direction with a great army, as if making the earth tremble.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 7. O King, then Bhima, possessing terrible prowess, marched towards the eastern direction with a great army, as if making the earth tremble.

sanskrit: सोऽश्वमेधेश्वरं राजन्रोचमानं सहानुजम् ।जिगाय समरे वीरो बलेन बलिनां वरः ॥८॥
transliteration: so'śvamedheśvaraṁ rājanrocamānaṁ sahānujam ,jigāya samare vīro balena balināṁ varaḥ.
english: O King, that hero, the foremost among the mighty, conquered in battle the resplendent lord of the (aśvamedha) horse sacrifice, along with his younger brother, by his great strength.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 8. O King, that hero, the foremost among the mighty, conquered in battle the resplendent lord of the (aśvamedha) horse sacrifice, along with his younger brother, by his great strength.

sanskrit: स तं निर्जित्य कौन्तेयो नातितीव्रेण कर्मणा ।पूर्वदेशं महावीर्यो विजिग्ये कुरुनन्दनः ॥९॥
transliteration: sa taṁ nirjitya kaunteyo nātitīvreṇa karmaṇā ,pūrvadeśaṁ mahāvīryo vijigye kurunandanaḥ.
english: Having conquered him, the son of Kunti (kaunteya), the delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), a man of great prowess, conquered the eastern region without excessively fierce action (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 9. Having conquered him, the son of Kunti (kaunteya), the delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), a man of great prowess, conquered the eastern region without excessively fierce action (karma).

sanskrit: ततो दक्षिणमागम्य पुलिन्दनगरं महत् ।सुकुमारं वशे चक्रे सुमित्रं च नराधिपम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tato dakṣiṇamāgamya pulindanagaraṁ mahat ,sukumāraṁ vaśe cakre sumitraṁ ca narādhipam.
english: Then, having proceeded south, he brought the great city of Pulindas, as well as King Sukumara and King Sumitra, under his control.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 10. Then, having proceeded south, he brought the great city of Pulindas, as well as King Sukumara and King Sumitra, under his control.

sanskrit: ततस्तु धर्मराजस्य शासनाद्भरतर्षभः ।शिशुपालं महावीर्यमभ्ययाज्जनमेजय ॥११॥
transliteration: tatastu dharmarājasya śāsanādbharatarṣabhaḥ ,śiśupālaṁ mahāvīryamabhyayājjanamejaya.
english: Then, O Janamejaya, the best among Bharatas (Sahadeva), by the command of Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), approached the extremely mighty Śiśupāla.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 11. Then, O Janamejaya, the best among Bharatas (Sahadeva), by the command of Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), approached the extremely mighty Śiśupāla.

sanskrit: चेदिराजोऽपि तच्छ्रुत्वा पाण्डवस्य चिकीर्षितम् ।उपनिष्क्रम्य नगरात्प्रत्यगृह्णात्परंतपः ॥१२॥
transliteration: cedirājo'pi tacchrutvā pāṇḍavasya cikīrṣitam ,upaniṣkramya nagarātpratyagṛhṇātparaṁtapaḥ.
english: The king of Chedi, that scorcher of foes (Śiśupāla), having heard the Pāṇḍava's (Sahadeva's) intention (for the rājasūya yajña), also came out from his city and received him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 12. The king of Chedi, that scorcher of foes (Śiśupāla), having heard the Pāṇḍava's (Sahadeva's) intention (for the rājasūya yajña), also came out from his city and received him.

sanskrit: तौ समेत्य महाराज कुरुचेदिवृषौ तदा ।उभयोरात्मकुलयोः कौशल्यं पर्यपृच्छताम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tau sametya mahārāja kurucedivṛṣau tadā ,ubhayorātmakulayoḥ kauśalyaṁ paryapṛcchatām.
english: Then, O great king (Janamejaya), those two, the chiefs of the Kurus (Sahadeva) and the Chedis (Śiśupāla), having met, inquired about the well-being of both their own families.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 13. Then, O great king (Janamejaya), those two, the chiefs of the Kurus (Sahadeva) and the Chedis (Śiśupāla), having met, inquired about the well-being of both their own families.

sanskrit: ततो निवेद्य तद्राष्ट्रं चेदिराजो विशां पते ।उवाच भीमं प्रहसन्किमिदं कुरुषेऽनघ ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato nivedya tadrāṣṭraṁ cedirājo viśāṁ pate ,uvāca bhīmaṁ prahasankimidaṁ kuruṣe'nagha.
english: Then, O lord of the people (Janamejaya), the king of Chedi, having announced his kingdom (or the purpose related to the kingdom), smilingly said to Bhīma, 'O sinless one, what is this you are doing?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 14. Then, O lord of the people (Janamejaya), the king of Chedi, having announced his kingdom (or the purpose related to the kingdom), smilingly said to Bhīma, 'O sinless one, what is this you are doing?'

sanskrit: तस्य भीमस्तदाचख्यौ धर्मराजचिकीर्षितम् ।स च तत्प्रतिगृह्यैव तथा चक्रे नराधिपः ॥१५॥
transliteration: tasya bhīmastadācakhyau dharmarājacikīrṣitam ,sa ca tatpratigṛhyaiva tathā cakre narādhipaḥ.
english: Bhima then told him what Yudhishthira intended to do. And that king, having accepted it, acted accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 15. Bhima then told him what Yudhishthira intended to do. And that king, having accepted it, acted accordingly.

sanskrit: ततो भीमस्तत्र राजन्नुषित्वा त्रिदशाः क्षपाः ।सत्कृतः शिशुपालेन ययौ सबलवाहनः ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato bhīmastatra rājannuṣitvā tridaśāḥ kṣapāḥ ,satkṛtaḥ śiśupālena yayau sabalavāhanaḥ.
english: Then, O king, Bhima, having stayed there for thirty nights, and having been honored by Shishupala, departed with his army and vehicles.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 26 Verse 16. Then, O king, Bhima, having stayed there for thirty nights, and having been honored by Shishupala, departed with his army and vehicles.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः कुमारविषये श्रेणिमन्तमथाजयत् ।कोसलाधिपतिं चैव बृहद्बलमरिंदमः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ kumāraviṣaye śreṇimantamathājayat ,kosalādhipatiṁ caiva bṛhadbalamariṁdamaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, in the Kumāra region, the subduer of enemies (Bhima) conquered King Śreṇimān, and also Bṛhadbala, the lord of Kosala.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, in the Kumāra region, the subduer of enemies (Bhima) conquered King Śreṇimān, and also Bṛhadbala, the lord of Kosala.

sanskrit: अयोध्यायां तु धर्मज्ञं दीर्घप्रज्ञं महाबलम् ।अजयत्पाण्डवश्रेष्ठो नातितीव्रेण कर्मणा ॥२॥
transliteration: ayodhyāyāṁ tu dharmajñaṁ dīrghaprajñaṁ mahābalam ,ajayatpāṇḍavaśreṣṭho nātitīvreṇa karmaṇā.
english: But in Ayodhya, the best of the Pandavas (Bhima) conquered the king who was a knower of natural law (dharma), profoundly wise, and exceedingly mighty, not by excessively fierce action.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 2. But in Ayodhya, the best of the Pandavas (Bhima) conquered the king who was a knower of natural law (dharma), profoundly wise, and exceedingly mighty, not by excessively fierce action.

sanskrit: ततो गोपालकच्छं च सोत्तमानपि चोत्तरान् ।मल्लानामधिपं चैव पार्थिवं व्यजयत्प्रभुः ॥३॥
transliteration: tato gopālakacchaṁ ca sottamānapi cottarān ,mallānāmadhipaṁ caiva pārthivaṁ vyajayatprabhuḥ.
english: Then, the mighty lord conquered Gopālakaccha. He also conquered even the excellent ones and the northerners, and indeed the king, the ruler of the Mallās.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 3. Then, the mighty lord conquered Gopālakaccha. He also conquered even the excellent ones and the northerners, and indeed the king, the ruler of the Mallās.

sanskrit: ततो हिमवतः पार्श्वे समभ्येत्य जरद्गवम् ।सर्वमल्पेन कालेन देशं चक्रे वशे बली ॥४॥
transliteration: tato himavataḥ pārśve samabhyetya jaradgavam ,sarvamalpena kālena deśaṁ cakre vaśe balī.
english: Then, the mighty one (balī), having approached Jaradgava near the Himalayas, brought all regions under his sway (vaśe) in a short time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 4. Then, the mighty one (balī), having approached Jaradgava near the Himalayas, brought all regions under his sway (vaśe) in a short time.

sanskrit: एवं बहुविधान्देशान्विजित्य पुरुषर्षभः ।उन्नाटमभितो जिग्ये कुक्षिमन्तं च पर्वतम् ।पाण्डवः सुमहावीर्यो बलेन बलिनां वरः ॥५॥
transliteration: evaṁ bahuvidhāndeśānvijitya puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,unnāṭamabhito jigye kukṣimantaṁ ca parvatam ,pāṇḍavaḥ sumahāvīryo balena balināṁ varaḥ.
english: Thus, having conquered many diverse regions, that bull among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ), the Pāṇḍava, who was of immense valor (sumahāvīryaḥ) and the best among the mighty (balinām varaḥ) by his strength, conquered Unnāṭa all around and the Kukṣimat mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 5. Thus, having conquered many diverse regions, that bull among men (puruṣarṣabhaḥ), the Pāṇḍava, who was of immense valor (sumahāvīryaḥ) and the best among the mighty (balinām varaḥ) by his strength, conquered Unnāṭa all around and the Kukṣimat mountain.

sanskrit: स काशिराजं समरे सुबन्धुमनिवर्तिनम् ।वशे चक्रे महाबाहुर्भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ॥६॥
transliteration: sa kāśirājaṁ samare subandhumanivartinam ,vaśe cakre mahābāhurbhīmo bhīmaparākramaḥ.
english: That mighty-armed Bhīma, possessing dreadful valor (bhīmaparākramaḥ), brought Subandhu, the unyielding king of Kāśi, under his control (vaśe) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 6. That mighty-armed Bhīma, possessing dreadful valor (bhīmaparākramaḥ), brought Subandhu, the unyielding king of Kāśi, under his control (vaśe) in battle.

sanskrit: ततः सुपार्श्वमभितस्तथा राजपतिं क्रथम् ।युध्यमानं बलात्संख्ये विजिग्ये पाण्डवर्षभः ॥७॥
transliteration: tataḥ supārśvamabhitastathā rājapatiṁ kratham ,yudhyamānaṁ balātsaṁkhye vijigye pāṇḍavarṣabhaḥ.
english: Then, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas conquered Suparśva and King Kratha, who were fighting powerfully in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 7. Then, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas conquered Suparśva and King Kratha, who were fighting powerfully in battle.

sanskrit: ततो मत्स्यान्महातेजा मलयांश्च महाबलान् ।अनवद्यान्गयांश्चैव पशुभूमिं च सर्वशः ॥८॥
transliteration: tato matsyānmahātejā malayāṁśca mahābalān ,anavadyāngayāṁścaiva paśubhūmiṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
