english: Then, the immensely powerful [Pāṇḍava] conquered the Matsyas, as well as the mighty Malayās, and also the blameless Gayās, and completely subjugated the region of Paśubhūmi.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 8. Then, the immensely powerful [Pāṇḍava] conquered the Matsyas, as well as the mighty Malayās, and also the blameless Gayās, and completely subjugated the region of Paśubhūmi.

sanskrit: निवृत्य च महाबाहुर्मदर्वीकं महीधरम् ।सोपदेशं विनिर्जित्य प्रययावुत्तरामुखः ।वत्सभूमिं च कौन्तेयो विजिग्ये बलवान्बलात् ॥९॥
transliteration: nivṛtya ca mahābāhurmadarvīkaṁ mahīdharam ,sopadeśaṁ vinirjitya prayayāvuttarāmukhaḥ ,vatsabhūmiṁ ca kaunteyo vijigye balavānbalāt.
english: And the mighty-armed one, after returning, completely conquered Madarvīka, the mountainous region, along with its territories, and then proceeded northwards. The powerful son of Kuntī (Kaunteya) also conquered the land of Vatsa by force.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 9. And the mighty-armed one, after returning, completely conquered Madarvīka, the mountainous region, along with its territories, and then proceeded northwards. The powerful son of Kuntī (Kaunteya) also conquered the land of Vatsa by force.

sanskrit: भर्गाणामधिपं चैव निषादाधिपतिं तथा ।विजिग्ये भूमिपालांश्च मणिमत्प्रमुखान्बहून् ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhargāṇāmadhipaṁ caiva niṣādādhipatiṁ tathā ,vijigye bhūmipālāṁśca maṇimatpramukhānbahūn.
english: He also conquered the chief of the Bhargas, and similarly the lord of the Niṣādas, as well as many other kings, prominent among them being Maṇimat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 10. He also conquered the chief of the Bhargas, and similarly the lord of the Niṣādas, as well as many other kings, prominent among them being Maṇimat.

sanskrit: ततो दक्षिणमल्लांश्च भोगवन्तं च पाण्डवः ।तरसैवाजयद्भीमो नातितीव्रेण कर्मणा ॥११॥
transliteration: tato dakṣiṇamallāṁśca bhogavantaṁ ca pāṇḍavaḥ ,tarasaivājayadbhīmo nātitīvreṇa karmaṇā.
english: Then Bhīma, the son of Pāṇḍu, swiftly conquered the Southern Mallās and Bhogavanta with no excessive exertion (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 11. Then Bhīma, the son of Pāṇḍu, swiftly conquered the Southern Mallās and Bhogavanta with no excessive exertion (karma).

sanskrit: शर्मकान्वर्मकांश्चैव सान्त्वेनैवाजयत्प्रभुः ।वैदेहकं च राजानं जनकं जगतीपतिम् ।विजिग्ये पुरुषव्याघ्रो नातितीव्रेण कर्मणा ॥१२॥
transliteration: śarmakānvarmakāṁścaiva sāntvenaivājayatprabhuḥ ,vaidehakaṁ ca rājānaṁ janakaṁ jagatīpatim ,vijigye puruṣavyāghro nātitīvreṇa karmaṇā.
english: The lord (Bhīma) conquered the Śarmakās and Varmakās merely by conciliation. That tiger among men (Bhīma) also conquered King Janaka of Videha, the protector of the earth, without excessive effort (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 12. The lord (Bhīma) conquered the Śarmakās and Varmakās merely by conciliation. That tiger among men (Bhīma) also conquered King Janaka of Videha, the protector of the earth, without excessive effort (karma).

sanskrit: वैदेहस्थस्तु कौन्तेय इन्द्रपर्वतमन्तिकात् ।किरातानामधिपतीन्व्यजयत्सप्त पाण्डवः ॥१३॥
transliteration: vaidehasthastu kaunteya indraparvatamantikāt ,kirātānāmadhipatīnvyajayatsapta pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Then, while in Videha, that son of Kuntī, the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma), conquered the seven chiefs of the Kirātas from the vicinity of Indrapervata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 13. Then, while in Videha, that son of Kuntī, the Pāṇḍava (Bhīma), conquered the seven chiefs of the Kirātas from the vicinity of Indrapervata.

sanskrit: ततः सुह्मान्प्राच्यसुह्मान्समक्षांश्चैव वीर्यवान् ।विजित्य युधि कौन्तेयो मागधानुपयाद्बली ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataḥ suhmānprācyasuhmānsamakṣāṁścaiva vīryavān ,vijitya yudhi kaunteyo māgadhānupayādbalī.
english: Then, that mighty and powerful son of Kuntī (Bhīma), having conquered the Suhmās, the Eastern Suhmās, and the Samakṣas in battle, proceeded towards the Māgadhas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 14. Then, that mighty and powerful son of Kuntī (Bhīma), having conquered the Suhmās, the Eastern Suhmās, and the Samakṣas in battle, proceeded towards the Māgadhas.

sanskrit: दण्डं च दण्डधारं च विजित्य पृथिवीपतीन् ।तैरेव सहितः सर्वैर्गिरिव्रजमुपाद्रवत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: daṇḍaṁ ca daṇḍadhāraṁ ca vijitya pṛthivīpatīn ,taireva sahitaḥ sarvairgirivrajamupādravat.
english: Having conquered Daṇḍa, Daṇḍadhāra, and other kings, he advanced towards Girivraja, accompanied by all of them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 15. Having conquered Daṇḍa, Daṇḍadhāra, and other kings, he advanced towards Girivraja, accompanied by all of them.

sanskrit: जारासंधिं सान्त्वयित्वा करे च विनिवेश्य ह ।तैरेव सहितो राजन्कर्णमभ्यद्रवद्बली ॥१६॥
transliteration: jārāsaṁdhiṁ sāntvayitvā kare ca viniveśya ha ,taireva sahito rājankarṇamabhyadravadbalī.
english: O King, having conciliated Jarāsandha and brought him under his control, the mighty one (Arjuna), accompanied by those very same kings, then advanced towards Karṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 16. O King, having conciliated Jarāsandha and brought him under his control, the mighty one (Arjuna), accompanied by those very same kings, then advanced towards Karṇa.

sanskrit: स कम्पयन्निव महीं बलेन चतुरङ्गिणा ।युयुधे पाण्डवश्रेष्ठः कर्णेनामित्रघातिना ॥१७॥
transliteration: sa kampayanniva mahīṁ balena caturaṅgiṇā ,yuyudhe pāṇḍavaśreṣṭhaḥ karṇenāmitraghātinā.
english: He (Arjuna), as if shaking the earth with his four-limbed army, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas, fought with Karṇa, the destroyer of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 17. He (Arjuna), as if shaking the earth with his four-limbed army, the foremost of the Pāṇḍavas, fought with Karṇa, the destroyer of enemies.

sanskrit: स कर्णं युधि निर्जित्य वशे कृत्वा च भारत ।ततो विजिग्ये बलवान्राज्ञः पर्वतवासिनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: sa karṇaṁ yudhi nirjitya vaśe kṛtvā ca bhārata ,tato vijigye balavānrājñaḥ parvatavāsinaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, having defeated Karṇa in battle and brought him under his sway, the powerful one (Arjuna) then conquered the mountain-dwelling kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 18. O Bhārata, having defeated Karṇa in battle and brought him under his sway, the powerful one (Arjuna) then conquered the mountain-dwelling kings.

sanskrit: अथ मोदागिरिं चैव राजानं बलवत्तरम् ।पाण्डवो बाहुवीर्येण निजघान महामृधे ॥१९॥
transliteration: atha modāgiriṁ caiva rājānaṁ balavattaram ,pāṇḍavo bāhuvīryeṇa nijaghāna mahāmṛdhe.
english: Then the Pāṇḍava, by the might of his arms, slew the very powerful King Modagiri in a great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 19. Then the Pāṇḍava, by the might of his arms, slew the very powerful King Modagiri in a great battle.

sanskrit: ततः पौण्ड्राधिपं वीरं वासुदेवं महाबलम् ।कौशिकीकच्छनिलयं राजानं च महौजसम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ pauṇḍrādhipaṁ vīraṁ vāsudevaṁ mahābalam ,kauśikīkacchanilayaṁ rājānaṁ ca mahaujasam.
english: Then the heroic Vāsudeva, the mighty lord of Pauṇḍra, and the greatly powerful King whose abode was on the banks of the Kauśikī...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 20. Then the heroic Vāsudeva, the mighty lord of Pauṇḍra, and the greatly powerful King whose abode was on the banks of the Kauśikī...

sanskrit: उभौ बलवृतौ वीरावुभौ तीव्रपराक्रमौ ।निर्जित्याजौ महाराज वङ्गराजमुपाद्रवत् ॥२१॥
transliteration: ubhau balavṛtau vīrāvubhau tīvraparākramau ,nirjityājau mahārāja vaṅgarājamupādravat.
english: O great king, having conquered both these kings, who were accompanied by their forces and possessed fierce valor, he then proceeded to attack the King of Vaṅga.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 21. O great king, having conquered both these kings, who were accompanied by their forces and possessed fierce valor, he then proceeded to attack the King of Vaṅga.

sanskrit: समुद्रसेनं निर्जित्य चन्द्रसेनं च पार्थिवम् ।ताम्रलिप्तं च राजानं काचं वङ्गाधिपं तथा ॥२२॥
transliteration: samudrasenaṁ nirjitya candrasenaṁ ca pārthivam ,tāmraliptaṁ ca rājānaṁ kācaṁ vaṅgādhipaṁ tathā.
english: Having conquered Samudrasena, as well as King Chandrasena, and King Tāmralipta, and similarly Kāca, the ruler of Vaṅga.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 22. Having conquered Samudrasena, as well as King Chandrasena, and King Tāmralipta, and similarly Kāca, the ruler of Vaṅga.

sanskrit: सुह्मानामधिपं चैव ये च सागरवासिनः ।सर्वान्म्लेच्छगणांश्चैव विजिग्ये भरतर्षभः ॥२३॥
transliteration: suhmānāmadhipaṁ caiva ye ca sāgaravāsinaḥ ,sarvānmlecchagaṇāṁścaiva vijigye bharatarṣabhaḥ.
english: The foremost among the Bharatas (Bhīma) conquered the lord of the Suhma people, as well as those who live by the ocean, and indeed all the barbarian (mleccha) tribes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 23. The foremost among the Bharatas (Bhīma) conquered the lord of the Suhma people, as well as those who live by the ocean, and indeed all the barbarian (mleccha) tribes.

sanskrit: एवं बहुविधान्देशान्विजित्य पवनात्मजः ।वसु तेभ्य उपादाय लौहित्यमगमद्बली ॥२४॥
transliteration: evaṁ bahuvidhāndeśānvijitya pavanātmajaḥ ,vasu tebhya upādāya lauhityamagamadbalī.
english: Having thus conquered many diverse lands and collected tribute (vasu) from them, the mighty son of Vayu (Bhīma) proceeded to the Lohitya region.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 24. Having thus conquered many diverse lands and collected tribute (vasu) from them, the mighty son of Vayu (Bhīma) proceeded to the Lohitya region.

sanskrit: स सर्वान्म्लेच्छनृपतीन्सागरद्वीपवासिनः ।करमाहारयामास रत्नानि विविधानि च ॥२५॥
transliteration: sa sarvānmlecchanṛpatīnsāgaradvīpavāsinaḥ ,karamāhārayāmāsa ratnāni vividhāni ca.
english: He collected tribute and various jewels from all the barbarian (mleccha) kings who resided on ocean islands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 25. He collected tribute and various jewels from all the barbarian (mleccha) kings who resided on ocean islands.

sanskrit: चन्दनागुरुवस्त्राणि मणिमुक्तमनुत्तमम् ।काञ्चनं रजतं वज्रं विद्रुमं च महाधनम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: candanāguruvastrāṇi maṇimuktamanuttamam ,kāñcanaṁ rajataṁ vajraṁ vidrumaṁ ca mahādhanam.
english: He collected sandalwood, aloewood, fine garments, excellent jewels and pearls, gold, silver, diamonds, and coral, all representing immense wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 26. He collected sandalwood, aloewood, fine garments, excellent jewels and pearls, gold, silver, diamonds, and coral, all representing immense wealth.

sanskrit: स कोटिशतसंख्येन धनेन महता तदा ।अभ्यवर्षदमेयात्मा धनवर्षेण पाण्डवम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sa koṭiśatasaṁkhyena dhanena mahatā tadā ,abhyavarṣadameyātmā dhanavarṣeṇa pāṇḍavam.
english: Then, that person of immeasurable spirit (ātman) showered the Pāṇḍava with a great rain of wealth, amounting to hundreds of millions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 27. Then, that person of immeasurable spirit (ātman) showered the Pāṇḍava with a great rain of wealth, amounting to hundreds of millions.

sanskrit: इन्द्रप्रस्थमथागम्य भीमो भीमपराक्रमः ।निवेदयामास तदा धर्मराजाय तद्धनम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: indraprasthamathāgamya bhīmo bhīmaparākramaḥ ,nivedayāmāsa tadā dharmarājāya taddhanam.
english: Then Bhīma, of formidable prowess, after arriving at Indraprastha, reported that wealth to the King of righteousness (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 27 Verse 28. Then Bhīma, of formidable prowess, after arriving at Indraprastha, reported that wealth to the King of righteousness (dharma), Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथैव सहदेवोऽपि धर्मराजेन पूजितः ।महत्या सेनया सार्धं प्रययौ दक्षिणां दिशम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathaiva sahadevo'pi dharmarājena pūjitaḥ ,mahatyā senayā sārdhaṁ prayayau dakṣiṇāṁ diśam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Similarly, Sahadeva also, honored by the King of righteousness (dharma), proceeded towards the southern direction with a large army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Similarly, Sahadeva also, honored by the King of righteousness (dharma), proceeded towards the southern direction with a large army.

sanskrit: स शूरसेनान्कार्त्स्न्येन पूर्वमेवाजयत्प्रभुः ।मत्स्यराजं च कौरव्यो वशे चक्रे बलाद्बली ॥२॥
transliteration: sa śūrasenānkārtsnyena pūrvamevājayatprabhuḥ ,matsyarājaṁ ca kauravyo vaśe cakre balādbalī.
english: That mighty lord had already completely conquered the Śūrasenas. And the powerful Kuru prince (Sahadeva) forcibly brought the Matsya king under his dominion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 2. That mighty lord had already completely conquered the Śūrasenas. And the powerful Kuru prince (Sahadeva) forcibly brought the Matsya king under his dominion.

sanskrit: अधिराजाधिपं चैव दन्तवक्रं महाहवे ।जिगाय करदं चैव स्वराज्ये संन्यवेशयत् ॥३॥
transliteration: adhirājādhipaṁ caiva dantavakraṁ mahāhave ,jigāya karadaṁ caiva svarājye saṁnyaveśayat.
english: He conquered Dantavakra, the supreme sovereign, in a great battle. He also made him pay tribute and established him in his own territory.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 3. He conquered Dantavakra, the supreme sovereign, in a great battle. He also made him pay tribute and established him in his own territory.

sanskrit: सुकुमारं वशे चक्रे सुमित्रं च नराधिपम् ।तथैवापरमत्स्यांश्च व्यजयत्स पटच्चरान् ॥४॥
transliteration: sukumāraṁ vaśe cakre sumitraṁ ca narādhipam ,tathaivāparamatsyāṁśca vyajayatsa paṭaccarān.
english: He brought Sukumara under his control and also King Sumitra. Similarly, he conquered the western Matsyas and the Paṭaccarās.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 4. He brought Sukumara under his control and also King Sumitra. Similarly, he conquered the western Matsyas and the Paṭaccarās.

sanskrit: निषादभूमिं गोशृङ्गं पर्वतप्रवरं तथा ।तरसा व्यजयद्धीमाञ्श्रेणिमन्तं च पार्थिवम् ॥५॥
transliteration: niṣādabhūmiṁ gośṛṅgaṁ parvatapravaraṁ tathā ,tarasā vyajayaddhīmāñśreṇimantaṁ ca pārthivam.
english: The intelligent one conquered the Niṣāda territory, Gośṛṅga, and the excellent mountain range. Similarly, he conquered King Śreṇimat with force.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 5. The intelligent one conquered the Niṣāda territory, Gośṛṅga, and the excellent mountain range. Similarly, he conquered King Śreṇimat with force.

sanskrit: नवराष्ट्रं विनिर्जित्य कुन्तिभोजमुपाद्रवत् ।प्रीतिपूर्वं च तस्यासौ प्रतिजग्राह शासनम् ॥६॥
transliteration: navarāṣṭraṁ vinirjitya kuntibhojamupādravat ,prītipūrvaṁ ca tasyāsau pratijagrāha śāsanam.
english: Having completely conquered Navarāṣṭra, he then approached Kuntibhoja. He amicably accepted Kuntibhoja's instruction (or sovereignty).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 6. Having completely conquered Navarāṣṭra, he then approached Kuntibhoja. He amicably accepted Kuntibhoja's instruction (or sovereignty).

sanskrit: ततश्चर्मण्वतीकूले जम्भकस्यात्मजं नृपम् ।ददर्श वासुदेवेन शेषितं पूर्ववैरिणा ॥७॥
transliteration: tataścarmaṇvatīkūle jambhakasyātmajaṁ nṛpam ,dadarśa vāsudevena śeṣitaṁ pūrvavairiṇā.
english: Then, on the bank of the Carmaṇvatī river, he saw the son of Jambhaka, a king who had been spared by his former enemy, Vāsudeva (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 7. Then, on the bank of the Carmaṇvatī river, he saw the son of Jambhaka, a king who had been spared by his former enemy, Vāsudeva (Krishna).

sanskrit: चक्रे तत्र स संग्रामं सह भोजेन भारत ।स तमाजौ विनिर्जित्य दक्षिणाभिमुखो ययौ ॥८॥
transliteration: cakre tatra sa saṁgrāmaṁ saha bhojena bhārata ,sa tamājau vinirjitya dakṣiṇābhimukho yayau.
english: O Bhārata, he then waged a battle there with Bhoja. Having completely defeated him (the son of Jambhaka) in that fight, he proceeded southwards.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 8. O Bhārata, he then waged a battle there with Bhoja. Having completely defeated him (the son of Jambhaka) in that fight, he proceeded southwards.

sanskrit: करांस्तेभ्य उपादाय रत्नानि विविधानि च ।ततस्तैरेव सहितो नर्मदामभितो ययौ ॥९॥
transliteration: karāṁstebhya upādāya ratnāni vividhāni ca ,tatastaireva sahito narmadāmabhito yayau.
english: Having collected various taxes and jewels from them, he then, accompanied by those very (allies), proceeded towards the Narmadā river.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 9. Having collected various taxes and jewels from them, he then, accompanied by those very (allies), proceeded towards the Narmadā river.

sanskrit: विन्दानुविन्दावावन्त्यौ सैन्येन महता वृतौ ।जिगाय समरे वीरावाश्विनेयः प्रतापवान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: vindānuvindāvāvantyau sainyena mahatā vṛtau ,jigāya samare vīrāvāśvineyaḥ pratāpavān.
english: The mighty Āśvineya (Puruṣottama) conquered in battle the two heroic kings, Vinda and Anuvinda of Avanti, who were surrounded by a great army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 10. The mighty Āśvineya (Puruṣottama) conquered in battle the two heroic kings, Vinda and Anuvinda of Avanti, who were surrounded by a great army.

sanskrit: ततो रत्नान्युपादाय पुरीं माहिष्मतीं ययौ ।तत्र नीलेन राज्ञा स चक्रे युद्धं नरर्षभः ॥११॥
transliteration: tato ratnānyupādāya purīṁ māhiṣmatīṁ yayau ,tatra nīlena rājñā sa cakre yuddhaṁ nararṣabhaḥ.
english: Then, having collected the treasures, that best among men (nararṣabha) went to the city of Mahiṣmatī. There, he waged war with King Nīla.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 11. Then, having collected the treasures, that best among men (nararṣabha) went to the city of Mahiṣmatī. There, he waged war with King Nīla.

sanskrit: पाण्डवः परवीरघ्नः सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।ततोऽस्य सुमहद्युद्धमासीद्भीरुभयंकरम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavaḥ paravīraghnaḥ sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,tato'sya sumahadyuddhamāsīdbhīrubhayaṁkaram.
english: Then, that valiant Pāṇḍava, Sahadeva, the slayer of enemy heroes, had a very great battle which was terrifying to the timid.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 12. Then, that valiant Pāṇḍava, Sahadeva, the slayer of enemy heroes, had a very great battle which was terrifying to the timid.

sanskrit: सैन्यक्षयकरं चैव प्राणानां संशयाय च ।चक्रे तस्य हि साहाय्यं भगवान्हव्यवाहनः ॥१३॥
transliteration: sainyakṣayakaraṁ caiva prāṇānāṁ saṁśayāya ca ,cakre tasya hi sāhāyyaṁ bhagavānhavyavāhanaḥ.
english: Indeed, that battle caused the destruction of armies and endangered lives. The divine carrier of oblations (havyavāhana), Agni, surely gave him (Sahadeva) assistance.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 13. Indeed, that battle caused the destruction of armies and endangered lives. The divine carrier of oblations (havyavāhana), Agni, surely gave him (Sahadeva) assistance.

sanskrit: ततो हया रथा नागाः पुरुषाः कवचानि च ।प्रदीप्तानि व्यदृश्यन्त सहदेवबले तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tato hayā rathā nāgāḥ puruṣāḥ kavacāni ca ,pradīptāni vyadṛśyanta sahadevabale tadā.
english: Then, at that time, horses, chariots, elephants, soldiers, and armors were seen blazing brightly in Sahadeva's army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 14. Then, at that time, horses, chariots, elephants, soldiers, and armors were seen blazing brightly in Sahadeva's army.

sanskrit: ततः सुसंभ्रान्तमना बभूव कुरुनन्दनः ।नोत्तरं प्रतिवक्तुं च शक्तोऽभूज्जनमेजय ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ susaṁbhrāntamanā babhūva kurunandanaḥ ,nottaraṁ prativaktuṁ ca śakto'bhūjjanamejaya.
english: Then the Kuru prince became greatly agitated in mind. And (O Janamejaya), he was unable to offer a response.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 15. Then the Kuru prince became greatly agitated in mind. And (O Janamejaya), he was unable to offer a response.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।किमर्थं भगवानग्निः प्रत्यमित्रोऽभवद्युधि ।सहदेवस्य यज्ञार्थं घटमानस्य वै द्विज ॥१६॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kimarthaṁ bhagavānagniḥ pratyamitro'bhavadyudhi ,sahadevasya yajñārthaṁ ghaṭamānasya vai dvija.
english: Janamejaya spoke: 'O brahmin (dvija), for what reason did the revered Agni become an adversary in battle when Sahadeva was striving for the Vedic ritual (yajña)?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 16. Janamejaya spoke: 'O brahmin (dvija), for what reason did the revered Agni become an adversary in battle when Sahadeva was striving for the Vedic ritual (yajña)?'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तत्र माहिष्मतीवासी भगवान्हव्यवाहनः ।श्रूयते निगृहीतो वै पुरस्तात्पारदारिकः ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tatra māhiṣmatīvāsī bhagavānhavyavāhanaḥ ,śrūyate nigṛhīto vai purastātpāradārikaḥ.
english: Vaishampayana replied: 'It is said that the divine Agni, the carrier of oblations, who resided in Māhiṣmatī, was indeed formerly apprehended as an adulterer.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 17. Vaishampayana replied: 'It is said that the divine Agni, the carrier of oblations, who resided in Māhiṣmatī, was indeed formerly apprehended as an adulterer.'

sanskrit: नीलस्य राज्ञः पूर्वेषामुपनीतश्च सोऽभवत् ।तदा ब्राह्मणरूपेण चरमाणो यदृच्छया ॥१८॥
transliteration: nīlasya rājñaḥ pūrveṣāmupanītaśca so'bhavat ,tadā brāhmaṇarūpeṇa caramāṇo yadṛcchayā.
english: And he (Agni) was then brought before the ancestors of King Nīla. At that time, he was wandering about freely in the form of a brahmin.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 18. And he (Agni) was then brought before the ancestors of King Nīla. At that time, he was wandering about freely in the form of a brahmin.

sanskrit: तं तु राजा यथाशास्त्रमन्वशाद्धार्मिकस्तदा ।प्रजज्वाल ततः कोपाद्भगवान्हव्यवाहनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: taṁ tu rājā yathāśāstramanvaśāddhārmikastadā ,prajajvāla tataḥ kopādbhagavānhavyavāhanaḥ.
english: Then, the righteous king instructed him according to the scriptures. Thereupon, the revered fire god blazed forth in anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 19. Then, the righteous king instructed him according to the scriptures. Thereupon, the revered fire god blazed forth in anger.

sanskrit: तं दृष्ट्वा विस्मितो राजा जगाम शिरसा कविम् ।चक्रे प्रसादं च तदा तस्य राज्ञो विभावसुः ॥२०॥
transliteration: taṁ dṛṣṭvā vismito rājā jagāma śirasā kavim ,cakre prasādaṁ ca tadā tasya rājño vibhāvasuḥ.
english: Having seen him, the astonished king approached the seer with a bowed head. Then, Vibhāvasu (the fire god) bestowed his grace upon that king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 20. Having seen him, the astonished king approached the seer with a bowed head. Then, Vibhāvasu (the fire god) bestowed his grace upon that king.

sanskrit: वरेण छन्दयामास तं नृपं स्विष्टकृत्तमः ।अभयं च स जग्राह स्वसैन्ये वै महीपतिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: vareṇa chandayāmāsa taṁ nṛpaṁ sviṣṭakṛttamaḥ ,abhayaṁ ca sa jagrāha svasainye vai mahīpatiḥ.
english: The supremely auspicious performer of sacrifices (sviṣṭakṛttama) granted a boon to that king. And indeed, the king (mahipati) chose freedom from fear (abhaya) for his own army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 21. The supremely auspicious performer of sacrifices (sviṣṭakṛttama) granted a boon to that king. And indeed, the king (mahipati) chose freedom from fear (abhaya) for his own army.

sanskrit: ततः प्रभृति ये केचिदज्ञानात्तां पुरीं नृपाः ।जिगीषन्ति बलाद्राजंस्ते दह्यन्तीह वह्निना ॥२२॥
transliteration: tataḥ prabhṛti ye kecidajñānāttāṁ purīṁ nṛpāḥ ,jigīṣanti balādrājaṁste dahyantīha vahninā.
english: From that time onwards, O King, any rulers (nṛpa) who, out of ignorance, desire to conquer that city by force, are consumed here by fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 22. From that time onwards, O King, any rulers (nṛpa) who, out of ignorance, desire to conquer that city by force, are consumed here by fire.

sanskrit: तस्यां पुर्यां तदा चैव माहिष्मत्यां कुरूद्वह ।बभूवुरनभिग्राह्या योषितश्छन्दतः किल ॥२३॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ puryāṁ tadā caiva māhiṣmatyāṁ kurūdvaha ,babhūvuranabhigrāhyā yoṣitaśchandataḥ kila.
english: O descendant of Kuru, it is said that in that city of Māhiṣmatī, at that time, women were uncontrollable and acted according to their own will.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 23. O descendant of Kuru, it is said that in that city of Māhiṣmatī, at that time, women were uncontrollable and acted according to their own will.

sanskrit: एवमग्निर्वरं प्रादात्स्त्रीणामप्रतिवारणे ।स्वैरिण्यस्तत्र नार्यो हि यथेष्टं प्रचरन्त्युत ॥२४॥
transliteration: evamagnirvaraṁ prādātstrīṇāmaprativāraṇe ,svairiṇyastatra nāryo hi yatheṣṭaṁ pracarantyuta.
english: Thus, Agni granted a boon to women regarding their unhindered movement. Indeed, there, women, being independent, move about as they please.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 24. Thus, Agni granted a boon to women regarding their unhindered movement. Indeed, there, women, being independent, move about as they please.

sanskrit: वर्जयन्ति च राजानस्तद्राष्ट्रं पुरुषोत्तम ।भयादग्नेर्महाराज तदा प्रभृति सर्वदा ॥२५॥
transliteration: varjayanti ca rājānastadrāṣṭraṁ puruṣottama ,bhayādagnermahārāja tadā prabhṛti sarvadā.
english: O best of men (puruṣa), O great king, from that time onwards, kings have always avoided that kingdom out of fear of Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 25. O best of men (puruṣa), O great king, from that time onwards, kings have always avoided that kingdom out of fear of Agni.

sanskrit: सहदेवस्तु धर्मात्मा सैन्यं दृष्ट्वा भयार्दितम् ।परीतमग्निना राजन्नाकम्पत यथा गिरिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: sahadevastu dharmātmā sainyaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhayārditam ,parītamagninā rājannākampata yathā giriḥ.
english: However, the righteous (dharma) Sahadeva, O king, seeing his army distressed by fear and enveloped by fire, did not tremble, but stood firm like a mountain.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 26. However, the righteous (dharma) Sahadeva, O king, seeing his army distressed by fear and enveloped by fire, did not tremble, but stood firm like a mountain.

sanskrit: उपस्पृश्य शुचिर्भूत्वा सोऽब्रवीत्पावकं ततः ।त्वदर्थोऽयं समारम्भः कृष्णवर्त्मन्नमोऽस्तु ते ॥२७॥
transliteration: upaspṛśya śucirbhūtvā so'bravītpāvakaṁ tataḥ ,tvadartho'yaṁ samārambhaḥ kṛṣṇavartmannamo'stu te.
english: Having purified himself with a ritual bath, he then spoke to Agni: 'O Agni (kr̥ṣṇavartman), this undertaking is for your sake. Salutations to you!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 27. Having purified himself with a ritual bath, he then spoke to Agni: 'O Agni (kr̥ṣṇavartman), this undertaking is for your sake. Salutations to you!'

sanskrit: मुखं त्वमसि देवानां यज्ञस्त्वमसि पावक ।पावनात्पावकश्चासि वहनाद्धव्यवाहनः ॥२८॥
transliteration: mukhaṁ tvamasi devānāṁ yajñastvamasi pāvaka ,pāvanātpāvakaścāsi vahanāddhavyavāhanaḥ.
english: You are the mouth of the gods, you are the Vedic ritual (yajña), O purifier (pāvaka)! Because of your purifying nature, you are called Pāvaka, and because you bear oblations, you are Havyavāhana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 28. You are the mouth of the gods, you are the Vedic ritual (yajña), O purifier (pāvaka)! Because of your purifying nature, you are called Pāvaka, and because you bear oblations, you are Havyavāhana.

sanskrit: वेदास्त्वदर्थं जाताश्च जातवेदास्ततो ह्यसि ।यज्ञविघ्नमिमं कर्तुं नार्हस्त्वं हव्यवाहन ॥२९॥
transliteration: vedāstvadarthaṁ jātāśca jātavedāstato hyasi ,yajñavighnamimaṁ kartuṁ nārhastvaṁ havyavāhana.
english: O Havyavāhana (Fire-god), the Vedas came into existence for your sake; hence, you are indeed known as Jātavedas (knower of all born beings). You ought not to obstruct this Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 29. O Havyavāhana (Fire-god), the Vedas came into existence for your sake; hence, you are indeed known as Jātavedas (knower of all born beings). You ought not to obstruct this Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा तु माद्रेयः कुशैरास्तीर्य मेदिनीम् ।विधिवत्पुरुषव्याघ्रः पावकं प्रत्युपाविशत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: evamuktvā tu mādreyaḥ kuśairāstīrya medinīm ,vidhivatpuruṣavyāghraḥ pāvakaṁ pratyupāviśat.
english: Having spoken thus, the son of Madri (mādreya), a tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), spread Kuśa grass on the ground and, following the prescribed rites, sat down facing Agni.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 30. Having spoken thus, the son of Madri (mādreya), a tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), spread Kuśa grass on the ground and, following the prescribed rites, sat down facing Agni.

sanskrit: प्रमुखे सर्वसैन्यस्य भीतोद्विग्नस्य भारत ।न चैनमत्यगाद्वह्निर्वेलामिव महोदधिः ॥३१॥
transliteration: pramukhe sarvasainyasya bhītodvignasya bhārata ,na cainamatyagādvahnirvelāmiva mahodadhiḥ.
english: O Bhārata, at the forefront of the entire frightened and agitated army, the fire (Agni) could not surpass him, just as a great ocean does not transgress its shore.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 31. O Bhārata, at the forefront of the entire frightened and agitated army, the fire (Agni) could not surpass him, just as a great ocean does not transgress its shore.

sanskrit: तमभ्येत्य शनैर्वह्निरुवाच कुरुनन्दनम् ।सहदेवं नृणां देवं सान्त्वपूर्वमिदं वचः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tamabhyetya śanairvahniruvāca kurunandanam ,sahadevaṁ nṛṇāṁ devaṁ sāntvapūrvamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Approaching him slowly, the fire (Agni) addressed Sahadeva, the delight of the Kurus and a god among men, with these consoling words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 32. Approaching him slowly, the fire (Agni) addressed Sahadeva, the delight of the Kurus and a god among men, with these consoling words.

sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठोत्तिष्ठ कौरव्य जिज्ञासेयं कृता मया ।वेद्मि सर्वमभिप्रायं तव धर्मसुतस्य च ॥३३॥
transliteration: uttiṣṭhottiṣṭha kauravya jijñāseyaṁ kṛtā mayā ,vedmi sarvamabhiprāyaṁ tava dharmasutasya ca.
english: Arise, arise, O Kauravya! This was merely an inquiry conducted by me. I know your entire intention and that of the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 33. Arise, arise, O Kauravya! This was merely an inquiry conducted by me. I know your entire intention and that of the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: मया तु रक्षितव्येयं पुरी भरतसत्तम ।यावद्राज्ञोऽस्य नीलस्य कुलवंशधरा इति ।ईप्सितं तु करिष्यामि मनसस्तव पाण्डव ॥३४॥
transliteration: mayā tu rakṣitavyeyaṁ purī bharatasattama ,yāvadrājño'sya nīlasya kulavaṁśadharā iti ,īpsitaṁ tu kariṣyāmi manasastava pāṇḍava.
english: Indeed, this city must be protected by me, O best of Bharatas, as long as there are those who uphold the family and lineage of this King Nīla. Moreover, I will fulfill the desire of your heart, O Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 34. Indeed, this city must be protected by me, O best of Bharatas, as long as there are those who uphold the family and lineage of this King Nīla. Moreover, I will fulfill the desire of your heart, O Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: तत उत्थाय हृष्टात्मा प्राञ्जलिः शिरसानतः ।पूजयामास माद्रेयः पावकं पुरुषर्षभः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tata utthāya hṛṣṭātmā prāñjaliḥ śirasānataḥ ,pūjayāmāsa mādreyaḥ pāvakaṁ puruṣarṣabhaḥ.
english: Then, with a joyful spirit (ātman), the son of Madri (Sahadeva), who was the best among men (puruṣarṣabha), rose up, bowed his head with folded hands, and worshipped the fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 35. Then, with a joyful spirit (ātman), the son of Madri (Sahadeva), who was the best among men (puruṣarṣabha), rose up, bowed his head with folded hands, and worshipped the fire.

sanskrit: पावके विनिवृत्ते तु नीलो राजाभ्ययात्तदा ।सत्कारेण नरव्याघ्रं सहदेवं युधां पतिम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: pāvake vinivṛtte tu nīlo rājābhyayāttadā ,satkāreṇa naravyāghraṁ sahadevaṁ yudhāṁ patim.
english: But then, when the fire (pāvaka) ritual had ceased, King Nīla respectfully approached Sahadeva, that tiger among men (naravyāghra) and lord of battles.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 36. But then, when the fire (pāvaka) ritual had ceased, King Nīla respectfully approached Sahadeva, that tiger among men (naravyāghra) and lord of battles.

sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य च तां पूजां करे च विनिवेश्य तम् ।माद्रीसुतस्ततः प्रायाद्विजयी दक्षिणां दिशम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: pratigṛhya ca tāṁ pūjāṁ kare ca viniveśya tam ,mādrīsutastataḥ prāyādvijayī dakṣiṇāṁ diśam.
english: And having accepted that worship (pūjā) and having established him (King Nīla's allegiance) under his control, the victorious son of Madri (Sahadeva) then departed towards the southern direction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 37. And having accepted that worship (pūjā) and having established him (King Nīla's allegiance) under his control, the victorious son of Madri (Sahadeva) then departed towards the southern direction.

sanskrit: त्रैपुरं स वशे कृत्वा राजानममितौजसम् ।निजग्राह महाबाहुस्तरसा पोतनेश्वरम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: traipuraṁ sa vaśe kṛtvā rājānamamitaujasam ,nijagrāha mahābāhustarasā potaneśvaram.
english: That mighty-armed one (mahābāhu) – Sahadeva – having brought the king of Tripura, who possessed immeasurable valor, under his control, then forcefully subdued the lord of Potana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 38. That mighty-armed one (mahābāhu) – Sahadeva – having brought the king of Tripura, who possessed immeasurable valor, under his control, then forcefully subdued the lord of Potana.

sanskrit: आहृतिं कौशिकाचार्यं यत्नेन महता ततः ।वशे चक्रे महाबाहुः सुराष्ट्राधिपतिं तथा ॥३९॥
transliteration: āhṛtiṁ kauśikācāryaṁ yatnena mahatā tataḥ ,vaśe cakre mahābāhuḥ surāṣṭrādhipatiṁ tathā.
english: Thereafter, the mighty-armed one, with great effort, brought Āhṛti and Kauśikācārya, and also the ruler of Surāṣṭra, under his control.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 39. Thereafter, the mighty-armed one, with great effort, brought Āhṛti and Kauśikācārya, and also the ruler of Surāṣṭra, under his control.

sanskrit: सुराष्ट्रविषयस्थश्च प्रेषयामास रुक्मिणे ।राज्ञे भोजकटस्थाय महामात्राय धीमते ॥४०॥
transliteration: surāṣṭraviṣayasthaśca preṣayāmāsa rukmiṇe ,rājñe bhojakaṭasthāya mahāmātrāya dhīmate.
english: And while he was in the Surāṣṭra region, he sent (a message) to Rukmin, the intelligent king (rājan) residing in Bhojakaṭa, a great dignitary (mahāmātra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 40. And while he was in the Surāṣṭra region, he sent (a message) to Rukmin, the intelligent king (rājan) residing in Bhojakaṭa, a great dignitary (mahāmātra).

sanskrit: भीष्मकाय स धर्मात्मा साक्षादिन्द्रसखाय वै ।स चास्य ससुतो राजन्प्रतिजग्राह शासनम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: bhīṣmakāya sa dharmātmā sākṣādindrasakhāya vai ,sa cāsya sasuto rājanpratijagrāha śāsanam.
english: That righteous one (dharmātmā) (sent it) to Bhīṣmaka, who was indeed directly a friend of Indra. And he (Bhīṣmaka), O king (rājan), along with his son, accepted his (the sender's) command (śāsanam).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 41. That righteous one (dharmātmā) (sent it) to Bhīṣmaka, who was indeed directly a friend of Indra. And he (Bhīṣmaka), O king (rājan), along with his son, accepted his (the sender's) command (śāsanam).

sanskrit: प्रीतिपूर्वं महाबाहुर्वासुदेवमवेक्ष्य च ।ततः स रत्नान्यादाय पुनः प्रायाद्युधां पतिः ॥४२॥
transliteration: prītipūrvaṁ mahābāhurvāsudevamavekṣya ca ,tataḥ sa ratnānyādāya punaḥ prāyādyudhāṁ patiḥ.
english: And with great delight, the mighty-armed one (Bhīṣmaka), having seen Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa); then he (Kṛṣṇa), the lord of battles, having received the jewels, departed again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 42. And with great delight, the mighty-armed one (Bhīṣmaka), having seen Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa); then he (Kṛṣṇa), the lord of battles, having received the jewels, departed again.

sanskrit: ततः शूर्पारकं चैव गणं चोपकृताह्वयम् ।वशे चक्रे महातेजा दण्डकांश्च महाबलः ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śūrpārakaṁ caiva gaṇaṁ copakṛtāhvayam ,vaśe cakre mahātejā daṇḍakāṁśca mahābalaḥ.
english: Then, the greatly energetic and mighty one subjugated both Shurparaka and the group known as Upakrita, along with the Dandaka region.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 43. Then, the greatly energetic and mighty one subjugated both Shurparaka and the group known as Upakrita, along with the Dandaka region.

sanskrit: सागरद्वीपवासांश्च नृपतीन्म्लेच्छयोनिजान् ।निषादान्पुरुषादांश्च कर्णप्रावरणानपि ॥४४॥
transliteration: sāgaradvīpavāsāṁśca nṛpatīnmlecchayonijān ,niṣādānpuruṣādāṁśca karṇaprāvaraṇānapi.
english: And he subjugated the kings residing on ocean islands, those of barbarian (mleccha) origin, the Niṣādas, the man-eaters, and also those whose ears serve as coverings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 44. And he subjugated the kings residing on ocean islands, those of barbarian (mleccha) origin, the Niṣādas, the man-eaters, and also those whose ears serve as coverings.

sanskrit: ये च कालमुखा नाम नरा राक्षसयोनयः ।कृत्स्नं कोल्लगिरिं चैव मुरचीपत्तनं तथा ॥४५॥
transliteration: ye ca kālamukhā nāma narā rākṣasayonayaḥ ,kṛtsnaṁ kollagiriṁ caiva muracīpattanaṁ tathā.
english: And he subjugated those people known as Kālamukhas, who were of demon origin, along with the entire Kollagiri mountain and the city of Murachipattana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 45. And he subjugated those people known as Kālamukhas, who were of demon origin, along with the entire Kollagiri mountain and the city of Murachipattana.

sanskrit: द्वीपं ताम्राह्वयं चैव पर्वतं रामकं तथा ।तिमिंगिलं च नृपतिं वशे चक्रे महामतिः ॥४६॥
transliteration: dvīpaṁ tāmrāhvayaṁ caiva parvataṁ rāmakaṁ tathā ,timiṁgilaṁ ca nṛpatiṁ vaśe cakre mahāmatiḥ.
english: And the greatly intelligent one subjugated the island named Tamra, and likewise the mountain Ramaka, as well as King Timingila.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 46. And the greatly intelligent one subjugated the island named Tamra, and likewise the mountain Ramaka, as well as King Timingila.

sanskrit: एकपादांश्च पुरुषान्केवलान्वनवासिनः ।नगरीं संजयन्तीं च पिच्छण्डं करहाटकम् ।दूतैरेव वशे चक्रे करं चैनानदापयत् ॥४७॥
transliteration: ekapādāṁśca puruṣānkevalānvanavāsinaḥ ,nagarīṁ saṁjayantīṁ ca picchaṇḍaṁ karahāṭakam ,dūtaireva vaśe cakre karaṁ cainānadāpayat.
english: He brought under his control, solely through messengers, the one-footed men, the isolated forest-dwellers, and the city of Saṃjayantī, as well as the regions of Picchaṇḍa and Karahāṭaka. He also made them pay tribute.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 47. He brought under his control, solely through messengers, the one-footed men, the isolated forest-dwellers, and the city of Saṃjayantī, as well as the regions of Picchaṇḍa and Karahāṭaka. He also made them pay tribute.

sanskrit: पाण्ड्यांश्च द्रविडांश्चैव सहितांश्चोड्रकेरलैः ।अन्ध्रांस्तलवनांश्चैव कलिङ्गानोष्ट्रकर्णिकान् ॥४८॥
transliteration: pāṇḍyāṁśca draviḍāṁścaiva sahitāṁścoḍrakeralaiḥ ,andhrāṁstalavanāṁścaiva kaliṅgānoṣṭrakarṇikān.
english: He also brought under control the Pāṇḍyas, the Draviḍas, along with the Uḍras and Keralas, as well as the Andhras, the Talavanas, the Kaliṅgas, and the Oṣṭrakarṇikas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 48. He also brought under control the Pāṇḍyas, the Draviḍas, along with the Uḍras and Keralas, as well as the Andhras, the Talavanas, the Kaliṅgas, and the Oṣṭrakarṇikas.

sanskrit: अन्ताखीं चैव रोमां च यवनानां पुरं तथा ।दूतैरेव वशे चक्रे करं चैनानदापयत् ॥४९॥
transliteration: antākhīṁ caiva romāṁ ca yavanānāṁ puraṁ tathā ,dūtaireva vaśe cakre karaṁ cainānadāpayat.
english: And he brought under his control, solely through messengers, Antākhī and Romā, and also the city of the Yavanas (Greeks). He further made them pay tribute.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 49. And he brought under his control, solely through messengers, Antākhī and Romā, and also the city of the Yavanas (Greeks). He further made them pay tribute.

sanskrit: भरुकच्छं गतो धीमान्दूतान्माद्रवतीसुतः ।प्रेषयामास राजेन्द्र पौलस्त्याय महात्मने ।विभीषणाय धर्मात्मा प्रीतिपूर्वमरिंदमः ॥५०॥
transliteration: bharukacchaṁ gato dhīmāndūtānmādravatīsutaḥ ,preṣayāmāsa rājendra paulastyāya mahātmane ,vibhīṣaṇāya dharmātmā prītipūrvamariṁdamaḥ.
english: O King of kings, the wise son of Mādravatī (Nakula), who was righteous (dharmātman) and a subduer of enemies, having arrived at Bharukaccha, sent messengers with affection to the great-souled Vibhīṣaṇa, the son of Pulastya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 50. O King of kings, the wise son of Mādravatī (Nakula), who was righteous (dharmātman) and a subduer of enemies, having arrived at Bharukaccha, sent messengers with affection to the great-souled Vibhīṣaṇa, the son of Pulastya.

sanskrit: स चास्य प्रतिजग्राह शासनं प्रीतिपूर्वकम् ।तच्च कालकृतं धीमानन्वमन्यत स प्रभुः ॥५१॥
transliteration: sa cāsya pratijagrāha śāsanaṁ prītipūrvakam ,tacca kālakṛtaṁ dhīmānanvamanyata sa prabhuḥ.
english: And he accepted his command with great pleasure. The intelligent lord, moreover, agreed that it was decreed by time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 51. And he accepted his command with great pleasure. The intelligent lord, moreover, agreed that it was decreed by time.

sanskrit: ततः संप्रेषयामास रत्नानि विविधानि च ।चन्दनागुरुमुख्यानि दिव्यान्याभरणानि च ॥५२॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁpreṣayāmāsa ratnāni vividhāni ca ,candanāgurumukhyāni divyānyābharaṇāni ca.
english: Then he dispatched various jewels, divine ornaments, and precious items like sandalwood and aguru.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 52. Then he dispatched various jewels, divine ornaments, and precious items like sandalwood and aguru.

sanskrit: वासांसि च महार्हाणि मणींश्चैव महाधनान् ।न्यवर्तत ततो धीमान्सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ॥५३॥
transliteration: vāsāṁsi ca mahārhāṇi maṇīṁścaiva mahādhanān ,nyavartata tato dhīmānsahadevaḥ pratāpavān.
english: And costly garments and exceedingly precious gems [were also sent]. Then, the intelligent and mighty Sahadeva returned.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 53. And costly garments and exceedingly precious gems [were also sent]. Then, the intelligent and mighty Sahadeva returned.

sanskrit: एवं निर्जित्य तरसा सान्त्वेन विजयेन च ।करदान्पार्थिवान्कृत्वा प्रत्यागच्छदरिंदमः ॥५४॥
transliteration: evaṁ nirjitya tarasā sāntvena vijayena ca ,karadānpārthivānkṛtvā pratyāgacchadariṁdamaḥ.
english: In this manner, having conquered kings through force, conciliation, and victory, and having made them tribute-paying, the vanquisher of foes returned.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 54. In this manner, having conquered kings through force, conciliation, and victory, and having made them tribute-paying, the vanquisher of foes returned.

sanskrit: धर्मराजाय तत्सर्वं निवेद्य भरतर्षभ ।कृतकर्मा सुखं राजन्नुवास जनमेजय ॥५५॥
transliteration: dharmarājāya tatsarvaṁ nivedya bharatarṣabha ,kṛtakarmā sukhaṁ rājannuvāsa janamejaya.
english: O best of Bharatas, O King Janamejaya, having reported all that to King Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmaraja), he (Nakula), having accomplished his task (karma), lived happily.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 28 Verse 55. O best of Bharatas, O King Janamejaya, having reported all that to King Yudhiṣṭhira (Dharmaraja), he (Nakula), having accomplished his task (karma), lived happily.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।नकुलस्य तु वक्ष्यामि कर्माणि विजयं तथा ।वासुदेवजितामाशां यथासौ व्यजयत्प्रभुः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,nakulasya tu vakṣyāmi karmāṇi vijayaṁ tathā ,vāsudevajitāmāśāṁ yathāsau vyajayatprabhuḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Now, I shall indeed describe the deeds (karma) and victories of Nakula, and how that mighty one (Nakula) conquered the direction that had already been conquered by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Now, I shall indeed describe the deeds (karma) and victories of Nakula, and how that mighty one (Nakula) conquered the direction that had already been conquered by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: निर्याय खाण्डवप्रस्थात्प्रतीचीमभितो दिशम् ।उद्दिश्य मतिमान्प्रायान्महत्या सेनया सह ॥२॥
transliteration: niryāya khāṇḍavaprasthātpratīcīmabhito diśam ,uddiśya matimānprāyānmahatyā senayā saha.
english: Having departed from Khāṇḍavaprastha, that intelligent one (Nakula) proceeded towards the western direction, accompanied by a great army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 2. Having departed from Khāṇḍavaprastha, that intelligent one (Nakula) proceeded towards the western direction, accompanied by a great army.

sanskrit: सिंहनादेन महता योधानां गर्जितेन च ।रथनेमिनिनादैश्च कम्पयन्वसुधामिमाम् ॥३॥
transliteration: siṁhanādena mahatā yodhānāṁ garjitena ca ,rathanemininādaiśca kampayanvasudhāmimām.
english: Causing this earth to tremble with the great lion-like roars and shouts of the warriors, and with the rumbling sounds of chariot wheels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 3. Causing this earth to tremble with the great lion-like roars and shouts of the warriors, and with the rumbling sounds of chariot wheels.

sanskrit: ततो बहुधनं रम्यं गवाश्वधनधान्यवत् ।कार्त्तिकेयस्य दयितं रोहीतकमुपाद्रवत् ॥४॥
transliteration: tato bahudhanaṁ ramyaṁ gavāśvadhanadhānyavat ,kārttikeyasya dayitaṁ rohītakamupādravat.
english: Thereafter, he proceeded towards Rohītaka, which was rich in much wealth, charming, abundant in cattle, horses, riches, and grain, and beloved by Kārttikeya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 4. Thereafter, he proceeded towards Rohītaka, which was rich in much wealth, charming, abundant in cattle, horses, riches, and grain, and beloved by Kārttikeya.

sanskrit: तत्र युद्धं महद्वृत्तं शूरैर्मत्तमयूरकैः ।मरुभूमिं च कार्त्स्न्येन तथैव बहुधान्यकम् ॥५॥
transliteration: tatra yuddhaṁ mahadvṛttaṁ śūrairmattamayūrakaiḥ ,marubhūmiṁ ca kārtsnyena tathaiva bahudhānyakam.
english: There, a great battle took place with the heroic Mattamayūrakas. And similarly, he completely subjugated the Marubhūmi (desert region) and Bahudhānyaka.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 5. There, a great battle took place with the heroic Mattamayūrakas. And similarly, he completely subjugated the Marubhūmi (desert region) and Bahudhānyaka.

sanskrit: शैरीषकं महेच्छं च वशे चक्रे महाद्युतिः ।शिबींस्त्रिगर्तानम्बष्ठान्मालवान्पञ्चकर्पटान् ॥६॥
transliteration: śairīṣakaṁ mahecchaṁ ca vaśe cakre mahādyutiḥ ,śibīṁstrigartānambaṣṭhānmālavānpañcakarpaṭān.
english: The greatly glorious one brought Śairīṣaka and Maheccha under his control. He also subjugated the Śibis, the Trigartas, the Ambaṣṭhas, the Mālavās, and the Pañcakarpṭas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 6. The greatly glorious one brought Śairīṣaka and Maheccha under his control. He also subjugated the Śibis, the Trigartas, the Ambaṣṭhas, the Mālavās, and the Pañcakarpṭas.

sanskrit: तथा मध्यमिकायांश्च वाटधानान्द्विजानथ ।पुनश्च परिवृत्याथ पुष्करारण्यवासिनः ॥७॥
transliteration: tathā madhyamikāyāṁśca vāṭadhānāndvijānatha ,punaśca parivṛtyātha puṣkarāraṇyavāsinaḥ.
english: Similarly, he also brought the Madhyamikās and the Vāṭadhānas under control. Then, he subjugated the twice-born (dvijāḥ) people, and again, having turned back, he conquered the inhabitants of the Puṣkara forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 7. Similarly, he also brought the Madhyamikās and the Vāṭadhānas under control. Then, he subjugated the twice-born (dvijāḥ) people, and again, having turned back, he conquered the inhabitants of the Puṣkara forest.

sanskrit: गणानुत्सवसंकेतान्व्यजयत्पुरुषर्षभः ।सिन्धुकूलाश्रिता ये च ग्रामणेया महाबलाः ॥८॥
transliteration: gaṇānutsavasaṁketānvyajayatpuruṣarṣabhaḥ ,sindhukūlāśritā ye ca grāmaṇeyā mahābalāḥ.
english: The best of men conquered the tribal groups that celebrated their agreements with festivals, as well as those extremely powerful village chieftains who resided along the banks of the Sindhu (river).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 8. The best of men conquered the tribal groups that celebrated their agreements with festivals, as well as those extremely powerful village chieftains who resided along the banks of the Sindhu (river).

sanskrit: शूद्राभीरगणाश्चैव ये चाश्रित्य सरस्वतीम् ।वर्तयन्ति च ये मत्स्यैर्ये च पर्वतवासिनः ॥९॥
transliteration: śūdrābhīragaṇāścaiva ye cāśritya sarasvatīm ,vartayanti ca ye matsyairye ca parvatavāsinaḥ.
english: And also the tribal groups of Śūdras and Ābhīras who dwelled along the Sarasvatī (river), as well as those who subsisted on fish, and those who lived in the mountains.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 9. And also the tribal groups of Śūdras and Ābhīras who dwelled along the Sarasvatī (river), as well as those who subsisted on fish, and those who lived in the mountains.

sanskrit: कृत्स्नं पञ्चनदं चैव तथैवापरपर्यटम् ।उत्तरज्योतिकं चैव तथा वृन्दाटकं पुरम् ।द्वारपालं च तरसा वशे चक्रे महाद्युतिः ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛtsnaṁ pañcanadaṁ caiva tathaivāparaparyaṭam ,uttarajyotikaṁ caiva tathā vṛndāṭakaṁ puram ,dvārapālaṁ ca tarasā vaśe cakre mahādyutiḥ.
english: And the extremely glorious one swiftly brought under his control the entire region of Pañcanada, as well as Aparaparyaṭa, and also Uttarajyotika, and similarly the city of Vṛndāṭaka, and Dvārapāla.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 10. And the extremely glorious one swiftly brought under his control the entire region of Pañcanada, as well as Aparaparyaṭa, and also Uttarajyotika, and similarly the city of Vṛndāṭaka, and Dvārapāla.

sanskrit: रमठान्हारहूणांश्च प्रतीच्याश्चैव ये नृपाः ।तान्सर्वान्स वशे चक्रे शासनादेव पाण्डवः ॥११॥
transliteration: ramaṭhānhārahūṇāṁśca pratīcyāścaiva ye nṛpāḥ ,tānsarvānsa vaśe cakre śāsanādeva pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: And the Pāṇḍava brought all of them under his control merely by his decree: the Ramaṭhas, the Hāra-Hūṇas, and also those kings who belonged to the Western regions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 11. And the Pāṇḍava brought all of them under his control merely by his decree: the Ramaṭhas, the Hāra-Hūṇas, and also those kings who belonged to the Western regions.

sanskrit: तत्रस्थः प्रेषयामास वासुदेवाय चाभिभुः ।स चास्य दशभी राज्यैः प्रतिजग्राह शासनम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: tatrasthaḥ preṣayāmāsa vāsudevāya cābhibhuḥ ,sa cāsya daśabhī rājyaiḥ pratijagrāha śāsanam.
english: The powerful one, who was staying there, sent a message to Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa). And Vasudeva, in turn, accepted his command along with ten kingdoms.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 12. The powerful one, who was staying there, sent a message to Vasudeva (Kṛṣṇa). And Vasudeva, in turn, accepted his command along with ten kingdoms.

sanskrit: ततः शाकलमभ्येत्य मद्राणां पुटभेदनम् ।मातुलं प्रीतिपूर्वेण शल्यं चक्रे वशे बली ॥१३॥
transliteration: tataḥ śākalamabhyetya madrāṇāṁ puṭabhedanam ,mātulaṁ prītipūrveṇa śalyaṁ cakre vaśe balī.
english: Thereafter, the mighty one went to Śākala, the capital of the Madras. With affection, he brought his maternal uncle, Śalya, under his control.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 13. Thereafter, the mighty one went to Śākala, the capital of the Madras. With affection, he brought his maternal uncle, Śalya, under his control.

sanskrit: स तस्मिन्सत्कृतो राज्ञा सत्कारार्हो विशां पते ।रत्नानि भूरीण्यादाय संप्रतस्थे युधां पतिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sa tasminsatkṛto rājñā satkārārho viśāṁ pate ,ratnāni bhūrīṇyādāya saṁpratasthe yudhāṁ patiḥ.
english: There, the lord of the people and lord of warriors, who was worthy of honor, was duly honored by the king. Having received many jewels, he set out.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 14. There, the lord of the people and lord of warriors, who was worthy of honor, was duly honored by the king. Having received many jewels, he set out.

sanskrit: ततः सागरकुक्षिस्थान्म्लेच्छान्परमदारुणान् ।पह्लवान्बर्बरांश्चैव तान्सर्वाननयद्वशम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tataḥ sāgarakukṣisthānmlecchānparamadāruṇān ,pahlavānbarbarāṁścaiva tānsarvānanayadvaśam.
english: Thereafter, he brought all those extremely fierce mlecchas, namely the Pahlavas and Barbaras, who resided by the ocean, under his control.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 15. Thereafter, he brought all those extremely fierce mlecchas, namely the Pahlavas and Barbaras, who resided by the ocean, under his control.

sanskrit: ततो रत्नान्युपादाय वशे कृत्वा च पार्थिवान् ।न्यवर्तत नरश्रेष्ठो नकुलश्चित्रमार्गवित् ॥१६॥
transliteration: tato ratnānyupādāya vaśe kṛtvā ca pārthivān ,nyavartata naraśreṣṭho nakulaścitramārgavit.
english: Then, having collected gems and brought the kings under control, Nakula, the chief among men and skilled in diverse routes, returned.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 16. Then, having collected gems and brought the kings under control, Nakula, the chief among men and skilled in diverse routes, returned.

sanskrit: करभाणां सहस्राणि कोशं तस्य महात्मनः ।ऊहुर्दश महाराज कृच्छ्रादिव महाधनम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: karabhāṇāṁ sahasrāṇi kośaṁ tasya mahātmanaḥ ,ūhurdaśa mahārāja kṛcchrādiva mahādhanam.
english: O great king, ten thousand camels carried that great wealth, the treasury belonging to the great-souled (mahātman) Nakula, as if with difficulty.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 17. O great king, ten thousand camels carried that great wealth, the treasury belonging to the great-souled (mahātman) Nakula, as if with difficulty.

sanskrit: इन्द्रप्रस्थगतं वीरमभ्येत्य स युधिष्ठिरम् ।ततो माद्रीसुतः श्रीमान्धनं तस्मै न्यवेदयत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: indraprasthagataṁ vīramabhyetya sa yudhiṣṭhiram ,tato mādrīsutaḥ śrīmāndhanaṁ tasmai nyavedayat.
english: Having approached the hero Yudhiṣṭhira, who was then in Indraprastha, the glorious son of Mādrī (Nakula) presented that wealth to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 18. Having approached the hero Yudhiṣṭhira, who was then in Indraprastha, the glorious son of Mādrī (Nakula) presented that wealth to him.

sanskrit: एवं प्रतीचीं नकुलो दिशं वरुणपालिताम् ।विजिग्ये वासुदेवेन निर्जितां भरतर्षभः ॥१९॥
transliteration: evaṁ pratīcīṁ nakulo diśaṁ varuṇapālitām ,vijigye vāsudevena nirjitāṁ bharatarṣabhaḥ.
english: Thus, Nakula, the best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), conquered the western direction, which is protected by Varuṇa and had already been subdued by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 29 Verse 19. Thus, Nakula, the best of the Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), conquered the western direction, which is protected by Varuṇa and had already been subdued by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।रक्षणाद्धर्मराजस्य सत्यस्य परिपालनात् ।शत्रूणां क्षपणाच्चैव स्वकर्मनिरताः प्रजाः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,rakṣaṇāddharmarājasya satyasya paripālanāt ,śatrūṇāṁ kṣapaṇāccaiva svakarmaniratāḥ prajāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Due to the protection provided by the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), the upholding of truth, and the destruction of enemies, the people were diligently engaged in their own prescribed actions (sva-karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Due to the protection provided by the king of righteousness (dharma-rāja), the upholding of truth, and the destruction of enemies, the people were diligently engaged in their own prescribed actions (sva-karma).

sanskrit: बलीनां सम्यगादानाद्धर्मतश्चानुशासनात् ।निकामवर्षी पर्जन्यः स्फीतो जनपदोऽभवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: balīnāṁ samyagādānāddharmataścānuśāsanāt ,nikāmavarṣī parjanyaḥ sphīto janapado'bhavat.
english: Due to the proper collection of tributes and righteous governance (anuśāsana) according to natural law (dharma), the rain god (parjanya) showered rain as desired, and the country (janapada) became prosperous.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 2. Due to the proper collection of tributes and righteous governance (anuśāsana) according to natural law (dharma), the rain god (parjanya) showered rain as desired, and the country (janapada) became prosperous.

sanskrit: सर्वारम्भाः सुप्रवृत्ता गोरक्षं कर्षणं वणिक् ।विशेषात्सर्वमेवैतत्संजज्ञे राजकर्मणः ॥३॥
transliteration: sarvārambhāḥ supravṛttā gorakṣaṁ karṣaṇaṁ vaṇik ,viśeṣātsarvamevaitatsaṁjajñe rājakarmaṇaḥ.
english: All undertakings, including cattle protection, agriculture, and trade, flourished. Indeed, all of this especially originated from the king's actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 3. All undertakings, including cattle protection, agriculture, and trade, flourished. Indeed, all of this especially originated from the king's actions (karma).

sanskrit: दस्युभ्यो वञ्चकेभ्यो वा राजन्प्रति परस्परम् ।राजवल्लभतश्चैव नाश्रूयन्त मृषा गिरः ॥४॥
transliteration: dasyubhyo vañcakebhyo vā rājanprati parasparam ,rājavallabhataścaiva nāśrūyanta mṛṣā giraḥ.
english: O King, false statements (mṛṣā giraḥ) were not heard from robbers, or from deceivers, nor from people mutually against each other, and certainly not even from the king's favored officials (rājavallabha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 4. O King, false statements (mṛṣā giraḥ) were not heard from robbers, or from deceivers, nor from people mutually against each other, and certainly not even from the king's favored officials (rājavallabha).

sanskrit: अवर्षं चातिवर्षं च व्याधिपावकमूर्छनम् ।सर्वमेतत्तदा नासीद्धर्मनित्ये युधिष्ठिरे ॥५॥
transliteration: avarṣaṁ cātivarṣaṁ ca vyādhipāvakamūrchanam ,sarvametattadā nāsīddharmanitye yudhiṣṭhire.
english: At that time, there was neither drought nor excessive rain, nor disease, fire, or unconsciousness, because Yudhiṣṭhira was always firm in his natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 5. At that time, there was neither drought nor excessive rain, nor disease, fire, or unconsciousness, because Yudhiṣṭhira was always firm in his natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: प्रियं कर्तुमुपस्थातुं बलिकर्म स्वभावजम् ।अभिहर्तुं नृपा जग्मुर्नान्यैः कार्यैः पृथक्पृथक् ॥६॥
transliteration: priyaṁ kartumupasthātuṁ balikarma svabhāvajam ,abhihartuṁ nṛpā jagmurnānyaiḥ kāryaiḥ pṛthakpṛthak.
english: Kings came to perform agreeable services, to present the customary tribute (bali-karma), and to offer taxes; they did not come for any other distinct or separate purposes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 6. Kings came to perform agreeable services, to present the customary tribute (bali-karma), and to offer taxes; they did not come for any other distinct or separate purposes.

sanskrit: धर्म्यैर्धनागमैस्तस्य ववृधे निचयो महान् ।कर्तुं यस्य न शक्येत क्षयो वर्षशतैरपि ॥७॥
transliteration: dharmyairdhanāgamaistasya vavṛdhe nicayo mahān ,kartuṁ yasya na śakyeta kṣayo varṣaśatairapi.
english: Through righteous (dharmya) acquisitions of wealth, his great accumulation (nicaya) grew, the depletion of which could not be accomplished even in a hundred years.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 7. Through righteous (dharmya) acquisitions of wealth, his great accumulation (nicaya) grew, the depletion of which could not be accomplished even in a hundred years.

sanskrit: स्वकोशस्य परीमाणं कोष्ठस्य च महीपतिः ।विज्ञाय राजा कौन्तेयो यज्ञायैव मनो दधे ॥८॥
transliteration: svakośasya parīmāṇaṁ koṣṭhasya ca mahīpatiḥ ,vijñāya rājā kaunteyo yajñāyaiva mano dadhe.
english: Having ascertained the full measure of his own treasury and his storehouse, the king, Kaunteya (son of Kuntī), truly fixed his mind upon the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 8. Having ascertained the full measure of his own treasury and his storehouse, the king, Kaunteya (son of Kuntī), truly fixed his mind upon the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: सुहृदश्चैव तं सर्वे पृथक्च सह चाब्रुवन् ।यज्ञकालस्तव विभो क्रियतामत्र सांप्रतम् ॥९॥
transliteration: suhṛdaścaiva taṁ sarve pṛthakca saha cābruvan ,yajñakālastava vibho kriyatāmatra sāṁpratam.
english: All his well-wishers, both individually and collectively, then spoke to him: "O mighty one, it is indeed your time for the Vedic ritual (yajña); let it be performed here now."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 9. All his well-wishers, both individually and collectively, then spoke to him: "O mighty one, it is indeed your time for the Vedic ritual (yajña); let it be performed here now."

sanskrit: अथैवं ब्रुवतामेव तेषामभ्याययौ हरिः ।ऋषिः पुराणो वेदात्मा दृश्यश्चापि विजानताम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: athaivaṁ bruvatāmeva teṣāmabhyāyayau hariḥ ,ṛṣiḥ purāṇo vedātmā dṛśyaścāpi vijānatām.
english: Then, as they were speaking thus, Hari approached them. He is the ancient sage (ṛṣi), the very essence of the Vedas, and is indeed visible to those who possess true knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 10. Then, as they were speaking thus, Hari approached them. He is the ancient sage (ṛṣi), the very essence of the Vedas, and is indeed visible to those who possess true knowledge.

sanskrit: जगतस्तस्थुषां श्रेष्ठः प्रभवश्चाप्ययश्च ह ।भूतभव्यभवन्नाथः केशवः केशिसूदनः ॥११॥
transliteration: jagatastasthuṣāṁ śreṣṭhaḥ prabhavaścāpyayaśca ha ,bhūtabhavyabhavannāthaḥ keśavaḥ keśisūdanaḥ.
english: He is the supreme among all moving and non-moving beings, the very origin and dissolution of all creation. He is the master of past, present, and future  -  Keśava, the slayer of the Keśin demon.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 11. He is the supreme among all moving and non-moving beings, the very origin and dissolution of all creation. He is the master of past, present, and future  -  Keśava, the slayer of the Keśin demon.

sanskrit: प्राकारः सर्ववृष्णीनामापत्स्वभयदोऽरिहा ।बलाधिकारे निक्षिप्य संहत्यानकदुन्दुभिम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: prākāraḥ sarvavṛṣṇīnāmāpatsvabhayado'rihā ,balādhikāre nikṣipya saṁhatyānakadundubhim.
english: He is a fortress for all the Vṛṣṇis, a bestower of fearlessness in times of calamity, and an annihilator of enemies. He (does all this) by placing Anakadundubhi in military command, having brought (the forces) together.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 12. He is a fortress for all the Vṛṣṇis, a bestower of fearlessness in times of calamity, and an annihilator of enemies. He (does all this) by placing Anakadundubhi in military command, having brought (the forces) together.

sanskrit: उच्चावचमुपादाय धर्मराजाय माधवः ।धनौघं पुरुषव्याघ्रो बलेन महता वृतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: uccāvacamupādāya dharmarājāya mādhavaḥ ,dhanaughaṁ puruṣavyāghro balena mahatā vṛtaḥ.
english: Having collected various kinds of wealth for King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja), Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), was accompanied by a great army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 13. Having collected various kinds of wealth for King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja), Mādhava (Kṛṣṇa), that tiger among men (puruṣavyāghra), was accompanied by a great army.

sanskrit: तं धनौघमपर्यन्तं रत्नसागरमक्षयम् ।नादयन्रथघोषेण प्रविवेश पुरोत्तमम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: taṁ dhanaughamaparyantaṁ ratnasāgaramakṣayam ,nādayanrathaghoṣeṇa praviveśa purottamam.
english: Making the excellent city (purottamam) resound with the clamor of chariots, he entered, bringing that boundless and inexhaustible multitude of wealth (dhanaugham), which was like an ocean of jewels (ratnasāgaram).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 14. Making the excellent city (purottamam) resound with the clamor of chariots, he entered, bringing that boundless and inexhaustible multitude of wealth (dhanaugham), which was like an ocean of jewels (ratnasāgaram).

sanskrit: असूर्यमिव सूर्येण निवातमिव वायुना ।कृष्णेन समुपेतेन जहृषे भारतं पुरम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: asūryamiva sūryeṇa nivātamiva vāyunā ,kṛṣṇena samupetena jahṛṣe bhārataṁ puram.
english: The city (puram) of Hastinapura (bhāratam) rejoiced with Kṛṣṇa's arrival, just as a sunless place (asūryam) is illuminated by the sun (sūryeṇa), or a windless place (nivātam) is stirred by the wind (vāyunā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 15. The city (puram) of Hastinapura (bhāratam) rejoiced with Kṛṣṇa's arrival, just as a sunless place (asūryam) is illuminated by the sun (sūryeṇa), or a windless place (nivātam) is stirred by the wind (vāyunā).

sanskrit: तं मुदाभिसमागम्य सत्कृत्य च यथाविधि ।संपृष्ट्वा कुशलं चैव सुखासीनं युधिष्ठिरः ॥१६॥
transliteration: taṁ mudābhisamāgamya satkṛtya ca yathāvidhi ,saṁpṛṣṭvā kuśalaṁ caiva sukhāsīnaṁ yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, having joyfully (mudā) gone to meet him (Kṛṣṇa) and having honored him properly (yathāvidhi), then inquired about his well-being (kuśalam) as he (Kṛṣṇa) sat comfortably (sukhāsīnam).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 16. Yudhiṣṭhira, having joyfully (mudā) gone to meet him (Kṛṣṇa) and having honored him properly (yathāvidhi), then inquired about his well-being (kuśalam) as he (Kṛṣṇa) sat comfortably (sukhāsīnam).

sanskrit: धौम्यद्वैपायनमुखैरृत्विग्भिः पुरुषर्षभः ।भीमार्जुनयमैश्चापि सहितः कृष्णमब्रवीत् ॥१७॥
transliteration: dhaumyadvaipāyanamukhairṛtvigbhiḥ puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,bhīmārjunayamaiścāpi sahitaḥ kṛṣṇamabravīt.
english: Accompanied by priests led by Dhaumya and Dvaipāyana, and also by Bhīma, Arjuna, and the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), Yudhishthira, the best among men, spoke to Krishna (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 17. Accompanied by priests led by Dhaumya and Dvaipāyana, and also by Bhīma, Arjuna, and the Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), Yudhishthira, the best among men, spoke to Krishna (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: त्वत्कृते पृथिवी सर्वा मद्वशे कृष्ण वर्तते ।धनं च बहु वार्ष्णेय त्वत्प्रसादादुपार्जितम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: tvatkṛte pṛthivī sarvā madvaśe kṛṣṇa vartate ,dhanaṁ ca bahu vārṣṇeya tvatprasādādupārjitam.
english: O Krishna (Kṛṣṇa), the entire earth is under my control because of you. And O Vārṣṇeya, much wealth has been acquired through your grace.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 18. O Krishna (Kṛṣṇa), the entire earth is under my control because of you. And O Vārṣṇeya, much wealth has been acquired through your grace.

sanskrit: सोऽहमिच्छामि तत्सर्वं विधिवद्देवकीसुत ।उपयोक्तुं द्विजाग्र्येषु हव्यवाहे च माधव ॥१९॥
transliteration: so'hamicchāmi tatsarvaṁ vidhivaddevakīsuta ,upayoktuṁ dvijāgryeṣu havyavāhe ca mādhava.
english: Therefore, O son of Devaki, O Madhava, I wish to utilize all that wealth properly, according to the sacred injunctions, by distributing it among the foremost Brahmins and offering it into the sacrificial fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 19. Therefore, O son of Devaki, O Madhava, I wish to utilize all that wealth properly, according to the sacred injunctions, by distributing it among the foremost Brahmins and offering it into the sacrificial fire.

sanskrit: तदहं यष्टुमिच्छामि दाशार्ह सहितस्त्वया ।अनुजैश्च महाबाहो तन्मानुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥२०॥
transliteration: tadahaṁ yaṣṭumicchāmi dāśārha sahitastvayā ,anujaiśca mahābāho tanmānujñātumarhasi.
english: O Dāśārha (Krishna), O mighty-armed one, I desire to perform that (Vedic ritual) together with you and my younger brothers. Therefore, you should permit me to do that.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 20. O Dāśārha (Krishna), O mighty-armed one, I desire to perform that (Vedic ritual) together with you and my younger brothers. Therefore, you should permit me to do that.

sanskrit: स दीक्षापय गोविन्द त्वमात्मानं महाभुज ।त्वयीष्टवति दाशार्ह विपाप्मा भविता ह्यहम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sa dīkṣāpaya govinda tvamātmānaṁ mahābhuja ,tvayīṣṭavati dāśārha vipāpmā bhavitā hyaham.
english: O Govinda, O mighty-armed one, you should perform the consecration (dīkṣā) yourself. Indeed, when you, O Dāśārha, have performed the Vedic ritual (yajña), I shall become free from sin.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 21. O Govinda, O mighty-armed one, you should perform the consecration (dīkṣā) yourself. Indeed, when you, O Dāśārha, have performed the Vedic ritual (yajña), I shall become free from sin.

sanskrit: मां वाप्यभ्यनुजानीहि सहैभिरनुजैर्विभो ।अनुज्ञातस्त्वया कृष्ण प्राप्नुयां क्रतुमुत्तमम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: māṁ vāpyabhyanujānīhi sahaibhiranujairvibho ,anujñātastvayā kṛṣṇa prāpnuyāṁ kratumuttamam.
english: Or, O Lord, permit me also along with these younger brothers. O Krishna, once I am permitted by you, I shall obtain the most excellent Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 22. Or, O Lord, permit me also along with these younger brothers. O Krishna, once I am permitted by you, I shall obtain the most excellent Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: तं कृष्णः प्रत्युवाचेदं बहूक्त्वा गुणविस्तरम् ।त्वमेव राजशार्दूल सम्राडर्हो महाक्रतुम् ।संप्राप्नुहि त्वया प्राप्ते कृतकृत्यास्ततो वयम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: taṁ kṛṣṇaḥ pratyuvācedaṁ bahūktvā guṇavistaram ,tvameva rājaśārdūla samrāḍarho mahākratum ,saṁprāpnuhi tvayā prāpte kṛtakṛtyāstato vayam.
english: Having spoken at length about his great qualities, Krishna replied to him: 'O tiger among kings, you alone, as emperor, are truly worthy of a great Vedic ritual (yajña). Therefore, you should undertake it! Once it is performed by you, then we shall consider our duty fulfilled (kṛtakṛtya).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 23. Having spoken at length about his great qualities, Krishna replied to him: 'O tiger among kings, you alone, as emperor, are truly worthy of a great Vedic ritual (yajña). Therefore, you should undertake it! Once it is performed by you, then we shall consider our duty fulfilled (kṛtakṛtya).'

sanskrit: यजस्वाभीप्सितं यज्ञं मयि श्रेयस्यवस्थिते ।नियुङ्क्ष्व चापि मां कृत्ये सर्वं कर्तास्मि ते वचः ॥२४॥
transliteration: yajasvābhīpsitaṁ yajñaṁ mayi śreyasyavasthite ,niyuṅkṣva cāpi māṁ kṛtye sarvaṁ kartāsmi te vacaḥ.
english: Perform the desired Vedic ritual (yajña) while I am present here for your prosperity. And also, assign me to any task; I will carry out all your commands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 24. Perform the desired Vedic ritual (yajña) while I am present here for your prosperity. And also, assign me to any task; I will carry out all your commands.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।सफलः कृष्ण संकल्पः सिद्धिश्च नियता मम ।यस्य मे त्वं हृषीकेश यथेप्सितमुपस्थितः ॥२५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,saphalaḥ kṛṣṇa saṁkalpaḥ siddhiśca niyatā mama ,yasya me tvaṁ hṛṣīkeśa yathepsitamupasthitaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: O Kṛṣṇa, my resolve (saṃkalpa) is fruitful, and my success is assured, because you, Hṛṣīkeśa, are present with me as I desired.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 25. Yudhiṣṭhira said: O Kṛṣṇa, my resolve (saṃkalpa) is fruitful, and my success is assured, because you, Hṛṣīkeśa, are present with me as I desired.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अनुज्ञातस्तु कृष्णेन पाण्डवो भ्रातृभिः सह ।ईहितुं राजसूयाय साधनान्युपचक्रमे ॥२६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,anujñātastu kṛṣṇena pāṇḍavo bhrātṛbhiḥ saha ,īhituṁ rājasūyāya sādhanānyupacakrame.
english: Vaishaṃpāyana said: Having been permitted by Krishna, the son of Pāṇḍu, along with his brothers, began to procure the means for the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 26. Vaishaṃpāyana said: Having been permitted by Krishna, the son of Pāṇḍu, along with his brothers, began to procure the means for the Rājasūya (Vedic ritual).

sanskrit: तत आज्ञापयामास पाण्डवोऽरिनिबर्हणः ।सहदेवं युधां श्रेष्ठं मन्त्रिणश्चैव सर्वशः ॥२७॥
transliteration: tata ājñāpayāmāsa pāṇḍavo'rinibarhaṇaḥ ,sahadevaṁ yudhāṁ śreṣṭhaṁ mantriṇaścaiva sarvaśaḥ.
english: Then, the Pāṇḍava, the destroyer of enemies (arinibarhaṇa), ordered Sahadeva, the best among warriors (yudhāṃ śreṣṭha), and all his ministers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 27. Then, the Pāṇḍava, the destroyer of enemies (arinibarhaṇa), ordered Sahadeva, the best among warriors (yudhāṃ śreṣṭha), and all his ministers.

sanskrit: अस्मिन्क्रतौ यथोक्तानि यज्ञाङ्गानि द्विजातिभिः ।तथोपकरणं सर्वं मङ्गलानि च सर्वशः ॥२८॥
transliteration: asminkratau yathoktāni yajñāṅgāni dvijātibhiḥ ,tathopakaraṇaṁ sarvaṁ maṅgalāni ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: In this (Vedic ritual), all the components of the (Vedic ritual) (yajña) as prescribed by the twice-born (dvijāti) ones, and likewise all the equipment and auspicious items, were entirely gathered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 28. In this (Vedic ritual), all the components of the (Vedic ritual) (yajña) as prescribed by the twice-born (dvijāti) ones, and likewise all the equipment and auspicious items, were entirely gathered.

sanskrit: अधियज्ञांश्च संभारान्धौम्योक्तान्क्षिप्रमेव हि ।समानयन्तु पुरुषा यथायोगं यथाक्रमम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: adhiyajñāṁśca saṁbhārāndhaumyoktānkṣiprameva hi ,samānayantu puruṣā yathāyogaṁ yathākramam.
english: Indeed, let the men quickly gather the provisions for the "yajña" (sacrifice) as instructed by Dhaumya, doing so appropriately and in proper sequence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 29. Indeed, let the men quickly gather the provisions for the "yajña" (sacrifice) as instructed by Dhaumya, doing so appropriately and in proper sequence.

sanskrit: इन्द्रसेनो विशोकश्च पूरुश्चार्जुनसारथिः ।अन्नाद्याहरणे युक्ताः सन्तु मत्प्रियकाम्यया ॥३०॥
transliteration: indraseno viśokaśca pūruścārjunasārathiḥ ,annādyāharaṇe yuktāḥ santu matpriyakāmyayā.
english: Let Indrasena, Viśoka, Pūru, and Arjuna's charioteer be appointed to gather food and other provisions, for the fulfillment of my wishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 30. Let Indrasena, Viśoka, Pūru, and Arjuna's charioteer be appointed to gather food and other provisions, for the fulfillment of my wishes.

sanskrit: सर्वकामाश्च कार्यन्तां रसगन्धसमन्विताः ।मनोहराः प्रीतिकरा द्विजानां कुरुसत्तम ॥३१॥
transliteration: sarvakāmāśca kāryantāṁ rasagandhasamanvitāḥ ,manoharāḥ prītikarā dvijānāṁ kurusattama.
english: O best of the Kurus, let all desired items be prepared, imbued with delicious tastes and fragrances, charming and delightful to the Brahmins (dvijānām).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 31. O best of the Kurus, let all desired items be prepared, imbued with delicious tastes and fragrances, charming and delightful to the Brahmins (dvijānām).

sanskrit: तद्वाक्यसमकालं तु कृतं सर्वमवेदयत् ।सहदेवो युधां श्रेष्ठो धर्मराजे महात्मनि ॥३२॥
transliteration: tadvākyasamakālaṁ tu kṛtaṁ sarvamavedayat ,sahadevo yudhāṁ śreṣṭho dharmarāje mahātmani.
english: But immediately upon hearing those words, Sahadeva, the best among warriors, informed the great-souled king of "dharma" (dharmarāja) that everything had been accomplished.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 32. But immediately upon hearing those words, Sahadeva, the best among warriors, informed the great-souled king of "dharma" (dharmarāja) that everything had been accomplished.

sanskrit: ततो द्वैपायनो राजन्नृत्विजः समुपानयत् ।वेदानिव महाभागान्साक्षान्मूर्तिमतो द्विजान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tato dvaipāyano rājannṛtvijaḥ samupānayat ,vedāniva mahābhāgānsākṣānmūrtimato dvijān.
english: Then, O King, Dvaipāyana brought together the officiating priests (ṛtvijaḥ), who were highly esteemed, directly embodied brahmins (dvijān), as if they were the Vedas themselves.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 33. Then, O King, Dvaipāyana brought together the officiating priests (ṛtvijaḥ), who were highly esteemed, directly embodied brahmins (dvijān), as if they were the Vedas themselves.

sanskrit: स्वयं ब्रह्मत्वमकरोत्तस्य सत्यवतीसुतः ।धनंजयानामृषभः सुसामा सामगोऽभवत् ॥३४॥
transliteration: svayaṁ brahmatvamakarottasya satyavatīsutaḥ ,dhanaṁjayānāmṛṣabhaḥ susāmā sāmago'bhavat.
english: The son of Satyavatī (Satyavatīsutaḥ) himself assumed the role of the chief priest (brahmatvam). Susāmā, the foremost (ṛṣabhaḥ) among those called Dhanañjaya, became the chanter of the Sāma Veda (sāmagah).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 34. The son of Satyavatī (Satyavatīsutaḥ) himself assumed the role of the chief priest (brahmatvam). Susāmā, the foremost (ṛṣabhaḥ) among those called Dhanañjaya, became the chanter of the Sāma Veda (sāmagah).

sanskrit: याज्ञवल्क्यो बभूवाथ ब्रह्मिष्ठोऽध्वर्युसत्तमः ।पैलो होता वसोः पुत्रो धौम्येन सहितोऽभवत् ॥३५॥
transliteration: yājñavalkyo babhūvātha brahmiṣṭho'dhvaryusattamaḥ ,pailo hotā vasoḥ putro dhaumyena sahito'bhavat.
english: Then Yājñavalkya became the most learned (brahmiṣṭhaḥ) and the chief among the Adhvaryu priests (adhvaryusattamaḥ). Paila, the son of Vasu, became the Hotā priest (hotā), accompanied by Dhaumya.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 35. Then Yājñavalkya became the most learned (brahmiṣṭhaḥ) and the chief among the Adhvaryu priests (adhvaryusattamaḥ). Paila, the son of Vasu, became the Hotā priest (hotā), accompanied by Dhaumya.

sanskrit: एतेषां शिष्यवर्गाश्च पुत्राश्च भरतर्षभ ।बभूवुर्होत्रगाः सर्वे वेदवेदाङ्गपारगाः ॥३६॥
transliteration: eteṣāṁ śiṣyavargāśca putrāśca bharatarṣabha ,babhūvurhotragāḥ sarve vedavedāṅgapāragāḥ.
english: O best of Bhāratas (Bharatārṣabha), all the groups of disciples (śiṣyavargāḥ) and sons (putrāḥ) of these priests became performers of sacrificial rites (hotragāḥ) and were experts in the Vedas and Vedāṅgas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 36. O best of Bhāratas (Bharatārṣabha), all the groups of disciples (śiṣyavargāḥ) and sons (putrāḥ) of these priests became performers of sacrificial rites (hotragāḥ) and were experts in the Vedas and Vedāṅgas.

sanskrit: ते वाचयित्वा पुण्याहमीहयित्वा च तं विधिम् ।शास्त्रोक्तं योजयामासुस्तद्देवयजनं महत् ॥३७॥
transliteration: te vācayitvā puṇyāhamīhayitvā ca taṁ vidhim ,śāstroktaṁ yojayāmāsustaddevayajanaṁ mahat.
english: After they had the auspicious day proclaimed and that ritual, prescribed by the scriptures, performed, they then prepared that grand sacrificial ground for the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 37. After they had the auspicious day proclaimed and that ritual, prescribed by the scriptures, performed, they then prepared that grand sacrificial ground for the gods.

sanskrit: तत्र चक्रुरनुज्ञाताः शरणान्युत शिल्पिनः ।रत्नवन्ति विशालानि वेश्मानीव दिवौकसाम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: tatra cakruranujñātāḥ śaraṇānyuta śilpinaḥ ,ratnavanti viśālāni veśmānīva divaukasām.
english: There, the artisans, having received permission, constructed shelters and also grand, jewel-studded mansions, resembling those of the celestials.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 38. There, the artisans, having received permission, constructed shelters and also grand, jewel-studded mansions, resembling those of the celestials.

sanskrit: तत आज्ञापयामास स राजा राजसत्तमः ।सहदेवं तदा सद्यो मन्त्रिणं कुरुसत्तमः ॥३९॥
transliteration: tata ājñāpayāmāsa sa rājā rājasattamaḥ ,sahadevaṁ tadā sadyo mantriṇaṁ kurusattamaḥ.
english: Then, that excellent king, the best among the Kurus, immediately commanded his minister Sahadeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 39. Then, that excellent king, the best among the Kurus, immediately commanded his minister Sahadeva.

sanskrit: आमन्त्रणार्थं दूतांस्त्वं प्रेषयस्वाशुगान्द्रुतम् ।उपश्रुत्य वचो राज्ञः स दूतान्प्राहिणोत्तदा ॥४०॥
transliteration: āmantraṇārthaṁ dūtāṁstvaṁ preṣayasvāśugāndrutam ,upaśrutya vaco rājñaḥ sa dūtānprāhiṇottadā.
english: (The king said to Sahadeva:) "You send forth swift messengers quickly for the purpose of invitation." Having heard the king's command, he (Sahadeva) then dispatched the messengers.
