search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 40. (The king said to Sahadeva:) "You send forth swift messengers quickly for the purpose of invitation." Having heard the king's command, he (Sahadeva) then dispatched the messengers.

sanskrit: आमन्त्रयध्वं राष्ट्रेषु ब्राह्मणान्भूमिपानपि ।विशश्च मान्याञ्शूद्रांश्च सर्वानानयतेति च ॥४१॥
transliteration: āmantrayadhvaṁ rāṣṭreṣu brāhmaṇānbhūmipānapi ,viśaśca mānyāñśūdrāṁśca sarvānānayateti ca.
english: Invite brahmins and kings from all the realms (rāṣṭra), and also the respectable Vaiśyas and Śūdras, saying, 'Bring all of them here.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 41. Invite brahmins and kings from all the realms (rāṣṭra), and also the respectable Vaiśyas and Śūdras, saying, 'Bring all of them here.'

sanskrit: ते सर्वान्पृथिवीपालान्पाण्डवेयस्य शासनात् ।आमन्त्रयां बभूवुश्च प्रेषयामास चापरान् ॥४२॥
transliteration: te sarvānpṛthivīpālānpāṇḍaveyasya śāsanāt ,āmantrayāṁ babhūvuśca preṣayāmāsa cāparān.
english: According to the command of the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍaveya), they invited all the kings and also dispatched others (to convey the invitations).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 42. According to the command of the son of Pāṇḍu (Pāṇḍaveya), they invited all the kings and also dispatched others (to convey the invitations).

sanskrit: ततस्ते तु यथाकालं कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ।दीक्षयां चक्रिरे विप्रा राजसूयाय भारत ॥४३॥
transliteration: tataste tu yathākālaṁ kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,dīkṣayāṁ cakrire viprā rājasūyāya bhārata.
english: Then, O Bhārata, at the proper time, the brahmins initiated Yudhiṣṭhira, Kuntī's son, for the Rājasūya (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 43. Then, O Bhārata, at the proper time, the brahmins initiated Yudhiṣṭhira, Kuntī's son, for the Rājasūya (yajña).

sanskrit: दीक्षितः स तु धर्मात्मा धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।जगाम यज्ञायतनं वृतो विप्रैः सहस्रशः ॥४४॥
transliteration: dīkṣitaḥ sa tu dharmātmā dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,jagāma yajñāyatanaṁ vṛto vipraiḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: That righteous (dharmātman) Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharmarāja), having been consecrated (for the ritual), went to the ritual-house (yajñāyatana), surrounded by thousands of Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 44. That righteous (dharmātman) Yudhishthira, the king of natural law (dharmarāja), having been consecrated (for the ritual), went to the ritual-house (yajñāyatana), surrounded by thousands of Brahmins.

sanskrit: भ्रातृभिर्ज्ञातिभिश्चैव सुहृद्भिः सचिवैस्तथा ।क्षत्रियैश्च मनुष्येन्द्र नानादेशसमागतैः ।अमात्यैश्च नृपश्रेष्ठो धर्मो विग्रहवानिव ॥४५॥
transliteration: bhrātṛbhirjñātibhiścaiva suhṛdbhiḥ sacivaistathā ,kṣatriyaiśca manuṣyendra nānādeśasamāgataiḥ ,amātyaiśca nṛpaśreṣṭho dharmo vigrahavāniva.
english: O king among men, the best of kings, who was like embodied natural law (dharma), was attended by brothers, relatives, friends, and ministers; and also by warriors (kṣatriyas) who had assembled from various countries, as well as by other ministers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 45. O king among men, the best of kings, who was like embodied natural law (dharma), was attended by brothers, relatives, friends, and ministers; and also by warriors (kṣatriyas) who had assembled from various countries, as well as by other ministers.

sanskrit: आजग्मुर्ब्राह्मणास्तत्र विषयेभ्यस्ततस्ततः ।सर्वविद्यासु निष्णाता वेदवेदाङ्गपारगाः ॥४६॥
transliteration: ājagmurbrāhmaṇāstatra viṣayebhyastatastataḥ ,sarvavidyāsu niṣṇātā vedavedāṅgapāragāḥ.
english: Brahmins (brāhmaṇa) arrived there from various regions, proficient in all sciences and masters of the Vedas (veda) and Vedangas (vedāṅga).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 46. Brahmins (brāhmaṇa) arrived there from various regions, proficient in all sciences and masters of the Vedas (veda) and Vedangas (vedāṅga).

sanskrit: तेषामावसथांश्चक्रुर्धर्मराजस्य शासनात् ।बह्वन्नाञ्शयनैर्युक्तान्सगणानां पृथक्पृथक् ।सर्वर्तुगुणसंपन्नाञ्शिल्पिनोऽथ सहस्रशः ॥४७॥
transliteration: teṣāmāvasathāṁścakrurdharmarājasya śāsanāt ,bahvannāñśayanairyuktānsagaṇānāṁ pṛthakpṛthak ,sarvartuguṇasaṁpannāñśilpino'tha sahasraśaḥ.
english: By the command of Dharmaraja (dharma-rāja), they prepared separate dwellings for those (Brahmins) with their retinues, dwellings which were abundant with food, equipped with beds, and provided with comforts suitable for all seasons. Then, thousands of craftsmen (śilpin) also arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 47. By the command of Dharmaraja (dharma-rāja), they prepared separate dwellings for those (Brahmins) with their retinues, dwellings which were abundant with food, equipped with beds, and provided with comforts suitable for all seasons. Then, thousands of craftsmen (śilpin) also arrived.

sanskrit: तेषु ते न्यवसन्राजन्ब्राह्मणा भृशसत्कृताः ।कथयन्तः कथा बह्वीः पश्यन्तो नटनर्तकान् ॥४८॥
transliteration: teṣu te nyavasanrājanbrāhmaṇā bhṛśasatkṛtāḥ ,kathayantaḥ kathā bahvīḥ paśyanto naṭanartakān.
english: O king, those greatly honored Brahmins (brāhmaṇa) resided in those (dwellings), narrating many stories and watching actors and dancers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 48. O king, those greatly honored Brahmins (brāhmaṇa) resided in those (dwellings), narrating many stories and watching actors and dancers.

sanskrit: भुञ्जतां चैव विप्राणां वदतां च महास्वनः ।अनिशं श्रूयते स्मात्र मुदितानां महात्मनाम् ॥४९॥
transliteration: bhuñjatāṁ caiva viprāṇāṁ vadatāṁ ca mahāsvanaḥ ,aniśaṁ śrūyate smātra muditānāṁ mahātmanām.
english: Here, a great and unceasing sound was heard from the joyful brahmins (viprāḥ) and other great-souled individuals (mahātmas) as they ate and conversed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 49. Here, a great and unceasing sound was heard from the joyful brahmins (viprāḥ) and other great-souled individuals (mahātmas) as they ate and conversed.

sanskrit: दीयतां दीयतामेषां भुज्यतां भुज्यतामिति ।एवंप्रकाराः संजल्पाः श्रूयन्ते स्मात्र नित्यशः ॥५०॥
transliteration: dīyatāṁ dīyatāmeṣāṁ bhujyatāṁ bhujyatāmiti ,evaṁprakārāḥ saṁjalpāḥ śrūyante smātra nityaśaḥ.
english: "Let it be given! Let it be given to them! Let it be eaten! Let it be eaten!"  -  such exclamations were constantly heard here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 50. "Let it be given! Let it be given to them! Let it be eaten! Let it be eaten!"  -  such exclamations were constantly heard here.

sanskrit: गवां शतसहस्राणि शयनानां च भारत ।रुक्मस्य योषितां चैव धर्मराजः पृथग्ददौ ॥५१॥
transliteration: gavāṁ śatasahasrāṇi śayanānāṁ ca bhārata ,rukmasya yoṣitāṁ caiva dharmarājaḥ pṛthagdadau.
english: O Bhārata, the King of (dharma) [Yudhiṣṭhira] gave away hundreds of thousands of cows, beds, gold, and women, each separately.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 51. O Bhārata, the King of (dharma) [Yudhiṣṭhira] gave away hundreds of thousands of cows, beds, gold, and women, each separately.

sanskrit: प्रावर्ततैवं यज्ञः स पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ।पृथिव्यामेकवीरस्य शक्रस्येव त्रिविष्टपे ॥५२॥
transliteration: prāvartataivaṁ yajñaḥ sa pāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ ,pṛthivyāmekavīrasya śakrasyeva triviṣṭape.
english: Thus, that great Vedic ritual (yajña) of the great-souled Pāṇḍava [Yudhiṣṭhira], the unique hero on earth, took place just as Indra's does in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 52. Thus, that great Vedic ritual (yajña) of the great-souled Pāṇḍava [Yudhiṣṭhira], the unique hero on earth, took place just as Indra's does in heaven.

sanskrit: ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा प्रेषयामास पाण्डवम् ।नकुलं हास्तिनपुरं भीष्माय भरतर्षभ ॥५३॥
transliteration: tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā preṣayāmāsa pāṇḍavam ,nakulaṁ hāstinapuraṁ bhīṣmāya bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas, King Yudhiṣṭhira then sent Nakula, the Pāṇḍava, to Hastināpura for Bhīṣma.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 53. O best of Bharatas, King Yudhiṣṭhira then sent Nakula, the Pāṇḍava, to Hastināpura for Bhīṣma.

sanskrit: द्रोणाय धृतराष्ट्राय विदुराय कृपाय च ।भ्रातॄणां चैव सर्वेषां येऽनुरक्ता युधिष्ठिरे ॥५४॥
transliteration: droṇāya dhṛtarāṣṭrāya vidurāya kṛpāya ca ,bhrātṝṇāṁ caiva sarveṣāṁ ye'nuraktā yudhiṣṭhire.
english: (He also sent Nakula) to Droṇa, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Vidura, and Kṛpa; and indeed, to all those brothers who were devoted to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 30 Verse 54. (He also sent Nakula) to Droṇa, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Vidura, and Kṛpa; and indeed, to all those brothers who were devoted to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स गत्वा हास्तिनपुरं नकुलः समितिंजयः ।भीष्ममामन्त्रयामास धृतराष्ट्रं च पाण्डवः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa gatvā hāstinapuraṁ nakulaḥ samitiṁjayaḥ ,bhīṣmamāmantrayāmāsa dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: That Nakula, the Pāṇḍava and conqueror of assemblies, having gone to Hastināpura, then addressed Bhīṣma and Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: That Nakula, the Pāṇḍava and conqueror of assemblies, having gone to Hastināpura, then addressed Bhīṣma and Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: प्रययुः प्रीतमनसो यज्ञं ब्रह्मपुरःसराः ।संश्रुत्य धर्मराजस्य यज्ञं यज्ञविदस्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: prayayuḥ prītamanaso yajñaṁ brahmapuraḥsarāḥ ,saṁśrutya dharmarājasya yajñaṁ yajñavidastadā.
english: Then, having heard about the Vedic ritual (yajña) of Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), those who knew about Vedic rituals (yajña), with pleased minds and with Brahmins at the forefront, went forth to the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 2. Then, having heard about the Vedic ritual (yajña) of Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of natural law (dharma), those who knew about Vedic rituals (yajña), with pleased minds and with Brahmins at the forefront, went forth to the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: अन्ये च शतशस्तुष्टैर्मनोभिर्मनुजर्षभ ।द्रष्टुकामाः सभां चैव धर्मराजं च पाण्डवम् ॥३॥
transliteration: anye ca śataśastuṣṭairmanobhirmanujarṣabha ,draṣṭukāmāḥ sabhāṁ caiva dharmarājaṁ ca pāṇḍavam.
english: O chief of men (manujarṣabha), hundreds of others, with contented minds, desired to see the assembly and also the virtuous king (dharmarāja), the son of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 3. O chief of men (manujarṣabha), hundreds of others, with contented minds, desired to see the assembly and also the virtuous king (dharmarāja), the son of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: दिग्भ्यः सर्वे समापेतुः पार्थिवास्तत्र भारत ।समुपादाय रत्नानि विविधानि महान्ति च ॥४॥
transliteration: digbhyaḥ sarve samāpetuḥ pārthivāstatra bhārata ,samupādāya ratnāni vividhāni mahānti ca.
english: O Bhārata, all the kings (pārthiva) from various directions gathered there, bringing with them many great and diverse jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 4. O Bhārata, all the kings (pārthiva) from various directions gathered there, bringing with them many great and diverse jewels.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रश्च भीष्मश्च विदुरश्च महामतिः ।दुर्योधनपुरोगाश्च भ्रातरः सर्व एव ते ॥५॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭraśca bhīṣmaśca viduraśca mahāmatiḥ ,duryodhanapurogāśca bhrātaraḥ sarva eva te.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Bhīṣma, and the great-minded (mahāmati) Vidura, along with all the brothers, led by Duryodhana, were also there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 5. Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Bhīṣma, and the great-minded (mahāmati) Vidura, along with all the brothers, led by Duryodhana, were also there.

sanskrit: सत्कृत्यामन्त्रिताः सर्वे आचार्यप्रमुखा नृपाः ।गान्धारराजः सुबलः शकुनिश्च महाबलः ॥६॥
transliteration: satkṛtyāmantritāḥ sarve ācāryapramukhā nṛpāḥ ,gāndhārarājaḥ subalaḥ śakuniśca mahābalaḥ.
english: All the kings, led by the preceptor (ācārya), were respectfully invited, including King Subala of Gandhāra, and the mighty (mahābala) Śakuni.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 6. All the kings, led by the preceptor (ācārya), were respectfully invited, including King Subala of Gandhāra, and the mighty (mahābala) Śakuni.

sanskrit: अचलो वृषकश्चैव कर्णश्च रथिनां वरः ।ऋतः शल्यो मद्रराजो बाह्लिकश्च महारथः ॥७॥
transliteration: acalo vṛṣakaścaiva karṇaśca rathināṁ varaḥ ,ṛtaḥ śalyo madrarājo bāhlikaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Achala, Vṛṣaka, and Karṇa, who was the foremost among charioteers. There was also Ṛta, Śalya, the king of Madra, and Bāhlika, a great charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 7. Achala, Vṛṣaka, and Karṇa, who was the foremost among charioteers. There was also Ṛta, Śalya, the king of Madra, and Bāhlika, a great charioteer.

sanskrit: सोमदत्तोऽथ कौरव्यो भूरिर्भूरिश्रवाः शलः ।अश्वत्थामा कृपो द्रोणः सैन्धवश्च जयद्रथः ॥८॥
transliteration: somadatto'tha kauravyo bhūrirbhūriśravāḥ śalaḥ ,aśvatthāmā kṛpo droṇaḥ saindhavaśca jayadrathaḥ.
english: There was Somadatta, and then Kauravya Bhūriśravas (who was also called Bhūri), and Śala. Also, Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, Droṇa, and Jayadratha, the king of Sindhu (Saindhava).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 8. There was Somadatta, and then Kauravya Bhūriśravas (who was also called Bhūri), and Śala. Also, Aśvatthāmā, Kṛpa, Droṇa, and Jayadratha, the king of Sindhu (Saindhava).

sanskrit: यज्ञसेनः सपुत्रश्च शाल्वश्च वसुधाधिपः ।प्राग्ज्योतिषश्च नृपतिर्भगदत्तो महायशाः ॥९॥
transliteration: yajñasenaḥ saputraśca śālvaśca vasudhādhipaḥ ,prāgjyotiṣaśca nṛpatirbhagadatto mahāyaśāḥ.
english: Yajñasena with his son, and Śālva the lord of the earth, and Bhagadatta, the renowned king of Prāgjyotiṣa (were present).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 9. Yajñasena with his son, and Śālva the lord of the earth, and Bhagadatta, the renowned king of Prāgjyotiṣa (were present).

sanskrit: सह सर्वैस्तथा म्लेच्छैः सागरानूपवासिभिः ।पार्वतीयाश्च राजानो राजा चैव बृहद्बलः ॥१०॥
transliteration: saha sarvaistathā mlecchaiḥ sāgarānūpavāsibhiḥ ,pārvatīyāśca rājāno rājā caiva bṛhadbalaḥ.
english: Along with all the Mlecchas, who reside in the marshy regions near the ocean, and the kings from the mountains. Also, King Bṛhadbala himself.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 10. Along with all the Mlecchas, who reside in the marshy regions near the ocean, and the kings from the mountains. Also, King Bṛhadbala himself.

sanskrit: पौण्ड्रको वासुदेवश्च वङ्गः कालिङ्गकस्तथा ।आकर्षः कुन्तलश्चैव वानवास्यान्ध्रकास्तथा ॥११॥
transliteration: pauṇḍrako vāsudevaśca vaṅgaḥ kāliṅgakastathā ,ākarṣaḥ kuntalaścaiva vānavāsyāndhrakāstathā.
english: Pauṇḍraka, Vāsudeva, Vaṅga, Kāliṅgaka, and similarly Ākarṣa, Kuntala, and also the King of Vānavāsa and the Āndhrakas were there, and so were others.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 11. Pauṇḍraka, Vāsudeva, Vaṅga, Kāliṅgaka, and similarly Ākarṣa, Kuntala, and also the King of Vānavāsa and the Āndhrakas were there, and so were others.

sanskrit: द्रविडाः सिंहलाश्चैव राजा काश्मीरकस्तथा ।कुन्तिभोजो महातेजाः सुह्मश्च सुमहाबलः ॥१२॥
transliteration: draviḍāḥ siṁhalāścaiva rājā kāśmīrakastathā ,kuntibhojo mahātejāḥ suhmaśca sumahābalaḥ.
english: The Draviḍas and Siṃhalas, and similarly the king of Kaśmīra; also Kuntibhoja, who was greatly effulgent, and Suhma, who was exceedingly powerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 12. The Draviḍas and Siṃhalas, and similarly the king of Kaśmīra; also Kuntibhoja, who was greatly effulgent, and Suhma, who was exceedingly powerful.

sanskrit: बाह्लिकाश्चापरे शूरा राजानः सर्व एव ते ।विराटः सह पुत्रैश्च माचेल्लश्च महारथः ।राजानो राजपुत्राश्च नानाजनपदेश्वराः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bāhlikāścāpare śūrā rājānaḥ sarva eva te ,virāṭaḥ saha putraiśca mācellaśca mahārathaḥ ,rājāno rājaputrāśca nānājanapadeśvarāḥ.
english: And other brave Bāhlikas, all those kings; Virāṭa along with his sons, and Mācella, a great chariot-warrior; also kings, princes, and rulers of various countries.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 13. And other brave Bāhlikas, all those kings; Virāṭa along with his sons, and Mācella, a great chariot-warrior; also kings, princes, and rulers of various countries.

sanskrit: शिशुपालो महावीर्यः सह पुत्रेण भारत ।आगच्छत्पाण्डवेयस्य यज्ञं संग्रामदुर्मदः ॥१४॥
transliteration: śiśupālo mahāvīryaḥ saha putreṇa bhārata ,āgacchatpāṇḍaveyasya yajñaṁ saṁgrāmadurmadaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, Śiśupāla, of great valor, accompanied by his son, approached the Vedic ritual (yajña) of Yudhiṣṭhira, being unconquerable in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 14. O Bhārata, Śiśupāla, of great valor, accompanied by his son, approached the Vedic ritual (yajña) of Yudhiṣṭhira, being unconquerable in battle.

sanskrit: रामश्चैवानिरुद्धश्च बभ्रुश्च सहसारणः ।गदप्रद्युम्नसाम्बाश्च चारुदेष्णश्च वीर्यवान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: rāmaścaivāniruddhaśca babhruśca sahasāraṇaḥ ,gadapradyumnasāmbāśca cārudeṣṇaśca vīryavān.
english: Rāma, Aniruddha, and Babhru along with Sāraṇa; Gada, Pradyumna, Sāmba, and the mighty Cārudeṣṇa [were present].
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 15. Rāma, Aniruddha, and Babhru along with Sāraṇa; Gada, Pradyumna, Sāmba, and the mighty Cārudeṣṇa [were present].

sanskrit: उल्मुको निशठश्चैव वीरः प्राद्युम्निरेव च ।वृष्णयो निखिलेनान्ये समाजग्मुर्महारथाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: ulmuko niśaṭhaścaiva vīraḥ prādyumnireva ca ,vṛṣṇayo nikhilenānye samājagmurmahārathāḥ.
english: Ulmuka, Niśaṭha too, and the heroic Prādyumni, along with all the other Vṛṣṇis, great charioteers (mahārathāḥ), assembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 16. Ulmuka, Niśaṭha too, and the heroic Prādyumni, along with all the other Vṛṣṇis, great charioteers (mahārathāḥ), assembled.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो राजानो मध्यदेशजाः ।आजग्मुः पाण्डुपुत्रस्य राजसूयं महाक्रतुम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo rājāno madhyadeśajāḥ ,ājagmuḥ pāṇḍuputrasya rājasūyaṁ mahākratum.
english: And these and many other kings from the central region came to the great Rājasūya sacrifice of the son of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 17. And these and many other kings from the central region came to the great Rājasūya sacrifice of the son of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: ददुस्तेषामावसथान्धर्मराजस्य शासनात् ।बहुकक्ष्यान्वितान्राजन्दीर्घिकावृक्षशोभितान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: dadusteṣāmāvasathāndharmarājasya śāsanāt ,bahukakṣyānvitānrājandīrghikāvṛkṣaśobhitān.
english: O king, by the command of Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), they provided them with residences that had many rooms, were adorned with canopies, and were beautified by ponds and trees.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 18. O king, by the command of Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), they provided them with residences that had many rooms, were adorned with canopies, and were beautified by ponds and trees.

sanskrit: तथा धर्मात्मजस्तेषां चक्रे पूजामनुत्तमाम् ।सत्कृताश्च यथोद्दिष्टाञ्जग्मुरावसथान्नृपाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tathā dharmātmajasteṣāṁ cakre pūjāmanuttamām ,satkṛtāśca yathoddiṣṭāñjagmurāvasathānnṛpāḥ.
english: Thus, the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) offered them unsurpassed honor. The kings, having been well-received, then went to the dwellings that had been assigned to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 19. Thus, the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) offered them unsurpassed honor. The kings, having been well-received, then went to the dwellings that had been assigned to them.

sanskrit: कैलासशिखरप्रख्यान्मनोज्ञान्द्रव्यभूषितान् ।सर्वतः संवृतानुच्चैः प्राकारैः सुकृतैः सितैः ॥२०॥
transliteration: kailāsaśikharaprakhyānmanojñāndravyabhūṣitān ,sarvataḥ saṁvṛtānuccaiḥ prākāraiḥ sukṛtaiḥ sitaiḥ.
english: (They went to dwellings) resembling the peaks of Mount Kailāsa, delightful, and adorned with treasures. They were surrounded on all sides by high, well-constructed, white walls.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 20. (They went to dwellings) resembling the peaks of Mount Kailāsa, delightful, and adorned with treasures. They were surrounded on all sides by high, well-constructed, white walls.

sanskrit: सुवर्णजालसंवीतान्मणिकुट्टिमशोभितान् ।सुखारोहणसोपानान्महासनपरिच्छदान् ॥२१॥
transliteration: suvarṇajālasaṁvītānmaṇikuṭṭimaśobhitān ,sukhārohaṇasopānānmahāsanaparicchadān.
english: (They went to dwellings) covered with golden nets, splendid with jeweled pavements, featuring staircases easy to climb, and furnished with magnificent seats.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 21. (They went to dwellings) covered with golden nets, splendid with jeweled pavements, featuring staircases easy to climb, and furnished with magnificent seats.

sanskrit: स्रग्दामसमवच्छन्नानुत्तमागुरुगन्धिनः ।हंसांशुवर्णसदृशानायोजनसुदर्शनान् ॥२२॥
transliteration: sragdāmasamavacchannānuttamāgurugandhinaḥ ,haṁsāṁśuvarṇasadṛśānāyojanasudarśanān.
english: (They went to dwellings) covered abundantly with garlands of flowers, fragrant with the finest agallochum (agarwood), and appearing as white as the rays of swans; they were so beautiful they could be seen clearly from a distance of a yojana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 22. (They went to dwellings) covered abundantly with garlands of flowers, fragrant with the finest agallochum (agarwood), and appearing as white as the rays of swans; they were so beautiful they could be seen clearly from a distance of a yojana.

sanskrit: असंबाधान्समद्वारान्युतानुच्चावचैर्गुणैः ।बहुधातुपिनद्धाङ्गान्हिमवच्छिखरानिव ॥२३॥
transliteration: asaṁbādhānsamadvārānyutānuccāvacairguṇaiḥ ,bahudhātupinaddhāṅgānhimavacchikharāniva.
english: Like the peaks of the Himalayas (Himavat), they were unobstructed, had uniform gateways, were endowed with various excellent qualities, and had their sides bound with many minerals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 23. Like the peaks of the Himalayas (Himavat), they were unobstructed, had uniform gateways, were endowed with various excellent qualities, and had their sides bound with many minerals.

sanskrit: विश्रान्तास्ते ततोऽपश्यन्भूमिपा भूरिदक्षिणम् ।वृतं सदस्यैर्बहुभिर्धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: viśrāntāste tato'paśyanbhūmipā bhūridakṣiṇam ,vṛtaṁ sadasyairbahubhirdharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Having rested, those kings then saw Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), who was surrounded by many members (of the assembly) and was rich in offerings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 24. Having rested, those kings then saw Yudhishthira, the king of righteousness (dharmarāja), who was surrounded by many members (of the assembly) and was rich in offerings.

sanskrit: तत्सदः पार्थिवैः कीर्णं ब्राह्मणैश्च महात्मभिः ।भ्राजते स्म तदा राजन्नाकपृष्ठमिवामरैः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tatsadaḥ pārthivaiḥ kīrṇaṁ brāhmaṇaiśca mahātmabhiḥ ,bhrājate sma tadā rājannākapṛṣṭhamivāmaraiḥ.
english: That assembly hall, O King, filled with kings and great-souled (mahātmā) Brahmins, then shone like the celestial realm (nākapṛṣṭha) with the immortals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 31 Verse 25. That assembly hall, O King, filled with kings and great-souled (mahātmā) Brahmins, then shone like the celestial realm (nākapṛṣṭha) with the immortals.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।पितामहं गुरुं चैव प्रत्युद्गम्य युधिष्ठिरः ।अभिवाद्य ततो राजन्निदं वचनमब्रवीत् ।भीष्मं द्रोणं कृपं द्रौणिं दुर्योधनविविंशती ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pitāmahaṁ guruṁ caiva pratyudgamya yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,abhivādya tato rājannidaṁ vacanamabravīt ,bhīṣmaṁ droṇaṁ kṛpaṁ drauṇiṁ duryodhanaviviṁśatī.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having gone to meet his grandfather and guru, and having greeted them, O King, Yudhishthira then spoke these words (to them, including) Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, Drauṇi, and Duryodhana and Viviṃśati.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Having gone to meet his grandfather and guru, and having greeted them, O King, Yudhishthira then spoke these words (to them, including) Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, Drauṇi, and Duryodhana and Viviṃśati.

sanskrit: अस्मिन्यज्ञे भवन्तो मामनुगृह्णन्तु सर्वशः ।इदं वः स्वमहं चैव यदिहास्ति धनं मम ।प्रीणयन्तु भवन्तो मां यथेष्टमनियन्त्रिताः ॥२॥
transliteration: asminyajñe bhavanto māmanugṛhṇantu sarvaśaḥ ,idaṁ vaḥ svamahaṁ caiva yadihāsti dhanaṁ mama ,prīṇayantu bhavanto māṁ yatheṣṭamaniyantritāḥ.
english: In this ritual (yajña), may all of you, in every way, be gracious to me. This wealth that is here, both yours and mine, may you all, freely and as you desire, satisfy me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 2. In this ritual (yajña), may all of you, in every way, be gracious to me. This wealth that is here, both yours and mine, may you all, freely and as you desire, satisfy me.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा स तान्सर्वान्दीक्षितः पाण्डवाग्रजः ।युयोज ह यथायोगमधिकारेष्वनन्तरम् ॥३॥
transliteration: evamuktvā sa tānsarvāndīkṣitaḥ pāṇḍavāgrajaḥ ,yuyoja ha yathāyogamadhikāreṣvanantaram.
english: Having spoken thus, the initiated elder of the Pāṇḍavas (Yudhiṣṭhira) then appropriately (yoga) assigned all of them to their respective duties.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 3. Having spoken thus, the initiated elder of the Pāṇḍavas (Yudhiṣṭhira) then appropriately (yoga) assigned all of them to their respective duties.

sanskrit: भक्ष्यभोज्याधिकारेषु दुःशासनमयोजयत् ।परिग्रहे ब्राह्मणानामश्वत्थामानमुक्तवान् ॥४॥
transliteration: bhakṣyabhojyādhikāreṣu duḥśāsanamayojayat ,parigrahe brāhmaṇānāmaśvatthāmānamuktavān.
english: He appointed Duḥśāsana to oversee the arrangements for food and drink. For the reception of the Brahmins, he instructed Aśvatthāmā.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 4. He appointed Duḥśāsana to oversee the arrangements for food and drink. For the reception of the Brahmins, he instructed Aśvatthāmā.

sanskrit: राज्ञां तु प्रतिपूजार्थं संजयं संन्ययोजयत् ।कृताकृतपरिज्ञाने भीष्मद्रोणौ महामती ॥५॥
transliteration: rājñāṁ tu pratipūjārthaṁ saṁjayaṁ saṁnyayojayat ,kṛtākṛtaparijñāne bhīṣmadroṇau mahāmatī.
english: As for the honoring of the kings, he appointed Sañjaya. Bhīṣma and Droṇa, both highly intelligent, were appointed to oversee what was done and what was not done (i.e., to ensure correct execution).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 5. As for the honoring of the kings, he appointed Sañjaya. Bhīṣma and Droṇa, both highly intelligent, were appointed to oversee what was done and what was not done (i.e., to ensure correct execution).

sanskrit: हिरण्यस्य सुवर्णस्य रत्नानां चान्ववेक्षणे ।दक्षिणानां च वै दाने कृपं राजा न्ययोजयत् ।तथान्यान्पुरुषव्याघ्रांस्तस्मिंस्तस्मिन्न्ययोजयत् ॥६॥
transliteration: hiraṇyasya suvarṇasya ratnānāṁ cānvavekṣaṇe ,dakṣiṇānāṁ ca vai dāne kṛpaṁ rājā nyayojayat ,tathānyānpuruṣavyāghrāṁstasmiṁstasminnyayojayat.
english: The king appointed Kṛpa for the supervision of gold, fine gold, and jewels, and also for the distribution of sacrificial gifts (dakṣiṇā). Similarly, he appointed other eminent men (puruṣavyāghra) to various tasks.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 6. The king appointed Kṛpa for the supervision of gold, fine gold, and jewels, and also for the distribution of sacrificial gifts (dakṣiṇā). Similarly, he appointed other eminent men (puruṣavyāghra) to various tasks.

sanskrit: बाह्लिको धृतराष्ट्रश्च सोमदत्तो जयद्रथः ।नकुलेन समानीताः स्वामिवत्तत्र रेमिरे ॥७॥
transliteration: bāhliko dhṛtarāṣṭraśca somadatto jayadrathaḥ ,nakulena samānītāḥ svāmivattatra remire.
english: Bāhlika, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Somadatta, and Jayadratha, having been brought there by Nakula, enjoyed themselves as if they were the masters.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 7. Bāhlika, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Somadatta, and Jayadratha, having been brought there by Nakula, enjoyed themselves as if they were the masters.

sanskrit: क्षत्ता व्ययकरस्त्वासीद्विदुरः सर्वधर्मवित् ।दुर्योधनस्त्वर्हणानि प्रतिजग्राह सर्वशः ॥८॥
transliteration: kṣattā vyayakarastvāsīdviduraḥ sarvadharmavit ,duryodhanastvarhaṇāni pratijagrāha sarvaśaḥ.
english: Vidura, who was the chamberlain (kṣattā) and knew all principles of natural law (dharma), served as the treasurer. Duryodhana, however, completely accepted all the offerings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 8. Vidura, who was the chamberlain (kṣattā) and knew all principles of natural law (dharma), served as the treasurer. Duryodhana, however, completely accepted all the offerings.

sanskrit: सर्वलोकः समावृत्तः पिप्रीषुः फलमुत्तमम् ।द्रष्टुकामः सभां चैव धर्मराजं च पाण्डवम् ॥९॥
transliteration: sarvalokaḥ samāvṛttaḥ piprīṣuḥ phalamuttamam ,draṣṭukāmaḥ sabhāṁ caiva dharmarājaṁ ca pāṇḍavam.
english: All people gathered, wishing to obtain the highest reward, and desiring to see both the assembly and the Pāṇḍava, King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 9. All people gathered, wishing to obtain the highest reward, and desiring to see both the assembly and the Pāṇḍava, King Yudhiṣṭhira (dharmarāja).

sanskrit: न कश्चिदाहरत्तत्र सहस्रावरमर्हणम् ।रत्नैश्च बहुभिस्तत्र धर्मराजमवर्धयन् ॥१०॥
transliteration: na kaścidāharattatra sahasrāvaramarhaṇam ,ratnaiśca bahubhistatra dharmarājamavardhayan.
english: No one there brought an offering worth less than a thousand. And with many jewels, they further enriched the king who upholds natural law (dharma-rāja) [Yudhiṣṭhira].
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 10. No one there brought an offering worth less than a thousand. And with many jewels, they further enriched the king who upholds natural law (dharma-rāja) [Yudhiṣṭhira].

sanskrit: कथं नु मम कौरव्यो रत्नदानैः समाप्नुयात् ।यज्ञमित्येव राजानः स्पर्धमाना ददुर्धनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: kathaṁ nu mama kauravyo ratnadānaiḥ samāpnuyāt ,yajñamityeva rājānaḥ spardhamānā dadurdhanam.
english: "How indeed could this Kaurava [Duryodhana] ever equal my (yajña) with his gifts of jewels?" – with such a thought, and competing with each other, the kings gave wealth (for the great Vedic ritual (yajña)).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 11. "How indeed could this Kaurava [Duryodhana] ever equal my (yajña) with his gifts of jewels?" – with such a thought, and competing with each other, the kings gave wealth (for the great Vedic ritual (yajña)).

sanskrit: भवनैः सविमानाग्रैः सोदर्कैर्बलसंवृतैः ।लोकराजविमानैश्च ब्राह्मणावसथैः सह ॥१२॥
transliteration: bhavanaiḥ savimānāgraiḥ sodarkairbalasaṁvṛtaiḥ ,lokarājavimānaiśca brāhmaṇāvasathaiḥ saha.
english: (They gave) magnificent mansions (bhavanāni) that had pinnacles resembling celestial chariots (vimāna), along with high-storied and strongly fortified structures; and also the celestial chariots (vimāna) of world-rulers, accompanied by dwellings for Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 12. (They gave) magnificent mansions (bhavanāni) that had pinnacles resembling celestial chariots (vimāna), along with high-storied and strongly fortified structures; and also the celestial chariots (vimāna) of world-rulers, accompanied by dwellings for Brahmins.

sanskrit: कृतैरावसथैर्दिव्यैर्विमानप्रतिमैस्तथा ।विचित्रै रत्नवद्भिश्च ऋद्ध्या परमया युतैः ॥१३॥
transliteration: kṛtairāvasathairdivyairvimānapratimaistathā ,vicitrai ratnavadbhiśca ṛddhyā paramayā yutaiḥ.
english: (They gave) divine dwellings (āvasatha) that had been constructed, resembling celestial chariots (vimāna), and furthermore, diverse and jewel-studded ones, endowed with supreme prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 13. (They gave) divine dwellings (āvasatha) that had been constructed, resembling celestial chariots (vimāna), and furthermore, diverse and jewel-studded ones, endowed with supreme prosperity.

sanskrit: राजभिश्च समावृत्तैरतीवश्रीसमृद्धिभिः ।अशोभत सदो राजन्कौन्तेयस्य महात्मनः ॥१४॥
transliteration: rājabhiśca samāvṛttairatīvaśrīsamṛddhibhiḥ ,aśobhata sado rājankaunteyasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: O King, the assembly hall of the great-souled Kaunteya (Yudhiṣṭhira) shone splendidly, surrounded by kings who were immensely wealthy and glorious.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 14. O King, the assembly hall of the great-souled Kaunteya (Yudhiṣṭhira) shone splendidly, surrounded by kings who were immensely wealthy and glorious.

sanskrit: ऋद्ध्या च वरुणं देवं स्पर्धमानो युधिष्ठिरः ।षडग्निनाथ यज्ञेन सोऽयजद्दक्षिणावता ।सर्वाञ्जनान्सर्वकामैः समृद्धैः समतर्पयत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: ṛddhyā ca varuṇaṁ devaṁ spardhamāno yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,ṣaḍagninātha yajñena so'yajaddakṣiṇāvatā ,sarvāñjanānsarvakāmaiḥ samṛddhaiḥ samatarpayat.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, competing with the god Varuṇa in prosperity, worshipped (ayajat) with the six-fire Vedic ritual (yajña) which included many offerings (dakṣiṇāvata). He completely satisfied all people with abundant provisions for all their desires.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 15. Yudhiṣṭhira, competing with the god Varuṇa in prosperity, worshipped (ayajat) with the six-fire Vedic ritual (yajña) which included many offerings (dakṣiṇāvata). He completely satisfied all people with abundant provisions for all their desires.

sanskrit: अन्नवान्बहुभक्ष्यश्च भुक्तवज्जनसंवृतः ।रत्नोपहारकर्मण्यो बभूव स समागमः ॥१६॥
transliteration: annavānbahubhakṣyaśca bhuktavajjanasaṁvṛtaḥ ,ratnopahārakarmaṇyo babhūva sa samāgamaḥ.
english: That assembly became one abundant with food and various delicacies, thronged by those who had feasted, and characterized by the activity of offering jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 16. That assembly became one abundant with food and various delicacies, thronged by those who had feasted, and characterized by the activity of offering jewels.

sanskrit: इडाज्यहोमाहुतिभिर्मन्त्रशिक्षासमन्वितैः ।तस्मिन्हि ततृपुर्देवास्तते यज्ञे महर्षिभिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: iḍājyahomāhutibhirmantraśikṣāsamanvitaiḥ ,tasminhi tatṛpurdevāstate yajñe maharṣibhiḥ.
english: Indeed, in that extensive Vedic ritual (yajña), the gods were satisfied by the offerings of melted butter (ājyahomāhutibhiḥ), accompanied by the study of sacred formulas (mantraśikṣā), and by the great sages.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 17. Indeed, in that extensive Vedic ritual (yajña), the gods were satisfied by the offerings of melted butter (ājyahomāhutibhiḥ), accompanied by the study of sacred formulas (mantraśikṣā), and by the great sages.

sanskrit: यथा देवास्तथा विप्रा दक्षिणान्नमहाधनैः ।ततृपुः सर्ववर्णाश्च तस्मिन्यज्ञे मुदान्विताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yathā devāstathā viprā dakṣiṇānnamahādhanaiḥ ,tatṛpuḥ sarvavarṇāśca tasminyajñe mudānvitāḥ.
english: In that ritual (yajña), just as the gods were satisfied, so also were the brahmins, along with all social classes, made joyful and content by the sacrificial fees, food, and great wealth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 32 Verse 18. In that ritual (yajña), just as the gods were satisfied, so also were the brahmins, along with all social classes, made joyful and content by the sacrificial fees, food, and great wealth.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततोऽभिषेचनीयेऽह्नि ब्राह्मणा राजभिः सह ।अन्तर्वेदीं प्रविविशुः सत्कारार्थं महर्षयः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato'bhiṣecanīye'hni brāhmaṇā rājabhiḥ saha ,antarvedīṁ praviviśuḥ satkārārthaṁ maharṣayaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, on the day of the consecration ceremony, the brahmins and the great sages, accompanied by the kings, entered the sacrificial enclosure to receive honor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, on the day of the consecration ceremony, the brahmins and the great sages, accompanied by the kings, entered the sacrificial enclosure to receive honor.

sanskrit: नारदप्रमुखास्तस्यामन्तर्वेद्यां महात्मनः ।समासीनाः शुशुभिरे सह राजर्षिभिस्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: nāradapramukhāstasyāmantarvedyāṁ mahātmanaḥ ,samāsīnāḥ śuśubhire saha rājarṣibhistadā.
english: Then, in that sacrificial enclosure, the great souls (mahātman), with Nārada as their chief, sat together with the royal sages and shone splendidly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 2. Then, in that sacrificial enclosure, the great souls (mahātman), with Nārada as their chief, sat together with the royal sages and shone splendidly.

sanskrit: समेता ब्रह्मभवने देवा देवर्षयो यथा ।कर्मान्तरमुपासन्तो जजल्पुरमितौजसः ॥३॥
transliteration: sametā brahmabhavane devā devarṣayo yathā ,karmāntaramupāsanto jajalpuramitaujasaḥ.
english: The assembled ones of immeasurable energy conversed, just as the gods and divine sages do, gathered in Brahmā's abode (brahmabhavana) while attending to other rituals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 3. The assembled ones of immeasurable energy conversed, just as the gods and divine sages do, gathered in Brahmā's abode (brahmabhavana) while attending to other rituals.

sanskrit: इदमेवं न चाप्येवमेवमेतन्न चान्यथा ।इत्यूचुर्बहवस्तत्र वितण्डानाः परस्परम् ॥४॥
transliteration: idamevaṁ na cāpyevamevametanna cānyathā ,ityūcurbahavastatra vitaṇḍānāḥ parasparam.
english: Many people there argued among themselves, saying, "This is so, and it is not so; it is indeed so, and it is not otherwise."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 4. Many people there argued among themselves, saying, "This is so, and it is not so; it is indeed so, and it is not otherwise."

sanskrit: कृशानर्थांस्तथा केचिदकृशांस्तत्र कुर्वते ।अकृशांश्च कृशांश्चक्रुर्हेतुभिः शास्त्रनिश्चितैः ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛśānarthāṁstathā kecidakṛśāṁstatra kurvate ,akṛśāṁśca kṛśāṁścakrurhetubhiḥ śāstraniścitaiḥ.
english: Some people there would make trivial meanings substantial, and substantial ones trivial, by means of arguments that were definitively established in the scriptures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 5. Some people there would make trivial meanings substantial, and substantial ones trivial, by means of arguments that were definitively established in the scriptures.

sanskrit: तत्र मेधाविनः केचिदर्थमन्यैः प्रपूरितम् ।विचिक्षिपुर्यथा श्येना नभोगतमिवामिषम् ॥६॥
transliteration: tatra medhāvinaḥ kecidarthamanyaiḥ prapūritam ,vicikṣipuryathā śyenā nabhogatamivāmiṣam.
english: Some discerning individuals there tossed aside the meaning that had been fully elaborated by others, just as hawks would scatter a piece of meat that had fallen from the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 6. Some discerning individuals there tossed aside the meaning that had been fully elaborated by others, just as hawks would scatter a piece of meat that had fallen from the sky.

sanskrit: केचिद्धर्मार्थसंयुक्ताः कथास्तत्र महाव्रताः ।रेमिरे कथयन्तश्च सर्ववेदविदां वराः ॥७॥
transliteration: keciddharmārthasaṁyuktāḥ kathāstatra mahāvratāḥ ,remire kathayantaśca sarvavedavidāṁ varāḥ.
english: Some individuals there, who observed great vows and were the foremost among those who knew all the Vedas, found joy in narrating stories that combined discussions on righteousness (dharma) and material pursuits (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 7. Some individuals there, who observed great vows and were the foremost among those who knew all the Vedas, found joy in narrating stories that combined discussions on righteousness (dharma) and material pursuits (artha).

sanskrit: सा वेदिर्वेदसंपन्नैर्देवद्विजमहर्षिभिः ।आबभासे समाकीर्णा नक्षत्रैर्द्यौरिवामला ॥८॥
transliteration: sā vedirvedasaṁpannairdevadvijamaharṣibhiḥ ,ābabhāse samākīrṇā nakṣatrairdyaurivāmalā.
english: That sacrificial altar, teeming with gods, Brahmins (dvija), and great sages (maharṣi) who were accomplished in Vedic knowledge, shone like a clear sky adorned with stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 8. That sacrificial altar, teeming with gods, Brahmins (dvija), and great sages (maharṣi) who were accomplished in Vedic knowledge, shone like a clear sky adorned with stars.

sanskrit: न तस्यां संनिधौ शूद्रः कश्चिदासीन्न चाव्रतः ।अन्तर्वेद्यां तदा राजन्युधिष्ठिरनिवेशने ॥९॥
transliteration: na tasyāṁ saṁnidhau śūdraḥ kaścidāsīnna cāvrataḥ ,antarvedyāṁ tadā rājanyudhiṣṭhiraniveśane.
english: At that time, O King, within Yudhiṣṭhira's abode and the sacrificial enclosure, there was neither any śūdra nor anyone who had not observed the proper vows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 9. At that time, O King, within Yudhiṣṭhira's abode and the sacrificial enclosure, there was neither any śūdra nor anyone who had not observed the proper vows.

sanskrit: तां तु लक्ष्मीवतो लक्ष्मीं तदा यज्ञविधानजाम् ।तुतोष नारदः पश्यन्धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tāṁ tu lakṣmīvato lakṣmīṁ tadā yajñavidhānajām ,tutoṣa nāradaḥ paśyandharmarājasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Indeed, Nārada, seeing that prosperity, which arose from the performance of the Vedic ritual (yajña) and belonged to the wise Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), was delighted.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 10. Indeed, Nārada, seeing that prosperity, which arose from the performance of the Vedic ritual (yajña) and belonged to the wise Dharmarāja (Yudhiṣṭhira), was delighted.

sanskrit: अथ चिन्तां समापेदे स मुनिर्मनुजाधिप ।नारदस्तं तदा पश्यन्सर्वक्षत्रसमागमम् ॥११॥
transliteration: atha cintāṁ samāpede sa munirmanujādhipa ,nāradastaṁ tadā paśyansarvakṣatrasamāgamam.
english: Then, O lord of men, that sage Nārada, observing the gathering of all the kṣatriyas at that time, fell into deep contemplation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 11. Then, O lord of men, that sage Nārada, observing the gathering of all the kṣatriyas at that time, fell into deep contemplation.

sanskrit: सस्मार च पुरावृत्तां कथां तां भरतर्षभ ।अंशावतरणे यासौ ब्रह्मणो भवनेऽभवत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: sasmāra ca purāvṛttāṁ kathāṁ tāṁ bharatarṣabha ,aṁśāvataraṇe yāsau brahmaṇo bhavane'bhavat.
english: O best of Bharatas, he remembered that ancient story concerning the descent of a divine portion, which had occurred in Brahmā's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 12. O best of Bharatas, he remembered that ancient story concerning the descent of a divine portion, which had occurred in Brahmā's abode.

sanskrit: देवानां संगमं तं तु विज्ञाय कुरुनन्दन ।नारदः पुण्डरीकाक्षं सस्मार मनसा हरिम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: devānāṁ saṁgamaṁ taṁ tu vijñāya kurunandana ,nāradaḥ puṇḍarīkākṣaṁ sasmāra manasā harim.
english: O delight of the Kurus, having indeed learned of that gathering of the gods, Narada remembered the lotus-eyed Hari (Viṣṇu) in his mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 13. O delight of the Kurus, having indeed learned of that gathering of the gods, Narada remembered the lotus-eyed Hari (Viṣṇu) in his mind.

sanskrit: साक्षात्स विबुधारिघ्नः क्षत्रे नारायणो विभुः ।प्रतिज्ञां पालयन्धीमाञ्जातः परपुरंजयः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sākṣātsa vibudhārighnaḥ kṣatre nārāyaṇo vibhuḥ ,pratijñāṁ pālayandhīmāñjātaḥ parapuraṁjayaḥ.
english: Indeed, He (Viṣṇu), the omnipresent Narayana, who slays the foes of the gods, was born in person in the warrior (kṣatra) class, wise and a conqueror of enemy cities, thereby fulfilling His promise.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 14. Indeed, He (Viṣṇu), the omnipresent Narayana, who slays the foes of the gods, was born in person in the warrior (kṣatra) class, wise and a conqueror of enemy cities, thereby fulfilling His promise.

sanskrit: संदिदेश पुरा योऽसौ विबुधान्भूतकृत्स्वयम् ।अन्योन्यमभिनिघ्नन्तः पुनर्लोकानवाप्स्यथ ॥१५॥
transliteration: saṁdideśa purā yo'sau vibudhānbhūtakṛtsvayam ,anyonyamabhinighnantaḥ punarlokānavāpsyatha.
english: He who, the creator of beings (bhūtakṛt) himself, had commanded the gods in the past: 'By striking one another, you will again attain the worlds.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 15. He who, the creator of beings (bhūtakṛt) himself, had commanded the gods in the past: 'By striking one another, you will again attain the worlds.'

sanskrit: इति नारायणः शंभुर्भगवाञ्जगतः प्रभुः ।आदिश्य विबुधान्सर्वानजायत यदुक्षये ॥१६॥
transliteration: iti nārāyaṇaḥ śaṁbhurbhagavāñjagataḥ prabhuḥ ,ādiśya vibudhānsarvānajāyata yadukṣaye.
english: Thus, Nārāyaṇa (nārāyaṇaḥ), who is also Śambhu (śaṃbhuḥ), the divine Lord (bhagavān) and master (prabhuḥ) of the universe (jagataḥ), having instructed all the demigods (vibudhān), was born in the dynasty (kṣaye) of Yadu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 16. Thus, Nārāyaṇa (nārāyaṇaḥ), who is also Śambhu (śaṃbhuḥ), the divine Lord (bhagavān) and master (prabhuḥ) of the universe (jagataḥ), having instructed all the demigods (vibudhān), was born in the dynasty (kṣaye) of Yadu.

sanskrit: क्षितावन्धकवृष्णीनां वंशे वंशभृतां वरः ।परया शुशुभे लक्ष्म्या नक्षत्राणामिवोडुराट् ॥१७॥
transliteration: kṣitāvandhakavṛṣṇīnāṁ vaṁśe vaṁśabhṛtāṁ varaḥ ,parayā śuśubhe lakṣmyā nakṣatrāṇāmivoḍurāṭ.
english: On earth (kṣitau), in the dynasty (vaṃśe) of the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis (andhakavṛṣṇīnām), he, the best (varaḥ) among those who uphold their lineage (vaṃśabhṛtām), shone with supreme (parayā) splendor (lakṣmyā), just like the moon (uḍurāṭ) shines among the stars (nakṣatrāṇām).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 17. On earth (kṣitau), in the dynasty (vaṃśe) of the Andhakas and Vṛṣṇis (andhakavṛṣṇīnām), he, the best (varaḥ) among those who uphold their lineage (vaṃśabhṛtām), shone with supreme (parayā) splendor (lakṣmyā), just like the moon (uḍurāṭ) shines among the stars (nakṣatrāṇām).

sanskrit: यस्य बाहुबलं सेन्द्राः सुराः सर्व उपासते ।सोऽयं मानुषवन्नाम हरिरास्तेऽरिमर्दनः ॥१८॥
transliteration: yasya bāhubalaṁ sendrāḥ surāḥ sarva upāsate ,so'yaṁ mānuṣavannāma harirāste'rimardanaḥ.
english: His (yasya) mighty arm (bāhubalam) is worshipped (upāsate) by all (sarve) the gods (surāḥ), including Indra (sendrāḥ). Yet, this same Hari (hariḥ), the vanquisher of foes (arimardanaḥ), now (nāma) lives (āste) as if (vat) he were a mere human (mānuṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 18. His (yasya) mighty arm (bāhubalam) is worshipped (upāsate) by all (sarve) the gods (surāḥ), including Indra (sendrāḥ). Yet, this same Hari (hariḥ), the vanquisher of foes (arimardanaḥ), now (nāma) lives (āste) as if (vat) he were a mere human (mānuṣa).

sanskrit: अहो बत महद्भूतं स्वयंभूर्यदिदं स्वयम् ।आदास्यति पुनः क्षत्रमेवं बलसमन्वितम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: aho bata mahadbhūtaṁ svayaṁbhūryadidaṁ svayam ,ādāsyati punaḥ kṣatramevaṁ balasamanvitam.
english: Oh, alas (aho bata)! What a great (mahat) and astonishing (bhūtam) thing it is, if (yadi) this Self-existent One (svayambhūḥ) Himself (svayam) will again (punar) take away (ādāsyati) the Kṣatriya class (kṣatram) which is thus (evam) endowed with such power (balasamanvitam)!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 19. Oh, alas (aho bata)! What a great (mahat) and astonishing (bhūtam) thing it is, if (yadi) this Self-existent One (svayambhūḥ) Himself (svayam) will again (punar) take away (ādāsyati) the Kṣatriya class (kṣatram) which is thus (evam) endowed with such power (balasamanvitam)!

sanskrit: इत्येतां नारदश्चिन्तां चिन्तयामास धर्मवित् ।हरिं नारायणं ज्ञात्वा यज्ञैरीड्यं तमीश्वरम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: ityetāṁ nāradaścintāṁ cintayāmāsa dharmavit ,hariṁ nārāyaṇaṁ jñātvā yajñairīḍyaṁ tamīśvaram.
english: Narada, the knower of natural law (dharma), having understood that Hari, Narayana, is that Lord who is to be worshipped through Vedic rituals (yajña), thus pondered this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 20. Narada, the knower of natural law (dharma), having understood that Hari, Narayana, is that Lord who is to be worshipped through Vedic rituals (yajña), thus pondered this matter.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्धर्मविदां श्रेष्ठो धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ।महाध्वरे महाबुद्धिस्तस्थौ स बहुमानतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tasmindharmavidāṁ śreṣṭho dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ ,mahādhvare mahābuddhistasthau sa bahumānataḥ.
english: He (Narada), the greatly intelligent one and the foremost among the knowers of natural law (dharma), stood respectfully in that great sacrifice, alongside the wise Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 21. He (Narada), the greatly intelligent one and the foremost among the knowers of natural law (dharma), stood respectfully in that great sacrifice, alongside the wise Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira).

sanskrit: ततो भीष्मोऽब्रवीद्राजन्धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।क्रियतामर्हणं राज्ञां यथार्हमिति भारत ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato bhīṣmo'bravīdrājandharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,kriyatāmarhaṇaṁ rājñāṁ yathārhamiti bhārata.
english: Then Bhishma addressed King Yudhishthira, also known as Dharmaraja, saying, 'O King, let the honors due to the kings be performed appropriately, O descendant of Bharata!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 22. Then Bhishma addressed King Yudhishthira, also known as Dharmaraja, saying, 'O King, let the honors due to the kings be performed appropriately, O descendant of Bharata!'

sanskrit: आचार्यमृत्विजं चैव संयुक्तं च युधिष्ठिर ।स्नातकं च प्रियं चाहुः षडर्घ्यार्हान्नृपं तथा ॥२३॥
transliteration: ācāryamṛtvijaṁ caiva saṁyuktaṁ ca yudhiṣṭhira ,snātakaṁ ca priyaṁ cāhuḥ ṣaḍarghyārhānnṛpaṁ tathā.
english: O Yudhishthira, they say that a preceptor, a ritual priest, a relative (or son-in-law), a student who has completed his studies (snātaka), a dear one, and a king are the six persons who deserve the honorific offering (arghya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 23. O Yudhishthira, they say that a preceptor, a ritual priest, a relative (or son-in-law), a student who has completed his studies (snātaka), a dear one, and a king are the six persons who deserve the honorific offering (arghya).

sanskrit: एतानर्हानभिगतानाहुः संवत्सरोषितान् ।त इमे कालपूगस्य महतोऽस्मानुपागताः ॥२४॥
transliteration: etānarhānabhigatānāhuḥ saṁvatsaroṣitān ,ta ime kālapūgasya mahato'smānupāgatāḥ.
english: It is said that these worthy individuals, who have resided here for a year, have now arrived before us after a considerable passage of time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 24. It is said that these worthy individuals, who have resided here for a year, have now arrived before us after a considerable passage of time.

sanskrit: एषामेकैकशो राजन्नर्घ्यमानीयतामिति ।अथ चैषां वरिष्ठाय समर्थायोपनीयताम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: eṣāmekaikaśo rājannarghyamānīyatāmiti ,atha caiṣāṁ variṣṭhāya samarthāyopanīyatām.
english: O King, let the respectful offering be brought to each of these individually. And moreover, among them, let it be presented to the most distinguished and capable one.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 25. O King, let the respectful offering be brought to each of these individually. And moreover, among them, let it be presented to the most distinguished and capable one.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कस्मै भवान्मन्यतेऽर्घमेकस्मै कुरुनन्दन ।उपनीयमानं युक्तं च तन्मे ब्रूहि पितामह ॥२६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kasmai bhavānmanyate'rghamekasmai kurunandana ,upanīyamānaṁ yuktaṁ ca tanme brūhi pitāmaha.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O delight of the Kurus, Grandfather, please tell me, to whom among these do you consider it appropriate to present the offering as a single individual?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 26. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O delight of the Kurus, Grandfather, please tell me, to whom among these do you consider it appropriate to present the offering as a single individual?'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो भीष्मः शांतनवो बुद्ध्या निश्चित्य भारत ।वार्ष्णेयं मन्यते कृष्णमर्हणीयतमं भुवि ॥२७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato bhīṣmaḥ śāṁtanavo buddhyā niścitya bhārata ,vārṣṇeyaṁ manyate kṛṣṇamarhaṇīyatamaṁ bhuvi.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Then, O descendant of Bharata, Bhishma, son of Śāntanu, having thoughtfully determined, considered Kṛṣṇa, the Vārṣṇeya, to be the most supremely honorable (arhaṇīyatama) one on earth.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 27. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Then, O descendant of Bharata, Bhishma, son of Śāntanu, having thoughtfully determined, considered Kṛṣṇa, the Vārṣṇeya, to be the most supremely honorable (arhaṇīyatama) one on earth.'

sanskrit: एष ह्येषां समेतानां तेजोबलपराक्रमैः ।मध्ये तपन्निवाभाति ज्योतिषामिव भास्करः ॥२८॥
transliteration: eṣa hyeṣāṁ sametānāṁ tejobalaparākramaiḥ ,madhye tapannivābhāti jyotiṣāmiva bhāskaraḥ.
english: Indeed, he shines brightly among these assembled individuals, with his brilliance, strength, and valor, just as the sun glows among the celestial bodies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 28. Indeed, he shines brightly among these assembled individuals, with his brilliance, strength, and valor, just as the sun glows among the celestial bodies.

sanskrit: असूर्यमिव सूर्येण निवातमिव वायुना ।भासितं ह्लादितं चैव कृष्णेनेदं सदो हि नः ॥२९॥
transliteration: asūryamiva sūryeṇa nivātamiva vāyunā ,bhāsitaṁ hlāditaṁ caiva kṛṣṇenedaṁ sado hi naḥ.
english: Indeed, this assembly (sadaḥ) of ours has been illuminated and refreshed by Kṛṣṇa, just as a sunless place is lit by the sun, and a windless place is invigorated by the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 29. Indeed, this assembly (sadaḥ) of ours has been illuminated and refreshed by Kṛṣṇa, just as a sunless place is lit by the sun, and a windless place is invigorated by the wind.

sanskrit: तस्मै भीष्माभ्यनुज्ञातः सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ।उपजह्रेऽथ विधिवद्वार्ष्णेयायार्घ्यमुत्तमम् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tasmai bhīṣmābhyanujñātaḥ sahadevaḥ pratāpavān ,upajahre'tha vidhivadvārṣṇeyāyārghyamuttamam.
english: Then, the mighty Sahadeva, having received permission from Bhishma, properly offered the excellent oblation (arghya) to the Vṛṣṇi descendant (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 30. Then, the mighty Sahadeva, having received permission from Bhishma, properly offered the excellent oblation (arghya) to the Vṛṣṇi descendant (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: प्रतिजग्राह तत्कृष्णः शास्त्रदृष्टेन कर्मणा ।शिशुपालस्तु तां पूजां वासुदेवे न चक्षमे ॥३१॥
transliteration: pratijagrāha tatkṛṣṇaḥ śāstradṛṣṭena karmaṇā ,śiśupālastu tāṁ pūjāṁ vāsudeve na cakṣame.
english: Kṛṣṇa accepted that offering (tat) in an act (karmaṇā) performed according to scriptural injunctions (śāstradṛṣṭena). However, Śiśupāla could not tolerate that homage (pūjām) shown to Vāsudeva.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 31. Kṛṣṇa accepted that offering (tat) in an act (karmaṇā) performed according to scriptural injunctions (śāstradṛṣṭena). However, Śiśupāla could not tolerate that homage (pūjām) shown to Vāsudeva.

sanskrit: स उपालभ्य भीष्मं च धर्मराजं च संसदि ।अपाक्षिपद्वासुदेवं चेदिराजो महाबलः ॥३२॥
transliteration: sa upālabhya bhīṣmaṁ ca dharmarājaṁ ca saṁsadi ,apākṣipadvāsudevaṁ cedirājo mahābalaḥ.
english: The mighty king of Chedi, having reproached both Bhishma and Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) in the assembly, then condemned Vasudeva (Krishna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 33 Verse 32. The mighty king of Chedi, having reproached both Bhishma and Dharmaraja (Yudhishthira) in the assembly, then condemned Vasudeva (Krishna).

sanskrit: शिशुपाल उवाच ।नायमर्हति वार्ष्णेयस्तिष्ठत्स्विह महात्मसु ।महीपतिषु कौरव्य राजवत्पार्थिवार्हणम् ॥१॥
transliteration: śiśupāla uvāca ,nāyamarhati vārṣṇeyastiṣṭhatsviha mahātmasu ,mahīpatiṣu kauravya rājavatpārthivārhaṇam.
english: Shishupala said, "O scion of Kuru (Yudhishthira), this descendant of Vrishni (Krishna) does not deserve the honor accorded to a king, especially while these great-souled kings are present here."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 1. Shishupala said, "O scion of Kuru (Yudhishthira), this descendant of Vrishni (Krishna) does not deserve the honor accorded to a king, especially while these great-souled kings are present here."

sanskrit: नायं युक्तः समाचारः पाण्डवेषु महात्मसु ।यत्कामात्पुण्डरीकाक्षं पाण्डवार्चितवानसि ॥२॥
transliteration: nāyaṁ yuktaḥ samācāraḥ pāṇḍaveṣu mahātmasu ,yatkāmātpuṇḍarīkākṣaṁ pāṇḍavārcitavānasi.
english: "This conduct is not fitting for the great-souled Pandavas, that out of desire, you have honored the lotus-eyed (Krishna)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 2. "This conduct is not fitting for the great-souled Pandavas, that out of desire, you have honored the lotus-eyed (Krishna)."

sanskrit: बाला यूयं न जानीध्वं धर्मः सूक्ष्मो हि पाण्डवाः ।अयं तत्राभ्यतिक्रान्त आपगेयोऽल्पदर्शनः ॥३॥
transliteration: bālā yūyaṁ na jānīdhvaṁ dharmaḥ sūkṣmo hi pāṇḍavāḥ ,ayaṁ tatrābhyatikrānta āpageyo'lpadarśanaḥ.
english: "You (Pandavas) are but children and do not understand, for (natural law) dharma is indeed subtle. This son of Ganga (Bhishma) of limited vision has transgressed in that matter."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 3. "You (Pandavas) are but children and do not understand, for (natural law) dharma is indeed subtle. This son of Ganga (Bhishma) of limited vision has transgressed in that matter."

sanskrit: त्वादृशो धर्मयुक्तो हि कुर्वाणः प्रियकाम्यया ।भवत्यभ्यधिकं भीष्मो लोकेष्ववमतः सताम् ॥४॥
transliteration: tvādṛśo dharmayukto hi kurvāṇaḥ priyakāmyayā ,bhavatyabhyadhikaṁ bhīṣmo lokeṣvavamataḥ satām.
english: One like you, who is indeed endowed with righteousness (dharma), performing actions out of desire for dear ones, Bhishma, becomes greatly disrespected by virtuous people in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 4. One like you, who is indeed endowed with righteousness (dharma), performing actions out of desire for dear ones, Bhishma, becomes greatly disrespected by virtuous people in the world.

sanskrit: कथं ह्यराजा दाशार्हो मध्ये सर्वमहीक्षिताम् ।अर्हणामर्हति तथा यथा युष्माभिरर्चितः ॥५॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hyarājā dāśārho madhye sarvamahīkṣitām ,arhaṇāmarhati tathā yathā yuṣmābhirarcitaḥ.
english: How, indeed, does Daśārha (Krishna), who is not a king, deserve such honor among all the monarchs as he has been worshipped by all of you?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 5. How, indeed, does Daśārha (Krishna), who is not a king, deserve such honor among all the monarchs as he has been worshipped by all of you?

sanskrit: अथ वा मन्यसे कृष्णं स्थविरं भरतर्षभ ।वसुदेवे स्थिते वृद्धे कथमर्हति तत्सुतः ॥६॥
transliteration: atha vā manyase kṛṣṇaṁ sthaviraṁ bharatarṣabha ,vasudeve sthite vṛddhe kathamarhati tatsutaḥ.
english: Or, O best of Bharatas, if you consider Krishna an elder, how can that son (Krishna) be worthy of honor when Vasudeva, an old man, is still present?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 6. Or, O best of Bharatas, if you consider Krishna an elder, how can that son (Krishna) be worthy of honor when Vasudeva, an old man, is still present?

sanskrit: अथ वा वासुदेवोऽपि प्रियकामोऽनुवृत्तवान् ।द्रुपदे तिष्ठति कथं माधवोऽर्हति पूजनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: atha vā vāsudevo'pi priyakāmo'nuvṛttavān ,drupade tiṣṭhati kathaṁ mādhavo'rhati pūjanam.
english: Or, even if Vasudeva (Krishna) has acted out of a desire for his beloved ones, how can Madhava (Krishna) deserve adoration when Drupada is present?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 7. Or, even if Vasudeva (Krishna) has acted out of a desire for his beloved ones, how can Madhava (Krishna) deserve adoration when Drupada is present?

sanskrit: आचार्यं मन्यसे कृष्णमथ वा कुरुपुंगव ।द्रोणे तिष्ठति वार्ष्णेयं कस्मादर्चितवानसि ॥८॥
transliteration: ācāryaṁ manyase kṛṣṇamatha vā kurupuṁgava ,droṇe tiṣṭhati vārṣṇeyaṁ kasmādarcitavānasi.
english: O best among the Kurus, do you consider Krishna a teacher? Or why have you honored him, the descendant of Vrishni, when Drona is present?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 8. O best among the Kurus, do you consider Krishna a teacher? Or why have you honored him, the descendant of Vrishni, when Drona is present?

sanskrit: ऋत्विजं मन्यसे कृष्णमथ वा कुरुनन्दन ।द्वैपायने स्थिते विप्रे कथं कृष्णोऽर्चितस्त्वया ॥९॥
transliteration: ṛtvijaṁ manyase kṛṣṇamatha vā kurunandana ,dvaipāyane sthite vipre kathaṁ kṛṣṇo'rcitastvayā.
english: O delight of the Kurus, do you consider Krishna a priest? Or, how was Krishna honored by you when the Brahmin Dvaipayana is present?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 9. O delight of the Kurus, do you consider Krishna a priest? Or, how was Krishna honored by you when the Brahmin Dvaipayana is present?

sanskrit: नैव ऋत्विङ्न चाचार्यो न राजा मधुसूदनः ।अर्चितश्च कुरुश्रेष्ठ किमन्यत्प्रियकाम्यया ॥१०॥
transliteration: naiva ṛtviṅna cācāryo na rājā madhusūdanaḥ ,arcitaśca kuruśreṣṭha kimanyatpriyakāmyayā.
english: Madhusudana (Krishna) is neither a priest, nor a teacher, nor a king. And yet he has been honored, O best of the Kurus. What other reason could there be but the desire for affection?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 10. Madhusudana (Krishna) is neither a priest, nor a teacher, nor a king. And yet he has been honored, O best of the Kurus. What other reason could there be but the desire for affection?

sanskrit: अथ वाप्यर्चनीयोऽयं युष्माकं मधुसूदनः ।किं राजभिरिहानीतैरवमानाय भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: atha vāpyarcanīyo'yaṁ yuṣmākaṁ madhusūdanaḥ ,kiṁ rājabhirihānītairavamānāya bhārata.
english: Or perhaps this Madhusudana (Krishna) is indeed worthy of honor by all of you. Then, O Bharata, why were these kings brought here, only to be insulted?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 11. Or perhaps this Madhusudana (Krishna) is indeed worthy of honor by all of you. Then, O Bharata, why were these kings brought here, only to be insulted?

sanskrit: वयं तु न भयादस्य कौन्तेयस्य महात्मनः ।प्रयच्छामः करान्सर्वे न लोभान्न च सान्त्वनात् ॥१२॥
transliteration: vayaṁ tu na bhayādasya kaunteyasya mahātmanaḥ ,prayacchāmaḥ karānsarve na lobhānna ca sāntvanāt.
english: We all give taxes, not out of fear of this great-souled son of Kunti (Arjuna), nor out of greed, nor out of appeasement.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 12. We all give taxes, not out of fear of this great-souled son of Kunti (Arjuna), nor out of greed, nor out of appeasement.

sanskrit: अस्य धर्मप्रवृत्तस्य पार्थिवत्वं चिकीर्षतः ।करानस्मै प्रयच्छामः सोऽयमस्मान्न मन्यते ॥१३॥
transliteration: asya dharmapravṛttasya pārthivatvaṁ cikīrṣataḥ ,karānasmai prayacchāmaḥ so'yamasmānna manyate.
english: We give taxes to him who is engaged in righteousness (dharma) and desires kingship, but this very person does not respect us.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 13. We give taxes to him who is engaged in righteousness (dharma) and desires kingship, but this very person does not respect us.

sanskrit: किमन्यदवमानाद्धि यदिमं राजसंसदि ।अप्राप्तलक्षणं कृष्णमर्घ्येणार्चितवानसि ॥१४॥
transliteration: kimanyadavamānāddhi yadimaṁ rājasaṁsadi ,aprāptalakṣaṇaṁ kṛṣṇamarghyeṇārcitavānasi.
english: What else is there but disgrace, when you honored this Krishna, who is devoid of royal characteristics, with a special offering (arghya) in the royal assembly?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 14. What else is there but disgrace, when you honored this Krishna, who is devoid of royal characteristics, with a special offering (arghya) in the royal assembly?

sanskrit: अकस्माद्धर्मपुत्रस्य धर्मात्मेति यशो गतम् ।को हि धर्मच्युते पूजामेवं युक्तां प्रयोजयेत् ।योऽयं वृष्णिकुले जातो राजानं हतवान्पुरा ॥१५॥
transliteration: akasmāddharmaputrasya dharmātmeti yaśo gatam ,ko hi dharmacyute pūjāmevaṁ yuktāṁ prayojayet ,yo'yaṁ vṛṣṇikule jāto rājānaṁ hatavānpurā.
english: Suddenly, the fame of the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) as 'the one whose essential nature (ātman) is righteousness (dharma)' has arisen. Indeed, who would offer such appropriate honor to one fallen from righteousness (dharma)? (Especially) this one, born in the Vṛṣṇi clan, who formerly killed a king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 15. Suddenly, the fame of the son of Dharma (Yudhiṣṭhira) as 'the one whose essential nature (ātman) is righteousness (dharma)' has arisen. Indeed, who would offer such appropriate honor to one fallen from righteousness (dharma)? (Especially) this one, born in the Vṛṣṇi clan, who formerly killed a king.

sanskrit: अद्य धर्मात्मता चैव व्यपकृष्टा युधिष्ठिरात् ।कृपणत्वं निविष्टं च कृष्णेऽर्घ्यस्य निवेदनात् ॥१६॥
transliteration: adya dharmātmatā caiva vyapakṛṣṭā yudhiṣṭhirāt ,kṛpaṇatvaṁ niviṣṭaṁ ca kṛṣṇe'rghyasya nivedanāt.
english: Indeed, today, the intrinsic nature of righteousness (dharmātmatā) has been removed from Yudhishthira. And a pitiable state has settled upon Krishna due to the presentation of the respectful offering to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 16. Indeed, today, the intrinsic nature of righteousness (dharmātmatā) has been removed from Yudhishthira. And a pitiable state has settled upon Krishna due to the presentation of the respectful offering to him.

sanskrit: यदि भीताश्च कौन्तेयाः कृपणाश्च तपस्विनः ।ननु त्वयापि बोद्धव्यं यां पूजां माधवोऽर्हति ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadi bhītāśca kaunteyāḥ kṛpaṇāśca tapasvinaḥ ,nanu tvayāpi boddhavyaṁ yāṁ pūjāṁ mādhavo'rhati.
english: If the sons of Kunti are fearful and pitiable ascetics (tapasvinaḥ), then surely even you should understand what kind of honor Madhava truly deserves.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 17. If the sons of Kunti are fearful and pitiable ascetics (tapasvinaḥ), then surely even you should understand what kind of honor Madhava truly deserves.

sanskrit: अथ वा कृपणैरेतामुपनीतां जनार्दन ।पूजामनर्हः कस्मात्त्वमभ्यनुज्ञातवानसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: atha vā kṛpaṇairetāmupanītāṁ janārdana ,pūjāmanarhaḥ kasmāttvamabhyanujñātavānasi.
english: Or else, O Janardana, why have you, though unworthy, permitted this honor presented by the pitiable?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 18. Or else, O Janardana, why have you, though unworthy, permitted this honor presented by the pitiable?

sanskrit: अयुक्तामात्मनः पूजां त्वं पुनर्बहु मन्यसे ।हविषः प्राप्य निष्यन्दं प्राशितुं श्वेव निर्जने ॥१९॥
transliteration: ayuktāmātmanaḥ pūjāṁ tvaṁ punarbahu manyase ,haviṣaḥ prāpya niṣyandaṁ prāśituṁ śveva nirjane.
english: But you, however, greatly value this honor, unfitting for yourself, just like a dog in a desolate place tastes the drippings of a sacrificial offering (haviṣ).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 19. But you, however, greatly value this honor, unfitting for yourself, just like a dog in a desolate place tastes the drippings of a sacrificial offering (haviṣ).

sanskrit: न त्वयं पार्थिवेन्द्राणामवमानः प्रयुज्यते ।त्वामेव कुरवो व्यक्तं प्रलम्भन्ते जनार्दन ॥२०॥
transliteration: na tvayaṁ pārthivendrāṇāmavamānaḥ prayujyate ,tvāmeva kuravo vyaktaṁ pralambhante janārdana.
english: This disrespect is certainly not shown to these kings. Indeed, O Janardana (janārdana), the Kurus are clearly deceiving you alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 20. This disrespect is certainly not shown to these kings. Indeed, O Janardana (janārdana), the Kurus are clearly deceiving you alone.

sanskrit: क्लीबे दारक्रिया यादृगन्धे वा रूपदर्शनम् ।अराज्ञो राजवत्पूजा तथा ते मधुसूदन ॥२१॥
transliteration: klībe dārakriyā yādṛgandhe vā rūpadarśanam ,arājño rājavatpūjā tathā te madhusūdana.
english: O Madhusudana (madhusūdana), just as the act of marriage (dārakriyā) is for an impotent man, or seeing a beautiful form (rūpadarśanam) is for a blind man, so too is king-like worship (pūjā) for one who is not a king for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 21. O Madhusudana (madhusūdana), just as the act of marriage (dārakriyā) is for an impotent man, or seeing a beautiful form (rūpadarśanam) is for a blind man, so too is king-like worship (pūjā) for one who is not a king for you.

sanskrit: दृष्टो युधिष्ठिरो राजा दृष्टो भीष्मश्च यादृशः ।वासुदेवोऽप्ययं दृष्टः सर्वमेतद्यथातथम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭo yudhiṣṭhiro rājā dṛṣṭo bhīṣmaśca yādṛśaḥ ,vāsudevo'pyayaṁ dṛṣṭaḥ sarvametadyathātatham.
english: King Yudhishthira has been seen, and Bhishma too has been seen, just as this Vasudeva (vāsudeva) has also been seen. All of this is exactly as it truly is.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 22. King Yudhishthira has been seen, and Bhishma too has been seen, just as this Vasudeva (vāsudeva) has also been seen. All of this is exactly as it truly is.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा शिशुपालस्तानुत्थाय परमासनात् ।निर्ययौ सदसस्तस्मात्सहितो राजभिस्तदा ॥२३॥
transliteration: ityuktvā śiśupālastānutthāya paramāsanāt ,niryayau sadasastasmātsahito rājabhistadā.
english: Having said this, Shishupala then rose from his prominent seat and departed from that assembly, accompanied by the other kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 34 Verse 23. Having said this, Shishupala then rose from his prominent seat and departed from that assembly, accompanied by the other kings.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो युधिष्ठिरो राजा शिशुपालमुपाद्रवत् ।उवाच चैनं मधुरं सान्त्वपूर्वमिदं वचः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato yudhiṣṭhiro rājā śiśupālamupādravat ,uvāca cainaṁ madhuraṁ sāntvapūrvamidaṁ vacaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira approached Śiśupāla and spoke these gentle, conciliatory words to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then King Yudhiṣṭhira approached Śiśupāla and spoke these gentle, conciliatory words to him.

sanskrit: नेदं युक्तं महीपाल यादृशं वै त्वमुक्तवान् ।अधर्मश्च परो राजन्पारुष्यं च निरर्थकम् ॥२॥
transliteration: nedaṁ yuktaṁ mahīpāla yādṛśaṁ vai tvamuktavān ,adharmaśca paro rājanpāruṣyaṁ ca nirarthakam.
english: O King, what you have said is not proper. O King, it is great unrighteousness (adharma) and useless harshness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 2. O King, what you have said is not proper. O King, it is great unrighteousness (adharma) and useless harshness.

sanskrit: न हि धर्मं परं जातु नावबुध्येत पार्थिव ।भीष्मः शांतनवस्त्वेनं मावमंस्था अतोऽन्यथा ॥३॥
transliteration: na hi dharmaṁ paraṁ jātu nāvabudhyeta pārthiva ,bhīṣmaḥ śāṁtanavastvenaṁ māvamaṁsthā ato'nyathā.
english: O King, Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, surely knows the highest (dharma) (natural law) and would never fail to understand it. Therefore, do not disrespect him, otherwise (there will be consequences).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 3. O King, Bhīṣma, the son of Śāntanu, surely knows the highest (dharma) (natural law) and would never fail to understand it. Therefore, do not disrespect him, otherwise (there will be consequences).

sanskrit: पश्य चेमान्महीपालांस्त्वत्तो वृद्धतमान्बहून् ।मृष्यन्ते चार्हणां कृष्णे तद्वत्त्वं क्षन्तुमर्हसि ॥४॥
transliteration: paśya cemānmahīpālāṁstvatto vṛddhatamānbahūn ,mṛṣyante cārhaṇāṁ kṛṣṇe tadvattvaṁ kṣantumarhasi.
english: See also these many kings, much older than you, who tolerate the honor shown to Kṛṣṇa. You too should likewise tolerate it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 4. See also these many kings, much older than you, who tolerate the honor shown to Kṛṣṇa. You too should likewise tolerate it.

sanskrit: वेद तत्त्वेन कृष्णं हि भीष्मश्चेदिपते भृशम् ।न ह्येनं त्वं तथा वेत्थ यथैनं वेद कौरवः ॥५॥
transliteration: veda tattvena kṛṣṇaṁ hi bhīṣmaścedipate bhṛśam ,na hyenaṁ tvaṁ tathā vettha yathainaṁ veda kauravaḥ.
english: O King of Chedi, Bhishma indeed knows Krishna (kṛṣṇa) truly and profoundly. You do not know him as well as Bhishma (the Kaurava) knows him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 5. O King of Chedi, Bhishma indeed knows Krishna (kṛṣṇa) truly and profoundly. You do not know him as well as Bhishma (the Kaurava) knows him.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।नास्मा अनुनयो देयो नायमर्हति सान्त्वनम् ।लोकवृद्धतमे कृष्णे योऽर्हणां नानुमन्यते ॥६॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,nāsmā anunayo deyo nāyamarhati sāntvanam ,lokavṛddhatame kṛṣṇe yo'rhaṇāṁ nānumanyate.
english: Bhishma said: No conciliation should be offered to this one (Shishupala), nor does he deserve appeasement, for he does not acknowledge the worship (arhaṇā) bestowed upon Krishna (kṛṣṇa), who is the greatest and most revered in the world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 6. Bhishma said: No conciliation should be offered to this one (Shishupala), nor does he deserve appeasement, for he does not acknowledge the worship (arhaṇā) bestowed upon Krishna (kṛṣṇa), who is the greatest and most revered in the world.

sanskrit: क्षत्रियः क्षत्रियं जित्वा रणे रणकृतां वरः ।यो मुञ्चति वशे कृत्वा गुरुर्भवति तस्य सः ॥७॥
transliteration: kṣatriyaḥ kṣatriyaṁ jitvā raṇe raṇakṛtāṁ varaḥ ,yo muñcati vaśe kṛtvā gururbhavati tasya saḥ.
english: The preeminent warrior (kṣatriya) who, after conquering another warrior in battle and bringing him under his control, then releases him, that victor becomes like a revered teacher (guru) to the defeated.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 7. The preeminent warrior (kṣatriya) who, after conquering another warrior in battle and bringing him under his control, then releases him, that victor becomes like a revered teacher (guru) to the defeated.

sanskrit: अस्यां च समितौ राज्ञामेकमप्यजितं युधि ।न पश्यामि महीपालं सात्वतीपुत्रतेजसा ॥८॥
transliteration: asyāṁ ca samitau rājñāmekamapyajitaṁ yudhi ,na paśyāmi mahīpālaṁ sātvatīputratejasā.
english: And in this assembly of kings, I do not see even one king who has remained unconquered in battle, due to the power (tejas) of the son of Sātvatī (Krishna (kṛṣṇa)).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 8. And in this assembly of kings, I do not see even one king who has remained unconquered in battle, due to the power (tejas) of the son of Sātvatī (Krishna (kṛṣṇa)).

sanskrit: न हि केवलमस्माकमयमर्च्यतमोऽच्युतः ।त्रयाणामपि लोकानामर्चनीयो जनार्दनः ॥९॥
transliteration: na hi kevalamasmākamayamarcyatamo'cyutaḥ ,trayāṇāmapi lokānāmarcanīyo janārdanaḥ.
english: Indeed, this Acyuta (acyuta) is not only the most revered among us, but Janardana (janārdana) is also worthy of worship by all three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 9. Indeed, this Acyuta (acyuta) is not only the most revered among us, but Janardana (janārdana) is also worthy of worship by all three worlds.

sanskrit: कृष्णेन हि जिता युद्धे बहवः क्षत्रियर्षभाः ।जगत्सर्वं च वार्ष्णेये निखिलेन प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇena hi jitā yuddhe bahavaḥ kṣatriyarṣabhāḥ ,jagatsarvaṁ ca vārṣṇeye nikhilena pratiṣṭhitam.
english: Indeed, many leading warriors (kṣatriyarṣabha) were conquered by Krishna in battle. And the entire universe (jagat) is fully established in Vārṣṇeya (vārṣṇeya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 10. Indeed, many leading warriors (kṣatriyarṣabha) were conquered by Krishna in battle. And the entire universe (jagat) is fully established in Vārṣṇeya (vārṣṇeya).

sanskrit: तस्मात्सत्स्वपि वृद्धेषु कृष्णमर्चाम नेतरान् ।एवं वक्तुं न चार्हस्त्वं मा भूत्ते बुद्धिरीदृशी ॥११॥
transliteration: tasmātsatsvapi vṛddheṣu kṛṣṇamarcāma netarān ,evaṁ vaktuṁ na cārhastvaṁ mā bhūtte buddhirīdṛśī.
english: Therefore, even with virtuous elders present, we worship Krishna and no one else. You should not speak like this, and may such a thought (buddhi) not arise in your mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 11. Therefore, even with virtuous elders present, we worship Krishna and no one else. You should not speak like this, and may such a thought (buddhi) not arise in your mind.

sanskrit: ज्ञानवृद्धा मया राजन्बहवः पर्युपासिताः ।तेषां कथयतां शौरेरहं गुणवतो गुणान् ।समागतानामश्रौषं बहून्बहुमतान्सताम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: jñānavṛddhā mayā rājanbahavaḥ paryupāsitāḥ ,teṣāṁ kathayatāṁ śaurerahaṁ guṇavato guṇān ,samāgatānāmaśrauṣaṁ bahūnbahumatānsatām.
english: O King, I have served and honored many elders who are rich in knowledge (jñānavṛddha). While they were assembled and narrating the many highly esteemed qualities of the virtuous Śauri (śaure), I heard these from many righteous people (sat).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 12. O King, I have served and honored many elders who are rich in knowledge (jñānavṛddha). While they were assembled and narrating the many highly esteemed qualities of the virtuous Śauri (śaure), I heard these from many righteous people (sat).

sanskrit: कर्माण्यपि च यान्यस्य जन्मप्रभृति धीमतः ।बहुशः कथ्यमानानि नरैर्भूयः श्रुतानि मे ॥१३॥
transliteration: karmāṇyapi ca yānyasya janmaprabhṛti dhīmataḥ ,bahuśaḥ kathyamānāni narairbhūyaḥ śrutāni me.
english: And I have repeatedly heard about the many actions (karma) of this intelligent one, which have been narrated by people since his birth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 13. And I have repeatedly heard about the many actions (karma) of this intelligent one, which have been narrated by people since his birth.

sanskrit: न केवलं वयं कामाच्चेदिराज जनार्दनम् ।न संबन्धं पुरस्कृत्य कृतार्थं वा कथंचन ॥१४॥
transliteration: na kevalaṁ vayaṁ kāmāccedirāja janārdanam ,na saṁbandhaṁ puraskṛtya kṛtārthaṁ vā kathaṁcana.
english: O King of Chedi, we do not [honor] Janārdana merely out of desire, nor by emphasizing any relationship, nor by having achieved any purpose (karma) through him in any way.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 14. O King of Chedi, we do not [honor] Janārdana merely out of desire, nor by emphasizing any relationship, nor by having achieved any purpose (karma) through him in any way.

sanskrit: अर्चामहेऽर्चितं सद्भिर्भुवि भौमसुखावहम् ।यशः शौर्यं जयं चास्य विज्ञायार्चां प्रयुज्महे ॥१५॥
transliteration: arcāmahe'rcitaṁ sadbhirbhuvi bhaumasukhāvaham ,yaśaḥ śauryaṁ jayaṁ cāsya vijñāyārcāṁ prayujmahe.
english: We worship him, who is honored by the virtuous on earth and brings happiness to the world. It is by understanding his fame, valor, and victory that we engage in his worship.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 15. We worship him, who is honored by the virtuous on earth and brings happiness to the world. It is by understanding his fame, valor, and victory that we engage in his worship.

sanskrit: न हि कश्चिदिहास्माभिः सुबालोऽप्यपरीक्षितः ।गुणैर्वृद्धानतिक्रम्य हरिरर्च्यतमो मतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: na hi kaścidihāsmābhiḥ subālo'pyaparīkṣitaḥ ,guṇairvṛddhānatikramya harirarcyatamo mataḥ.
english: Indeed, here, no one among us, not even a very young child, has been unexamined. Hari is considered the most worshipable due to his qualities, surpassing even the elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 16. Indeed, here, no one among us, not even a very young child, has been unexamined. Hari is considered the most worshipable due to his qualities, surpassing even the elders.

sanskrit: ज्ञानवृद्धो द्विजातीनां क्षत्रियाणां बलाधिकः ।पूज्ये ताविह गोविन्दे हेतू द्वावपि संस्थितौ ॥१७॥
transliteration: jñānavṛddho dvijātīnāṁ kṣatriyāṇāṁ balādhikaḥ ,pūjye tāviha govinde hetū dvāvapi saṁsthitau.
english: Among the twice-born (dvijāti), one is revered for wisdom; among the warriors (kṣatriya), for superior strength. Both these qualifications for reverence are indeed perfectly established in Govinda.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 17. Among the twice-born (dvijāti), one is revered for wisdom; among the warriors (kṣatriya), for superior strength. Both these qualifications for reverence are indeed perfectly established in Govinda.

sanskrit: वेदवेदाङ्गविज्ञानं बलं चाप्यमितं तथा ।नृणां हि लोके कस्यास्ति विशिष्टं केशवादृते ॥१८॥
transliteration: vedavedāṅgavijñānaṁ balaṁ cāpyamitaṁ tathā ,nṛṇāṁ hi loke kasyāsti viśiṣṭaṁ keśavādṛte.
english: Indeed, in this world, among men, who else but Keśava possesses such distinguished knowledge of the Vedas and Vedangas, as well as immeasurable strength?
