search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 18. Indeed, in this world, among men, who else but Keśava possesses such distinguished knowledge of the Vedas and Vedangas, as well as immeasurable strength?

sanskrit: दानं दाक्ष्यं श्रुतं शौर्यं ह्रीः कीर्तिर्बुद्धिरुत्तमा ।संनतिः श्रीर्धृतिस्तुष्टिः पुष्टिश्च नियताच्युते ॥१९॥
transliteration: dānaṁ dākṣyaṁ śrutaṁ śauryaṁ hrīḥ kīrtirbuddhiruttamā ,saṁnatiḥ śrīrdhṛtistuṣṭiḥ puṣṭiśca niyatācyute.
english: Generosity (dāna), skill, learning, valor, modesty, fame, supreme intelligence, humility, prosperity, fortitude, contentment, and fullness - all these are firmly established in Acyuta.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 19. Generosity (dāna), skill, learning, valor, modesty, fame, supreme intelligence, humility, prosperity, fortitude, contentment, and fullness - all these are firmly established in Acyuta.

sanskrit: तमिमं सर्वसंपन्नमाचार्यं पितरं गुरुम् ।अर्च्यमर्चितमर्चार्हं सर्वे संमन्तुमर्हथ ॥२०॥
transliteration: tamimaṁ sarvasaṁpannamācāryaṁ pitaraṁ gurum ,arcyamarcitamarcārhaṁ sarve saṁmantumarhatha.
english: All of you ought to assent to him - this preceptor (ācārya), father, and venerable teacher (guru) - who is endowed with all virtues, worthy of reverence, already revered, and deserving of worship.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 20. All of you ought to assent to him - this preceptor (ācārya), father, and venerable teacher (guru) - who is endowed with all virtues, worthy of reverence, already revered, and deserving of worship.

sanskrit: ऋत्विग्गुरुर्विवाह्यश्च स्नातको नृपतिः प्रियः ।सर्वमेतद्धृषीकेशे तस्मादभ्यर्चितोऽच्युतः ॥२१॥
transliteration: ṛtviggururvivāhyaśca snātako nṛpatiḥ priyaḥ ,sarvametaddhṛṣīkeśe tasmādabhyarcito'cyutaḥ.
english: The priest, the spiritual teacher (guru), the groom, the Vedic graduate (snātaka), the king, and the dear friend – all these are Hṛṣīkeśa himself. Therefore, Acyuta should be worshipped.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 21. The priest, the spiritual teacher (guru), the groom, the Vedic graduate (snātaka), the king, and the dear friend – all these are Hṛṣīkeśa himself. Therefore, Acyuta should be worshipped.

sanskrit: कृष्ण एव हि लोकानामुत्पत्तिरपि चाप्ययः ।कृष्णस्य हि कृते भूतमिदं विश्वं समर्पितम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa eva hi lokānāmutpattirapi cāpyayaḥ ,kṛṣṇasya hi kṛte bhūtamidaṁ viśvaṁ samarpitam.
english: Krishna is indeed the origin and also the dissolution of all worlds. This entire universe was created and dedicated for Krishna's sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 22. Krishna is indeed the origin and also the dissolution of all worlds. This entire universe was created and dedicated for Krishna's sake.

sanskrit: एष प्रकृतिरव्यक्ता कर्ता चैव सनातनः ।परश्च सर्वभूतेभ्यस्तस्माद्वृद्धतमोऽच्युतः ॥२३॥
transliteration: eṣa prakṛtiravyaktā kartā caiva sanātanaḥ ,paraśca sarvabhūtebhyastasmādvṛddhatamo'cyutaḥ.
english: He is the unmanifest intrinsic nature (prakṛti) and also the eternal doer/creator. He is superior to all beings, therefore Acyuta is the most ancient.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 23. He is the unmanifest intrinsic nature (prakṛti) and also the eternal doer/creator. He is superior to all beings, therefore Acyuta is the most ancient.

sanskrit: बुद्धिर्मनो महान्वायुस्तेजोऽम्भः खं मही च या ।चतुर्विधं च यद्भूतं सर्वं कृष्णे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: buddhirmano mahānvāyustejo'mbhaḥ khaṁ mahī ca yā ,caturvidhaṁ ca yadbhūtaṁ sarvaṁ kṛṣṇe pratiṣṭhitam.
english: Intellect, mind, the great (principle), air, fire, water, space, and earth; and whatever feminine principle that may be; and also whatever four kinds of beings exist – all this is established in Krishna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 24. Intellect, mind, the great (principle), air, fire, water, space, and earth; and whatever feminine principle that may be; and also whatever four kinds of beings exist – all this is established in Krishna.

sanskrit: आदित्यश्चन्द्रमाश्चैव नक्षत्राणि ग्रहाश्च ये ।दिशश्चोपदिशश्चैव सर्वं कृष्णे प्रतिष्ठितम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: ādityaścandramāścaiva nakṣatrāṇi grahāśca ye ,diśaścopadiśaścaiva sarvaṁ kṛṣṇe pratiṣṭhitam.
english: The sun, the moon, the constellations, and all the planets, as well as the cardinal directions and sub-directions - all these are established in Kṛṣṇa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 25. The sun, the moon, the constellations, and all the planets, as well as the cardinal directions and sub-directions - all these are established in Kṛṣṇa.

sanskrit: अयं तु पुरुषो बालः शिशुपालो न बुध्यते ।सर्वत्र सर्वदा कृष्णं तस्मादेवं प्रभाषते ॥२६॥
transliteration: ayaṁ tu puruṣo bālaḥ śiśupālo na budhyate ,sarvatra sarvadā kṛṣṇaṁ tasmādevaṁ prabhāṣate.
english: But this foolish person (puruṣa), Śiśupāla, does not understand Kṛṣṇa, who is everywhere and always present. Therefore, he speaks in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 26. But this foolish person (puruṣa), Śiśupāla, does not understand Kṛṣṇa, who is everywhere and always present. Therefore, he speaks in this manner.

sanskrit: यो हि धर्मं विचिनुयादुत्कृष्टं मतिमान्नरः ।स वै पश्येद्यथाधर्मं न तथा चेदिराडयम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: yo hi dharmaṁ vicinuyādutkṛṣṭaṁ matimānnaraḥ ,sa vai paśyedyathādharmaṁ na tathā cedirāḍayam.
english: Any intelligent person (nara) who diligently discerns the superior aspect of natural law (dharma) would indeed perceive the true nature of natural law (dharma). But this king of Cedi does not see it that way.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 27. Any intelligent person (nara) who diligently discerns the superior aspect of natural law (dharma) would indeed perceive the true nature of natural law (dharma). But this king of Cedi does not see it that way.

sanskrit: सवृद्धबालेष्वथ वा पार्थिवेषु महात्मसु ।को नार्हं मन्यते कृष्णं को वाप्येनं न पूजयेत् ॥२८॥
transliteration: savṛddhabāleṣvatha vā pārthiveṣu mahātmasu ,ko nārhaṁ manyate kṛṣṇaṁ ko vāpyenaṁ na pūjayet.
english: Among those comprising old and young, or among kings and great souls (mahātman), who would not consider Kṛṣṇa worthy? And who else would not honor him?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 28. Among those comprising old and young, or among kings and great souls (mahātman), who would not consider Kṛṣṇa worthy? And who else would not honor him?

sanskrit: अथेमां दुष्कृतां पूजां शिशुपालो व्यवस्यति ।दुष्कृतायां यथान्यायं तथायं कर्तुमर्हति ॥२९॥
transliteration: athemāṁ duṣkṛtāṁ pūjāṁ śiśupālo vyavasyati ,duṣkṛtāyāṁ yathānyāyaṁ tathāyaṁ kartumarhati.
english: Now, Shishupala intends to perform this improper worship. Indeed, for such an improper act, he is fit to behave exactly as is appropriate for it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 35 Verse 29. Now, Shishupala intends to perform this improper worship. Indeed, for such an improper act, he is fit to behave exactly as is appropriate for it.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा ततो भीष्मो विरराम महायशाः ।व्याजहारोत्तरं तत्र सहदेवोऽर्थवद्वचः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā tato bhīṣmo virarāma mahāyaśāḥ ,vyājahārottaraṁ tatra sahadevo'rthavadvacaḥ.
english: Vaishampayana said: Having spoken thus, Bhishma, of great fame, then ceased. There, Sahadeva spoke a meaningful reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said: Having spoken thus, Bhishma, of great fame, then ceased. There, Sahadeva spoke a meaningful reply.

sanskrit: केशवं केशिहन्तारमप्रमेयपराक्रमम् ।पूज्यमानं मया यो वः कृष्णं न सहते नृपाः ॥२॥
transliteration: keśavaṁ keśihantāramaprameyaparākramam ,pūjyamānaṁ mayā yo vaḥ kṛṣṇaṁ na sahate nṛpāḥ.
english: O kings, whoever among you cannot tolerate Krishna  -  who is Keśava, the slayer of Keśi, of immeasurable valor, and who is being worshipped by me  -
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 2. O kings, whoever among you cannot tolerate Krishna  -  who is Keśava, the slayer of Keśi, of immeasurable valor, and who is being worshipped by me  -

sanskrit: सर्वेषां बलिनां मूर्ध्नि मयेदं निहितं पदम् ।एवमुक्ते मया सम्यगुत्तरं प्रब्रवीतु सः ॥३॥
transliteration: sarveṣāṁ balināṁ mūrdhni mayedaṁ nihitaṁ padam ,evamukte mayā samyaguttaraṁ prabravītu saḥ.
english: I have placed this foot (symbolizing supremacy) upon the heads of all mighty ones. When this has been declared by me, let him (who cannot tolerate Krishna) give a proper reply.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 3. I have placed this foot (symbolizing supremacy) upon the heads of all mighty ones. When this has been declared by me, let him (who cannot tolerate Krishna) give a proper reply.

sanskrit: मतिमन्तस्तु ये केचिदाचार्यं पितरं गुरुम् ।अर्च्यमर्चितमर्चार्हमनुजानन्तु ते नृपाः ॥४॥
transliteration: matimantastu ye kecidācāryaṁ pitaraṁ gurum ,arcyamarcitamarcārhamanujānantu te nṛpāḥ.
english: Indeed, may those intelligent ones, especially those kings among them, acknowledge [him] as a preceptor, a father, and a spiritual teacher (guru), who is worthy of veneration, already venerated, and truly deserving of reverence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 4. Indeed, may those intelligent ones, especially those kings among them, acknowledge [him] as a preceptor, a father, and a spiritual teacher (guru), who is worthy of veneration, already venerated, and truly deserving of reverence.

sanskrit: ततो न व्याजहारैषां कश्चिद्बुद्धिमतां सताम् ।मानिनां बलिनां राज्ञां मध्ये संदर्शिते पदे ॥५॥
transliteration: tato na vyājahāraiṣāṁ kaścidbuddhimatāṁ satām ,mānināṁ balināṁ rājñāṁ madhye saṁdarśite pade.
english: Then, when the proposal was presented in their midst, none among those intelligent, virtuous, proud, and powerful kings uttered a word.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 5. Then, when the proposal was presented in their midst, none among those intelligent, virtuous, proud, and powerful kings uttered a word.

sanskrit: ततोऽपतत्पुष्पवृष्टिः सहदेवस्य मूर्धनि ।अदृश्यरूपा वाचश्चाप्यब्रुवन्साधु साध्विति ॥६॥
transliteration: tato'patatpuṣpavṛṣṭiḥ sahadevasya mūrdhani ,adṛśyarūpā vācaścāpyabruvansādhu sādhviti.
english: Then a shower of flowers fell upon Sahadeva's head, and invisible voices also exclaimed, 'Excellent! Excellent!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 6. Then a shower of flowers fell upon Sahadeva's head, and invisible voices also exclaimed, 'Excellent! Excellent!'

sanskrit: आविध्यदजिनं कृष्णं भविष्यद्भूतजल्पकः ।सर्वसंशयनिर्मोक्ता नारदः सर्वलोकवित् ॥७॥
transliteration: āvidhyadajinaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ bhaviṣyadbhūtajalpakaḥ ,sarvasaṁśayanirmoktā nāradaḥ sarvalokavit.
english: Narada, the speaker of future and past events, the dispeller of all doubts, and the knower of all worlds, waved the black deerskin.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 7. Narada, the speaker of future and past events, the dispeller of all doubts, and the knower of all worlds, waved the black deerskin.

sanskrit: तत्राहूतागताः सर्वे सुनीथप्रमुखा गणाः ।संप्रादृश्यन्त संक्रुद्धा विवर्णवदनास्तथा ॥८॥
transliteration: tatrāhūtāgatāḥ sarve sunīthapramukhā gaṇāḥ ,saṁprādṛśyanta saṁkruddhā vivarṇavadanāstathā.
english: Then, all the assembled groups, led by Sunītha, appeared there, very angry and also with pale faces.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 8. Then, all the assembled groups, led by Sunītha, appeared there, very angry and also with pale faces.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिराभिषेकं च वासुदेवस्य चार्हणम् ।अब्रुवंस्तत्र राजानो निर्वेदादात्मनिश्चयात् ॥९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirābhiṣekaṁ ca vāsudevasya cārhaṇam ,abruvaṁstatra rājāno nirvedādātmaniścayāt.
english: The kings present there, driven by their despair and firm resolve, then spoke of Yudhiṣṭhira's consecration and the honor for Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 9. The kings present there, driven by their despair and firm resolve, then spoke of Yudhiṣṭhira's consecration and the honor for Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: सुहृद्भिर्वार्यमाणानां तेषां हि वपुराबभौ ।आमिषादपकृष्टानां सिंहानामिव गर्जताम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: suhṛdbhirvāryamāṇānāṁ teṣāṁ hi vapurābabhau ,āmiṣādapakṛṣṭānāṁ siṁhānāmiva garjatām.
english: Indeed, the forms of those who were being restrained by their friends shone like those of roaring lions pulled away from their prey.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 10. Indeed, the forms of those who were being restrained by their friends shone like those of roaring lions pulled away from their prey.

sanskrit: तं बलौघमपर्यन्तं राजसागरमक्षयम् ।कुर्वाणं समयं कृष्णो युद्धाय बुबुधे तदा ॥११॥
transliteration: taṁ balaughamaparyantaṁ rājasāgaramakṣayam ,kurvāṇaṁ samayaṁ kṛṣṇo yuddhāya bubudhe tadā.
english: Then, Kṛṣṇa perceived that this boundless and inexhaustible ocean of kings, an immense mass of forces, was making preparations for battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 11. Then, Kṛṣṇa perceived that this boundless and inexhaustible ocean of kings, an immense mass of forces, was making preparations for battle.

sanskrit: पूजयित्वा तु पूजार्हं ब्रह्मक्षत्रं विशेषतः ।सहदेवो नृणां देवः समापयत कर्म तत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: pūjayitvā tu pūjārhaṁ brahmakṣatraṁ viśeṣataḥ ,sahadevo nṛṇāṁ devaḥ samāpayata karma tat.
english: Sahadeva, the chief among men, having worshipped those worthy of honor, especially the Brahmins and Kshatriyas, then completed that ritual (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 12. Sahadeva, the chief among men, having worshipped those worthy of honor, especially the Brahmins and Kshatriyas, then completed that ritual (karma).

sanskrit: तस्मिन्नभ्यर्चिते कृष्णे सुनीथः शत्रुकर्षणः ।अतिताम्रेक्षणः कोपादुवाच मनुजाधिपान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: tasminnabhyarcite kṛṣṇe sunīthaḥ śatrukarṣaṇaḥ ,atitāmrekṣaṇaḥ kopāduvāca manujādhipān.
english: After Kṛṣṇa had been worshipped, Sunītha, the subjugator of foes, his eyes exceedingly red with anger, spoke to the kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 13. After Kṛṣṇa had been worshipped, Sunītha, the subjugator of foes, his eyes exceedingly red with anger, spoke to the kings.

sanskrit: स्थितः सेनापतिर्वोऽहं मन्यध्वं किं नु सांप्रतम् ।युधि तिष्ठाम संनह्य समेतान्वृष्णिपाण्डवान् ॥१४॥
transliteration: sthitaḥ senāpatirvo'haṁ manyadhvaṁ kiṁ nu sāṁpratam ,yudhi tiṣṭhāma saṁnahya sametānvṛṣṇipāṇḍavān.
english: I am your general; what do you all think now? Having prepared ourselves, let us stand in battle against the assembled Vṛṣṇis and Pāṇḍavas!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 14. I am your general; what do you all think now? Having prepared ourselves, let us stand in battle against the assembled Vṛṣṇis and Pāṇḍavas!

sanskrit: इति सर्वान्समुत्साह्य राज्ञस्तांश्चेदिपुंगवः ।यज्ञोपघाताय ततः सोऽमन्त्रयत राजभिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: iti sarvānsamutsāhya rājñastāṁścedipuṁgavaḥ ,yajñopaghātāya tataḥ so'mantrayata rājabhiḥ.
english: Thus, having incited all those kings, the chief of the Cedi kingdom then consulted with the kings present there, aiming to obstruct the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 36 Verse 15. Thus, having incited all those kings, the chief of the Cedi kingdom then consulted with the kings present there, aiming to obstruct the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः सागरसंकाशं दृष्ट्वा नृपतिसागरम् ।रोषात्प्रचलितं सर्वमिदमाह युधिष्ठिरः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ sāgarasaṁkāśaṁ dṛṣṭvā nṛpatisāgaram ,roṣātpracalitaṁ sarvamidamāha yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, upon seeing that entire ocean-like multitude of kings agitated with anger, Yudhishthira spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, upon seeing that entire ocean-like multitude of kings agitated with anger, Yudhishthira spoke these words.

sanskrit: भीष्मं मतिमतां श्रेष्ठं वृद्धं कुरुपितामहम् ।बृहस्पतिं बृहत्तेजाः पुरुहूत इवारिहा ॥२॥
transliteration: bhīṣmaṁ matimatāṁ śreṣṭhaṁ vṛddhaṁ kurupitāmaham ,bṛhaspatiṁ bṛhattejāḥ puruhūta ivārihā.
english: He (Yudhishthira), possessing great brilliance, spoke to Bhishma, who was the best among the wise, aged, and the grandfather of the Kurus, just as Indra, the slayer of foes, would address Brihaspati.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 2. He (Yudhishthira), possessing great brilliance, spoke to Bhishma, who was the best among the wise, aged, and the grandfather of the Kurus, just as Indra, the slayer of foes, would address Brihaspati.

sanskrit: असौ रोषात्प्रचलितो महान्नृपतिसागरः ।अत्र यत्प्रतिपत्तव्यं तन्मे ब्रूहि पितामह ॥३॥
transliteration: asau roṣātpracalito mahānnṛpatisāgaraḥ ,atra yatpratipattavyaṁ tanme brūhi pitāmaha.
english: This great ocean of kings is agitated with anger. Therefore, tell me, O Grandsire, what course of action should be taken in this situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 3. This great ocean of kings is agitated with anger. Therefore, tell me, O Grandsire, what course of action should be taken in this situation.

sanskrit: यज्ञस्य च न विघ्नः स्यात्प्रजानां च शिवं भवेत् ।यथा सर्वत्र तत्सर्वं ब्रूहि मेऽद्य पितामह ॥४॥
transliteration: yajñasya ca na vighnaḥ syātprajānāṁ ca śivaṁ bhavet ,yathā sarvatra tatsarvaṁ brūhi me'dya pitāmaha.
english: O Grandfather, tell me today how all this can be ensured everywhere, so that there are no impediments to the Vedic ritual (yajña) and welfare prevails among the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 4. O Grandfather, tell me today how all this can be ensured everywhere, so that there are no impediments to the Vedic ritual (yajña) and welfare prevails among the people.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तवति धर्मज्ञे धर्मराजे युधिष्ठिरे ।उवाचेदं वचो भीष्मस्ततः कुरुपितामहः ॥५॥
transliteration: ityuktavati dharmajñe dharmarāje yudhiṣṭhire ,uvācedaṁ vaco bhīṣmastataḥ kurupitāmahaḥ.
english: When Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma) and knower of natural law (dharma), had thus spoken, Bhīṣma, the grandfather of the Kurus, then uttered these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 5. When Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma) and knower of natural law (dharma), had thus spoken, Bhīṣma, the grandfather of the Kurus, then uttered these words.

sanskrit: मा भैस्त्वं कुरुशार्दूल श्वा सिंहं हन्तुमर्हति ।शिवः पन्थाः सुनीतोऽत्र मया पूर्वतरं वृतः ॥६॥
transliteration: mā bhaistvaṁ kuruśārdūla śvā siṁhaṁ hantumarhati ,śivaḥ panthāḥ sunīto'tra mayā pūrvataraṁ vṛtaḥ.
english: O tiger among the Kurus, do not be afraid! A dog is not able to kill a lion. Indeed, an auspicious and well-guided path has already been chosen by me in this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 6. O tiger among the Kurus, do not be afraid! A dog is not able to kill a lion. Indeed, an auspicious and well-guided path has already been chosen by me in this matter.

sanskrit: प्रसुप्ते हि यथा सिंहे श्वानस्तत्र समागताः ।भषेयुः सहिताः सर्वे तथेमे वसुधाधिपाः ॥७॥
transliteration: prasupte hi yathā siṁhe śvānastatra samāgatāḥ ,bhaṣeyuḥ sahitāḥ sarve tatheme vasudhādhipāḥ.
english: Indeed, just as all the dogs would bark together, having gathered around a lion when it is fast asleep, similarly do these rulers of the earth behave.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 7. Indeed, just as all the dogs would bark together, having gathered around a lion when it is fast asleep, similarly do these rulers of the earth behave.

sanskrit: वृष्णिसिंहस्य सुप्तस्य तथेमे प्रमुखे स्थिताः ।भषन्ते तात संक्रुद्धाः श्वानः सिंहस्य संनिधौ ॥८॥
transliteration: vṛṣṇisiṁhasya suptasya tatheme pramukhe sthitāḥ ,bhaṣante tāta saṁkruddhāḥ śvānaḥ siṁhasya saṁnidhau.
english: Similarly, O dear one, these greatly enraged dogs are barking in front of the sleeping lion among the Vṛṣṇis, in the lion's very presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 8. Similarly, O dear one, these greatly enraged dogs are barking in front of the sleeping lion among the Vṛṣṇis, in the lion's very presence.

sanskrit: न हि संबुध्यते तावत्सुप्तः सिंह इवाच्युतः ।तेन सिंहीकरोत्येतान्नृसिंहश्चेदिपुंगवः ॥९॥
transliteration: na hi saṁbudhyate tāvatsuptaḥ siṁha ivācyutaḥ ,tena siṁhīkarotyetānnṛsiṁhaścedipuṁgavaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa (Acyuta), like a sleeping lion, does not awaken immediately. For that reason, Śiśupāla, the bull among the Cedis, is making these kings into his prey.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 9. Kṛṣṇa (Acyuta), like a sleeping lion, does not awaken immediately. For that reason, Śiśupāla, the bull among the Cedis, is making these kings into his prey.

sanskrit: पार्थिवान्पार्थिवश्रेष्ठ शिशुपालोऽल्पचेतनः ।सर्वान्सर्वात्मना तात नेतुकामो यमक्षयम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: pārthivānpārthivaśreṣṭha śiśupālo'lpacetanaḥ ,sarvānsarvātmanā tāta netukāmo yamakṣayam.
english: O best of kings, O dear one, foolish Śiśupāla wishes to lead all these rulers to the abode of Yama (death) with all his might.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 10. O best of kings, O dear one, foolish Śiśupāla wishes to lead all these rulers to the abode of Yama (death) with all his might.

sanskrit: नूनमेतत्समादातुं पुनरिच्छत्यधोक्षजः ।यदस्य शिशुपालस्थं तेजस्तिष्ठति भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: nūnametatsamādātuṁ punaricchatyadhokṣajaḥ ,yadasya śiśupālasthaṁ tejastiṣṭhati bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, surely Adhokṣaja (Kṛṣṇa) wishes to take back again that splendor (tejas) which currently resides in Śiśupāla.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 11. O Bhārata, surely Adhokṣaja (Kṛṣṇa) wishes to take back again that splendor (tejas) which currently resides in Śiśupāla.

sanskrit: विप्लुता चास्य भद्रं ते बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतां वर ।चेदिराजस्य कौन्तेय सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: viplutā cāsya bhadraṁ te buddhirbuddhimatāṁ vara ,cedirājasya kaunteya sarveṣāṁ ca mahīkṣitām.
english: O best among the intelligent, O son of Kuntī, may good fortune be with you; the intellect of the king of the Cedis is bewildered, and also that of all these kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 12. O best among the intelligent, O son of Kuntī, may good fortune be with you; the intellect of the king of the Cedis is bewildered, and also that of all these kings.

sanskrit: आदातुं हि नरव्याघ्रो यं यमिच्छत्ययं यदा ।तस्य विप्लवते बुद्धिरेवं चेदिपतेर्यथा ॥१३॥
transliteration: ādātuṁ hi naravyāghro yaṁ yamicchatyayaṁ yadā ,tasya viplavate buddhirevaṁ cedipateryathā.
english: Indeed, when this tiger among men (naravyāghra) desires to seize whatever he wishes, his intellect (buddhi) becomes bewildered, just as the intellect (buddhi) of the King of Cedi (Cedipati) does.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 13. Indeed, when this tiger among men (naravyāghra) desires to seize whatever he wishes, his intellect (buddhi) becomes bewildered, just as the intellect (buddhi) of the King of Cedi (Cedipati) does.

sanskrit: चतुर्विधानां भूतानां त्रिषु लोकेषु माधवः ।प्रभवश्चैव सर्वेषां निधनं च युधिष्ठिर ॥१४॥
transliteration: caturvidhānāṁ bhūtānāṁ triṣu lokeṣu mādhavaḥ ,prabhavaścaiva sarveṣāṁ nidhanaṁ ca yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, Mādhava (Krishna) is indeed the origin and the end of all four types of beings throughout the three worlds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 14. O Yudhiṣṭhira, Mādhava (Krishna) is indeed the origin and the end of all four types of beings throughout the three worlds.

sanskrit: इति तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा ततश्चेदिपतिर्नृपः ।भीष्मं रूक्षाक्षरा वाचः श्रावयामास भारत ॥१५॥
transliteration: iti tasya vacaḥ śrutvā tataścedipatirnṛpaḥ ,bhīṣmaṁ rūkṣākṣarā vācaḥ śrāvayāmāsa bhārata.
english: O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata), having thus heard his words, the King of Cedi (Cedipati) then spoke harsh words (rūkṣākṣarā vācaḥ) to Bhīṣma.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 37 Verse 15. O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata), having thus heard his words, the King of Cedi (Cedipati) then spoke harsh words (rūkṣākṣarā vācaḥ) to Bhīṣma.

sanskrit: शिशुपाल उवाच ।विभीषिकाभिर्बह्वीभिर्भीषयन्सर्वपार्थिवान् ।न व्यपत्रपसे कस्माद्वृद्धः सन्कुलपांसनः ॥१॥
transliteration: śiśupāla uvāca ,vibhīṣikābhirbahvībhirbhīṣayansarvapārthivān ,na vyapatrapase kasmādvṛddhaḥ sankulapāṁsanaḥ.
english: Śiśupāla said: 'Being old and a disgrace to your family (kulapāṃsana), why are you not ashamed, constantly intimidating all kings with so many threats?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 1. Śiśupāla said: 'Being old and a disgrace to your family (kulapāṃsana), why are you not ashamed, constantly intimidating all kings with so many threats?'

sanskrit: युक्तमेतत्तृतीयायां प्रकृतौ वर्तता त्वया ।वक्तुं धर्मादपेतार्थं त्वं हि सर्वकुरूत्तमः ॥२॥
transliteration: yuktametattṛtīyāyāṁ prakṛtau vartatā tvayā ,vaktuṁ dharmādapetārthaṁ tvaṁ hi sarvakurūttamaḥ.
english: It is appropriate that you, who are acting in a neutral capacity, speak words that deviate from (natural) law (dharma), for you are indeed the foremost among all Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 2. It is appropriate that you, who are acting in a neutral capacity, speak words that deviate from (natural) law (dharma), for you are indeed the foremost among all Kurus.

sanskrit: नावि नौरिव संबद्धा यथान्धो वान्धमन्वियात् ।तथाभूता हि कौरव्या भीष्म येषां त्वमग्रणीः ॥३॥
transliteration: nāvi nauriva saṁbaddhā yathāndho vāndhamanviyāt ,tathābhūtā hi kauravyā bhīṣma yeṣāṁ tvamagraṇīḥ.
english: Like a boat tied to another boat, or just as a blind man would follow another blind man, such is indeed the state of the Kauravas, O Bhishma, of whom you are the leader.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 3. Like a boat tied to another boat, or just as a blind man would follow another blind man, such is indeed the state of the Kauravas, O Bhishma, of whom you are the leader.

sanskrit: पूतनाघातपूर्वाणि कर्माण्यस्य विशेषतः ।त्वया कीर्तयतास्माकं भूयः प्रच्यावितं मनः ॥४॥
transliteration: pūtanāghātapūrvāṇi karmāṇyasya viśeṣataḥ ,tvayā kīrtayatāsmākaṁ bhūyaḥ pracyāvitaṁ manaḥ.
english: By your recounting of his deeds, especially those beginning with the slaying of Pūtanā, our minds have been further agitated.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 4. By your recounting of his deeds, especially those beginning with the slaying of Pūtanā, our minds have been further agitated.

sanskrit: अवलिप्तस्य मूर्खस्य केशवं स्तोतुमिच्छतः ।कथं भीष्म न ते जिह्वा शतधेयं विदीर्यते ॥५॥
transliteration: avaliptasya mūrkhasya keśavaṁ stotumicchataḥ ,kathaṁ bhīṣma na te jihvā śatadheyaṁ vidīryate.
english: O Bhishma, how is it that your tongue does not split into a hundred pieces, while you, arrogant and foolish, desire to praise Keśava (Krishna)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 5. O Bhishma, how is it that your tongue does not split into a hundred pieces, while you, arrogant and foolish, desire to praise Keśava (Krishna)?

sanskrit: यत्र कुत्सा प्रयोक्तव्या भीष्म बालतरैर्नरैः ।तमिमं ज्ञानवृद्धः सन्गोपं संस्तोतुमिच्छसि ॥६॥
transliteration: yatra kutsā prayoktavyā bhīṣma bālatarairnaraiḥ ,tamimaṁ jñānavṛddhaḥ sangopaṁ saṁstotumicchasi.
english: O Bhishma, where even very young men should express censure, you, despite being advanced in knowledge, wish to praise this cowherd.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 6. O Bhishma, where even very young men should express censure, you, despite being advanced in knowledge, wish to praise this cowherd.

sanskrit: यद्यनेन हता बाल्ये शकुनिश्चित्रमत्र किम् ।तौ वाश्ववृषभौ भीष्म यौ न युद्धविशारदौ ॥७॥
transliteration: yadyanena hatā bālye śakuniścitramatra kim ,tau vāśvavṛṣabhau bhīṣma yau na yuddhaviśāradau.
english: If a bird-demon was killed by him in childhood, what is so extraordinary in that? Or if it was those two, O Bhishma, the horse and the bull, who were not skilled in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 7. If a bird-demon was killed by him in childhood, what is so extraordinary in that? Or if it was those two, O Bhishma, the horse and the bull, who were not skilled in battle.

sanskrit: चेतनारहितं काष्ठं यद्यनेन निपातितम् ।पादेन शकटं भीष्म तत्र किं कृतमद्भुतम् ॥८॥
transliteration: cetanārahitaṁ kāṣṭhaṁ yadyanena nipātitam ,pādena śakaṭaṁ bhīṣma tatra kiṁ kṛtamadbhutam.
english: If by him an inanimate wooden cart was toppled with his foot, O Bhishma, what amazing deed was performed there?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 8. If by him an inanimate wooden cart was toppled with his foot, O Bhishma, what amazing deed was performed there?

sanskrit: वल्मीकमात्रः सप्ताहं यद्यनेन धृतोऽचलः ।तदा गोवर्धनो भीष्म न तच्चित्रं मतं मम ॥९॥
transliteration: valmīkamātraḥ saptāhaṁ yadyanena dhṛto'calaḥ ,tadā govardhano bhīṣma na taccitraṁ mataṁ mama.
english: Even if a mountain (Govardhana), merely the size of an anthill, was held up by him for a week, O Bhishma, I would not consider that a marvel.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 9. Even if a mountain (Govardhana), merely the size of an anthill, was held up by him for a week, O Bhishma, I would not consider that a marvel.

sanskrit: भुक्तमेतेन बह्वन्नं क्रीडता नगमूर्धनि ।इति ते भीष्म शृण्वानाः परं विस्मयमागताः ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhuktametena bahvannaṁ krīḍatā nagamūrdhani ,iti te bhīṣma śṛṇvānāḥ paraṁ vismayamāgatāḥ.
english: Bhishma, when they heard that much food had been consumed by him while he was playing on the mountaintop, they were greatly astonished.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 10. Bhishma, when they heard that much food had been consumed by him while he was playing on the mountaintop, they were greatly astonished.

sanskrit: यस्य चानेन धर्मज्ञ भुक्तमन्नं बलीयसः ।स चानेन हतः कंस इत्येतन्न महाद्भुतम् ॥११॥
transliteration: yasya cānena dharmajña bhuktamannaṁ balīyasaḥ ,sa cānena hataḥ kaṁsa ityetanna mahādbhutam.
english: O knower of natural law (dharma), since this one ate the food of a mighty individual, it is no great wonder that Kaṃsa was killed by him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 11. O knower of natural law (dharma), since this one ate the food of a mighty individual, it is no great wonder that Kaṃsa was killed by him.

sanskrit: न ते श्रुतमिदं भीष्म नूनं कथयतां सताम् ।यद्वक्ष्ये त्वामधर्मज्ञ वाक्यं कुरुकुलाधम ॥१२॥
transliteration: na te śrutamidaṁ bhīṣma nūnaṁ kathayatāṁ satām ,yadvakṣye tvāmadharmajña vākyaṁ kurukulādhama.
english: Bhishma, you surely have not heard this from virtuous people who narrate such things. What I am about to declare to you, O ignorant of natural law (dharma) and lowest of the Kuru family, is a statement.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 12. Bhishma, you surely have not heard this from virtuous people who narrate such things. What I am about to declare to you, O ignorant of natural law (dharma) and lowest of the Kuru family, is a statement.

sanskrit: स्त्रीषु गोषु न शस्त्राणि पातयेद्ब्राह्मणेषु च ।यस्य चान्नानि भुञ्जीत यश्च स्याच्छरणागतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: strīṣu goṣu na śastrāṇi pātayedbrāhmaṇeṣu ca ,yasya cānnāni bhuñjīta yaśca syāccharaṇāgataḥ.
english: One should not use weapons against women, cows, or Brahmins. Likewise, one should not use them against someone whose food one has consumed, or against anyone who has taken refuge.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 13. One should not use weapons against women, cows, or Brahmins. Likewise, one should not use them against someone whose food one has consumed, or against anyone who has taken refuge.

sanskrit: इति सन्तोऽनुशासन्ति सज्जना धर्मिणः सदा ।भीष्म लोके हि तत्सर्वं वितथं त्वयि दृश्यते ॥१४॥
transliteration: iti santo'nuśāsanti sajjanā dharmiṇaḥ sadā ,bhīṣma loke hi tatsarvaṁ vitathaṁ tvayi dṛśyate.
english: Thus, saints, good people, and those who uphold natural law (dharma) always give instruction. Yet, O Bhishma, all of that (teaching) appears false in your case in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 14. Thus, saints, good people, and those who uphold natural law (dharma) always give instruction. Yet, O Bhishma, all of that (teaching) appears false in your case in this world.

sanskrit: ज्ञानवृद्धं च वृद्धं च भूयांसं केशवं मम ।अजानत इवाख्यासि संस्तुवन्कुरुसत्तम ।गोघ्नः स्त्रीघ्नश्च सन्भीष्म कथं संस्तवमर्हति ॥१५॥
transliteration: jñānavṛddhaṁ ca vṛddhaṁ ca bhūyāṁsaṁ keśavaṁ mama ,ajānata ivākhyāsi saṁstuvankurusattama ,goghnaḥ strīghnaśca sanbhīṣma kathaṁ saṁstavamarhati.
english: O best of Kurus, you speak as if ignorant, even while praising my Keshava, who is great, advanced in knowledge, and aged. O Bhishma, how can someone who is a killer of cows and women deserve such praise?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 15. O best of Kurus, you speak as if ignorant, even while praising my Keshava, who is great, advanced in knowledge, and aged. O Bhishma, how can someone who is a killer of cows and women deserve such praise?

sanskrit: असौ मतिमतां श्रेष्ठो य एष जगतः प्रभुः ।संभावयति यद्येवं त्वद्वाक्याच्च जनार्दनः ।एवमेतत्सर्वमिति सर्वं तद्वितथं ध्रुवम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: asau matimatāṁ śreṣṭho ya eṣa jagataḥ prabhuḥ ,saṁbhāvayati yadyevaṁ tvadvākyācca janārdanaḥ ,evametatsarvamiti sarvaṁ tadvitathaṁ dhruvam.
english: If this Janardana, who is the foremost among the wise and the lord of the world, were to accept (saṃbhāvayati) something as true just based on your words, then everything declared as 'this is so' would certainly be false (vitatham).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 16. If this Janardana, who is the foremost among the wise and the lord of the world, were to accept (saṃbhāvayati) something as true just based on your words, then everything declared as 'this is so' would certainly be false (vitatham).

sanskrit: न गाथा गाथिनं शास्ति बहु चेदपि गायति ।प्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि भूलिङ्गशकुनिर्यथा ॥१७॥
transliteration: na gāthā gāthinaṁ śāsti bahu cedapi gāyati ,prakṛtiṁ yānti bhūtāni bhūliṅgaśakuniryathā.
english: A song does not instruct the singer, even if he sings it extensively. Living beings follow their intrinsic nature (prakṛti), just like the Bhulinga bird (bhūliṅgaśakuni).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 17. A song does not instruct the singer, even if he sings it extensively. Living beings follow their intrinsic nature (prakṛti), just like the Bhulinga bird (bhūliṅgaśakuni).

sanskrit: नूनं प्रकृतिरेषा ते जघन्या नात्र संशयः ।अतः पापीयसी चैषां पाण्डवानामपीष्यते ॥१८॥
transliteration: nūnaṁ prakṛtireṣā te jaghanyā nātra saṁśayaḥ ,ataḥ pāpīyasī caiṣāṁ pāṇḍavānāmapīṣyate.
english: Certainly, this intrinsic nature (prakṛti) of yours is the lowest; there is no doubt about that. Therefore, it is regarded as even more wicked than that of these Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 18. Certainly, this intrinsic nature (prakṛti) of yours is the lowest; there is no doubt about that. Therefore, it is regarded as even more wicked than that of these Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: येषामर्च्यतमः कृष्णस्त्वं च येषां प्रदर्शकः ।धर्मवाक्त्वमधर्मज्ञः सतां मार्गादवप्लुतः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yeṣāmarcyatamaḥ kṛṣṇastvaṁ ca yeṣāṁ pradarśakaḥ ,dharmavāktvamadharmajñaḥ satāṁ mārgādavaplutaḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa is the most worshipful to them, and you are their guide. Yet, you, who speak of natural law (dharma), are truly ignorant of natural law (dharma) and have deviated from the path of the virtuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 19. Kṛṣṇa is the most worshipful to them, and you are their guide. Yet, you, who speak of natural law (dharma), are truly ignorant of natural law (dharma) and have deviated from the path of the virtuous.

sanskrit: को हि धर्मिणमात्मानं जानञ्ज्ञानवतां वरः ।कुर्याद्यथा त्वया भीष्म कृतं धर्ममवेक्षता ॥२०॥
transliteration: ko hi dharmiṇamātmānaṁ jānañjñānavatāṁ varaḥ ,kuryādyathā tvayā bhīṣma kṛtaṁ dharmamavekṣatā.
english: Indeed, who, being the best among the wise, would know his own self (ātman) to be a person of natural law (dharmin) and yet act as you, Bhīṣma, have done, despite being one who considers natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 20. Indeed, who, being the best among the wise, would know his own self (ātman) to be a person of natural law (dharmin) and yet act as you, Bhīṣma, have done, despite being one who considers natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: अन्यकामा हि धर्मज्ञ कन्यका प्राज्ञमानिना ।अम्बा नामेति भद्रं ते कथं सापहृता त्वया ॥२१॥
transliteration: anyakāmā hi dharmajña kanyakā prājñamāninā ,ambā nāmeti bhadraṁ te kathaṁ sāpahṛtā tvayā.
english: O knower of natural law (dharma), how indeed was that maiden, named Ambā, who desired another man, abducted by you, you who considered yourself wise? May you be well.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 21. O knower of natural law (dharma), how indeed was that maiden, named Ambā, who desired another man, abducted by you, you who considered yourself wise? May you be well.

sanskrit: यां त्वयापहृतां भीष्म कन्यां नैषितवान्नृपः ।भ्राता विचित्रवीर्यस्ते सतां वृत्तमनुष्ठितः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yāṁ tvayāpahṛtāṁ bhīṣma kanyāṁ naiṣitavānnṛpaḥ ,bhrātā vicitravīryaste satāṁ vṛttamanuṣṭhitaḥ.
english: O Bhishma, your brother Vichitravirya, the king, did not accept the maiden whom you had abducted, thereby upholding the conduct of the virtuous.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 22. O Bhishma, your brother Vichitravirya, the king, did not accept the maiden whom you had abducted, thereby upholding the conduct of the virtuous.

sanskrit: दारयोर्यस्य चान्येन मिषतः प्राज्ञमानिनः ।तव जातान्यपत्यानि सज्जनाचरिते पथि ॥२३॥
transliteration: dārayoryasya cānyena miṣataḥ prājñamāninaḥ ,tava jātānyapatyāni sajjanācarite pathi.
english: And while you, who consider yourself wise, merely watched, children were born to your (late) brother's two wives by another man, following the path sanctioned by the virtuous (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 23. And while you, who consider yourself wise, merely watched, children were born to your (late) brother's two wives by another man, following the path sanctioned by the virtuous (dharma).

sanskrit: न हि धर्मोऽस्ति ते भीष्म ब्रह्मचर्यमिदं वृथा ।यद्धारयसि मोहाद्वा क्लीबत्वाद्वा न संशयः ॥२४॥
transliteration: na hi dharmo'sti te bhīṣma brahmacaryamidaṁ vṛthā ,yaddhārayasi mohādvā klībatvādvā na saṁśayaḥ.
english: O Bhishma, there is indeed no (true) righteousness (dharma) in this celibacy (brahmacarya) of yours; it is futile. You uphold it either out of delusion or out of impotence; there is no doubt about this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 24. O Bhishma, there is indeed no (true) righteousness (dharma) in this celibacy (brahmacarya) of yours; it is futile. You uphold it either out of delusion or out of impotence; there is no doubt about this.

sanskrit: न त्वहं तव धर्मज्ञ पश्याम्युपचयं क्वचित् ।न हि ते सेविता वृद्धा य एवं धर्ममब्रुवन् ॥२५॥
transliteration: na tvahaṁ tava dharmajña paśyāmyupacayaṁ kvacit ,na hi te sevitā vṛddhā ya evaṁ dharmamabruvan.
english: But O knower of (dharma), I do not see any advantage for you anywhere (in your conduct). For you have surely not served those elders who spoke of (dharma) in such a way.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 25. But O knower of (dharma), I do not see any advantage for you anywhere (in your conduct). For you have surely not served those elders who spoke of (dharma) in such a way.

sanskrit: इष्टं दत्तमधीतं च यज्ञाश्च बहुदक्षिणाः ।सर्वमेतदपत्यस्य कलां नार्हति षोडशीम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: iṣṭaṁ dattamadhītaṁ ca yajñāśca bahudakṣiṇāḥ ,sarvametadapatyasya kalāṁ nārhati ṣoḍaśīm.
english: All acts of worship, charitable giving, scriptural study, and Vedic rituals (yajña) accompanied by many gifts – none of this amounts to even a sixteenth part for a person without offspring.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 26. All acts of worship, charitable giving, scriptural study, and Vedic rituals (yajña) accompanied by many gifts – none of this amounts to even a sixteenth part for a person without offspring.

sanskrit: व्रतोपवासैर्बहुभिः कृतं भवति भीष्म यत् ।सर्वं तदनपत्यस्य मोघं भवति निश्चयात् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vratopavāsairbahubhiḥ kṛtaṁ bhavati bhīṣma yat ,sarvaṁ tadanapatyasya moghaṁ bhavati niścayāt.
english: O Bhishma, whatever is accomplished through many vows and fasts – all that, for a childless person, surely becomes fruitless.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 27. O Bhishma, whatever is accomplished through many vows and fasts – all that, for a childless person, surely becomes fruitless.

sanskrit: सोऽनपत्यश्च वृद्धश्च मिथ्याधर्मानुशासनात् ।हंसवत्त्वमपीदानीं ज्ञातिभ्यः प्राप्नुया वधम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: so'napatyaśca vṛddhaśca mithyādharmānuśāsanāt ,haṁsavattvamapīdānīṁ jñātibhyaḥ prāpnuyā vadham.
english: He, being childless and old, due to misguided adherence to natural law (dharma), may now even face destruction from his kinsmen, like a swan.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 28. He, being childless and old, due to misguided adherence to natural law (dharma), may now even face destruction from his kinsmen, like a swan.

sanskrit: एवं हि कथयन्त्यन्ये नरा ज्ञानविदः पुरा ।भीष्म यत्तदहं सम्यग्वक्ष्यामि तव शृण्वतः ॥२९॥
transliteration: evaṁ hi kathayantyanye narā jñānavidaḥ purā ,bhīṣma yattadahaṁ samyagvakṣyāmi tava śṛṇvataḥ.
english: O Bhishma, indeed, this is what other wise men (jñānavidaḥ) spoke of in ancient times. I will now explain that (matter) clearly to you while you listen.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 29. O Bhishma, indeed, this is what other wise men (jñānavidaḥ) spoke of in ancient times. I will now explain that (matter) clearly to you while you listen.

sanskrit: वृद्धः किल समुद्रान्ते कश्चिद्धंसोऽभवत्पुरा ।धर्मवागन्यथावृत्तः पक्षिणः सोऽनुशास्ति ह ॥३०॥
transliteration: vṛddhaḥ kila samudrānte kaściddhaṁso'bhavatpurā ,dharmavāganyathāvṛttaḥ pakṣiṇaḥ so'nuśāsti ha.
english: It is said that long ago, at the edge of the ocean, there lived a certain old swan. Although his own conduct was not in accordance with it, he would instruct the other birds on natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 30. It is said that long ago, at the edge of the ocean, there lived a certain old swan. Although his own conduct was not in accordance with it, he would instruct the other birds on natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: धर्मं चरत माधर्ममिति तस्य वचः किल ।पक्षिणः शुश्रुवुर्भीष्म सततं धर्मवादिनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: dharmaṁ carata mādharmamiti tasya vacaḥ kila ,pakṣiṇaḥ śuśruvurbhīṣma satataṁ dharmavādinaḥ.
english: They say that his words were: 'Practice natural law (dharma), do not practice unrighteousness (adharma)!' O Bhishma, the birds, who themselves constantly spoke of natural law (dharma), heard him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 31. They say that his words were: 'Practice natural law (dharma), do not practice unrighteousness (adharma)!' O Bhishma, the birds, who themselves constantly spoke of natural law (dharma), heard him.

sanskrit: अथास्य भक्ष्यमाजह्रुः समुद्रजलचारिणः ।अण्डजा भीष्म तस्यान्ये धर्मार्थमिति शुश्रुम ॥३२॥
transliteration: athāsya bhakṣyamājahruḥ samudrajalacāriṇaḥ ,aṇḍajā bhīṣma tasyānye dharmārthamiti śuśruma.
english: Then, O Bhishma, other egg-born creatures, those dwelling in the ocean waters, brought him food. We heard that they did this for the sake of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 32. Then, O Bhishma, other egg-born creatures, those dwelling in the ocean waters, brought him food. We heard that they did this for the sake of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: तस्य चैव समभ्याशे निक्षिप्याण्डानि सर्वशः ।समुद्राम्भस्यमोदन्त चरन्तो भीष्म पक्षिणः ॥३३॥
transliteration: tasya caiva samabhyāśe nikṣipyāṇḍāni sarvaśaḥ ,samudrāmbhasyamodanta caranto bhīṣma pakṣiṇaḥ.
english: And indeed, O Bhishma, having deposited all their eggs completely near him, the birds rejoiced while moving about in the ocean waters.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 33. And indeed, O Bhishma, having deposited all their eggs completely near him, the birds rejoiced while moving about in the ocean waters.

sanskrit: तेषामण्डानि सर्वेषां भक्षयामास पापकृत् ।स हंसः संप्रमत्तानामप्रमत्तः स्वकर्मणि ॥३४॥
transliteration: teṣāmaṇḍāni sarveṣāṁ bhakṣayāmāsa pāpakṛt ,sa haṁsaḥ saṁpramattānāmapramattaḥ svakarmaṇi.
english: The wicked (swan), diligent in his own actions (karma), devoured the eggs of all those (other birds) who were extremely negligent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 34. The wicked (swan), diligent in his own actions (karma), devoured the eggs of all those (other birds) who were extremely negligent.

sanskrit: ततः प्रक्षीयमाणेषु तेष्वण्डेष्वण्डजोऽपरः ।अशङ्कत महाप्राज्ञस्तं कदाचिद्ददर्श ह ॥३५॥
transliteration: tataḥ prakṣīyamāṇeṣu teṣvaṇḍeṣvaṇḍajo'paraḥ ,aśaṅkata mahāprājñastaṁ kadāciddadarśa ha.
english: Then, as those eggs were perishing, another very wise bird (aṇḍaja) became suspicious, and indeed, he saw him (the wicked swan) one day.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 35. Then, as those eggs were perishing, another very wise bird (aṇḍaja) became suspicious, and indeed, he saw him (the wicked swan) one day.

sanskrit: ततः स कथयामास दृष्ट्वा हंसस्य किल्बिषम् ।तेषां परमदुःखार्तः स पक्षी सर्वपक्षिणाम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa kathayāmāsa dṛṣṭvā haṁsasya kilbiṣam ,teṣāṁ paramaduḥkhārtaḥ sa pakṣī sarvapakṣiṇām.
english: Then, that bird, greatly afflicted by the immense sorrow (of the other birds), having seen the swan's offense, narrated it to all the birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 36. Then, that bird, greatly afflicted by the immense sorrow (of the other birds), having seen the swan's offense, narrated it to all the birds.

sanskrit: ततः प्रत्यक्षतो दृष्ट्वा पक्षिणस्ते समागताः ।निजघ्नुस्तं तदा हंसं मिथ्यावृत्तं कुरूद्वह ॥३७॥
transliteration: tataḥ pratyakṣato dṛṣṭvā pakṣiṇaste samāgatāḥ ,nijaghnustaṁ tadā haṁsaṁ mithyāvṛttaṁ kurūdvaha.
english: O scion of Kuru, then those birds, having gathered and witnessed (the misdeed) directly, immediately killed that deceitful swan.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 37. O scion of Kuru, then those birds, having gathered and witnessed (the misdeed) directly, immediately killed that deceitful swan.

sanskrit: ते त्वां हंससधर्माणमपीमे वसुधाधिपाः ।निहन्युर्भीष्म संक्रुद्धाः पक्षिणस्तमिवाण्डजम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: te tvāṁ haṁsasadharmāṇamapīme vasudhādhipāḥ ,nihanyurbhīṣma saṁkruddhāḥ pakṣiṇastamivāṇḍajam.
english: O Bhishma, these kings, intensely enraged, might even kill you, who are noble like a swan, just as other birds would kill a bird that preys on eggs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 38. O Bhishma, these kings, intensely enraged, might even kill you, who are noble like a swan, just as other birds would kill a bird that preys on eggs.

sanskrit: गाथामप्यत्र गायन्ति ये पुराणविदो जनाः ।भीष्म यां तां च ते सम्यक्कथयिष्यामि भारत ॥३९॥
transliteration: gāthāmapyatra gāyanti ye purāṇavido janāḥ ,bhīṣma yāṁ tāṁ ca te samyakkathayiṣyāmi bhārata.
english: O Bhishma, O Bhārata, I will fully recount to you that ancient verse which those who know the Puranas recite concerning this matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 39. O Bhishma, O Bhārata, I will fully recount to you that ancient verse which those who know the Puranas recite concerning this matter.

sanskrit: अन्तरात्मनि विनिहिते रौषि पत्ररथ वितथम् ।अण्डभक्षणमशुचि ते कर्म वाचमतिशयते ॥४०॥
transliteration: antarātmani vinihite; rauṣi patraratha vitatham ,aṇḍabhakṣaṇamaśuci te; karma vācamatiśayate.
english: O bird, though your inner being (ātman) is established (in purity), you speak (or boast) in vain. Your act of eating eggs is impure; your action (karma) far outweighs your words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 38 Verse 40. O bird, though your inner being (ātman) is established (in purity), you speak (or boast) in vain. Your act of eating eggs is impure; your action (karma) far outweighs your words.

sanskrit: शिशुपाल उवाच ।स मे बहुमतो राजा जरासंधो महाबलः ।योऽनेन युद्धं नेयेष दासोऽयमिति संयुगे ॥१॥
transliteration: śiśupāla uvāca ,sa me bahumato rājā jarāsaṁdho mahābalaḥ ,yo'nena yuddhaṁ neyeṣa dāso'yamiti saṁyuge.
english: Shishupala said: 'That very powerful King Jarasandha, whom I highly esteemed, did not wish to fight this one (Krishna) in battle, considering, "This one is merely a servant."'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 1. Shishupala said: 'That very powerful King Jarasandha, whom I highly esteemed, did not wish to fight this one (Krishna) in battle, considering, "This one is merely a servant."'

sanskrit: केशवेन कृतं यत्तु जरासंधवधे तदा ।भीमसेनार्जुनाभ्यां च कस्तत्साध्विति मन्यते ॥२॥
transliteration: keśavena kṛtaṁ yattu jarāsaṁdhavadhe tadā ,bhīmasenārjunābhyāṁ ca kastatsādhviti manyate.
english: Who would consider proper that action which Keśava, accompanied by Bhīmasena and Arjuna, undertook at that time for the slaying of Jarāsandha?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 2. Who would consider proper that action which Keśava, accompanied by Bhīmasena and Arjuna, undertook at that time for the slaying of Jarāsandha?

sanskrit: अद्वारेण प्रविष्टेन छद्मना ब्रह्मवादिना ।दृष्टः प्रभावः कृष्णेन जरासंधस्य धीमतः ॥३॥
transliteration: advāreṇa praviṣṭena chadmanā brahmavādinā ,dṛṣṭaḥ prabhāvaḥ kṛṣṇena jarāsaṁdhasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa, having entered through an improper means and by deceit, posing as a speaker of brahman (brahmavādin), observed the power of the intelligent Jarāsandha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 3. Kṛṣṇa, having entered through an improper means and by deceit, posing as a speaker of brahman (brahmavādin), observed the power of the intelligent Jarāsandha.

sanskrit: येन धर्मात्मनात्मानं ब्रह्मण्यमभिजानता ।नैषितं पाद्यमस्मै तद्दातुमग्रे दुरात्मने ॥४॥
transliteration: yena dharmātmanātmānaṁ brahmaṇyamabhijānatā ,naiṣitaṁ pādyamasmai taddātumagre durātmane.
english: By whom (Kṛṣṇa), who possessed a righteous nature (dharma) and considered himself (ātman) to be devoted to brahman (brahmaṇya), the ceremonial foot-water was not desired to be offered to that evil-minded one (durātman) in his presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 4. By whom (Kṛṣṇa), who possessed a righteous nature (dharma) and considered himself (ātman) to be devoted to brahman (brahmaṇya), the ceremonial foot-water was not desired to be offered to that evil-minded one (durātman) in his presence.

sanskrit: भुज्यतामिति तेनोक्ताः कृष्णभीमधनंजयाः ।जरासंधेन कौरव्य कृष्णेन विकृतं कृतम् ॥५॥
transliteration: bhujyatāmiti tenoktāḥ kṛṣṇabhīmadhanaṁjayāḥ ,jarāsaṁdhena kauravya kṛṣṇena vikṛtaṁ kṛtam.
english: Jarāsandha addressed Kṛṣṇa, Bhīma, and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) with the words, 'Let food be enjoyed!' O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), Kṛṣṇa acted improperly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 5. Jarāsandha addressed Kṛṣṇa, Bhīma, and Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) with the words, 'Let food be enjoyed!' O descendant of Kuru (kauravya), Kṛṣṇa acted improperly.

sanskrit: यद्ययं जगतः कर्ता यथैनं मूर्ख मन्यसे ।कस्मान्न ब्राह्मणं सम्यगात्मानमवगच्छति ॥६॥
transliteration: yadyayaṁ jagataḥ kartā yathainaṁ mūrkha manyase ,kasmānna brāhmaṇaṁ samyagātmānamavagacchati.
english: If this person is the creator of the world, as you foolishly believe him to be, then why does he not properly understand his own divine self (ātman)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 6. If this person is the creator of the world, as you foolishly believe him to be, then why does he not properly understand his own divine self (ātman)?

sanskrit: इदं त्वाश्चर्यभूतं मे यदिमे पाण्डवास्त्वया ।अपकृष्टाः सतां मार्गान्मन्यन्ते तच्च साध्विति ॥७॥
transliteration: idaṁ tvāścaryabhūtaṁ me yadime pāṇḍavāstvayā ,apakṛṣṭāḥ satāṁ mārgānmanyante tacca sādhviti.
english: What is truly astonishing to me is that these Pandavas, despite being led astray by you from the path of virtuous people, consider that to be proper.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 7. What is truly astonishing to me is that these Pandavas, despite being led astray by you from the path of virtuous people, consider that to be proper.

sanskrit: अथ वा नैतदाश्चर्यं येषां त्वमसि भारत ।स्त्रीसधर्मा च वृद्धश्च सर्वार्थानां प्रदर्शकः ॥८॥
transliteration: atha vā naitadāścaryaṁ yeṣāṁ tvamasi bhārata ,strīsadharmā ca vṛddhaśca sarvārthānāṁ pradarśakaḥ.
english: Or rather, this is not surprising, O Bhārata, for those whose leader you are - you who are effeminate, and old, yet claim to be the guide for all objectives.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 8. Or rather, this is not surprising, O Bhārata, for those whose leader you are - you who are effeminate, and old, yet claim to be the guide for all objectives.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा रूक्षं रूक्षाक्षरं बहु ।चुकोप बलिनां श्रेष्ठो भीमसेनः प्रतापवान् ॥९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasya tadvacanaṁ śrutvā rūkṣaṁ rūkṣākṣaraṁ bahu ,cukopa balināṁ śreṣṭho bhīmasenaḥ pratāpavān.
english: Vaiśaṁpāyana said: Having heard that speech of his, which was crude, filled with many harsh words, Bhīmasena, the foremost among the powerful and mighty, became enraged.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 9. Vaiśaṁpāyana said: Having heard that speech of his, which was crude, filled with many harsh words, Bhīmasena, the foremost among the powerful and mighty, became enraged.

sanskrit: तस्य पद्मप्रतीकाशे स्वभावायतविस्तृते ।भूयः क्रोधाभिताम्रान्ते रक्ते नेत्रे बभूवतुः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tasya padmapratīkāśe svabhāvāyatavistṛte ,bhūyaḥ krodhābhitāmrānte rakte netre babhūvatuḥ.
english: His two eyes, naturally long and wide and resembling lotuses, became further reddened, their corners flushed crimson with anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 10. His two eyes, naturally long and wide and resembling lotuses, became further reddened, their corners flushed crimson with anger.

sanskrit: त्रिशिखां भ्रुकुटीं चास्य ददृशुः सर्वपार्थिवाः ।ललाटस्थां त्रिकूटस्थां गङ्गां त्रिपथगामिव ॥११॥
transliteration: triśikhāṁ bhrukuṭīṁ cāsya dadṛśuḥ sarvapārthivāḥ ,lalāṭasthāṁ trikūṭasthāṁ gaṅgāṁ tripathagāmiva.
english: All the kings saw his three-pointed frown on his forehead, which appeared like the Gaṅgā (Gaṅgā) situated on Mount Trikūṭa and flowing in three streams.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 11. All the kings saw his three-pointed frown on his forehead, which appeared like the Gaṅgā (Gaṅgā) situated on Mount Trikūṭa and flowing in three streams.

sanskrit: दन्तान्संदशतस्तस्य कोपाद्ददृशुराननम् ।युगान्ते सर्वभूतानि कालस्येव दिधक्षतः ॥१२॥
transliteration: dantānsaṁdaśatastasya kopāddadṛśurānanam ,yugānte sarvabhūtāni kālasyeva didhakṣataḥ.
english: They saw his face, as he gnashed his teeth in anger, resembling the face of Time (kāla) at the end of a cosmic age (yuga), intent on consuming all beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 12. They saw his face, as he gnashed his teeth in anger, resembling the face of Time (kāla) at the end of a cosmic age (yuga), intent on consuming all beings.

sanskrit: उत्पतन्तं तु वेगेन जग्राहैनं मनस्विनम् ।भीष्म एव महाबाहुर्महासेनमिवेश्वरः ॥१३॥
transliteration: utpatantaṁ tu vegena jagrāhainaṁ manasvinam ,bhīṣma eva mahābāhurmahāsenamiveśvaraḥ.
english: But the mighty-armed Bhīṣma indeed seized that spirited man as he surged forward with great force, just as Lord Īśvara (Īśvara) seized Mahāsena (Mahāsena).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 13. But the mighty-armed Bhīṣma indeed seized that spirited man as he surged forward with great force, just as Lord Īśvara (Īśvara) seized Mahāsena (Mahāsena).

sanskrit: तस्य भीमस्य भीष्मेण वार्यमाणस्य भारत ।गुरुणा विविधैर्वाक्यैः क्रोधः प्रशममागतः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tasya bhīmasya bhīṣmeṇa vāryamāṇasya bhārata ,guruṇā vividhairvākyaiḥ krodhaḥ praśamamāgataḥ.
english: O descendant of Bharata, when that Bhima was being restrained by Bhishma, his venerable elder, with various words, his anger subsided.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 14. O descendant of Bharata, when that Bhima was being restrained by Bhishma, his venerable elder, with various words, his anger subsided.

sanskrit: नातिचक्राम भीष्मस्य स हि वाक्यमरिंदमः ।समुद्धूतो घनापाये वेलामिव महोदधिः ॥१५॥
transliteration: nāticakrāma bhīṣmasya sa hi vākyamariṁdamaḥ ,samuddhūto ghanāpāye velāmiva mahodadhiḥ.
english: O vanquisher of foes, he (Bhima) indeed did not transgress Bhishma's command, just as the great ocean, stirred up at the end of the monsoon season, does not overstep its shore.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 15. O vanquisher of foes, he (Bhima) indeed did not transgress Bhishma's command, just as the great ocean, stirred up at the end of the monsoon season, does not overstep its shore.

sanskrit: शिशुपालस्तु संक्रुद्धे भीमसेने नराधिप ।नाकम्पत तदा वीरः पौरुषे स्वे व्यवस्थितः ॥१६॥
transliteration: śiśupālastu saṁkruddhe bhīmasene narādhipa ,nākampata tadā vīraḥ pauruṣe sve vyavasthitaḥ.
english: But, O king of men, when Bhimasena was greatly enraged, that hero Shishupala did not tremble at that time, remaining steadfast in his own valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 16. But, O king of men, when Bhimasena was greatly enraged, that hero Shishupala did not tremble at that time, remaining steadfast in his own valor.

sanskrit: उत्पतन्तं तु वेगेन पुनः पुनररिंदमः ।न स तं चिन्तयामास सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगं यथा ॥१७॥
transliteration: utpatantaṁ tu vegena punaḥ punarariṁdamaḥ ,na sa taṁ cintayāmāsa siṁhaḥ kṣudramṛgaṁ yathā.
english: But, O vanquisher of foes, he (Shishupala) did not bother about him (Bhima) who was repeatedly leaping up with great force, just as a lion does not concern itself with a small animal.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 17. But, O vanquisher of foes, he (Shishupala) did not bother about him (Bhima) who was repeatedly leaping up with great force, just as a lion does not concern itself with a small animal.

sanskrit: प्रहसंश्चाब्रवीद्वाक्यं चेदिराजः प्रतापवान् ।भीमसेनमतिक्रुद्धं दृष्ट्वा भीमपराक्रमम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: prahasaṁścābravīdvākyaṁ cedirājaḥ pratāpavān ,bhīmasenamatikruddhaṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmaparākramam.
english: The mighty King of Cedi, seeing Bhimasena exceedingly enraged and possessing terrible valor, spoke these words while laughing.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 18. The mighty King of Cedi, seeing Bhimasena exceedingly enraged and possessing terrible valor, spoke these words while laughing.

sanskrit: मुञ्चैनं भीष्म पश्यन्तु यावदेनं नराधिपाः ।मत्प्रतापाग्निनिर्दग्धं पतंगमिव वह्निना ॥१९॥
transliteration: muñcainaṁ bhīṣma paśyantu yāvadenaṁ narādhipāḥ ,matpratāpāgninirdagdhaṁ pataṁgamiva vahninā.
english: "O Bhishma, release him! Let these kings see him, burnt by the fire of my valor, just like a moth consumed by fire."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 19. "O Bhishma, release him! Let these kings see him, burnt by the fire of my valor, just like a moth consumed by fire."

sanskrit: ततश्चेदिपतेर्वाक्यं तच्छ्रुत्वा कुरुसत्तमः ।भीमसेनमुवाचेदं भीष्मो मतिमतां वरः ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataścedipatervākyaṁ tacchrutvā kurusattamaḥ ,bhīmasenamuvācedaṁ bhīṣmo matimatāṁ varaḥ.
english: Then, having heard those words of the King of Cedi, Bhishma, the best of the Kurus and the foremost among the intelligent, said this to Bhimasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 39 Verse 20. Then, having heard those words of the King of Cedi, Bhishma, the best of the Kurus and the foremost among the intelligent, said this to Bhimasena.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।चेदिराजकुले जातस्त्र्यक्ष एष चतुर्भुजः ।रासभारावसदृशं रुराव च ननाद च ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,cedirājakule jātastryakṣa eṣa caturbhujaḥ ,rāsabhārāvasadṛśaṁ rurāva ca nanāda ca.
english: Bhishma said: "This one (Śiśupāla), born in the royal family of the Cedi king, was (originally) three-eyed and four-armed. He then roared and bellowed like the braying of a donkey."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 1. Bhishma said: "This one (Śiśupāla), born in the royal family of the Cedi king, was (originally) three-eyed and four-armed. He then roared and bellowed like the braying of a donkey."

sanskrit: तेनास्य मातापितरौ त्रेसतुस्तौ सबान्धवौ ।वैकृतं तच्च तौ दृष्ट्वा त्यागाय कुरुतां मतिम् ॥२॥
transliteration: tenāsya mātāpitarau tresatustau sabāndhavau ,vaikṛtaṁ tacca tau dṛṣṭvā tyāgāya kurutāṁ matim.
english: His mother and father, along with their relatives, trembled because of him. And when they saw that monstrous deformity, they decided to abandon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 2. His mother and father, along with their relatives, trembled because of him. And when they saw that monstrous deformity, they decided to abandon him.

sanskrit: ततः सभार्यं नृपतिं सामात्यं सपुरोहितम् ।चिन्तासंमूढहृदयं वागुवाचाशरीरिणी ॥३॥
transliteration: tataḥ sabhāryaṁ nṛpatiṁ sāmātyaṁ sapurohitam ,cintāsaṁmūḍhahṛdayaṁ vāguvācāśarīriṇī.
english: Then, a bodiless voice spoke to the king, who was present with his wife, ministers, and priest, and whose heart was bewildered by worry.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 3. Then, a bodiless voice spoke to the king, who was present with his wife, ministers, and priest, and whose heart was bewildered by worry.

sanskrit: एष ते नृपते पुत्रः श्रीमाञ्जातो महाबलः ।तस्मादस्मान्न भेतव्यमव्यग्रः पाहि वै शिशुम् ॥४॥
transliteration: eṣa te nṛpate putraḥ śrīmāñjāto mahābalaḥ ,tasmādasmānna bhetavyamavyagraḥ pāhi vai śiśum.
english: O King, this glorious and greatly powerful son is born to you. Therefore, there is no need to fear him. Indeed, protect the child, remaining undisturbed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 4. O King, this glorious and greatly powerful son is born to you. Therefore, there is no need to fear him. Indeed, protect the child, remaining undisturbed.

sanskrit: न चैवैतस्य मृत्युस्त्वं न कालः प्रत्युपस्थितः ।मृत्युर्हन्तास्य शस्त्रेण स चोत्पन्नो नराधिप ॥५॥
transliteration: na caivaitasya mṛtyustvaṁ na kālaḥ pratyupasthitaḥ ,mṛtyurhantāsya śastreṇa sa cotpanno narādhipa.
english: And you are certainly not the cause of his death, nor is his time (kāla) at hand. O King, his slayer, who will use a weapon, has already been born.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 5. And you are certainly not the cause of his death, nor is his time (kāla) at hand. O King, his slayer, who will use a weapon, has already been born.

sanskrit: संश्रुत्योदाहृतं वाक्यं भूतमन्तर्हितं ततः ।पुत्रस्नेहाभिसंतप्ता जननी वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥६॥
transliteration: saṁśrutyodāhṛtaṁ vākyaṁ bhūtamantarhitaṁ tataḥ ,putrasnehābhisaṁtaptā jananī vākyamabravīt.
english: Having heard the words spoken by the hidden being, the mother, greatly agitated by affection for her son, then spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 6. Having heard the words spoken by the hidden being, the mother, greatly agitated by affection for her son, then spoke these words.

sanskrit: येनेदमीरितं वाक्यं ममैव तनयं प्रति ।प्राञ्जलिस्तं नमस्यामि ब्रवीतु स पुनर्वचः ॥७॥
transliteration: yenedamīritaṁ vākyaṁ mamaiva tanayaṁ prati ,prāñjalistaṁ namasyāmi bravītu sa punarvacaḥ.
english: "I salute him with folded hands by whom these words were spoken concerning my very son. Let him speak again."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 7. "I salute him with folded hands by whom these words were spoken concerning my very son. Let him speak again."

sanskrit: श्रोतुमिच्छामि पुत्रस्य कोऽस्य मृत्युर्भविष्यति ।अन्तर्हितं ततो भूतमुवाचेदं पुनर्वचः ॥८॥
transliteration: śrotumicchāmi putrasya ko'sya mṛtyurbhaviṣyati ,antarhitaṁ tato bhūtamuvācedaṁ punarvacaḥ.
english: "I wish to hear what the death of my son will be." Then the hidden being spoke these words again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 8. "I wish to hear what the death of my son will be." Then the hidden being spoke these words again.

sanskrit: येनोत्सङ्गे गृहीतस्य भुजावभ्यधिकावुभौ ।पतिष्यतः क्षितितले पञ्चशीर्षाविवोरगौ ॥९॥
transliteration: yenotsaṅge gṛhītasya bhujāvabhyadhikāvubhau ,patiṣyataḥ kṣititale pañcaśīrṣāvivoragau.
english: "The two exceedingly strong arms of him who has been taken into your lap will fall upon the ground like two five-headed snakes."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 9. "The two exceedingly strong arms of him who has been taken into your lap will fall upon the ground like two five-headed snakes."

sanskrit: तृतीयमेतद्बालस्य ललाटस्थं च लोचनम् ।निमज्जिष्यति यं दृष्ट्वा सोऽस्य मृत्युर्भविष्यति ॥१०॥
transliteration: tṛtīyametadbālasya lalāṭasthaṁ ca locanam ,nimajjiṣyati yaṁ dṛṣṭvā so'sya mṛtyurbhaviṣyati.
english: This is the child's third eye, located on his forehead. Whoever sees it will drown, and that very person will become the child's demise.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 10. This is the child's third eye, located on his forehead. Whoever sees it will drown, and that very person will become the child's demise.

sanskrit: त्र्यक्षं चतुर्भुजं श्रुत्वा तथा च समुदाहृतम् ।धरण्यां पार्थिवाः सर्वे अभ्यगच्छन्दिदृक्षवः ॥११॥
transliteration: tryakṣaṁ caturbhujaṁ śrutvā tathā ca samudāhṛtam ,dharaṇyāṁ pārthivāḥ sarve abhyagacchandidṛkṣavaḥ.
english: Having heard that (the child was) three-eyed and four-armed, as it was declared, all the kings on Earth approached, desirous of seeing him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 11. Having heard that (the child was) three-eyed and four-armed, as it was declared, all the kings on Earth approached, desirous of seeing him.

sanskrit: तान्पूजयित्वा संप्राप्तान्यथार्हं स महीपतिः ।एकैकस्य नृपस्याङ्के पुत्रमारोपयत्तदा ॥१२॥
transliteration: tānpūjayitvā saṁprāptānyathārhaṁ sa mahīpatiḥ ,ekaikasya nṛpasyāṅke putramāropayattadā.
english: Having honored those arrived kings appropriately, that ruler then placed his son on the lap of each and every king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 12. Having honored those arrived kings appropriately, that ruler then placed his son on the lap of each and every king.

sanskrit: एवं राजसहस्राणां पृथक्त्वेन यथाक्रमम् ।शिशुरङ्के समारूढो न तत्प्राप निदर्शनम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: evaṁ rājasahasrāṇāṁ pṛthaktvena yathākramam ,śiśuraṅke samārūḍho na tatprāpa nidarśanam.
english: In this manner, the child was successively placed on the laps of thousands of kings, one by one, yet he did not exhibit (or achieve) that specific manifestation (of his divine form).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 13. In this manner, the child was successively placed on the laps of thousands of kings, one by one, yet he did not exhibit (or achieve) that specific manifestation (of his divine form).

sanskrit: ततश्चेदिपुरं प्राप्तौ संकर्षणजनार्दनौ ।यादवौ यादवीं द्रष्टुं स्वसारं तां पितुस्तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tataścedipuraṁ prāptau saṁkarṣaṇajanārdanau ,yādavau yādavīṁ draṣṭuṁ svasāraṁ tāṁ pitustadā.
english: Then, the two Yadavas, Saṅkarṣaṇa and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), arrived at Cedipura to see their father's sister, that Yādava woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 14. Then, the two Yadavas, Saṅkarṣaṇa and Janārdana (Kṛṣṇa), arrived at Cedipura to see their father's sister, that Yādava woman.

sanskrit: अभिवाद्य यथान्यायं यथाज्येष्ठं नृपांश्च तान् ।कुशलानामयं पृष्ट्वा निषण्णौ रामकेशवौ ॥१५॥
transliteration: abhivādya yathānyāyaṁ yathājyeṣṭhaṁ nṛpāṁśca tān ,kuśalānāmayaṁ pṛṣṭvā niṣaṇṇau rāmakeśavau.
english: Having respectfully saluted those kings according to seniority and inquired about their welfare and freedom from disease, Rāma and Keśava sat down.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 15. Having respectfully saluted those kings according to seniority and inquired about their welfare and freedom from disease, Rāma and Keśava sat down.

sanskrit: अभ्यर्चितौ तदा वीरौ प्रीत्या चाभ्यधिकं ततः ।पुत्रं दामोदरोत्सङ्गे देवी संन्यदधात्स्वयम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: abhyarcitau tadā vīrau prītyā cābhyadhikaṁ tataḥ ,putraṁ dāmodarotsaṅge devī saṁnyadadhātsvayam.
english: Then, when the two heroes were greatly honored with affection, the queen herself placed her son on Dāmodara's (Kṛṣṇa's) lap.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 16. Then, when the two heroes were greatly honored with affection, the queen herself placed her son on Dāmodara's (Kṛṣṇa's) lap.

sanskrit: न्यस्तमात्रस्य तस्याङ्के भुजावभ्यधिकावुभौ ।पेततुस्तच्च नयनं निममज्ज ललाटजम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: nyastamātrasya tasyāṅke bhujāvabhyadhikāvubhau ,petatustacca nayanaṁ nimamajja lalāṭajam.
english: And when he was merely placed in his lap, both of his two additional arms fell, and that forehead-born eye disappeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 17. And when he was merely placed in his lap, both of his two additional arms fell, and that forehead-born eye disappeared.

sanskrit: तद्दृष्ट्वा व्यथिता त्रस्ता वरं कृष्णमयाचत ।ददस्व मे वरं कृष्ण भयार्ताया महाभुज ॥१८॥
transliteration: taddṛṣṭvā vyathitā trastā varaṁ kṛṣṇamayācata ,dadasva me varaṁ kṛṣṇa bhayārtāyā mahābhuja.
english: Seeing that, agitated and terrified, she requested a boon from Kṛṣṇa. (She said), "O mighty-armed Kṛṣṇa, grant a boon to me who am afflicted by fear."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 18. Seeing that, agitated and terrified, she requested a boon from Kṛṣṇa. (She said), "O mighty-armed Kṛṣṇa, grant a boon to me who am afflicted by fear."

sanskrit: त्वं ह्यार्तानां समाश्वासो भीतानामभयंकरः ।पितृष्वसारं मा भैषीरित्युवाच जनार्दनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: tvaṁ hyārtānāṁ samāśvāso bhītānāmabhayaṁkaraḥ ,pitṛṣvasāraṁ mā bhaiṣīrityuvāca janārdanaḥ.
english: “Indeed, you are the solace of the distressed and the giver of fearlessness to the terrified.” Thus Janārdana said to his paternal aunt, “Do not fear.”
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 19. “Indeed, you are the solace of the distressed and the giver of fearlessness to the terrified.” Thus Janārdana said to his paternal aunt, “Do not fear.”

sanskrit: ददानि कं वरं किं वा करवाणि पितृष्वसः ।शक्यं वा यदि वाशक्यं करिष्यामि वचस्तव ॥२०॥
transliteration: dadāni kaṁ varaṁ kiṁ vā karavāṇi pitṛṣvasaḥ ,śakyaṁ vā yadi vāśakyaṁ kariṣyāmi vacastava.
english: “What boon shall I grant, or what shall I do, O paternal aunt? Whether it is possible or impossible, I will carry out your word.”
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 20. “What boon shall I grant, or what shall I do, O paternal aunt? Whether it is possible or impossible, I will carry out your word.”

sanskrit: एवमुक्ता ततः कृष्णमब्रवीद्यदुनन्दनम् ।शिशुपालस्यापराधान्क्षमेथास्त्वं महाबल ॥२१॥
transliteration: evamuktā tataḥ kṛṣṇamabravīdyadunandanam ,śiśupālasyāparādhānkṣamethāstvaṁ mahābala.
english: Having been addressed thus, she then said to Kṛṣṇa, the delight of the Yadus: “O mighty one, you should forgive the offenses of Śiśupāla.”
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 21. Having been addressed thus, she then said to Kṛṣṇa, the delight of the Yadus: “O mighty one, you should forgive the offenses of Śiśupāla.”

sanskrit: कृष्ण उवाच ।अपराधशतं क्षाम्यं मया ह्यस्य पितृष्वसः ।पुत्रस्य ते वधार्हाणां मा त्वं शोके मनः कृथाः ॥२२॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇa uvāca ,aparādhaśataṁ kṣāmyaṁ mayā hyasya pitṛṣvasaḥ ,putrasya te vadhārhāṇāṁ mā tvaṁ śoke manaḥ kṛthāḥ.
english: Kṛṣṇa said: O aunt (pitṛṣvasṛ), I have indeed forgiven a hundred offenses committed by your son. Do not let your mind be troubled with sorrow for those who deserve death.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 22. Kṛṣṇa said: O aunt (pitṛṣvasṛ), I have indeed forgiven a hundred offenses committed by your son. Do not let your mind be troubled with sorrow for those who deserve death.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।एवमेष नृपः पापः शिशुपालः सुमन्दधीः ।त्वां समाह्वयते वीर गोविन्दवरदर्पितः ॥२३॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,evameṣa nṛpaḥ pāpaḥ śiśupālaḥ sumandadhīḥ ,tvāṁ samāhvayate vīra govindavaradarpitaḥ.
english: Bhīṣma said: O hero, this sinful, extremely dull-witted King Śiśupāla indeed challenges you, arrogant due to the boon granted by Govinda.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 40 Verse 23. Bhīṣma said: O hero, this sinful, extremely dull-witted King Śiśupāla indeed challenges you, arrogant due to the boon granted by Govinda.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।नैषा चेदिपतेर्बुद्धिर्यया त्वाह्वयतेऽच्युतम् ।नूनमेष जगद्भर्तुः कृष्णस्यैव विनिश्चयः ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,naiṣā cedipaterbuddhiryayā tvāhvayate'cyutam ,nūnameṣa jagadbhartuḥ kṛṣṇasyaiva viniścayaḥ.
english: Bhīṣma said: This is not the intellect (buddhi) of the king of Cedi, by which he challenges you, Acyuta. Surely, this is indeed the firm resolve of Kṛṣṇa, the maintainer of the universe.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 1. Bhīṣma said: This is not the intellect (buddhi) of the king of Cedi, by which he challenges you, Acyuta. Surely, this is indeed the firm resolve of Kṛṣṇa, the maintainer of the universe.

sanskrit: को हि मां भीमसेनाद्य क्षितावर्हति पार्थिवः ।क्षेप्तुं दैवपरीतात्मा यथैष कुलपांसनः ॥२॥
transliteration: ko hi māṁ bhīmasenādya kṣitāvarhati pārthivaḥ ,kṣeptuṁ daivaparītātmā yathaiṣa kulapāṁsanaḥ.
english: O Bhīmasena and others (Bhīmasenādya), which king on this earth, whose inner being (ātman) is afflicted by destiny, would indeed dare to insult me, just as this disgracer of his family does?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 2. O Bhīmasena and others (Bhīmasenādya), which king on this earth, whose inner being (ātman) is afflicted by destiny, would indeed dare to insult me, just as this disgracer of his family does?

sanskrit: एष ह्यस्य महाबाहो तेजोंशश्च हरेर्ध्रुवम् ।तमेव पुनरादातुमिच्छत्पृथुयशा हरिः ॥३॥
transliteration: eṣa hyasya mahābāho tejoṁśaśca harerdhruvam ,tameva punarādātumicchatpṛthuyaśā hariḥ.
english: O mighty-armed one, this [Śiśupāla] is indeed a certain portion of Hari's (Viṣṇu's) splendor and power. The widely renowned Hari (Viṣṇu) Himself desires to reclaim him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 3. O mighty-armed one, this [Śiśupāla] is indeed a certain portion of Hari's (Viṣṇu's) splendor and power. The widely renowned Hari (Viṣṇu) Himself desires to reclaim him.

sanskrit: येनैष कुरुशार्दूल शार्दूल इव चेदिराट् ।गर्जत्यतीव दुर्बुद्धिः सर्वानस्मानचिन्तयन् ॥४॥
transliteration: yenaiṣa kuruśārdūla śārdūla iva cedirāṭ ,garjatyatīva durbuddhiḥ sarvānasmānacintayan.
english: O tiger among the Kurus, it is by this (power) that this foolish king of Cedi roars excessively like a tiger, utterly disregarding all of us.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 4. O tiger among the Kurus, it is by this (power) that this foolish king of Cedi roars excessively like a tiger, utterly disregarding all of us.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो न ममृषे चैद्यस्तद्भीष्मवचनं तदा ।उवाच चैनं संक्रुद्धः पुनर्भीष्ममथोत्तरम् ॥५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato na mamṛṣe caidyastadbhīṣmavacanaṁ tadā ,uvāca cainaṁ saṁkruddhaḥ punarbhīṣmamathottaram.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thereupon, the king of Cedi (Śiśupāla) could not tolerate those words of Bhīṣma. Then, greatly enraged, he replied to Bhīṣma once again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 5. Vaiśampāyana said: Thereupon, the king of Cedi (Śiśupāla) could not tolerate those words of Bhīṣma. Then, greatly enraged, he replied to Bhīṣma once again.

sanskrit: शिशुपाल उवाच ।द्विषतां नोऽस्तु भीष्मैष प्रभावः केशवस्य यः ।यस्य संस्तववक्ता त्वं बन्दिवत्सततोत्थितः ॥६॥
transliteration: śiśupāla uvāca ,dviṣatāṁ no'stu bhīṣmaiṣa prabhāvaḥ keśavasya yaḥ ,yasya saṁstavavaktā tvaṁ bandivatsatatotthitaḥ.
english: Śiśupāla said: O Bhīṣma, let this influence of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) be for our enemies! You are his eulogizer, always standing up like a bard (bandi).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 6. Śiśupāla said: O Bhīṣma, let this influence of Keśava (Kṛṣṇa) be for our enemies! You are his eulogizer, always standing up like a bard (bandi).

sanskrit: संस्तवाय मनो भीष्म परेषां रमते सदा ।यदि संस्तौषि राज्ञस्त्वमिमं हित्वा जनार्दनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: saṁstavāya mano bhīṣma pareṣāṁ ramate sadā ,yadi saṁstauṣi rājñastvamimaṁ hitvā janārdanam.
english: O Bhishma, your mind always delights in praising others. If you praise the king, abandoning this Janardana (Kṛṣṇa)...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 7. O Bhishma, your mind always delights in praising others. If you praise the king, abandoning this Janardana (Kṛṣṇa)...

sanskrit: दरदं स्तुहि बाह्लीकमिमं पार्थिवसत्तमम् ।जायमानेन येनेयमभवद्दारिता मही ॥८॥
transliteration: daradaṁ stuhi bāhlīkamimaṁ pārthivasattamam ,jāyamānena yeneyamabhavaddāritā mahī.
english: Praise this Darada Bahlika, the best among kings, by whom, at his birth, this earth was torn apart.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 8. Praise this Darada Bahlika, the best among kings, by whom, at his birth, this earth was torn apart.

sanskrit: वङ्गाङ्गविषयाध्यक्षं सहस्राक्षसमं बले ।स्तुहि कर्णमिमं भीष्म महाचापविकर्षणम् ॥९॥
transliteration: vaṅgāṅgaviṣayādhyakṣaṁ sahasrākṣasamaṁ bale ,stuhi karṇamimaṁ bhīṣma mahācāpavikarṣaṇam.
english: O Bhishma, praise this Karna, the lord of the Vanga and Anga regions, who is equal to Indra (Sahasrākṣa) in strength, and who wields the mighty bow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 9. O Bhishma, praise this Karna, the lord of the Vanga and Anga regions, who is equal to Indra (Sahasrākṣa) in strength, and who wields the mighty bow.

sanskrit: द्रोणं द्रौणिं च साधु त्वं पितापुत्रौ महारथौ ।स्तुहि स्तुत्याविमौ भीष्म सततं द्विजसत्तमौ ॥१०॥
transliteration: droṇaṁ drauṇiṁ ca sādhu tvaṁ pitāputrau mahārathau ,stuhi stutyāvimau bhīṣma satataṁ dvijasattamau.
english: O Bhishma, you should indeed constantly praise these two, Drona and Drona's son (Drauṇi) – the father and son, both great charioteers and the best among the twice-born (dvija) – for they are worthy of praise.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 10. O Bhishma, you should indeed constantly praise these two, Drona and Drona's son (Drauṇi) – the father and son, both great charioteers and the best among the twice-born (dvija) – for they are worthy of praise.

sanskrit: ययोरन्यतरो भीष्म संक्रुद्धः सचराचराम् ।इमां वसुमतीं कुर्यादशेषामिति मे मतिः ॥११॥
transliteration: yayoranyataro bhīṣma saṁkruddhaḥ sacarācarām ,imāṁ vasumatīṁ kuryādaśeṣāmiti me matiḥ.
english: O Bhishma, it is my opinion that one of these two, if greatly enraged, could utterly destroy this earth with all its moving and non-moving beings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 11. O Bhishma, it is my opinion that one of these two, if greatly enraged, could utterly destroy this earth with all its moving and non-moving beings.

sanskrit: द्रोणस्य हि समं युद्धे न पश्यामि नराधिपम् ।अश्वत्थाम्नस्तथा भीष्म न चैतौ स्तोतुमिच्छसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: droṇasya hi samaṁ yuddhe na paśyāmi narādhipam ,aśvatthāmnastathā bhīṣma na caitau stotumicchasi.
english: O Bhishma, I truly do not see any king equal to Drona in battle, nor any equal to Ashwatthama. Yet, you do not wish to praise these two.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 12. O Bhishma, I truly do not see any king equal to Drona in battle, nor any equal to Ashwatthama. Yet, you do not wish to praise these two.

sanskrit: शल्यादीनपि कस्मात्त्वं न स्तौषि वसुधाधिपान् ।स्तवाय यदि ते बुद्धिर्वर्तते भीष्म सर्वदा ॥१३॥
