transliteration: śalyādīnapi kasmāttvaṁ na stauṣi vasudhādhipān ,stavāya yadi te buddhirvartate bhīṣma sarvadā.
english: O Bhishma, if your intention is always to praise, why do you not also praise kings like Shalya and the others?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 13. O Bhishma, if your intention is always to praise, why do you not also praise kings like Shalya and the others?

sanskrit: किं हि शक्यं मया कर्तुं यद्वृद्धानां त्वया नृप ।पुरा कथयतां नूनं न श्रुतं धर्मवादिनाम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: kiṁ hi śakyaṁ mayā kartuṁ yadvṛddhānāṁ tvayā nṛpa ,purā kathayatāṁ nūnaṁ na śrutaṁ dharmavādinām.
english: O King, what can I possibly do now, since you certainly did not listen previously to the elders and those who spoke about natural law (dharma)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 14. O King, what can I possibly do now, since you certainly did not listen previously to the elders and those who spoke about natural law (dharma)?

sanskrit: आत्मनिन्दात्मपूजा च परनिन्दा परस्तवः ।अनाचरितमार्याणां वृत्तमेतच्चतुर्विधम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: ātmanindātmapūjā ca paranindā parastavaḥ ,anācaritamāryāṇāṁ vṛttametaccaturvidham.
english: Self-criticism, self-praise, criticizing others, and praising others - this four-fold behavior is not practiced by noble individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 15. Self-criticism, self-praise, criticizing others, and praising others - this four-fold behavior is not practiced by noble individuals.

sanskrit: यदस्तव्यमिमं शश्वन्मोहात्संस्तौषि भक्तितः ।केशवं तच्च ते भीष्म न कश्चिदनुमन्यते ॥१६॥
transliteration: yadastavyamimaṁ śaśvanmohātsaṁstauṣi bhaktitaḥ ,keśavaṁ tacca te bhīṣma na kaścidanumanyate.
english: O Bhīṣma, because you constantly praise this Keśava, who is unworthy of praise, out of delusion and devotion (bhakti), no one approves of that (praise) of yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 16. O Bhīṣma, because you constantly praise this Keśava, who is unworthy of praise, out of delusion and devotion (bhakti), no one approves of that (praise) of yours.

sanskrit: कथं भोजस्य पुरुषे वर्गपाले दुरात्मनि ।समावेशयसे सर्वं जगत्केवलकाम्यया ॥१७॥
transliteration: kathaṁ bhojasya puruṣe vargapāle durātmani ,samāveśayase sarvaṁ jagatkevalakāmyayā.
english: How can you, merely out of desire, encompass the entire universe within this person (puruṣa) of Bhoja's lineage, who is a chief of a faction and a wicked individual (durātman)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 17. How can you, merely out of desire, encompass the entire universe within this person (puruṣa) of Bhoja's lineage, who is a chief of a faction and a wicked individual (durātman)?

sanskrit: अथ वैषा न ते भक्तिः प्रकृतिं याति भारत ।मयैव कथितं पूर्वं भूलिङ्गशकुनिर्यथा ॥१८॥
transliteration: atha vaiṣā na te bhaktiḥ prakṛtiṁ yāti bhārata ,mayaiva kathitaṁ pūrvaṁ bhūliṅgaśakuniryathā.
english: Or else, O Bhārata, this devotion (bhakti) of yours does not attain its true intrinsic nature (prakṛti). Indeed, I have said this before, just like the bhūliṅga bird.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 18. Or else, O Bhārata, this devotion (bhakti) of yours does not attain its true intrinsic nature (prakṛti). Indeed, I have said this before, just like the bhūliṅga bird.

sanskrit: भूलिङ्गशकुनिर्नाम पार्श्वे हिमवतः परे ।भीष्म तस्याः सदा वाचः श्रूयन्तेऽर्थविगर्हिताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhūliṅgaśakunirnāma pārśve himavataḥ pare ,bhīṣma tasyāḥ sadā vācaḥ śrūyante'rthavigarhitāḥ.
english: O Bhishma, beyond the Himalayas, there is a bird named Bhūliṅga. Its utterances are always heard, full of reprehensible meaning.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 19. O Bhishma, beyond the Himalayas, there is a bird named Bhūliṅga. Its utterances are always heard, full of reprehensible meaning.

sanskrit: मा साहसमितीदं सा सततं वाशते किल ।साहसं चात्मनातीव चरन्ती नावबुध्यते ॥२०॥
transliteration: mā sāhasamitīdaṁ sā satataṁ vāśate kila ,sāhasaṁ cātmanātīva carantī nāvabudhyate.
english: "Do not be rash!" This is what she constantly cries out. Yet, she herself, acting with extreme recklessness, does not comprehend her own actions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 20. "Do not be rash!" This is what she constantly cries out. Yet, she herself, acting with extreme recklessness, does not comprehend her own actions.

sanskrit: सा हि मांसार्गलं भीष्म मुखात्सिंहस्य खादतः ।दन्तान्तरविलग्नं यत्तदादत्तेऽल्पचेतना ॥२१॥
transliteration: sā hi māṁsārgalaṁ bhīṣma mukhātsiṁhasya khādataḥ ,dantāntaravilagnaṁ yattadādatte'lpacetanā.
english: O Bhishma, indeed, that foolish bird snatches a bolt of meat that is stuck between the teeth of a lion as it is eating.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 21. O Bhishma, indeed, that foolish bird snatches a bolt of meat that is stuck between the teeth of a lion as it is eating.

sanskrit: इच्छतः सा हि सिंहस्य भीष्म जीवत्यसंशयम् ।तद्वत्त्वमप्यधर्मज्ञ सदा वाचः प्रभाषसे ॥२२॥
transliteration: icchataḥ sā hi siṁhasya bhīṣma jīvatyasaṁśayam ,tadvattvamapyadharmajña sadā vācaḥ prabhāṣase.
english: O Bhishma, indeed, she survives without doubt only because the lion allows it. Similarly, you, who are ignorant of natural law (dharma), constantly utter such words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 22. O Bhishma, indeed, she survives without doubt only because the lion allows it. Similarly, you, who are ignorant of natural law (dharma), constantly utter such words.

sanskrit: इच्छतां पार्थिवेन्द्राणां भीष्म जीवस्यसंशयम् ।लोकविद्विष्टकर्मा हि नान्योऽस्ति भवता समः ॥२३॥
transliteration: icchatāṁ pārthivendrāṇāṁ bhīṣma jīvasyasaṁśayam ,lokavidviṣṭakarmā hi nānyo'sti bhavatā samaḥ.
english: O Bhishma, among these supreme rulers who are undoubtedly eager for your life (death), there is indeed no one else whose actions (karma) are as detested by the people as yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 23. O Bhishma, among these supreme rulers who are undoubtedly eager for your life (death), there is indeed no one else whose actions (karma) are as detested by the people as yours.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततश्चेदिपतेः श्रुत्वा भीष्मः स कटुकं वचः ।उवाचेदं वचो राजंश्चेदिराजस्य शृण्वतः ॥२४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataścedipateḥ śrutvā bhīṣmaḥ sa kaṭukaṁ vacaḥ ,uvācedaṁ vaco rājaṁścedirājasya śṛṇvataḥ.
english: Vaishampayana said: Then, after hearing those bitter words from the lord of Chedi, Bhishma, O King (Janamejaya), spoke these words while the king of Chedi himself was listening.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 24. Vaishampayana said: Then, after hearing those bitter words from the lord of Chedi, Bhishma, O King (Janamejaya), spoke these words while the king of Chedi himself was listening.

sanskrit: इच्छतां किल नामाहं जीवाम्येषां महीक्षिताम् ।योऽहं न गणयाम्येतांस्तृणानीव नराधिपान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: icchatāṁ kila nāmāhaṁ jīvāmyeṣāṁ mahīkṣitām ,yo'haṁ na gaṇayāmyetāṁstṛṇānīva narādhipān.
english: It is said that I live merely to fulfill the desires of these very kings. But I, for my part, do not value these rulers of men any more than blades of grass.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 25. It is said that I live merely to fulfill the desires of these very kings. But I, for my part, do not value these rulers of men any more than blades of grass.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते तु भीष्मेण ततः संचुक्रुधुर्नृपाः ।केचिज्जहृषिरे तत्र केचिद्भीष्मं जगर्हिरे ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamukte tu bhīṣmeṇa tataḥ saṁcukrudhurnṛpāḥ ,kecijjahṛṣire tatra kecidbhīṣmaṁ jagarhire.
english: When Bhishma had spoken thus, the kings then indeed became enraged. Some among them rejoiced there, while others censured Bhishma.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 26. When Bhishma had spoken thus, the kings then indeed became enraged. Some among them rejoiced there, while others censured Bhishma.

sanskrit: केचिदूचुर्महेष्वासाः श्रुत्वा भीष्मस्य तद्वचः ।पापोऽवलिप्तो वृद्धश्च नायं भीष्मोऽर्हति क्षमाम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: kecidūcurmaheṣvāsāḥ śrutvā bhīṣmasya tadvacaḥ ,pāpo'valipto vṛddhaśca nāyaṁ bhīṣmo'rhati kṣamām.
english: Some great archers, having heard those words of Bhishma, said: "This Bhishma, who is sinful, arrogant, and old, does not deserve forgiveness."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 27. Some great archers, having heard those words of Bhishma, said: "This Bhishma, who is sinful, arrogant, and old, does not deserve forgiveness."

sanskrit: हन्यतां दुर्मतिर्भीष्मः पशुवत्साध्वयं नृपैः ।सर्वैः समेत्य संरब्धैर्दह्यतां वा कटाग्निना ॥२८॥
transliteration: hanyatāṁ durmatirbhīṣmaḥ paśuvatsādhvayaṁ nṛpaiḥ ,sarvaiḥ sametya saṁrabdhairdahyatāṁ vā kaṭāgninā.
english: Let this foolish (durmati) Bhishma be killed like an animal by all the enraged kings who have gathered, or let him be burned with fire from dry grass (kaṭāgninā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 28. Let this foolish (durmati) Bhishma be killed like an animal by all the enraged kings who have gathered, or let him be burned with fire from dry grass (kaṭāgninā).

sanskrit: इति तेषां वचः श्रुत्वा ततः कुरुपितामहः ।उवाच मतिमान्भीष्मस्तानेव वसुधाधिपान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: iti teṣāṁ vacaḥ śrutvā tataḥ kurupitāmahaḥ ,uvāca matimānbhīṣmastāneva vasudhādhipān.
english: Having heard those words of theirs, then Bhishma, the wise (matimān) grandfather (pitāmaha) of the Kurus, spoke to those very rulers of the earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 29. Having heard those words of theirs, then Bhishma, the wise (matimān) grandfather (pitāmaha) of the Kurus, spoke to those very rulers of the earth.

sanskrit: उक्तस्योक्तस्य नेहान्तमहं समुपलक्षये ।यत्तु वक्ष्यामि तत्सर्वं शृणुध्वं वसुधाधिपाः ॥३०॥
transliteration: uktasyoktasya nehāntamahaṁ samupalakṣaye ,yattu vakṣyāmi tatsarvaṁ śṛṇudhvaṁ vasudhādhipāḥ.
english: Bhishma said: "I do not see the end of what has been said (by you) in this matter. But whatever I am about to say, all of that, O rulers of the earth, please hear."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 30. Bhishma said: "I do not see the end of what has been said (by you) in this matter. But whatever I am about to say, all of that, O rulers of the earth, please hear."

sanskrit: पशुवद्घातनं वा मे दहनं वा कटाग्निना ।क्रियतां मूर्ध्नि वो न्यस्तं मयेदं सकलं पदम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: paśuvadghātanaṁ vā me dahanaṁ vā kaṭāgninā ,kriyatāṁ mūrdhni vo nyastaṁ mayedaṁ sakalaṁ padam.
english: Whether I am killed like an animal or burned with a fire of dry grass, let it be done. I have placed this entire responsibility on your heads.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 31. Whether I am killed like an animal or burned with a fire of dry grass, let it be done. I have placed this entire responsibility on your heads.

sanskrit: एष तिष्ठति गोविन्दः पूजितोऽस्माभिरच्युतः ।यस्य वस्त्वरते बुद्धिर्मरणाय स माधवम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: eṣa tiṣṭhati govindaḥ pūjito'smābhiracyutaḥ ,yasya vastvarate buddhirmaraṇāya sa mādhavam.
english: This Govinda, the unfailing (Acyuta), stands here, worshipped by us. Whoever's intellect (buddhi) is engrossed in material objects (vastvarati) will find death instead of [the protection of] Mādhava.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 32. This Govinda, the unfailing (Acyuta), stands here, worshipped by us. Whoever's intellect (buddhi) is engrossed in material objects (vastvarati) will find death instead of [the protection of] Mādhava.

sanskrit: कृष्णमाह्वयतामद्य युद्धे शार्ङ्गगदाधरम् ।यावदस्यैव देवस्य देहं विशतु पातितः ॥३३॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇamāhvayatāmadya yuddhe śārṅgagadādharam ,yāvadasyaiva devasya dehaṁ viśatu pātitaḥ.
english: Let them summon Kṛṣṇa today in battle, him who holds the Śārṅga bow and mace! Let the one who is struck down enter the body of this very god (deva).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 41 Verse 33. Let them summon Kṛṣṇa today in battle, him who holds the Śārṅga bow and mace! Let the one who is struck down enter the body of this very god (deva).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः श्रुत्वैव भीष्मस्य चेदिराडुरुविक्रमः ।युयुत्सुर्वासुदेवेन वासुदेवमुवाच ह ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ śrutvaiva bhīṣmasya cedirāḍuruvikramaḥ ,yuyutsurvāsudevena vāsudevamuvāca ha.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, upon hearing Bhīṣma's words, the Cedi king, mighty in valor and eager to fight with Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), spoke to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), indeed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then, upon hearing Bhīṣma's words, the Cedi king, mighty in valor and eager to fight with Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), spoke to Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), indeed.

sanskrit: आह्वये त्वां रणं गच्छ मया सार्धं जनार्दन ।यावदद्य निहन्मि त्वां सहितं सर्वपाण्डवैः ॥२॥
transliteration: āhvaye tvāṁ raṇaṁ gaccha mayā sārdhaṁ janārdana ,yāvadadya nihanmi tvāṁ sahitaṁ sarvapāṇḍavaiḥ.
english: O Janardana, I challenge you; come to battle with me! Today I will kill you along with all the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 2. O Janardana, I challenge you; come to battle with me! Today I will kill you along with all the Pandavas.

sanskrit: सह त्वया हि मे वध्याः पाण्डवाः कृष्ण सर्वथा ।नृपतीन्समतिक्रम्य यैरराजा त्वमर्चितः ॥३॥
transliteration: saha tvayā hi me vadhyāḥ pāṇḍavāḥ kṛṣṇa sarvathā ,nṛpatīnsamatikramya yairarājā tvamarcitaḥ.
english: O Krishna, indeed, the Pandavas, along with you, are certainly to be killed by me. This is because, disregarding all other kings, they worshipped you, even though you are not a king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 3. O Krishna, indeed, the Pandavas, along with you, are certainly to be killed by me. This is because, disregarding all other kings, they worshipped you, even though you are not a king.

sanskrit: ये त्वां दासमराजानं बाल्यादर्चन्ति दुर्मतिम् ।अनर्हमर्हवत्कृष्ण वध्यास्त इति मे मतिः ।इत्युक्त्वा राजशार्दूलस्तस्थौ गर्जन्नमर्षणः ॥४॥
transliteration: ye tvāṁ dāsamarājānaṁ bālyādarcanti durmatim ,anarhamarhavatkṛṣṇa vadhyāsta iti me matiḥ ,ityuktvā rājaśārdūlastasthau garjannamarṣaṇaḥ.
english: O Krishna, it is my conviction that those who, out of foolishness, worship you  -  a servant, not a king, and evil-minded  -  treating one unworthy as if worthy, are to be killed. Having spoken thus, Duryodhana, the foremost of kings, stood roaring, filled with anger.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 4. O Krishna, it is my conviction that those who, out of foolishness, worship you  -  a servant, not a king, and evil-minded  -  treating one unworthy as if worthy, are to be killed. Having spoken thus, Duryodhana, the foremost of kings, stood roaring, filled with anger.

sanskrit: एवमुक्ते ततः कृष्णो मृदुपूर्वमिदं वचः ।उवाच पार्थिवान्सर्वांस्तत्समक्षं च पाण्डवान् ॥५॥
transliteration: evamukte tataḥ kṛṣṇo mṛdupūrvamidaṁ vacaḥ ,uvāca pārthivānsarvāṁstatsamakṣaṁ ca pāṇḍavān.
english: After Duryodhana had spoken thus, Krishna then addressed all the kings with these gentle words, and similarly spoke to the Pandavas in their presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 5. After Duryodhana had spoken thus, Krishna then addressed all the kings with these gentle words, and similarly spoke to the Pandavas in their presence.

sanskrit: एष नः शत्रुरत्यन्तं पार्थिवाः सात्वतीसुतः ।सात्वतानां नृशंसात्मा न हितोऽनपकारिणाम् ॥६॥
transliteration: eṣa naḥ śatruratyantaṁ pārthivāḥ sātvatīsutaḥ ,sātvatānāṁ nṛśaṁsātmā na hito'napakāriṇām.
english: O kings, this son of Sātvatī is our absolute enemy. His nature (ātman) is cruel towards the Sātvatas, and he is not benevolent even to those who cause him no harm.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 6. O kings, this son of Sātvatī is our absolute enemy. His nature (ātman) is cruel towards the Sātvatas, and he is not benevolent even to those who cause him no harm.

sanskrit: प्राग्ज्योतिषपुरं यातानस्माञ्ज्ञात्वा नृशंसकृत् ।अदहद्द्वारकामेष स्वस्रीयः सन्नराधिपाः ॥७॥
transliteration: prāgjyotiṣapuraṁ yātānasmāñjñātvā nṛśaṁsakṛt ,adahaddvārakāmeṣa svasrīyaḥ sannarādhipāḥ.
english: O kings, knowing that we had gone to Prāgjyotiṣapura, this cruel individual burned Dvārakā, even though he was our own nephew.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 7. O kings, knowing that we had gone to Prāgjyotiṣapura, this cruel individual burned Dvārakā, even though he was our own nephew.

sanskrit: क्रीडतो भोजराजन्यानेष रैवतके गिरौ ।हत्वा बद्ध्वा च तान्सर्वानुपायात्स्वपुरं पुरा ॥८॥
transliteration: krīḍato bhojarājanyāneṣa raivatake girau ,hatvā baddhvā ca tānsarvānupāyātsvapuraṁ purā.
english: Formerly, this individual (Kṛṣṇa), having killed and captured all those Bhoja and Rājanya kings who were engaged in sport on Mount Raivataka, returned to his own city.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 8. Formerly, this individual (Kṛṣṇa), having killed and captured all those Bhoja and Rājanya kings who were engaged in sport on Mount Raivataka, returned to his own city.

sanskrit: अश्वमेधे हयं मेध्यमुत्सृष्टं रक्षिभिर्वृतम् ।पितुर्मे यज्ञविघ्नार्थमहरत्पापनिश्चयः ॥९॥
transliteration: aśvamedhe hayaṁ medhyamutsṛṣṭaṁ rakṣibhirvṛtam ,piturme yajñavighnārthamaharatpāpaniścayaḥ.
english: This evil-minded one (pāpaniścaya), for the purpose of obstructing my father's horse sacrifice (aśvamedha), carried away the sacrificial (medhya) horse that had been released and was surrounded by guards.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 9. This evil-minded one (pāpaniścaya), for the purpose of obstructing my father's horse sacrifice (aśvamedha), carried away the sacrificial (medhya) horse that had been released and was surrounded by guards.

sanskrit: सौवीरान्प्रतिपत्तौ च बभ्रोरेष यशस्विनः ।भार्यामभ्यहरन्मोहादकामां तामितो गताम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: sauvīrānpratipattau ca babhroreṣa yaśasvinaḥ ,bhāryāmabhyaharanmohādakāmāṁ tāmito gatām.
english: This glorious Babhru, in the matter of the Sauvīras, out of delusion (moha), abducted his unwilling wife, who had gone there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 10. This glorious Babhru, in the matter of the Sauvīras, out of delusion (moha), abducted his unwilling wife, who had gone there.

sanskrit: एष मायाप्रतिच्छन्नः करूषार्थे तपस्विनीम् ।जहार भद्रां वैशालीं मातुलस्य नृशंसकृत् ॥११॥
transliteration: eṣa māyāpraticchannaḥ karūṣārthe tapasvinīm ,jahāra bhadrāṁ vaiśālīṁ mātulasya nṛśaṁsakṛt.
english: This cruel person, disguised by illusion (māyā), abducted the ascetic Bhadrā, a princess from Vaiśālī, for his maternal uncle's sake concerning the Karūṣas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 11. This cruel person, disguised by illusion (māyā), abducted the ascetic Bhadrā, a princess from Vaiśālī, for his maternal uncle's sake concerning the Karūṣas.

sanskrit: पितृष्वसुः कृते दुःखं सुमहन्मर्षयाम्यहम् ।दिष्ट्या त्विदं सर्वराज्ञां संनिधावद्य वर्तते ॥१२॥
transliteration: pitṛṣvasuḥ kṛte duḥkhaṁ sumahanmarṣayāmyaham ,diṣṭyā tvidaṁ sarvarājñāṁ saṁnidhāvadya vartate.
english: I endure this very great sorrow on account of my paternal aunt. Fortunately, this matter is now unfolding in the presence of all kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 12. I endure this very great sorrow on account of my paternal aunt. Fortunately, this matter is now unfolding in the presence of all kings.

sanskrit: पश्यन्ति हि भवन्तोऽद्य मय्यतीव व्यतिक्रमम् ।कृतानि तु परोक्षं मे यानि तानि निबोधत ॥१३॥
transliteration: paśyanti hi bhavanto'dya mayyatīva vyatikramam ,kṛtāni tu parokṣaṁ me yāni tāni nibodhata.
english: Indeed, you all now observe a great transgression in me. But concerning those deeds of mine that were performed secretly, please understand them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 13. Indeed, you all now observe a great transgression in me. But concerning those deeds of mine that were performed secretly, please understand them.

sanskrit: इमं त्वस्य न शक्ष्यामि क्षन्तुमद्य व्यतिक्रमम् ।अवलेपाद्वधार्हस्य समग्रे राजमण्डले ॥१४॥
transliteration: imaṁ tvasya na śakṣyāmi kṣantumadya vyatikramam ,avalepādvadhārhasya samagre rājamaṇḍale.
english: Today, I cannot forgive this transgression of his. This man, deserving of death, committed this offense out of sheer arrogance in front of the entire assembly of kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 14. Today, I cannot forgive this transgression of his. This man, deserving of death, committed this offense out of sheer arrogance in front of the entire assembly of kings.

sanskrit: रुक्मिण्यामस्य मूढस्य प्रार्थनासीन्मुमूर्षतः ।न च तां प्राप्तवान्मूढः शूद्रो वेदश्रुतिं यथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: rukmiṇyāmasya mūḍhasya prārthanāsīnmumūrṣataḥ ,na ca tāṁ prāptavānmūḍhaḥ śūdro vedaśrutiṁ yathā.
english: This foolish man, who was as good as dead, had a desire for Rukmiṇī. Yet, this deluded one did not obtain her, just as a Śūdra cannot obtain Vedic knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 15. This foolish man, who was as good as dead, had a desire for Rukmiṇī. Yet, this deluded one did not obtain her, just as a Śūdra cannot obtain Vedic knowledge.

sanskrit: एवमादि ततः सर्वे सहितास्ते नराधिपाः ।वासुदेववचः श्रुत्वा चेदिराजं व्यगर्हयन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: evamādi tataḥ sarve sahitāste narādhipāḥ ,vāsudevavacaḥ śrutvā cedirājaṁ vyagarhayan.
english: After hearing the words of Vāsudeva (Krishna), all those assembled kings thus censured the King of Cedī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 16. After hearing the words of Vāsudeva (Krishna), all those assembled kings thus censured the King of Cedī.

sanskrit: ततस्तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा शिशुपालः प्रतापवान् ।जहास स्वनवद्धासं प्रहस्येदमुवाच ह ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatastadvacanaṁ śrutvā śiśupālaḥ pratāpavān ,jahāsa svanavaddhāsaṁ prahasyedamuvāca ha.
english: Then, the mighty Śiśupāla, upon hearing those words, laughed a resounding laugh. After laughing, he indeed spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 17. Then, the mighty Śiśupāla, upon hearing those words, laughed a resounding laugh. After laughing, he indeed spoke these words.

sanskrit: मत्पूर्वां रुक्मिणीं कृष्ण संसत्सु परिकीर्तयन् ।विशेषतः पार्थिवेषु व्रीडां न कुरुषे कथम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: matpūrvāṁ rukmiṇīṁ kṛṣṇa saṁsatsu parikīrtayan ,viśeṣataḥ pārthiveṣu vrīḍāṁ na kuruṣe katham.
english: O Krishna, how is it that you do not feel shame (vrīḍā) when you proclaim Rukmini, who was previously mine, in assemblies, especially among kings?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 18. O Krishna, how is it that you do not feel shame (vrīḍā) when you proclaim Rukmini, who was previously mine, in assemblies, especially among kings?

sanskrit: मन्यमानो हि कः सत्सु पुरुषः परिकीर्तयेत् ।अन्यपूर्वां स्त्रियं जातु त्वदन्यो मधुसूदन ॥१९॥
transliteration: manyamāno hi kaḥ satsu puruṣaḥ parikīrtayet ,anyapūrvāṁ striyaṁ jātu tvadanyo madhusūdana.
english: Indeed, what man (puruṣa), considering (what is proper), would ever proclaim a woman who was previously betrothed to another, in assemblies of good people? Who but you, O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 19. Indeed, what man (puruṣa), considering (what is proper), would ever proclaim a woman who was previously betrothed to another, in assemblies of good people? Who but you, O Madhusūdana (madhusūdana)?

sanskrit: क्षम वा यदि ते श्रद्धा मा वा कृष्ण मम क्षम ।क्रुद्धाद्वापि प्रसन्नाद्वा किं मे त्वत्तो भविष्यति ॥२०॥
transliteration: kṣama vā yadi te śraddhā mā vā kṛṣṇa mama kṣama ,kruddhādvāpi prasannādvā kiṁ me tvatto bhaviṣyati.
english: Forgive me, O Krishna, if you have the inclination (śraddhā) to do so, or do not forgive me. What can I gain from you, whether you are angry or pleased?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 20. Forgive me, O Krishna, if you have the inclination (śraddhā) to do so, or do not forgive me. What can I gain from you, whether you are angry or pleased?

sanskrit: तथा ब्रुवत एवास्य भगवान्मधुसूदनः ।व्यपाहरच्छिरः क्रुद्धश्चक्रेणामित्रकर्षणः ।स पपात महाबाहुर्वज्राहत इवाचलः ॥२१॥
transliteration: tathā bruvata evāsya bhagavānmadhusūdanaḥ ,vyapāharacchiraḥ kruddhaścakreṇāmitrakarṣaṇaḥ ,sa papāta mahābāhurvajrāhata ivācalaḥ.
english: As he was speaking thus, the glorious (bhagavān) Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), who destroys his enemies (amitrakarṣaṇa) and was enraged, severed his head with his discus. The mighty-armed (mahābāhu) Śiśupāla then fell like a mountain struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 21. As he was speaking thus, the glorious (bhagavān) Madhusūdana (madhusūdana), who destroys his enemies (amitrakarṣaṇa) and was enraged, severed his head with his discus. The mighty-armed (mahābāhu) Śiśupāla then fell like a mountain struck by a thunderbolt (vajra).

sanskrit: ततश्चेदिपतेर्देहात्तेजोऽग्र्यं ददृशुर्नृपाः ।उत्पतन्तं महाराज गगनादिव भास्करम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tataścedipaterdehāttejo'gryaṁ dadṛśurnṛpāḥ ,utpatantaṁ mahārāja gaganādiva bhāskaram.
english: Then, O great king, the kings saw an excellent light (tejaḥ) rising from the body of the Cedi king, just as the sun rises from the sky.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 22. Then, O great king, the kings saw an excellent light (tejaḥ) rising from the body of the Cedi king, just as the sun rises from the sky.

sanskrit: ततः कमलपत्राक्षं कृष्णं लोकनमस्कृतम् ।ववन्दे तत्तदा तेजो विवेश च नराधिप ॥२३॥
transliteration: tataḥ kamalapatrākṣaṁ kṛṣṇaṁ lokanamaskṛtam ,vavande tattadā tejo viveśa ca narādhipa.
english: Then, O king of men, that light (tejaḥ) saluted Kṛṣṇa, who has eyes like lotus petals and is revered by all worlds, and then it entered him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 23. Then, O king of men, that light (tejaḥ) saluted Kṛṣṇa, who has eyes like lotus petals and is revered by all worlds, and then it entered him.

sanskrit: तदद्भुतममन्यन्त दृष्ट्वा सर्वे महीक्षितः ।यद्विवेश महाबाहुं तत्तेजः पुरुषोत्तमम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tadadbhutamamanyanta dṛṣṭvā sarve mahīkṣitaḥ ,yadviveśa mahābāhuṁ tattejaḥ puruṣottamam.
english: All the kings, having witnessed that, considered it truly astounding - that very light (tejaḥ) which entered the mighty-armed supreme person (puruṣottama).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 24. All the kings, having witnessed that, considered it truly astounding - that very light (tejaḥ) which entered the mighty-armed supreme person (puruṣottama).

sanskrit: अनभ्रे प्रववर्ष द्यौः पपात ज्वलिताशनिः ।कृष्णेन निहते चैद्ये चचाल च वसुंधरा ॥२५॥
transliteration: anabhre pravavarṣa dyauḥ papāta jvalitāśaniḥ ,kṛṣṇena nihate caidye cacāla ca vasuṁdharā.
english: The sky (dyauḥ) rained without clouds, and a flaming thunderbolt fell. When the Cedi king was slain by Kṛṣṇa, the earth (vasundharā) trembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 25. The sky (dyauḥ) rained without clouds, and a flaming thunderbolt fell. When the Cedi king was slain by Kṛṣṇa, the earth (vasundharā) trembled.

sanskrit: ततः केचिन्महीपाला नाब्रुवंस्तत्र किंचन ।अतीतवाक्पथे काले प्रेक्षमाणा जनार्दनम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ kecinmahīpālā nābruvaṁstatra kiṁcana ,atītavākpathe kāle prekṣamāṇā janārdanam.
english: Then, while observing Janardana (Krishna), some kings remained silent, not uttering anything there, as the opportune moment for speaking had passed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 26. Then, while observing Janardana (Krishna), some kings remained silent, not uttering anything there, as the opportune moment for speaking had passed.

sanskrit: हस्तैर्हस्ताग्रमपरे प्रत्यपीषन्नमर्षिताः ।अपरे दशनैरोष्ठानदशन्क्रोधमूर्छिताः ॥२७॥
transliteration: hastairhastāgramapare pratyapīṣannamarṣitāḥ ,apare daśanairoṣṭhānadaśankrodhamūrchitāḥ.
english: Others, filled with indignation, clenched their fists (lit. pressed their palms with their hands). And still others, overcome by rage, bit their lips with their teeth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 27. Others, filled with indignation, clenched their fists (lit. pressed their palms with their hands). And still others, overcome by rage, bit their lips with their teeth.

sanskrit: रहस्तु केचिद्वार्ष्णेयं प्रशशंसुर्नराधिपाः ।केचिदेव तु संरब्धा मध्यस्थास्त्वपरेऽभवन् ॥२८॥
transliteration: rahastu kecidvārṣṇeyaṁ praśaśaṁsurnarādhipāḥ ,kecideva tu saṁrabdhā madhyasthāstvapare'bhavan.
english: Some kings, however, praised Vārṣṇeya (Krishna) in private. But others, indeed, were greatly agitated, while still others remained neutral.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 28. Some kings, however, praised Vārṣṇeya (Krishna) in private. But others, indeed, were greatly agitated, while still others remained neutral.

sanskrit: प्रहृष्टाः केशवं जग्मुः संस्तुवन्तो महर्षयः ।ब्राह्मणाश्च महात्मानः पार्थिवाश्च महाबलाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: prahṛṣṭāḥ keśavaṁ jagmuḥ saṁstuvanto maharṣayaḥ ,brāhmaṇāśca mahātmānaḥ pārthivāśca mahābalāḥ.
english: The delighted great sages, along with noble-minded Brahmins and very powerful kings, went to Keśava (Krishna), praising him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 29. The delighted great sages, along with noble-minded Brahmins and very powerful kings, went to Keśava (Krishna), praising him.

sanskrit: पाण्डवस्त्वब्रवीद्भ्रातॄन्सत्कारेण महीपतिम् ।दमघोषात्मजं वीरं संसाधयत मा चिरम् ।तथा च कृतवन्तस्ते भ्रातुर्वै शासनं तदा ॥३०॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavastvabravīdbhrātṝnsatkāreṇa mahīpatim ,damaghoṣātmajaṁ vīraṁ saṁsādhayata mā ciram ,tathā ca kṛtavantaste bhrāturvai śāsanaṁ tadā.
english: The Pāṇḍava, however, told his brothers, "With honor (satkāra), bring forth the king, the heroic son of Damaghoṣa, without delay!" And they indeed carried out their brother's command then.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 30. The Pāṇḍava, however, told his brothers, "With honor (satkāra), bring forth the king, the heroic son of Damaghoṣa, without delay!" And they indeed carried out their brother's command then.

sanskrit: चेदीनामाधिपत्ये च पुत्रमस्य महीपतिम् ।अभ्यषिञ्चत्तदा पार्थः सह तैर्वसुधाधिपैः ॥३१॥
transliteration: cedīnāmādhipatye ca putramasya mahīpatim ,abhyaṣiñcattadā pārthaḥ saha tairvasudhādhipaiḥ.
english: And then Pārtha, along with those kings, anointed his son as king over the dominion (ādhipatya) of the Cedis.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 31. And then Pārtha, along with those kings, anointed his son as king over the dominion (ādhipatya) of the Cedis.

sanskrit: ततः स कुरुराजस्य क्रतुः सर्वसमृद्धिमान् ।यूनां प्रीतिकरो राजन्संबभौ विपुलौजसः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tataḥ sa kururājasya kratuḥ sarvasamṛddhimān ,yūnāṁ prītikaro rājansaṁbabhau vipulaujasaḥ.
english: Then, that ritual (kratu) of the Kuru king, filled with all prosperity, and delightful to the young men, O King, shone with immense splendor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 32. Then, that ritual (kratu) of the Kuru king, filled with all prosperity, and delightful to the young men, O King, shone with immense splendor.

sanskrit: शान्तविघ्नः सुखारम्भः प्रभूतधनधान्यवान् ।अन्नवान्बहुभक्ष्यश्च केशवेन सुरक्षितः ॥३३॥
transliteration: śāntavighnaḥ sukhārambhaḥ prabhūtadhanadhānyavān ,annavānbahubhakṣyaśca keśavena surakṣitaḥ.
english: That ritual was free from obstacles, with a pleasant (sukha) commencement (ārambha), abundant in wealth (dhana) and grain (dhānya), rich in food (anna) and many kinds of edibles (bhakṣya), and well-protected by Keśava.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 33. That ritual was free from obstacles, with a pleasant (sukha) commencement (ārambha), abundant in wealth (dhana) and grain (dhānya), rich in food (anna) and many kinds of edibles (bhakṣya), and well-protected by Keśava.

sanskrit: समापयामास च तं राजसूयं महाक्रतुम् ।तं तु यज्ञं महाबाहुरा समाप्तेर्जनार्दनः ।ररक्ष भगवाञ्शौरिः शार्ङ्गचक्रगदाधरः ॥३४॥
transliteration: samāpayāmāsa ca taṁ rājasūyaṁ mahākratum ,taṁ tu yajñaṁ mahābāhurā samāpterjanārdanaḥ ,rarakṣa bhagavāñśauriḥ śārṅgacakragadādharaḥ.
english: And he completed that great royal consecration (rājasūya) sacrifice. Indeed, the mighty-armed Janārdana, the divine Śauri (Kṛṣṇa), who bore the Śārṅga bow, discus, and mace, protected that sacrifice until its very completion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 34. And he completed that great royal consecration (rājasūya) sacrifice. Indeed, the mighty-armed Janārdana, the divine Śauri (Kṛṣṇa), who bore the Śārṅga bow, discus, and mace, protected that sacrifice until its very completion.

sanskrit: ततस्त्ववभृथस्नातं धर्मराजं युधिष्ठिरम् ।समस्तं पार्थिवं क्षत्रमभिगम्येदमब्रवीत् ॥३५॥
transliteration: tatastvavabhṛthasnātaṁ dharmarājaṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,samastaṁ pārthivaṁ kṣatramabhigamyedamabravīt.
english: Then, all the kings and warriors approached Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of righteousness (dharma), who had bathed after the ceremonial "avabhṛtha" bath, and spoke to him thus.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 35. Then, all the kings and warriors approached Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of righteousness (dharma), who had bathed after the ceremonial "avabhṛtha" bath, and spoke to him thus.

sanskrit: दिष्ट्या वर्धसि धर्मज्ञ साम्राज्यं प्राप्तवान्विभो ।आजमीढाजमीढानां यशः संवर्धितं त्वया ।कर्मणैतेन राजेन्द्र धर्मश्च सुमहान्कृतः ॥३६॥
transliteration: diṣṭyā vardhasi dharmajña sāmrājyaṁ prāptavānvibho ,ājamīḍhājamīḍhānāṁ yaśaḥ saṁvardhitaṁ tvayā ,karmaṇaitena rājendra dharmaśca sumahānkṛtaḥ.
english: By good fortune, O knower of righteousness (dharma), O lord, you are prospering, having attained sovereignty. The fame of the Ajāmīḍhas has been greatly enhanced by you. And through this deed, O chief of kings, a truly great righteousness (dharma) has been established.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 36. By good fortune, O knower of righteousness (dharma), O lord, you are prospering, having attained sovereignty. The fame of the Ajāmīḍhas has been greatly enhanced by you. And through this deed, O chief of kings, a truly great righteousness (dharma) has been established.

sanskrit: आपृच्छामो नरव्याघ्र सर्वकामैः सुपूजिताः ।स्वराष्ट्राणि गमिष्यामस्तदनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥३७॥
transliteration: āpṛcchāmo naravyāghra sarvakāmaiḥ supūjitāḥ ,svarāṣṭrāṇi gamiṣyāmastadanujñātumarhasi.
english: O tiger among men (naravyāghra), we, having been thoroughly honored with all our desires fulfilled, now bid farewell. We intend to return to our own kingdoms, and therefore, you should grant us permission for that.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 37. O tiger among men (naravyāghra), we, having been thoroughly honored with all our desires fulfilled, now bid farewell. We intend to return to our own kingdoms, and therefore, you should grant us permission for that.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तु वचनं राज्ञां धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।यथार्हं पूज्य नृपतीन्भ्रातॄन्सर्वानुवाच ह ॥३८॥
transliteration: śrutvā tu vacanaṁ rājñāṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,yathārhaṁ pūjya nṛpatīnbhrātṝnsarvānuvāca ha.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), having heard the words of the kings, appropriately honored those rulers and then spoke to all his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 38. Yudhiṣṭhira, the king of righteousness (dharma), having heard the words of the kings, appropriately honored those rulers and then spoke to all his brothers.

sanskrit: राजानः सर्व एवैते प्रीत्यास्मान्समुपागताः ।प्रस्थिताः स्वानि राष्ट्राणि मामापृच्छ्य परंतपाः ।तेऽनुव्रजत भद्रं वो विषयान्तं नृपोत्तमान् ॥३९॥
transliteration: rājānaḥ sarva evaite prītyāsmānsamupāgatāḥ ,prasthitāḥ svāni rāṣṭrāṇi māmāpṛcchya paraṁtapāḥ ,te'nuvrajata bhadraṁ vo viṣayāntaṁ nṛpottamān.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O tormentors of foes (parantapa), all these kings came to us with affection. They have now departed for their own kingdoms without taking leave of me. Therefore, you (my brothers) should accompany these excellent kings up to the border of their territories. May good fortune be upon you!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 39. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O tormentors of foes (parantapa), all these kings came to us with affection. They have now departed for their own kingdoms without taking leave of me. Therefore, you (my brothers) should accompany these excellent kings up to the border of their territories. May good fortune be upon you!'

sanskrit: भ्रातुर्वचनमाज्ञाय पाण्डवा धर्मचारिणः ।यथार्हं नृपमुख्यांस्तानेकैकं समनुव्रजन् ॥४०॥
transliteration: bhrāturvacanamājñāya pāṇḍavā dharmacāriṇaḥ ,yathārhaṁ nṛpamukhyāṁstānekaikaṁ samanuvrajan.
english: The Pāṇḍavas, who adhered to righteousness (dharma), understanding their brother's instruction, accompanied those principal kings one by one, as was appropriate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 40. The Pāṇḍavas, who adhered to righteousness (dharma), understanding their brother's instruction, accompanied those principal kings one by one, as was appropriate.

sanskrit: विराटमन्वयात्तूर्णं धृष्टद्युम्नः प्रतापवान् ।धनंजयो यज्ञसेनं महात्मानं महारथः ॥४१॥
transliteration: virāṭamanvayāttūrṇaṁ dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ pratāpavān ,dhanaṁjayo yajñasenaṁ mahātmānaṁ mahārathaḥ.
english: The mighty Dhṛṣṭadyumna swiftly followed King Virāṭa. Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the great charioteer, accompanied the magnanimous, great-souled (mahātman) King Yajñasena (Drupada).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 41. The mighty Dhṛṣṭadyumna swiftly followed King Virāṭa. Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), the great charioteer, accompanied the magnanimous, great-souled (mahātman) King Yajñasena (Drupada).

sanskrit: भीष्मं च धृतराष्ट्रं च भीमसेनो महाबलः ।द्रोणं च ससुतं वीरं सहदेवो महारथः ॥४२॥
transliteration: bhīṣmaṁ ca dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,droṇaṁ ca sasutaṁ vīraṁ sahadevo mahārathaḥ.
english: The greatly powerful Bhimasena [honored] Bhishma and Dhritarashtra, and the great charioteer Sahadeva [honored] the hero Drona along with his son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 42. The greatly powerful Bhimasena [honored] Bhishma and Dhritarashtra, and the great charioteer Sahadeva [honored] the hero Drona along with his son.

sanskrit: नकुलः सुबलं राजन्सहपुत्रं समन्वयात् ।द्रौपदेयाः ससौभद्राः पार्वतीयान्महीपतीन् ॥४३॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ subalaṁ rājansahaputraṁ samanvayāt ,draupadeyāḥ sasaubhadrāḥ pārvatīyānmahīpatīn.
english: O King, Nakula [honored] Subala along with his son in an agreeable manner, and the sons of Draupadi (Draupadeyas) with the son of Subhadra (Saubhadras) [honored] the mountain kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 43. O King, Nakula [honored] Subala along with his son in an agreeable manner, and the sons of Draupadi (Draupadeyas) with the son of Subhadra (Saubhadras) [honored] the mountain kings.

sanskrit: अन्वगच्छंस्तथैवान्यान्क्षत्रियान्क्षत्रियर्षभाः ।एवं संपूजितास्ते वै जग्मुर्विप्राश्च सर्वशः ॥४४॥
transliteration: anvagacchaṁstathaivānyānkṣatriyānkṣatriyarṣabhāḥ ,evaṁ saṁpūjitāste vai jagmurviprāśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: The foremost among warriors (kṣatriyarṣabhāḥ) followed other warriors (kṣatriyān) in the same way. Thus, having been honored, they indeed departed, and the Brahmins also departed completely.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 44. The foremost among warriors (kṣatriyarṣabhāḥ) followed other warriors (kṣatriyān) in the same way. Thus, having been honored, they indeed departed, and the Brahmins also departed completely.

sanskrit: गतेषु पार्थिवेन्द्रेषु सर्वेषु भरतर्षभ ।युधिष्ठिरमुवाचेदं वासुदेवः प्रतापवान् ॥४५॥
transliteration: gateṣu pārthivendreṣu sarveṣu bharatarṣabha ,yudhiṣṭhiramuvācedaṁ vāsudevaḥ pratāpavān.
english: O best of Bharatas, when all the chief kings had departed, the glorious Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 45. O best of Bharatas, when all the chief kings had departed, the glorious Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa) spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira.

sanskrit: आपृच्छे त्वां गमिष्यामि द्वारकां कुरुनन्दन ।राजसूयं क्रतुश्रेष्ठं दिष्ट्या त्वं प्राप्तवानसि ॥४६॥
transliteration: āpṛcche tvāṁ gamiṣyāmi dvārakāṁ kurunandana ,rājasūyaṁ kratuśreṣṭhaṁ diṣṭyā tvaṁ prāptavānasi.
english: O scion of Kuru, I bid you farewell and shall depart for Dvaraka. Fortunately, you have successfully performed the Rājasūya, the foremost of Vedic rituals (kratu).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 46. O scion of Kuru, I bid you farewell and shall depart for Dvaraka. Fortunately, you have successfully performed the Rājasūya, the foremost of Vedic rituals (kratu).

sanskrit: तमुवाचैवमुक्तस्तु धर्मराण्मधुसूदनम् ।तव प्रसादाद्गोविन्द प्राप्तवानस्मि वै क्रतुम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tamuvācaivamuktastu dharmarāṇmadhusūdanam ,tava prasādādgovinda prāptavānasmi vai kratum.
english: When thus addressed, the King of natural law (dharmarāj Yudhiṣṭhira) replied to Madhusūdana (Krishna): 'By your grace, O Govinda, I have indeed successfully accomplished this Vedic ritual (kratu).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 47. When thus addressed, the King of natural law (dharmarāj Yudhiṣṭhira) replied to Madhusūdana (Krishna): 'By your grace, O Govinda, I have indeed successfully accomplished this Vedic ritual (kratu).'

sanskrit: समस्तं पार्थिवं क्षत्रं त्वत्प्रसादाद्वशानुगम् ।उपादाय बलिं मुख्यं मामेव समुपस्थितम् ॥४८॥
transliteration: samastaṁ pārthivaṁ kṣatraṁ tvatprasādādvaśānugam ,upādāya baliṁ mukhyaṁ māmeva samupasthitam.
english: By your grace, the entire earthly warrior class (kṣatra) became subservient, bringing their main tribute and presenting themselves to me alone.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 48. By your grace, the entire earthly warrior class (kṣatra) became subservient, bringing their main tribute and presenting themselves to me alone.

sanskrit: न वयं त्वामृते वीर रंस्यामेह कथंचन ।अवश्यं चापि गन्तव्या त्वया द्वारवती पुरी ॥४९॥
transliteration: na vayaṁ tvāmṛte vīra raṁsyāmeha kathaṁcana ,avaśyaṁ cāpi gantavyā tvayā dvāravatī purī.
english: O hero, we will certainly not find joy without you in any way. Furthermore, the city of Dvārakā must surely be visited by you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 49. O hero, we will certainly not find joy without you in any way. Furthermore, the city of Dvārakā must surely be visited by you.

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः स धर्मात्मा युधिष्ठिरसहायवान् ।अभिगम्याब्रवीत्प्रीतः पृथां पृथुयशा हरिः ॥५०॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ sa dharmātmā yudhiṣṭhirasahāyavān ,abhigamyābravītprītaḥ pṛthāṁ pṛthuyaśā hariḥ.
english: Being thus addressed, that righteous-souled (dharma) Hari (Krishna), who was of great renown and had Yudhiṣṭhira as his companion, approached Pṛthā and spoke, feeling very pleased.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 50. Being thus addressed, that righteous-souled (dharma) Hari (Krishna), who was of great renown and had Yudhiṣṭhira as his companion, approached Pṛthā and spoke, feeling very pleased.

sanskrit: साम्राज्यं समनुप्राप्ताः पुत्रास्तेऽद्य पितृष्वसः ।सिद्धार्था वसुमन्तश्च सा त्वं प्रीतिमवाप्नुहि ॥५१॥
transliteration: sāmrājyaṁ samanuprāptāḥ putrāste'dya pitṛṣvasaḥ ,siddhārthā vasumantaśca sā tvaṁ prītimavāpnuhi.
english: O paternal aunt, your sons have now attained imperial dominion; their aims are fulfilled, and they are prosperous. Therefore, you should now experience great joy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 51. O paternal aunt, your sons have now attained imperial dominion; their aims are fulfilled, and they are prosperous. Therefore, you should now experience great joy.

sanskrit: अनुज्ञातस्त्वया चाहं द्वारकां गन्तुमुत्सहे ।सुभद्रां द्रौपदीं चैव सभाजयत केशवः ॥५२॥
transliteration: anujñātastvayā cāhaṁ dvārakāṁ gantumutsahe ,subhadrāṁ draupadīṁ caiva sabhājayata keśavaḥ.
english: Permitted by you, I am eager to go to Dvārakā. Let Keśava honor Subhadrā and Draupadī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 52. Permitted by you, I am eager to go to Dvārakā. Let Keśava honor Subhadrā and Draupadī.

sanskrit: निष्क्रम्यान्तःपुराच्चैव युधिष्ठिरसहायवान् ।स्नातश्च कृतजप्यश्च ब्राह्मणान्स्वस्ति वाच्य च ॥५३॥
transliteration: niṣkramyāntaḥpurāccaiva yudhiṣṭhirasahāyavān ,snātaśca kṛtajapyaśca brāhmaṇānsvasti vācya ca.
english: Having exited from the inner apartments, and accompanied by Yudhiṣṭhira, he bathed, performed his sacred recitations (mantra), and then requested the brāhmaṇas to pronounce blessings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 53. Having exited from the inner apartments, and accompanied by Yudhiṣṭhira, he bathed, performed his sacred recitations (mantra), and then requested the brāhmaṇas to pronounce blessings.

sanskrit: ततो मेघवरप्रख्यं स्यन्दनं वै सुकल्पितम् ।योजयित्वा महाराज दारुकः प्रत्युपस्थितः ॥५४॥
transliteration: tato meghavaraprakhyaṁ syandanaṁ vai sukalpitam ,yojayitvā mahārāja dārukaḥ pratyupasthitaḥ.
english: Then, O great king, Daruka appeared, having prepared the excellently equipped chariot that resembled a magnificent cloud.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 54. Then, O great king, Daruka appeared, having prepared the excellently equipped chariot that resembled a magnificent cloud.

sanskrit: उपस्थितं रथं दृष्ट्वा तार्क्ष्यप्रवरकेतनम् ।प्रदक्षिणमुपावृत्य समारुह्य महामनाः ।प्रययौ पुण्डरीकाक्षस्ततो द्वारवतीं पुरीम् ॥५५॥
transliteration: upasthitaṁ rathaṁ dṛṣṭvā tārkṣyapravaraketanam ,pradakṣiṇamupāvṛtya samāruhya mahāmanāḥ ,prayayau puṇḍarīkākṣastato dvāravatīṁ purīm.
english: Having seen the chariot, which bore the emblem of the supreme Garuda and stood ready, the great-souled, lotus-eyed one (Krishna) circumambulated it clockwise, mounted it, and then departed for the city of Dvaravati.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 55. Having seen the chariot, which bore the emblem of the supreme Garuda and stood ready, the great-souled, lotus-eyed one (Krishna) circumambulated it clockwise, mounted it, and then departed for the city of Dvaravati.

sanskrit: तं पद्भ्यामनुवव्राज धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।भ्रातृभिः सहितः श्रीमान्वासुदेवं महाबलम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: taṁ padbhyāmanuvavrāja dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhrātṛbhiḥ sahitaḥ śrīmānvāsudevaṁ mahābalam.
english: King Yudhishthira (dharmarājaḥ), glorious and accompanied by his brothers, followed the mighty Vasudeva (Krishna) on foot.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 56. King Yudhishthira (dharmarājaḥ), glorious and accompanied by his brothers, followed the mighty Vasudeva (Krishna) on foot.

sanskrit: ततो मुहूर्तं संगृह्य स्यन्दनप्रवरं हरिः ।अब्रवीत्पुण्डरीकाक्षः कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥५७॥
transliteration: tato muhūrtaṁ saṁgṛhya syandanapravaraṁ hariḥ ,abravītpuṇḍarīkākṣaḥ kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Then, having paused for a moment to take hold of the excellent chariot, Hari (Krishna), the lotus-eyed one, spoke to Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 57. Then, having paused for a moment to take hold of the excellent chariot, Hari (Krishna), the lotus-eyed one, spoke to Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti.

sanskrit: अप्रमत्तः स्थितो नित्यं प्रजाः पाहि विशां पते ।पर्जन्यमिव भूतानि महाद्रुममिवाण्डजाः ।बान्धवास्त्वोपजीवन्तु सहस्राक्षमिवामराः ॥५८॥
transliteration: apramattaḥ sthito nityaṁ prajāḥ pāhi viśāṁ pate ,parjanyamiva bhūtāni mahādrumamivāṇḍajāḥ ,bāndhavāstvopajīvantu sahasrākṣamivāmarāḥ.
english: O lord of the people, remain ever vigilant and protect your subjects. May all beings depend on you just as they depend on the rain-god Parjanya, and may the egg-born creatures (birds) depend on you as they depend on a great tree. Furthermore, may your kinsmen depend on you just as the immortal gods depend on Indra, the thousand-eyed one.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 58. O lord of the people, remain ever vigilant and protect your subjects. May all beings depend on you just as they depend on the rain-god Parjanya, and may the egg-born creatures (birds) depend on you as they depend on a great tree. Furthermore, may your kinsmen depend on you just as the immortal gods depend on Indra, the thousand-eyed one.

sanskrit: कृत्वा परस्परेणैवं संविदं कृष्णपाण्डवौ ।अन्योन्यं समनुज्ञाप्य जग्मतुः स्वगृहान्प्रति ॥५९॥
transliteration: kṛtvā paraspareṇaivaṁ saṁvidaṁ kṛṣṇapāṇḍavau ,anyonyaṁ samanujñāpya jagmatuḥ svagṛhānprati.
english: Having thus made a mutual agreement (saṃvid) with each other, Kṛṣṇa and the Pāṇḍavas, after bidding farewell to one another, both departed towards their own homes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 59. Having thus made a mutual agreement (saṃvid) with each other, Kṛṣṇa and the Pāṇḍavas, after bidding farewell to one another, both departed towards their own homes.

sanskrit: गते द्वारवतीं कृष्णे सात्वतप्रवरे नृप ।एको दुर्योधनो राजा शकुनिश्चापि सौबलः ।तस्यां सभायां दिव्यायामूषतुस्तौ नरर्षभौ ॥६०॥
transliteration: gate dvāravatīṁ kṛṣṇe sātvatapravare nṛpa ,eko duryodhano rājā śakuniścāpi saubalaḥ ,tasyāṁ sabhāyāṁ divyāyāmūṣatustau nararṣabhau.
english: O King, after Kṛṣṇa, the foremost among the Sātvatas, had departed for Dvārakā, then King Duryodhana and also Śakuni, the son of Subala, those two best among men, remained in that magnificent assembly hall.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 42 Verse 60. O King, after Kṛṣṇa, the foremost among the Sātvatas, had departed for Dvārakā, then King Duryodhana and also Śakuni, the son of Subala, those two best among men, remained in that magnificent assembly hall.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।वसन्दुर्योधनस्तस्यां सभायां भरतर्षभ ।शनैर्ददर्श तां सर्वां सभां शकुनिना सह ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vasanduryodhanastasyāṁ sabhāyāṁ bharatarṣabha ,śanairdadarśa tāṁ sarvāṁ sabhāṁ śakuninā saha.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O best of Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), Duryodhana, while dwelling in that assembly hall with Śakuni, slowly observed the entire hall."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "O best of Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), Duryodhana, while dwelling in that assembly hall with Śakuni, slowly observed the entire hall."

sanskrit: तस्यां दिव्यानभिप्रायान्ददर्श कुरुनन्दनः ।न दृष्टपूर्वा ये तेन नगरे नागसाह्वये ॥२॥
transliteration: tasyāṁ divyānabhiprāyāndadarśa kurunandanaḥ ,na dṛṣṭapūrvā ye tena nagare nāgasāhvaye.
english: The son of Kuru (Duryodhana) saw in that (hall) extraordinary designs, which he had never seen before in the city named Hastinapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 2. The son of Kuru (Duryodhana) saw in that (hall) extraordinary designs, which he had never seen before in the city named Hastinapura.

sanskrit: स कदाचित्सभामध्ये धार्तराष्ट्रो महीपतिः ।स्फाटिकं तलमासाद्य जलमित्यभिशङ्कया ॥३॥
transliteration: sa kadācitsabhāmadhye dhārtarāṣṭro mahīpatiḥ ,sphāṭikaṁ talamāsādya jalamityabhiśaṅkayā.
english: One day, that king, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), having come upon a crystal floor in the assembly hall, mistook it for water out of apprehension.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 3. One day, that king, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), having come upon a crystal floor in the assembly hall, mistook it for water out of apprehension.

sanskrit: स्ववस्त्रोत्कर्षणं राजा कृतवान्बुद्धिमोहितः ।दुर्मना विमुखश्चैव परिचक्राम तां सभाम् ॥४॥
transliteration: svavastrotkarṣaṇaṁ rājā kṛtavānbuddhimohitaḥ ,durmanā vimukhaścaiva paricakrāma tāṁ sabhām.
english: The king, whose intellect was confused, pulled up his garments. With a dejected and averted face, he then circled that assembly hall.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 4. The king, whose intellect was confused, pulled up his garments. With a dejected and averted face, he then circled that assembly hall.

sanskrit: ततः स्फाटिकतोयां वै स्फाटिकाम्बुजशोभिताम् ।वापीं मत्वा स्थलमिति सवासाः प्रापतज्जले ॥५॥
transliteration: tataḥ sphāṭikatoyāṁ vai sphāṭikāmbujaśobhitām ,vāpīṁ matvā sthalamiti savāsāḥ prāpatajjale.
english: Then, mistaking for dry ground a pond (vāpī) that had crystal-clear water and was adorned with crystal lotuses, he indeed fell into the water with his garments on.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 5. Then, mistaking for dry ground a pond (vāpī) that had crystal-clear water and was adorned with crystal lotuses, he indeed fell into the water with his garments on.

sanskrit: जले निपतितं दृष्ट्वा किंकरा जहसुर्भृशम् ।वासांसि च शुभान्यस्मै प्रददू राजशासनात् ॥६॥
transliteration: jale nipatitaṁ dṛṣṭvā kiṁkarā jahasurbhṛśam ,vāsāṁsi ca śubhānyasmai pradadū rājaśāsanāt.
english: When the servants saw him fallen into the water, they laughed heartily. Then, by the king's command, they gave him clean clothes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 6. When the servants saw him fallen into the water, they laughed heartily. Then, by the king's command, they gave him clean clothes.

sanskrit: तथागतं तु तं दृष्ट्वा भीमसेनो महाबलः ।अर्जुनश्च यमौ चोभौ सर्वे ते प्राहसंस्तदा ॥७॥
transliteration: tathāgataṁ tu taṁ dṛṣṭvā bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,arjunaśca yamau cobhau sarve te prāhasaṁstadā.
english: When the mighty Bhimasena, Arjuna, and both the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) saw him in that condition, all of them burst out laughing at that moment.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 7. When the mighty Bhimasena, Arjuna, and both the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) saw him in that condition, all of them burst out laughing at that moment.

sanskrit: नामर्षयत्ततस्तेषामवहासममर्षणः ।आकारं रक्षमाणस्तु न स तान्समुदैक्षत ॥८॥
transliteration: nāmarṣayattatasteṣāmavahāsamamarṣaṇaḥ ,ākāraṁ rakṣamāṇastu na sa tānsamudaikṣata.
english: Indignant, he did not tolerate their laughter. However, maintaining his composure, he did not look at them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 8. Indignant, he did not tolerate their laughter. However, maintaining his composure, he did not look at them.

sanskrit: पुनर्वसनमुत्क्षिप्य प्रतरिष्यन्निव स्थलम् ।आरुरोह ततः सर्वे जहसुस्ते पुनर्जनाः ॥९॥
transliteration: punarvasanamutkṣipya pratariṣyanniva sthalam ,āruroha tataḥ sarve jahasuste punarjanāḥ.
english: Again, gathering up his garment, as if he were about to traverse dry land, he climbed out. Then all those people laughed once more.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 9. Again, gathering up his garment, as if he were about to traverse dry land, he climbed out. Then all those people laughed once more.

sanskrit: द्वारं च विवृताकारं ललाटेन समाहनत् ।संवृतं चेति मन्वानो द्वारदेशादुपारमत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dvāraṁ ca vivṛtākāraṁ lalāṭena samāhanat ,saṁvṛtaṁ ceti manvāno dvāradeśādupāramat.
english: He struck the apparently open door with his forehead, and thinking it was closed, he stopped at the doorway.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 10. He struck the apparently open door with his forehead, and thinking it was closed, he stopped at the doorway.

sanskrit: एवं प्रलम्भान्विविधान्प्राप्य तत्र विशां पते ।पाण्डवेयाभ्यनुज्ञातस्ततो दुर्योधनो नृपः ॥११॥
transliteration: evaṁ pralambhānvividhānprāpya tatra viśāṁ pate ,pāṇḍaveyābhyanujñātastato duryodhano nṛpaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, having thus experienced various deceptions there, King Duryodhana, then permitted by the sons of Pāṇḍu, (departed).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 11. O lord of the people, having thus experienced various deceptions there, King Duryodhana, then permitted by the sons of Pāṇḍu, (departed).

sanskrit: अप्रहृष्टेन मनसा राजसूये महाक्रतौ ।प्रेक्ष्य तामद्भुतामृद्धिं जगाम गजसाह्वयम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: aprahṛṣṭena manasā rājasūye mahākratau ,prekṣya tāmadbhutāmṛddhiṁ jagāma gajasāhvayam.
english: At the great Rājasūya sacrifice, with an unelated mind, having seen that wonderful prosperity, he departed for Hastināpura.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 12. At the great Rājasūya sacrifice, with an unelated mind, having seen that wonderful prosperity, he departed for Hastināpura.

sanskrit: पाण्डवश्रीप्रतप्तस्य ध्यानग्लानस्य गच्छतः ।दुर्योधनस्य नृपतेः पापा मतिरजायत ॥१३॥
transliteration: pāṇḍavaśrīprataptasya dhyānaglānasya gacchataḥ ,duryodhanasya nṛpateḥ pāpā matirajāyata.
english: An evil thought arose in King Duryodhana, who was tormented by the prosperity of the Pāṇḍavas and, as he departed, was worn out by deep contemplation (dhyāna).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 13. An evil thought arose in King Duryodhana, who was tormented by the prosperity of the Pāṇḍavas and, as he departed, was worn out by deep contemplation (dhyāna).

sanskrit: पार्थान्सुमनसो दृष्ट्वा पार्थिवांश्च वशानुगान् ।कृत्स्नं चापि हितं लोकमाकुमारं कुरूद्वह ॥१४॥
transliteration: pārthānsumanaso dṛṣṭvā pārthivāṁśca vaśānugān ,kṛtsnaṁ cāpi hitaṁ lokamākumāraṁ kurūdvaha.
english: O foremost among the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), having observed the cheerful Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), and the kings who were compliant (to them), and also the entire populace, from children onwards, well-disposed (towards them).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 14. O foremost among the Kurus (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), having observed the cheerful Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), and the kings who were compliant (to them), and also the entire populace, from children onwards, well-disposed (towards them).

sanskrit: महिमानं परं चापि पाण्डवानां महात्मनाम् ।दुर्योधनो धार्तराष्ट्रो विवर्णः समपद्यत ॥१५॥
transliteration: mahimānaṁ paraṁ cāpi pāṇḍavānāṁ mahātmanām ,duryodhano dhārtarāṣṭro vivarṇaḥ samapadyata.
english: And having also observed the supreme greatness of the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, Duryodhana, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, became discolored (i.e., pale and dejected).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 15. And having also observed the supreme greatness of the great-souled Pāṇḍavas, Duryodhana, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, became discolored (i.e., pale and dejected).

sanskrit: स तु गच्छन्ननेकाग्रः सभामेवानुचिन्तयन् ।श्रियं च तामनुपमां धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ॥१६॥
transliteration: sa tu gacchannanekāgraḥ sabhāmevānucintayan ,śriyaṁ ca tāmanupamāṁ dharmarājasya dhīmataḥ.
english: But as he went, he was distracted, continually thinking only of the assembly hall, and of that unequalled prosperity of the wise king of (dharma) natural law (Yudhiṣṭhira).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 16. But as he went, he was distracted, continually thinking only of the assembly hall, and of that unequalled prosperity of the wise king of (dharma) natural law (Yudhiṣṭhira).

sanskrit: प्रमत्तो धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तदा ।नाभ्यभाषत्सुबलजं भाषमाणं पुनः पुनः ॥१७॥
transliteration: pramatto dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putro duryodhanastadā ,nābhyabhāṣatsubalajaṁ bhāṣamāṇaṁ punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Then, deluded, Duryodhana, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, did not respond to Subala's son (Śakuni) who was repeatedly speaking to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 17. Then, deluded, Duryodhana, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, did not respond to Subala's son (Śakuni) who was repeatedly speaking to him.

sanskrit: अनेकाग्रं तु तं दृष्ट्वा शकुनिः प्रत्यभाषत ।दुर्योधन कुतोमूलं निःश्वसन्निव गच्छसि ॥१८॥
transliteration: anekāgraṁ tu taṁ dṛṣṭvā śakuniḥ pratyabhāṣata ,duryodhana kutomūlaṁ niḥśvasanniva gacchasi.
english: Seeing him distracted, Shakuni addressed him: 'O Duryodhana, what is the cause for you to go about sighing?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 18. Seeing him distracted, Shakuni addressed him: 'O Duryodhana, what is the cause for you to go about sighing?'

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।दृष्ट्वेमां पृथिवीं कृत्स्नां युधिष्ठिरवशानुगाम् ।जितामस्त्रप्रतापेन श्वेताश्वस्य महात्मनः ॥१९॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,dṛṣṭvemāṁ pṛthivīṁ kṛtsnāṁ yudhiṣṭhiravaśānugām ,jitāmastrapratāpena śvetāśvasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: 'Having seen this entire earth, which has been conquered by the might of the weapons of the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, the white-horsed one, and is now subservient to Yudhishthira...'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 19. Duryodhana said: 'Having seen this entire earth, which has been conquered by the might of the weapons of the great-souled (mahātman) Arjuna, the white-horsed one, and is now subservient to Yudhishthira...'

sanskrit: तं च यज्ञं तथाभूतं दृष्ट्वा पार्थस्य मातुल ।यथा शक्रस्य देवेषु तथाभूतं महाद्युते ॥२०॥
transliteration: taṁ ca yajñaṁ tathābhūtaṁ dṛṣṭvā pārthasya mātula ,yathā śakrasya deveṣu tathābhūtaṁ mahādyute.
english: O maternal uncle of Pārtha (Arjuna), O greatly refulgent one, having beheld that Vedic ritual (yajña) of such a nature, just as Indra's (ritual) among the gods (is of such a nature)...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 20. O maternal uncle of Pārtha (Arjuna), O greatly refulgent one, having beheld that Vedic ritual (yajña) of such a nature, just as Indra's (ritual) among the gods (is of such a nature)...

sanskrit: अमर्षेण सुसंपूर्णो दह्यमानो दिवानिशम् ।शुचिशुक्रागमे काले शुष्ये तोयमिवाल्पकम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: amarṣeṇa susaṁpūrṇo dahyamāno divāniśam ,śuciśukrāgame kāle śuṣye toyamivālpakam.
english: Completely filled with indignation (amarṣa) and burning day and night, I would dry up like a small amount of water in the season of early summer (śuciśukrāgama kāla).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 21. Completely filled with indignation (amarṣa) and burning day and night, I would dry up like a small amount of water in the season of early summer (śuciśukrāgama kāla).

sanskrit: पश्य सात्वतमुख्येन शिशुपालं निपातितम् ।न च तत्र पुमानासीत्कश्चित्तस्य पदानुगः ॥२२॥
transliteration: paśya sātvatamukhyena śiśupālaṁ nipātitam ,na ca tatra pumānāsītkaścittasya padānugaḥ.
english: Behold Śiśupāla, slain by the chief of the Sātvatas (Krishna). There was no one present who was his follower.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 22. Behold Śiśupāla, slain by the chief of the Sātvatas (Krishna). There was no one present who was his follower.

sanskrit: दह्यमाना हि राजानः पाण्डवोत्थेन वह्निना ।क्षान्तवन्तोऽपराधं तं को हि तं क्षन्तुमर्हति ॥२३॥
transliteration: dahyamānā hi rājānaḥ pāṇḍavotthena vahninā ,kṣāntavanto'parādhaṁ taṁ ko hi taṁ kṣantumarhati.
english: Indeed, the kings, scorched by the fire ignited by the Pāṇḍavas, endured that offense. For who among them could have forgiven it?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 23. Indeed, the kings, scorched by the fire ignited by the Pāṇḍavas, endured that offense. For who among them could have forgiven it?

sanskrit: वासुदेवेन तत्कर्म तथायुक्तं महत्कृतम् ।सिद्धं च पाण्डवेयानां प्रतापेन महात्मनाम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: vāsudevena tatkarma tathāyuktaṁ mahatkṛtam ,siddhaṁ ca pāṇḍaveyānāṁ pratāpena mahātmanām.
english: That deed, performed by Vāsudeva (Krishna) in such a fitting and great manner, was also successfully accomplished due to the valor of the great-souled Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 24. That deed, performed by Vāsudeva (Krishna) in such a fitting and great manner, was also successfully accomplished due to the valor of the great-souled Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तथा हि रत्नान्यादाय विविधानि नृपा नृपम् ।उपतिष्ठन्ति कौन्तेयं वैश्या इव करप्रदाः ॥२५॥
transliteration: tathā hi ratnānyādāya vividhāni nṛpā nṛpam ,upatiṣṭhanti kaunteyaṁ vaiśyā iva karapradāḥ.
english: Indeed, just as merchants (vaiśyāḥ) offer taxes, so too do kings approach the son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), bringing various jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 25. Indeed, just as merchants (vaiśyāḥ) offer taxes, so too do kings approach the son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), bringing various jewels.

sanskrit: श्रियं तथाविधां दृष्ट्वा ज्वलन्तीमिव पाण्डवे ।अमर्षवशमापन्नो दह्येऽहमतथोचितः ॥२६॥
transliteration: śriyaṁ tathāvidhāṁ dṛṣṭvā jvalantīmiva pāṇḍave ,amarṣavaśamāpanno dahye'hamatathocitaḥ.
english: Upon seeing such glory (śrī), as if blazing, among the Pāṇḍavas, I, who am unsuited to such a fate, became overcome by indignation and am consumed [by anguish].
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 26. Upon seeing such glory (śrī), as if blazing, among the Pāṇḍavas, I, who am unsuited to such a fate, became overcome by indignation and am consumed [by anguish].

sanskrit: वह्निमेव प्रवेक्ष्यामि भक्षयिष्यामि वा विषम् ।अपो वापि प्रवेक्ष्यामि न हि शक्ष्यामि जीवितुम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vahnimeva pravekṣyāmi bhakṣayiṣyāmi vā viṣam ,apo vāpi pravekṣyāmi na hi śakṣyāmi jīvitum.
english: I will either enter fire, or I will consume poison, or I will drown myself in water. Indeed, I will not be able to live.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 27. I will either enter fire, or I will consume poison, or I will drown myself in water. Indeed, I will not be able to live.

sanskrit: को हि नाम पुमाँल्लोके मर्षयिष्यति सत्त्ववान् ।सपत्नानृध्यतो दृष्ट्वा हानिमात्मन एव च ॥२८॥
transliteration: ko hi nāma pumāँlloke marṣayiṣyati sattvavān ,sapatnānṛdhyato dṛṣṭvā hānimātmana eva ca.
english: What man in this world, possessing courage (sattva), would indeed tolerate seeing his rivals prosper and his own loss (ātman)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 28. What man in this world, possessing courage (sattva), would indeed tolerate seeing his rivals prosper and his own loss (ātman)?

sanskrit: सोऽहं न स्त्री न चाप्यस्त्री न पुमान्नापुमानपि ।योऽहं तां मर्षयाम्यद्य तादृशीं श्रियमागताम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: so'haṁ na strī na cāpyastrī na pumānnāpumānapi ,yo'haṁ tāṁ marṣayāmyadya tādṛśīṁ śriyamāgatām.
english: I am neither a woman, nor a non-woman, neither a man, nor a non-man (puruṣa); I, who today endure such glory (śrī) that has come [to others].
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 29. I am neither a woman, nor a non-woman, neither a man, nor a non-man (puruṣa); I, who today endure such glory (śrī) that has come [to others].

sanskrit: ईश्वरत्वं पृथिव्याश्च वसुमत्तां च तादृशीम् ।यज्ञं च तादृशं दृष्ट्वा मादृशः को न संज्वरेत् ॥३०॥
transliteration: īśvaratvaṁ pṛthivyāśca vasumattāṁ ca tādṛśīm ,yajñaṁ ca tādṛśaṁ dṛṣṭvā mādṛśaḥ ko na saṁjvaret.
english: Who, like me, would not be distressed upon seeing such dominion over the earth, such immense wealth, and such a grand Vedic ritual (yajña)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 30. Who, like me, would not be distressed upon seeing such dominion over the earth, such immense wealth, and such a grand Vedic ritual (yajña)?

sanskrit: अशक्तश्चैक एवाहं तामाहर्तुं नृपश्रियम् ।सहायांश्च न पश्यामि तेन मृत्युं विचिन्तये ॥३१॥
transliteration: aśaktaścaika evāhaṁ tāmāhartuṁ nṛpaśriyam ,sahāyāṁśca na paśyāmi tena mṛtyuṁ vicintaye.
english: I alone am incapable of obtaining that royal prosperity. And since I see no allies, I contemplate death.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 31. I alone am incapable of obtaining that royal prosperity. And since I see no allies, I contemplate death.

sanskrit: दैवमेव परं मन्ये पौरुषं तु निरर्थकम् ।दृष्ट्वा कुन्तीसुते शुभ्रां श्रियं तामाहृतां तथा ॥३२॥
transliteration: daivameva paraṁ manye pauruṣaṁ tu nirarthakam ,dṛṣṭvā kuntīsute śubhrāṁ śriyaṁ tāmāhṛtāṁ tathā.
english: I consider destiny alone to be supreme, while human effort is meaningless, having seen that brilliant prosperity thus obtained by Kunti's son (Yudhishthira).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 32. I consider destiny alone to be supreme, while human effort is meaningless, having seen that brilliant prosperity thus obtained by Kunti's son (Yudhishthira).

sanskrit: कृतो यत्नो मया पूर्वं विनाशे तस्य सौबल ।तच्च सर्वमतिक्रम्य स वृद्धोऽप्स्विव पङ्कजम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: kṛto yatno mayā pūrvaṁ vināśe tasya saubala ,tacca sarvamatikramya sa vṛddho'psviva paṅkajam.
english: O son of Subala (Saubala), I indeed made efforts for his destruction previously. But, transcending all of that, he has flourished like a lotus in water.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 33. O son of Subala (Saubala), I indeed made efforts for his destruction previously. But, transcending all of that, he has flourished like a lotus in water.

sanskrit: तेन दैवं परं मन्ये पौरुषं तु निरर्थकम् ।धार्तराष्ट्रा हि हीयन्ते पार्था वर्धन्ति नित्यशः ॥३४॥
transliteration: tena daivaṁ paraṁ manye pauruṣaṁ tu nirarthakam ,dhārtarāṣṭrā hi hīyante pārthā vardhanti nityaśaḥ.
english: Thus, I consider destiny to be supreme, while human effort is futile. Indeed, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra are diminished, whereas the sons of Pṛthā (Pāṇḍavas) always flourish.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 34. Thus, I consider destiny to be supreme, while human effort is futile. Indeed, the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra are diminished, whereas the sons of Pṛthā (Pāṇḍavas) always flourish.

sanskrit: सोऽहं श्रियं च तां दृष्ट्वा सभां तां च तथाविधाम् ।रक्षिभिश्चावहासं तं परितप्ये यथाग्निना ॥३५॥
transliteration: so'haṁ śriyaṁ ca tāṁ dṛṣṭvā sabhāṁ tāṁ ca tathāvidhām ,rakṣibhiścāvahāsaṁ taṁ paritapye yathāgninā.
english: Having seen that splendor and that kind of assembly, as well as that ridicule by the guards, I am tormented as if by fire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 35. Having seen that splendor and that kind of assembly, as well as that ridicule by the guards, I am tormented as if by fire.

sanskrit: स मामभ्यनुजानीहि मातुलाद्य सुदुःखितम् ।अमर्षं च समाविष्टं धृतराष्ट्रे निवेदय ॥३६॥
transliteration: sa māmabhyanujānīhi mātulādya suduḥkhitam ,amarṣaṁ ca samāviṣṭaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭre nivedaya.
english: Therefore, O uncle, grant leave to me, who is extremely distressed and overcome with indignation. And report (this) indignation to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 43 Verse 36. Therefore, O uncle, grant leave to me, who is extremely distressed and overcome with indignation. And report (this) indignation to Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।दुर्योधन न तेऽमर्षः कार्यः प्रति युधिष्ठिरम् ।भागधेयानि हि स्वानि पाण्डवा भुञ्जते सदा ॥१॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,duryodhana na te'marṣaḥ kāryaḥ prati yudhiṣṭhiram ,bhāgadheyāni hi svāni pāṇḍavā bhuñjate sadā.
english: Shakuni said: Duryodhana, you should not harbor anger towards Yudhiṣṭhira. For the Pāṇḍavas always enjoy their own destined shares.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 1. Shakuni said: Duryodhana, you should not harbor anger towards Yudhiṣṭhira. For the Pāṇḍavas always enjoy their own destined shares.

sanskrit: अनेकैरभ्युपायैश्च त्वयारब्धाः पुरासकृत् ।विमुक्ताश्च नरव्याघ्रा भागधेयपुरस्कृताः ॥२॥
transliteration: anekairabhyupāyaiśca tvayārabdhāḥ purāsakṛt ,vimuktāśca naravyāghrā bhāgadheyapuraskṛtāḥ.
english: Those tigers among men, whom you repeatedly attacked in the past with many schemes, were [yet] liberated, favored by their destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 2. Those tigers among men, whom you repeatedly attacked in the past with many schemes, were [yet] liberated, favored by their destiny.

sanskrit: तैर्लब्धा द्रौपदी भार्या द्रुपदश्च सुतैः सह ।सहायः पृथिवीलाभे वासुदेवश्च वीर्यवान् ॥३॥
transliteration: tairlabdhā draupadī bhāryā drupadaśca sutaiḥ saha ,sahāyaḥ pṛthivīlābhe vāsudevaśca vīryavān.
english: By them, Draupadī was obtained as a wife, and Drupada with his sons [became an ally], and the powerful Vāsudeva (kṛṣṇa) [became] a helper in gaining the kingdom (pṛthivī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 3. By them, Draupadī was obtained as a wife, and Drupada with his sons [became an ally], and the powerful Vāsudeva (kṛṣṇa) [became] a helper in gaining the kingdom (pṛthivī).

sanskrit: लब्धश्च नाभिभूतोऽर्थः पित्र्योंऽशः पृथिवीपते ।विवृद्धस्तेजसा तेषां तत्र का परिदेवना ॥४॥
transliteration: labdhaśca nābhibhūto'rthaḥ pitryoṁ'śaḥ pṛthivīpate ,vivṛddhastejasā teṣāṁ tatra kā paridevanā.
english: And, O ruler of the earth (pṛthivīpati), their paternal share, the objective, was obtained and was not suppressed; it increased by their valor. What is there to lament about that?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 4. And, O ruler of the earth (pṛthivīpati), their paternal share, the objective, was obtained and was not suppressed; it increased by their valor. What is there to lament about that?

sanskrit: धनंजयेन गाण्डीवमक्षय्यौ च महेषुधी ।लब्धान्यस्त्राणि दिव्यानि तर्पयित्वा हुताशनम् ॥५॥
transliteration: dhanaṁjayena gāṇḍīvamakṣayyau ca maheṣudhī ,labdhānyastrāṇi divyāni tarpayitvā hutāśanam.
english: By Dhananjaya (Arjuna), having satisfied the fire god (hutāśana), the Gāṇḍīva bow, two inexhaustible great quivers, and divine weapons were obtained.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 5. By Dhananjaya (Arjuna), having satisfied the fire god (hutāśana), the Gāṇḍīva bow, two inexhaustible great quivers, and divine weapons were obtained.

sanskrit: तेन कार्मुकमुख्येन बाहुवीर्येण चात्मनः ।कृता वशे महीपालास्तत्र का परिदेवना ॥६॥
transliteration: tena kārmukamukhyena bāhuvīryeṇa cātmanaḥ ,kṛtā vaśe mahīpālāstatra kā paridevanā.
english: By means of that excellent bow and his own (ātman) arm-strength, kings were brought under control. What cause for lamentation is there in that?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 6. By means of that excellent bow and his own (ātman) arm-strength, kings were brought under control. What cause for lamentation is there in that?

sanskrit: अग्निदाहान्मयं चापि मोक्षयित्वा स दानवम् ।सभां तां कारयामास सव्यसाची परंतपः ॥७॥
transliteration: agnidāhānmayaṁ cāpi mokṣayitvā sa dānavam ,sabhāṁ tāṁ kārayāmāsa savyasācī paraṁtapaḥ.
english: Having saved the demon Maya from the conflagration, Savyasācin, the tormentor of foes, caused that assembly hall to be constructed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 7. Having saved the demon Maya from the conflagration, Savyasācin, the tormentor of foes, caused that assembly hall to be constructed.

sanskrit: तेन चैव मयेनोक्ताः किंकरा नाम राक्षसाः ।वहन्ति तां सभां भीमास्तत्र का परिदेवना ॥८॥
transliteration: tena caiva mayenoktāḥ kiṁkarā nāma rākṣasāḥ ,vahanti tāṁ sabhāṁ bhīmāstatra kā paridevanā.
english: And by that very Maya, formidable Rākṣasas named Kiṅkaras were instructed; they carry that assembly hall. What cause for lamentation is there in that?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 8. And by that very Maya, formidable Rākṣasas named Kiṅkaras were instructed; they carry that assembly hall. What cause for lamentation is there in that?

sanskrit: यच्चासहायतां राजन्नुक्तवानसि भारत ।तन्मिथ्या भ्रातरो हीमे सहायास्ते महारथाः ॥९॥
transliteration: yaccāsahāyatāṁ rājannuktavānasi bhārata ,tanmithyā bhrātaro hīme sahāyāste mahārathāḥ.
english: And as you, O King (rājan), O Bhārata, spoke of helplessness, that is indeed false, for these brothers of yours are mighty warriors and allies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 9. And as you, O King (rājan), O Bhārata, spoke of helplessness, that is indeed false, for these brothers of yours are mighty warriors and allies.

sanskrit: द्रोणस्तव महेष्वासः सह पुत्रेण धीमता ।सूतपुत्रश्च राधेयो गौतमश्च महारथः ॥१०॥
transliteration: droṇastava maheṣvāsaḥ saha putreṇa dhīmatā ,sūtaputraśca rādheyo gautamaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Your great archer Drona, along with his intelligent son, and Karna (the son of Suta, the son of Radha), and also Gautama (Kripa), the great charioteer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 10. Your great archer Drona, along with his intelligent son, and Karna (the son of Suta, the son of Radha), and also Gautama (Kripa), the great charioteer.

sanskrit: अहं च सह सोदर्यैः सौमदत्तिश्च वीर्यवान् ।एतैस्त्वं सहितः सर्वैर्जय कृत्स्नां वसुंधराम् ॥११॥
transliteration: ahaṁ ca saha sodaryaiḥ saumadattiśca vīryavān ,etaistvaṁ sahitaḥ sarvairjaya kṛtsnāṁ vasuṁdharām.
english: And I, together with my brothers, and the powerful Saumadatti (Bhuriśravas) – you, accompanied by all of them, should conquer the entire earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 11. And I, together with my brothers, and the powerful Saumadatti (Bhuriśravas) – you, accompanied by all of them, should conquer the entire earth.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।त्वया च सहितो राजन्नेतैश्चान्यैर्महारथैः ।एतानेव विजेष्यामि यदि त्वमनुमन्यसे ॥१२॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,tvayā ca sahito rājannetaiścānyairmahārathaiḥ ,etāneva vijeṣyāmi yadi tvamanumanyase.
english: Duryodhana said: "O King, if you grant permission, I, accompanied by you and by these and other great charioteers, will indeed conquer these very ones."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 12. Duryodhana said: "O King, if you grant permission, I, accompanied by you and by these and other great charioteers, will indeed conquer these very ones."

sanskrit: एतेषु विजितेष्वद्य भविष्यति मही मम ।सर्वे च पृथिवीपालाः सभा सा च महाधना ॥१३॥
transliteration: eteṣu vijiteṣvadya bhaviṣyati mahī mama ,sarve ca pṛthivīpālāḥ sabhā sā ca mahādhanā.
english: When these (enemies) are conquered today, the earth will be mine. And all the kings of the earth, along with that greatly wealthy assembly hall, will also be mine.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 13. When these (enemies) are conquered today, the earth will be mine. And all the kings of the earth, along with that greatly wealthy assembly hall, will also be mine.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।धनंजयो वासुदेवो भीमसेनो युधिष्ठिरः ।नकुलः सहदेवश्च द्रुपदश्च सहात्मजैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,dhanaṁjayo vāsudevo bhīmaseno yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,nakulaḥ sahadevaśca drupadaśca sahātmajaiḥ.
english: Śakuni said, "Dhanañjaya, Vāsudeva, Bhīmasena, Yudhiṣṭhira, Nakula, Sahadeva, and Drupada with his sons...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 14. Śakuni said, "Dhanañjaya, Vāsudeva, Bhīmasena, Yudhiṣṭhira, Nakula, Sahadeva, and Drupada with his sons...

sanskrit: नैते युधि बलाज्जेतुं शक्याः सुरगणैरपि ।महारथा महेष्वासाः कृतास्त्रा युद्धदुर्मदाः ॥१५॥
transliteration: naite yudhi balājjetuṁ śakyāḥ suragaṇairapi ,mahārathā maheṣvāsāḥ kṛtāstrā yuddhadurmadāḥ.
english: These individuals cannot be conquered by force in battle, not even by hosts of gods. They are great chariot warriors, mighty archers, highly skilled in weapons, and fiercely formidable in war.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 15. These individuals cannot be conquered by force in battle, not even by hosts of gods. They are great chariot warriors, mighty archers, highly skilled in weapons, and fiercely formidable in war.

sanskrit: अहं तु तद्विजानामि विजेतुं येन शक्यते ।युधिष्ठिरं स्वयं राजंस्तन्निबोध जुषस्व च ॥१६॥
transliteration: ahaṁ tu tadvijānāmi vijetuṁ yena śakyate ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ svayaṁ rājaṁstannibodha juṣasva ca.
english: But I, O king, know the way by which Yudhiṣṭhira himself can be conquered. Listen to that (method) and accept it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 16. But I, O king, know the way by which Yudhiṣṭhira himself can be conquered. Listen to that (method) and accept it.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।अप्रमादेन सुहृदामन्येषां च महात्मनाम् ।यदि शक्या विजेतुं ते तन्ममाचक्ष्व मातुल ॥१७॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,apramādena suhṛdāmanyeṣāṁ ca mahātmanām ,yadi śakyā vijetuṁ te tanmamācakṣva mātula.
english: Duryodhana said, "O maternal uncle, if they can indeed be conquered by the diligent efforts of (our) friends and other great-souled individuals, then please tell me that (method)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 17. Duryodhana said, "O maternal uncle, if they can indeed be conquered by the diligent efforts of (our) friends and other great-souled individuals, then please tell me that (method)."

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।द्यूतप्रियश्च कौन्तेयो न च जानाति देवितुम् ।समाहूतश्च राजेन्द्रो न शक्ष्यति निवर्तितुम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,dyūtapriyaśca kaunteyo na ca jānāti devitum ,samāhūtaśca rājendro na śakṣyati nivartitum.
