english: Shakuni said, "Kaunteya is fond of gambling, but he doesn't know how to play. And once that king of kings has been summoned, he will not be able to refuse."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 18. Shakuni said, "Kaunteya is fond of gambling, but he doesn't know how to play. And once that king of kings has been summoned, he will not be able to refuse."

sanskrit: देवने कुशलश्चाहं न मेऽस्ति सदृशो भुवि ।त्रिषु लोकेषु कौन्तेयं तं त्वं द्यूते समाह्वय ॥१९॥
transliteration: devane kuśalaścāhaṁ na me'sti sadṛśo bhuvi ,triṣu lokeṣu kaunteyaṁ taṁ tvaṁ dyūte samāhvaya.
english: I am indeed skilled in gambling; there is no one like me on earth. You should summon that Kaunteya to a game of dice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 19. I am indeed skilled in gambling; there is no one like me on earth. You should summon that Kaunteya to a game of dice.

sanskrit: तस्याक्षकुशलो राजन्नादास्येऽहमसंशयम् ।राज्यं श्रियं च तां दीप्तां त्वदर्थं पुरुषर्षभ ॥२०॥
transliteration: tasyākṣakuśalo rājannādāsye'hamasaṁśayam ,rājyaṁ śriyaṁ ca tāṁ dīptāṁ tvadarthaṁ puruṣarṣabha.
english: O King, O chief of persons (puruṣa), I, skilled in dice, will undoubtedly take his kingdom and that shining fortune for your sake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 20. O King, O chief of persons (puruṣa), I, skilled in dice, will undoubtedly take his kingdom and that shining fortune for your sake.

sanskrit: इदं तु सर्वं त्वं राज्ञे दुर्योधन निवेदय ।अनुज्ञातस्तु ते पित्रा विजेष्ये तं न संशयः ॥२१॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu sarvaṁ tvaṁ rājñe duryodhana nivedaya ,anujñātastu te pitrā vijeṣye taṁ na saṁśayaḥ.
english: But you, Duryodhana, report all this to King Dhritarashtra. Once permitted by your father, I will conquer him; there is no doubt.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 21. But you, Duryodhana, report all this to King Dhritarashtra. Once permitted by your father, I will conquer him; there is no doubt.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।त्वमेव कुरुमुख्याय धृतराष्ट्राय सौबल ।निवेदय यथान्यायं नाहं शक्ष्ये निशंसितुम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,tvameva kurumukhyāya dhṛtarāṣṭrāya saubala ,nivedaya yathānyāyaṁ nāhaṁ śakṣye niśaṁsitum.
english: Duryodhana said: "O son of Subala (Śakuni), you yourself should report this properly to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the chief of the Kurus. I am unable to describe it."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 44 Verse 22. Duryodhana said: "O son of Subala (Śakuni), you yourself should report this properly to Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the chief of the Kurus. I am unable to describe it."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अनुभूय तु राज्ञस्तं राजसूयं महाक्रतुम् ।युधिष्ठिरस्य नृपतेर्गान्धारीपुत्रसंयुतः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,anubhūya tu rājñastaṁ rājasūyaṁ mahākratum ,yudhiṣṭhirasya nṛpatergāndhārīputrasaṁyutaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: And so, accompanied by a son of Gāndhārī, having indeed experienced that great Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) of King Yudhiṣṭhira...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: And so, accompanied by a son of Gāndhārī, having indeed experienced that great Rājasūya (Vedic ritual) of King Yudhiṣṭhira...

sanskrit: प्रियकृन्मतमाज्ञाय पूर्वं दुर्योधनस्य तत् ।प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमासीनं शकुनिः सौबलस्तदा ॥२॥
transliteration: priyakṛnmatamājñāya pūrvaṁ duryodhanasya tat ,prajñācakṣuṣamāsīnaṁ śakuniḥ saubalastadā.
english: Śakuni, the son of Subala, then understood that previous intention of Duryodhana (to please Dhṛtarāṣṭra, or simply his current state of mind), and approaching the blind king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) who was seated...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 2. Śakuni, the son of Subala, then understood that previous intention of Duryodhana (to please Dhṛtarāṣṭra, or simply his current state of mind), and approaching the blind king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) who was seated...

sanskrit: दुर्योधनवचः श्रुत्वा धृतराष्ट्रं जनाधिपम् ।उपगम्य महाप्राज्ञं शकुनिर्वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥३॥
transliteration: duryodhanavacaḥ śrutvā dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janādhipam ,upagamya mahāprājñaṁ śakunirvākyamabravīt.
english: Having heard Duryodhana's words, Śakuni approached the great sage Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of the people, and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 3. Having heard Duryodhana's words, Śakuni approached the great sage Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of the people, and spoke these words.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो महाराज विवर्णो हरिणः कृशः ।दीनश्चिन्तापरश्चैव तद्विद्धि भरतर्षभ ॥४॥
transliteration: duryodhano mahārāja vivarṇo hariṇaḥ kṛśaḥ ,dīnaścintāparaścaiva tadviddhi bharatarṣabha.
english: O great king (mahārāja), Duryodhana is pale, faint, and emaciated. He is distressed and entirely absorbed in worry. Know this, O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 4. O great king (mahārāja), Duryodhana is pale, faint, and emaciated. He is distressed and entirely absorbed in worry. Know this, O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha).

sanskrit: न वै परीक्षसे सम्यगसह्यं शत्रुसंभवम् ।ज्येष्ठपुत्रस्य शोकं त्वं किमर्थं नावबुध्यसे ॥५॥
transliteration: na vai parīkṣase samyagasahyaṁ śatrusaṁbhavam ,jyeṣṭhaputrasya śokaṁ tvaṁ kimarthaṁ nāvabudhyase.
english: You certainly do not properly observe the unbearable sorrow arising from enemies in your eldest son. Why do you not understand it?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 5. You certainly do not properly observe the unbearable sorrow arising from enemies in your eldest son. Why do you not understand it?

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।दुर्योधन कुतोमूलं भृशमार्तोऽसि पुत्रक ।श्रोतव्यश्चेन्मया सोऽर्थो ब्रूहि मे कुरुनन्दन ॥६॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,duryodhana kutomūlaṁ bhṛśamārto'si putraka ,śrotavyaścenmayā so'rtho brūhi me kurunandana.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'My dear son (putraka) Duryodhana, what is the origin of your extreme distress? If that matter (artha) is to be heard by me, O delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), then tell me.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 6. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'My dear son (putraka) Duryodhana, what is the origin of your extreme distress? If that matter (artha) is to be heard by me, O delight of the Kurus (kurunandana), then tell me.'

sanskrit: अयं त्वां शकुनिः प्राह विवर्णं हरिणं कृशम् ।चिन्तयंश्च न पश्यामि शोकस्य तव संभवम् ॥७॥
transliteration: ayaṁ tvāṁ śakuniḥ prāha vivarṇaṁ hariṇaṁ kṛśam ,cintayaṁśca na paśyāmi śokasya tava saṁbhavam.
english: This Śakuni says that you are pale, faint, and emaciated. And even though I am thinking, I do not see the origin of your sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 7. This Śakuni says that you are pale, faint, and emaciated. And even though I am thinking, I do not see the origin of your sorrow.

sanskrit: ऐश्वर्यं हि महत्पुत्र त्वयि सर्वं समर्पितम् ।भ्रातरः सुहृदश्चैव नाचरन्ति तवाप्रियम् ॥८॥
transliteration: aiśvaryaṁ hi mahatputra tvayi sarvaṁ samarpitam ,bhrātaraḥ suhṛdaścaiva nācaranti tavāpriyam.
english: Indeed, great sovereignty (aiśvarya) and everything is entrusted to you, my son. Your brothers and friends certainly do not act against your wishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 8. Indeed, great sovereignty (aiśvarya) and everything is entrusted to you, my son. Your brothers and friends certainly do not act against your wishes.

sanskrit: आच्छादयसि प्रावारानश्नासि पिशितौदनम् ।आजानेया वहन्ति त्वां केनासि हरिणः कृशः ॥९॥
transliteration: ācchādayasi prāvārānaśnāsi piśitaudanam ,ājāneyā vahanti tvāṁ kenāsi hariṇaḥ kṛśaḥ.
english: You cover yourself with cloaks (prāvāra), and you eat meat and rice (piśitāudana). Thoroughbred horses (ājāneya) carry you, so by what means are you thin like a deer?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 9. You cover yourself with cloaks (prāvāra), and you eat meat and rice (piśitāudana). Thoroughbred horses (ājāneya) carry you, so by what means are you thin like a deer?

sanskrit: शयनानि महार्हाणि योषितश्च मनोरमाः ।गुणवन्ति च वेश्मानि विहाराश्च यथासुखम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: śayanāni mahārhāṇi yoṣitaśca manoramāḥ ,guṇavanti ca veśmāni vihārāśca yathāsukham.
english: You have valuable beds, charming women, well-appointed houses, and enjoyments according to your desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 10. You have valuable beds, charming women, well-appointed houses, and enjoyments according to your desire.

sanskrit: देवानामिव ते सर्वं वाचि बद्धं न संशयः ।स दीन इव दुर्धर्षः कस्माच्छोचसि पुत्रक ॥११॥
transliteration: devānāmiva te sarvaṁ vāci baddhaṁ na saṁśayaḥ ,sa dīna iva durdharṣaḥ kasmācchocasi putraka.
english: For you, everything is like that of the gods; it is fixed by declaration, there is no doubt. Though you are formidable, you appear like a poor man. Why do you lament, my son?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 11. For you, everything is like that of the gods; it is fixed by declaration, there is no doubt. Though you are formidable, you appear like a poor man. Why do you lament, my son?

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।अश्नाम्याच्छादये चाहं यथा कुपुरुषस्तथा ।अमर्षं धारये चोग्रं तितिक्षन्कालपर्ययम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,aśnāmyācchādaye cāhaṁ yathā kupuruṣastathā ,amarṣaṁ dhāraye cograṁ titikṣankālaparyayam.
english: Duryodhana said: "I eat and clothe myself just like an ignoble man. I also harbor fierce indignation, enduring the turn of fate."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 12. Duryodhana said: "I eat and clothe myself just like an ignoble man. I also harbor fierce indignation, enduring the turn of fate."

sanskrit: अमर्षणः स्वाः प्रकृतीरभिभूय परे स्थिताः ।क्लेशान्मुमुक्षुः परजान्स वै पुरुष उच्यते ॥१३॥
transliteration: amarṣaṇaḥ svāḥ prakṛtīrabhibhūya pare sthitāḥ ,kleśānmumukṣuḥ parajānsa vai puruṣa ucyate.
english: A person (puruṣa) who is intolerant [of injustice], and who overcomes his own internal dispositions (prakṛti) that arise in interaction with others, and desires to be free from troubles originating from others - he is indeed called a [true] man (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 13. A person (puruṣa) who is intolerant [of injustice], and who overcomes his own internal dispositions (prakṛti) that arise in interaction with others, and desires to be free from troubles originating from others - he is indeed called a [true] man (puruṣa).

sanskrit: संतोषो वै श्रियं हन्ति अभिमानश्च भारत ।अनुक्रोशभये चोभे यैर्वृतो नाश्नुते महत् ॥१४॥
transliteration: saṁtoṣo vai śriyaṁ hanti abhimānaśca bhārata ,anukrośabhaye cobhe yairvṛto nāśnute mahat.
english: Indeed, excessive contentment and arrogance destroy prosperity (śrī), O Bhārata. And both pity and fear - a person encompassed by these does not attain greatness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 14. Indeed, excessive contentment and arrogance destroy prosperity (śrī), O Bhārata. And both pity and fear - a person encompassed by these does not attain greatness.

sanskrit: न मामवति तद्भुक्तं श्रियं दृष्ट्वा युधिष्ठिरे ।ज्वलन्तीमिव कौन्तेये विवर्णकरणीं मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: na māmavati tadbhuktaṁ śriyaṁ dṛṣṭvā yudhiṣṭhire ,jvalantīmiva kaunteye vivarṇakaraṇīṁ mama.
english: O Yudhishthira, that which I have enjoyed does not protect me when I behold the prosperity (śrī) of Kaunteya (Arjuna), which blazes like fire and makes me turn pale.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 15. O Yudhishthira, that which I have enjoyed does not protect me when I behold the prosperity (śrī) of Kaunteya (Arjuna), which blazes like fire and makes me turn pale.

sanskrit: सपत्नानृध्यतोऽऽत्मानं हीयमानं निशाम्य च ।अदृश्यामपि कौन्तेये स्थितां पश्यन्निवोद्यताम् ।तस्मादहं विवर्णश्च दीनश्च हरिणः कृशः ॥१६॥
transliteration: sapatnānṛdhyato''tmānaṁ hīyamānaṁ niśāmya ca ,adṛśyāmapi kaunteye sthitāṁ paśyannivodyatām ,tasmādahaṁ vivarṇaśca dīnaśca hariṇaḥ kṛśaḥ.
english: Having observed my enemies prospering and myself (ātman) diminishing, and as if seeing my own unseen and rising indignation residing within Arjuna (Kaunteya), I am therefore pale, miserable, and emaciated like a frightened deer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 16. Having observed my enemies prospering and myself (ātman) diminishing, and as if seeing my own unseen and rising indignation residing within Arjuna (Kaunteya), I am therefore pale, miserable, and emaciated like a frightened deer.

sanskrit: अष्टाशीतिसहस्राणि स्नातका गृहमेधिनः ।त्रिंशद्दासीक एकैको यान्बिभर्ति युधिष्ठिरः ॥१७॥
transliteration: aṣṭāśītisahasrāṇi snātakā gṛhamedhinaḥ ,triṁśaddāsīka ekaiko yānbibharti yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Yudhishthira maintains eighty-eight thousand householder graduates, each of whom has thirty female servants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 17. Yudhishthira maintains eighty-eight thousand householder graduates, each of whom has thirty female servants.

sanskrit: दशान्यानि सहस्राणि नित्यं तत्रान्नमुत्तमम् ।भुञ्जते रुक्मपात्रीभिर्युधिष्ठिरनिवेशने ॥१८॥
transliteration: daśānyāni sahasrāṇi nityaṁ tatrānnamuttamam ,bhuñjate rukmapātrībhiryudhiṣṭhiraniveśane.
english: Ten other thousands of people daily partake of excellent food there in Yudhishthira's residence, using golden plates.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 18. Ten other thousands of people daily partake of excellent food there in Yudhishthira's residence, using golden plates.

sanskrit: कदलीमृगमोकानि कृष्णश्यामारुणानि च ।काम्बोजः प्राहिणोत्तस्मै परार्ध्यानपि कम्बलान् ॥१९॥
transliteration: kadalīmṛgamokāni kṛṣṇaśyāmāruṇāni ca ,kāmbojaḥ prāhiṇottasmai parārdhyānapi kambalān.
english: The Kambojan king sent to him (Yudhishthira) blankets that were made of kadalī deer hair, black, dark-blue, and reddish, and even of the highest value.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 19. The Kambojan king sent to him (Yudhishthira) blankets that were made of kadalī deer hair, black, dark-blue, and reddish, and even of the highest value.

sanskrit: रथयोषिद्गवाश्वस्य शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः ।त्रिंशतं चोष्ट्रवामीनां शतानि विचरन्त्युत ॥२०॥
transliteration: rathayoṣidgavāśvasya śataśo'tha sahasraśaḥ ,triṁśataṁ coṣṭravāmīnāṁ śatāni vicarantyuta.
english: And then, hundreds and thousands of chariots, women, cows, and horses, and also three thousand female camels, move about.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 20. And then, hundreds and thousands of chariots, women, cows, and horses, and also three thousand female camels, move about.

sanskrit: पृथग्विधानि रत्नानि पार्थिवाः पृथिवीपते ।आहरन्क्रतुमुख्येऽस्मिन्कुन्तीपुत्राय भूरिशः ॥२१॥
transliteration: pṛthagvidhāni ratnāni pārthivāḥ pṛthivīpate ,āharankratumukhye'sminkuntīputrāya bhūriśaḥ.
english: O lord of the earth, kings brought various kinds of jewels and treasures in great quantities for the son of Kunti (Arjuna) in this principal Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 21. O lord of the earth, kings brought various kinds of jewels and treasures in great quantities for the son of Kunti (Arjuna) in this principal Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: न क्वचिद्धि मया दृष्टस्तादृशो नैव च श्रुतः ।यादृग्धनागमो यज्ञे पाण्डुपुत्रस्य धीमतः ॥२२॥
transliteration: na kvaciddhi mayā dṛṣṭastādṛśo naiva ca śrutaḥ ,yādṛgdhanāgamo yajñe pāṇḍuputrasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Indeed, I have never seen or even heard of such an accumulation of wealth as was received in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of the intelligent son of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 22. Indeed, I have never seen or even heard of such an accumulation of wealth as was received in the Vedic ritual (yajña) of the intelligent son of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: अपर्यन्तं धनौघं तं दृष्ट्वा शत्रोरहं नृप ।शर्म नैवाधिगच्छामि चिन्तयानोऽनिशं विभो ॥२३॥
transliteration: aparyantaṁ dhanaughaṁ taṁ dṛṣṭvā śatrorahaṁ nṛpa ,śarma naivādhigacchāmi cintayāno'niśaṁ vibho.
english: O King, O mighty one, upon seeing that endless flood of wealth belonging to the enemy, I am constantly worried and never find peace.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 23. O King, O mighty one, upon seeing that endless flood of wealth belonging to the enemy, I am constantly worried and never find peace.

sanskrit: ब्राह्मणा वाटधानाश्च गोमन्तः शतसंघशः ।त्रैखर्वं बलिमादाय द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥२४॥
transliteration: brāhmaṇā vāṭadhānāśca gomantaḥ śatasaṁghaśaḥ ,traikharvaṁ balimādāya dvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: Hundreds of groups of Brahmins and Vāṭadhānas, rich in cows, stood at the gate, prevented from entering, having brought a tribute of three-fourths.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 24. Hundreds of groups of Brahmins and Vāṭadhānas, rich in cows, stood at the gate, prevented from entering, having brought a tribute of three-fourths.

sanskrit: कमण्डलूनुपादाय जातरूपमयाञ्शुभान् ।एवं बलिं समादाय प्रवेशं लेभिरे ततः ॥२५॥
transliteration: kamaṇḍalūnupādāya jātarūpamayāñśubhān ,evaṁ baliṁ samādāya praveśaṁ lebhire tataḥ.
english: They gained entrance thereafter, having thus collected the tribute and carrying auspicious, golden water-pots.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 25. They gained entrance thereafter, having thus collected the tribute and carrying auspicious, golden water-pots.

sanskrit: यन्नैव मधु शक्राय धारयन्त्यमरस्त्रियः ।तदस्मै कांस्यमाहार्षीद्वारुणं कलशोदधिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: yannaiva madhu śakrāya dhārayantyamarastriyaḥ ,tadasmai kāṁsyamāhārṣīdvāruṇaṁ kalaśodadhiḥ.
english: The pitcher-ocean brought to him that bronze vessel, sacred to Varuṇa, which celestial women never even offer as honey to Indra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 26. The pitcher-ocean brought to him that bronze vessel, sacred to Varuṇa, which celestial women never even offer as honey to Indra.

sanskrit: शैक्यं रुक्मसहस्रस्य बहुरत्नविभूषितम् ।दृष्ट्वा च मम तत्सर्वं ज्वररूपमिवाभवत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: śaikyaṁ rukmasahasrasya bahuratnavibhūṣitam ,dṛṣṭvā ca mama tatsarvaṁ jvararūpamivābhavat.
english: When I saw that entire necklace, worth a thousand gold pieces and adorned with many jewels, it became like a fever to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 27. When I saw that entire necklace, worth a thousand gold pieces and adorned with many jewels, it became like a fever to me.

sanskrit: गृहीत्वा तत्तु गच्छन्ति समुद्रौ पूर्वदक्षिणौ ।तथैव पश्चिमं यान्ति गृहीत्वा भरतर्षभ ॥२८॥
transliteration: gṛhītvā tattu gacchanti samudrau pūrvadakṣiṇau ,tathaiva paścimaṁ yānti gṛhītvā bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), taking that, they proceed to the eastern and southern oceans. Similarly, they journey west, also taking it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 28. O best among the Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), taking that, they proceed to the eastern and southern oceans. Similarly, they journey west, also taking it.

sanskrit: उत्तरं तु न गच्छन्ति विना तात पतत्रिभिः ।इदं चाद्भुतमत्रासीत्तन्मे निगदतः शृणु ॥२९॥
transliteration: uttaraṁ tu na gacchanti vinā tāta patatribhiḥ ,idaṁ cādbhutamatrāsīttanme nigadataḥ śṛṇu.
english: O dear one (tāta), they do not, however, go north without birds. And this was a wonder here; listen to that as I relate it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 29. O dear one (tāta), they do not, however, go north without birds. And this was a wonder here; listen to that as I relate it.

sanskrit: पूर्णे शतसहस्रे तु विप्राणां परिविष्यताम् ।स्थापिता तत्र संज्ञाभूच्छङ्खो ध्मायति नित्यशः ॥३०॥
transliteration: pūrṇe śatasahasre tu viprāṇāṁ pariviṣyatām ,sthāpitā tatra saṁjñābhūcchaṅkho dhmāyati nityaśaḥ.
english: When a full hundred thousand brahmins had been served, a signal (saṃjñā) was established there: a conch shell would constantly blow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 30. When a full hundred thousand brahmins had been served, a signal (saṃjñā) was established there: a conch shell would constantly blow.

sanskrit: मुहुर्मुहुः प्रणदतस्तस्य शङ्खस्य भारत ।उत्तमं शब्दमश्रौषं ततो रोमाणि मेऽहृषन् ॥३१॥
transliteration: muhurmuhuḥ praṇadatastasya śaṅkhasya bhārata ,uttamaṁ śabdamaśrauṣaṁ tato romāṇi me'hṛṣan.
english: O Bhārata, I repeatedly heard the excellent sound of that resounding conch shell. Then, my body hairs (romāṇi) stood on end.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 31. O Bhārata, I repeatedly heard the excellent sound of that resounding conch shell. Then, my body hairs (romāṇi) stood on end.

sanskrit: पार्थिवैर्बहुभिः कीर्णमुपस्थानं दिदृक्षुभिः ।सर्वरत्नान्युपादाय पार्थिवा वै जनेश्वर ॥३२॥
transliteration: pārthivairbahubhiḥ kīrṇamupasthānaṁ didṛkṣubhiḥ ,sarvaratnānyupādāya pārthivā vai janeśvara.
english: O lord of men, the assembly hall was crowded with many kings who desired to witness [the event], having brought all sorts of jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 32. O lord of men, the assembly hall was crowded with many kings who desired to witness [the event], having brought all sorts of jewels.

sanskrit: यज्ञे तस्य महाराज पाण्डुपुत्रस्य धीमतः ।वैश्या इव महीपाला द्विजातिपरिवेषकाः ॥३३॥
transliteration: yajñe tasya mahārāja pāṇḍuputrasya dhīmataḥ ,vaiśyā iva mahīpālā dvijātipariveṣakāḥ.
english: O great king, at the Vedic ritual (yajña) of that wise son of Pāṇḍu, kings themselves acted like members of the merchant class (vaiśyas), serving the twice-born (dvijātis).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 33. O great king, at the Vedic ritual (yajña) of that wise son of Pāṇḍu, kings themselves acted like members of the merchant class (vaiśyas), serving the twice-born (dvijātis).

sanskrit: न सा श्रीर्देवराजस्य यमस्य वरुणस्य वा ।गुह्यकाधिपतेर्वापि या श्री राजन्युधिष्ठिरे ॥३४॥
transliteration: na sā śrīrdevarājasya yamasya varuṇasya vā ,guhyakādhipatervāpi yā śrī rājanyudhiṣṭhire.
english: O king, the splendor (śrī) that Yudhiṣṭhira possesses is not found with the king of gods, Indra, nor with Yama, nor Varuṇa, nor even with Kubera, the lord of the Guhyakas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 34. O king, the splendor (śrī) that Yudhiṣṭhira possesses is not found with the king of gods, Indra, nor with Yama, nor Varuṇa, nor even with Kubera, the lord of the Guhyakas.

sanskrit: तां दृष्ट्वा पाण्डुपुत्रस्य श्रियं परमिकामहम् ।शान्तिं न परिगच्छामि दह्यमानेन चेतसा ॥३५॥
transliteration: tāṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍuputrasya śriyaṁ paramikāmaham ,śāntiṁ na parigacchāmi dahyamānena cetasā.
english: Having witnessed that supreme splendor (śrī) of the son of Pāṇḍu, I cannot find peace, as my mind (chetas) is burning [with envy].
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 35. Having witnessed that supreme splendor (śrī) of the son of Pāṇḍu, I cannot find peace, as my mind (chetas) is burning [with envy].

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।यामेतामुत्तमां लक्ष्मीं दृष्टवानसि पाण्डवे ।तस्याः प्राप्तावुपायं मे शृणु सत्यपराक्रम ॥३६॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,yāmetāmuttamāṁ lakṣmīṁ dṛṣṭavānasi pāṇḍave ,tasyāḥ prāptāvupāyaṁ me śṛṇu satyaparākrama.
english: Śakuni said, "O Pāṇḍava, you have seen this excellent prosperity (lakṣmī). O truth-valiant one, hear from me the method to attain it."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 36. Śakuni said, "O Pāṇḍava, you have seen this excellent prosperity (lakṣmī). O truth-valiant one, hear from me the method to attain it."

sanskrit: अहमक्षेष्वभिज्ञातः पृथिव्यामपि भारत ।हृदयज्ञः पणज्ञश्च विशेषज्ञश्च देवने ॥३७॥
transliteration: ahamakṣeṣvabhijñātaḥ pṛthivyāmapi bhārata ,hṛdayajñaḥ paṇajñaśca viśeṣajñaśca devane.
english: O Bhārata, I am renowned throughout the world for my skill in dice. I am an expert in gambling, knowing the opponent's mind and understanding the stakes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 37. O Bhārata, I am renowned throughout the world for my skill in dice. I am an expert in gambling, knowing the opponent's mind and understanding the stakes.

sanskrit: द्यूतप्रियश्च कौन्तेयो न च जानाति देवितुम् ।आहूतश्चैष्यति व्यक्तं दीव्यावेत्याह्वयस्व तम् ॥३८॥
transliteration: dyūtapriyaśca kaunteyo na ca jānāti devitum ,āhūtaścaiṣyati vyaktaṁ dīvyāvetyāhvayasva tam.
english: The son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira) is fond of gambling but does not know how to play it. If invited, he will certainly come. Therefore, invite him by saying, 'Let us play!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 38. The son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira) is fond of gambling but does not know how to play it. If invited, he will certainly come. Therefore, invite him by saying, 'Let us play!'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तः शकुनिना राजा दुर्योधनस्तदा ।धृतराष्ट्रमिदं वाक्यमपदान्तरमब्रवीत् ॥३९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktaḥ śakuninā rājā duryodhanastadā ,dhṛtarāṣṭramidaṁ vākyamapadāntaramabravīt.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "Thus addressed by Śakuni, King Duryodhana then spoke these words directly to Dhṛtarāṣṭra."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 39. Vaiśaṃpāyana said, "Thus addressed by Śakuni, King Duryodhana then spoke these words directly to Dhṛtarāṣṭra."

sanskrit: अयमुत्सहते राजञ्श्रियमाहर्तुमक्षवित् ।द्यूतेन पाण्डुपुत्रस्य तदनुज्ञातुमर्हसि ॥४०॥
transliteration: ayamutsahate rājañśriyamāhartumakṣavit ,dyūtena pāṇḍuputrasya tadanujñātumarhasi.
english: O King, this expert in dice is eager to acquire the wealth of Pāṇḍu's son through gambling. Therefore, you should permit this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 40. O King, this expert in dice is eager to acquire the wealth of Pāṇḍu's son through gambling. Therefore, you should permit this.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।क्षत्ता मन्त्री महाप्राज्ञः स्थितो यस्यास्मि शासने ।तेन संगम्य वेत्स्यामि कार्यस्यास्य विनिश्चयम् ॥४१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,kṣattā mantrī mahāprājñaḥ sthito yasyāsmi śāsane ,tena saṁgamya vetsyāmi kāryasyāsya viniścayam.
english: Dhritarashtra said: 'I will ascertain the final decision regarding this matter only after consulting with Vidura (kṣattā), the greatly wise minister, whose instruction I follow.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 41. Dhritarashtra said: 'I will ascertain the final decision regarding this matter only after consulting with Vidura (kṣattā), the greatly wise minister, whose instruction I follow.'

sanskrit: स हि धर्मं पुरस्कृत्य दीर्घदर्शी परं हितम् ।उभयोः पक्षयोर्युक्तं वक्ष्यत्यर्थविनिश्चयम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: sa hi dharmaṁ puraskṛtya dīrghadarśī paraṁ hitam ,ubhayoḥ pakṣayoryuktaṁ vakṣyatyarthaviniścayam.
english: For he (Vidura), being far-sighted and making natural law (dharma) paramount, will declare the supreme welfare (hitam) and a just decision (arthaviniścayam) that is appropriate for both parties.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 42. For he (Vidura), being far-sighted and making natural law (dharma) paramount, will declare the supreme welfare (hitam) and a just decision (arthaviniścayam) that is appropriate for both parties.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।निवर्तयिष्यति त्वासौ यदि क्षत्ता समेष्यति ।निवृत्ते त्वयि राजेन्द्र मरिष्येऽहमसंशयम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,nivartayiṣyati tvāsau yadi kṣattā sameṣyati ,nivṛtte tvayi rājendra mariṣye'hamasaṁśayam.
english: Duryodhana said: 'If that Vidura (kṣattā) arrives, he will turn you away (from this proposal). And if you, O king of kings (rājendra), are deterred, I will undoubtedly die.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 43. Duryodhana said: 'If that Vidura (kṣattā) arrives, he will turn you away (from this proposal). And if you, O king of kings (rājendra), are deterred, I will undoubtedly die.'

sanskrit: स मयि त्वं मृते राजन्विदुरेण सुखी भव ।भोक्ष्यसे पृथिवीं कृत्स्नां किं मया त्वं करिष्यसि ॥४४॥
transliteration: sa mayi tvaṁ mṛte rājanvidureṇa sukhī bhava ,bhokṣyase pṛthivīṁ kṛtsnāṁ kiṁ mayā tvaṁ kariṣyasi.
english: O King, when I am dead, may you be happy with Vidura. You will then enjoy the entire earth. What will you do with me?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 44. O King, when I am dead, may you be happy with Vidura. You will then enjoy the entire earth. What will you do with me?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।आर्तवाक्यं तु तत्तस्य प्रणयोक्तं निशम्य सः ।धृतराष्ट्रोऽब्रवीत्प्रेष्यान्दुर्योधनमते स्थितः ॥४५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ārtavākyaṁ tu tattasya praṇayoktaṁ niśamya saḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭro'bravītpreṣyānduryodhanamate sthitaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that distressed utterance of his, spoken with affection, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, being firmly aligned with Duryodhana's views, then spoke to his servants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 45. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard that distressed utterance of his, spoken with affection, Dhṛtarāṣṭra, being firmly aligned with Duryodhana's views, then spoke to his servants.

sanskrit: स्थूणासहस्रैर्बृहतीं शतद्वारां सभां मम ।मनोरमां दर्शनीयामाशु कुर्वन्तु शिल्पिनः ॥४६॥
transliteration: sthūṇāsahasrairbṛhatīṁ śatadvārāṁ sabhāṁ mama ,manoramāṁ darśanīyāmāśu kurvantu śilpinaḥ.
english: Let the artisans swiftly construct for me a grand, magnificent hall, adorned with a thousand pillars and a hundred gates, beautiful and delightful to behold.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 46. Let the artisans swiftly construct for me a grand, magnificent hall, adorned with a thousand pillars and a hundred gates, beautiful and delightful to behold.

sanskrit: ततः संस्तीर्य रत्नैस्तामक्षानावाप्य सर्वशः ।सुकृतां सुप्रवेशां च निवेदयत मे शनैः ॥४७॥
transliteration: tataḥ saṁstīrya ratnaistāmakṣānāvāpya sarvaśaḥ ,sukṛtāṁ supraveśāṁ ca nivedayata me śanaiḥ.
english: Then, after carpeting that hall completely with jewels and scattering dice everywhere, you should report to me, when it is meticulously completed and easily accessible.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 47. Then, after carpeting that hall completely with jewels and scattering dice everywhere, you should report to me, when it is meticulously completed and easily accessible.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य शान्त्यर्थमिति निश्चित्य भूमिपः ।धृतराष्ट्रो महाराज प्राहिणोद्विदुराय वै ॥४८॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya śāntyarthamiti niścitya bhūmipaḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭro mahārāja prāhiṇodvidurāya vai.
english: O great king, having thus decided for the appeasement of Duryodhana, King Dhritarashtra indeed sent [a message] to Vidura.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 48. O great king, having thus decided for the appeasement of Duryodhana, King Dhritarashtra indeed sent [a message] to Vidura.

sanskrit: अपृष्ट्वा विदुरं ह्यस्य नासीत्कश्चिद्विनिश्चयः ।द्यूतदोषांश्च जानन्स पुत्रस्नेहादकृष्यत ॥४९॥
transliteration: apṛṣṭvā viduraṁ hyasya nāsītkaścidviniścayaḥ ,dyūtadoṣāṁśca jānansa putrasnehādakṛṣyata.
english: Indeed, without consulting Vidura, there was no firm decision for him. And though he knew the faults of gambling, he was drawn [to it] by his affection for his son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 49. Indeed, without consulting Vidura, there was no firm decision for him. And though he knew the faults of gambling, he was drawn [to it] by his affection for his son.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा विदुरो धीमान्कलिद्वारमुपस्थितम् ।विनाशमुखमुत्पन्नं धृतराष्ट्रमुपाद्रवत् ॥५०॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā viduro dhīmānkalidvāramupasthitam ,vināśamukhamutpannaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭramupādravat.
english: Having heard that, the intelligent Vidura, realizing that the gateway to discord (kali) had appeared and destruction had manifested, rushed towards Dhritarashtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 50. Having heard that, the intelligent Vidura, realizing that the gateway to discord (kali) had appeared and destruction had manifested, rushed towards Dhritarashtra.

sanskrit: सोऽभिगम्य महात्मानं भ्राता भ्रातरमग्रजम् ।मूर्ध्ना प्रणम्य चरणाविदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥५१॥
transliteration: so'bhigamya mahātmānaṁ bhrātā bhrātaramagrajam ,mūrdhnā praṇamya caraṇāvidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: He, the brother, having approached his great-souled elder brother, and having bowed down to his feet with his head, spoke this word.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 51. He, the brother, having approached his great-souled elder brother, and having bowed down to his feet with his head, spoke this word.

sanskrit: नाभिनन्दामि ते राजन्व्यवसायमिमं प्रभो ।पुत्रैर्भेदो यथा न स्याद्द्यूतहेतोस्तथा कुरु ॥५२॥
transliteration: nābhinandāmi te rājanvyavasāyamimaṁ prabho ,putrairbhedo yathā na syāddyūtahetostathā kuru.
english: O King, O Lord, I do not approve of this undertaking of yours. Please act in such a way that there is no division among the sons on account of gambling.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 52. O King, O Lord, I do not approve of this undertaking of yours. Please act in such a way that there is no division among the sons on account of gambling.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।क्षत्तः पुत्रेषु पुत्रैर्मे कलहो न भविष्यति ।दिवि देवाः प्रसादं नः करिष्यन्ति न संशयः ॥५३॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,kṣattaḥ putreṣu putrairme kalaho na bhaviṣyati ,divi devāḥ prasādaṁ naḥ kariṣyanti na saṁśayaḥ.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "O Vidura, there will be no quarrel among my sons. The gods in heaven will surely bestow their favor upon us; there is no doubt about it."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 53. Dhritarashtra said: "O Vidura, there will be no quarrel among my sons. The gods in heaven will surely bestow their favor upon us; there is no doubt about it."

sanskrit: अशुभं वा शुभं वापि हितं वा यदि वाहितम् ।प्रवर्ततां सुहृद्द्यूतं दिष्टमेतन्न संशयः ॥५४॥
transliteration: aśubhaṁ vā śubhaṁ vāpi hitaṁ vā yadi vāhitam ,pravartatāṁ suhṛddyūtaṁ diṣṭametanna saṁśayaḥ.
english: Whether inauspicious or auspicious, beneficial or harmful, let this friendly game of dice proceed. This is destined; there is no doubt about it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 54. Whether inauspicious or auspicious, beneficial or harmful, let this friendly game of dice proceed. This is destined; there is no doubt about it.

sanskrit: मयि संनिहिते चैव भीष्मे च भरतर्षभे ।अनयो दैवविहितो न कथंचिद्भविष्यति ॥५५॥
transliteration: mayi saṁnihite caiva bhīṣme ca bharatarṣabhe ,anayo daivavihito na kathaṁcidbhaviṣyati.
english: While I am present, and indeed while Bhishma, the foremost among the Bharatas, is also present, no calamity ordained by fate will possibly occur.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 55. While I am present, and indeed while Bhishma, the foremost among the Bharatas, is also present, no calamity ordained by fate will possibly occur.

sanskrit: गच्छ त्वं रथमास्थाय हयैर्वातसमैर्जवे ।खाण्डवप्रस्थमद्यैव समानय युधिष्ठिरम् ॥५६॥
transliteration: gaccha tvaṁ rathamāsthāya hayairvātasamairjave ,khāṇḍavaprasthamadyaiva samānaya yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: You go, mounted on a chariot drawn by horses swift as the wind, and bring Yudhiṣṭhira to Khāṇḍavaprastha today itself.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 56. You go, mounted on a chariot drawn by horses swift as the wind, and bring Yudhiṣṭhira to Khāṇḍavaprastha today itself.

sanskrit: न वार्यो व्यवसायो मे विदुरैतद्ब्रवीमि ते ।दैवमेव परं मन्ये येनैतदुपपद्यते ॥५७॥
transliteration: na vāryo vyavasāyo me viduraitadbravīmi te ,daivameva paraṁ manye yenaitadupapadyate.
english: O Vidura, my resolve cannot be stopped; I tell you this. I consider destiny (daiva) to be supreme, for it is by destiny that this is coming to pass.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 57. O Vidura, my resolve cannot be stopped; I tell you this. I consider destiny (daiva) to be supreme, for it is by destiny that this is coming to pass.

sanskrit: इत्युक्तो विदुरो धीमान्नैतदस्तीति चिन्तयन् ।आपगेयं महाप्राज्ञमभ्यगच्छत्सुदुःखितः ॥५८॥
transliteration: ityukto viduro dhīmānnaitadastīti cintayan ,āpageyaṁ mahāprājñamabhyagacchatsuduḥkhitaḥ.
english: Having been told this, the wise Vidura, thinking, 'This is not right,' went to the greatly intelligent Bhīṣma (Āpageya), feeling extremely distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 45 Verse 58. Having been told this, the wise Vidura, thinking, 'This is not right,' went to the greatly intelligent Bhīṣma (Āpageya), feeling extremely distressed.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।कथं समभवद्द्यूतं भ्रातॄणां तन्महात्ययम् ।यत्र तद्व्यसनं प्राप्तं पाण्डवैर्मे पितामहैः ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,kathaṁ samabhavaddyūtaṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ tanmahātyayam ,yatra tadvyasanaṁ prāptaṁ pāṇḍavairme pitāmahaiḥ.
english: Janamejaya said: 'How did that dice game, which brought great destruction, arise among the brothers? And how did my grandfathers, the Pāṇḍavas, incur that misfortune in it?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 1. Janamejaya said: 'How did that dice game, which brought great destruction, arise among the brothers? And how did my grandfathers, the Pāṇḍavas, incur that misfortune in it?'

sanskrit: के च तत्र सभास्तारा राजानो ब्रह्मवित्तम ।के चैनमन्वमोदन्त के चैनं प्रत्यषेधयन् ॥२॥
transliteration: ke ca tatra sabhāstārā rājāno brahmavittama ,ke cainamanvamodanta ke cainaṁ pratyaṣedhayan.
english: O best knower of Brahman (brahman), who were the kings present in that assembly? And who among them approved of him, and who opposed him?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 2. O best knower of Brahman (brahman), who were the kings present in that assembly? And who among them approved of him, and who opposed him?

sanskrit: विस्तरेणैतदिच्छामि कथ्यमानं त्वया द्विज ।मूलं ह्येतद्विनाशस्य पृथिव्या द्विजसत्तम ॥३॥
transliteration: vistareṇaitadicchāmi kathyamānaṁ tvayā dvija ,mūlaṁ hyetadvināśasya pṛthivyā dvijasattama.
english: O brahmin (dvija), I wish for this to be narrated by you in detail, because this is indeed the root cause of the earth's (kingdom's) destruction, O best among brahmins (dvija).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 3. O brahmin (dvija), I wish for this to be narrated by you in detail, because this is indeed the root cause of the earth's (kingdom's) destruction, O best among brahmins (dvija).

sanskrit: सूत उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तदा राज्ञा व्यासशिष्यः प्रतापवान् ।आचचक्षे यथावृत्तं तत्सर्वं सर्ववेदवित् ॥४॥
transliteration: sūta uvāca ,evamuktastadā rājñā vyāsaśiṣyaḥ pratāpavān ,ācacakṣe yathāvṛttaṁ tatsarvaṁ sarvavedavit.
english: Sūta said: Thus addressed by the king, the glorious disciple of Vyāsa, who knew all the Vedas, then narrated everything just as it had happened.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 4. Sūta said: Thus addressed by the king, the glorious disciple of Vyāsa, who knew all the Vedas, then narrated everything just as it had happened.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।शृणु मे विस्तरेणेमां कथां भरतसत्तम ।भूय एव महाराज यदि ते श्रवणे मतिः ॥५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,śṛṇu me vistareṇemāṁ kathāṁ bharatasattama ,bhūya eva mahārāja yadi te śravaṇe matiḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: O best of the Bhāratas, listen to this story from me in detail, O great king, if you still have an inclination to hear.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 5. Vaiśampāyana said: O best of the Bhāratas, listen to this story from me in detail, O great king, if you still have an inclination to hear.

sanskrit: विदुरस्य मतं ज्ञात्वा धृतराष्ट्रोऽम्बिकासुतः ।दुर्योधनमिदं वाक्यमुवाच विजने पुनः ॥६॥
transliteration: vidurasya mataṁ jñātvā dhṛtarāṣṭro'mbikāsutaḥ ,duryodhanamidaṁ vākyamuvāca vijane punaḥ.
english: Understanding Vidura's advice, Dhritarashtra, the son of Ambika, spoke these words again to Duryodhana in a secluded place.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 6. Understanding Vidura's advice, Dhritarashtra, the son of Ambika, spoke these words again to Duryodhana in a secluded place.

sanskrit: अलं द्यूतेन गान्धारे विदुरो न प्रशंसति ।न ह्यसौ सुमहाबुद्धिरहितं नो वदिष्यति ॥७॥
transliteration: alaṁ dyūtena gāndhāre viduro na praśaṁsati ,na hyasau sumahābuddhirahitaṁ no vadiṣyati.
english: Enough with this gambling, O son of Gandhari! Vidura does not approve of it. Indeed, he possesses a very great intellect and would certainly not advise anything harmful to us.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 7. Enough with this gambling, O son of Gandhari! Vidura does not approve of it. Indeed, he possesses a very great intellect and would certainly not advise anything harmful to us.

sanskrit: हितं हि परमं मन्ये विदुरो यत्प्रभाषते ।क्रियतां पुत्र तत्सर्वमेतन्मन्ये हितं तव ॥८॥
transliteration: hitaṁ hi paramaṁ manye viduro yatprabhāṣate ,kriyatāṁ putra tatsarvametanmanye hitaṁ tava.
english: Indeed, I consider what Vidura says to be of supreme benefit. O my son, let all of that be done, for I consider this to be beneficial for you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 8. Indeed, I consider what Vidura says to be of supreme benefit. O my son, let all of that be done, for I consider this to be beneficial for you.

sanskrit: देवर्षिर्वासवगुरुर्देवराजाय धीमते ।यत्प्राह शास्त्रं भगवान्बृहस्पतिरुदारधीः ॥९॥
transliteration: devarṣirvāsavagururdevarājāya dhīmate ,yatprāha śāstraṁ bhagavānbṛhaspatirudāradhīḥ.
english: (This is) what the divine sage, the venerable Brihaspati, the preceptor of Indra and a man of noble intellect, instructed as a treatise (śāstra) to the wise king of the gods.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 9. (This is) what the divine sage, the venerable Brihaspati, the preceptor of Indra and a man of noble intellect, instructed as a treatise (śāstra) to the wise king of the gods.

sanskrit: तद्वेद विदुरः सर्वं सरहस्यं महाकविः ।स्थितश्च वचने तस्य सदाहमपि पुत्रक ॥१०॥
transliteration: tadveda viduraḥ sarvaṁ sarahasyaṁ mahākaviḥ ,sthitaśca vacane tasya sadāhamapi putraka.
english: O son, the great sage Vidura knows all of that, including its secrets. And I, too, always stand by his word.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 10. O son, the great sage Vidura knows all of that, including its secrets. And I, too, always stand by his word.

sanskrit: विदुरो वापि मेधावी कुरूणां प्रवरो मतः ।उद्धवो वा महाबुद्धिर्वृष्णीनामर्चितो नृप ॥११॥
transliteration: viduro vāpi medhāvī kurūṇāṁ pravaro mataḥ ,uddhavo vā mahābuddhirvṛṣṇīnāmarcito nṛpa.
english: O king, Vidura is considered the most excellent and wise among the Kurus; or Uddhava, who is greatly intelligent, is revered among the Vṛṣṇis.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 11. O king, Vidura is considered the most excellent and wise among the Kurus; or Uddhava, who is greatly intelligent, is revered among the Vṛṣṇis.

sanskrit: द्यूतेन तदलं पुत्र द्यूते भेदो हि दृश्यते ।भेदे विनाशो राज्यस्य तत्पुत्र परिवर्जय ॥१२॥
transliteration: dyūtena tadalaṁ putra dyūte bhedo hi dṛśyate ,bhede vināśo rājyasya tatputra parivarjaya.
english: O son, enough of this gambling! Indeed, discord is seen to arise from gambling. From discord comes the destruction of the kingdom, O son; therefore, avoid it!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 12. O son, enough of this gambling! Indeed, discord is seen to arise from gambling. From discord comes the destruction of the kingdom, O son; therefore, avoid it!

sanskrit: पित्रा मात्रा च पुत्रस्य यद्वै कार्यं परं स्मृतम् ।प्राप्तस्त्वमसि तत्तात पितृपैतामहं पदम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: pitrā mātrā ca putrasya yadvai kāryaṁ paraṁ smṛtam ,prāptastvamasi tattāta pitṛpaitāmahaṁ padam.
english: O dear one (tāta), you have indeed attained that supreme ancestral position, which is considered the highest duty (kārya) for a son by his father and mother.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 13. O dear one (tāta), you have indeed attained that supreme ancestral position, which is considered the highest duty (kārya) for a son by his father and mother.

sanskrit: अधीतवान्कृती शास्त्रे लालितः सततं गृहे ।भ्रातृज्येष्ठः स्थितो राज्ये विन्दसे किं न शोभनम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: adhītavānkṛtī śāstre lālitaḥ satataṁ gṛhe ,bhrātṛjyeṣṭhaḥ sthito rājye vindase kiṁ na śobhanam.
english: You are learned and skilled in the scriptures, constantly cherished at home. As the eldest brother, you are established in the kingdom. What excellent thing is it that you do not find?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 14. You are learned and skilled in the scriptures, constantly cherished at home. As the eldest brother, you are established in the kingdom. What excellent thing is it that you do not find?

sanskrit: पृथग्जनैरलभ्यं यद्भोजनाच्छादनं परम् ।तत्प्राप्तोऽसि महाबाहो कस्माच्छोचसि पुत्रक ॥१५॥
transliteration: pṛthagjanairalabhyaṁ yadbhojanācchādanaṁ param ,tatprāpto'si mahābāho kasmācchocasi putraka.
english: O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O son, you have obtained that excellent food and clothing which is unattainable by ordinary people. Why do you grieve?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 15. O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), O son, you have obtained that excellent food and clothing which is unattainable by ordinary people. Why do you grieve?

sanskrit: स्फीतं राष्ट्रं महाबाहो पितृपैतामहं महत् ।नित्यमाज्ञापयन्भासि दिवि देवेश्वरो यथा ॥१६॥
transliteration: sphītaṁ rāṣṭraṁ mahābāho pitṛpaitāmahaṁ mahat ,nityamājñāpayanbhāsi divi deveśvaro yathā.
english: O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), you continually rule this great, prosperous, and ancestral kingdom, and you shine like the lord of the gods (deveśvara) in heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 16. O mighty-armed one (mahābāho), you continually rule this great, prosperous, and ancestral kingdom, and you shine like the lord of the gods (deveśvara) in heaven.

sanskrit: तस्य ते विदितप्रज्ञ शोकमूलमिदं कथम् ।समुत्थितं दुःखतरं तन्मे शंसितुमर्हसि ॥१७॥
transliteration: tasya te viditaprajña śokamūlamidaṁ katham ,samutthitaṁ duḥkhataraṁ tanme śaṁsitumarhasi.
english: O wise one, you ought to explain to me how this source of even greater sorrow has arisen in you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 17. O wise one, you ought to explain to me how this source of even greater sorrow has arisen in you.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।अश्नाम्याच्छादयामीति प्रपश्यन्पापपूरुषः ।नामर्षं कुरुते यस्तु पुरुषः सोऽधमः स्मृतः ॥१८॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,aśnāmyācchādayāmīti prapaśyanpāpapūruṣaḥ ,nāmarṣaṁ kurute yastu puruṣaḥ so'dhamaḥ smṛtaḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: "The sinful man who, merely perceiving that he eats and clothes himself, feels no indignation (amarṣa), that man is considered to be the lowest."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 18. Duryodhana said: "The sinful man who, merely perceiving that he eats and clothes himself, feels no indignation (amarṣa), that man is considered to be the lowest."

sanskrit: न मां प्रीणाति राजेन्द्र लक्ष्मीः साधारणा विभो ।ज्वलितामिव कौन्तेये श्रियं दृष्ट्वा च विव्यथे ॥१९॥
transliteration: na māṁ prīṇāti rājendra lakṣmīḥ sādhāraṇā vibho ,jvalitāmiva kaunteye śriyaṁ dṛṣṭvā ca vivyathe.
english: O King of kings, O mighty one, mere common prosperity (lakṣmī) does not satisfy me. Rather, seeing the blazing splendor (śrī) of the son of Kunti, I am truly distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 19. O King of kings, O mighty one, mere common prosperity (lakṣmī) does not satisfy me. Rather, seeing the blazing splendor (śrī) of the son of Kunti, I am truly distressed.

sanskrit: सर्वां हि पृथिवीं दृष्ट्वा युधिष्ठिरवशानुगाम् ।स्थिरोऽस्मि योऽहं जीवामि दुःखादेतद्ब्रवीमि ते ॥२०॥
transliteration: sarvāṁ hi pṛthivīṁ dṛṣṭvā yudhiṣṭhiravaśānugām ,sthiro'smi yo'haṁ jīvāmi duḥkhādetadbravīmi te.
english: Indeed, having seen the entire earth subjugated to Yudhiṣṭhira, I, who am still alive, remain. It is out of sorrow that I tell you this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 20. Indeed, having seen the entire earth subjugated to Yudhiṣṭhira, I, who am still alive, remain. It is out of sorrow that I tell you this.

sanskrit: आवर्जिता इवाभान्ति निघ्नाश्चैत्रकिकौकुराः ।कारस्करा लोहजङ्घा युधिष्ठिरनिवेशने ॥२१॥
transliteration: āvarjitā ivābhānti nighnāścaitrakikaukurāḥ ,kāraskarā lohajaṅghā yudhiṣṭhiraniveśane.
english: The Caitrakas and Kukuras, along with the Karaskaras and Lohajaṅghas, appear as if bowed down and subdued in Yudhiṣṭhira's capital.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 21. The Caitrakas and Kukuras, along with the Karaskaras and Lohajaṅghas, appear as if bowed down and subdued in Yudhiṣṭhira's capital.

sanskrit: हिमवत्सागरानूपाः सर्वरत्नाकरास्तथा ।अन्त्याः सर्वे पर्युदस्ता युधिष्ठिरनिवेशने ॥२२॥
transliteration: himavatsāgarānūpāḥ sarvaratnākarāstathā ,antyāḥ sarve paryudastā yudhiṣṭhiraniveśane.
english: The Himalayas (Himavat), oceans, and marshlands, all of which are sources of jewels, as well as all the border regions, were completely subjugated for Yudhishthira's abode.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 22. The Himalayas (Himavat), oceans, and marshlands, all of which are sources of jewels, as well as all the border regions, were completely subjugated for Yudhishthira's abode.

sanskrit: ज्येष्ठोऽयमिति मां मत्वा श्रेष्ठश्चेति विशां पते ।युधिष्ठिरेण सत्कृत्य युक्तो रत्नपरिग्रहे ॥२३॥
transliteration: jyeṣṭho'yamiti māṁ matvā śreṣṭhaśceti viśāṁ pate ,yudhiṣṭhireṇa satkṛtya yukto ratnaparigrahe.
english: Thinking 'this one is the eldest and also the best,' O lord of the people (viśām pate), Yudhishthira honored me and appointed me to accept the jewels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 23. Thinking 'this one is the eldest and also the best,' O lord of the people (viśām pate), Yudhishthira honored me and appointed me to accept the jewels.

sanskrit: उपस्थितानां रत्नानां श्रेष्ठानामर्घहारिणाम् ।नादृश्यत परः प्रान्तो नापरस्तत्र भारत ॥२४॥
transliteration: upasthitānāṁ ratnānāṁ śreṣṭhānāmarghahāriṇām ,nādṛśyata paraḥ prānto nāparastatra bhārata.
english: Of the countless excellent and valuable jewels that were brought forth, neither their beginning nor their end could be seen there, O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 24. Of the countless excellent and valuable jewels that were brought forth, neither their beginning nor their end could be seen there, O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata).

sanskrit: न मे हस्तः समभवद्वसु तत्प्रतिगृह्णतः ।प्रातिष्ठन्त मयि श्रान्ते गृह्य दूराहृतं वसु ॥२५॥
transliteration: na me hastaḥ samabhavadvasu tatpratigṛhṇataḥ ,prātiṣṭhanta mayi śrānte gṛhya dūrāhṛtaṁ vasu.
english: My hand was unable to keep receiving that wealth. When I was exhausted, the wealth that had been brought from afar was collected and remained there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 25. My hand was unable to keep receiving that wealth. When I was exhausted, the wealth that had been brought from afar was collected and remained there.

sanskrit: कृतां बिन्दुसरोरत्नैर्मयेन स्फाटिकच्छदाम् ।अपश्यं नलिनीं पूर्णामुदकस्येव भारत ॥२६॥
transliteration: kṛtāṁ bindusaroratnairmayena sphāṭikacchadām ,apaśyaṁ nalinīṁ pūrṇāmudakasyeva bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, I saw a lotus pond, which Maya had constructed with jewels from Lake Bindu. Its surface appeared crystal-like, full of water.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 26. O Bhārata, I saw a lotus pond, which Maya had constructed with jewels from Lake Bindu. Its surface appeared crystal-like, full of water.

sanskrit: वस्त्रमुत्कर्षति मयि प्राहसत्स वृकोदरः ।शत्रोरृद्धिविशेषेण विमूढं रत्नवर्जितम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: vastramutkarṣati mayi prāhasatsa vṛkodaraḥ ,śatrorṛddhiviśeṣeṇa vimūḍhaṁ ratnavarjitam.
english: While I was pulling up my garment, that Bhīma (vṛkodara) laughed aloud. Bewildered by the exceptional prosperity of the enemy (Pāṇḍavas), I felt utterly humiliated.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 27. While I was pulling up my garment, that Bhīma (vṛkodara) laughed aloud. Bewildered by the exceptional prosperity of the enemy (Pāṇḍavas), I felt utterly humiliated.

sanskrit: तत्र स्म यदि शक्तः स्यां पातयेयं वृकोदरम् ।सपत्नेनावहासो हि स मां दहति भारत ॥२८॥
transliteration: tatra sma yadi śaktaḥ syāṁ pātayeyaṁ vṛkodaram ,sapatnenāvahāso hi sa māṁ dahati bhārata.
english: At that moment, if I had been capable, I would have struck down Bhīma (vṛkodara). Indeed, that ridicule from my rival consumes me, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 28. At that moment, if I had been capable, I would have struck down Bhīma (vṛkodara). Indeed, that ridicule from my rival consumes me, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: पुनश्च तादृशीमेव वापीं जलजशालिनीम् ।मत्वा शिलासमां तोये पतितोऽस्मि नराधिप ॥२९॥
transliteration: punaśca tādṛśīmeva vāpīṁ jalajaśālinīm ,matvā śilāsamāṁ toye patito'smi narādhipa.
english: And again, O king (narādhipa), I fell into the water, having mistaken another similar pond, adorned with lotuses, for a solid stone surface.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 29. And again, O king (narādhipa), I fell into the water, having mistaken another similar pond, adorned with lotuses, for a solid stone surface.

sanskrit: तत्र मां प्राहसत्कृष्णः पार्थेन सह सस्वनम् ।द्रौपदी च सह स्त्रीभिर्व्यथयन्ती मनो मम ॥३०॥
transliteration: tatra māṁ prāhasatkṛṣṇaḥ pārthena saha sasvanam ,draupadī ca saha strībhirvyathayantī mano mama.
english: There, Krishna, along with Arjuna, laughed loudly at me. And Draupadi, accompanied by other women, distressed my mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 30. There, Krishna, along with Arjuna, laughed loudly at me. And Draupadi, accompanied by other women, distressed my mind.

sanskrit: क्लिन्नवस्त्रस्य च जले किंकरा राजचोदिताः ।ददुर्वासांसि मेऽन्यानि तच्च दुःखतरं मम ॥३१॥
transliteration: klinnavastrasya ca jale kiṁkarā rājacoditāḥ ,dadurvāsāṁsi me'nyāni tacca duḥkhataraṁ mama.
english: And when my garments were wet in the water, the servants, at the king's command, gave me other clothes. That was even more distressing to me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 31. And when my garments were wet in the water, the servants, at the king's command, gave me other clothes. That was even more distressing to me.

sanskrit: प्रलम्भं च शृणुष्वान्यं गदतो मे नराधिप ।अद्वारेण विनिर्गच्छन्द्वारसंस्थानरूपिणा ।अभिहत्य शिलां भूयो ललाटेनास्मि विक्षतः ॥३२॥
transliteration: pralambhaṁ ca śṛṇuṣvānyaṁ gadato me narādhipa ,advāreṇa vinirgacchandvārasaṁsthānarūpiṇā ,abhihatya śilāṁ bhūyo lalāṭenāsmi vikṣataḥ.
english: O king of men (narādhipa), listen to another humiliation from me. As I was exiting through what seemed to be a door but was not, I struck a stone again with my forehead and was wounded.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 32. O king of men (narādhipa), listen to another humiliation from me. As I was exiting through what seemed to be a door but was not, I struck a stone again with my forehead and was wounded.

sanskrit: तत्र मां यमजौ दूरादालोक्य ललितौ किल ।बाहुभिः परिगृह्णीतां शोचन्तौ सहितावुभौ ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatra māṁ yamajau dūrādālokya lalitau kila ,bāhubhiḥ parigṛhṇītāṁ śocantau sahitāvubhau.
english: Indeed, there, the handsome twins (yamajau), having seen me from afar, both together embraced me with their arms while lamenting.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 33. Indeed, there, the handsome twins (yamajau), having seen me from afar, both together embraced me with their arms while lamenting.

sanskrit: उवाच सहदेवस्तु तत्र मां विस्मयन्निव ।इदं द्वारमितो गच्छ राजन्निति पुनः पुनः ॥३४॥
transliteration: uvāca sahadevastu tatra māṁ vismayanniva ,idaṁ dvāramito gaccha rājanniti punaḥ punaḥ.
english: Sahadeva, as if astounding me, repeatedly said there, "This is the door; go this way, O King."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 34. Sahadeva, as if astounding me, repeatedly said there, "This is the door; go this way, O King."

sanskrit: नामधेयानि रत्नानां पुरस्तान्न श्रुतानि मे ।यानि दृष्टानि मे तस्यां मनस्तपति तच्च मे ॥३५॥
transliteration: nāmadheyāni ratnānāṁ purastānna śrutāni me ,yāni dṛṣṭāni me tasyāṁ manastapati tacca me.
english: The names of the gems that I saw there had never been heard by me before. That also distresses my mind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 46 Verse 35. The names of the gems that I saw there had never been heard by me before. That also distresses my mind.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।यन्मया पाण्डवानां तु दृष्टं तच्छृणु भारत ।आहृतं भूमिपालैर्हि वसु मुख्यं ततस्ततः ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,yanmayā pāṇḍavānāṁ tu dṛṣṭaṁ tacchṛṇu bhārata ,āhṛtaṁ bhūmipālairhi vasu mukhyaṁ tatastataḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: "O Bhārata, listen to what I saw concerning the Pāṇḍavas. Indeed, considerable wealth was brought from various places by the kings."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: "O Bhārata, listen to what I saw concerning the Pāṇḍavas. Indeed, considerable wealth was brought from various places by the kings."

sanskrit: न विन्दे दृढमात्मानं दृष्ट्वाहं तदरेर्धनम् ।फलतो भूमितो वापि प्रतिपद्यस्व भारत ॥२॥
transliteration: na vinde dṛḍhamātmānaṁ dṛṣṭvāhaṁ tadarerdhanam ,phalato bhūmito vāpi pratipadyasva bhārata.
english: After seeing that wealth of my enemy, I cannot find my resolute self (ātman). O Bhārata, you should certainly acquire wealth, whether from produce or from land.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 2. After seeing that wealth of my enemy, I cannot find my resolute self (ātman). O Bhārata, you should certainly acquire wealth, whether from produce or from land.

sanskrit: ऐडांश्चैलान्वार्षदंशाञ्जातरूपपरिष्कृतान् ।प्रावाराजिनमुख्यांश्च काम्बोजः प्रददौ वसु ॥३॥
transliteration: aiḍāṁścailānvārṣadaṁśāñjātarūpapariṣkṛtān ,prāvārājinamukhyāṁśca kāmbojaḥ pradadau vasu.
english: The Kamboja king presented wealth, consisting of woolen blankets from the wild ram, vārṣadaṃśa sheepskins adorned with gold, and other excellent blankets and hides.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 3. The Kamboja king presented wealth, consisting of woolen blankets from the wild ram, vārṣadaṃśa sheepskins adorned with gold, and other excellent blankets and hides.

sanskrit: अश्वांस्तित्तिरिकल्माषांस्त्रिशतं शुकनासिकान् ।उष्ट्रवामीस्त्रिशतं च पुष्टाः पीलुशमीङ्गुदैः ॥४॥
transliteration: aśvāṁstittirikalmāṣāṁstriśataṁ śukanāsikān ,uṣṭravāmīstriśataṁ ca puṣṭāḥ pīluśamīṅgudaiḥ.
english: He also presented three hundred horses, speckled like partridges and with parrot-like noses, as well as three hundred stout female camels, well-nourished with pilu, śamī, and iṅguda fodder.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 4. He also presented three hundred horses, speckled like partridges and with parrot-like noses, as well as three hundred stout female camels, well-nourished with pilu, śamī, and iṅguda fodder.

sanskrit: गोवासना ब्राह्मणाश्च दासमीयाश्च सर्वशः ।प्रीत्यर्थं ते महाभागा धर्मराज्ञो महात्मनः ।त्रिखर्वं बलिमादाय द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥५॥
transliteration: govāsanā brāhmaṇāśca dāsamīyāśca sarvaśaḥ ,prītyarthaṁ te mahābhāgā dharmarājño mahātmanaḥ ,trikharvaṁ balimādāya dvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: Brahmins from Govāsana and all the Dāsamīyas, those highly esteemed individuals, stand restrained at the gate, having brought three kharvas of tribute to please the great-souled king of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 5. Brahmins from Govāsana and all the Dāsamīyas, those highly esteemed individuals, stand restrained at the gate, having brought three kharvas of tribute to please the great-souled king of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: कमण्डलूनुपादाय जातरूपमयाञ्शुभान् ।एवं बलिं प्रदायाथ प्रवेशं लेभिरे ततः ॥६॥
transliteration: kamaṇḍalūnupādāya jātarūpamayāñśubhān ,evaṁ baliṁ pradāyātha praveśaṁ lebhire tataḥ.
english: Having taken up their beautiful water-pots made of gold, and then having offered their tribute in this manner, they obtained entry from there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 6. Having taken up their beautiful water-pots made of gold, and then having offered their tribute in this manner, they obtained entry from there.

sanskrit: शतं दासीसहस्राणां कार्पासिकनिवासिनाम् ।श्यामास्तन्व्यो दीर्घकेश्यो हेमाभरणभूषिताः ।शूद्रा विप्रोत्तमार्हाणि राङ्कवान्यजिनानि च ॥७॥
transliteration: śataṁ dāsīsahasrāṇāṁ kārpāsikanivāsinām ,śyāmāstanvyo dīrghakeśyo hemābharaṇabhūṣitāḥ ,śūdrā viprottamārhāṇi rāṅkavānyajināni ca.
english: A hundred thousand dark-complexioned, slender female servants with long hair, wearing cotton garments and adorned with gold ornaments (were presented). Additionally, female Shudras were presented along with raṅku deer skins and other valuable items suitable for the most excellent Brahmins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 7. A hundred thousand dark-complexioned, slender female servants with long hair, wearing cotton garments and adorned with gold ornaments (were presented). Additionally, female Shudras were presented along with raṅku deer skins and other valuable items suitable for the most excellent Brahmins.

sanskrit: बलिं च कृत्स्नमादाय भरुकच्छनिवासिनः ।उपनिन्युर्महाराज हयान्गान्धारदेशजान् ॥८॥
transliteration: baliṁ ca kṛtsnamādāya bharukacchanivāsinaḥ ,upaninyurmahārāja hayāngāndhāradeśajān.
english: And having collected all the tribute, O great king, the residents of Bharukaccha presented horses originating from the region of Gandhara.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 8. And having collected all the tribute, O great king, the residents of Bharukaccha presented horses originating from the region of Gandhara.

sanskrit: इन्द्रकृष्टैर्वर्तयन्ति धान्यैर्नदीमुखैश्च ये ।समुद्रनिष्कुटे जाताः परिसिन्धु च मानवाः ॥९॥
transliteration: indrakṛṣṭairvartayanti dhānyairnadīmukhaiśca ye ,samudraniṣkuṭe jātāḥ parisindhu ca mānavāḥ.
english: And those people who subsist on rain-fed grains and grains from river mouths, who are born in the coastal groves and along the Sindhu river...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 9. And those people who subsist on rain-fed grains and grains from river mouths, who are born in the coastal groves and along the Sindhu river...

sanskrit: ते वैरामाः पारदाश्च वङ्गाश्च कितवैः सह ।विविधं बलिमादाय रत्नानि विविधानि च ॥१०॥
transliteration: te vairāmāḥ pāradāśca vaṅgāśca kitavaiḥ saha ,vividhaṁ balimādāya ratnāni vividhāni ca.
english: Those Vairamas, Paradas, and Vangas, along with the Kitavas, collected various tributes and diverse jewels (to present).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 10. Those Vairamas, Paradas, and Vangas, along with the Kitavas, collected various tributes and diverse jewels (to present).

sanskrit: अजाविकं गोहिरण्यं खरोष्ट्रं फलजं मधु ।कम्बलान्विविधांश्चैव द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥११॥
transliteration: ajāvikaṁ gohiraṇyaṁ kharoṣṭraṁ phalajaṁ madhu ,kambalānvividhāṁścaiva dvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: Goats and sheep, cows, gold, donkeys, camels, fruit-born honey, and various kinds of blankets stand detained at the gate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 11. Goats and sheep, cows, gold, donkeys, camels, fruit-born honey, and various kinds of blankets stand detained at the gate.

sanskrit: प्राग्ज्योतिषाधिपः शूरो म्लेच्छानामधिपो बली ।यवनैः सहितो राजा भगदत्तो महारथः ॥१२॥
transliteration: prāgjyotiṣādhipaḥ śūro mlecchānāmadhipo balī ,yavanaiḥ sahito rājā bhagadatto mahārathaḥ.
english: The brave King Bhagadatta, ruler of Pragjyotisha, a powerful chief of the Mlecchas (foreigners) and a great charioteer, was accompanied by the Yavanas (foreigners).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 12. The brave King Bhagadatta, ruler of Pragjyotisha, a powerful chief of the Mlecchas (foreigners) and a great charioteer, was accompanied by the Yavanas (foreigners).

sanskrit: आजानेयान्हयाञ्शीघ्रानादायानिलरंहसः ।बलिं च कृत्स्नमादाय द्वारि तिष्ठति वारितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: ājāneyānhayāñśīghrānādāyānilaraṁhasaḥ ,baliṁ ca kṛtsnamādāya dvāri tiṣṭhati vāritaḥ.
english: Having brought swift, noble-bred horses that possessed the speed of wind, and having taken the complete tribute, he stood prevented at the gate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 13. Having brought swift, noble-bred horses that possessed the speed of wind, and having taken the complete tribute, he stood prevented at the gate.

sanskrit: अश्मसारमयं भाण्डं शुद्धदन्तत्सरूनसीन् ।प्राग्ज्योतिषोऽथ तद्दत्त्वा भगदत्तोऽव्रजत्तदा ॥१४॥
transliteration: aśmasāramayaṁ bhāṇḍaṁ śuddhadantatsarūnasīn ,prāgjyotiṣo'tha taddattvā bhagadatto'vrajattadā.
english: Then, Bhagadatta, the ruler of Pragjyotisha, having presented steel articles and swords with pure ivory handles, departed at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 14. Then, Bhagadatta, the ruler of Pragjyotisha, having presented steel articles and swords with pure ivory handles, departed at that time.

sanskrit: द्व्यक्षांस्त्र्यक्षाँल्ललाटाक्षान्नानादिग्भ्यः समागतान् ।औष्णीषाननिवासांश्च बाहुकान्पुरुषादकान् ॥१५॥
transliteration: dvyakṣāṁstryakṣāँllalāṭākṣānnānādigbhyaḥ samāgatān ,auṣṇīṣānanivāsāṁśca bāhukānpuruṣādakān.
english: I saw two-eyed, three-eyed, and forehead-eyed beings who had arrived from various directions; some wore turbans, some were unclothed, some had long arms, and some were man-eaters.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 15. I saw two-eyed, three-eyed, and forehead-eyed beings who had arrived from various directions; some wore turbans, some were unclothed, some had long arms, and some were man-eaters.

sanskrit: एकपादांश्च तत्राहमपश्यं द्वारि वारितान् ।बल्यर्थं ददतस्तस्मै हिरण्यं रजतं बहु ॥१६॥
transliteration: ekapādāṁśca tatrāhamapaśyaṁ dvāri vāritān ,balyarthaṁ dadatastasmai hiraṇyaṁ rajataṁ bahu.
english: There, at the gate, I saw one-footed beings who were being stopped, offering gold and much silver to him for the sake of an offering (bali).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 16. There, at the gate, I saw one-footed beings who were being stopped, offering gold and much silver to him for the sake of an offering (bali).

sanskrit: इन्द्रगोपकवर्णाभाञ्शुकवर्णान्मनोजवान् ।तथैवेन्द्रायुधनिभान्संध्याभ्रसदृशानपि ॥१७॥
transliteration: indragopakavarṇābhāñśukavarṇānmanojavān ,tathaivendrāyudhanibhānsaṁdhyābhrasadṛśānapi.
english: They had the shining color of fireflies (indragopaka), the color of parrots, and were swift as thought. Likewise, they resembled rainbows (indrāyudha) and were like twilight clouds, too.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 17. They had the shining color of fireflies (indragopaka), the color of parrots, and were swift as thought. Likewise, they resembled rainbows (indrāyudha) and were like twilight clouds, too.

sanskrit: अनेकवर्णानारण्यान्गृहीत्वाश्वान्मनोजवान् ।जातरूपमनर्घ्यं च ददुस्तस्यैकपादकाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: anekavarṇānāraṇyāngṛhītvāśvānmanojavān ,jātarūpamanarghyaṁ ca dadustasyaikapādakāḥ.
english: Having taken wild horses of many colors and swift as thought, as well as priceless gold (jātarūpa), the one-footed ones offered them to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 18. Having taken wild horses of many colors and swift as thought, as well as priceless gold (jātarūpa), the one-footed ones offered them to him.

sanskrit: चीनान्हूणाञ्शकानोड्रान्पर्वतान्तरवासिनः ।वार्ष्णेयान्हारहूणांश्च कृष्णान्हैमवतांस्तथा ॥१९॥
transliteration: cīnānhūṇāñśakānoḍrānparvatāntaravāsinaḥ ,vārṣṇeyānhārahūṇāṁśca kṛṣṇānhaimavatāṁstathā.
english: The Chinese, Huns, Shakas, Odras, mountain-dwellers, Vārṣṇeyas, Harahūṇas, Kṛṣṇas, and similarly, the Himalayan people (were among them).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 19. The Chinese, Huns, Shakas, Odras, mountain-dwellers, Vārṣṇeyas, Harahūṇas, Kṛṣṇas, and similarly, the Himalayan people (were among them).

sanskrit: न पारयाम्यभिगतान्विविधान्द्वारि वारितान् ।बल्यर्थं ददतस्तस्य नानारूपाननेकशः ॥२०॥
transliteration: na pārayāmyabhigatānvividhāndvāri vāritān ,balyarthaṁ dadatastasya nānārūpānanekaśaḥ.
english: I am unable to manage the various groups who have arrived and are being held at the gate, offering him numerous tributes of many different kinds in manifold ways.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 20. I am unable to manage the various groups who have arrived and are being held at the gate, offering him numerous tributes of many different kinds in manifold ways.

sanskrit: कृष्णग्रीवान्महाकायान्रासभाञ्शतपातिनः ।आहार्षुर्दशसाहस्रान्विनीतान्दिक्षु विश्रुतान् ॥२१॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇagrīvānmahākāyānrāsabhāñśatapātinaḥ ,āhārṣurdaśasāhasrānvinītāndikṣu viśrutān.
english: They brought ten thousand donkeys  -  black-necked, large-bodied, swift, well-trained, and renowned in all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 21. They brought ten thousand donkeys  -  black-necked, large-bodied, swift, well-trained, and renowned in all directions.

sanskrit: प्रमाणरागस्पर्शाढ्यं बाह्लीचीनसमुद्भवम् ।और्णं च राङ्कवं चैव कीटजं पट्टजं तथा ॥२२॥
transliteration: pramāṇarāgasparśāḍhyaṁ bāhlīcīnasamudbhavam ,aurṇaṁ ca rāṅkavaṁ caiva kīṭajaṁ paṭṭajaṁ tathā.
english: They also brought woolen, rāṅkava (made from deer hair), kīṭaja (worm-born silk), and similarly paṭṭaja (made from silk strips) cloth, all rich in measure, color, and texture, and originating from Bāhlīka and Cīna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 22. They also brought woolen, rāṅkava (made from deer hair), kīṭaja (worm-born silk), and similarly paṭṭaja (made from silk strips) cloth, all rich in measure, color, and texture, and originating from Bāhlīka and Cīna.

sanskrit: कुट्टीकृतं तथैवान्यत्कमलाभं सहस्रशः ।श्लक्ष्णं वस्त्रमकार्पासमाविकं मृदु चाजिनम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: kuṭṭīkṛtaṁ tathaivānyatkamalābhaṁ sahasraśaḥ ,ślakṣṇaṁ vastramakārpāsamāvikaṁ mṛdu cājinam.
english: Similarly, thousands of shredded, lotus-hued items were present, along with smooth, non-cotton, woolen cloth and soft animal hides.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 23. Similarly, thousands of shredded, lotus-hued items were present, along with smooth, non-cotton, woolen cloth and soft animal hides.

sanskrit: निशितांश्चैव दीर्घासीनृष्टिशक्तिपरश्वधान् ।अपरान्तसमुद्भूतांस्तथैव परशूञ्शितान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: niśitāṁścaiva dīrghāsīnṛṣṭiśaktiparaśvadhān ,aparāntasamudbhūtāṁstathaiva paraśūñśitān.
english: Sharpened, long swords, lances, javelins, and battle-axes; and similarly, sharpened axes originating from Aparānta.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 24. Sharpened, long swords, lances, javelins, and battle-axes; and similarly, sharpened axes originating from Aparānta.

sanskrit: रसान्गन्धांश्च विविधान्रत्नानि च सहस्रशः ।बलिं च कृत्स्नमादाय द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥२५॥
transliteration: rasāngandhāṁśca vividhānratnāni ca sahasraśaḥ ,baliṁ ca kṛtsnamādāya dvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: Bringing various essences and fragrances, thousands of jewels, and the complete tribute, they wait, restrained, at the gate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 25. Bringing various essences and fragrances, thousands of jewels, and the complete tribute, they wait, restrained, at the gate.

sanskrit: शकास्तुखाराः कङ्काश्च रोमशाः शृङ्गिणो नराः ।महागमान्दूरगमान्गणितानर्बुदं हयान् ॥२६॥
transliteration: śakāstukhārāḥ kaṅkāśca romaśāḥ śṛṅgiṇo narāḥ ,mahāgamāndūragamāngaṇitānarbudaṁ hayān.
english: The Śakas, Tukhāras, Kaṅkas, Romaśas, and Śṛṅgiṇas (horned men); and horses, capable of great speeds and long journeys, numbering a myriad (ten million).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 26. The Śakas, Tukhāras, Kaṅkas, Romaśas, and Śṛṅgiṇas (horned men); and horses, capable of great speeds and long journeys, numbering a myriad (ten million).

sanskrit: कोटिशश्चैव बहुशः सुवर्णं पद्मसंमितम् ।बलिमादाय विविधं द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥२७॥
transliteration: koṭiśaścaiva bahuśaḥ suvarṇaṁ padmasaṁmitam ,balimādāya vividhaṁ dvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: Millions upon millions, bearing abundant gold (in quantities equal to a padma, a vast number) and diverse tribute (bali), stand prevented at the gate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 27. Millions upon millions, bearing abundant gold (in quantities equal to a padma, a vast number) and diverse tribute (bali), stand prevented at the gate.

sanskrit: आसनानि महार्हाणि यानानि शयनानि च ।मणिकाञ्चनचित्राणि गजदन्तमयानि च ॥२८॥
transliteration: āsanāni mahārhāṇi yānāni śayanāni ca ,maṇikāñcanacitrāṇi gajadantamayāni ca.
english: Very valuable seats, vehicles, and beds, adorned with gems and gold, and also made of ivory.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 28. Very valuable seats, vehicles, and beds, adorned with gems and gold, and also made of ivory.

sanskrit: रथांश्च विविधाकाराञ्जातरूपपरिष्कृतान् ।हयैर्विनीतैः संपन्नान्वैयाघ्रपरिवारणान् ॥२९॥
transliteration: rathāṁśca vividhākārāñjātarūpapariṣkṛtān ,hayairvinītaiḥ saṁpannānvaiyāghraparivāraṇān.
english: And chariots of diverse forms, embellished with gold, endowed with well-trained horses, and furnished with tiger-skin coverings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 29. And chariots of diverse forms, embellished with gold, endowed with well-trained horses, and furnished with tiger-skin coverings.

sanskrit: विचित्रांश्च परिस्तोमान्रत्नानि च सहस्रशः ।नाराचानर्धनाराचाञ्शस्त्राणि विविधानि च ॥३०॥
transliteration: vicitrāṁśca paristomānratnāni ca sahasraśaḥ ,nārācānardhanārācāñśastrāṇi vividhāni ca.
english: And diverse coverings, and thousands of gems, iron arrows (nārācān), half-iron arrows, and various other weapons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 30. And diverse coverings, and thousands of gems, iron arrows (nārācān), half-iron arrows, and various other weapons.

sanskrit: एतद्दत्त्वा महद्द्रव्यं पूर्वदेशाधिपो नृपः ।प्रविष्टो यज्ञसदनं पाण्डवस्य महात्मनः ॥३१॥
transliteration: etaddattvā mahaddravyaṁ pūrvadeśādhipo nṛpaḥ ,praviṣṭo yajñasadanaṁ pāṇḍavasya mahātmanaḥ.
english: The king, ruler of the eastern country, having bestowed this great wealth, entered the sacrificial hall (yajñasadanam) of the great-souled Pāṇḍava.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 47 Verse 31. The king, ruler of the eastern country, having bestowed this great wealth, entered the sacrificial hall (yajñasadanam) of the great-souled Pāṇḍava.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।दायं तु तस्मै विविधं शृणु मे गदतोऽनघ ।यज्ञार्थं राजभिर्दत्तं महान्तं धनसंचयम् ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,dāyaṁ tu tasmai vividhaṁ śṛṇu me gadato'nagha ,yajñārthaṁ rājabhirdattaṁ mahāntaṁ dhanasaṁcayam.
english: Duryodhana said: O sinless one (anagha), listen to me as I relate the various gifts, a great accumulation of wealth, given by kings to him for the purpose of the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: O sinless one (anagha), listen to me as I relate the various gifts, a great accumulation of wealth, given by kings to him for the purpose of the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: मेरुमन्दरयोर्मध्ये शैलोदामभितो नदीम् ।ये ते कीचकवेणूनां छायां रम्यामुपासते ॥२॥
transliteration: merumandarayormadhye śailodāmabhito nadīm ,ye te kīcakaveṇūnāṁ chāyāṁ ramyāmupāsate.
english: Those people who reside between Mount Meru and Mount Mandara, all along the river Śailodā, and who inhabit the delightful shade of the Kīcaka bamboos.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 2. Those people who reside between Mount Meru and Mount Mandara, all along the river Śailodā, and who inhabit the delightful shade of the Kīcaka bamboos.

sanskrit: खशा एकाशनाज्योहाः प्रदरा दीर्घवेणवः ।पशुपाश्च कुणिन्दाश्च तङ्गणाः परतङ्गणाः ॥३॥
transliteration: khaśā ekāśanājyohāḥ pradarā dīrghaveṇavaḥ ,paśupāśca kuṇindāśca taṅgaṇāḥ parataṅgaṇāḥ.
english: The Khaśas, the Ekāśanas (those who eat alone or live in isolation), the Ajyohas (those without ploughs), the Pradaras, and the Dīrghaveṇavas (those with long bamboos); and also the cowherds (paśupāḥ), the Kuṇindas, the Taṅgaṇas, and the Para-Taṅgaṇas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 3. The Khaśas, the Ekāśanas (those who eat alone or live in isolation), the Ajyohas (those without ploughs), the Pradaras, and the Dīrghaveṇavas (those with long bamboos); and also the cowherds (paśupāḥ), the Kuṇindas, the Taṅgaṇas, and the Para-Taṅgaṇas.

sanskrit: ते वै पिपीलिकं नाम वरदत्तं पिपीलिकैः ।जातरूपं द्रोणमेयमहार्षुः पुञ्जशो नृपाः ॥४॥
transliteration: te vai pipīlikaṁ nāma varadattaṁ pipīlikaiḥ ,jātarūpaṁ droṇameyamahārṣuḥ puñjaśo nṛpāḥ.
english: Indeed, those kings brought heaps of gold, known as Pipīlika gold, which had been bestowed by the Pipīlikas and was measurable by the droṇa (a unit of measure).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 4. Indeed, those kings brought heaps of gold, known as Pipīlika gold, which had been bestowed by the Pipīlikas and was measurable by the droṇa (a unit of measure).

sanskrit: कृष्णाँल्ललामांश्चमराञ्शुक्लांश्चान्याञ्शशिप्रभान् ।हिमवत्पुष्पजं चैव स्वादु क्षौद्रं तथा बहु ॥५॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇāँllalāmāṁścamarāñśuklāṁścānyāñśaśiprabhān ,himavatpuṣpajaṁ caiva svādu kṣaudraṁ tathā bahu.
english: (They also brought) black and distinctively marked chamara yaks, and other white ones shining like the moon. And much sweet honey, born of Himalayan flowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 5. (They also brought) black and distinctively marked chamara yaks, and other white ones shining like the moon. And much sweet honey, born of Himalayan flowers.

sanskrit: उत्तरेभ्यः कुरुभ्यश्चाप्यपोढं माल्यमम्बुभिः ।उत्तरादपि कैलासादोषधीः सुमहाबलाः ॥६॥
transliteration: uttarebhyaḥ kurubhyaścāpyapoḍhaṁ mālyamambubhiḥ ,uttarādapi kailāsādoṣadhīḥ sumahābalāḥ.
english: And also from the Northern Kurus, garlands of flowers carried by the rivers (as tribute). And also from Northern Kailāsa, very potent herbs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 6. And also from the Northern Kurus, garlands of flowers carried by the rivers (as tribute). And also from Northern Kailāsa, very potent herbs.

sanskrit: पार्वतीया बलिं चान्यमाहृत्य प्रणताः स्थिताः ।अजातशत्रोर्नृपतेर्द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥७॥
transliteration: pārvatīyā baliṁ cānyamāhṛtya praṇatāḥ sthitāḥ ,ajātaśatrornṛpaterdvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: And the mountain dwellers, having brought other tribute, stand humbly restrained at the gate of King Ajātaśatru (ajātaśatru).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 7. And the mountain dwellers, having brought other tribute, stand humbly restrained at the gate of King Ajātaśatru (ajātaśatru).

sanskrit: ये परार्धे हिमवतः सूर्योदयगिरौ नृपाः ।वारिषेणसमुद्रान्ते लोहित्यमभितश्च ये ।फलमूलाशना ये च किराताश्चर्मवाससः ॥८॥
transliteration: ye parārdhe himavataḥ sūryodayagirau nṛpāḥ ,vāriṣeṇasamudrānte lohityamabhitaśca ye ,phalamūlāśanā ye ca kirātāścarmavāsasaḥ.
english: Those kings who reside on the far side of the Himalayas, on the eastern mountain (where the sun rises), near the Varishena Sea, and around the Lohitya (river); as well as those Kirāta people who subsist on fruits and roots and wear animal skins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 8. Those kings who reside on the far side of the Himalayas, on the eastern mountain (where the sun rises), near the Varishena Sea, and around the Lohitya (river); as well as those Kirāta people who subsist on fruits and roots and wear animal skins.

sanskrit: चन्दनागुरुकाष्ठानां भारान्कालीयकस्य च ।चर्मरत्नसुवर्णानां गन्धानां चैव राशयः ॥९॥
transliteration: candanāgurukāṣṭhānāṁ bhārānkālīyakasya ca ,carmaratnasuvarṇānāṁ gandhānāṁ caiva rāśayaḥ.
english: Loads of sandalwood, aguru (aloe-wood), and kālīyaka (a type of fragrant wood); also, heaps of skins, jewels, gold, and various fragrant substances.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 9. Loads of sandalwood, aguru (aloe-wood), and kālīyaka (a type of fragrant wood); also, heaps of skins, jewels, gold, and various fragrant substances.

sanskrit: कैरातिकानामयुतं दासीनां च विशां पते ।आहृत्य रमणीयार्थान्दूरजान्मृगपक्षिणः ॥१०॥
