transliteration: kairātikānāmayutaṁ dāsīnāṁ ca viśāṁ pate ,āhṛtya ramaṇīyārthāndūrajānmṛgapakṣiṇaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, having brought ten thousand Kirāta women as female servants, and also desirable animals and birds from distant lands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 10. O lord of the people, having brought ten thousand Kirāta women as female servants, and also desirable animals and birds from distant lands.

sanskrit: निचितं पर्वतेभ्यश्च हिरण्यं भूरिवर्चसम् ।बलिं च कृत्स्नमादाय द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥११॥
transliteration: nicitaṁ parvatebhyaśca hiraṇyaṁ bhūrivarcasam ,baliṁ ca kṛtsnamādāya dvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: Having collected shining gold from the mountains, and having also gathered all the tribute, they stand restrained at the gate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 11. Having collected shining gold from the mountains, and having also gathered all the tribute, they stand restrained at the gate.

sanskrit: कायव्या दरदा दार्वाः शूरा वैयमकास्तथा ।औदुम्बरा दुर्विभागाः पारदा बाह्लिकैः सह ॥१२॥
transliteration: kāyavyā daradā dārvāḥ śūrā vaiyamakāstathā ,audumbarā durvibhāgāḥ pāradā bāhlikaiḥ saha.
english: The Kāyavyas, Daradas, Dārvas, Śūras, and Vaiyamakas, along with the Audumbaras, Durvibhāgas, Pāradas, and Bāhlikas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 12. The Kāyavyas, Daradas, Dārvas, Śūras, and Vaiyamakas, along with the Audumbaras, Durvibhāgas, Pāradas, and Bāhlikas.

sanskrit: काश्मीराः कुन्दमानाश्च पौरका हंसकायनाः ।शिबित्रिगर्तयौधेया राजन्या मद्रकेकयाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: kāśmīrāḥ kundamānāśca paurakā haṁsakāyanāḥ ,śibitrigartayaudheyā rājanyā madrakekayāḥ.
english: The Kashmiris, Kundamānas, Paurakas, and Haṃsakāyanas, along with the Śibis, Trigartas, Yaudheyas, Rājanyas, Madras, and Kekayas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 13. The Kashmiris, Kundamānas, Paurakas, and Haṃsakāyanas, along with the Śibis, Trigartas, Yaudheyas, Rājanyas, Madras, and Kekayas.

sanskrit: अम्बष्ठाः कौकुरास्तार्क्ष्या वस्त्रपाः पह्लवैः सह ।वसातयः समौलेयाः सह क्षुद्रकमालवैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: ambaṣṭhāḥ kaukurāstārkṣyā vastrapāḥ pahlavaiḥ saha ,vasātayaḥ samauleyāḥ saha kṣudrakamālavaiḥ.
english: The Ambaṣṭhas, Kaukūras, Tārkṣyas, and Vastrapas, along with the Pahlavas, and the Vasātayas and Samaulīyas, together with the Kṣudrakas and Mālavās.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 14. The Ambaṣṭhas, Kaukūras, Tārkṣyas, and Vastrapas, along with the Pahlavas, and the Vasātayas and Samaulīyas, together with the Kṣudrakas and Mālavās.

sanskrit: शौण्डिकाः कुक्कुराश्चैव शकाश्चैव विशां पते ।अङ्गा वङ्गाश्च पुण्ड्राश्च शानवत्या गयास्तथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: śauṇḍikāḥ kukkurāścaiva śakāścaiva viśāṁ pate ,aṅgā vaṅgāśca puṇḍrāśca śānavatyā gayāstathā.
english: O lord of the people, the Śauṇḍikas, Kukkūras, and Śakas; and the Aṅgas, Vaṅgas, Puṇḍras, Śānavatyas, and Gayas also.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 15. O lord of the people, the Śauṇḍikas, Kukkūras, and Śakas; and the Aṅgas, Vaṅgas, Puṇḍras, Śānavatyas, and Gayas also.

sanskrit: सुजातयः श्रेणिमन्तः श्रेयांसः शस्त्रपाणयः ।आहार्षुः क्षत्रिया वित्तं शतशोऽजातशत्रवे ॥१६॥
transliteration: sujātayaḥ śreṇimantaḥ śreyāṁsaḥ śastrapāṇayaḥ ,āhārṣuḥ kṣatriyā vittaṁ śataśo'jātaśatrave.
english: The well-born, prominent, and excellent warriors, holding weapons in their hands, brought hundreds of riches to Ajātaśatru.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 16. The well-born, prominent, and excellent warriors, holding weapons in their hands, brought hundreds of riches to Ajātaśatru.

sanskrit: वङ्गाः कलिङ्गपतयस्ताम्रलिप्ताः सपुण्ड्रकाः ।दुकूलं कौशिकं चैव पत्रोर्णं प्रावरानपि ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaṅgāḥ kaliṅgapatayastāmraliptāḥ sapuṇḍrakāḥ ,dukūlaṁ kauśikaṁ caiva patrorṇaṁ prāvarānapi.
english: The people of Vanga, the lords of Kalinga, those from Tāmralipta, and also those from Puṇḍra, brought fine fabrics, silk (kauśika), 'leaf-wool' (patrorṇa), and outer garments.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 17. The people of Vanga, the lords of Kalinga, those from Tāmralipta, and also those from Puṇḍra, brought fine fabrics, silk (kauśika), 'leaf-wool' (patrorṇa), and outer garments.

sanskrit: तत्र स्म द्वारपालैस्ते प्रोच्यन्ते राजशासनात् ।कृतकाराः सुबलयस्ततो द्वारमवाप्स्यथ ॥१८॥
transliteration: tatra sma dvārapālaiste procyante rājaśāsanāt ,kṛtakārāḥ subalayastato dvāramavāpsyatha.
english: There, the gatekeepers, by the king's command, would tell them: "You who have paid your tributes and made excellent offerings may then reach the gate."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 18. There, the gatekeepers, by the king's command, would tell them: "You who have paid your tributes and made excellent offerings may then reach the gate."

sanskrit: ईषादन्तान्हेमकक्षान्पद्मवर्णान्कुथावृतान् ।शैलाभान्नित्यमत्तांश्च अभितः काम्यकं सरः ॥१९॥
transliteration: īṣādantānhemakakṣānpadmavarṇānkuthāvṛtān ,śailābhānnityamattāṁśca abhitaḥ kāmyakaṁ saraḥ.
english: Huge-tusked elephants, adorned with golden girths, lotus-colored, covered with rich housings, mountain-like in appearance, and always in rut, were all around Kāmyaka Lake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 19. Huge-tusked elephants, adorned with golden girths, lotus-colored, covered with rich housings, mountain-like in appearance, and always in rut, were all around Kāmyaka Lake.

sanskrit: दत्त्वैकैको दशशतान्कुञ्जरान्कवचावृतान् ।क्षमावतः कुलीनांश्च द्वारेण प्राविशंस्ततः ॥२०॥
transliteration: dattvaikaiko daśaśatānkuñjarānkavacāvṛtān ,kṣamāvataḥ kulīnāṁśca dvāreṇa prāviśaṁstataḥ.
english: Then, each of them, having presented a thousand armored elephants that were patient and of noble lineage, entered through the gate.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 20. Then, each of them, having presented a thousand armored elephants that were patient and of noble lineage, entered through the gate.

sanskrit: एते चान्ये च बहवो गणा दिग्भ्यः समागताः ।अन्यैश्चोपाहृतान्यत्र रत्नानीह महात्मभिः ॥२१॥
transliteration: ete cānye ca bahavo gaṇā digbhyaḥ samāgatāḥ ,anyaiścopāhṛtānyatra ratnānīha mahātmabhiḥ.
english: These and many other hosts arrived from various directions. Here, jewels and treasures were also offered by other great-souled (mahātman) individuals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 21. These and many other hosts arrived from various directions. Here, jewels and treasures were also offered by other great-souled (mahātman) individuals.

sanskrit: राजा चित्ररथो नाम गन्धर्वो वासवानुगः ।शतानि चत्वार्यददद्धयानां वातरंहसाम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: rājā citraratho nāma gandharvo vāsavānugaḥ ,śatāni catvāryadadaddhayānāṁ vātaraṁhasām.
english: King Citraratha, a Gandharva and a follower of Vasava (Indra), presented four hundred horses that were swift as the wind.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 22. King Citraratha, a Gandharva and a follower of Vasava (Indra), presented four hundred horses that were swift as the wind.

sanskrit: तुम्बुरुस्तु प्रमुदितो गन्धर्वो वाजिनां शतम् ।आम्रपत्रसवर्णानामददद्धेममालिनाम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: tumburustu pramudito gandharvo vājināṁ śatam ,āmrapatrasavarṇānāmadadaddhemamālinām.
english: But Tumburu, the delighted Gandharva, presented a hundred horses that were the color of mango leaves and adorned with golden garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 23. But Tumburu, the delighted Gandharva, presented a hundred horses that were the color of mango leaves and adorned with golden garlands.

sanskrit: कृती तु राजा कौरव्य शूकराणां विशां पते ।अददद्गजरत्नानां शतानि सुबहून्यपि ॥२४॥
transliteration: kṛtī tu rājā kauravya śūkarāṇāṁ viśāṁ pate ,adadadgajaratnānāṁ śatāni subahūnyapi.
english: O scion of Kuru, O lord of the people, the accomplished king of the Śūkaras indeed also gave very many hundreds of splendid elephants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 24. O scion of Kuru, O lord of the people, the accomplished king of the Śūkaras indeed also gave very many hundreds of splendid elephants.

sanskrit: विराटेन तु मत्स्येन बल्यर्थं हेममालिनाम् ।कुञ्जराणां सहस्रे द्वे मत्तानां समुपाहृते ॥२५॥
transliteration: virāṭena tu matsyena balyarthaṁ hemamālinām ,kuñjarāṇāṁ sahasre dve mattānāṁ samupāhṛte.
english: Indeed, by Virāṭa, the king of the Matsyas, two thousand intoxicated elephants adorned with golden garlands were offered as tribute.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 25. Indeed, by Virāṭa, the king of the Matsyas, two thousand intoxicated elephants adorned with golden garlands were offered as tribute.

sanskrit: पांशुराष्ट्राद्वसुदानो राजा षड्विंशतिं गजान् ।अश्वानां च सहस्रे द्वे राजन्काञ्चनमालिनाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: pāṁśurāṣṭrādvasudāno rājā ṣaḍviṁśatiṁ gajān ,aśvānāṁ ca sahasre dve rājankāñcanamālinām.
english: O King, from Pāṃśurāṣṭra, King Vasudāna [brought] twenty-six elephants and two thousand horses adorned with golden garlands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 26. O King, from Pāṃśurāṣṭra, King Vasudāna [brought] twenty-six elephants and two thousand horses adorned with golden garlands.

sanskrit: जवसत्त्वोपपन्नानां वयःस्थानां नराधिप ।बलिं च कृत्स्नमादाय पाण्डवेभ्यो न्यवेदयत् ॥२७॥
transliteration: javasattvopapannānāṁ vayaḥsthānāṁ narādhipa ,baliṁ ca kṛtsnamādāya pāṇḍavebhyo nyavedayat.
english: O lord of men, having accepted the entire tribute - consisting of [animals] endowed with speed and strength, and in their prime - he then offered it to the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 27. O lord of men, having accepted the entire tribute - consisting of [animals] endowed with speed and strength, and in their prime - he then offered it to the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: यज्ञसेनेन दासीनां सहस्राणि चतुर्दश ।दासानामयुतं चैव सदाराणां विशां पते ॥२८॥
transliteration: yajñasenena dāsīnāṁ sahasrāṇi caturdaśa ,dāsānāmayutaṁ caiva sadārāṇāṁ viśāṁ pate.
english: O protector of the people, by Drupada (Yajñasena), fourteen thousand maidservants and ten thousand male servants along with their wives were offered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 28. O protector of the people, by Drupada (Yajñasena), fourteen thousand maidservants and ten thousand male servants along with their wives were offered.

sanskrit: गजयुक्ता महाराज रथाः षड्विंशतिस्तथा ।राज्यं च कृत्स्नं पार्थेभ्यो यज्ञार्थं वै निवेदितम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: gajayuktā mahārāja rathāḥ ṣaḍviṁśatistathā ,rājyaṁ ca kṛtsnaṁ pārthebhyo yajñārthaṁ vai niveditam.
english: O great king (mahārāja), twenty-six chariots drawn by elephants, and indeed the entire kingdom, were offered to the Pāṇḍavas for the purpose of the Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 29. O great king (mahārāja), twenty-six chariots drawn by elephants, and indeed the entire kingdom, were offered to the Pāṇḍavas for the purpose of the Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: समुद्रसारं वैडूर्यं मुक्ताः शङ्खांस्तथैव च ।शतशश्च कुथांस्तत्र सिंहलाः समुपाहरन् ॥३०॥
transliteration: samudrasāraṁ vaiḍūryaṁ muktāḥ śaṅkhāṁstathaiva ca ,śataśaśca kuthāṁstatra siṁhalāḥ samupāharan.
english: There, the Siṃhalas (people of Sri Lanka) offered hundreds of emeralds (samudrasāra), cat's eye gems, pearls, conch shells, and likewise many blankets.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 30. There, the Siṃhalas (people of Sri Lanka) offered hundreds of emeralds (samudrasāra), cat's eye gems, pearls, conch shells, and likewise many blankets.

sanskrit: संवृता मणिचीरैस्तु श्यामास्ताम्रान्तलोचनाः ।तान्गृहीत्वा नरास्तत्र द्वारि तिष्ठन्ति वारिताः ॥३१॥
transliteration: saṁvṛtā maṇicīraistu śyāmāstāmrāntalocanāḥ ,tāngṛhītvā narāstatra dvāri tiṣṭhanti vāritāḥ.
english: However, there at the gate, men who are dark-skinned, with reddish-cornered eyes, and adorned with jewel-studded garments, stand restrained, having brought those (gifts).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 31. However, there at the gate, men who are dark-skinned, with reddish-cornered eyes, and adorned with jewel-studded garments, stand restrained, having brought those (gifts).

sanskrit: प्रीत्यर्थं ब्राह्मणाश्चैव क्षत्रियाश्च विनिर्जिताः ।उपाजह्रुर्विशश्चैव शूद्राः शुश्रूषवोऽपि च ।प्रीत्या च बहुमानाच्च अभ्यगच्छन्युधिष्ठिरम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: prītyarthaṁ brāhmaṇāścaiva kṣatriyāśca vinirjitāḥ ,upājahrurviśaścaiva śūdrāḥ śuśrūṣavo'pi ca ,prītyā ca bahumānācca abhyagacchanyudhiṣṭhiram.
english: For the sake of goodwill, Brahmins and Kshatriyas, even those who had been conquered, brought gifts. Vaishyas and even obedient Shudras also came. With affection and great respect, they approached Yudhishthira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 32. For the sake of goodwill, Brahmins and Kshatriyas, even those who had been conquered, brought gifts. Vaishyas and even obedient Shudras also came. With affection and great respect, they approached Yudhishthira.

sanskrit: सर्वे म्लेच्छाः सर्ववर्णा आदिमध्यान्तजास्तथा ।नानादेशसमुत्थैश्च नानाजातिभिरागतैः ।पर्यस्त इव लोकोऽयं युधिष्ठिरनिवेशने ॥३३॥
transliteration: sarve mlecchāḥ sarvavarṇā ādimadhyāntajāstathā ,nānādeśasamutthaiśca nānājātibhirāgataiḥ ,paryasta iva loko'yaṁ yudhiṣṭhiraniveśane.
english: All foreign peoples (mlecchāḥ) of all social classes (varṇāḥ), including those born at the beginning, middle, and end of the social order, and those who came from various countries and different ethnic groups, were present. It was as if this entire world had been gathered in Yudhishthira's dwelling.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 33. All foreign peoples (mlecchāḥ) of all social classes (varṇāḥ), including those born at the beginning, middle, and end of the social order, and those who came from various countries and different ethnic groups, were present. It was as if this entire world had been gathered in Yudhishthira's dwelling.

sanskrit: उच्चावचानुपग्राहान्राजभिः प्रहितान्बहून् ।शत्रूणां पश्यतो दुःखान्मुमूर्षा मेऽद्य जायते ॥३४॥
transliteration: uccāvacānupagrāhānrājabhiḥ prahitānbahūn ,śatrūṇāṁ paśyato duḥkhānmumūrṣā me'dya jāyate.
english: As I see these numerous and diverse tributes sent by kings, a desire to die (mumūrṣā) arises in me today, born of the distress of observing our enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 34. As I see these numerous and diverse tributes sent by kings, a desire to die (mumūrṣā) arises in me today, born of the distress of observing our enemies.

sanskrit: भृत्यास्तु ये पाण्डवानां तांस्ते वक्ष्यामि भारत ।येषामामं च पक्वं च संविधत्ते युधिष्ठिरः ॥३५॥
transliteration: bhṛtyāstu ye pāṇḍavānāṁ tāṁste vakṣyāmi bhārata ,yeṣāmāmaṁ ca pakvaṁ ca saṁvidhatte yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: O Bhārata, I shall tell you about those servants of the Pāṇḍavas for whom Yudhishthira arranges both raw and cooked food.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 35. O Bhārata, I shall tell you about those servants of the Pāṇḍavas for whom Yudhishthira arranges both raw and cooked food.

sanskrit: अयुतं त्रीणि पद्मानि गजारोहाः ससादिनः ।रथानामर्बुदं चापि पादाता बहवस्तथा ॥३६॥
transliteration: ayutaṁ trīṇi padmāni gajārohāḥ sasādinaḥ ,rathānāmarbudaṁ cāpi pādātā bahavastathā.
english: There were ten thousand and three billion elephant riders along with horsemen, and also one hundred million chariots, and similarly many foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 36. There were ten thousand and three billion elephant riders along with horsemen, and also one hundred million chariots, and similarly many foot soldiers.

sanskrit: प्रमीयमाणमारब्धं पच्यमानं तथैव च ।विसृज्यमानं चान्यत्र पुण्याहस्वन एव च ॥३७॥
transliteration: pramīyamāṇamārabdhaṁ pacyamānaṁ tathaiva ca ,visṛjyamānaṁ cānyatra puṇyāhasvana eva ca.
english: Things were being measured out, undertaken, and similarly being prepared. And elsewhere, things were being distributed, and indeed, there was the sound of auspicious pronouncements (puṇyāhasvana).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 37. Things were being measured out, undertaken, and similarly being prepared. And elsewhere, things were being distributed, and indeed, there was the sound of auspicious pronouncements (puṇyāhasvana).

sanskrit: नाभुक्तवन्तं नाहृष्टं नासुभिक्षं कथंचन ।अपश्यं सर्ववर्णानां युधिष्ठिरनिवेशने ॥३८॥
transliteration: nābhuktavantaṁ nāhṛṣṭaṁ nāsubhikṣaṁ kathaṁcana ,apaśyaṁ sarvavarṇānāṁ yudhiṣṭhiraniveśane.
english: In Yudhishthira's encampment, I never saw anyone from any social class (varṇa) who was unfed, unhappy, or in want.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 38. In Yudhishthira's encampment, I never saw anyone from any social class (varṇa) who was unfed, unhappy, or in want.

sanskrit: अष्टाशीतिसहस्राणि स्नातका गृहमेधिनः ।त्रिंशद्दासीक एकैको यान्बिभर्ति युधिष्ठिरः ।सुप्रीताः परितुष्टाश्च तेऽप्याशंसन्त्यरिक्षयम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: aṣṭāśītisahasrāṇi snātakā gṛhamedhinaḥ ,triṁśaddāsīka ekaiko yānbibharti yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,suprītāḥ parituṣṭāśca te'pyāśaṁsantyarikṣayam.
english: Yudhishthira supported eighty-eight thousand "snātaka" householders, each of whom had thirty maidservants. These householders, being very pleased and greatly satisfied, also prayed for the destruction of (Yudhishthira's) enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 39. Yudhishthira supported eighty-eight thousand "snātaka" householders, each of whom had thirty maidservants. These householders, being very pleased and greatly satisfied, also prayed for the destruction of (Yudhishthira's) enemies.

sanskrit: दशान्यानि सहस्राणि यतीनामूर्ध्वरेतसाम् ।भुञ्जते रुक्मपात्रीषु युधिष्ठिरनिवेशने ॥४०॥
transliteration: daśānyāni sahasrāṇi yatīnāmūrdhvaretasām ,bhuñjate rukmapātrīṣu yudhiṣṭhiraniveśane.
english: In Yudhishthira's abode, ten thousands of other ascetics (yati) who observe celibacy are fed in golden vessels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 40. In Yudhishthira's abode, ten thousands of other ascetics (yati) who observe celibacy are fed in golden vessels.

sanskrit: भुक्ताभुक्तं कृताकृतं सर्वमाकुब्जवामनम् ।अभुञ्जाना याज्ञसेनी प्रत्यवैक्षद्विशां पते ॥४१॥
transliteration: bhuktābhuktaṁ kṛtākṛtaṁ sarvamākubjavāmanam ,abhuñjānā yājñasenī pratyavaikṣadviśāṁ pate.
english: O Lord of men, Draupadi (Yajñasenī), without eating, observed everything, both the eaten and the uneaten, the done and the undone, right down to the hunchbacks and dwarfs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 41. O Lord of men, Draupadi (Yajñasenī), without eating, observed everything, both the eaten and the uneaten, the done and the undone, right down to the hunchbacks and dwarfs.

sanskrit: द्वौ करं न प्रयच्छेतां कुन्तीपुत्राय भारत ।वैवाहिकेन पाञ्चालाः सख्येनान्धकवृष्णयः ॥४२॥
transliteration: dvau karaṁ na prayacchetāṁ kuntīputrāya bhārata ,vaivāhikena pāñcālāḥ sakhyenāndhakavṛṣṇayaḥ.
english: O descendant of Bhārata, two would not offer aid to the son of Kuntī: the Pāñcālas because of the marriage alliance, and the Andhaka-Vṛṣṇis because of friendship.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 48 Verse 42. O descendant of Bhārata, two would not offer aid to the son of Kuntī: the Pāñcālas because of the marriage alliance, and the Andhaka-Vṛṣṇis because of friendship.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।आर्यास्तु ये वै राजानः सत्यसंधा महाव्रताः ।पर्याप्तविद्या वक्तारो वेदान्तावभृथाप्लुताः ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,āryāstu ye vai rājānaḥ satyasaṁdhā mahāvratāḥ ,paryāptavidyā vaktāro vedāntāvabhṛthāplutāḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: But those kings who are noble, indeed truthful, observing great vows, possessing ample knowledge, eloquent speakers, and purified by the concluding ritual bath (avabhṛtha) of Vedāntic study  -
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: But those kings who are noble, indeed truthful, observing great vows, possessing ample knowledge, eloquent speakers, and purified by the concluding ritual bath (avabhṛtha) of Vedāntic study  -

sanskrit: धृतिमन्तो ह्रीनिषेधा धर्मात्मानो यशस्विनः ।मूर्धाभिषिक्तास्ते चैनं राजानः पर्युपासते ॥२॥
transliteration: dhṛtimanto hrīniṣedhā dharmātmāno yaśasvinaḥ ,mūrdhābhiṣiktāste cainaṁ rājānaḥ paryupāsate.
english: Resolute, modest, possessing a righteous nature (dharmātmānaḥ), and renowned, those consecrated kings attend upon him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 2. Resolute, modest, possessing a righteous nature (dharmātmānaḥ), and renowned, those consecrated kings attend upon him.

sanskrit: दक्षिणार्थं समानीता राजभिः कांस्यदोहनाः ।आरण्या बहुसाहस्रा अपश्यं तत्र तत्र गाः ॥३॥
transliteration: dakṣiṇārthaṁ samānītā rājabhiḥ kāṁsyadohanāḥ ,āraṇyā bahusāhasrā apaśyaṁ tatra tatra gāḥ.
english: I saw thousands of wild cows (gāḥ), whose milk was collected in bronze vessels, brought by kings here and there for the purpose of sacrificial fees (dakṣiṇā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 3. I saw thousands of wild cows (gāḥ), whose milk was collected in bronze vessels, brought by kings here and there for the purpose of sacrificial fees (dakṣiṇā).

sanskrit: आजह्रुस्तत्र सत्कृत्य स्वयमुद्यम्य भारत ।अभिषेकार्थमव्यग्रा भाण्डमुच्चावचं नृपाः ॥४॥
transliteration: ājahrustatra satkṛtya svayamudyamya bhārata ,abhiṣekārthamavyagrā bhāṇḍamuccāvacaṁ nṛpāḥ.
english: O Bhārata, the kings, acting diligently and with great respect, personally exerted themselves there to bring various vessels for the consecration (abhiṣeka).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 4. O Bhārata, the kings, acting diligently and with great respect, personally exerted themselves there to bring various vessels for the consecration (abhiṣeka).

sanskrit: बाह्लीको रथमाहार्षीज्जाम्बूनदपरिष्कृतम् ।सुदक्षिणस्तं युयुजे श्वेतैः काम्बोजजैर्हयैः ॥५॥
transliteration: bāhlīko rathamāhārṣījjāmbūnadapariṣkṛtam ,sudakṣiṇastaṁ yuyuje śvetaiḥ kāmbojajairhayaiḥ.
english: King Bāhlīka brought forth a chariot, which was adorned with Jāmbūnada gold. Then, Sudakṣiṇa yoked it with white horses (hayaiḥ) from Kāmboja.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 5. King Bāhlīka brought forth a chariot, which was adorned with Jāmbūnada gold. Then, Sudakṣiṇa yoked it with white horses (hayaiḥ) from Kāmboja.

sanskrit: सुनीथोऽप्रतिमं तस्य अनुकर्षं महायशाः ।ध्वजं चेदिपतिः क्षिप्रमहार्षीत्स्वयमुद्यतम् ॥६॥
transliteration: sunītho'pratimaṁ tasya anukarṣaṁ mahāyaśāḥ ,dhvajaṁ cedipatiḥ kṣipramahārṣītsvayamudyatam.
english: The greatly renowned Sunitha presented an unrivaled streamer. The lord of Cedi swiftly seized his own raised banner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 6. The greatly renowned Sunitha presented an unrivaled streamer. The lord of Cedi swiftly seized his own raised banner.

sanskrit: दाक्षिणात्यः संनहनं स्रगुष्णीषे च मागधः ।वसुदानो महेष्वासो गजेन्द्रं षष्टिहायनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: dākṣiṇātyaḥ saṁnahanaṁ sraguṣṇīṣe ca māgadhaḥ ,vasudāno maheṣvāso gajendraṁ ṣaṣṭihāyanam.
english: The Southerner presented armor. The Magadhan presented a garland and a turban. Vasudana, the great archer, presented a sixty-year-old lordly elephant.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 7. The Southerner presented armor. The Magadhan presented a garland and a turban. Vasudana, the great archer, presented a sixty-year-old lordly elephant.

sanskrit: मत्स्यस्त्वक्षानवाबध्नादेकलव्य उपानहौ ।आवन्त्यस्त्वभिषेकार्थमापो बहुविधास्तथा ॥८॥
transliteration: matsyastvakṣānavābadhnādekalavya upānahau ,āvantyastvabhiṣekārthamāpo bahuvidhāstathā.
english: The Matsya king, however, strengthened the axles. Ekalavya presented a pair of shoes. The Avanti king, in like manner, provided various kinds of waters for the anointing (abhiṣeka) ceremony.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 8. The Matsya king, however, strengthened the axles. Ekalavya presented a pair of shoes. The Avanti king, in like manner, provided various kinds of waters for the anointing (abhiṣeka) ceremony.

sanskrit: चेकितान उपासङ्गं धनुः काश्य उपाहरत् ।असिं रुक्मत्सरुं शल्यः शैक्यं काञ्चनभूषणम् ॥९॥
transliteration: cekitāna upāsaṅgaṁ dhanuḥ kāśya upāharat ,asiṁ rukmatsaruṁ śalyaḥ śaikyaṁ kāñcanabhūṣaṇam.
english: Chekitana presented a quiver. The king of Kashi presented a bow. Shalya presented a golden-hilted sword and a gold-adorned shield.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 9. Chekitana presented a quiver. The king of Kashi presented a bow. Shalya presented a golden-hilted sword and a gold-adorned shield.

sanskrit: अभ्यषिञ्चत्ततो धौम्यो व्यासश्च सुमहातपाः ।नारदं वै पुरस्कृत्य देवलं चासितं मुनिम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: abhyaṣiñcattato dhaumyo vyāsaśca sumahātapāḥ ,nāradaṁ vai puraskṛtya devalaṁ cāsitaṁ munim.
english: Then Dhaumya and Vyasa, who was a greatly ascetic (tapas) sage, performed the consecration ritual, having placed Narada, Devala, and the sage Asita at the forefront.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 10. Then Dhaumya and Vyasa, who was a greatly ascetic (tapas) sage, performed the consecration ritual, having placed Narada, Devala, and the sage Asita at the forefront.

sanskrit: प्रीतिमन्त उपातिष्ठन्नभिषेकं महर्षयः ।जामदग्न्येन सहितास्तथान्ये वेदपारगाः ॥११॥
transliteration: prītimanta upātiṣṭhannabhiṣekaṁ maharṣayaḥ ,jāmadagnyena sahitāstathānye vedapāragāḥ.
english: The great sages, full of joy, attended the consecration ritual, accompanied by Jamadagni's son (Parashurama) and other masters of the Vedas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 11. The great sages, full of joy, attended the consecration ritual, accompanied by Jamadagni's son (Parashurama) and other masters of the Vedas.

sanskrit: अभिजग्मुर्महात्मानं मन्त्रवद्भूरिदक्षिणम् ।महेन्द्रमिव देवेन्द्रं दिवि सप्तर्षयो यथा ॥१२॥
transliteration: abhijagmurmahātmānaṁ mantravadbhūridakṣiṇam ,mahendramiva devendraṁ divi saptarṣayo yathā.
english: Just as the seven sages in heaven approach Mahendra, the lord of the gods, they approached the great-souled one, who was endowed with sacred hymns (mantra) and had given abundant sacrificial fees.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 12. Just as the seven sages in heaven approach Mahendra, the lord of the gods, they approached the great-souled one, who was endowed with sacred hymns (mantra) and had given abundant sacrificial fees.

sanskrit: अधारयच्छत्रमस्य सात्यकिः सत्यविक्रमः ।धनंजयश्च व्यजने भीमसेनश्च पाण्डवः ॥१३॥
transliteration: adhārayacchatramasya sātyakiḥ satyavikramaḥ ,dhanaṁjayaśca vyajane bhīmasenaśca pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Satyaki, known for his true valor, held his royal umbrella, while Dhananjaya (Arjuna) and Bhimasena, the son of Pandu, held the fans.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 13. Satyaki, known for his true valor, held his royal umbrella, while Dhananjaya (Arjuna) and Bhimasena, the son of Pandu, held the fans.

sanskrit: उपागृह्णाद्यमिन्द्राय पुराकल्पे प्रजापतिः ।तमस्मै शङ्खमाहार्षीद्वारुणं कलशोदधिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: upāgṛhṇādyamindrāya purākalpe prajāpatiḥ ,tamasmai śaṅkhamāhārṣīdvāruṇaṁ kalaśodadhiḥ.
english: Prajapati, in a previous cosmic age, had taken a conch for Indra. That conch, associated with Varuna, the ocean (kalaśodadhi) then brought to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 14. Prajapati, in a previous cosmic age, had taken a conch for Indra. That conch, associated with Varuna, the ocean (kalaśodadhi) then brought to him.

sanskrit: सिक्तं निष्कसहस्रेण सुकृतं विश्वकर्मणा ।तेनाभिषिक्तः कृष्णेन तत्र मे कश्मलोऽभवत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: siktaṁ niṣkasahasreṇa sukṛtaṁ viśvakarmaṇā ,tenābhiṣiktaḥ kṛṣṇena tatra me kaśmalo'bhavat.
english: That conch, well-crafted by Viśvakarma, had been consecrated with a thousand niṣkas. When I was anointed with it by Krishna, confusion (kaśmala) arose within me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 15. That conch, well-crafted by Viśvakarma, had been consecrated with a thousand niṣkas. When I was anointed with it by Krishna, confusion (kaśmala) arose within me.

sanskrit: गच्छन्ति पूर्वादपरं समुद्रं चापि दक्षिणम् ।उत्तरं तु न गच्छन्ति विना तात पतत्रिभिः ॥१६॥
transliteration: gacchanti pūrvādaparaṁ samudraṁ cāpi dakṣiṇam ,uttaraṁ tu na gacchanti vinā tāta patatribhiḥ.
english: They travel from the eastern (ocean) to the western ocean, and also to the southern (ocean). But, dear father, they do not go to the northern (ocean) without birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 16. They travel from the eastern (ocean) to the western ocean, and also to the southern (ocean). But, dear father, they do not go to the northern (ocean) without birds.

sanskrit: तत्र स्म दध्मुः शतशः शङ्खान्मङ्गल्यकारणात् ।प्राणदंस्ते समाध्मातास्तत्र रोमाणि मेऽहृषन् ॥१७॥
transliteration: tatra sma dadhmuḥ śataśaḥ śaṅkhānmaṅgalyakāraṇāt ,prāṇadaṁste samādhmātāstatra romāṇi me'hṛṣan.
english: There, for the sake of auspiciousness, they blew hundreds of conches. When those conches were sounded together, they made a resounding noise, and then my body-hairs stood on end.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 17. There, for the sake of auspiciousness, they blew hundreds of conches. When those conches were sounded together, they made a resounding noise, and then my body-hairs stood on end.

sanskrit: प्रणता भूमिपाश्चापि पेतुर्हीनाः स्वतेजसा ।धृष्टद्युम्नः पाण्डवाश्च सात्यकिः केशवोऽष्टमः ॥१८॥
transliteration: praṇatā bhūmipāścāpi peturhīnāḥ svatejasā ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaḥ pāṇḍavāśca sātyakiḥ keśavo'ṣṭamaḥ.
english: Even the prostrate kings fell, deprived of their own radiance. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the Pāṇḍavas, Sātyaki, and Keśava (Krishna), the eighth, were present.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 18. Even the prostrate kings fell, deprived of their own radiance. Dhṛṣṭadyumna, the Pāṇḍavas, Sātyaki, and Keśava (Krishna), the eighth, were present.

sanskrit: सत्त्वस्थाः शौर्यसंपन्ना अन्योन्यप्रियकारिणः ।विसंज्ञान्भूमिपान्दृष्ट्वा मां च ते प्राहसंस्तदा ॥१९॥
transliteration: sattvasthāḥ śauryasaṁpannā anyonyapriyakāriṇaḥ ,visaṁjñānbhūmipāndṛṣṭvā māṁ ca te prāhasaṁstadā.
english: Established in goodness (sattva), endowed with valor, and acting affectionately towards each other, they then laughed, having seen the unconscious kings and me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 19. Established in goodness (sattva), endowed with valor, and acting affectionately towards each other, they then laughed, having seen the unconscious kings and me.

sanskrit: ततः प्रहृष्टो बीभत्सुः प्रादाद्धेमविषाणिनाम् ।शतान्यनडुहां पञ्च द्विजमुख्येषु भारत ॥२०॥
transliteration: tataḥ prahṛṣṭo bībhatsuḥ prādāddhemaviṣāṇinām ,śatānyanaḍuhāṁ pañca dvijamukhyeṣu bhārata.
english: Then, the greatly delighted Bhīmatsu (Arjuna) gave five hundred oxen with golden horns to the chief among the twice-born (dvija), O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 20. Then, the greatly delighted Bhīmatsu (Arjuna) gave five hundred oxen with golden horns to the chief among the twice-born (dvija), O Bhārata.

sanskrit: नैवं शम्बरहन्ताभूद्यौवनाश्वो मनुर्न च ।न च राजा पृथुर्वैन्यो न चाप्यासीद्भगीरथः ॥२१॥
transliteration: naivaṁ śambarahantābhūdyauvanāśvo manurna ca ,na ca rājā pṛthurvainyo na cāpyāsīdbhagīrathaḥ.
english: Neither the slayer of Śambara, nor Yauvanāśva, nor Manu did such a deed. Nor did King Pṛthu (Vainya), nor indeed was Bhagīratha capable of it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 21. Neither the slayer of Śambara, nor Yauvanāśva, nor Manu did such a deed. Nor did King Pṛthu (Vainya), nor indeed was Bhagīratha capable of it.

sanskrit: यथातिमात्रं कौन्तेयः श्रिया परमया युतः ।राजसूयमवाप्यैवं हरिश्चन्द्र इव प्रभुः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yathātimātraṁ kaunteyaḥ śriyā paramayā yutaḥ ,rājasūyamavāpyaivaṁ hariścandra iva prabhuḥ.
english: Just as Kaunteya (Yudhiṣṭhira), endowed with supreme glory, attained the Rājasūya (yajña), so indeed was he like the mighty king Hariśchandra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 22. Just as Kaunteya (Yudhiṣṭhira), endowed with supreme glory, attained the Rājasūya (yajña), so indeed was he like the mighty king Hariśchandra.

sanskrit: एतां दृष्ट्वा श्रियं पार्थे हरिश्चन्द्रे यथा विभो ।कथं नु जीवितं श्रेयो मम पश्यसि भारत ॥२३॥
transliteration: etāṁ dṛṣṭvā śriyaṁ pārthe hariścandre yathā vibho ,kathaṁ nu jīvitaṁ śreyo mama paśyasi bhārata.
english: Seeing this glory (śrī) in Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira), similar to what Hariśchandra possessed, O powerful one, how indeed do you consider my life superior, O Bhārata?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 23. Seeing this glory (śrī) in Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira), similar to what Hariśchandra possessed, O powerful one, how indeed do you consider my life superior, O Bhārata?

sanskrit: अन्धेनेव युगं नद्धं विपर्यस्तं नराधिप ।कनीयांसो विवर्धन्ते ज्येष्ठा हीयन्ति भारत ॥२४॥
transliteration: andheneva yugaṁ naddhaṁ viparyastaṁ narādhipa ,kanīyāṁso vivardhante jyeṣṭhā hīyanti bhārata.
english: O King (narādhipa), it is as if a blind man has wrongly fastened the yoke; the younger ones prosper, while the elders decline, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 24. O King (narādhipa), it is as if a blind man has wrongly fastened the yoke; the younger ones prosper, while the elders decline, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: एवं दृष्ट्वा नाभिविन्दामि शर्म परीक्षमाणोऽपि कुरुप्रवीर ।तेनाहमेवं कृशतां गतश्च विवर्णतां चैव सशोकतां च ॥२५॥
transliteration: evaṁ dṛṣṭvā nābhivindāmi śarma; parīkṣamāṇo'pi kurupravīra ,tenāhamevaṁ kṛśatāṁ gataśca; vivarṇatāṁ caiva saśokatāṁ ca.
english: Seeing this, O foremost of Kurus (kuruprvīra), I find no peace, even after examining everything. Therefore, I have become emaciated, discolored, and overcome with sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 49 Verse 25. Seeing this, O foremost of Kurus (kuruprvīra), I find no peace, even after examining everything. Therefore, I have become emaciated, discolored, and overcome with sorrow.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।त्वं वै ज्येष्ठो ज्यैष्ठिनेयः पुत्र मा पाण्डवान्द्विषः ।द्वेष्टा ह्यसुखमादत्ते यथैव निधनं तथा ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,tvaṁ vai jyeṣṭho jyaiṣṭhineyaḥ putra mā pāṇḍavāndviṣaḥ ,dveṣṭā hyasukhamādatte yathaiva nidhanaṁ tathā.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "You are indeed the eldest son of the eldest (brother), my son. Do not hate the Pandavas. For a hater incurs misery, just as they bring about their own destruction."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: "You are indeed the eldest son of the eldest (brother), my son. Do not hate the Pandavas. For a hater incurs misery, just as they bring about their own destruction."

sanskrit: अव्युत्पन्नं समानार्थं तुल्यमित्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ।अद्विषन्तं कथं द्विष्यात्त्वादृशो भरतर्षभ ॥२॥
transliteration: avyutpannaṁ samānārthaṁ tulyamitraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram ,adviṣantaṁ kathaṁ dviṣyāttvādṛśo bharatarṣabha.
english: O chief of the Bharatas, how can a person like you hate Yudhishthira, who is innocent, who shares the same purpose, who has friends just like yours, and who himself bears no hatred?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 2. O chief of the Bharatas, how can a person like you hate Yudhishthira, who is innocent, who shares the same purpose, who has friends just like yours, and who himself bears no hatred?

sanskrit: तुल्याभिजनवीर्यश्च कथं भ्रातुः श्रियं नृप ।पुत्र कामयसे मोहान्मैवं भूः शाम्य साध्विह ॥३॥
transliteration: tulyābhijanavīryaśca kathaṁ bhrātuḥ śriyaṁ nṛpa ,putra kāmayase mohānmaivaṁ bhūḥ śāmya sādhviha.
english: O king, O son, with a lineage and valor equal to theirs, how can you desire your brother's prosperity out of delusion (moha)? Do not be like this! Be calm and act righteously here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 3. O king, O son, with a lineage and valor equal to theirs, how can you desire your brother's prosperity out of delusion (moha)? Do not be like this! Be calm and act righteously here.

sanskrit: अथ यज्ञविभूतिं तां काङ्क्षसे भरतर्षभ ।ऋत्विजस्तव तन्वन्तु सप्ततन्तुं महाध्वरम् ॥४॥
transliteration: atha yajñavibhūtiṁ tāṁ kāṅkṣase bharatarṣabha ,ṛtvijastava tanvantu saptatantuṁ mahādhvaram.
english: O best among the Bharatas, if you now desire that glory associated with the Vedic ritual (yajña), then let your priests perform that great seven-stringed Vedic ritual (yajña).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 4. O best among the Bharatas, if you now desire that glory associated with the Vedic ritual (yajña), then let your priests perform that great seven-stringed Vedic ritual (yajña).

sanskrit: आहरिष्यन्ति राजानस्तवापि विपुलं धनम् ।प्रीत्या च बहुमानाच्च रत्नान्याभरणानि च ॥५॥
transliteration: āhariṣyanti rājānastavāpi vipulaṁ dhanam ,prītyā ca bahumānācca ratnānyābharaṇāni ca.
english: Kings will bring you abundant wealth, and also jewels and ornaments, out of both affection and great respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 5. Kings will bring you abundant wealth, and also jewels and ornaments, out of both affection and great respect.

sanskrit: अनर्थाचरितं तात परस्वस्पृहणं भृशम् ।स्वसंतुष्टः स्वधर्मस्थो यः स वै सुखमेधते ॥६॥
transliteration: anarthācaritaṁ tāta parasvaspṛhaṇaṁ bhṛśam ,svasaṁtuṣṭaḥ svadharmastho yaḥ sa vai sukhamedhate.
english: My dear child, coveting the wealth of others leads to great misfortune. Indeed, whoever is content with their own lot and steadfast in their own natural law (svadharma) prospers happily.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 6. My dear child, coveting the wealth of others leads to great misfortune. Indeed, whoever is content with their own lot and steadfast in their own natural law (svadharma) prospers happily.

sanskrit: अव्यापारः परार्थेषु नित्योद्योगः स्वकर्मसु ।उद्यमो रक्षणे स्वेषामेतद्वैभवलक्षणम् ॥७॥
transliteration: avyāpāraḥ parārtheṣu nityodyogaḥ svakarmasu ,udyamo rakṣaṇe sveṣāmetadvaibhavalakṣaṇam.
english: Not interfering in the affairs of others, constant diligence in one's own actions (karma), and endeavoring to protect one's own – these are the characteristics of prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 7. Not interfering in the affairs of others, constant diligence in one's own actions (karma), and endeavoring to protect one's own – these are the characteristics of prosperity.

sanskrit: विपत्तिष्वव्यथो दक्षो नित्यमुत्थानवान्नरः ।अप्रमत्तो विनीतात्मा नित्यं भद्राणि पश्यति ॥८॥
transliteration: vipattiṣvavyatho dakṣo nityamutthānavānnaraḥ ,apramatto vinītātmā nityaṁ bhadrāṇi paśyati.
english: A person who remains undisturbed in adversities, is skillful, always energetic, vigilant, and possesses a disciplined self (ātman), constantly perceives auspicious outcomes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 8. A person who remains undisturbed in adversities, is skillful, always energetic, vigilant, and possesses a disciplined self (ātman), constantly perceives auspicious outcomes.

sanskrit: अन्तर्वेद्यां ददद्वित्तं कामाननुभवन्प्रियान् ।क्रीडन्स्त्रीभिर्निरातङ्कः प्रशाम्य भरतर्षभ ॥९॥
transliteration: antarvedyāṁ dadadvittaṁ kāmānanubhavanpriyān ,krīḍanstrībhirnirātaṅkaḥ praśāmya bharatarṣabha.
english: O best among the Bharatas, after giving wealth within the sacred enclosure, experiencing beloved pleasures, and enjoying yourself with women, become tranquil and free from anxiety.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 9. O best among the Bharatas, after giving wealth within the sacred enclosure, experiencing beloved pleasures, and enjoying yourself with women, become tranquil and free from anxiety.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।जानन्वै मोहयसि मां नावि नौरिव संयता ।स्वार्थे किं नावधानं ते उताहो द्वेष्टि मां भवान् ॥१०॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,jānanvai mohayasi māṁ nāvi nauriva saṁyatā ,svārthe kiṁ nāvadhānaṁ te utāho dveṣṭi māṁ bhavān.
english: Duryodhana said, "Indeed, knowing everything, you delude me, just like a boat held back on the water. Why is your own interest not your concern, or do you hate me, sir?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 10. Duryodhana said, "Indeed, knowing everything, you delude me, just like a boat held back on the water. Why is your own interest not your concern, or do you hate me, sir?"

sanskrit: न सन्तीमे धार्तराष्ट्रा येषां त्वमनुशासिता ।भविष्यमर्थमाख्यासि सदा त्वं कृत्यमात्मनः ॥११॥
transliteration: na santīme dhārtarāṣṭrā yeṣāṁ tvamanuśāsitā ,bhaviṣyamarthamākhyāsi sadā tvaṁ kṛtyamātmanaḥ.
english: These Dhārtarāṣṭras, whose instructor you are, are not in effect (or are deemed insignificant). Instead, you always proclaim what is your own future interest and duty for yourself (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 11. These Dhārtarāṣṭras, whose instructor you are, are not in effect (or are deemed insignificant). Instead, you always proclaim what is your own future interest and duty for yourself (ātman).

sanskrit: परप्रणेयोऽग्रणीर्हि यश्च मार्गात्प्रमुह्यति ।पन्थानमनुगच्छेयुः कथं तस्य पदानुगाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: parapraṇeyo'graṇīrhi yaśca mārgātpramuhyati ,panthānamanugaccheyuḥ kathaṁ tasya padānugāḥ.
english: Indeed, how can followers adhere to the path of someone who, though a leader, is led by others and deviates from the proper course?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 12. Indeed, how can followers adhere to the path of someone who, though a leader, is led by others and deviates from the proper course?

sanskrit: राजन्परिगतप्रज्ञो वृद्धसेवी जितेन्द्रियः ।प्रतिपन्नान्स्वकार्येषु संमोहयसि नो भृशम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: rājanparigataprajño vṛddhasevī jitendriyaḥ ,pratipannānsvakāryeṣu saṁmohayasi no bhṛśam.
english: O King, you, who are endowed with profound wisdom, who serve the elders, and who have mastered your senses, greatly bewilder us, who are committed to our own responsibilities.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 13. O King, you, who are endowed with profound wisdom, who serve the elders, and who have mastered your senses, greatly bewilder us, who are committed to our own responsibilities.

sanskrit: लोकवृत्ताद्राजवृत्तमन्यदाह बृहस्पतिः ।तस्माद्राज्ञा प्रयत्नेन स्वार्थश्चिन्त्यः सदैव हि ॥१४॥
transliteration: lokavṛttādrājavṛttamanyadāha bṛhaspatiḥ ,tasmādrājñā prayatnena svārthaścintyaḥ sadaiva hi.
english: Brihaspati has declared that the conduct of a king differs from that of ordinary people. Therefore, a king should always carefully consider his own interest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 14. Brihaspati has declared that the conduct of a king differs from that of ordinary people. Therefore, a king should always carefully consider his own interest.

sanskrit: क्षत्रियस्य महाराज जये वृत्तिः समाहिता ।स वै धर्मोऽस्त्वधर्मो वा स्ववृत्तौ भरतर्षभ ॥१५॥
transliteration: kṣatriyasya mahārāja jaye vṛttiḥ samāhitā ,sa vai dharmo'stvadharmo vā svavṛttau bharatarṣabha.
english: O great king, O best of the Bharatas, a kshatriya's very existence is found in victory. Whether it is considered proper conduct (dharma) or improper conduct (adharma), this is his intrinsic nature (dharma) according to his specific role.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 15. O great king, O best of the Bharatas, a kshatriya's very existence is found in victory. Whether it is considered proper conduct (dharma) or improper conduct (adharma), this is his intrinsic nature (dharma) according to his specific role.

sanskrit: प्रकालयेद्दिशः सर्वाः प्रतोदेनेव सारथिः ।प्रत्यमित्रश्रियं दीप्तां बुभूषुर्भरतर्षभ ॥१६॥
transliteration: prakālayeddiśaḥ sarvāḥ pratodeneva sārathiḥ ,pratyamitraśriyaṁ dīptāṁ bubhūṣurbharatarṣabha.
english: O best of the Bharatas, desiring to become victorious, a king should drive away the blazing splendor of his adversaries from all directions, just as a charioteer uses a goad.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 16. O best of the Bharatas, desiring to become victorious, a king should drive away the blazing splendor of his adversaries from all directions, just as a charioteer uses a goad.

sanskrit: प्रच्छन्नो वा प्रकाशो वा यो योगो रिपुबाधनः ।तद्वै शस्त्रं शस्त्रविदां न शस्त्रं छेदनं स्मृतम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: pracchanno vā prakāśo vā yo yogo ripubādhanaḥ ,tadvai śastraṁ śastravidāṁ na śastraṁ chedanaṁ smṛtam.
english: Whether it is hidden or manifest, that strategic application (yoga) which harasses enemies is indeed the true weapon of those who understand weaponry; mere cutting is not considered a weapon.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 17. Whether it is hidden or manifest, that strategic application (yoga) which harasses enemies is indeed the true weapon of those who understand weaponry; mere cutting is not considered a weapon.

sanskrit: असंतोषः श्रियो मूलं तस्मात्तं कामयाम्यहम् ।समुच्छ्रये यो यतते स राजन्परमो नयी ॥१८॥
transliteration: asaṁtoṣaḥ śriyo mūlaṁ tasmāttaṁ kāmayāmyaham ,samucchraye yo yatate sa rājanparamo nayī.
english: Discontent is the root of prosperity, therefore I desire it. O king, he who strives for elevation is indeed the foremost leader.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 18. Discontent is the root of prosperity, therefore I desire it. O king, he who strives for elevation is indeed the foremost leader.

sanskrit: ममत्वं हि न कर्तव्यमैश्वर्ये वा धनेऽपि वा ।पूर्वावाप्तं हरन्त्यन्ये राजधर्मं हि तं विदुः ॥१९॥
transliteration: mamatvaṁ hi na kartavyamaiśvarye vā dhane'pi vā ,pūrvāvāptaṁ harantyanye rājadharmaṁ hi taṁ viduḥ.
english: Indeed, one should not feel possessive regarding power or wealth. Others take away what was acquired previously, and they truly consider that to be the natural law (dharma) for kings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 19. Indeed, one should not feel possessive regarding power or wealth. Others take away what was acquired previously, and they truly consider that to be the natural law (dharma) for kings.

sanskrit: अद्रोहे समयं कृत्वा चिच्छेद नमुचेः शिरः ।शक्रः सा हि मता तस्य रिपौ वृत्तिः सनातनी ॥२०॥
transliteration: adrohe samayaṁ kṛtvā ciccheda namuceḥ śiraḥ ,śakraḥ sā hi matā tasya ripau vṛttiḥ sanātanī.
english: Having made a pledge (samaya) of non-aggression (adroha), Śakra (Indra) severed Namuci's head. Indeed, that is considered his eternal conduct (vṛtti) toward an enemy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 20. Having made a pledge (samaya) of non-aggression (adroha), Śakra (Indra) severed Namuci's head. Indeed, that is considered his eternal conduct (vṛtti) toward an enemy.

sanskrit: द्वावेतौ ग्रसते भूमिः सर्पो बिलशयानिव ।राजानं चाविरोद्धारं ब्राह्मणं चाप्रवासिनम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: dvāvetau grasate bhūmiḥ sarpo bilaśayāniva ,rājānaṁ cāviroddhāraṁ brāhmaṇaṁ cāpravāsinam.
english: The realm (bhūmi) swallows up these two, just as a serpent devours creatures living in burrows: a king who does not resist (his enemies), and a brahmin who does not travel (seeking knowledge or livelihood).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 21. The realm (bhūmi) swallows up these two, just as a serpent devours creatures living in burrows: a king who does not resist (his enemies), and a brahmin who does not travel (seeking knowledge or livelihood).

sanskrit: नास्ति वै जातितः शत्रुः पुरुषस्य विशां पते ।येन साधारणी वृत्तिः स शत्रुर्नेतरो जनः ॥२२॥
transliteration: nāsti vai jātitaḥ śatruḥ puruṣasya viśāṁ pate ,yena sādhāraṇī vṛttiḥ sa śatrurnetaro janaḥ.
english: O lord of the people, truly, there is no enemy for a person based on birth (jāti). Only he with whom one shares a common interest or livelihood (vṛtti) is an enemy; no other person is.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 22. O lord of the people, truly, there is no enemy for a person based on birth (jāti). Only he with whom one shares a common interest or livelihood (vṛtti) is an enemy; no other person is.

sanskrit: शत्रुपक्षं समृध्यन्तं यो मोहात्समुपेक्षते ।व्याधिराप्यायित इव तस्य मूलं छिनत्ति सः ॥२३॥
transliteration: śatrupakṣaṁ samṛdhyantaṁ yo mohātsamupekṣate ,vyādhirāpyāyita iva tasya mūlaṁ chinatti saḥ.
english: The person who, out of delusion (moha), overlooks a prospering enemy faction, that (neglect) cuts his very foundation, just as a disease that has been allowed to grow (or nourished) does.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 23. The person who, out of delusion (moha), overlooks a prospering enemy faction, that (neglect) cuts his very foundation, just as a disease that has been allowed to grow (or nourished) does.

sanskrit: अल्पोऽपि ह्यरिरत्यन्तं वर्धमानपराक्रमः ।वल्मीको मूलज इव ग्रसते वृक्षमन्तिकात् ॥२४॥
transliteration: alpo'pi hyariratyantaṁ vardhamānaparākramaḥ ,valmīko mūlaja iva grasate vṛkṣamantikāt.
english: Even a small enemy, whose might is exceedingly increasing, devours a tree from its base, just as an anthill originating at the root (grows and consumes) a tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 24. Even a small enemy, whose might is exceedingly increasing, devours a tree from its base, just as an anthill originating at the root (grows and consumes) a tree.

sanskrit: आजमीढ रिपोर्लक्ष्मीर्मा ते रोचिष्ट भारत ।एष भारः सत्त्ववतां नयः शिरसि धिष्ठितः ॥२५॥
transliteration: ājamīḍha riporlakṣmīrmā te rociṣṭa bhārata ,eṣa bhāraḥ sattvavatāṁ nayaḥ śirasi dhiṣṭhitaḥ.
english: O Ajamiḍha, O Bhārata, may the prosperity of your enemy not be appealing to you. This burden of policy (naya) is placed upon the shoulders of the courageous.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 25. O Ajamiḍha, O Bhārata, may the prosperity of your enemy not be appealing to you. This burden of policy (naya) is placed upon the shoulders of the courageous.

sanskrit: जन्मवृद्धिमिवार्थानां यो वृद्धिमभिकाङ्क्षते ।एधते ज्ञातिषु स वै सद्योवृद्धिर्हि विक्रमः ॥२६॥
transliteration: janmavṛddhimivārthānāṁ yo vṛddhimabhikāṅkṣate ,edhate jñātiṣu sa vai sadyovṛddhirhi vikramaḥ.
english: He who desires prosperity for his possessions as if it were their intrinsic nature (janmavṛddhi) to grow, he indeed prospers among his relatives, for valor (vikrama) truly brings swift growth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 26. He who desires prosperity for his possessions as if it were their intrinsic nature (janmavṛddhi) to grow, he indeed prospers among his relatives, for valor (vikrama) truly brings swift growth.

sanskrit: नाप्राप्य पाण्डवैश्वर्यं संशयो मे भविष्यति ।अवाप्स्ये वा श्रियं तां हि शेष्ये वा निहतो युधि ॥२७॥
transliteration: nāprāpya pāṇḍavaiśvaryaṁ saṁśayo me bhaviṣyati ,avāpsye vā śriyaṁ tāṁ hi śeṣye vā nihato yudhi.
english: I shall not experience doubt without obtaining sovereignty (aiśvarya) from the Pandavas. I will either obtain that prosperity (śrī) or, indeed, I will be slain (nihata) in battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 27. I shall not experience doubt without obtaining sovereignty (aiśvarya) from the Pandavas. I will either obtain that prosperity (śrī) or, indeed, I will be slain (nihata) in battle.

sanskrit: अतादृशस्य किं मेऽद्य जीवितेन विशां पते ।वर्धन्ते पाण्डवा नित्यं वयं तु स्थिरवृद्धयः ॥२८॥
transliteration: atādṛśasya kiṁ me'dya jīvitena viśāṁ pate ,vardhante pāṇḍavā nityaṁ vayaṁ tu sthiravṛddhayaḥ.
english: O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), what is the purpose of life (jīvita) for me now, who am not like that [i.e., victorious or expanding]? The Pandavas are constantly increasing, but our growth is stagnant.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 50 Verse 28. O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), what is the purpose of life (jīvita) for me now, who am not like that [i.e., victorious or expanding]? The Pandavas are constantly increasing, but our growth is stagnant.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।यां त्वमेतां श्रियं दृष्ट्वा पाण्डुपुत्रे युधिष्ठिरे ।तप्यसे तां हरिष्यामि द्यूतेनाहूयतां परः ॥१॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,yāṁ tvametāṁ śriyaṁ dṛṣṭvā pāṇḍuputre yudhiṣṭhire ,tapyase tāṁ hariṣyāmi dyūtenāhūyatāṁ paraḥ.
english: Śakuni said: "That prosperity (śrī) which you see in Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu, and for which you are tormented - I will seize it through gambling. Let the other be summoned!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 1. Śakuni said: "That prosperity (śrī) which you see in Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pāṇḍu, and for which you are tormented - I will seize it through gambling. Let the other be summoned!"

sanskrit: अगत्वा संशयमहमयुद्ध्वा च चमूमुखे ।अक्षान्क्षिपन्नक्षतः सन्विद्वानविदुषो जये ॥२॥
transliteration: agatvā saṁśayamahamayuddhvā ca camūmukhe ,akṣānkṣipannakṣataḥ sanvidvānaviduṣo jaye.
english: Without incurring doubt, and without fighting on the battlefield, by throwing the dice, I, the uninjured expert, will defeat the non-expert.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 2. Without incurring doubt, and without fighting on the battlefield, by throwing the dice, I, the uninjured expert, will defeat the non-expert.

sanskrit: ग्लहान्धनूंषि मे विद्धि शरानक्षांश्च भारत ।अक्षाणां हृदयं मे ज्यां रथं विद्धि ममास्तरम् ॥३॥
transliteration: glahāndhanūṁṣi me viddhi śarānakṣāṁśca bhārata ,akṣāṇāṁ hṛdayaṁ me jyāṁ rathaṁ viddhi mamāstaram.
english: O Bhārata (Duryodhana), know my stakes (glaha) to be bows, and my arrows to be dice. Know the heart of the dice to be my bowstring, and my chariot to be my mat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 3. O Bhārata (Duryodhana), know my stakes (glaha) to be bows, and my arrows to be dice. Know the heart of the dice to be my bowstring, and my chariot to be my mat.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।अयमुत्सहते राजञ्श्रियमाहर्तुमक्षवित् ।द्यूतेन पाण्डुपुत्रेभ्यस्तत्तुभ्यं तात रोचताम् ॥४॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,ayamutsahate rājañśriyamāhartumakṣavit ,dyūtena pāṇḍuputrebhyastattubhyaṁ tāta rocatām.
english: Duryodhana said: "This expert in dice (akṣavit) is eager, O King, to seize the prosperity (śrī) from the sons of Pāṇḍu through gambling. May that be pleasing to you, dear father!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 4. Duryodhana said: "This expert in dice (akṣavit) is eager, O King, to seize the prosperity (śrī) from the sons of Pāṇḍu through gambling. May that be pleasing to you, dear father!"

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।स्थितोऽस्मि शासने भ्रातुर्विदुरस्य महात्मनः ।तेन संगम्य वेत्स्यामि कार्यस्यास्य विनिश्चयम् ॥५॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,sthito'smi śāsane bhrāturvidurasya mahātmanaḥ ,tena saṁgamya vetsyāmi kāryasyāsya viniścayam.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "I am committed to the command of my brother, the great-souled Vidura. After consulting with him, I will ascertain the definitive decision regarding this matter."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 5. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "I am committed to the command of my brother, the great-souled Vidura. After consulting with him, I will ascertain the definitive decision regarding this matter."

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।विहनिष्यति ते बुद्धिं विदुरो मुक्तसंशयः ।पाण्डवानां हिते युक्तो न तथा मम कौरव ॥६॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,vihaniṣyati te buddhiṁ viduro muktasaṁśayaḥ ,pāṇḍavānāṁ hite yukto na tathā mama kaurava.
english: Duryodhana said: "Vidura, being free from doubt, will certainly corrupt your intellect. He is engaged in the welfare of the Pāṇḍavas, and not similarly in my welfare, O Kaurava."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 6. Duryodhana said: "Vidura, being free from doubt, will certainly corrupt your intellect. He is engaged in the welfare of the Pāṇḍavas, and not similarly in my welfare, O Kaurava."

sanskrit: नारभेत्परसामर्थ्यात्पुरुषः कार्यमात्मनः ।मतिसाम्यं द्वयोर्नास्ति कार्येषु कुरुनन्दन ॥७॥
transliteration: nārabhetparasāmarthyātpuruṣaḥ kāryamātmanaḥ ,matisāmyaṁ dvayornāsti kāryeṣu kurunandana.
english: A person should not undertake their own task based on another's capability. There is no equality of intellect between two individuals in matters of action, O joy of the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 7. A person should not undertake their own task based on another's capability. There is no equality of intellect between two individuals in matters of action, O joy of the Kurus.

sanskrit: भयं परिहरन्मन्द आत्मानं परिपालयन् ।वर्षासु क्लिन्नकटवत्तिष्ठन्नेवावसीदति ॥८॥
transliteration: bhayaṁ pariharanmanda ātmānaṁ paripālayan ,varṣāsu klinnakaṭavattiṣṭhannevāvasīdati.
english: A dull-witted person, abandoning fear and protecting their self (ātman), still perishes like a soaked reed in the rainy season.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 8. A dull-witted person, abandoning fear and protecting their self (ātman), still perishes like a soaked reed in the rainy season.

sanskrit: न व्याधयो नापि यमः श्रेयःप्राप्तिं प्रतीक्षते ।यावदेव भवेत्कल्पस्तावच्छ्रेयः समाचरेत् ॥९॥
transliteration: na vyādhayo nāpi yamaḥ śreyaḥprāptiṁ pratīkṣate ,yāvadeva bhavetkalpastāvacchreyaḥ samācaret.
english: Diseases do not, nor does Yama (the god of death), wait for the achievement of what is beneficial. As long as one's lifespan (kalpa) endures, one should always strive for what is good.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 9. Diseases do not, nor does Yama (the god of death), wait for the achievement of what is beneficial. As long as one's lifespan (kalpa) endures, one should always strive for what is good.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।सर्वथा पुत्र बलिभिर्विग्रहं ते न रोचये ।वैरं विकारं सृजति तद्वै शस्त्रमनायसम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,sarvathā putra balibhirvigrahaṁ te na rocaye ,vairaṁ vikāraṁ sṛjati tadvai śastramanāyasam.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "My son, I absolutely do not approve of your conflict with the powerful ones. Enmity produces discord; indeed, that weapon (of hostility) brings misfortune."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 10. Dhritarashtra said: "My son, I absolutely do not approve of your conflict with the powerful ones. Enmity produces discord; indeed, that weapon (of hostility) brings misfortune."

sanskrit: अनर्थमर्थं मन्यसे राजपुत्र संग्रन्थनं कलहस्यातिघोरम् ।तद्वै प्रवृत्तं तु यथा कथंचिद्विमोक्षयेच्चाप्यसिसायकांश्च ॥११॥
transliteration: anarthamarthaṁ manyase rājaputra; saṁgranthanaṁ kalahasyātighoram ,tadvai pravṛttaṁ tu yathā kathaṁci;dvimokṣayeccāpyasisāyakāṁśca.
english: "O prince, you consider misfortune to be fortune, an extremely dreadful entanglement of conflict. Indeed, that (conflict) which has already begun, one should somehow avert, along with swords and arrows."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 11. "O prince, you consider misfortune to be fortune, an extremely dreadful entanglement of conflict. Indeed, that (conflict) which has already begun, one should somehow avert, along with swords and arrows."

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।द्यूते पुराणैर्व्यवहारः प्रणीतस्तत्रात्ययो नास्ति न संप्रहारः ।तद्रोचतां शकुनेर्वाक्यमद्य सभां क्षिप्रं त्वमिहाज्ञापयस्व ॥१२॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,dyūte purāṇairvyavahāraḥ praṇīta;statrātyayo nāsti na saṁprahāraḥ ,tadrocatāṁ śakunervākyamadya; sabhāṁ kṣipraṁ tvamihājñāpayasva.
english: Duryodhana said: "Regarding the dice game, the custom (vyavahāra) was established by the ancients; in it, there is no transgression and no assault. Therefore, let Shakuni's words be agreeable today. You should quickly command this assembly."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 12. Duryodhana said: "Regarding the dice game, the custom (vyavahāra) was established by the ancients; in it, there is no transgression and no assault. Therefore, let Shakuni's words be agreeable today. You should quickly command this assembly."

sanskrit: स्वर्गद्वारं दीव्यतां नो विशिष्टं तद्वर्तिनां चापि तथैव युक्तम् ।भवेदेवं ह्यात्मना तुल्यमेव दुरोदरं पाण्डवैस्त्वं कुरुष्व ॥१३॥
transliteration: svargadvāraṁ dīvyatāṁ no viśiṣṭaṁ; tadvartināṁ cāpi tathaiva yuktam ,bhavedevaṁ hyātmanā tulyameva; durodaraṁ pāṇḍavaistvaṁ kuruṣva.
english: For us, the gate to heaven is truly eminent for those who engage in gambling. And for those residing there (in heaven), it is likewise suitable. Therefore, you should arrange this dice game with the Pāṇḍavas, with a stake equivalent to one's very self (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 13. For us, the gate to heaven is truly eminent for those who engage in gambling. And for those residing there (in heaven), it is likewise suitable. Therefore, you should arrange this dice game with the Pāṇḍavas, with a stake equivalent to one's very self (ātman).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।वाक्यं न मे रोचते यत्त्वयोक्तं यत्ते प्रियं तत्क्रियतां नरेन्द्र ।पश्चात्तप्स्यसे तदुपाक्रम्य वाक्यं न हीदृशं भावि वचो हि धर्म्यम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,vākyaṁ na me rocate yattvayoktaṁ; yatte priyaṁ tatkriyatāṁ narendra ,paścāttapsyase tadupākramya vākyaṁ; na hīdṛśaṁ bhāvi vaco hi dharmyam.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "The words you have spoken do not please me. O King, let whatever is dear to you be done. However, having embarked upon that course of action, you will surely repent later. For, indeed, such speech (vacaḥ) is not in accordance with (dharmya) natural law (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 14. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "The words you have spoken do not please me. O King, let whatever is dear to you be done. However, having embarked upon that course of action, you will surely repent later. For, indeed, such speech (vacaḥ) is not in accordance with (dharmya) natural law (dharma)."

sanskrit: दृष्टं ह्येतद्विदुरेणैवमेव सर्वं पूर्वं बुद्धिविद्यानुगेन ।तदेवैतदवशस्याभ्युपैति महद्भयं क्षत्रियबीजघाति ॥१५॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭaṁ hyetadvidureṇaivameva; sarvaṁ pūrvaṁ buddhividyānugena ,tadevaitadavaśasyābhyupaiti; mahadbhayaṁ kṣatriyabījaghāti.
english: Indeed, all this was foreseen by Vidura, who adheres to wisdom and knowledge, in precisely this manner. And precisely this great fear (mahat bhayam), which destroys the very seed of Kṣatriyas, will befall the one who is powerless.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 15. Indeed, all this was foreseen by Vidura, who adheres to wisdom and knowledge, in precisely this manner. And precisely this great fear (mahat bhayam), which destroys the very seed of Kṣatriyas, will befall the one who is powerless.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा धृतराष्ट्रो मनीषी दैवं मत्वा परमं दुस्तरं च ।शशासोच्चैः पुरुषान्पुत्रवाक्ये स्थितो राजा दैवसंमूढचेताः ॥१६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā dhṛtarāṣṭro manīṣī; daivaṁ matvā paramaṁ dustaraṁ ca ,śaśāsoccaiḥ puruṣānputravākye; sthito rājā daivasaṁmūḍhacetāḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having spoken thus, King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the thoughtful one, considering destiny (daiva) to be supreme and insurmountable, became bewildered in mind by that very destiny (daiva). Yet, abiding by his son's request, he loudly commanded his men (to act)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 16. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having spoken thus, King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the thoughtful one, considering destiny (daiva) to be supreme and insurmountable, became bewildered in mind by that very destiny (daiva). Yet, abiding by his son's request, he loudly commanded his men (to act)."

sanskrit: सहस्रस्तम्भां हेमवैडूर्यचित्रां शतद्वारां तोरणस्फाटिशृङ्गाम् ।सभामग्र्यां क्रोशमात्रायतां मे तद्विस्तारामाशु कुर्वन्तु युक्ताः ॥१७॥
transliteration: sahasrastambhāṁ hemavaiḍūryacitrāṁ; śatadvārāṁ toraṇasphāṭiśṛṅgām ,sabhāmagryāṁ krośamātrāyatāṁ me; tadvistārāmāśu kurvantu yuktāḥ.
english: Let the diligent workers quickly construct for me that splendid hall, which should have a thousand pillars, be adorned with gold and cat's eye gems, have a hundred doors, crystal archways, and measure a krośa in length and the same in width.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 17. Let the diligent workers quickly construct for me that splendid hall, which should have a thousand pillars, be adorned with gold and cat's eye gems, have a hundred doors, crystal archways, and measure a krośa in length and the same in width.

sanskrit: श्रुत्वा तस्य त्वरिता निर्विशङ्काः प्राज्ञा दक्षास्तां तथा चक्रुराशु ।सर्वद्रव्याण्युपजह्रुः सभायां सहस्रशः शिल्पिनश्चापि युक्ताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: śrutvā tasya tvaritā nirviśaṅkāḥ; prājñā dakṣāstāṁ tathā cakrurāśu ,sarvadravyāṇyupajahruḥ sabhāyāṁ; sahasraśaḥ śilpinaścāpi yuktāḥ.
english: Upon hearing his command, the swift, fearless, wise, and skilled ones quickly constructed it accordingly. They brought all materials into the hall, and thousands of diligent artisans (śilpin) also joined in.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 18. Upon hearing his command, the swift, fearless, wise, and skilled ones quickly constructed it accordingly. They brought all materials into the hall, and thousands of diligent artisans (śilpin) also joined in.

sanskrit: कालेनाल्पेनाथ निष्ठां गतां तां सभां रम्यां बहुरत्नां विचित्राम् ।चित्रैर्हैमैरासनैरभ्युपेतामाचख्युस्ते तस्य राज्ञः प्रतीताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: kālenālpenātha niṣṭhāṁ gatāṁ tāṁ; sabhāṁ ramyāṁ bahuratnāṁ vicitrām ,citrairhaimairāsanairabhyupetā;mācakhyuste tasya rājñaḥ pratītāḥ.
english: Then, in a short time, they, feeling satisfied, reported to that king that the beautiful hall, rich in numerous gems, wonderfully adorned, and furnished with various golden seats, had reached completion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 19. Then, in a short time, they, feeling satisfied, reported to that king that the beautiful hall, rich in numerous gems, wonderfully adorned, and furnished with various golden seats, had reached completion.

sanskrit: ततो विद्वान्विदुरं मन्त्रिमुख्यमुवाचेदं धृतराष्ट्रो नरेन्द्रः ।युधिष्ठिरं राजपुत्रं हि गत्वा मद्वाक्येन क्षिप्रमिहानयस्व ॥२०॥
transliteration: tato vidvānviduraṁ mantrimukhya;muvācedaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭro narendraḥ ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ rājaputraṁ hi gatvā; madvākyena kṣipramihānayasva.
english: Then the wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra said this to Vidura, the chief minister: 'Go to Prince Yudhiṣṭhira, indeed, and quickly bring him here at my command.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 20. Then the wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra said this to Vidura, the chief minister: 'Go to Prince Yudhiṣṭhira, indeed, and quickly bring him here at my command.'

sanskrit: सभेयं मे बहुरत्ना विचित्रा शय्यासनैरुपपन्ना महार्हैः ।सा दृश्यतां भ्रातृभिः सार्धमेत्य सुहृद्द्यूतं वर्ततामत्र चेति ॥२१॥
transliteration: sabheyaṁ me bahuratnā vicitrā; śayyāsanairupapannā mahārhaiḥ ,sā dṛśyatāṁ bhrātṛbhiḥ sārdhametya; suhṛddyūtaṁ vartatāmatra ceti.
english: This assembly hall of mine, adorned with many jewels, wonderfully diverse, and furnished with very valuable couches and seats, should be seen. So, come here with your brothers, and let a friendly game of dice take place.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 21. This assembly hall of mine, adorned with many jewels, wonderfully diverse, and furnished with very valuable couches and seats, should be seen. So, come here with your brothers, and let a friendly game of dice take place.

sanskrit: मतमाज्ञाय पुत्रस्य धृतराष्ट्रो नराधिपः ।मत्वा च दुस्तरं दैवमेतद्राजा चकार ह ॥२२॥
transliteration: matamājñāya putrasya dhṛtarāṣṭro narādhipaḥ ,matvā ca dustaraṁ daivametadrājā cakāra ha.
english: King Dhritarashtra, having understood his son's opinion and considering this destiny (daivam) to be insurmountable, indeed acted accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 22. King Dhritarashtra, having understood his son's opinion and considering this destiny (daivam) to be insurmountable, indeed acted accordingly.

sanskrit: अन्यायेन तथोक्तस्तु विदुरो विदुषां वरः ।नाभ्यनन्दद्वचो भ्रातुर्वचनं चेदमब्रवीत् ॥२३॥
transliteration: anyāyena tathoktastu viduro viduṣāṁ varaḥ ,nābhyanandadvaco bhrāturvacanaṁ cedamabravīt.
english: But Vidura, the best among the wise, thus addressed unjustly, did not approve of his brother's words, and he spoke the following.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 23. But Vidura, the best among the wise, thus addressed unjustly, did not approve of his brother's words, and he spoke the following.

sanskrit: नाभिनन्दामि नृपते प्रैषमेतं मैवं कृथाः कुलनाशाद्बिभेमि ।पुत्रैर्भिन्नैः कलहस्ते ध्रुवं स्यादेतच्छङ्के द्यूतकृते नरेन्द्र ॥२४॥
transliteration: nābhinandāmi nṛpate praiṣametaṁ; maivaṁ kṛthāḥ kulanāśādbibhemi ,putrairbhinnaiḥ kalahaste dhruvaṁ syā;detacchaṅke dyūtakṛte narendra.
english: O King (nṛpate), I do not approve of this command. Do not do such a thing. I fear the destruction of the family. I apprehend that a quarrel will certainly arise for you from divided sons because of this gambling (dyūtakṛte), O King (narendra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 24. O King (nṛpate), I do not approve of this command. Do not do such a thing. I fear the destruction of the family. I apprehend that a quarrel will certainly arise for you from divided sons because of this gambling (dyūtakṛte), O King (narendra).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।नेह क्षत्तः कलहस्तप्स्यते मां न चेद्दैवं प्रतिलोमं भविष्यत् ।धात्रा तु दिष्टस्य वशे किलेदं सर्वं जगच्चेष्टति न स्वतन्त्रम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,neha kṣattaḥ kalahastapsyate māṁ; na ceddaivaṁ pratilomaṁ bhaviṣyat ,dhātrā tu diṣṭasya vaśe kiledaṁ; sarvaṁ jagacceṣṭati na svatantram.
english: Dhritarashtra said, "O Vidura, this quarrel would not torment me if destiny (daiva) were not adverse. Indeed, this entire world acts under the control of destiny (diṣṭa) by the ordainer (dhātṛ), and not independently."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 25. Dhritarashtra said, "O Vidura, this quarrel would not torment me if destiny (daiva) were not adverse. Indeed, this entire world acts under the control of destiny (diṣṭa) by the ordainer (dhātṛ), and not independently."

sanskrit: तदद्य विदुर प्राप्य राजानं मम शासनात् ।क्षिप्रमानय दुर्धर्षं कुन्तीपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tadadya vidura prāpya rājānaṁ mama śāsanāt ,kṣipramānaya durdharṣaṁ kuntīputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: Therefore, O Vidura, today, by my command, having approached King Yudhishthira, quickly bring the unconquerable son of Kunti.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 51 Verse 26. Therefore, O Vidura, today, by my command, having approached King Yudhishthira, quickly bring the unconquerable son of Kunti.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततः प्रायाद्विदुरोऽश्वैरुदारैर्महाजवैर्बलिभिः साधुदान्तैः ।बलान्नियुक्तो धृतराष्ट्रेण राज्ञा मनीषिणां पाण्डवानां सकाशम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tataḥ prāyādviduro'śvairudārai;rmahājavairbalibhiḥ sādhudāntaiḥ ,balānniyukto dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa rājñā; manīṣiṇāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ sakāśam.
english: Vaishampayana said, "Thereupon, Vidura departed on excellent, very swift, strong, and well-tamed horses. He had been compelled by King Dhritarashtra to go to the wise Pandavas."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 1. Vaishampayana said, "Thereupon, Vidura departed on excellent, very swift, strong, and well-tamed horses. He had been compelled by King Dhritarashtra to go to the wise Pandavas."

sanskrit: सोऽभिपत्य तदध्वानमासाद्य नृपतेः पुरम् ।प्रविवेश महाबुद्धिः पूज्यमानो द्विजातिभिः ॥२॥
transliteration: so'bhipatya tadadhvānamāsādya nṛpateḥ puram ,praviveśa mahābuddhiḥ pūjyamāno dvijātibhiḥ.
english: Having traversed that journey and reached the city of the king (Pāṇḍavas' residence), the greatly intelligent Vidura entered, being honored by the twice-born (dvijāti) persons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 2. Having traversed that journey and reached the city of the king (Pāṇḍavas' residence), the greatly intelligent Vidura entered, being honored by the twice-born (dvijāti) persons.

sanskrit: स राजगृहमासाद्य कुबेरभवनोपमम् ।अभ्यगच्छत धर्मात्मा धर्मपुत्रं युधिष्ठिरम् ॥३॥
transliteration: sa rājagṛhamāsādya kuberabhavanopamam ,abhyagacchata dharmātmā dharmaputraṁ yudhiṣṭhiram.
english: The righteous Vidura (dharmātmā), having arrived at Rājagṛha, a city resembling the abode of Kubera, went to meet Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharmaputra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 3. The righteous Vidura (dharmātmā), having arrived at Rājagṛha, a city resembling the abode of Kubera, went to meet Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Dharma (dharmaputra).

sanskrit: तं वै राजा सत्यधृतिर्महात्मा अजातशत्रुर्विदुरं यथावत् ।पूजापूर्वं प्रतिगृह्याजमीढस्ततोऽपृच्छद्धृतराष्ट्रं सपुत्रम् ॥४॥
transliteration: taṁ vai rājā satyadhṛtirmahātmā; ajātaśatrurviduraṁ yathāvat ,pūjāpūrvaṁ pratigṛhyājamīḍha;stato'pṛcchaddhṛtarāṣṭraṁ saputram.
english: King Yudhiṣṭhira, who was firm in truth, great-souled (mahātmā), and without enemies, the scion of Ajāmīḍha, duly received Vidura with proper honors. Thereafter, he inquired about Dhṛtarāṣṭra and his sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 4. King Yudhiṣṭhira, who was firm in truth, great-souled (mahātmā), and without enemies, the scion of Ajāmīḍha, duly received Vidura with proper honors. Thereafter, he inquired about Dhṛtarāṣṭra and his sons.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।विज्ञायते ते मनसो न प्रहर्षः कच्चित्क्षत्तः कुशलेनागतोऽसि ।कच्चित्पुत्राः स्थविरस्यानुलोमा वशानुगाश्चापि विशोऽपि कच्चित् ॥५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,vijñāyate te manaso na praharṣaḥ; kaccitkṣattaḥ kuśalenāgato'si ,kaccitputrāḥ sthavirasyānulomā; vaśānugāścāpi viśo'pi kaccit.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'It seems there is no joy (praharsa) in your mind. O Vidura (kṣattṛ), I hope you have arrived in good health? And are the old king's sons compliant and obedient? Are the subjects also following orders?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 5. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'It seems there is no joy (praharsa) in your mind. O Vidura (kṣattṛ), I hope you have arrived in good health? And are the old king's sons compliant and obedient? Are the subjects also following orders?'

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।राजा महात्मा कुशली सपुत्र आस्ते वृतो ज्ञातिभिरिन्द्रकल्पैः ।प्रीतो राजन्पुत्रगणैर्विनीतैर्विशोक एवात्मरतिर्दृढात्मा ॥६॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,rājā mahātmā kuśalī saputra; āste vṛto jñātibhirindrakalpaiḥ ,prīto rājanputragaṇairvinītai;rviśoka evātmaratirdṛḍhātmā.
english: Vidura said: 'O King (rājan), the great-souled (mahātmā) king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), along with his sons, is well. He is surrounded by kinsmen who are like Indra, and he is pleased by his well-behaved multitude of sons. He is indeed free from sorrow (viśoka), delights in his own self (ātmarati), and is firm-minded (dṛḍhātmā).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 6. Vidura said: 'O King (rājan), the great-souled (mahātmā) king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra), along with his sons, is well. He is surrounded by kinsmen who are like Indra, and he is pleased by his well-behaved multitude of sons. He is indeed free from sorrow (viśoka), delights in his own self (ātmarati), and is firm-minded (dṛḍhātmā).'

sanskrit: इदं तु त्वां कुरुराजोऽभ्युवाच पूर्वं पृष्ट्वा कुशलं चाव्ययं च ।इयं सभा त्वत्सभातुल्यरूपा भ्रातॄणां ते पश्य तामेत्य पुत्र ॥७॥
transliteration: idaṁ tu tvāṁ kururājo'bhyuvāca; pūrvaṁ pṛṣṭvā kuśalaṁ cāvyayaṁ ca ,iyaṁ sabhā tvatsabhātulyarūpā; bhrātṝṇāṁ te paśya tāmetya putra.
english: But the Kuru King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) first inquired about your welfare and stability, then said this to you: 'This assembly hall, which belongs to your brothers, is similar in form to your assembly hall. Go and see it, my son.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 7. But the Kuru King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) first inquired about your welfare and stability, then said this to you: 'This assembly hall, which belongs to your brothers, is similar in form to your assembly hall. Go and see it, my son.'

sanskrit: समागम्य भ्रातृभिः पार्थ तस्यां सुहृद्द्यूतं क्रियतां रम्यतां च ।प्रीयामहे भवतः संगमेन समागताः कुरवश्चैव सर्वे ॥८॥
transliteration: samāgamya bhrātṛbhiḥ pārtha tasyāṁ; suhṛddyūtaṁ kriyatāṁ ramyatāṁ ca ,prīyāmahe bhavataḥ saṁgamena; samāgatāḥ kuravaścaiva sarve.
english: O Pārtha, having gathered with your brothers in that hall, let a friendly game of dice be played, and let there be enjoyment. We are all the Kurus who have assembled, and we are pleased by your presence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 8. O Pārtha, having gathered with your brothers in that hall, let a friendly game of dice be played, and let there be enjoyment. We are all the Kurus who have assembled, and we are pleased by your presence.

sanskrit: दुरोदरा विहिता ये तु तत्र महात्मना धृतराष्ट्रेण राज्ञा ।तान्द्रक्ष्यसे कितवान्संनिविष्टानित्यागतोऽहं नृपते तज्जुषस्व ॥९॥
transliteration: durodarā vihitā ye tu tatra; mahātmanā dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa rājñā ,tāndrakṣyase kitavānsaṁniviṣṭā;nityāgato'haṁ nṛpate tajjuṣasva.
english: Indeed, the great-souled (ātman) King Dhṛtarāṣṭra has arranged for dice-players there. You will see those gamblers seated. So, I have come, O King; please partake in this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 9. Indeed, the great-souled (ātman) King Dhṛtarāṣṭra has arranged for dice-players there. You will see those gamblers seated. So, I have come, O King; please partake in this.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।द्यूते क्षत्तः कलहो विद्यते नः को वै द्यूतं रोचयेद्बुध्यमानः ।किं वा भवान्मन्यते युक्तरूपं भवद्वाक्ये सर्व एव स्थिताः स्म ॥१०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,dyūte kṣattaḥ kalaho vidyate naḥ; ko vai dyūtaṁ rocayedbudhyamānaḥ ,kiṁ vā bhavānmanyate yuktarūpaṁ; bhavadvākye sarva eva sthitāḥ sma.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O charioteer, gambling always leads to disputes for us. Who, if they are discerning, would indeed take pleasure in gambling? So, what do you, sir, consider appropriate? We are all ready to abide by your word.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 10. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O charioteer, gambling always leads to disputes for us. Who, if they are discerning, would indeed take pleasure in gambling? So, what do you, sir, consider appropriate? We are all ready to abide by your word.'

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।जानाम्यहं द्यूतमनर्थमूलं कृतश्च यत्नोऽस्य मया निवारणे ।राजा तु मां प्राहिणोत्त्वत्सकाशं श्रुत्वा विद्वञ्श्रेय इहाचरस्व ॥११॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,jānāmyahaṁ dyūtamanarthamūlaṁ; kṛtaśca yatno'sya mayā nivāraṇe ,rājā tu māṁ prāhiṇottvatsakāśaṁ; śrutvā vidvañśreya ihācarasva.
english: Vidura said, "I know that gambling is the root of misfortune, and I made an effort to prevent it. However, the king sent me to you after hearing [about the situation]. O wise one, please do what is auspicious here."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 11. Vidura said, "I know that gambling is the root of misfortune, and I made an effort to prevent it. However, the king sent me to you after hearing [about the situation]. O wise one, please do what is auspicious here."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।के तत्रान्ये कितवा दीव्यमाना विना राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रैः ।पृच्छामि त्वां विदुर ब्रूहि नस्तान्यैर्दीव्यामः शतशः संनिपत्य ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ke tatrānye kitavā dīvyamānā; vinā rājño dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putraiḥ ,pṛcchāmi tvāṁ vidura brūhi nastā;nyairdīvyāmaḥ śataśaḥ saṁnipatya.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said, "Who else among the gamblers are playing there, besides the sons of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra? Tell us, Vidura, whom shall we play against, gathering in vast numbers?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 12. Yudhiṣṭhira said, "Who else among the gamblers are playing there, besides the sons of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra? Tell us, Vidura, whom shall we play against, gathering in vast numbers?"

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।गान्धारराजः शकुनिर्विशां पते राजातिदेवी कृतहस्तो मताक्षः ।विविंशतिश्चित्रसेनश्च राजा सत्यव्रतः पुरुमित्रो जयश्च ॥१३॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,gāndhārarājaḥ śakunirviśāṁ pate; rājātidevī kṛtahasto matākṣaḥ ,viviṁśatiścitrasenaśca rājā; satyavrataḥ purumitro jayaśca.
english: Vidura said, "O lord of the people, King Śakuni, the ruler of Gāndhāra, is a supremely skilled gambler, expert, and cunning with dice. Also present are Viviṃśati, Citrasena, King Satyavrata, Purumitra, and Jaya."
