search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 13. Vidura said, "O lord of the people, King Śakuni, the ruler of Gāndhāra, is a supremely skilled gambler, expert, and cunning with dice. Also present are Viviṃśati, Citrasena, King Satyavrata, Purumitra, and Jaya."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।महाभयाः कितवाः संनिविष्टा मायोपधा देवितारोऽत्र सन्ति ।धात्रा तु दिष्टस्य वशे किलेदं नादेवनं कितवैरद्य तैर्मे ॥१४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,mahābhayāḥ kitavāḥ saṁniviṣṭā; māyopadhā devitāro'tra santi ,dhātrā tu diṣṭasya vaśe kiledaṁ; nādevanaṁ kitavairadya tairme.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said, "These formidable and deceitful gamblers have assembled here. Yet, it is said that this situation is under the control of destiny (diṣṭa), ordained by the Creator. Therefore, there can be no avoiding gambling with these tricksters for me today."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 14. Yudhiṣṭhira said, "These formidable and deceitful gamblers have assembled here. Yet, it is said that this situation is under the control of destiny (diṣṭa), ordained by the Creator. Therefore, there can be no avoiding gambling with these tricksters for me today."

sanskrit: नाहं राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रस्य शासनान्न गन्तुमिच्छामि कवे दुरोदरम् ।इष्टो हि पुत्रस्य पिता सदैव तदस्मि कर्ता विदुरात्थ मां यथा ॥१५॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ rājño dhṛtarāṣṭrasya śāsanā;nna gantumicchāmi kave durodaram ,iṣṭo hi putrasya pitā sadaiva; tadasmi kartā vidurāttha māṁ yathā.
english: O wise one (kavi), I do not wish to go to the gambling match by King Dhṛtarāṣṭra's command. Indeed, a father is always dear to his son. Therefore, I will act accordingly, just as you, Vidura, instruct me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 15. O wise one (kavi), I do not wish to go to the gambling match by King Dhṛtarāṣṭra's command. Indeed, a father is always dear to his son. Therefore, I will act accordingly, just as you, Vidura, instruct me.

sanskrit: न चाकामः शकुनिना देविताहं न चेन्मां धृष्णुराह्वयिता सभायाम् ।आहूतोऽहं न निवर्ते कदाचित्तदाहितं शाश्वतं वै व्रतं मे ॥१६॥
transliteration: na cākāmaḥ śakuninā devitāhaṁ; na cenmāṁ dhṛṣṇurāhvayitā sabhāyām ,āhūto'haṁ na nivarte kadāci;ttadāhitaṁ śāśvataṁ vai vrataṁ me.
english: I am not unwilling to be challenged by Śakuni, nor will I refuse if that insolent one (Duryodhana) challenges me in the assembly. Once summoned, I never retreat. That is indeed my eternal vow (vrata).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 16. I am not unwilling to be challenged by Śakuni, nor will I refuse if that insolent one (Duryodhana) challenges me in the assembly. Once summoned, I never retreat. That is indeed my eternal vow (vrata).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा विदुरं धर्मराजः प्रायात्रिकं सर्वमाज्ञाप्य तूर्णम् ।प्रायाच्छ्वोभूते सगणः सानुयात्रः सह स्त्रीभिर्द्रौपदीमादिकृत्वा ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā viduraṁ dharmarājaḥ; prāyātrikaṁ sarvamājñāpya tūrṇam ,prāyācchvobhūte sagaṇaḥ sānuyātraḥ; saha strībhirdraupadīmādikṛtvā.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having thus spoken to Vidura, Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of (dharma), swiftly commanded all the preparations for the journey. On the following day, he set out with his retinue and followers, accompanied by the women, including Draupadī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 17. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having thus spoken to Vidura, Yudhiṣṭhira, the King of (dharma), swiftly commanded all the preparations for the journey. On the following day, he set out with his retinue and followers, accompanied by the women, including Draupadī.

sanskrit: दैवं प्रज्ञां तु मुष्णाति तेजश्चक्षुरिवापतत् ।धातुश्च वशमन्वेति पाशैरिव नरः सितः ॥१८॥
transliteration: daivaṁ prajñāṁ tu muṣṇāti tejaścakṣurivāpatat ,dhātuśca vaśamanveti pāśairiva naraḥ sitaḥ.
english: Fate, however, steals one's wisdom, just as the brilliance of the eye fades away. And man submits to the will of the creator (dhātu), like a person bound with ropes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 18. Fate, however, steals one's wisdom, just as the brilliance of the eye fades away. And man submits to the will of the creator (dhātu), like a person bound with ropes.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा प्रययौ राजा सह क्षत्त्रा युधिष्ठिरः ।अमृष्यमाणस्तत्पार्थः समाह्वानमरिंदमः ॥१९॥
transliteration: ityuktvā prayayau rājā saha kṣattrā yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,amṛṣyamāṇastatpārthaḥ samāhvānamariṁdamaḥ.
english: Having said this, King Yudhishthira, the subduer of enemies, went forth with Vidura, unable to tolerate that challenge. He, the son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), could not endure that summons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 19. Having said this, King Yudhishthira, the subduer of enemies, went forth with Vidura, unable to tolerate that challenge. He, the son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), could not endure that summons.

sanskrit: बाह्लिकेन रथं दत्तमास्थाय परवीरहा ।परिच्छन्नो ययौ पार्थो भ्रातृभिः सह पाण्डवः ॥२०॥
transliteration: bāhlikena rathaṁ dattamāsthāya paravīrahā ,paricchanno yayau pārtho bhrātṛbhiḥ saha pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Having mounted the chariot given by Bahlika, the son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), the son of Pandu (Pāṇḍava), the slayer of hostile heroes (paravīrahā), went forth well-covered/disguised along with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 20. Having mounted the chariot given by Bahlika, the son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), the son of Pandu (Pāṇḍava), the slayer of hostile heroes (paravīrahā), went forth well-covered/disguised along with his brothers.

sanskrit: राजश्रिया दीप्यमानो ययौ ब्रह्मपुरःसरः ।धृतराष्ट्रेण चाहूतः कालस्य समयेन च ॥२१॥
transliteration: rājaśriyā dīpyamāno yayau brahmapuraḥsaraḥ ,dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa cāhūtaḥ kālasya samayena ca.
english: Shining with royal splendor, he went forth, preceded by Brahmins, summoned both by Dhritarashtra and by the dictates of time or destiny.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 21. Shining with royal splendor, he went forth, preceded by Brahmins, summoned both by Dhritarashtra and by the dictates of time or destiny.

sanskrit: स हास्तिनपुरं गत्वा धृतराष्ट्रगृहं ययौ ।समियाय च धर्मात्मा धृतराष्ट्रेण पाण्डवः ॥२२॥
transliteration: sa hāstinapuraṁ gatvā dhṛtarāṣṭragṛhaṁ yayau ,samiyāya ca dharmātmā dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Having gone to Hastinapura, that righteous-souled (dharmātmā) son of Pandu (Pāṇḍava) then went to Dhritarashtra's house and met with Dhritarashtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 22. Having gone to Hastinapura, that righteous-souled (dharmātmā) son of Pandu (Pāṇḍava) then went to Dhritarashtra's house and met with Dhritarashtra.

sanskrit: तथा द्रोणेन भीष्मेण कर्णेन च कृपेण च ।समियाय यथान्यायं द्रौणिना च विभुः सह ॥२३॥
transliteration: tathā droṇena bhīṣmeṇa karṇena ca kṛpeṇa ca ,samiyāya yathānyāyaṁ drauṇinā ca vibhuḥ saha.
english: Thus, the powerful one (Duryodhana) duly met with Drona, Bhishma, Karna, Kripa, and Drona's son (Ashvatthama).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 23. Thus, the powerful one (Duryodhana) duly met with Drona, Bhishma, Karna, Kripa, and Drona's son (Ashvatthama).

sanskrit: समेत्य च महाबाहुः सोमदत्तेन चैव ह ।दुर्योधनेन शल्येन सौबलेन च वीर्यवान् ॥२४॥
transliteration: sametya ca mahābāhuḥ somadattena caiva ha ,duryodhanena śalyena saubalena ca vīryavān.
english: And the mighty-armed and valorous one (Duryodhana), having met with Somadatta, and indeed with Duryodhana, Shalya, and Saubala (Shakuni)...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 24. And the mighty-armed and valorous one (Duryodhana), having met with Somadatta, and indeed with Duryodhana, Shalya, and Saubala (Shakuni)...

sanskrit: ये चान्ये तत्र राजानः पूर्वमेव समागताः ।जयद्रथेन च तथा कुरुभिश्चापि सर्वशः ॥२५॥
transliteration: ye cānye tatra rājānaḥ pūrvameva samāgatāḥ ,jayadrathena ca tathā kurubhiścāpi sarvaśaḥ.
english: And whichever other kings had already assembled there, (he met) also with Jayadratha and completely with the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 25. And whichever other kings had already assembled there, (he met) also with Jayadratha and completely with the Kurus.

sanskrit: ततः सर्वैर्महाबाहुर्भ्रातृभिः परिवारितः ।प्रविवेश गृहं राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रस्य धीमतः ॥२६॥
transliteration: tataḥ sarvairmahābāhurbhrātṛbhiḥ parivāritaḥ ,praviveśa gṛhaṁ rājño dhṛtarāṣṭrasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Then, the mighty-armed one (Duryodhana), surrounded by all his brothers, entered the house of the wise King Dhritarashtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 26. Then, the mighty-armed one (Duryodhana), surrounded by all his brothers, entered the house of the wise King Dhritarashtra.

sanskrit: ददर्श तत्र गान्धारीं देवीं पतिमनुव्रताम् ।स्नुषाभिः संवृतां शश्वत्ताराभिरिव रोहिणीम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: dadarśa tatra gāndhārīṁ devīṁ patimanuvratām ,snuṣābhiḥ saṁvṛtāṁ śaśvattārābhiriva rohiṇīm.
english: There he saw Gāndhārī, the queen-like lady devoted to her husband, who was constantly surrounded by her daughters-in-law, just as Rohiṇī is surrounded by stars.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 27. There he saw Gāndhārī, the queen-like lady devoted to her husband, who was constantly surrounded by her daughters-in-law, just as Rohiṇī is surrounded by stars.

sanskrit: अभिवाद्य स गान्धारीं तया च प्रतिनन्दितः ।ददर्श पितरं वृद्धं प्रज्ञाचक्षुषमीश्वरम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: abhivādya sa gāndhārīṁ tayā ca pratinanditaḥ ,dadarśa pitaraṁ vṛddhaṁ prajñācakṣuṣamīśvaram.
english: After saluting Gāndhārī and being welcomed by her, he saw his aged father, the mighty lord Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose eyes were wisdom (prajñācakṣuṣam).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 28. After saluting Gāndhārī and being welcomed by her, he saw his aged father, the mighty lord Dhṛtarāṣṭra, whose eyes were wisdom (prajñācakṣuṣam).

sanskrit: राज्ञा मूर्धन्युपाघ्रातास्ते च कौरवनन्दनाः ।चत्वारः पाण्डवा राजन्भीमसेनपुरोगमाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: rājñā mūrdhanyupāghrātāste ca kauravanandanāḥ ,catvāraḥ pāṇḍavā rājanbhīmasenapurogamāḥ.
english: O King, the king then kissed the heads of those four Pāṇḍavas, the delight of the Kurus (kauravanandanāḥ), who were led by Bhīmasena.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 29. O King, the king then kissed the heads of those four Pāṇḍavas, the delight of the Kurus (kauravanandanāḥ), who were led by Bhīmasena.

sanskrit: ततो हर्षः समभवत्कौरवाणां विशां पते ।तान्दृष्ट्वा पुरुषव्याघ्रान्पाण्डवान्प्रियदर्शनान् ॥३०॥
transliteration: tato harṣaḥ samabhavatkauravāṇāṁ viśāṁ pate ,tāndṛṣṭvā puruṣavyāghrānpāṇḍavānpriyadarśanān.
english: Then, O lord of the people (viśām pate), great joy arose among the Kurus (kauravāṇām) after they had seen those Pāṇḍavas of pleasing appearance, who were like tigers among men (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 30. Then, O lord of the people (viśām pate), great joy arose among the Kurus (kauravāṇām) after they had seen those Pāṇḍavas of pleasing appearance, who were like tigers among men (puruṣa).

sanskrit: विविशुस्तेऽभ्यनुज्ञाता रत्नवन्ति गृहाण्यथ ।ददृशुश्चोपयातास्तान्द्रौपदीप्रमुखाः स्त्रियः ॥३१॥
transliteration: viviśuste'bhyanujñātā ratnavanti gṛhāṇyatha ,dadṛśuścopayātāstāndraupadīpramukhāḥ striyaḥ.
english: Then, having been granted permission, they entered the jewel-filled palaces. And the women, led by Draupadi, having arrived, saw them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 31. Then, having been granted permission, they entered the jewel-filled palaces. And the women, led by Draupadi, having arrived, saw them.

sanskrit: याज्ञसेन्याः परामृद्धिं दृष्ट्वा प्रज्वलितामिव ।स्नुषास्ता धृतराष्ट्रस्य नातिप्रमनसोऽभवन् ॥३२॥
transliteration: yājñasenyāḥ parāmṛddhiṁ dṛṣṭvā prajvalitāmiva ,snuṣāstā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya nātipramanaso'bhavan.
english: Having seen the supreme splendor of Draupadi, which was as if blazing, Dhritarashtra's daughters-in-law did not become very cheerful.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 32. Having seen the supreme splendor of Draupadi, which was as if blazing, Dhritarashtra's daughters-in-law did not become very cheerful.

sanskrit: ततस्ते पुरुषव्याघ्रा गत्वा स्त्रीभिस्तु संविदम् ।कृत्वा व्यायामपूर्वाणि कृत्यानि प्रतिकर्म च ॥३३॥
transliteration: tataste puruṣavyāghrā gatvā strībhistu saṁvidam ,kṛtvā vyāyāmapūrvāṇi kṛtyāni pratikarma ca.
english: Then those excellent men, after consulting with the women and having performed their duties, which included physical exercise, and also after attending to their personal grooming,
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 33. Then those excellent men, after consulting with the women and having performed their duties, which included physical exercise, and also after attending to their personal grooming,

sanskrit: ततः कृताह्निकाः सर्वे दिव्यचन्दनरूषिताः ।कल्याणमनसश्चैव ब्राह्मणान्स्वस्ति वाच्य च ॥३४॥
transliteration: tataḥ kṛtāhnikāḥ sarve divyacandanarūṣitāḥ ,kalyāṇamanasaścaiva brāhmaṇānsvasti vācya ca.
english: Then all of them, having completed their daily rites, smeared themselves with divine sandalwood paste, and with auspicious minds, they had Brahmins pronounce blessings.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 34. Then all of them, having completed their daily rites, smeared themselves with divine sandalwood paste, and with auspicious minds, they had Brahmins pronounce blessings.

sanskrit: मनोज्ञमशनं भुक्त्वा विविशुः शरणान्यथ ।उपगीयमाना नारीभिरस्वपन्कुरुनन्दनाः ॥३५॥
transliteration: manojñamaśanaṁ bhuktvā viviśuḥ śaraṇānyatha ,upagīyamānā nārībhirasvapankurunandanāḥ.
english: The sons of Kuru, having eaten a delicious meal, then entered their quarters. There, serenaded by women, they fell asleep.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 35. The sons of Kuru, having eaten a delicious meal, then entered their quarters. There, serenaded by women, they fell asleep.

sanskrit: जगाम तेषां सा रात्रिः पुण्या रतिविहारिणाम् ।स्तूयमानाश्च विश्रान्ताः काले निद्रामथात्यजन् ॥३६॥
transliteration: jagāma teṣāṁ sā rātriḥ puṇyā rativihāriṇām ,stūyamānāśca viśrāntāḥ kāle nidrāmathātyajan.
english: That auspicious night passed for them, who were enjoying themselves. Then, rested and being praised, they gave up sleep at the proper time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 36. That auspicious night passed for them, who were enjoying themselves. Then, rested and being praised, they gave up sleep at the proper time.

sanskrit: सुखोषितास्तां रजनीं प्रातः सर्वे कृताह्निकाः ।सभां रम्यां प्रविविशुः कितवैरभिसंवृताम् ॥३७॥
transliteration: sukhoṣitāstāṁ rajanīṁ prātaḥ sarve kṛtāhnikāḥ ,sabhāṁ ramyāṁ praviviśuḥ kitavairabhisaṁvṛtām.
english: Having slept comfortably through that night, all of them, in the morning after performing their daily rituals, entered the beautiful assembly hall which was surrounded by gamblers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 52 Verse 37. Having slept comfortably through that night, all of them, in the morning after performing their daily rituals, entered the beautiful assembly hall which was surrounded by gamblers.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।उपस्तीर्णा सभा राजन्रन्तुं चैते कृतक्षणाः ।अक्षानुप्त्वा देवनस्य समयोऽस्तु युधिष्ठिर ॥१॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,upastīrṇā sabhā rājanrantuṁ caite kṛtakṣaṇāḥ ,akṣānuptvā devanasya samayo'stu yudhiṣṭhira.
english: Shakuni said, "O King, the assembly hall is prepared, and these people are ready to play. Having cast the dice, let the time for gambling begin, O Yudhishthira!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 1. Shakuni said, "O King, the assembly hall is prepared, and these people are ready to play. Having cast the dice, let the time for gambling begin, O Yudhishthira!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।निकृतिर्देवनं पापं न क्षात्रोऽत्र पराक्रमः ।न च नीतिर्ध्रुवा राजन्किं त्वं द्यूतं प्रशंससि ॥२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,nikṛtirdevanaṁ pāpaṁ na kṣātro'tra parākramaḥ ,na ca nītirdhruvā rājankiṁ tvaṁ dyūtaṁ praśaṁsasi.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "Deception, gambling, and sin are not a warrior's valor (kṣātra) here, nor is it a constant policy (nīti), O King. Why do you praise gambling?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 2. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "Deception, gambling, and sin are not a warrior's valor (kṣātra) here, nor is it a constant policy (nīti), O King. Why do you praise gambling?"

sanskrit: न हि मानं प्रशंसन्ति निकृतौ कितवस्य ह ।शकुने मैव नो जैषीरमार्गेण नृशंसवत् ॥३॥
transliteration: na hi mānaṁ praśaṁsanti nikṛtau kitavasya ha ,śakune maiva no jaiṣīramārgeṇa nṛśaṁsavat.
english: Indeed, they do not praise honor (māna) in the deception practiced by a gambler. O Śakuni, do not ever defeat us by unfair means, like a cruel person!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 3. Indeed, they do not praise honor (māna) in the deception practiced by a gambler. O Śakuni, do not ever defeat us by unfair means, like a cruel person!

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।योऽन्वेति संख्यां निकृतौ विधिज्ञश्चेष्टास्वखिन्नः कितवोऽक्षजासु ।महामतिर्यश्च जानाति द्यूतं स वै सर्वं सहते प्रक्रियासु ॥४॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,yo'nveti saṁkhyāṁ nikṛtau vidhijña;śceṣṭāsvakhinnaḥ kitavo'kṣajāsu ,mahāmatiryaśca jānāti dyūtaṁ; sa vai sarvaṁ sahate prakriyāsu.
english: Śakuni said: "That gambler (kitava) who follows calculation in deception (nikṛti), is skilled in method (vidhijña), untiring in his maneuvers with dice (akṣajāsū ceṣṭāsu), and is of great intellect (mahāmati), he indeed endures everything in the processes (of gambling)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 4. Śakuni said: "That gambler (kitava) who follows calculation in deception (nikṛti), is skilled in method (vidhijña), untiring in his maneuvers with dice (akṣajāsū ceṣṭāsu), and is of great intellect (mahāmati), he indeed endures everything in the processes (of gambling)."

sanskrit: अक्षग्लहः सोऽभिभवेत्परं नस्तेनैव कालो भवतीदमात्थ ।दीव्यामहे पार्थिव मा विशङ्कां कुरुष्व पाणं च चिरं च मा कृथाः ॥५॥
transliteration: akṣaglahaḥ so'bhibhavetparaṁ na;stenaiva kālo bhavatīdamāttha ,dīvyāmahe pārthiva mā viśaṅkāṁ; kuruṣva pāṇaṁ ca ciraṁ ca mā kṛthāḥ.
english: That dice-gambler (akṣaglaha) will surely overpower us; thus, time has come (for action), as you have said. O King (pārthiva), let us play! Do not have any apprehension, and do not delay the stakes (pāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 5. That dice-gambler (akṣaglaha) will surely overpower us; thus, time has come (for action), as you have said. O King (pārthiva), let us play! Do not have any apprehension, and do not delay the stakes (pāṇa).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।एवमाहायमसितो देवलो मुनिसत्तमः ।इमानि लोकद्वाराणि यो वै संचरते सदा ॥६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,evamāhāyamasito devalo munisattamaḥ ,imāni lokadvārāṇi yo vai saṁcarate sadā.
english: Yudhishthira said: "Asita Devala, the foremost among sages, spoke thus concerning these gates to the various realms, through which one indeed always passes."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 6. Yudhishthira said: "Asita Devala, the foremost among sages, spoke thus concerning these gates to the various realms, through which one indeed always passes."

sanskrit: इदं वै देवनं पापं मायया कितवैः सह ।धर्मेण तु जयो युद्धे तत्परं साधु देवनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: idaṁ vai devanaṁ pāpaṁ māyayā kitavaiḥ saha ,dharmeṇa tu jayo yuddhe tatparaṁ sādhu devanam.
english: This gambling, with its deception (māyā) and association with cheats, is indeed sinful. However, victory in battle achieved through adherence to natural law (dharma) is truly superior; that is a righteous contest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 7. This gambling, with its deception (māyā) and association with cheats, is indeed sinful. However, victory in battle achieved through adherence to natural law (dharma) is truly superior; that is a righteous contest.

sanskrit: नार्या म्लेच्छन्ति भाषाभिर्मायया न चरन्त्युत ।अजिह्ममशठं युद्धमेतत्सत्पुरुषव्रतम् ॥८॥
transliteration: nāryā mlecchanti bhāṣābhirmāyayā na carantyuta ,ajihmamaśaṭhaṁ yuddhametatsatpuruṣavratam.
english: One should not speak in barbarian tongues through women, nor should one act with deception (māyā). This battle, free from crookedness and deceit, is indeed the vow of a noble person.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 8. One should not speak in barbarian tongues through women, nor should one act with deception (māyā). This battle, free from crookedness and deceit, is indeed the vow of a noble person.

sanskrit: शक्तितो ब्राह्मणान्वन्द्याञ्शिक्षितुं प्रयतामहे ।तद्वै वित्तं मातिदेवीर्मा जैषीः शकुने परम् ॥९॥
transliteration: śaktito brāhmaṇānvandyāñśikṣituṁ prayatāmahe ,tadvai vittaṁ mātidevīrmā jaiṣīḥ śakune param.
english: We strive, to the best of our ability, to be instructed by venerable Brahmins. That, indeed, is true wealth. You must not, through Shakuni, attempt to win any more precious stakes (mātidevīḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 9. We strive, to the best of our ability, to be instructed by venerable Brahmins. That, indeed, is true wealth. You must not, through Shakuni, attempt to win any more precious stakes (mātidevīḥ).

sanskrit: नाहं निकृत्या कामये सुखान्युत धनानि वा ।कितवस्याप्यनिकृतेर्वृत्तमेतन्न पूज्यते ॥१०॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ nikṛtyā kāmaye sukhānyuta dhanāni vā ,kitavasyāpyanikṛtervṛttametanna pūjyate.
english: I do not desire pleasures or wealth through deceit. For even the non-deceitful conduct of a gambler is not praised.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 10. I do not desire pleasures or wealth through deceit. For even the non-deceitful conduct of a gambler is not praised.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।श्रोत्रियोऽश्रोत्रियमुत निकृत्यैव युधिष्ठिर ।विद्वानविदुषोऽभ्येति नाहुस्तां निकृतिं जनाः ॥११॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,śrotriyo'śrotriyamuta nikṛtyaiva yudhiṣṭhira ,vidvānaviduṣo'bhyeti nāhustāṁ nikṛtiṁ janāḥ.
english: Śakuni said, 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, a learned Vedic scholar (śrotriya) surely overcomes a non-Vedic scholar (aśrotriya) by deceit. People do not call that deceit when a wise person (vidvān) triumphs over an ignorant one (aviduṣaḥ).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 11. Śakuni said, 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, a learned Vedic scholar (śrotriya) surely overcomes a non-Vedic scholar (aśrotriya) by deceit. People do not call that deceit when a wise person (vidvān) triumphs over an ignorant one (aviduṣaḥ).'

sanskrit: एवं त्वं मामिहाभ्येत्य निकृतिं यदि मन्यसे ।देवनाद्विनिवर्तस्व यदि ते विद्यते भयम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: evaṁ tvaṁ māmihābhyetya nikṛtiṁ yadi manyase ,devanādvinivartasva yadi te vidyate bhayam.
english: If you, having approached me here, consider this to be deceit, then desist from gambling if you have any fear.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 12. If you, having approached me here, consider this to be deceit, then desist from gambling if you have any fear.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।आहूतो न निवर्तेयमिति मे व्रतमाहितम् ।विधिश्च बलवान्राजन्दिष्टस्यास्मि वशे स्थितः ॥१३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,āhūto na nivarteyamiti me vratamāhitam ,vidhiśca balavānrājandiṣṭasyāsmi vaśe sthitaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said, 'I have taken this vow: "I will not turn back when summoned." And destiny (vidhi) is powerful, O King; I remain under the control of fate (diṣṭa).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 13. Yudhiṣṭhira said, 'I have taken this vow: "I will not turn back when summoned." And destiny (vidhi) is powerful, O King; I remain under the control of fate (diṣṭa).'

sanskrit: अस्मिन्समागमे केन देवनं मे भविष्यति ।प्रतिपाणश्च कोऽन्योऽस्ति ततो द्यूतं प्रवर्तताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: asminsamāgame kena devanaṁ me bhaviṣyati ,pratipāṇaśca ko'nyo'sti tato dyūtaṁ pravartatām.
english: In this gathering, by whom will my gambling be conducted? And who else will be my opponent? Therefore, let the game of dice (dyūta) commence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 14. In this gathering, by whom will my gambling be conducted? And who else will be my opponent? Therefore, let the game of dice (dyūta) commence.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।अहं दातास्मि रत्नानां धनानां च विशां पते ।मदर्थे देविता चायं शकुनिर्मातुलो मम ॥१५॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,ahaṁ dātāsmi ratnānāṁ dhanānāṁ ca viśāṁ pate ,madarthe devitā cāyaṁ śakunirmātulo mama.
english: Duryodhana said: 'O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), I am the giver of jewels and wealth. For my sake, this Śakuni, my maternal uncle (mātula), shall play the game.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 15. Duryodhana said: 'O Lord of the people (viśāṃ pate), I am the giver of jewels and wealth. For my sake, this Śakuni, my maternal uncle (mātula), shall play the game.'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अन्येनान्यस्य विषमं देवनं प्रतिभाति मे ।एतद्विद्वन्नुपादत्स्व काममेवं प्रवर्तताम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,anyenānyasya viṣamaṁ devanaṁ pratibhāti me ,etadvidvannupādatsva kāmamevaṁ pravartatām.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'It seems improper (viṣamam) to me for one person to play the game of dice (devanam) on behalf of another. Knowing this, accept it as you wish; let it proceed thus.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 16. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'It seems improper (viṣamam) to me for one person to play the game of dice (devanam) on behalf of another. Knowing this, accept it as you wish; let it proceed thus.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।उपोह्यमाने द्यूते तु राजानः सर्व एव ते ।धृतराष्ट्रं पुरस्कृत्य विविशुस्तां सभां ततः ॥१७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,upohyamāne dyūte tu rājānaḥ sarva eva te ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ puraskṛtya viviśustāṁ sabhāṁ tataḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'As the game of dice (dyūta) was being prepared, all those kings, led by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, then entered that assembly hall.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 17. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'As the game of dice (dyūta) was being prepared, all those kings, led by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, then entered that assembly hall.'

sanskrit: भीष्मो द्रोणः कृपश्चैव विदुरश्च महामतिः ।नातीवप्रीतमनसस्तेऽन्ववर्तन्त भारत ॥१८॥
transliteration: bhīṣmo droṇaḥ kṛpaścaiva viduraśca mahāmatiḥ ,nātīvaprītamanasaste'nvavartanta bhārata.
english: Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, and also Vidura, the greatly intelligent one, were not very pleased in their hearts; they followed along, O descendant of Bharata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 18. Bhishma, Drona, Kripa, and also Vidura, the greatly intelligent one, were not very pleased in their hearts; they followed along, O descendant of Bharata.

sanskrit: ते द्वंद्वशः पृथक्चैव सिंहग्रीवा महौजसः ।सिंहासनानि भूरीणि विचित्राणि च भेजिरे ॥१९॥
transliteration: te dvaṁdvaśaḥ pṛthakcaiva siṁhagrīvā mahaujasaḥ ,siṁhāsanāni bhūrīṇi vicitrāṇi ca bhejire.
english: Those mighty ones, with necks like lions, sat on numerous and varied thrones, both in pairs and separately.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 19. Those mighty ones, with necks like lions, sat on numerous and varied thrones, both in pairs and separately.

sanskrit: शुशुभे सा सभा राजन्राजभिस्तैः समागतैः ।देवैरिव महाभागैः समवेतैस्त्रिविष्टपम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: śuśubhe sā sabhā rājanrājabhistaiḥ samāgataiḥ ,devairiva mahābhāgaiḥ samavetaistriviṣṭapam.
english: That assembly, O King, shone like heaven (triviṣṭapa) graced by gods (deva) of great fortune who had gathered together.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 20. That assembly, O King, shone like heaven (triviṣṭapa) graced by gods (deva) of great fortune who had gathered together.

sanskrit: सर्वे वेदविदः शूराः सर्वे भास्वरमूर्तयः ।प्रावर्तत महाराज सुहृद्द्यूतमनन्तरम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: sarve vedavidaḥ śūrāḥ sarve bhāsvaramūrtayaḥ ,prāvartata mahārāja suhṛddyūtamanantaram.
english: All were knowers of the Vedas, all were brave, all possessed shining forms. Then, O Great King, the game of dice with friends began.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 21. All were knowers of the Vedas, all were brave, all possessed shining forms. Then, O Great King, the game of dice with friends began.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अयं बहुधनो राजन्सागरावर्तसंभवः ।मणिर्हारोत्तरः श्रीमान्कनकोत्तमभूषणः ॥२२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ayaṁ bahudhano rājansāgarāvartasaṁbhavaḥ ,maṇirhārottaraḥ śrīmānkanakottamabhūṣaṇaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O King, this gem (maṇi) is immensely valuable, originating from the ocean's whirlpools. It is splendid, adorned with a prominent necklace, and embellished with the finest gold.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 22. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'O King, this gem (maṇi) is immensely valuable, originating from the ocean's whirlpools. It is splendid, adorned with a prominent necklace, and embellished with the finest gold.'

sanskrit: एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं प्रतिपाणस्तु कस्तव ।भवत्वेष क्रमस्तात जयाम्येनं दुरोदरम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ pratipāṇastu kastava ,bhavatveṣa kramastāta jayāmyenaṁ durodaram.
english: O King, this wealth is mine. But what is your counter-bet? Let this be the procedure, dear one; I shall conquer this gambling (durodaram).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 23. O King, this wealth is mine. But what is your counter-bet? Let this be the procedure, dear one; I shall conquer this gambling (durodaram).

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।सन्ति मे मणयश्चैव धनानि विविधानि च ।मत्सरश्च न मेऽर्थेषु जयाम्येनं दुरोदरम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,santi me maṇayaścaiva dhanāni vividhāni ca ,matsaraśca na me'rtheṣu jayāmyenaṁ durodaram.
english: Duryodhana said: 'I possess many gems and various kinds of wealth, and I have no possessiveness (matsara) towards my riches. I shall conquer this gambling (durodaram).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 24. Duryodhana said: 'I possess many gems and various kinds of wealth, and I have no possessiveness (matsara) towards my riches. I shall conquer this gambling (durodaram).'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो जग्राह शकुनिस्तानक्षानक्षतत्त्ववित् ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato jagrāha śakunistānakṣānakṣatattvavit ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Then Śakuni, an expert in the game of dice (akṣatatvavit), took those dice. Śakuni then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "It is indeed won!"'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 53 Verse 25. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Then Śakuni, an expert in the game of dice (akṣatatvavit), took those dice. Śakuni then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "It is indeed won!"'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।मत्तः कैतवकेनैव यज्जितोऽस्मि दुरोदरम् ।शकुने हन्त दीव्यामो ग्लहमानाः सहस्रशः ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,mattaḥ kaitavakenaiva yajjito'smi durodaram ,śakune hanta dīvyāmo glahamānāḥ sahasraśaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O Śakuni, I have indeed been defeated by your deceit in the game of dice (durāudaram). Now, alas, we play, staking thousands."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 1. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O Śakuni, I have indeed been defeated by your deceit in the game of dice (durāudaram). Now, alas, we play, staking thousands."

sanskrit: इमे निष्कसहस्रस्य कुण्डिनो भरिताः शतम् ।कोशो हिरण्यमक्षय्यं जातरूपमनेकशः ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥२॥
transliteration: ime niṣkasahasrasya kuṇḍino bharitāḥ śatam ,kośo hiraṇyamakṣayyaṁ jātarūpamanekaśaḥ ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: "These hundred containers, each filled with a thousand niṣkas, are mine. Also mine is an inexhaustible treasury of natural gold (jātarūpam) in various forms. O King, this is my wealth (dhanam); with this, I will play against you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 2. "These hundred containers, each filled with a thousand niṣkas, are mine. Also mine is an inexhaustible treasury of natural gold (jātarūpam) in various forms. O King, this is my wealth (dhanam); with this, I will play against you."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तः शकुनिः प्राह जितमित्येव तं नृपम् ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktaḥ śakuniḥ prāha jitamityeva taṁ nṛpam.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Thus addressed, Śakuni merely said to that king (Yudhiṣṭhira), 'It is won!'"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 3. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Thus addressed, Śakuni merely said to that king (Yudhiṣṭhira), 'It is won!'"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अयं सहस्रसमितो वैयाघ्रः सुप्रवर्तितः ।सुचक्रोपस्करः श्रीमान्किङ्किणीजालमण्डितः ॥४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ayaṁ sahasrasamito vaiyāghraḥ supravartitaḥ ,sucakropaskaraḥ śrīmānkiṅkiṇījālamaṇḍitaḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "This, worth a thousand, made of tiger-skin, well-constructed, with excellent wheels and accoutrements, glorious, and adorned with a network of small bells, is mine."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 4. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "This, worth a thousand, made of tiger-skin, well-constructed, with excellent wheels and accoutrements, glorious, and adorned with a network of small bells, is mine."

sanskrit: संह्रादनो राजरथो य इहास्मानुपावहत् ।जैत्रो रथवरः पुण्यो मेघसागरनिःस्वनः ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁhrādano rājaratho ya ihāsmānupāvahat ,jaitro rathavaraḥ puṇyo meghasāgaraniḥsvanaḥ.
english: The king's chariot, named Saṃhrādana, which brought us here, is the best of victorious chariots, auspicious, and its roar is like that of clouds and the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 5. The king's chariot, named Saṃhrādana, which brought us here, is the best of victorious chariots, auspicious, and its roar is like that of clouds and the ocean.

sanskrit: अष्टौ यं कुररच्छायाः सदश्वा राष्ट्रसंमताः ।वहन्ति नैषामुच्येत पदा भूमिमुपस्पृशन् ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥६॥
transliteration: aṣṭau yaṁ kuraracchāyāḥ sadaśvā rāṣṭrasaṁmatāḥ ,vahanti naiṣāmucyeta padā bhūmimupaspṛśan ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Eight excellent, dove-colored horses, approved by the realm, draw it. It cannot be said that any of them touches the ground with its foot. O King, this is my wealth; with this, I will gamble with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 6. Eight excellent, dove-colored horses, approved by the realm, draw it. It cannot be said that any of them touches the ground with its foot. O King, this is my wealth; with this, I will gamble with you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resorting to deceit and resolved, then said to Yudhishthira, 'It is indeed won!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 7. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resorting to deceit and resolved, then said to Yudhishthira, 'It is indeed won!'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।सहस्रसंख्या नागा मे मत्तास्तिष्ठन्ति सौबल ।हेमकक्षाः कृतापीडाः पद्मिनो हेममालिनः ॥८॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,sahasrasaṁkhyā nāgā me mattāstiṣṭhanti saubala ,hemakakṣāḥ kṛtāpīḍāḥ padmino hemamālinaḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: O son of Subala (saubala), I have thousands of intoxicated elephants, adorned with golden girdles, forehead plates, and golden garlands, and marked with auspicious lotuses.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 8. Yudhishthira said: O son of Subala (saubala), I have thousands of intoxicated elephants, adorned with golden girdles, forehead plates, and golden garlands, and marked with auspicious lotuses.

sanskrit: सुदान्ता राजवहनाः सर्वशब्दक्षमा युधि ।ईषादन्ता महाकायाः सर्वे चाष्टकरेणवः ॥९॥
transliteration: sudāntā rājavahanāḥ sarvaśabdakṣamā yudhi ,īṣādantā mahākāyāḥ sarve cāṣṭakareṇavaḥ.
english: My royal mounts are well-trained and capable of enduring all sounds in battle. They possess sharp, plowshare-like tusks, have colossal bodies, and all of them are endowed with eight limbs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 9. My royal mounts are well-trained and capable of enduring all sounds in battle. They possess sharp, plowshare-like tusks, have colossal bodies, and all of them are endowed with eight limbs.

sanskrit: सर्वे च पुरभेत्तारो नगमेघनिभा गजाः ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥१०॥
transliteration: sarve ca purabhettāro nagameghanibhā gajāḥ ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: All these elephants are indeed destroyers of fortresses, resembling mountains and clouds in their imposing size and might. This, O King, is my wealth, and with this I will gamble with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 10. All these elephants are indeed destroyers of fortresses, resembling mountains and clouds in their imposing size and might. This, O King, is my wealth, and with this I will gamble with you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तमेवंवादिनं पार्थं प्रहसन्निव सौबलः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tamevaṁvādinaṁ pārthaṁ prahasanniva saubalaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: As Pārtha (Yudhishthira) was speaking in that manner, Śakuni, the son of Subala, addressed him as if laughing, saying, 'It is won!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 11. Vaiśampāyana said: As Pārtha (Yudhishthira) was speaking in that manner, Śakuni, the son of Subala, addressed him as if laughing, saying, 'It is won!'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।शतं दासीसहस्राणि तरुण्यो मे प्रभद्रिकाः ।कम्बुकेयूरधारिण्यो निष्ककण्ठ्यः स्वलंकृताः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,śataṁ dāsīsahasrāṇi taruṇyo me prabhadrikāḥ ,kambukeyūradhāriṇyo niṣkakaṇṭhyaḥ svalaṁkṛtāḥ.
english: Yudhishthira said: I have a hundred thousand young and excellent maidservants, adorned with conch-shell bracelets, golden necklaces, and otherwise beautifully decorated.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 12. Yudhishthira said: I have a hundred thousand young and excellent maidservants, adorned with conch-shell bracelets, golden necklaces, and otherwise beautifully decorated.

sanskrit: महार्हमाल्याभरणाः सुवस्त्राश्चन्दनोक्षिताः ।मणीन्हेम च बिभ्रत्यः सर्वा वै सूक्ष्मवाससः ॥१३॥
transliteration: mahārhamālyābharaṇāḥ suvastrāścandanokṣitāḥ ,maṇīnhema ca bibhratyaḥ sarvā vai sūkṣmavāsasaḥ.
english: All of them wore delicate garments, adorned with costly garlands and ornaments, beautifully dressed, anointed with sandalwood paste, and bearing jewels and gold.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 13. All of them wore delicate garments, adorned with costly garlands and ornaments, beautifully dressed, anointed with sandalwood paste, and bearing jewels and gold.

sanskrit: अनुसेवां चरन्तीमाः कुशला नृत्यसामसु ।स्नातकानाममात्यानां राज्ञां च मम शासनात् ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥१४॥
transliteration: anusevāṁ carantīmāḥ kuśalā nṛtyasāmasu ,snātakānāmamātyānāṁ rājñāṁ ca mama śāsanāt ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: These women, performing service and skilled in songs and dances, were by my command for the householders, ministers, and kings. O King, this wealth is mine, and with it, I gamble with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 14. These women, performing service and skilled in songs and dances, were by my command for the householders, ministers, and kings. O King, this wealth is mine, and with it, I gamble with you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resolute and resorting to trickery, addressed Yudhishthira, exclaiming, 'It is won!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 15. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resolute and resorting to trickery, addressed Yudhishthira, exclaiming, 'It is won!'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।एतावन्त्येव दासानां सहस्राण्युत सन्ति मे ।प्रदक्षिणानुलोमाश्च प्रावारवसनाः सदा ॥१६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,etāvantyeva dāsānāṁ sahasrāṇyuta santi me ,pradakṣiṇānulomāśca prāvāravasanāḥ sadā.
english: Yudhishthira said: 'Indeed, I have so many thousands of servants who are always respectful and wear cloaks.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 16. Yudhishthira said: 'Indeed, I have so many thousands of servants who are always respectful and wear cloaks.'

sanskrit: प्राज्ञा मेधाविनो दक्षा युवानो मृष्टकुण्डलाः ।पात्रीहस्ता दिवारात्रमतिथीन्भोजयन्त्युत ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥१७॥
transliteration: prājñā medhāvino dakṣā yuvāno mṛṣṭakuṇḍalāḥ ,pātrīhastā divārātramatithīnbhojayantyuta ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Wise, intelligent, and skillful young men with polished earrings and bowls in their hands feed guests day and night. O king, this is my wealth, and with it, I will gamble with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 17. Wise, intelligent, and skillful young men with polished earrings and bowls in their hands feed guests day and night. O king, this is my wealth, and with it, I will gamble with you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥१८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolute and having resorted to deceit, then said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 18. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolute and having resorted to deceit, then said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।रथास्तावन्त एवेमे हेमभाण्डाः पताकिनः ।हयैर्विनीतैः संपन्ना रथिभिश्चित्रयोधिभिः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,rathāstāvanta eveme hemabhāṇḍāḥ patākinaḥ ,hayairvinītaiḥ saṁpannā rathibhiścitrayodhibhiḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: Indeed, these same chariots, adorned with gold and bearing banners, are equipped with well-trained horses and with charioteers who fight wonderfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 19. Yudhiṣṭhira said: Indeed, these same chariots, adorned with gold and bearing banners, are equipped with well-trained horses and with charioteers who fight wonderfully.

sanskrit: एकैको यत्र लभते सहस्रपरमां भृतिम् ।युध्यतोऽयुध्यतो वापि वेतनं मासकालिकम् ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥२०॥
transliteration: ekaiko yatra labhate sahasraparamāṁ bhṛtim ,yudhyato'yudhyato vāpi vetanaṁ māsakālikam ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Where each one receives a monthly wage (vetana) of at least a thousand (coins/units) as pay (bhṛti), whether fighting or not fighting. O king, this is my wealth, and with it, I will gamble with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 20. Where each one receives a monthly wage (vetana) of at least a thousand (coins/units) as pay (bhṛti), whether fighting or not fighting. O king, this is my wealth, and with it, I will gamble with you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्येवमुक्ते पार्थेन कृतवैरो दुरात्मवान् ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityevamukte pārthena kṛtavairo durātmavān ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When Pārtha had spoken thus, the evil-minded (durātman) Śakuni, who had fostered enmity (kṛtavaira), said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "I have won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 21. Vaiśampāyana said: When Pārtha had spoken thus, the evil-minded (durātman) Śakuni, who had fostered enmity (kṛtavaira), said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "I have won!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अश्वांस्तित्तिरिकल्माषान्गान्धर्वान्हेममालिनः ।ददौ चित्ररथस्तुष्टो यांस्तान्गाण्डीवधन्वने ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥२२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,aśvāṁstittirikalmāṣāngāndharvānhemamālinaḥ ,dadau citrarathastuṣṭo yāṁstāngāṇḍīvadhanvane ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "King Citraratha, being pleased, gave those Gandharva horses, speckled like partridges and adorned with golden garlands, to the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna). O King, this wealth is mine, and with it, I shall gamble with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 22. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "King Citraratha, being pleased, gave those Gandharva horses, speckled like partridges and adorned with golden garlands, to the wielder of the Gāṇḍīva bow (Arjuna). O King, this wealth is mine, and with it, I shall gamble with you."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, determined and resorting to deceit (nikṛti), said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "I have won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 23. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, determined and resorting to deceit (nikṛti), said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "I have won!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।रथानां शकटानां च हयानां चायुतानि मे ।युक्तानामेव तिष्ठन्ति वाहैरुच्चावचैर्वृताः ॥२४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,rathānāṁ śakaṭānāṁ ca hayānāṁ cāyutāni me ,yuktānāmeva tiṣṭhanti vāhairuccāvacairvṛtāḥ.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "I possess tens of thousands of chariots, carts, and horses. They stand ready, yoked and attended by various drivers."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 24. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "I possess tens of thousands of chariots, carts, and horses. They stand ready, yoked and attended by various drivers."

sanskrit: एवं वर्णस्य वर्णस्य समुच्चीय सहस्रशः ।क्षीरं पिबन्तस्तिष्ठन्ति भुञ्जानाः शालितण्डुलान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: evaṁ varṇasya varṇasya samuccīya sahasraśaḥ ,kṣīraṁ pibantastiṣṭhanti bhuñjānāḥ śālitaṇḍulān.
english: In this manner, having gathered thousands upon thousands of individuals of every sort, they are present, drinking milk and eating rice grains.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 25. In this manner, having gathered thousands upon thousands of individuals of every sort, they are present, drinking milk and eating rice grains.

sanskrit: षष्टिस्तानि सहस्राणि सर्वे पृथुलवक्षसः ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥२६॥
transliteration: ṣaṣṭistāni sahasrāṇi sarve pṛthulavakṣasaḥ ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Those sixty thousand broad-chested ones are all my wealth, O King. With that, I shall gamble with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 26. Those sixty thousand broad-chested ones are all my wealth, O King. With that, I shall gamble with you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolute and having resorted to deception, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, 'It is won!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 27. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolute and having resorted to deception, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, 'It is won!'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।ताम्रलोहैः परिवृता निधयो मे चतुःशताः ।पञ्चद्रौणिक एकैकः सुवर्णस्याहतस्य वै ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥२८॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,tāmralohaiḥ parivṛtā nidhayo me catuḥśatāḥ ,pañcadrauṇika ekaikaḥ suvarṇasyāhatasya vai ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'I possess four hundred treasure-hoards, secured with copper and iron, each containing five droṇas of refined gold. This, O King, is my wealth, and with it, I shall gamble with you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 28. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'I possess four hundred treasure-hoards, secured with copper and iron, each containing five droṇas of refined gold. This, O King, is my wealth, and with it, I shall gamble with you.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resolute and resorting to deceit, said to Yudhishthira, "It is won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 54 Verse 29. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resolute and resorting to deceit, said to Yudhishthira, "It is won!"

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।महाराज विजानीहि यत्त्वां वक्ष्यामि तच्छृणु ।मुमूर्षोरौषधमिव न रोचेतापि ते श्रुतम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,mahārāja vijānīhi yattvāṁ vakṣyāmi tacchṛṇu ,mumūrṣorauṣadhamiva na rocetāpi te śrutam.
english: Vidura said: "O great king, understand well what I shall say to you, and listen to it. Even what you hear will not be pleasing to you, just like medicine is not pleasing to one who desires death."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 1. Vidura said: "O great king, understand well what I shall say to you, and listen to it. Even what you hear will not be pleasing to you, just like medicine is not pleasing to one who desires death."

sanskrit: यद्वै पुरा जातमात्रो रुराव गोमायुवद्विस्वरं पापचेताः ।दुर्योधनो भारतानां कुलघ्नः सोऽयं युक्तो भविता कालहेतुः ॥२॥
transliteration: yadvai purā jātamātro rurāva; gomāyuvadvisvaraṁ pāpacetāḥ ,duryodhano bhāratānāṁ kulaghnaḥ; so'yaṁ yukto bhavitā kālahetuḥ.
english: Indeed, this very Duryodhana, that evil-minded (pāpacetas) destroyer of the Bharata lineage, who long ago, immediately upon birth, howled with a terrible cry like a jackal - he will certainly be the agent of destruction (kāla-hetu).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 2. Indeed, this very Duryodhana, that evil-minded (pāpacetas) destroyer of the Bharata lineage, who long ago, immediately upon birth, howled with a terrible cry like a jackal - he will certainly be the agent of destruction (kāla-hetu).

sanskrit: गृहे वसन्तं गोमायुं त्वं वै मत्वा न बुध्यसे ।दुर्योधनस्य रूपेण शृणु काव्यां गिरं मम ॥३॥
transliteration: gṛhe vasantaṁ gomāyuṁ tvaṁ vai matvā na budhyase ,duryodhanasya rūpeṇa śṛṇu kāvyāṁ giraṁ mama.
english: Indeed, you do not truly comprehend, despite recognizing the jackal dwelling within your house, manifest in the form of Duryodhana. Therefore, listen to my wise counsel (kāvyaṃ giraṃ mama).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 3. Indeed, you do not truly comprehend, despite recognizing the jackal dwelling within your house, manifest in the form of Duryodhana. Therefore, listen to my wise counsel (kāvyaṃ giraṃ mama).

sanskrit: मधु वै माध्विको लब्ध्वा प्रपातं नावबुध्यते ।आरुह्य तं मज्जति वा पतनं वाधिगच्छति ॥४॥
transliteration: madhu vai mādhviko labdhvā prapātaṁ nāvabudhyate ,āruhya taṁ majjati vā patanaṁ vādhigacchati.
english: Indeed, a honey-gatherer, having obtained honey, does not perceive the precipice. After climbing it, he either falls into the water or experiences a fall.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 4. Indeed, a honey-gatherer, having obtained honey, does not perceive the precipice. After climbing it, he either falls into the water or experiences a fall.

sanskrit: सोऽयं मत्तोऽक्षदेवेन मधुवन्न परीक्षते ।प्रपातं बुध्यते नैव वैरं कृत्वा महारथैः ॥५॥
transliteration: so'yaṁ matto'kṣadevena madhuvanna parīkṣate ,prapātaṁ budhyate naiva vairaṁ kṛtvā mahārathaiḥ.
english: This person, intoxicated by gambling, does not assess the situation like a honey-gatherer. He certainly does not perceive the precipice, having created enmity with great warriors.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 5. This person, intoxicated by gambling, does not assess the situation like a honey-gatherer. He certainly does not perceive the precipice, having created enmity with great warriors.

sanskrit: विदितं ते महाराज राजस्वेवासमञ्जसम् ।अन्धका यादवा भोजाः समेताः कंसमत्यजन् ॥६॥
transliteration: viditaṁ te mahārāja rājasvevāsamañjasam ,andhakā yādavā bhojāḥ sametāḥ kaṁsamatyajan.
english: It is known to you, O great king, how inappropriate the situation was among the kings. The Andhakas, Yadavas, and Bhojas, having gathered, abandoned Kaṃsa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 6. It is known to you, O great king, how inappropriate the situation was among the kings. The Andhakas, Yadavas, and Bhojas, having gathered, abandoned Kaṃsa.

sanskrit: नियोगाच्च हते तस्मिन्कृष्णेनामित्रघातिना ।एवं ते ज्ञातयः सर्वे मोदमानाः शतं समाः ॥७॥
transliteration: niyogācca hate tasminkṛṣṇenāmitraghātinā ,evaṁ te jñātayaḥ sarve modamānāḥ śataṁ samāḥ.
english: And by divine decree, when he (Kaṃsa) was slain by Kṛṣṇa, the destroyer of foes, all those kinsmen thus rejoiced for a hundred years.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 7. And by divine decree, when he (Kaṃsa) was slain by Kṛṣṇa, the destroyer of foes, all those kinsmen thus rejoiced for a hundred years.

sanskrit: त्वन्नियुक्तः सव्यसाची निगृह्णातु सुयोधनम् ।निग्रहादस्य पापस्य मोदन्तां कुरवः सुखम् ॥८॥
transliteration: tvanniyuktaḥ savyasācī nigṛhṇātu suyodhanam ,nigrahādasya pāpasya modantāṁ kuravaḥ sukham.
english: Arjuna (savyasācī), appointed by you, should subdue Suyodhana. By the vanquishing of this wicked person, may the Kurus rejoice in happiness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 8. Arjuna (savyasācī), appointed by you, should subdue Suyodhana. By the vanquishing of this wicked person, may the Kurus rejoice in happiness.

sanskrit: काकेनेमांश्चित्रबर्हाञ्शार्दूलान्क्रोष्टुकेन च ।क्रीणीष्व पाण्डवान्राजन्मा मज्जीः शोकसागरे ॥९॥
transliteration: kākenemāṁścitrabarhāñśārdūlānkroṣṭukena ca ,krīṇīṣva pāṇḍavānrājanmā majjīḥ śokasāgare.
english: O King, do not try to purchase these beautifully feathered peacocks (citrabarhān) and tigers (śārdūlān) with a crow and a jackal. Do not abandon the Pandavas and thereby plunge into an ocean of sorrow (śokasāgare).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 9. O King, do not try to purchase these beautifully feathered peacocks (citrabarhān) and tigers (śārdūlān) with a crow and a jackal. Do not abandon the Pandavas and thereby plunge into an ocean of sorrow (śokasāgare).

sanskrit: त्यजेत्कुलार्थे पुरुषं ग्रामस्यार्थे कुलं त्यजेत् ।ग्रामं जनपदस्यार्थे आत्मार्थे पृथिवीं त्यजेत् ॥१०॥
transliteration: tyajetkulārthe puruṣaṁ grāmasyārthe kulaṁ tyajet ,grāmaṁ janapadasyārthe ātmārthe pṛthivīṁ tyajet.
english: One should sacrifice an individual (puruṣa) for the sake of the family. One should abandon the family for the sake of the village. One should give up the village for the sake of the district. For the sake of the self (ātman), one should renounce the entire world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 10. One should sacrifice an individual (puruṣa) for the sake of the family. One should abandon the family for the sake of the village. One should give up the village for the sake of the district. For the sake of the self (ātman), one should renounce the entire world.

sanskrit: सर्वज्ञः सर्वभावज्ञः सर्वशत्रुभयंकरः ।इति स्म भाषते काव्यो जम्भत्यागे महासुरान् ॥११॥
transliteration: sarvajñaḥ sarvabhāvajñaḥ sarvaśatrubhayaṁkaraḥ ,iti sma bhāṣate kāvyo jambhatyāge mahāsurān.
english: Kāvya (Śukra), who is omniscient (sarvajña), cognizant of all beings' intrinsic natures, and a terror to all enemies, thus spoke to the great Asuras concerning the defeat of Jambha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 11. Kāvya (Śukra), who is omniscient (sarvajña), cognizant of all beings' intrinsic natures, and a terror to all enemies, thus spoke to the great Asuras concerning the defeat of Jambha.

sanskrit: हिरण्यष्ठीविनः कश्चित्पक्षिणो वनगोचरान् ।गृहे किल कृतावासाँल्लोभाद्राजन्नपीडयत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: hiraṇyaṣṭhīvinaḥ kaścitpakṣiṇo vanagocarān ,gṛhe kila kṛtāvāsāँllobhādrājannapīḍayat.
english: O king, it is said that a certain greedy man oppressed some gold-spitting birds that frequented the forest but had made their dwelling in his house.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 12. O king, it is said that a certain greedy man oppressed some gold-spitting birds that frequented the forest but had made their dwelling in his house.

sanskrit: सदोपभोज्याँल्लोभान्धो हिरण्यार्थे परंतप ।आयतिं च तदात्वं च उभे सद्यो व्यनाशयत् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sadopabhojyāँllobhāndho hiraṇyārthe paraṁtapa ,āyatiṁ ca tadātvaṁ ca ubhe sadyo vyanāśayat.
english: O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), blinded by greed for gold, that man instantly destroyed both his present prosperity and future prospects, which were perpetually available for his enjoyment.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 13. O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa), blinded by greed for gold, that man instantly destroyed both his present prosperity and future prospects, which were perpetually available for his enjoyment.

sanskrit: तदात्वकामः पाण्डूंस्त्वं मा द्रुहो भरतर्षभ ।मोहात्मा तप्यसे पश्चात्पक्षिहा पुरुषो यथा ॥१४॥
transliteration: tadātvakāmaḥ pāṇḍūṁstvaṁ mā druho bharatarṣabha ,mohātmā tapyase paścātpakṣihā puruṣo yathā.
english: O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), you, being desirous of immediate results (tadātvakāma), must not harm the Pāṇḍavas. Otherwise, with a deluded mind (mohātmā), you will suffer later, just like that man who killed the birds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 14. O best of Bharatas (bharatarṣabha), you, being desirous of immediate results (tadātvakāma), must not harm the Pāṇḍavas. Otherwise, with a deluded mind (mohātmā), you will suffer later, just like that man who killed the birds.

sanskrit: जातं जातं पाण्डवेभ्यः पुष्पमादत्स्व भारत ।मालाकार इवारामे स्नेहं कुर्वन्पुनः पुनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: jātaṁ jātaṁ pāṇḍavebhyaḥ puṣpamādatsva bhārata ,mālākāra ivārāme snehaṁ kurvanpunaḥ punaḥ.
english: O Bhārata, take from the Pāṇḍavas each blossom as it appears, just as a gardener in a garden repeatedly gathers flowers while cultivating them with care and affection.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 15. O Bhārata, take from the Pāṇḍavas each blossom as it appears, just as a gardener in a garden repeatedly gathers flowers while cultivating them with care and affection.

sanskrit: वृक्षानङ्गारकारीव मैनान्धाक्षीः समूलकान् ।मा गमः ससुतामात्यः सबलश्च पराभवम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: vṛkṣānaṅgārakārīva maināndhākṣīḥ samūlakān ,mā gamaḥ sasutāmātyaḥ sabalaśca parābhavam.
english: Like a charcoal burner, do not burn them (your kinsmen) with their roots. Do not yourself go to ruin, along with your sons, ministers, and army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 16. Like a charcoal burner, do not burn them (your kinsmen) with their roots. Do not yourself go to ruin, along with your sons, ministers, and army.

sanskrit: समवेतान्हि कः पार्थान्प्रतियुध्येत भारत ।मरुद्भिः सहितो राजन्नपि साक्षान्मरुत्पतिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: samavetānhi kaḥ pārthānpratiyudhyeta bhārata ,marudbhiḥ sahito rājannapi sākṣānmarutpatiḥ.
english: Indeed, O Bhārata, who would fight against the united Pārthas (the Pāṇḍavas)? O King, not even Indra (Marutpati), accompanied directly by the Maruts (storm gods), could do so.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 55 Verse 17. Indeed, O Bhārata, who would fight against the united Pārthas (the Pāṇḍavas)? O King, not even Indra (Marutpati), accompanied directly by the Maruts (storm gods), could do so.

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।द्यूतं मूलं कलहस्यानुपाति मिथोभेदाय महते वा रणाय ।यदास्थितोऽयं धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रो दुर्योधनः सृजते वैरमुग्रम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,dyūtaṁ mūlaṁ kalahasyānupāti; mithobhedāya mahate vā raṇāya ,yadāsthito'yaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putro; duryodhanaḥ sṛjate vairamugram.
english: Vidura said: Gambling is the root of conflict, leading to great mutual division or even war. This son of Dhritarashtra, Duryodhana, having resorted to it, now creates fierce enmity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 1. Vidura said: Gambling is the root of conflict, leading to great mutual division or even war. This son of Dhritarashtra, Duryodhana, having resorted to it, now creates fierce enmity.

sanskrit: प्रातिपीयाः शांतनवा भैमसेनाः सबाह्लिकाः ।दुर्योधनापराधेन कृच्छ्रं प्राप्स्यन्ति सर्वशः ॥२॥
transliteration: prātipīyāḥ śāṁtanavā bhaimasenāḥ sabāhlikāḥ ,duryodhanāparādhena kṛcchraṁ prāpsyanti sarvaśaḥ.
english: The descendants of Pratīpa, the descendants of Śantanu, Bhīmasena and his followers, and the Bāhlikas - all of them will, in every way, encounter great difficulty and distress due to Duryodhana's offense.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 2. The descendants of Pratīpa, the descendants of Śantanu, Bhīmasena and his followers, and the Bāhlikas - all of them will, in every way, encounter great difficulty and distress due to Duryodhana's offense.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो मदेनैव क्षेमं राष्ट्रादपोहति ।विषाणं गौरिव मदात्स्वयमारुजते बलात् ॥३॥
transliteration: duryodhano madenaiva kṣemaṁ rāṣṭrādapohati ,viṣāṇaṁ gauriva madātsvayamārujate balāt.
english: Duryodhana, solely due to his pride, drives away prosperity from the kingdom. Like an intoxicated bull that forcibly breaks its own horn, he himself causes destruction out of arrogance.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 3. Duryodhana, solely due to his pride, drives away prosperity from the kingdom. Like an intoxicated bull that forcibly breaks its own horn, he himself causes destruction out of arrogance.

sanskrit: यश्चित्तमन्वेति परस्य राजन्वीरः कविः स्वामतिपत्य दृष्टिम् ।नावं समुद्र इव बालनेत्रामारुह्य घोरे व्यसने निमज्जेत् ॥४॥
transliteration: yaścittamanveti parasya rāja;nvīraḥ kaviḥ svāmatipatya dṛṣṭim ,nāvaṁ samudra iva bālanetrā;māruhya ghore vyasane nimajjet.
english: O King, a brave and wise person (kavi) who follows another's thoughts, disregarding his own judgment, will sink into dreadful misfortune, just like someone who boards a boat steered by an inexperienced child and launches it into the ocean.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 4. O King, a brave and wise person (kavi) who follows another's thoughts, disregarding his own judgment, will sink into dreadful misfortune, just like someone who boards a boat steered by an inexperienced child and launches it into the ocean.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनो ग्लहते पाण्डवेन प्रियायसे त्वं जयतीति तच्च ।अतिनर्माज्जायते संप्रहारो यतो विनाशः समुपैति पुंसाम् ॥५॥
transliteration: duryodhano glahate pāṇḍavena; priyāyase tvaṁ jayatīti tacca ,atinarmājjāyate saṁprahāro; yato vināśaḥ samupaiti puṁsām.
english: Duryodhana gambles with Yudhishthira (the Pandava), and you (Duryodhana) desire victory (thinking 'he wins') through that very game. From excessive jesting, conflict (saṃprahāra) arises, from which the destruction of people approaches.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 5. Duryodhana gambles with Yudhishthira (the Pandava), and you (Duryodhana) desire victory (thinking 'he wins') through that very game. From excessive jesting, conflict (saṃprahāra) arises, from which the destruction of people approaches.

sanskrit: आकर्षस्तेऽवाक्फलः कुप्रणीतो हृदि प्रौढो मन्त्रपदः समाधिः ।युधिष्ठिरेण सफलः संस्तवोऽस्तु साम्नः सुरिक्तोऽरिमतेः सुधन्वा ॥६॥
transliteration: ākarṣaste'vākphalaḥ kupraṇīto; hṛdi prauḍho mantrapadaḥ samādhiḥ ,yudhiṣṭhireṇa saphalaḥ saṁstavo'stu; sāmnaḥ surikto'rimateḥ sudhanvā.
english: Your son's (Duryodhana's) gambling (or manipulation) is ill-conducted and leads to adverse outcomes. In his heart lies a firm, mature concentration (samādhi) on strategic counsel (mantrapada). May a successful friendship (saṃstava) with Yudhishthira endure. However, the mind of this enemy (Duryodhana), though he is a skilled archer (sudhanvā), is completely devoid of conciliation (sāman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 6. Your son's (Duryodhana's) gambling (or manipulation) is ill-conducted and leads to adverse outcomes. In his heart lies a firm, mature concentration (samādhi) on strategic counsel (mantrapada). May a successful friendship (saṃstava) with Yudhishthira endure. However, the mind of this enemy (Duryodhana), though he is a skilled archer (sudhanvā), is completely devoid of conciliation (sāman).

sanskrit: प्रातिपीयाः शांतनवाश्च राजन्काव्यां वाचं शृणुत मात्यगाद्वः ।वैश्वानरं प्रज्वलितं सुघोरमयुद्धेन प्रशमयतोत्पतन्तम् ॥७॥
transliteration: prātipīyāḥ śāṁtanavāśca rāja;nkāvyāṁ vācaṁ śṛṇuta mātyagādvaḥ ,vaiśvānaraṁ prajvalitaṁ sughora;mayuddhena praśamayatotpatantam.
english: O King, descendants of Pratipa (Prātipīyas) and Śantanu (Śāṃtanavas), listen to this wise counsel; do not let it pass you by! Without resorting to war, you must pacify this exceedingly dreadful, blazing, and ever-rising universal fire (vaiśvānara) (of conflict).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 7. O King, descendants of Pratipa (Prātipīyas) and Śantanu (Śāṃtanavas), listen to this wise counsel; do not let it pass you by! Without resorting to war, you must pacify this exceedingly dreadful, blazing, and ever-rising universal fire (vaiśvānara) (of conflict).

sanskrit: यदा मन्युं पाण्डवोऽजातशत्रुर्न संयच्छेदक्षमयाभिभूतः ।वृकोदरः सव्यसाची यमौ च कोऽत्र द्वीपः स्यात्तुमुले वस्तदानीम् ॥८॥
transliteration: yadā manyuṁ pāṇḍavo'jātaśatru;rna saṁyacchedakṣamayābhibhūtaḥ ,vṛkodaraḥ savyasācī yamau ca; ko'tra dvīpaḥ syāttumule vastadānīm.
english: If the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍava) Yudhiṣṭhira (Ajātaśatru), overcome by impatience, does not control his anger, and if Bhīma (Vṛkodara), Arjuna (Savyasācī), and the two younger brothers (Yamau) are also present, who then would be your refuge in that tumultuous conflict?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 8. If the Pāṇḍava (Pāṇḍava) Yudhiṣṭhira (Ajātaśatru), overcome by impatience, does not control his anger, and if Bhīma (Vṛkodara), Arjuna (Savyasācī), and the two younger brothers (Yamau) are also present, who then would be your refuge in that tumultuous conflict?

sanskrit: महाराज प्रभवस्त्वं धनानां पुरा द्यूतान्मनसा यावदिच्छेः ।बहु वित्तं पाण्डवांश्चेज्जयेस्त्वं किं तेन स्याद्वसु विन्देह पार्थान् ॥९॥
transliteration: mahārāja prabhavastvaṁ dhanānāṁ; purā dyūtānmanasā yāvadiccheḥ ,bahu vittaṁ pāṇḍavāṁścejjayestvaṁ; kiṁ tena syādvasu vindeha pārthān.
english: O great king, you are the master of wealth! Before the game of dice, you could mentally wish for as much (wealth) as you desired. If you were to win much wealth and the Pāṇḍavas (Pāṇḍavas), what good would that wealth do? Instead, you would find the Pārthas (Pārthas) (as a burden) in this world.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 9. O great king, you are the master of wealth! Before the game of dice, you could mentally wish for as much (wealth) as you desired. If you were to win much wealth and the Pāṇḍavas (Pāṇḍavas), what good would that wealth do? Instead, you would find the Pārthas (Pārthas) (as a burden) in this world.

sanskrit: जानीमहे देवितं सौबलस्य वेद द्यूते निकृतिं पार्वतीयः ।यतः प्राप्तः शकुनिस्तत्र यातु मायायोधी भारत पार्वतीयः ॥१०॥
transliteration: jānīmahe devitaṁ saubalasya; veda dyūte nikṛtiṁ pārvatīyaḥ ,yataḥ prāptaḥ śakunistatra yātu; māyāyodhī bhārata pārvatīyaḥ.
english: We are aware of Saubala's (Śakuni's) gambling. That mountain-dweller (pārvatīya) (Śakuni) knows deceit (nikṛti) in the game of dice. O Bhārata, let that deceitful fighter (māyāyodhī), the mountain-dweller (pārvatīya), go back to where he came from!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 56 Verse 10. We are aware of Saubala's (Śakuni's) gambling. That mountain-dweller (pārvatīya) (Śakuni) knows deceit (nikṛti) in the game of dice. O Bhārata, let that deceitful fighter (māyāyodhī), the mountain-dweller (pārvatīya), go back to where he came from!

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।परेषामेव यशसा श्लाघसे त्वं सदा छन्नः कुत्सयन्धार्तराष्ट्रान् ।जानीमस्त्वां विदुर यत्प्रियस्त्वं बालानिवास्मानवमन्यसे त्वम् ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,pareṣāmeva yaśasā ślāghase tvaṁ; sadā channaḥ kutsayandhārtarāṣṭrān ,jānīmastvāṁ vidura yatpriyastvaṁ; bālānivāsmānavamanyase tvam.
english: Duryodhana said: You constantly praise the glory of others, always concealed, while you condemn the sons of Dhritarashtra. We know you, Vidura, that you are dear (to us), yet you disrespect us as if we were mere children.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: You constantly praise the glory of others, always concealed, while you condemn the sons of Dhritarashtra. We know you, Vidura, that you are dear (to us), yet you disrespect us as if we were mere children.

sanskrit: सुविज्ञेयः पुरुषोऽन्यत्रकामो निन्दाप्रशंसे हि तथा युनक्ति ।जिह्वा मनस्ते हृदयं निर्व्यनक्ति ज्यायो निराह मनसः प्रातिकूल्यम् ॥२॥
transliteration: suvijñeyaḥ puruṣo'nyatrakāmo; nindāpraśaṁse hi tathā yunakti ,jihvā manaste hṛdayaṁ nirvyanakti; jyāyo nirāha manasaḥ prātikūlyam.
english: A person (puruṣa) whose desire is for another is easily known, for they engage in blame and praise accordingly. Your tongue reveals the thoughts of your heart; indeed, the antagonism of the mind speaks more powerfully.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 2. A person (puruṣa) whose desire is for another is easily known, for they engage in blame and praise accordingly. Your tongue reveals the thoughts of your heart; indeed, the antagonism of the mind speaks more powerfully.

sanskrit: उत्सङ्गेन व्याल इवाहृतोऽसि मार्जारवत्पोषकं चोपहंसि ।भर्तृघ्नत्वान्न हि पापीय आहुस्तस्मात्क्षत्तः किं न बिभेषि पापात् ॥३॥
transliteration: utsaṅgena vyāla ivāhṛto'si; mārjāravatpoṣakaṁ copahaṁsi ,bhartṛghnatvānna hi pāpīya āhu;stasmātkṣattaḥ kiṁ na bibheṣi pāpāt.
english: You have been taken into our lap like a snake. And like a cat, you deceive your nurturer. Indeed, they say there is nothing more sinful (pāpīyas) than treachery against one's master. Therefore, O Vidura (kṣattā), why do you not fear sin?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 3. You have been taken into our lap like a snake. And like a cat, you deceive your nurturer. Indeed, they say there is nothing more sinful (pāpīyas) than treachery against one's master. Therefore, O Vidura (kṣattā), why do you not fear sin?

sanskrit: जित्वा शत्रून्फलमाप्तं महन्नो मास्मान्क्षत्तः परुषाणीह वोचः ।द्विषद्भिस्त्वं संप्रयोगाभिनन्दी मुहुर्द्वेषं यासि नः संप्रमोहात् ॥४॥
transliteration: jitvā śatrūnphalamāptaṁ mahanno; māsmānkṣattaḥ paruṣāṇīha vocaḥ ,dviṣadbhistvaṁ saṁprayogābhinandī; muhurdveṣaṁ yāsi naḥ saṁpramohāt.
english: We have achieved a great outcome (phala) by conquering our enemies. O Vidura (kṣattā), do not speak harsh words to us here. You delight in associating with our adversaries, and out of great delusion, you repeatedly show enmity towards us.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 4. We have achieved a great outcome (phala) by conquering our enemies. O Vidura (kṣattā), do not speak harsh words to us here. You delight in associating with our adversaries, and out of great delusion, you repeatedly show enmity towards us.

sanskrit: अमित्रतां याति नरोऽक्षमं ब्रुवन्निगूहते गुह्यममित्रसंस्तवे ।तदाश्रितापत्रपा किं न बाधते यदिच्छसि त्वं तदिहाद्य भाषसे ॥५॥
transliteration: amitratāṁ yāti naro'kṣamaṁ bruva;nnigūhate guhyamamitrasaṁstave ,tadāśritāpatrapā kiṁ na bādhate; yadicchasi tvaṁ tadihādya bhāṣase.
english: A man who speaks inappropriate things (akṣamam) conceals a secret in the company of enemies. Does not the shame arising from such a situation trouble him? Whatever you wish, that you speak here today.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 5. A man who speaks inappropriate things (akṣamam) conceals a secret in the company of enemies. Does not the shame arising from such a situation trouble him? Whatever you wish, that you speak here today.

sanskrit: मा नोऽवमंस्था विद्म मनस्तवेदं शिक्षस्व बुद्धिं स्थविराणां सकाशात् ।यशो रक्षस्व विदुर संप्रणीतं मा व्यापृतः परकार्येषु भूस्त्वम् ॥६॥
transliteration: mā no'vamaṁsthā vidma manastavedaṁ; śikṣasva buddhiṁ sthavirāṇāṁ sakāśāt ,yaśo rakṣasva vidura saṁpraṇītaṁ; mā vyāpṛtaḥ parakāryeṣu bhūstvam.
english: O Vidura, do not disrespect us; we know your mind (manas) well. Learn wisdom from the elders. Protect your well-established reputation (yaśas), and do not become involved in others' affairs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 6. O Vidura, do not disrespect us; we know your mind (manas) well. Learn wisdom from the elders. Protect your well-established reputation (yaśas), and do not become involved in others' affairs.

sanskrit: अहं कर्तेति विदुर मावमंस्था मा नो नित्यं परुषाणीह वोचः ।न त्वां पृच्छामि विदुर यद्धितं मे स्वस्ति क्षत्तर्मा तितिक्षून्क्षिणु त्वम् ॥७॥
transliteration: ahaṁ karteti vidura māvamaṁsthā; mā no nityaṁ paruṣāṇīha vocaḥ ,na tvāṁ pṛcchāmi vidura yaddhitaṁ me; svasti kṣattarmā titikṣūnkṣiṇu tvam.
english: O Vidura, do not disrespect (me) by (thinking) 'I am the doer.' Do not always speak harsh words (paruṣāṇi) to us here. O Vidura, I am not asking you what is beneficial for me. Hail, O Kṣattar! Do not oppress those who are forbearing.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 7. O Vidura, do not disrespect (me) by (thinking) 'I am the doer.' Do not always speak harsh words (paruṣāṇi) to us here. O Vidura, I am not asking you what is beneficial for me. Hail, O Kṣattar! Do not oppress those who are forbearing.

sanskrit: एकः शास्ता न द्वितीयोऽस्ति शास्ता गर्भे शयानं पुरुषं शास्ति शास्ता ।तेनानुशिष्टः प्रवणादिवाम्भो यथा नियुक्तोऽस्मि तथा वहामि ॥८॥
transliteration: ekaḥ śāstā na dvitīyo'sti śāstā; garbhe śayānaṁ puruṣaṁ śāsti śāstā ,tenānuśiṣṭaḥ pravaṇādivāmbho; yathā niyukto'smi tathā vahāmi.
english: There is only one true ruler (śāstṛ); there is no second ruler. That Ruler (śāstṛ) guides the individual (puruṣa) even while lying in the womb. Instructed by Him, I act just as water flows down a slope, according to how I have been appointed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 8. There is only one true ruler (śāstṛ); there is no second ruler. That Ruler (śāstṛ) guides the individual (puruṣa) even while lying in the womb. Instructed by Him, I act just as water flows down a slope, according to how I have been appointed.

sanskrit: भिनत्ति शिरसा शैलमहिं भोजयते च यः ।स एव तस्य कुरुते कार्याणामनुशासनम् ॥९॥
transliteration: bhinatti śirasā śailamahiṁ bhojayate ca yaḥ ,sa eva tasya kurute kāryāṇāmanuśāsanam.
english: Indeed, he who shatters a mountain with his head and feeds a snake is the very one who imposes his will on his own affairs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 9. Indeed, he who shatters a mountain with his head and feeds a snake is the very one who imposes his will on his own affairs.

sanskrit: यो बलादनुशास्तीह सोऽमित्रं तेन विन्दति ।मित्रतामनुवृत्तं तु समुपेक्षेत पण्डितः ॥१०॥
transliteration: yo balādanuśāstīha so'mitraṁ tena vindati ,mitratāmanuvṛttaṁ tu samupekṣeta paṇḍitaḥ.
english: He who governs by force in this world finds an enemy through that. But a wise person should disregard a friendship that is merely customary.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 10. He who governs by force in this world finds an enemy through that. But a wise person should disregard a friendship that is merely customary.

sanskrit: प्रदीप्य यः प्रदीप्ताग्निं प्राक्त्वरन्नाभिधावति ।भस्मापि न स विन्देत शिष्टं क्वचन भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: pradīpya yaḥ pradīptāgniṁ prāktvarannābhidhāvati ,bhasmāpi na sa vindeta śiṣṭaṁ kvacana bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, he who, having ignited a blazing fire, does not immediately hasten towards it, will not find even the remaining ash anywhere.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 11. O Bhārata, he who, having ignited a blazing fire, does not immediately hasten towards it, will not find even the remaining ash anywhere.

sanskrit: न वासयेत्पारवर्ग्यं द्विषन्तं विशेषतः क्षत्तरहितं मनुष्यम् ।स यत्रेच्छसि विदुर तत्र गच्छ सुसान्त्वितापि ह्यसती स्त्री जहाति ॥१२॥
transliteration: na vāsayetpāravargyaṁ dviṣantaṁ; viśeṣataḥ kṣattarahitaṁ manuṣyam ,sa yatrecchasi vidura tatra gaccha; susāntvitāpi hyasatī strī jahāti.
english: One should not allow an enemy to dwell, especially a hostile man who is unprotected. O Vidura, go wherever you wish, for even a well-consoled unchaste woman will abandon (her husband).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 12. One should not allow an enemy to dwell, especially a hostile man who is unprotected. O Vidura, go wherever you wish, for even a well-consoled unchaste woman will abandon (her husband).

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।एतावता ये पुरुषं त्यजन्ति तेषां सख्यमन्तवद्ब्रूहि राजन् ।राज्ञां हि चित्तानि परिप्लुतानि सान्त्वं दत्त्वा मुसलैर्घातयन्ति ॥१३॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,etāvatā ye puruṣaṁ tyajanti; teṣāṁ sakhyamantavadbrūhi rājan ,rājñāṁ hi cittāni pariplutāni; sāntvaṁ dattvā musalairghātayanti.
english: Vidura said: "O King, tell me, the friendship of those who abandon a person (puruṣa) for such slight reasons is ephemeral. Indeed, the minds of kings are fickle; after offering solace, they strike with clubs."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 13. Vidura said: "O King, tell me, the friendship of those who abandon a person (puruṣa) for such slight reasons is ephemeral. Indeed, the minds of kings are fickle; after offering solace, they strike with clubs."

sanskrit: अबालस्त्वं मन्यसे राजपुत्र बालोऽहमित्येव सुमन्दबुद्धे ।यः सौहृदे पुरुषं स्थापयित्वा पश्चादेनं दूषयते स बालः ॥१४॥
transliteration: abālastvaṁ manyase rājaputra; bālo'hamityeva sumandabuddhe ,yaḥ sauhṛde puruṣaṁ sthāpayitvā; paścādenaṁ dūṣayate sa bālaḥ.
english: "O Prince, you consider yourself not a child. However, O exceedingly dull-witted one, you are indeed a child. For he who, after establishing a person (puruṣa) in friendship, later betrays him - that one is a child."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 14. "O Prince, you consider yourself not a child. However, O exceedingly dull-witted one, you are indeed a child. For he who, after establishing a person (puruṣa) in friendship, later betrays him - that one is a child."

sanskrit: न श्रेयसे नीयते मन्दबुद्धिः स्त्री श्रोत्रियस्येव गृहे प्रदुष्टा ।ध्रुवं न रोचेद्भरतर्षभस्य पतिः कुमार्या इव षष्टिवर्षः ॥१५॥
transliteration: na śreyase nīyate mandabuddhiḥ; strī śrotriyasyeva gṛhe praduṣṭā ,dhruvaṁ na rocedbharatarṣabhasya; patiḥ kumāryā iva ṣaṣṭivarṣaḥ.
english: A dull-witted person is not led to prosperity, just as a defiled woman in the house of a Vedic scholar (śrotriya) is not. Surely, this will not please the best of Bharatas, any more than a sixty-year-old husband would please a young maiden.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 15. A dull-witted person is not led to prosperity, just as a defiled woman in the house of a Vedic scholar (śrotriya) is not. Surely, this will not please the best of Bharatas, any more than a sixty-year-old husband would please a young maiden.

sanskrit: अनुप्रियं चेदनुकाङ्क्षसे त्वं सर्वेषु कार्येषु हिताहितेषु ।स्त्रियश्च राजञ्जडपङ्गुकांश्च पृच्छ त्वं वै तादृशांश्चैव मूढान् ॥१६॥
transliteration: anupriyaṁ cedanukāṅkṣase tvaṁ; sarveṣu kāryeṣu hitāhiteṣu ,striyaśca rājañjaḍapaṅgukāṁśca; pṛccha tvaṁ vai tādṛśāṁścaiva mūḍhān.
english: "O King, if you wish to pursue what is merely pleasing in all matters, whether they are beneficial or harmful, then consult women, the dull-witted, the lame, and indeed, such foolish people."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 16. "O King, if you wish to pursue what is merely pleasing in all matters, whether they are beneficial or harmful, then consult women, the dull-witted, the lame, and indeed, such foolish people."
