sanskrit: लभ्यः खलु प्रातिपीय नरोऽनुप्रियवागिह ।अप्रियस्य तु पथ्यस्य वक्ता श्रोता च दुर्लभः ॥१७॥
transliteration: labhyaḥ khalu prātipīya naro'nupriyavāgiha ,apriyasya tu pathyasya vaktā śrotā ca durlabhaḥ.
english: Indeed, a man who speaks pleasing words (prīti), seeking favor, is easily found in this world. However, both a speaker and a listener of unpleasant but beneficial (pathya) advice are rare.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 17. Indeed, a man who speaks pleasing words (prīti), seeking favor, is easily found in this world. However, both a speaker and a listener of unpleasant but beneficial (pathya) advice are rare.

sanskrit: यस्तु धर्मे पराश्वस्य हित्वा भर्तुः प्रियाप्रिये ।अप्रियाण्याह पथ्यानि तेन राजा सहायवान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: yastu dharme parāśvasya hitvā bhartuḥ priyāpriye ,apriyāṇyāha pathyāni tena rājā sahāyavān.
english: The king finds a true helper in that person who, being steadfast in (his) natural law (dharma) and disregarding the master's personal likes and dislikes, speaks words that are unpleasant yet beneficial (pathya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 18. The king finds a true helper in that person who, being steadfast in (his) natural law (dharma) and disregarding the master's personal likes and dislikes, speaks words that are unpleasant yet beneficial (pathya).

sanskrit: अव्याधिजं कटुकं तीक्ष्णमुष्णं यशोमुषं परुषं पूतिगन्धि ।सतां पेयं यन्न पिबन्त्यसन्तो मन्युं महाराज पिब प्रशाम्य ॥१९॥
transliteration: avyādhijaṁ kaṭukaṁ tīkṣṇamuṣṇaṁ; yaśomuṣaṁ paruṣaṁ pūtigandhi ,satāṁ peyaṁ yanna pibantyasanto; manyuṁ mahārāja piba praśāmya.
english: O great king, quell your wrath (manyu)! You must suppress that anger (manyu) which, though not born of illness (avyādhija), is bitter (kaṭuka), sharp (tīkṣṇa), fierce (uṣṇa), destructive of reputation (yaśomuṣa), harsh (paruṣa), and foul-smelling (pūtigandhi). This is the anger that the virtuous (sat) must internalize (literally, 'drink'), but the wicked (asat) do not. Drink (piba) it and become completely calm (praśāmya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 19. O great king, quell your wrath (manyu)! You must suppress that anger (manyu) which, though not born of illness (avyādhija), is bitter (kaṭuka), sharp (tīkṣṇa), fierce (uṣṇa), destructive of reputation (yaśomuṣa), harsh (paruṣa), and foul-smelling (pūtigandhi). This is the anger that the virtuous (sat) must internalize (literally, 'drink'), but the wicked (asat) do not. Drink (piba) it and become completely calm (praśāmya).

sanskrit: वैचित्रवीर्यस्य यशो धनं च वाञ्छाम्यहं सहपुत्रस्य शश्वत् ।यथा तथा वोऽस्तु नमश्च वोऽस्तु ममापि च स्वस्ति दिशन्तु विप्राः ॥२०॥
transliteration: vaicitravīryasya yaśo dhanaṁ ca; vāñchāmyahaṁ sahaputrasya śaśvat ,yathā tathā vo'stu namaśca vo'stu; mamāpi ca svasti diśantu viprāḥ.
english: I always desire the lasting glory and prosperity of Vaicitravīrya (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and his sons. So be it for you all, and salutations to you all. And may the learned Brahmins (vipra) also bestow well-being (svasti) upon me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 20. I always desire the lasting glory and prosperity of Vaicitravīrya (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) and his sons. So be it for you all, and salutations to you all. And may the learned Brahmins (vipra) also bestow well-being (svasti) upon me.

sanskrit: आशीविषान्नेत्रविषान्कोपयेन्न तु पण्डितः ।एवं तेऽहं वदामीदं प्रयतः कुरुनन्दन ॥२१॥
transliteration: āśīviṣānnetraviṣānkopayenna tu paṇḍitaḥ ,evaṁ te'haṁ vadāmīdaṁ prayataḥ kurunandana.
english: A wise person should not provoke poisonous snakes (āśīviṣa) or those with venom in their eyes (netraviṣa). Thus, O delight of the Kurus, I diligently convey this to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 57 Verse 21. A wise person should not provoke poisonous snakes (āśīviṣa) or those with venom in their eyes (netraviṣa). Thus, O delight of the Kurus, I diligently convey this to you.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।बहु वित्तं पराजैषीः पाण्डवानां युधिष्ठिर ।आचक्ष्व वित्तं कौन्तेय यदि तेऽस्त्यपराजितम् ॥१॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,bahu vittaṁ parājaiṣīḥ pāṇḍavānāṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,ācakṣva vittaṁ kaunteya yadi te'styaparājitam.
english: Śakuni said: O Yudhishthira, you have lost much wealth belonging to the Pāṇḍavas. Tell me, O son of Kuntī, if you still possess any undefeated wealth (aparājita).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 1. Śakuni said: O Yudhishthira, you have lost much wealth belonging to the Pāṇḍavas. Tell me, O son of Kuntī, if you still possess any undefeated wealth (aparājita).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।मम वित्तमसंख्येयं यदहं वेद सौबल ।अथ त्वं शकुने कस्माद्वित्तं समनुपृच्छसि ॥२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,mama vittamasaṁkhyeyaṁ yadahaṁ veda saubala ,atha tvaṁ śakune kasmādvittaṁ samanupṛcchasi.
english: Yudhishthira said: O son of Subala, my wealth is immeasurable (asaṃkhyeyam), of which I am aware. Then, O Śakuni, why are you inquiring about wealth?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 2. Yudhishthira said: O son of Subala, my wealth is immeasurable (asaṃkhyeyam), of which I am aware. Then, O Śakuni, why are you inquiring about wealth?

sanskrit: अयुतं प्रयुतं चैव खर्वं पद्मं तथार्बुदम् ।शङ्खं चैव निखर्वं च समुद्रं चात्र पण्यताम् ।एतन्मम धनं राजंस्तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥३॥
transliteration: ayutaṁ prayutaṁ caiva kharvaṁ padmaṁ tathārbudam ,śaṅkhaṁ caiva nikharvaṁ ca samudraṁ cātra paṇyatām ,etanmama dhanaṁ rājaṁstena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Let ten thousand (ayutam), a million (prayutam), and also ten billion (kharvam), a quadrillion (padmam), and likewise ten million (arbudam), and ten trillion (śaṅkham), and a hundred billion (nikharvam), and ten quintillion (samudram) be wagered here. O King, this is my wealth, and with this, I will gamble with you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 3. Let ten thousand (ayutam), a million (prayutam), and also ten billion (kharvam), a quadrillion (padmam), and likewise ten million (arbudam), and ten trillion (śaṅkham), and a hundred billion (nikharvam), and ten quintillion (samudram) be wagered here. O King, this is my wealth, and with this, I will gamble with you.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolved and resorting to trickery, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, declaring, "It is won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolved and resorting to trickery, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, declaring, "It is won!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।गवाश्वं बहुधेनूकमसंख्येयमजाविकम् ।यत्किंचिदनुवर्णानां प्राक्सिन्धोरपि सौबल ।एतन्मम धनं राजंस्तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥५॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,gavāśvaṁ bahudhenūkamasaṁkhyeyamajāvikam ,yatkiṁcidanuvarṇānāṁ prāksindhorapi saubala ,etanmama dhanaṁ rājaṁstena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O son of Subala, O King, all my wealth consisting of cows, horses, numerous milch cows, countless goats and sheep, and whatever belongs to the other castes east of the Sindhu river - with all this, I shall now gamble with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 5. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O son of Subala, O King, all my wealth consisting of cows, horses, numerous milch cows, countless goats and sheep, and whatever belongs to the other castes east of the Sindhu river - with all this, I shall now gamble with you."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolved and resorting to trickery, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, declaring, "It is won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 6. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, resolved and resorting to trickery, addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, declaring, "It is won!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।पुरं जनपदो भूमिरब्राह्मणधनैः सह ।अब्राह्मणाश्च पुरुषा राजञ्शिष्टं धनं मम ।एतद्राजन्धनं मह्यं तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,puraṁ janapado bhūmirabrāhmaṇadhanaiḥ saha ,abrāhmaṇāśca puruṣā rājañśiṣṭaṁ dhanaṁ mama ,etadrājandhanaṁ mahyaṁ tena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O King, the city, the district, the land, along with the wealth of non-Brāhmaṇas, and the non-Brāhmaṇa men themselves - this is my remaining wealth. With this, O King, I shall gamble with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 7. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O King, the city, the district, the land, along with the wealth of non-Brāhmaṇas, and the non-Brāhmaṇa men themselves - this is my remaining wealth. With this, O King, I shall gamble with you."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard this, and being resolute and having resorted to deceit, Śakuni addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "I have won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 8. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard this, and being resolute and having resorted to deceit, Śakuni addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "I have won!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।राजपुत्रा इमे राजञ्शोभन्ते येन भूषिताः ।कुण्डलानि च निष्काश्च सर्वं चाङ्गविभूषणम् ।एतन्मम धनं राजंस्तेन दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,rājaputrā ime rājañśobhante yena bhūṣitāḥ ,kuṇḍalāni ca niṣkāśca sarvaṁ cāṅgavibhūṣaṇam ,etanmama dhanaṁ rājaṁstena dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O King, these princes are adorned and shine with these (ornaments). The earrings, the gold necklaces, and all the body ornaments  -  this is my wealth, O King. With these, I shall gamble with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 9. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "O King, these princes are adorned and shine with these (ornaments). The earrings, the gold necklaces, and all the body ornaments  -  this is my wealth, O King. With these, I shall gamble with you."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥१०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard this, and being resolute and having resorted to deceit, Śakuni addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "I have won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 10. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard this, and being resolute and having resorted to deceit, Śakuni addressed Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "I have won!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।श्यामो युवा लोहिताक्षः सिंहस्कन्धो महाभुजः ।नकुलो ग्लह एको मे यच्चैतत्स्वगतं धनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,śyāmo yuvā lohitākṣaḥ siṁhaskandho mahābhujaḥ ,nakulo glaha eko me yaccaitatsvagataṁ dhanam.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "The dark-complexioned, youthful one with reddish eyes, lion-like shoulders, and mighty arms - Nakula - is my one stake, along with whatever wealth I personally possess."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 11. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "The dark-complexioned, youthful one with reddish eyes, lion-like shoulders, and mighty arms - Nakula - is my one stake, along with whatever wealth I personally possess."

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।प्रियस्ते नकुलो राजन्राजपुत्रो युधिष्ठिर ।अस्माकं धनतां प्राप्तो भूयस्त्वं केन दीव्यसि ॥१२॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,priyaste nakulo rājanrājaputro yudhiṣṭhira ,asmākaṁ dhanatāṁ prāpto bhūyastvaṁ kena dīvyasi.
english: Shakuni said: 'O King Yudhiṣṭhira, Nakula, this prince who is dear to you, has become our property. With whom will you gamble again?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 12. Shakuni said: 'O King Yudhiṣṭhira, Nakula, this prince who is dear to you, has become our property. With whom will you gamble again?'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा तु शकुनिस्तानक्षान्प्रत्यपद्यत ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥१३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā tu śakunistānakṣānpratyapadyata ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having spoken thus, Shakuni then accepted those dice. Shakuni then said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 13. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having spoken thus, Shakuni then accepted those dice. Shakuni then said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"'

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अयं धर्मान्सहदेवोऽनुशास्ति लोके ह्यस्मिन्पण्डिताख्यां गतश्च ।अनर्हता राजपुत्रेण तेन त्वया दीव्याम्यप्रियवत्प्रियेण ॥१४॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ayaṁ dharmānsahadevo'nuśāsti; loke hyasminpaṇḍitākhyāṁ gataśca ,anarhatā rājaputreṇa tena; tvayā dīvyāmyapriyavatpriyeṇa.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'This Sahadeva teaches natural law (dharma) in this world, and he has indeed earned the title of 'wise man' (paṇḍita). I will gamble with you, staking that unworthy prince, who is dear to me but, in this situation, seems like an enemy.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 14. Yudhiṣṭhira said: 'This Sahadeva teaches natural law (dharma) in this world, and he has indeed earned the title of 'wise man' (paṇḍita). I will gamble with you, staking that unworthy prince, who is dear to me but, in this situation, seems like an enemy.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having heard this, Shakuni, resolute and having resorted to deceit, then said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 15. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having heard this, Shakuni, resolute and having resorted to deceit, then said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"'

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।माद्रीपुत्रौ प्रियौ राजंस्तवेमौ विजितौ मया ।गरीयांसौ तु ते मन्ये भीमसेनधनंजयौ ॥१६॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,mādrīputrau priyau rājaṁstavemau vijitau mayā ,garīyāṁsau tu te manye bhīmasenadhanaṁjayau.
english: Shakuni said: "O King, these two sons of Madri, who are dear to you, have been won by me. However, I consider Bhimasena and Dhananjaya (Arjuna) to be more significant to you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 16. Shakuni said: "O King, these two sons of Madri, who are dear to you, have been won by me. However, I consider Bhimasena and Dhananjaya (Arjuna) to be more significant to you."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अधर्मं चरसे नूनं यो नावेक्षसि वै नयम् ।यो नः सुमनसां मूढ विभेदं कर्तुमिच्छसि ॥१७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,adharmaṁ carase nūnaṁ yo nāvekṣasi vai nayam ,yo naḥ sumanasāṁ mūḍha vibhedaṁ kartumicchasi.
english: Yudhishthira said: "You are certainly acting unrighteously (adharma), for you show no regard for proper policy (naya). You, foolish one, wish to cause a rift among us, who are well-disposed."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 17. Yudhishthira said: "You are certainly acting unrighteously (adharma), for you show no regard for proper policy (naya). You, foolish one, wish to cause a rift among us, who are well-disposed."

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।गर्ते मत्तः प्रपतति प्रमत्तः स्थाणुमृच्छति ।ज्येष्ठो राजन्वरिष्ठोऽसि नमस्ते भरतर्षभ ॥१८॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,garte mattaḥ prapatati pramattaḥ sthāṇumṛcchati ,jyeṣṭho rājanvariṣṭho'si namaste bharatarṣabha.
english: Shakuni said: "An intoxicated person falls into a pit, and a careless person stumbles against a post. O King, you are the eldest and most excellent. Salutations to you, O Bull among the Bharatas."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 18. Shakuni said: "An intoxicated person falls into a pit, and a careless person stumbles against a post. O King, you are the eldest and most excellent. Salutations to you, O Bull among the Bharatas."

sanskrit: स्वप्ने न तानि पश्यन्ति जाग्रतो वा युधिष्ठिर ।कितवा यानि दीव्यन्तः प्रलपन्त्युत्कटा इव ॥१९॥
transliteration: svapne na tāni paśyanti jāgrato vā yudhiṣṭhira ,kitavā yāni dīvyantaḥ pralapantyutkaṭā iva.
english: O Yudhishthira, they do not see those things in a dream, nor even while awake, which gamblers, while playing, rave about as if overwhelmed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 19. O Yudhishthira, they do not see those things in a dream, nor even while awake, which gamblers, while playing, rave about as if overwhelmed.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।यो नः संख्ये नौरिव पारनेता जेता रिपूणां राजपुत्रस्तरस्वी ।अनर्हता लोकवीरेण तेन दीव्याम्यहं शकुने फल्गुनेन ॥२०॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,yo naḥ saṁkhye nauriva pāranetā; jetā ripūṇāṁ rājaputrastarasvī ,anarhatā lokavīreṇa tena; dīvyāmyahaṁ śakune phalgunena.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: O Śakuni, I gamble with Arjuna (Phalguna), that heroic warrior of the world who is undeserving of such a fate. He is the one who, in battle, is like a boat for us, leading us across, the conqueror of foes, the powerful prince.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 20. Yudhiṣṭhira said: O Śakuni, I gamble with Arjuna (Phalguna), that heroic warrior of the world who is undeserving of such a fate. He is the one who, in battle, is like a boat for us, leading us across, the conqueror of foes, the powerful prince.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Hearing this, Śakuni, having resolved to commit fraud and resorting to deception, said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 21. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Hearing this, Śakuni, having resolved to commit fraud and resorting to deception, said to Yudhiṣṭhira, "It is won!"

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।अयं मया पाण्डवानां धनुर्धरः पराजितः पाण्डवः सव्यसाची ।भीमेन राजन्दयितेन दीव्य यत्कैतव्यं पाण्डव तेऽवशिष्टम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,ayaṁ mayā pāṇḍavānāṁ dhanurdharaḥ; parājitaḥ pāṇḍavaḥ savyasācī ,bhīmena rājandayitena dīvya; yatkaitavyaṁ pāṇḍava te'vaśiṣṭam.
english: Śakuni said: "By me, this archer of the Pāṇḍavas, Arjuna (Savyasācī), has been defeated. O King, O Pāṇḍava, gamble with your beloved Bhīma, whatever remains as a stake for you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 22. Śakuni said: "By me, this archer of the Pāṇḍavas, Arjuna (Savyasācī), has been defeated. O King, O Pāṇḍava, gamble with your beloved Bhīma, whatever remains as a stake for you."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।यो नो नेता यो युधां नः प्रणेता यथा वज्री दानवशत्रुरेकः ।तिर्यक्प्रेक्षी संहतभ्रूर्महात्मा सिंहस्कन्धो यश्च सदात्यमर्षी ॥२३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,yo no netā yo yudhāṁ naḥ praṇetā; yathā vajrī dānavaśatrurekaḥ ,tiryakprekṣī saṁhatabhrūrmahātmā; siṁhaskandho yaśca sadātyamarṣī.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "He who is our leader, who is our chief guide in battles, just as the one Vajra-wielder (Indra) is the foe of the Dānavas - that great-souled one who glances sidelong, whose brows are furrowed, who has shoulders like a lion, and who is always exceedingly wrathful (or intolerant of wrong)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 23. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "He who is our leader, who is our chief guide in battles, just as the one Vajra-wielder (Indra) is the foe of the Dānavas - that great-souled one who glances sidelong, whose brows are furrowed, who has shoulders like a lion, and who is always exceedingly wrathful (or intolerant of wrong)."

sanskrit: बलेन तुल्यो यस्य पुमान्न विद्यते गदाभृतामग्र्य इहारिमर्दनः ।अनर्हता राजपुत्रेण तेन दीव्याम्यहं भीमसेनेन राजन् ॥२४॥
transliteration: balena tulyo yasya pumānna vidyate; gadābhṛtāmagrya ihārimardanaḥ ,anarhatā rājaputreṇa tena; dīvyāmyahaṁ bhīmasenena rājan.
english: O King, I wager with that prince Bhīmasena, who is unworthy (to be gambled away), for there is no man equal to him in strength in this world; he is the foremost among mace-wielders and the subduer of enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 24. O King, I wager with that prince Bhīmasena, who is unworthy (to be gambled away), for there is no man equal to him in strength in this world; he is the foremost among mace-wielders and the subduer of enemies.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resorting to deceit, became resolute. 'It is won!' - thus he spoke to Yudhishthira.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 25. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Shakuni, resorting to deceit, became resolute. 'It is won!' - thus he spoke to Yudhishthira.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।बहु वित्तं पराजैषीर्भ्रातॄंश्च सहयद्विपान् ।आचक्ष्व वित्तं कौन्तेय यदि तेऽस्त्यपराजितम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,bahu vittaṁ parājaiṣīrbhrātṝṁśca sahayadvipān ,ācakṣva vittaṁ kaunteya yadi te'styaparājitam.
english: Shakuni said: "You have lost much wealth (vitta), your brothers, and horses and elephants. Tell me, O son of Kunti, if any unconquered wealth (vitta) remains with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 26. Shakuni said: "You have lost much wealth (vitta), your brothers, and horses and elephants. Tell me, O son of Kunti, if any unconquered wealth (vitta) remains with you."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।अहं विशिष्टः सर्वेषां भ्रातॄणां दयितस्तथा ।कुर्यामस्ते जिताः कर्म स्वयमात्मन्युपप्लवे ॥२७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,ahaṁ viśiṣṭaḥ sarveṣāṁ bhrātṝṇāṁ dayitastathā ,kuryāmaste jitāḥ karma svayamātmanyupaplave.
english: Yudhishthira said: "I am the most distinguished and beloved of all my brothers. Now, we, having been conquered, should perform actions for you and for ourselves during this calamity."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 27. Yudhishthira said: "I am the most distinguished and beloved of all my brothers. Now, we, having been conquered, should perform actions for you and for ourselves during this calamity."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एतच्छ्रुत्वा व्यवसितो निकृतिं समुपाश्रितः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,etacchrutvā vyavasito nikṛtiṁ samupāśritaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, determined and having resorted to deceit, then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "It is won!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 28. Vaiśampāyana said: Having heard this, Śakuni, determined and having resorted to deceit, then spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, "It is won!"

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।एतत्पापिष्ठमकरोर्यदात्मानं पराजितः ।शिष्टे सति धने राजन्पाप आत्मपराजयः ॥२९॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,etatpāpiṣṭhamakaroryadātmānaṁ parājitaḥ ,śiṣṭe sati dhane rājanpāpa ātmaparājayaḥ.
english: Śakuni said: "O King, you have committed this gravest sin (pāpiṣṭham) by losing yourself (ātman) even while wealth remained. Losing one's own self (ātman) is a great transgression."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 29. Śakuni said: "O King, you have committed this gravest sin (pāpiṣṭham) by losing yourself (ātman) even while wealth remained. Losing one's own self (ātman) is a great transgression."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्त्वा मताक्षस्तान्ग्लहे सर्वानवस्थितान् ।पराजयल्लोकवीरानाक्षेपेण पृथक्पृथक् ॥३०॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktvā matākṣastānglahe sarvānavasthitān ,parājayallokavīrānākṣepeṇa pṛthakpṛthak.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Śakuni, the expert dice-player, then individually defeated all those heroes of the world who were still engaged in the game, using scornful accusations.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 30. Vaiśampāyana said: Having spoken thus, Śakuni, the expert dice-player, then individually defeated all those heroes of the world who were still engaged in the game, using scornful accusations.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।अस्ति वै ते प्रिया देवी ग्लह एकोऽपराजितः ।पणस्व कृष्णां पाञ्चालीं तयात्मानं पुनर्जय ॥३१॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,asti vai te priyā devī glaha eko'parājitaḥ ,paṇasva kṛṣṇāṁ pāñcālīṁ tayātmānaṁ punarjaya.
english: Śakuni said: "Indeed, your beloved queen (devī), Kṛṣṇā, the Pāñcālī, remains as one unconquered stake. Bet Kṛṣṇā, the Pāñcālī, and through her, win back your self (ātman)!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 31. Śakuni said: "Indeed, your beloved queen (devī), Kṛṣṇā, the Pāñcālī, remains as one unconquered stake. Bet Kṛṣṇā, the Pāñcālī, and through her, win back your self (ātman)!"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।नैव ह्रस्वा न महती नातिकृष्णा न रोहिणी ।सरागरक्तनेत्रा च तया दीव्याम्यहं त्वया ॥३२॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,naiva hrasvā na mahatī nātikṛṣṇā na rohiṇī ,sarāgaraktanetrā ca tayā dīvyāmyahaṁ tvayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "She is neither short nor very tall, neither excessively dark nor fair-skinned. Her eyes are red with passion. For her, I will gamble with you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 32. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "She is neither short nor very tall, neither excessively dark nor fair-skinned. Her eyes are red with passion. For her, I will gamble with you."

sanskrit: शारदोत्पलपत्राक्ष्या शारदोत्पलगन्धया ।शारदोत्पलसेविन्या रूपेण श्रीसमानया ॥३३॥
transliteration: śāradotpalapatrākṣyā śāradotpalagandhayā ,śāradotpalasevinyā rūpeṇa śrīsamānayā.
english: With her, whose eyes are like the petals of an autumn lotus, whose fragrance is like an autumn lotus, who is adorned with autumn lotuses, and whose beauty is equal to Śrī (Lakṣmī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 33. With her, whose eyes are like the petals of an autumn lotus, whose fragrance is like an autumn lotus, who is adorned with autumn lotuses, and whose beauty is equal to Śrī (Lakṣmī).

sanskrit: तथैव स्यादानृशंस्यात्तथा स्याद्रूपसंपदा ।तथा स्याच्छीलसंपत्त्या यामिच्छेत्पुरुषः स्त्रियम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: tathaiva syādānṛśaṁsyāttathā syādrūpasaṁpadā ,tathā syācchīlasaṁpattyā yāmicchetpuruṣaḥ striyam.
english: May she be so by virtue of her kindness, by her perfect beauty, and by her perfect character - the woman a man would desire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 34. May she be so by virtue of her kindness, by her perfect beauty, and by her perfect character - the woman a man would desire.

sanskrit: चरमं संविशति या प्रथमं प्रतिबुध्यते ।आ गोपालाविपालेभ्यः सर्वं वेद कृताकृतम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: caramaṁ saṁviśati yā prathamaṁ pratibudhyate ,ā gopālāvipālebhyaḥ sarvaṁ veda kṛtākṛtam.
english: She is the one who retires last and wakes up first. She knows everything that has been done and what remains to be done, concerning all, from cowherds to shepherds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 35. She is the one who retires last and wakes up first. She knows everything that has been done and what remains to be done, concerning all, from cowherds to shepherds.

sanskrit: आभाति पद्मवद्वक्त्रं सस्वेदं मल्लिकेव च ।वेदीमध्या दीर्घकेशी ताम्राक्षी नातिरोमशा ॥३६॥
transliteration: ābhāti padmavadvaktraṁ sasvedaṁ mallikeva ca ,vedīmadhyā dīrghakeśī tāmrākṣī nātiromaśā.
english: Her face, glistening with sweat, shines like a lotus and also like a jasmine. She possesses a slender waist (like a sacrificial altar), long hair, copper-colored eyes, and is not excessively hairy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 36. Her face, glistening with sweat, shines like a lotus and also like a jasmine. She possesses a slender waist (like a sacrificial altar), long hair, copper-colored eyes, and is not excessively hairy.

sanskrit: तयैवंविधया राजन्पाञ्चाल्याहं सुमध्यया ।ग्लहं दीव्यामि चार्वङ्ग्या द्रौपद्या हन्त सौबल ॥३७॥
transliteration: tayaivaṁvidhayā rājanpāñcālyāhaṁ sumadhyayā ,glahaṁ dīvyāmi cārvaṅgyā draupadyā hanta saubala.
english: O King, alas, O son of Subala! I am gambling this stake (glaham) – this Pāñcālī, Draupadī, who is of such a noble nature, slender-waisted, and beautiful-limbed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 37. O King, alas, O son of Subala! I am gambling this stake (glaham) – this Pāñcālī, Draupadī, who is of such a noble nature, slender-waisted, and beautiful-limbed.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्ते तु वचने धर्मराजेन भारत ।धिग्धिगित्येव वृद्धानां सभ्यानां निःसृता गिरः ॥३८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamukte tu vacane dharmarājena bhārata ,dhigdhigityeva vṛddhānāṁ sabhyānāṁ niḥsṛtā giraḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Indeed, O descendant of Bharata, when these words were uttered by the King of natural law (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), cries of 'Shame! Shame!' immediately issued forth from the elders and the assembly members."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 38. Vaiśampāyana said: "Indeed, O descendant of Bharata, when these words were uttered by the King of natural law (dharma) (Yudhiṣṭhira), cries of 'Shame! Shame!' immediately issued forth from the elders and the assembly members."

sanskrit: चुक्षुभे सा सभा राजन्राज्ञां संजज्ञिरे कथाः ।भीष्मद्रोणकृपादीनां स्वेदश्च समजायत ॥३९॥
transliteration: cukṣubhe sā sabhā rājanrājñāṁ saṁjajñire kathāḥ ,bhīṣmadroṇakṛpādīnāṁ svedaśca samajāyata.
english: O King, that assembly was agitated. Discussions arose among the kings, and perspiration (sveda) appeared on Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kṛpa, and others.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 39. O King, that assembly was agitated. Discussions arose among the kings, and perspiration (sveda) appeared on Bhīṣma, Droṇa, Kṛpa, and others.

sanskrit: शिरो गृहीत्वा विदुरो गतसत्त्व इवाभवत् ।आस्ते ध्यायन्नधोवक्त्रो निःश्वसन्पन्नगो यथा ॥४०॥
transliteration: śiro gṛhītvā viduro gatasattva ivābhavat ,āste dhyāyannadhovaktro niḥśvasanpannago yathā.
english: Vidura, holding his head, became as if lifeless. He sat meditating with his face cast downwards, sighing like a serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 40. Vidura, holding his head, became as if lifeless. He sat meditating with his face cast downwards, sighing like a serpent.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्रस्तु संहृष्टः पर्यपृच्छत्पुनः पुनः ।किं जितं किं जितमिति ह्याकारं नाभ्यरक्षत ॥४१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭrastu saṁhṛṣṭaḥ paryapṛcchatpunaḥ punaḥ ,kiṁ jitaṁ kiṁ jitamiti hyākāraṁ nābhyarakṣata.
english: But Dhṛtarāṣṭra, extremely delighted, repeatedly asked, "What has been won? What has been won?" Indeed, he could not control his demeanor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 41. But Dhṛtarāṣṭra, extremely delighted, repeatedly asked, "What has been won? What has been won?" Indeed, he could not control his demeanor.

sanskrit: जहर्ष कर्णोऽतिभृशं सह दुःशासनादिभिः ।इतरेषां तु सभ्यानां नेत्रेभ्यः प्रापतज्जलम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: jaharṣa karṇo'tibhṛśaṁ saha duḥśāsanādibhiḥ ,itareṣāṁ tu sabhyānāṁ netrebhyaḥ prāpatajjalam.
english: Karṇa rejoiced immensely, accompanied by Duḥśāsana and others. However, tears fell from the eyes of the other members of the assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 42. Karṇa rejoiced immensely, accompanied by Duḥśāsana and others. However, tears fell from the eyes of the other members of the assembly.

sanskrit: सौबलस्त्वविचार्यैव जितकाशी मदोत्कटः ।जितमित्येव तानक्षान्पुनरेवान्वपद्यत ॥४३॥
transliteration: saubalastvavicāryaiva jitakāśī madotkaṭaḥ ,jitamityeva tānakṣānpunarevānvapadyata.
english: But Saubala (Śakuni), without any consideration, boasting of his victory and arrogant, merely saying "Won!", once again seized those dice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 58 Verse 43. But Saubala (Śakuni), without any consideration, boasting of his victory and arrogant, merely saying "Won!", once again seized those dice.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।एहि क्षत्तर्द्रौपदीमानयस्व प्रियां भार्यां संमतां पाण्डवानाम् ।संमार्जतां वेश्म परैतु शीघ्रमानन्दो नः सह दासीभिरस्तु ॥१॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,ehi kṣattardraupadīmānayasva; priyāṁ bhāryāṁ saṁmatāṁ pāṇḍavānām ,saṁmārjatāṁ veśma paraitu śīghra;mānando naḥ saha dāsībhirastu.
english: Duryodhana said: "Come, O charioteer, bring Draupadī, the beloved wife esteemed by the Pāṇḍavas. Let her quickly clean the house, and let our happiness be with the female servants."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 1. Duryodhana said: "Come, O charioteer, bring Draupadī, the beloved wife esteemed by the Pāṇḍavas. Let her quickly clean the house, and let our happiness be with the female servants."

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।दुर्विभाव्यं भवति त्वादृशेन न मन्द संबुध्यसि पाशबद्धः ।प्रपाते त्वं लम्बमानो न वेत्सि व्याघ्रान्मृगः कोपयसेऽतिबाल्यात् ॥२॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,durvibhāvyaṁ bhavati tvādṛśena; na manda saṁbudhyasi pāśabaddhaḥ ,prapāte tvaṁ lambamāno na vetsi; vyāghrānmṛgaḥ kopayase'tibālyāt.
english: Vidura said: "It is difficult for someone like you to understand; O dull-witted one, you do not realize that you are bound by a snare. You are dangling on the edge of a precipice, yet you do not know it. Like a deer, you are angering tigers out of sheer foolishness."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 2. Vidura said: "It is difficult for someone like you to understand; O dull-witted one, you do not realize that you are bound by a snare. You are dangling on the edge of a precipice, yet you do not know it. Like a deer, you are angering tigers out of sheer foolishness."

sanskrit: आशीविषाः शिरसि ते पूर्णकोशा महाविषाः ।मा कोपिष्ठाः सुमन्दात्मन्मा गमस्त्वं यमक्षयम् ॥३॥
transliteration: āśīviṣāḥ śirasi te pūrṇakośā mahāviṣāḥ ,mā kopiṣṭhāḥ sumandātmanmā gamastvaṁ yamakṣayam.
english: Highly venomous snakes, with full venom sacs, are upon your head. O utterly foolish soul, do not provoke them, and do not go to the abode of Yama (death).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 3. Highly venomous snakes, with full venom sacs, are upon your head. O utterly foolish soul, do not provoke them, and do not go to the abode of Yama (death).

sanskrit: न हि दासीत्वमापन्ना कृष्णा भवति भारत ।अनीशेन हि राज्ञैषा पणे न्यस्तेति मे मतिः ॥४॥
transliteration: na hi dāsītvamāpannā kṛṣṇā bhavati bhārata ,anīśena hi rājñaiṣā paṇe nyasteti me matiḥ.
english: Vidura says: "Indeed, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) does not truly become a slave by having fallen into such a state, O Bhārata. For, it is my conviction that she was staked in the wager by a king who was not her master."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 4. Vidura says: "Indeed, Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) does not truly become a slave by having fallen into such a state, O Bhārata. For, it is my conviction that she was staked in the wager by a king who was not her master."

sanskrit: अयं धत्ते वेणुरिवात्मघाती फलं राजा धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रः ।द्यूतं हि वैराय महाभयाय पक्वो न बुध्यत्ययमन्तकाले ॥५॥
transliteration: ayaṁ dhatte veṇurivātmaghātī; phalaṁ rājā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putraḥ ,dyūtaṁ hi vairāya mahābhayāya; pakvo na budhyatyayamantakāle.
english: This son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, like a self-destructive bamboo, brings about his own ruin. Indeed, gambling leads to enmity and great fear. He does not comprehend this, although he is ripe for destruction, as the end approaches.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 5. This son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, like a self-destructive bamboo, brings about his own ruin. Indeed, gambling leads to enmity and great fear. He does not comprehend this, although he is ripe for destruction, as the end approaches.

sanskrit: नारुंतुदः स्यान्न नृशंसवादी न हीनतः परमभ्याददीत ।ययास्य वाचा पर उद्विजेत न तां वदेद्रुशतीं पापलोक्याम् ॥६॥
transliteration: nāruṁtudaḥ syānna nṛśaṁsavādī; na hīnataḥ paramabhyādadīta ,yayāsya vācā para udvijeta; na tāṁ vadedruśatīṁ pāpalokyām.
english: One should not inflict wounds (upon others) nor speak cruelly. One should not seize another's possession from an inferior person. One should not utter such harsh speech, leading to ill repute, by which another person would become agitated.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 6. One should not inflict wounds (upon others) nor speak cruelly. One should not seize another's possession from an inferior person. One should not utter such harsh speech, leading to ill repute, by which another person would become agitated.

sanskrit: समुच्चरन्त्यतिवादा हि वक्त्राद्यैराहतः शोचति रात्र्यहानि ।परस्य नामर्मसु ते पतन्ति तान्पण्डितो नावसृजेत्परेषु ॥७॥
transliteration: samuccarantyativādā hi vaktrā;dyairāhataḥ śocati rātryahāni ,parasya nāmarmasu te patanti; tānpaṇḍito nāvasṛjetpareṣu.
english: Indeed, excessive words issue forth from the mouth. One who is struck by them grieves day and night. Such words pierce the vital spots of others. Therefore, a wise person should not utter them against others.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 7. Indeed, excessive words issue forth from the mouth. One who is struck by them grieves day and night. Such words pierce the vital spots of others. Therefore, a wise person should not utter them against others.

sanskrit: अजो हि शस्त्रमखनत्किलैकः शस्त्रे विपन्ने पद्भिरपास्य भूमिम् ।निकृन्तनं स्वस्य कण्ठस्य घोरं तद्वद्वैरं मा खनीः पाण्डुपुत्रैः ॥८॥
transliteration: ajo hi śastramakhanatkilaikaḥ; śastre vipanne padbhirapāsya bhūmim ,nikṛntanaṁ svasya kaṇṭhasya ghoraṁ; tadvadvairaṁ mā khanīḥ pāṇḍuputraiḥ.
english: Indeed, a goat once dug up a weapon all by itself. When the weapon was found, it scraped away the earth with its feet, leading to the dreadful cutting of its own throat. Similarly, do not stir up enmity (vaira) with the sons of Pāṇḍu.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 8. Indeed, a goat once dug up a weapon all by itself. When the weapon was found, it scraped away the earth with its feet, leading to the dreadful cutting of its own throat. Similarly, do not stir up enmity (vaira) with the sons of Pāṇḍu.

sanskrit: न किंचिदीड्यं प्रवदन्ति पापं वनेचरं वा गृहमेधिनं वा ।तपस्विनं संपरिपूर्णविद्यं भषन्ति हैवं श्वनराः सदैव ॥९॥
transliteration: na kiṁcidīḍyaṁ pravadanti pāpaṁ; vanecaraṁ vā gṛhamedhinaṁ vā ,tapasvinaṁ saṁparipūrṇavidyaṁ; bhaṣanti haivaṁ śvanarāḥ sadaiva.
english: These dog-men (śvanarāḥ) always bark in such a way that they do not declare anything praiseworthy to be a sin, whether it pertains to a forest dweller, a householder, or even an ascetic (tapasvin) possessing complete knowledge.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 9. These dog-men (śvanarāḥ) always bark in such a way that they do not declare anything praiseworthy to be a sin, whether it pertains to a forest dweller, a householder, or even an ascetic (tapasvin) possessing complete knowledge.

sanskrit: द्वारं सुघोरं नरकस्य जिह्मं न बुध्यसे धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्र ।त्वामन्वेतारो बहवः कुरूणां द्यूतोदये सह दुःशासनेन ॥१०॥
transliteration: dvāraṁ sughoraṁ narakasya jihmaṁ; na budhyase dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putra ,tvāmanvetāro bahavaḥ kurūṇāṁ; dyūtodaye saha duḥśāsanena.
english: O son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, you do not comprehend this exceedingly dreadful and crooked gateway to hell. Many Kurus will follow you there, along with Duḥśāsana, because of this gambling (dyūta) enterprise.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 10. O son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, you do not comprehend this exceedingly dreadful and crooked gateway to hell. Many Kurus will follow you there, along with Duḥśāsana, because of this gambling (dyūta) enterprise.

sanskrit: मज्जन्त्यलाबूनि शिलाः प्लवन्ते मुह्यन्ति नावोऽम्भसि शश्वदेव ।मूढो राजा धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रो न मे वाचः पथ्यरूपाः शृणोति ॥११॥
transliteration: majjantyalābūni śilāḥ plavante; muhyanti nāvo'mbhasi śaśvadeva ,mūḍho rājā dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putro; na me vācaḥ pathyarūpāḥ śṛṇoti.
english: Gourds sink, rocks float, and boats are always bewildered in the water (ambhas). Similarly, the foolish king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, does not listen to my beneficial words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 11. Gourds sink, rocks float, and boats are always bewildered in the water (ambhas). Similarly, the foolish king, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son, does not listen to my beneficial words.

sanskrit: अन्तो नूनं भवितायं कुरूणां सुदारुणः सर्वहरो विनाशः ।वाचः काव्याः सुहृदां पथ्यरूपा न श्रूयन्ते वर्धते लोभ एव ॥१२॥
transliteration: anto nūnaṁ bhavitāyaṁ kurūṇāṁ; sudāruṇaḥ sarvaharo vināśaḥ ,vācaḥ kāvyāḥ suhṛdāṁ pathyarūpā; na śrūyante vardhate lobha eva.
english: Indeed, this will be a most dreadful, all-consuming destruction, signifying the end of the Kurus. The eloquent and beneficial words of friends are not being heeded; instead, only greed is growing.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 59 Verse 12. Indeed, this will be a most dreadful, all-consuming destruction, signifying the end of the Kurus. The eloquent and beneficial words of friends are not being heeded; instead, only greed is growing.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।धिगस्तु क्षत्तारमिति ब्रुवाणो दर्पेण मत्तो धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रः ।अवैक्षत प्रातिकामीं सभायामुवाच चैनं परमार्यमध्ये ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,dhigastu kṣattāramiti bruvāṇo; darpeṇa matto dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putraḥ ,avaikṣata prātikāmīṁ sabhāyā;muvāca cainaṁ paramāryamadhye.
english: Vaiśampaayana said: "Woe to the charioteer (Kṣattā)!" Uttering these words, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), who was intoxicated by arrogance, looked at Prātikāmī in the assembly hall and then spoke to him in the presence of the most respected nobles.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 1. Vaiśampaayana said: "Woe to the charioteer (Kṣattā)!" Uttering these words, Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), who was intoxicated by arrogance, looked at Prātikāmī in the assembly hall and then spoke to him in the presence of the most respected nobles.

sanskrit: त्वं प्रातिकामिन्द्रौपदीमानयस्व न ते भयं विद्यते पाण्डवेभ्यः ।क्षत्ता ह्ययं विवदत्येव भीरुर्न चास्माकं वृद्धिकामः सदैव ॥२॥
transliteration: tvaṁ prātikāmindraupadīmānayasva; na te bhayaṁ vidyate pāṇḍavebhyaḥ ,kṣattā hyayaṁ vivadatyeva bhīru;rna cāsmākaṁ vṛddhikāmaḥ sadaiva.
english: (Duryodhana said to Prātikāmī) "You, Prātikāmī, bring Draupadī! You have nothing to fear from the Pāṇḍavas. This charioteer (Kṣattā), being a coward, merely argues, and he is never truly concerned with our prosperity (vṛddhikāma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 2. (Duryodhana said to Prātikāmī) "You, Prātikāmī, bring Draupadī! You have nothing to fear from the Pāṇḍavas. This charioteer (Kṣattā), being a coward, merely argues, and he is never truly concerned with our prosperity (vṛddhikāma)."

sanskrit: एवमुक्तः प्रातिकामी स सूतः प्रायाच्छीघ्रं राजवचो निशम्य ।प्रविश्य च श्वेव स सिंहगोष्ठं समासदन्महिषीं पाण्डवानाम् ॥३॥
transliteration: evamuktaḥ prātikāmī sa sūtaḥ; prāyācchīghraṁ rājavaco niśamya ,praviśya ca śveva sa siṁhagoṣṭhaṁ; samāsadanmahiṣīṁ pāṇḍavānām.
english: Thus spoken to, that charioteer (Sūta) Prātikāmī, having heard the king's command, swiftly departed. And like a dog entering a lion's den, he approached the chief queen (mahiṣī) of the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 3. Thus spoken to, that charioteer (Sūta) Prātikāmī, having heard the king's command, swiftly departed. And like a dog entering a lion's den, he approached the chief queen (mahiṣī) of the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: प्रातिकाम्युवाच ।युधिष्ठिरे द्यूतमदेन मत्ते दुर्योधनो द्रौपदि त्वामजैषीत् ।सा प्रपद्य त्वं धृतराष्ट्रस्य वेश्म नयामि त्वां कर्मणे याज्ञसेनि ॥४॥
transliteration: prātikāmyuvāca ,yudhiṣṭhire dyūtamadena matte; duryodhano draupadi tvāmajaiṣīt ,sā prapadya tvaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭrasya veśma; nayāmi tvāṁ karmaṇe yājñaseni.
english: Prātikāmī said: "O Draupadī, Duryodhana has won you while Yudhiṣṭhira was intoxicated by the madness of gambling. Therefore, O Yājñasenī, come (prāpya) to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's palace (veśma); I am taking you for service (karma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 4. Prātikāmī said: "O Draupadī, Duryodhana has won you while Yudhiṣṭhira was intoxicated by the madness of gambling. Therefore, O Yājñasenī, come (prāpya) to Dhṛtarāṣṭra's palace (veśma); I am taking you for service (karma)."

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।कथं त्वेवं वदसि प्रातिकामिन्को वै दीव्येद्भार्यया राजपुत्रः ।मूढो राजा द्यूतमदेन मत्त आहो नान्यत्कैतवमस्य किंचित् ॥५॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,kathaṁ tvevaṁ vadasi prātikāmi;nko vai dīvyedbhāryayā rājaputraḥ ,mūḍho rājā dyūtamadena matta; āho nānyatkaitavamasya kiṁcit.
english: Draupadi said: "How can you speak like this, O Prātīkāmin? What prince would gamble away his wife? Is the king deluded, intoxicated by the madness of gambling, or did he truly have no other stakes (kaitava) at all?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 5. Draupadi said: "How can you speak like this, O Prātīkāmin? What prince would gamble away his wife? Is the king deluded, intoxicated by the madness of gambling, or did he truly have no other stakes (kaitava) at all?"

sanskrit: प्रातिकाम्युवाच ।यदा नाभूत्कैतवमन्यदस्य तदादेवीत्पाण्डवोऽजातशत्रुः ।न्यस्ताः पूर्वं भ्रातरस्तेन राज्ञा स्वयं चात्मा त्वमथो राजपुत्रि ॥६॥
transliteration: prātikāmyuvāca ,yadā nābhūtkaitavamanyadasya; tadādevītpāṇḍavo'jātaśatruḥ ,nyastāḥ pūrvaṁ bhrātarastena rājñā; svayaṁ cātmā tvamatho rājaputri.
english: Prātīkāmin said: "When he (Yudhiṣṭhira) had no other stakes (kaitava) left, then that Pāṇḍava, Ajātaśatru, gambled (with you). Previously, that king had already staked his brothers, and also his own self (ātman), O princess, and then you."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 6. Prātīkāmin said: "When he (Yudhiṣṭhira) had no other stakes (kaitava) left, then that Pāṇḍava, Ajātaśatru, gambled (with you). Previously, that king had already staked his brothers, and also his own self (ātman), O princess, and then you."

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।गच्छ त्वं कितवं गत्वा सभायां पृच्छ सूतज ।किं नु पूर्वं पराजैषीरात्मानं मां नु भारत ।एतज्ज्ञात्वा त्वमागच्छ ततो मां नय सूतज ॥७॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,gaccha tvaṁ kitavaṁ gatvā sabhāyāṁ pṛccha sūtaja ,kiṁ nu pūrvaṁ parājaiṣīrātmānaṁ māṁ nu bhārata ,etajjñātvā tvamāgaccha tato māṁ naya sūtaja.
english: Draupadi said: "You go to the gambler, O son of a charioteer (sūtaja), and ask in the assembly: 'Did you first lose your own self (ātman) or me, O Bhārata?' After learning this, you come back, and then, O son of a charioteer (sūtaja), take me."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 7. Draupadi said: "You go to the gambler, O son of a charioteer (sūtaja), and ask in the assembly: 'Did you first lose your own self (ātman) or me, O Bhārata?' After learning this, you come back, and then, O son of a charioteer (sūtaja), take me."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सभां गत्वा स चोवाच द्रौपद्यास्तद्वचस्तदा ।कस्येशो नः पराजैषीरिति त्वामाह द्रौपदी ।किं नु पूर्वं पराजैषीरात्मानमथ वापि माम् ॥८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sabhāṁ gatvā sa covāca draupadyāstadvacastadā ,kasyeśo naḥ parājaiṣīriti tvāmāha draupadī ,kiṁ nu pūrvaṁ parājaiṣīrātmānamatha vāpi mām.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having gone to the assembly, he then repeated Draupadi's words (to Yudhiṣṭhira): 'You who gambled us away, whose master are we now? Did you first lose your own self (ātman), or me?'"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 8. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: "Having gone to the assembly, he then repeated Draupadi's words (to Yudhiṣṭhira): 'You who gambled us away, whose master are we now? Did you first lose your own self (ātman), or me?'"

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु निश्चेष्टो गतसत्त्व इवाभवत् ।न तं सूतं प्रत्युवाच वचनं साध्वसाधु वा ॥९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu niśceṣṭo gatasattva ivābhavat ,na taṁ sūtaṁ pratyuvāca vacanaṁ sādhvasādhu vā.
english: Yudhishthira, however, became motionless, as if his vital spirit had departed. He did not reply to that charioteer with a single word, whether good or bad.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 9. Yudhishthira, however, became motionless, as if his vital spirit had departed. He did not reply to that charioteer with a single word, whether good or bad.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।इहैत्य कृष्णा पाञ्चाली प्रश्नमेतं प्रभाषताम् ।इहैव सर्वे शृण्वन्तु तस्या अस्य च यद्वचः ॥१०॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,ihaitya kṛṣṇā pāñcālī praśnametaṁ prabhāṣatām ,ihaiva sarve śṛṇvantu tasyā asya ca yadvacaḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: "Let Krishna (Draupadi), the princess of Panchala, come here and pose this question. Let everyone present here listen to whatever she and he (Yudhishthira) have to say."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 10. Duryodhana said: "Let Krishna (Draupadi), the princess of Panchala, come here and pose this question. Let everyone present here listen to whatever she and he (Yudhishthira) have to say."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।स गत्वा राजभवनं दुर्योधनवशानुगः ।उवाच द्रौपदीं सूतः प्रातिकामी व्यथन्निव ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sa gatvā rājabhavanaṁ duryodhanavaśānugaḥ ,uvāca draupadīṁ sūtaḥ prātikāmī vyathanniva.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Having reached the royal palace, the charioteer Pratikamin, who was subservient to Duryodhana, addressed Draupadi, appearing as if in great distress."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 11. Vaishampayana said: "Having reached the royal palace, the charioteer Pratikamin, who was subservient to Duryodhana, addressed Draupadi, appearing as if in great distress."

sanskrit: सभ्यास्त्वमी राजपुत्र्याह्वयन्ति मन्ये प्राप्तः संक्षयः कौरवाणाम् ।न वै समृद्धिं पालयते लघीयान्यत्त्वं सभामेष्यसि राजपुत्रि ॥१२॥
transliteration: sabhyāstvamī rājaputryāhvayanti; manye prāptaḥ saṁkṣayaḥ kauravāṇām ,na vai samṛddhiṁ pālayate laghīyā;nyattvaṁ sabhāmeṣyasi rājaputri.
english: O princess, these members of the assembly are summoning you. I believe the ultimate ruin (saṃkṣaya) of the Kauravas has arrived. Surely, an insignificant person (Duryodhana) cannot preserve his prosperity by compelling you, O princess, to come to this assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 12. O princess, these members of the assembly are summoning you. I believe the ultimate ruin (saṃkṣaya) of the Kauravas has arrived. Surely, an insignificant person (Duryodhana) cannot preserve his prosperity by compelling you, O princess, to come to this assembly.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।एवं नूनं व्यदधात्संविधाता स्पर्शावुभौ स्पृशतो धीरबालौ ।धर्मं त्वेकं परमं प्राह लोके स नः शमं धास्यति गोप्यमानः ॥१३॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,evaṁ nūnaṁ vyadadhātsaṁvidhātā; sparśāvubhau spṛśato dhīrabālau ,dharmaṁ tvekaṁ paramaṁ prāha loke; sa naḥ śamaṁ dhāsyati gopyamānaḥ.
english: Draupadi said: "Indeed, the Ordainer has thus ordained both experiences (of pleasure and pain), which affect both the wise and the ignorant. However, He has proclaimed one supreme natural law (dharma) in the world. He, remaining concealed, will grant us peace."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 13. Draupadi said: "Indeed, the Ordainer has thus ordained both experiences (of pleasure and pain), which affect both the wise and the ignorant. However, He has proclaimed one supreme natural law (dharma) in the world. He, remaining concealed, will grant us peace."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।युधिष्ठिरस्तु तच्छ्रुत्वा दुर्योधनचिकीर्षितम् ।द्रौपद्याः संमतं दूतं प्राहिणोद्भरतर्षभ ॥१४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,yudhiṣṭhirastu tacchrutvā duryodhanacikīrṣitam ,draupadyāḥ saṁmataṁ dūtaṁ prāhiṇodbharatarṣabha.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Yudhishthira, upon hearing what Duryodhana intended, and with Draupadi's consent, then sent a messenger, O best of the Bharatas."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 14. Vaiśampāyana said: "Yudhishthira, upon hearing what Duryodhana intended, and with Draupadi's consent, then sent a messenger, O best of the Bharatas."

sanskrit: एकवस्त्रा अधोनीवी रोदमाना रजस्वला ।सभामागम्य पाञ्चाली श्वशुरस्याग्रतोऽभवत् ॥१५॥
transliteration: ekavastrā adhonīvī rodamānā rajasvalā ,sabhāmāgamya pāñcālī śvaśurasyāgrato'bhavat.
english: Wearing a single garment, with her lower garment loosened, weeping, and in her menses, Panchali (Draupadi), having arrived at the assembly hall, stood before her father-in-law.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 15. Wearing a single garment, with her lower garment loosened, weeping, and in her menses, Panchali (Draupadi), having arrived at the assembly hall, stood before her father-in-law.

sanskrit: ततस्तेषां मुखमालोक्य राजा दुर्योधनः सूतमुवाच हृष्टः ।इहैवैतामानय प्रातिकामिन्प्रत्यक्षमस्याः कुरवो ब्रुवन्तु ॥१६॥
transliteration: tatasteṣāṁ mukhamālokya rājā; duryodhanaḥ sūtamuvāca hṛṣṭaḥ ,ihaivaitāmānaya prātikāmi;npratyakṣamasyāḥ kuravo bruvantu.
english: Then, seeing their faces, King Duryodhana, delighted, said to the charioteer: "Prātikāmin, bring her here directly! Let the Kurus speak in her presence."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 16. Then, seeing their faces, King Duryodhana, delighted, said to the charioteer: "Prātikāmin, bring her here directly! Let the Kurus speak in her presence."

sanskrit: ततः सूतस्तस्य वशानुगामी भीतश्च कोपाद्द्रुपदात्मजायाः ।विहाय मानं पुनरेव सभ्यानुवाच कृष्णां किमहं ब्रवीमि ॥१७॥
transliteration: tataḥ sūtastasya vaśānugāmī; bhītaśca kopāddrupadātmajāyāḥ ,vihāya mānaṁ punareva sabhyā;nuvāca kṛṣṇāṁ kimahaṁ bravīmi.
english: Then the charioteer, obedient to him and frightened by the anger of Draupadī's daughter, abandoning his pride, spoke again to the assembly members regarding Kṛṣṇā, saying, 'What shall I say?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 17. Then the charioteer, obedient to him and frightened by the anger of Draupadī's daughter, abandoning his pride, spoke again to the assembly members regarding Kṛṣṇā, saying, 'What shall I say?'

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।दुःशासनैष मम सूतपुत्रो वृकोदरादुद्विजतेऽल्पचेताः ।स्वयं प्रगृह्यानय याज्ञसेनीं किं ते करिष्यन्त्यवशाः सपत्नाः ॥१८॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,duḥśāsanaiṣa mama sūtaputro; vṛkodarādudvijate'lpacetāḥ ,svayaṁ pragṛhyānaya yājñasenīṁ; kiṁ te kariṣyantyavaśāḥ sapatnāḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: 'Oh Duḥśāsana, this charioteer's son is faint-hearted and shrinks from Bhīma (Vṛkodara). Go and seize Yājñasenī yourself. What can your powerless enemies (sapatnāḥ) do?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 18. Duryodhana said: 'Oh Duḥśāsana, this charioteer's son is faint-hearted and shrinks from Bhīma (Vṛkodara). Go and seize Yājñasenī yourself. What can your powerless enemies (sapatnāḥ) do?'

sanskrit: ततः समुत्थाय स राजपुत्रः श्रुत्वा भ्रातुः कोपविरक्तदृष्टिः ।प्रविश्य तद्वेश्म महारथानामित्यब्रवीद्द्रौपदीं राजपुत्रीम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: tataḥ samutthāya sa rājaputraḥ; śrutvā bhrātuḥ kopaviraktadṛṣṭiḥ ,praviśya tadveśma mahārathānā;mityabravīddraupadīṁ rājaputrīm.
english: Then that prince (Duḥśāsana), rising up, having heard his brother's words, with eyes inflamed by anger, entered the chamber of those great warriors and spoke thus to Princess Draupadī.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 19. Then that prince (Duḥśāsana), rising up, having heard his brother's words, with eyes inflamed by anger, entered the chamber of those great warriors and spoke thus to Princess Draupadī.

sanskrit: एह्येहि पाञ्चालि जितासि कृष्णे दुर्योधनं पश्य विमुक्तलज्जा ।कुरून्भजस्वायतपद्मनेत्रे धर्मेण लब्धासि सभां परैहि ॥२०॥
transliteration: ehyehi pāñcāli jitāsi kṛṣṇe; duryodhanaṁ paśya vimuktalajjā ,kurūnbhajasvāyatapadmanetre; dharmeṇa labdhāsi sabhāṁ paraihi.
english: 'Come, come, O Pāñcālī (Draupadī)! You have been conquered, O Kṛṣṇā! Cast off shame and behold Duryodhana. Serve the Kurus, O you with eyes like broad lotuses! You have been won by the natural law (dharma); now go to the assembly!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 20. 'Come, come, O Pāñcālī (Draupadī)! You have been conquered, O Kṛṣṇā! Cast off shame and behold Duryodhana. Serve the Kurus, O you with eyes like broad lotuses! You have been won by the natural law (dharma); now go to the assembly!'

sanskrit: ततः समुत्थाय सुदुर्मनाः सा विवर्णमामृज्य मुखं करेण ।आर्ता प्रदुद्राव यतः स्त्रियस्ता वृद्धस्य राज्ञः कुरुपुंगवस्य ॥२१॥
transliteration: tataḥ samutthāya sudurmanāḥ sā; vivarṇamāmṛjya mukhaṁ kareṇa ,ārtā pradudrāva yataḥ striyastā; vṛddhasya rājñaḥ kurupuṁgavasya.
english: Then, that deeply distressed woman rose up, wiped her pale face with her hand, and, full of anguish, ran towards where the women of the aged king, the chief of the Kurus, were gathered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 21. Then, that deeply distressed woman rose up, wiped her pale face with her hand, and, full of anguish, ran towards where the women of the aged king, the chief of the Kurus, were gathered.

sanskrit: ततो जवेनाभिससार रोषाद्दुःशासनस्तामभिगर्जमानः ।दीर्घेषु नीलेष्वथ चोर्मिमत्सु जग्राह केशेषु नरेन्द्रपत्नीम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: tato javenābhisasāra roṣā;dduḥśāsanastāmabhigarjamānaḥ ,dīrgheṣu nīleṣvatha cormimatsu; jagrāha keśeṣu narendrapatnīm.
english: Then, Duhshasana, roaring with rage, swiftly rushed towards her and seized the queen (narendrapatnī) by her long, dark, and wavy hair.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 22. Then, Duhshasana, roaring with rage, swiftly rushed towards her and seized the queen (narendrapatnī) by her long, dark, and wavy hair.

sanskrit: ये राजसूयावभृथे जलेन महाक्रतौ मन्त्रपूतेन सिक्ताः ।ते पाण्डवानां परिभूय वीर्यं बलात्प्रमृष्टा धृतराष्ट्रजेन ॥२३॥
transliteration: ye rājasūyāvabhṛthe jalena; mahākratau mantrapūtena siktāḥ ,te pāṇḍavānāṁ paribhūya vīryaṁ; balātpramṛṣṭā dhṛtarāṣṭrajena.
english: Those who were sprinkled with water purified by sacred syllables (mantra) at the concluding bath of the great Rājasūya Vedic ritual (yajña) – they, having overcome the valor of the Pāṇḍavas, were forcibly wiped away by the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 23. Those who were sprinkled with water purified by sacred syllables (mantra) at the concluding bath of the great Rājasūya Vedic ritual (yajña) – they, having overcome the valor of the Pāṇḍavas, were forcibly wiped away by the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: स तां परामृश्य सभासमीपमानीय कृष्णामतिकृष्णकेशीम् ।दुःशासनो नाथवतीमनाथवच्चकर्ष वायुः कदलीमिवार्ताम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: sa tāṁ parāmṛśya sabhāsamīpa;mānīya kṛṣṇāmatikṛṣṇakeśīm ,duḥśāsano nāthavatīmanāthava;ccakarṣa vāyuḥ kadalīmivārtām.
english: Duhshasana, having violently seized and brought that Draupadī (kṛṣṇā), whose hair was exceedingly dark, to the vicinity of the assembly hall, dragged her, who possessed protectors, as if she were utterly unprotected, just as the wind drags an afflicted banana tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 24. Duhshasana, having violently seized and brought that Draupadī (kṛṣṇā), whose hair was exceedingly dark, to the vicinity of the assembly hall, dragged her, who possessed protectors, as if she were utterly unprotected, just as the wind drags an afflicted banana tree.

sanskrit: सा कृष्यमाणा नमिताङ्गयष्टिः शनैरुवाचाद्य रजस्वलास्मि ।एकं च वासो मम मन्दबुद्धे सभां नेतुं नार्हसि मामनार्य ॥२५॥
transliteration: sā kṛṣyamāṇā namitāṅgayaṣṭiḥ; śanairuvācādya rajasvalāsmi ,ekaṁ ca vāso mama mandabuddhe; sabhāṁ netuṁ nārhasi māmanārya.
english: As she was being dragged, her slender frame bowed, she slowly said, "Today I am menstruating. And, O dull-witted one, with only one garment, you, ignoble one, ought not to lead me to the assembly."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 25. As she was being dragged, her slender frame bowed, she slowly said, "Today I am menstruating. And, O dull-witted one, with only one garment, you, ignoble one, ought not to lead me to the assembly."

sanskrit: ततोऽब्रवीत्तां प्रसभं निगृह्य केशेषु कृष्णेषु तदा स कृष्णाम् ।कृष्णं च जिष्णुं च हरिं नरं च त्राणाय विक्रोश नयामि हि त्वाम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tato'bravīttāṁ prasabhaṁ nigṛhya; keśeṣu kṛṣṇeṣu tadā sa kṛṣṇām ,kṛṣṇaṁ ca jiṣṇuṁ ca hariṁ naraṁ ca; trāṇāya vikrośa nayāmi hi tvām.
english: Then, forcibly seizing her by her dark hair, he (Duḥśāsana) said to Draupadi (kṛṣṇā) at that time, "Even if you cry out for kṛṣṇa, and Jishnu (Arjuna), and Hari (kṛṣṇa), and Nara (Arjuna) for protection, I will certainly drag you away."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 26. Then, forcibly seizing her by her dark hair, he (Duḥśāsana) said to Draupadi (kṛṣṇā) at that time, "Even if you cry out for kṛṣṇa, and Jishnu (Arjuna), and Hari (kṛṣṇa), and Nara (Arjuna) for protection, I will certainly drag you away."

sanskrit: रजस्वला वा भव याज्ञसेनि एकाम्बरा वाप्यथ वा विवस्त्रा ।द्यूते जिता चासि कृतासि दासी दासीषु कामश्च यथोपजोषम् ॥२७॥
transliteration: rajasvalā vā bhava yājñaseni; ekāmbarā vāpyatha vā vivastrā ,dyūte jitā cāsi kṛtāsi dāsī; dāsīṣu kāmaśca yathopajoṣam.
english: "Whether you are menstruating, O daughter of Drupada (yājñaseni), or you have only one garment, or you are unclothed; you have been won in the game of dice and made a slave. And among slaves, one's desires are fulfilled as one pleases."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 27. "Whether you are menstruating, O daughter of Drupada (yājñaseni), or you have only one garment, or you are unclothed; you have been won in the game of dice and made a slave. And among slaves, one's desires are fulfilled as one pleases."

sanskrit: प्रकीर्णकेशी पतितार्धवस्त्रा दुःशासनेन व्यवधूयमाना ।ह्रीमत्यमर्षेण च दह्यमाना शनैरिदं वाक्यमुवाच कृष्णा ॥२८॥
transliteration: prakīrṇakeśī patitārdhavastrā; duḥśāsanena vyavadhūyamānā ,hrīmatyamarṣeṇa ca dahyamānā; śanairidaṁ vākyamuvāca kṛṣṇā.
english: With her hair dishevelled, her garment half-fallen, and being tossed about by Duḥśāsana, Draupadi (kṛṣṇā), burning with shame and indignation, slowly uttered these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 28. With her hair dishevelled, her garment half-fallen, and being tossed about by Duḥśāsana, Draupadi (kṛṣṇā), burning with shame and indignation, slowly uttered these words.

sanskrit: इमे सभायामुपदिष्टशास्त्राः क्रियावन्तः सर्व एवेन्द्रकल्पाः ।गुरुस्थाना गुरवश्चैव सर्वे तेषामग्रे नोत्सहे स्थातुमेवम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: ime sabhāyāmupadiṣṭaśāstrāḥ; kriyāvantaḥ sarva evendrakalpāḥ ,gurusthānā guravaścaiva sarve; teṣāmagre notsahe sthātumevam.
english: All these individuals in the assembly are learned in the scriptures, active, and mighty like Indra. All of them are venerable as teachers and elders. I cannot bring myself to stand before them in this manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 29. All these individuals in the assembly are learned in the scriptures, active, and mighty like Indra. All of them are venerable as teachers and elders. I cannot bring myself to stand before them in this manner.

sanskrit: नृशंसकर्मंस्त्वमनार्यवृत्त मा मां विवस्त्रां कृधि मा विकार्षीः ।न मर्षयेयुस्तव राजपुत्राः सेन्द्रापि देवा यदि ते सहायाः ॥३०॥
transliteration: nṛśaṁsakarmaṁstvamanāryavṛtta; mā māṁ vivastrāṁ kṛdhi mā vikārṣīḥ ,na marṣayeyustava rājaputrāḥ; sendrāpi devā yadi te sahāyāḥ.
english: O you whose actions are cruel, you with ignoble conduct, do not make me unclothed, do not dishonor me! Your own princes would not tolerate such an act, even if the gods, led by Indra, were your allies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 30. O you whose actions are cruel, you with ignoble conduct, do not make me unclothed, do not dishonor me! Your own princes would not tolerate such an act, even if the gods, led by Indra, were your allies.

sanskrit: धर्मे स्थितो धर्मसुतश्च राजा धर्मश्च सूक्ष्मो निपुणोपलभ्यः ।वाचापि भर्तुः परमाणुमात्रं नेच्छामि दोषं स्वगुणान्विसृज्य ॥३१॥
transliteration: dharme sthito dharmasutaśca rājā; dharmaśca sūkṣmo nipuṇopalabhyaḥ ,vācāpi bhartuḥ paramāṇumātraṁ; necchāmi doṣaṁ svaguṇānvisṛjya.
english: The king, son of Dharma (dharma), is firm in the natural law (dharma), and the natural law (dharma) itself is subtle, discernible only by the discerning. Therefore, I do not wish to abandon my own good qualities and, even by a mere word, find the slightest fault in my husband.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 31. The king, son of Dharma (dharma), is firm in the natural law (dharma), and the natural law (dharma) itself is subtle, discernible only by the discerning. Therefore, I do not wish to abandon my own good qualities and, even by a mere word, find the slightest fault in my husband.

sanskrit: इदं त्वनार्यं कुरुवीरमध्ये रजस्वलां यत्परिकर्षसे माम् ।न चापि कश्चित्कुरुतेऽत्र पूजां ध्रुवं तवेदं मतमन्वपद्यन् ॥३२॥
transliteration: idaṁ tvanāryaṁ kuruvīramadhye; rajasvalāṁ yatparikarṣase mām ,na cāpi kaścitkurute'tra pūjāṁ; dhruvaṁ tavedaṁ matamanvapadyan.
english: But this act is indeed ignoble, that you drag me, a menstruating woman, into the midst of the Kuru heroes. Moreover, not a single person here shows me any respect. Surely, they have all adopted this intention of yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 32. But this act is indeed ignoble, that you drag me, a menstruating woman, into the midst of the Kuru heroes. Moreover, not a single person here shows me any respect. Surely, they have all adopted this intention of yours.

sanskrit: धिगस्तु नष्टः खलु भारतानां धर्मस्तथा क्षत्रविदां च वृत्तम् ।यत्राभ्यतीतां कुरुधर्मवेलां प्रेक्षन्ति सर्वे कुरवः सभायाम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: dhigastu naṣṭaḥ khalu bhāratānāṁ; dharmastathā kṣatravidāṁ ca vṛttam ,yatrābhyatītāṁ kurudharmavelāṁ; prekṣanti sarve kuravaḥ sabhāyām.
english: Shame! The constitution (dharma) of the Bhāratas and the conduct of those who uphold the warrior code are certainly ruined. All the Kurus in the assembly merely watch as the boundary of Kuru constitution (dharma) is being transgressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 33. Shame! The constitution (dharma) of the Bhāratas and the conduct of those who uphold the warrior code are certainly ruined. All the Kurus in the assembly merely watch as the boundary of Kuru constitution (dharma) is being transgressed.

sanskrit: द्रोणस्य भीष्मस्य च नास्ति सत्त्वं ध्रुवं तथैवास्य महात्मनोऽपि ।राज्ञस्तथा हीममधर्ममुग्रं न लक्षयन्ते कुरुवृद्धमुख्याः ॥३४॥
transliteration: droṇasya bhīṣmasya ca nāsti sattvaṁ; dhruvaṁ tathaivāsya mahātmano'pi ,rājñastathā hīmamadharmamugraṁ; na lakṣayante kuruvṛddhamukhyāḥ.
english: There is no strength (sattva) in Droṇa or Bhīṣma, and certainly not even in this great soul (mahātman) (Dhṛtarāṣṭra). Similarly, the chief elders among the Kurus do not perceive this terrible, despicable violation of constitution (adharma) of the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 34. There is no strength (sattva) in Droṇa or Bhīṣma, and certainly not even in this great soul (mahātman) (Dhṛtarāṣṭra). Similarly, the chief elders among the Kurus do not perceive this terrible, despicable violation of constitution (adharma) of the king.

sanskrit: तथा ब्रुवन्ती करुणं सुमध्यमा काक्षेण भर्तॄन्कुपितानपश्यत् ।सा पाण्डवान्कोपपरीतदेहान्संदीपयामास कटाक्षपातैः ॥३५॥
transliteration: tathā bruvantī karuṇaṁ sumadhyamā; kākṣeṇa bhartṝnkupitānapaśyat ,sā pāṇḍavānkopaparītadehā;nsaṁdīpayāmāsa kaṭākṣapātaiḥ.
english: Thus, the slender-waisted Draupadī, speaking mournfully, looked at her enraged husbands. She ignited the Pāṇḍavas, whose bodies were overcome with anger, with her side-long glances.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 35. Thus, the slender-waisted Draupadī, speaking mournfully, looked at her enraged husbands. She ignited the Pāṇḍavas, whose bodies were overcome with anger, with her side-long glances.

sanskrit: हृतेन राज्येन तथा धनेन रत्नैश्च मुख्यैर्न तथा बभूव ।यथार्तया कोपसमीरितेन कृष्णाकटाक्षेण बभूव दुःखम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: hṛtena rājyena tathā dhanena; ratnaiśca mukhyairna tathā babhūva ,yathārtayā kopasamīritena; kṛṣṇākaṭākṣeṇa babhūva duḥkham.
english: The sorrow caused by the seizing of the kingdom, the wealth, and the principal jewels was not as profound as the suffering (duḥkha) that occurred due to the distressed Draupadī's anger-stirred side-long glance.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 36. The sorrow caused by the seizing of the kingdom, the wealth, and the principal jewels was not as profound as the suffering (duḥkha) that occurred due to the distressed Draupadī's anger-stirred side-long glance.

sanskrit: दुःशासनश्चापि समीक्ष्य कृष्णामवेक्षमाणां कृपणान्पतींस्तान् ।आधूय वेगेन विसंज्ञकल्पामुवाच दासीति हसन्निवोग्रः ॥३७॥
transliteration: duḥśāsanaścāpi samīkṣya kṛṣṇā;mavekṣamāṇāṁ kṛpaṇānpatīṁstān ,ādhūya vegena visaṁjñakalpā;muvāca dāsīti hasannivograḥ.
english: And the fierce Duśśāsana, laughing, seeing Kṛṣṇā looking at her pitiable husbands, violently shook her, who was almost senseless, and called her a slave girl.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 37. And the fierce Duśśāsana, laughing, seeing Kṛṣṇā looking at her pitiable husbands, violently shook her, who was almost senseless, and called her a slave girl.

sanskrit: कर्णस्तु तद्वाक्यमतीव हृष्टः संपूजयामास हसन्सशब्दम् ।गान्धारराजः सुबलस्य पुत्रस्तथैव दुःशासनमभ्यनन्दत् ॥३८॥
transliteration: karṇastu tadvākyamatīva hṛṣṭaḥ; saṁpūjayāmāsa hasansaśabdam ,gāndhārarājaḥ subalasya putra;stathaiva duḥśāsanamabhyanandat.
english: Karṇa, indeed, was exceedingly delighted by that statement and applauded loudly while laughing. Similarly, the king of Gandhāra, Subala's son, congratulated Duśśāsana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 38. Karṇa, indeed, was exceedingly delighted by that statement and applauded loudly while laughing. Similarly, the king of Gandhāra, Subala's son, congratulated Duśśāsana.

sanskrit: सभ्यास्तु ये तत्र बभूवुरन्ये ताभ्यामृते धार्तराष्ट्रेण चैव ।तेषामभूद्दुःखमतीव कृष्णां दृष्ट्वा सभायां परिकृष्यमाणाम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: sabhyāstu ye tatra babhūvuranye; tābhyāmṛte dhārtarāṣṭreṇa caiva ,teṣāmabhūdduḥkhamatīva kṛṣṇāṁ; dṛṣṭvā sabhāyāṁ parikṛṣyamāṇām.
english: But for those other courtiers present there, excluding those two (Karṇa and Duśśāsana) and Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), great sorrow arose upon seeing Kṛṣṇā being dragged in the assembly hall.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 39. But for those other courtiers present there, excluding those two (Karṇa and Duśśāsana) and Dhṛtarāṣṭra's son (Duryodhana), great sorrow arose upon seeing Kṛṣṇā being dragged in the assembly hall.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।न धर्मसौक्ष्म्यात्सुभगे विवक्तुं शक्नोमि ते प्रश्नमिमं यथावत् ।अस्वो ह्यशक्तः पणितुं परस्वं स्त्रियश्च भर्तुर्वशतां समीक्ष्य ॥४०॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,na dharmasaukṣmyātsubhage vivaktuṁ; śaknomi te praśnamimaṁ yathāvat ,asvo hyaśaktaḥ paṇituṁ parasvaṁ; striyaśca bharturvaśatāṁ samīkṣya.
english: Bhīṣma said: "O fortunate one, due to the extreme subtlety of the natural law (dharma), I am unable to properly answer this question for you. This is because a man without possessions is indeed unable to stake another's property, and one must also consider a wife's complete subjection to her husband."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 40. Bhīṣma said: "O fortunate one, due to the extreme subtlety of the natural law (dharma), I am unable to properly answer this question for you. This is because a man without possessions is indeed unable to stake another's property, and one must also consider a wife's complete subjection to her husband."

sanskrit: त्यजेत सर्वां पृथिवीं समृद्धां युधिष्ठिरः सत्यमथो न जह्यात् ।उक्तं जितोऽस्मीति च पाण्डवेन तस्मान्न शक्नोमि विवेक्तुमेतत् ॥४१॥
transliteration: tyajeta sarvāṁ pṛthivīṁ samṛddhāṁ; yudhiṣṭhiraḥ satyamatho na jahyāt ,uktaṁ jito'smīti ca pāṇḍavena; tasmānna śaknomi vivektumetat.
english: Yudhishthira would give up the entire prosperous earth, but he would never abandon truth. Moreover, the son of Pandu himself declared, "I am conquered." Therefore, I am unable to make sense of this situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 41. Yudhishthira would give up the entire prosperous earth, but he would never abandon truth. Moreover, the son of Pandu himself declared, "I am conquered." Therefore, I am unable to make sense of this situation.

sanskrit: द्यूतेऽद्वितीयः शकुनिर्नरेषु कुन्तीसुतस्तेन निसृष्टकामः ।न मन्यते तां निकृतिं महात्मा तस्मान्न ते प्रश्नमिमं ब्रवीमि ॥४२॥
transliteration: dyūte'dvitīyaḥ śakunirnareṣu; kuntīsutastena nisṛṣṭakāmaḥ ,na manyate tāṁ nikṛtiṁ mahātmā; tasmānna te praśnamimaṁ bravīmi.
english: Shakuni is unequaled among men in dice-play. The son of Kunti (Yudhishthira) had all his desires (kāma) surrendered by him (through Shakuni's actions). Yet, that great-souled one (Yudhishthira) does not consider that act to be deceit. Therefore, I cannot answer this question of yours.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 42. Shakuni is unequaled among men in dice-play. The son of Kunti (Yudhishthira) had all his desires (kāma) surrendered by him (through Shakuni's actions). Yet, that great-souled one (Yudhishthira) does not consider that act to be deceit. Therefore, I cannot answer this question of yours.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।आहूय राजा कुशलैः सभायां दुष्टात्मभिर्नैकृतिकैरनार्यैः ।द्यूतप्रियैर्नातिकृतप्रयत्नः कस्मादयं नाम निसृष्टकामः ॥४३॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,āhūya rājā kuśalaiḥ sabhāyāṁ; duṣṭātmabhirnaikṛtikairanāryaiḥ ,dyūtapriyairnātikṛtaprayatnaḥ; kasmādayaṁ nāma nisṛṣṭakāmaḥ.
english: Draupadi said: "When the king (Yudhishthira) was summoned to the assembly by those skilled in dice, by evil-minded, deceitful, ignoble individuals who were fond of gambling, and he himself had not made sufficient effort, why then is this one called 'one who has surrendered all his desires (kāma)'?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 43. Draupadi said: "When the king (Yudhishthira) was summoned to the assembly by those skilled in dice, by evil-minded, deceitful, ignoble individuals who were fond of gambling, and he himself had not made sufficient effort, why then is this one called 'one who has surrendered all his desires (kāma)'?"

sanskrit: स शुद्धभावो निकृतिप्रवृत्तिमबुध्यमानः कुरुपाण्डवाग्र्यः ।संभूय सर्वैश्च जितोऽपि यस्मात्पश्चाच्च यत्कैतवमभ्युपेतः ॥४४॥
transliteration: sa śuddhabhāvo nikṛtipravṛtti;mabudhyamānaḥ kurupāṇḍavāgryaḥ ,saṁbhūya sarvaiśca jito'pi yasmā;tpaścācca yatkaitavamabhyupetaḥ.
english: That pure-minded one (Yudhishthira), the foremost of the Kurus and Pandavas, not comprehending the nature of deceit, was conquered even by all (of them) combined. And because he subsequently accepted that fraudulent outcome...
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 44. That pure-minded one (Yudhishthira), the foremost of the Kurus and Pandavas, not comprehending the nature of deceit, was conquered even by all (of them) combined. And because he subsequently accepted that fraudulent outcome...

sanskrit: तिष्ठन्ति चेमे कुरवः सभायामीशाः सुतानां च तथा स्नुषाणाम् ।समीक्ष्य सर्वे मम चापि वाक्यं विब्रूत मे प्रश्नमिमं यथावत् ॥४५॥
transliteration: tiṣṭhanti ceme kuravaḥ sabhāyā;mīśāḥ sutānāṁ ca tathā snuṣāṇām ,samīkṣya sarve mama cāpi vākyaṁ; vibrūta me praśnamimaṁ yathāvat.
english: These Kurus are present in this assembly, and they are the masters of their sons and likewise of their daughters-in-law. Having considered my words, all of you should properly answer this question of mine.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 45. These Kurus are present in this assembly, and they are the masters of their sons and likewise of their daughters-in-law. Having considered my words, all of you should properly answer this question of mine.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा ब्रुवन्तीं करुणं रुदन्तीमवेक्षमाणामसकृत्पतींस्तान् ।दुःशासनः परुषाण्यप्रियाणि वाक्यान्युवाचामधुराणि चैव ॥४६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā bruvantīṁ karuṇaṁ rudantī;mavekṣamāṇāmasakṛtpatīṁstān ,duḥśāsanaḥ paruṣāṇyapriyāṇi; vākyānyuvācāmadhurāṇi caiva.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: To her who was speaking so mournfully, weeping, and repeatedly looking at those husbands, Duḥśāsana spoke harsh, unpleasant, and bitter words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 46. Vaiśampāyana said: To her who was speaking so mournfully, weeping, and repeatedly looking at those husbands, Duḥśāsana spoke harsh, unpleasant, and bitter words.

sanskrit: तां कृष्यमाणां च रजस्वलां च स्रस्तोत्तरीयामतदर्हमाणाम् ।वृकोदरः प्रेक्ष्य युधिष्ठिरं च चकार कोपं परमार्तरूपः ॥४७॥
transliteration: tāṁ kṛṣyamāṇāṁ ca rajasvalāṁ ca; srastottarīyāmatadarhamāṇām ,vṛkodaraḥ prekṣya yudhiṣṭhiraṁ ca; cakāra kopaṁ paramārtarūpaḥ.
english: When Vṛkodara (Bhīma) saw her being dragged while menstruating, with her upper garment fallen, and undeserving of such treatment, he then looked at Yudhiṣṭhira and, in a state of extreme distress, became furious.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 60 Verse 47. When Vṛkodara (Bhīma) saw her being dragged while menstruating, with her upper garment fallen, and undeserving of such treatment, he then looked at Yudhiṣṭhira and, in a state of extreme distress, became furious.

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।भवन्ति देशे बन्धक्यः कितवानां युधिष्ठिर ।न ताभिरुत दीव्यन्ति दया चैवास्ति तास्वपि ॥१॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,bhavanti deśe bandhakyaḥ kitavānāṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,na tābhiruta dīvyanti dayā caivāsti tāsvapi.
english: Bhīma said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, in this country, gamblers have their female slaves. However, they do not gamble with them, and even compassion is shown towards them.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 1. Bhīma said: 'O Yudhiṣṭhira, in this country, gamblers have their female slaves. However, they do not gamble with them, and even compassion is shown towards them.'

sanskrit: काश्यो यद्बलिमाहार्षीद्द्रव्यं यच्चान्यदुत्तमम् ।तथान्ये पृथिवीपाला यानि रत्नान्युपाहरन् ॥२॥
transliteration: kāśyo yadbalimāhārṣīddravyaṁ yaccānyaduttamam ,tathānye pṛthivīpālā yāni ratnānyupāharan.
english: The tribute that the king of Kashi brought, along with other excellent wealth, and similarly, the jewels that other rulers offered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 2. The tribute that the king of Kashi brought, along with other excellent wealth, and similarly, the jewels that other rulers offered.

sanskrit: वाहनानि धनं चैव कवचान्यायुधानि च ।राज्यमात्मा वयं चैव कैतवेन हृतं परैः ॥३॥
transliteration: vāhanāni dhanaṁ caiva kavacānyāyudhāni ca ,rājyamātmā vayaṁ caiva kaitavena hṛtaṁ paraiḥ.
english: Vehicles, wealth, armors, and weapons - indeed, even the kingdom, our very selves (ātman), and we ourselves - have all been seized by enemies through trickery.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 3. Vehicles, wealth, armors, and weapons - indeed, even the kingdom, our very selves (ātman), and we ourselves - have all been seized by enemies through trickery.

sanskrit: न च मे तत्र कोपोऽभूत्सर्वस्येशो हि नो भवान् ।इदं त्वतिकृतं मन्ये द्रौपदी यत्र पण्यते ॥४॥
transliteration: na ca me tatra kopo'bhūtsarvasyeśo hi no bhavān ,idaṁ tvatikṛtaṁ manye draupadī yatra paṇyate.
english: And in that situation, I felt no anger, for you are indeed our lord of everything. But this current act, I consider it utterly outrageous, where Draupadi is being gambled away.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 4. And in that situation, I felt no anger, for you are indeed our lord of everything. But this current act, I consider it utterly outrageous, where Draupadi is being gambled away.

sanskrit: एषा ह्यनर्हती बाला पाण्डवान्प्राप्य कौरवैः ।त्वत्कृते क्लिश्यते क्षुद्रैर्नृशंसैर्निकृतिप्रियैः ॥५॥
transliteration: eṣā hyanarhatī bālā pāṇḍavānprāpya kauravaiḥ ,tvatkṛte kliśyate kṣudrairnṛśaṁsairnikṛtipriyaiḥ.
