english: This young woman, indeed undeserving (of such a fate), after joining the Pāṇḍavas, is being tormented by these vile, cruel, and deceit-loving Kauravas because of you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 5. This young woman, indeed undeserving (of such a fate), after joining the Pāṇḍavas, is being tormented by these vile, cruel, and deceit-loving Kauravas because of you.

sanskrit: अस्याः कृते मन्युरयं त्वयि राजन्निपात्यते ।बाहू ते संप्रधक्ष्यामि सहदेवाग्निमानय ॥६॥
transliteration: asyāḥ kṛte manyurayaṁ tvayi rājannipātyate ,bāhū te saṁpradhakṣyāmi sahadevāgnimānaya.
english: O King, this rage is being unleashed upon you because of her. I will completely burn your arms. Sahadeva, bring the fire!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 6. O King, this rage is being unleashed upon you because of her. I will completely burn your arms. Sahadeva, bring the fire!

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।न पुरा भीमसेन त्वमीदृशीर्वदिता गिरः ।परैस्ते नाशितं नूनं नृशंसैर्धर्मगौरवम् ॥७॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,na purā bhīmasena tvamīdṛśīrvaditā giraḥ ,paraiste nāśitaṁ nūnaṁ nṛśaṁsairdharmagauravam.
english: Arjuna said: 'O Bhimasena, you have never spoken such words before. Surely, your esteem for what is right (dharma) has been destroyed by these cruel enemies.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 7. Arjuna said: 'O Bhimasena, you have never spoken such words before. Surely, your esteem for what is right (dharma) has been destroyed by these cruel enemies.'

sanskrit: न सकामाः परे कार्या धर्ममेवाचरोत्तमम् ।भ्रातरं धार्मिकं ज्येष्ठं नातिक्रमितुमर्हति ॥८॥
transliteration: na sakāmāḥ pare kāryā dharmamevācarottamam ,bhrātaraṁ dhārmikaṁ jyeṣṭhaṁ nātikramitumarhati.
english: Do not fulfill the desires of the enemies; instead, adhere only to the highest (dharma). You ought not to transgress your righteous elder brother.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 8. Do not fulfill the desires of the enemies; instead, adhere only to the highest (dharma). You ought not to transgress your righteous elder brother.

sanskrit: आहूतो हि परै राजा क्षात्रधर्ममनुस्मरन् ।दीव्यते परकामेन तन्नः कीर्तिकरं महत् ॥९॥
transliteration: āhūto hi parai rājā kṣātradharmamanusmaran ,dīvyate parakāmena tannaḥ kīrtikaraṁ mahat.
english: For a king, when challenged by enemies and remembering his warrior's duty (kṣātram dharma), plays at the opponent's will; that is a great source of glory for us.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 9. For a king, when challenged by enemies and remembering his warrior's duty (kṣātram dharma), plays at the opponent's will; that is a great source of glory for us.

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।एवमस्मिकृतं विद्यां यद्यस्याहं धनंजय ।दीप्तेऽग्नौ सहितौ बाहू निर्दहेयं बलादिव ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,evamasmikṛtaṁ vidyāṁ yadyasyāhaṁ dhanaṁjaya ,dīpte'gnau sahitau bāhū nirdaheyaṁ balādiva.
english: Bhīmasena said: 'O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), if I, who am her protector, allow this (humiliation) to be seen as accomplished, then I would rather burn my two arms together in a blazing fire, as if compelled by force.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 10. Bhīmasena said: 'O Dhanañjaya (Arjuna), if I, who am her protector, allow this (humiliation) to be seen as accomplished, then I would rather burn my two arms together in a blazing fire, as if compelled by force.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा तान्दुःखितान्दृष्ट्वा पाण्डवान्धृतराष्ट्रजः ।क्लिश्यमानां च पाञ्चालीं विकर्ण इदमब्रवीत् ॥११॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā tānduḥkhitāndṛṣṭvā pāṇḍavāndhṛtarāṣṭrajaḥ ,kliśyamānāṁ ca pāñcālīṁ vikarṇa idamabravīt.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having thus seen those distressed Pāṇḍavas and Pāñcālī (Draupadi) suffering, Vikarṇa, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, spoke these words.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 11. Vaiśampāyana said: 'Having thus seen those distressed Pāṇḍavas and Pāñcālī (Draupadi) suffering, Vikarṇa, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, spoke these words.'

sanskrit: याज्ञसेन्या यदुक्तं तद्वाक्यं विब्रूत पार्थिवाः ।अविवेकेन वाक्यस्य नरकः सद्य एव नः ॥१२॥
transliteration: yājñasenyā yaduktaṁ tadvākyaṁ vibrūta pārthivāḥ ,avivekena vākyasya narakaḥ sadya eva naḥ.
english: O kings, explain what Yājñasenī (Draupadi) has said! For by (our) lack of discernment regarding her statement, hell will immediately be ours.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 12. O kings, explain what Yājñasenī (Draupadi) has said! For by (our) lack of discernment regarding her statement, hell will immediately be ours.

sanskrit: भीष्मश्च धृतराष्ट्रश्च कुरुवृद्धतमावुभौ ।समेत्य नाहतुः किंचिद्विदुरश्च महामतिः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhīṣmaśca dhṛtarāṣṭraśca kuruvṛddhatamāvubhau ,sametya nāhatuḥ kiṁcidviduraśca mahāmatiḥ.
english: Bhīṣma and Dhṛtarāṣṭra, both the oldest among the Kurus, having gathered, did not say anything; nor did the great-minded Vidura.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 13. Bhīṣma and Dhṛtarāṣṭra, both the oldest among the Kurus, having gathered, did not say anything; nor did the great-minded Vidura.

sanskrit: भारद्वाजोऽपि सर्वेषामाचार्यः कृप एव च ।अत एतावपि प्रश्नं नाहतुर्द्विजसत्तमौ ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhāradvājo'pi sarveṣāmācāryaḥ kṛpa eva ca ,ata etāvapi praśnaṁ nāhaturdvijasattamau.
english: Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) was the preceptor of all, and Kṛpa was also there. Therefore, even these two, the foremost among the twice-born (dvijasattamau), did not address the question.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 14. Bhāradvāja (Droṇa) was the preceptor of all, and Kṛpa was also there. Therefore, even these two, the foremost among the twice-born (dvijasattamau), did not address the question.

sanskrit: ये त्वन्ये पृथिवीपालाः समेताः सर्वतो दिशः ।कामक्रोधौ समुत्सृज्य ते ब्रुवन्तु यथामति ॥१५॥
transliteration: ye tvanye pṛthivīpālāḥ sametāḥ sarvato diśaḥ ,kāmakrodhau samutsṛjya te bruvantu yathāmati.
english: But as for the other kings, assembled from all directions, let them abandon desire (kāma) and anger and speak according to their intelligence.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 15. But as for the other kings, assembled from all directions, let them abandon desire (kāma) and anger and speak according to their intelligence.

sanskrit: यदिदं द्रौपदी वाक्यमुक्तवत्यसकृच्छुभा ।विमृश्य कस्य कः पक्षः पार्थिवा वदतोत्तरम् ॥१६॥
transliteration: yadidaṁ draupadī vākyamuktavatyasakṛcchubhā ,vimṛśya kasya kaḥ pakṣaḥ pārthivā vadatottaram.
english: O kings (pārthivāḥ), having deliberated on this statement that the virtuous (śubhā) Draupadī has repeatedly uttered, and considering whose side is whose, you should then declare your answer.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 16. O kings (pārthivāḥ), having deliberated on this statement that the virtuous (śubhā) Draupadī has repeatedly uttered, and considering whose side is whose, you should then declare your answer.

sanskrit: एवं स बहुशः सर्वानुक्तवांस्तान्सभासदः ।न च ते पृथिवीपालास्तमूचुः साध्वसाधु वा ॥१७॥
transliteration: evaṁ sa bahuśaḥ sarvānuktavāṁstānsabhāsadaḥ ,na ca te pṛthivīpālāstamūcuḥ sādhvasādhu vā.
english: Thus, he (Vidura) repeatedly addressed all those members of the assembly. However, those kings (pṛthivīpālāḥ) did not say anything to him, neither right (sādhu) nor wrong.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 17. Thus, he (Vidura) repeatedly addressed all those members of the assembly. However, those kings (pṛthivīpālāḥ) did not say anything to him, neither right (sādhu) nor wrong.

sanskrit: उक्त्वा तथासकृत्सर्वान्विकर्णः पृथिवीपतीन् ।पाणिं पाणौ विनिष्पिष्य निःश्वसन्निदमब्रवीत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: uktvā tathāsakṛtsarvānvikarṇaḥ pṛthivīpatīn ,pāṇiṁ pāṇau viniṣpiṣya niḥśvasannidamabravīt.
english: After speaking thus repeatedly to all the kings, Vikarna, pressing his hands together, sighed and said this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 18. After speaking thus repeatedly to all the kings, Vikarna, pressing his hands together, sighed and said this.

sanskrit: विब्रूत पृथिवीपाला वाक्यं मा वा कथंचन ।मन्ये न्याय्यं यदत्राहं तद्धि वक्ष्यामि कौरवाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: vibrūta pṛthivīpālā vākyaṁ mā vā kathaṁcana ,manye nyāyyaṁ yadatrāhaṁ taddhi vakṣyāmi kauravāḥ.
english: O rulers of the earth, either speak your mind or say nothing at all. I, for my part, will indeed speak what I consider just in this matter, O Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 19. O rulers of the earth, either speak your mind or say nothing at all. I, for my part, will indeed speak what I consider just in this matter, O Kauravas.

sanskrit: चत्वार्याहुर्नरश्रेष्ठा व्यसनानि महीक्षिताम् ।मृगयां पानमक्षांश्च ग्राम्ये चैवातिसक्तताम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: catvāryāhurnaraśreṣṭhā vyasanāni mahīkṣitām ,mṛgayāṁ pānamakṣāṁśca grāmye caivātisaktatām.
english: The most excellent among men (naraśreṣṭhāḥ) declare that there are four vices (vyasanāni) for rulers (mahīkṣitām): hunting, drinking, dice (gambling), and excessive attachment to sensual pleasures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 20. The most excellent among men (naraśreṣṭhāḥ) declare that there are four vices (vyasanāni) for rulers (mahīkṣitām): hunting, drinking, dice (gambling), and excessive attachment to sensual pleasures.

sanskrit: एतेषु हि नरः सक्तो धर्ममुत्सृज्य वर्तते ।तथायुक्तेन च कृतां क्रियां लोको न मन्यते ॥२१॥
transliteration: eteṣu hi naraḥ sakto dharmamutsṛjya vartate ,tathāyuktena ca kṛtāṁ kriyāṁ loko na manyate.
english: Indeed, a man (naraḥ) who is addicted (saktaḥ) to these (vices), abandoning his natural law (dharma), conducts himself accordingly. And the world (lokaḥ) does not approve of an action (karma) performed by one thus engaged.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 21. Indeed, a man (naraḥ) who is addicted (saktaḥ) to these (vices), abandoning his natural law (dharma), conducts himself accordingly. And the world (lokaḥ) does not approve of an action (karma) performed by one thus engaged.

sanskrit: तदयं पाण्डुपुत्रेण व्यसने वर्तता भृशम् ।समाहूतेन कितवैरास्थितो द्रौपदीपणः ॥२२॥
transliteration: tadayaṁ pāṇḍuputreṇa vyasane vartatā bhṛśam ,samāhūtena kitavairāsthito draupadīpaṇaḥ.
english: Therefore, this son of Pāṇḍu, being deeply afflicted by misfortune, was invited by the gamblers, and Draupadī was made the stake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 22. Therefore, this son of Pāṇḍu, being deeply afflicted by misfortune, was invited by the gamblers, and Draupadī was made the stake.

sanskrit: साधारणी च सर्वेषां पाण्डवानामनिन्दिता ।जितेन पूर्वं चानेन पाण्डवेन कृतः पणः ॥२३॥
transliteration: sādhāraṇī ca sarveṣāṁ pāṇḍavānāmaninditā ,jitena pūrvaṁ cānena pāṇḍavena kṛtaḥ paṇaḥ.
english: And she, irreproachable, is common to all the Pāṇḍavas. Moreover, by this Pāṇḍava, who was previously conquered, she was made a stake.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 23. And she, irreproachable, is common to all the Pāṇḍavas. Moreover, by this Pāṇḍava, who was previously conquered, she was made a stake.

sanskrit: इयं च कीर्तिता कृष्णा सौबलेन पणार्थिना ।एतत्सर्वं विचार्याहं मन्ये न विजितामिमाम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: iyaṁ ca kīrtitā kṛṣṇā saubalena paṇārthinā ,etatsarvaṁ vicāryāhaṁ manye na vijitāmimām.
english: And this Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was declared (a stake) by Śakuni, who was eager for the wager. Considering all this, I believe that she was not rightfully won.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 24. And this Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) was declared (a stake) by Śakuni, who was eager for the wager. Considering all this, I believe that she was not rightfully won.

sanskrit: एतच्छ्रुत्वा महान्नादः सभ्यानामुदतिष्ठत ।विकर्णं शंसमानानां सौबलं च विनिन्दताम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: etacchrutvā mahānnādaḥ sabhyānāmudatiṣṭhata ,vikarṇaṁ śaṁsamānānāṁ saubalaṁ ca vinindatām.
english: Having heard this, a great uproar arose from the assembly members, as they praised Vikarna and condemned Śakuni.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 25. Having heard this, a great uproar arose from the assembly members, as they praised Vikarna and condemned Śakuni.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्नुपरते शब्दे राधेयः क्रोधमूर्छितः ।प्रगृह्य रुचिरं बाहुमिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥२६॥
transliteration: tasminnuparate śabde rādheyaḥ krodhamūrchitaḥ ,pragṛhya ruciraṁ bāhumidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: When that sound ceased, Karṇa, maddened with rage, grasped his beautiful arm and spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 26. When that sound ceased, Karṇa, maddened with rage, grasped his beautiful arm and spoke these words.

sanskrit: दृश्यन्ते वै विकर्णे हि वैकृतानि बहून्यपि ।तज्जस्तस्य विनाशाय यथाग्निररणिप्रजः ॥२७॥
transliteration: dṛśyante vai vikarṇe hi vaikṛtāni bahūnyapi ,tajjastasya vināśāya yathāgniraraṇiprajaḥ.
english: Indeed, many disgraceful acts are seen even in Vikarṇa. What is born from something leads to its destruction, just as fire born from friction sticks (araṇi) destroys them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 27. Indeed, many disgraceful acts are seen even in Vikarṇa. What is born from something leads to its destruction, just as fire born from friction sticks (araṇi) destroys them.

sanskrit: एते न किंचिदप्याहुश्चोद्यमानापि कृष्णया ।धर्मेण विजितां मन्ये मन्यन्ते द्रुपदात्मजाम् ॥२८॥
transliteration: ete na kiṁcidapyāhuścodyamānāpi kṛṣṇayā ,dharmeṇa vijitāṁ manye manyante drupadātmajām.
english: These (elders) say nothing at all, even when urged by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī). I think they consider the daughter of Drupada (Draupadī) to have been won according to natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 28. These (elders) say nothing at all, even when urged by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī). I think they consider the daughter of Drupada (Draupadī) to have been won according to natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: त्वं तु केवलबाल्येन धार्तराष्ट्र विदीर्यसे ।यद्ब्रवीषि सभामध्ये बालः स्थविरभाषितम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: tvaṁ tu kevalabālyena dhārtarāṣṭra vidīryase ,yadbravīṣi sabhāmadhye bālaḥ sthavirabhāṣitam.
english: But you, O scion of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Vikarṇa), are merely bewildered by your youth, because in this assembly, you, a mere boy, speak words fit for an elder.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 29. But you, O scion of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Vikarṇa), are merely bewildered by your youth, because in this assembly, you, a mere boy, speak words fit for an elder.

sanskrit: न च धर्मं यथातत्त्वं वेत्सि दुर्योधनावर ।यद्ब्रवीषि जितां कृष्णामजितेति सुमन्दधीः ॥३०॥
transliteration: na ca dharmaṁ yathātattvaṁ vetsi duryodhanāvara ,yadbravīṣi jitāṁ kṛṣṇāmajiteti sumandadhīḥ.
english: O younger brother of Duryodhana, you do not truly know the righteous conduct (dharma), because you, with your extremely dull intellect, declare Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) to be unconquered, even though she has been won.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 30. O younger brother of Duryodhana, you do not truly know the righteous conduct (dharma), because you, with your extremely dull intellect, declare Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) to be unconquered, even though she has been won.

sanskrit: कथं ह्यविजितां कृष्णां मन्यसे धृतराष्ट्रज ।यदा सभायां सर्वस्वं न्यस्तवान्पाण्डवाग्रजः ॥३१॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hyavijitāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ manyase dhṛtarāṣṭraja ,yadā sabhāyāṁ sarvasvaṁ nyastavānpāṇḍavāgrajaḥ.
english: How indeed, O son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, do you consider Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) to be unconquered, when the eldest of the Pāṇḍavas (Yudhiṣṭhira) had already staked all his possessions in the assembly?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 31. How indeed, O son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, do you consider Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) to be unconquered, when the eldest of the Pāṇḍavas (Yudhiṣṭhira) had already staked all his possessions in the assembly?

sanskrit: अभ्यन्तरा च सर्वस्वे द्रौपदी भरतर्षभ ।एवं धर्मजितां कृष्णां मन्यसे न जितां कथम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: abhyantarā ca sarvasve draupadī bharatarṣabha ,evaṁ dharmajitāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ manyase na jitāṁ katham.
english: And Draupadī, O best of the Bharatas, was included among all those possessions. How then do you not consider Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was thus won according to righteous conduct (dharma), to be truly won?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 32. And Draupadī, O best of the Bharatas, was included among all those possessions. How then do you not consider Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), who was thus won according to righteous conduct (dharma), to be truly won?

sanskrit: कीर्तिता द्रौपदी वाचा अनुज्ञाता च पाण्डवैः ।भवत्यविजिता केन हेतुनैषा मता तव ॥३३॥
transliteration: kīrtitā draupadī vācā anujñātā ca pāṇḍavaiḥ ,bhavatyavijitā kena hetunaiṣā matā tava.
english: Draupadī was declared by name and assented to by the Pāṇḍavas. By what reason is she, then, considered unconquered by you?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 33. Draupadī was declared by name and assented to by the Pāṇḍavas. By what reason is she, then, considered unconquered by you?

sanskrit: मन्यसे वा सभामेतामानीतामेकवाससम् ।अधर्मेणेति तत्रापि शृणु मे वाक्यमुत्तरम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: manyase vā sabhāmetāmānītāmekavāsasam ,adharmeṇeti tatrāpi śṛṇu me vākyamuttaram.
english: Do you consider her having been brought into this assembly (sabhā) wearing a single garment to be an act of unrighteousness (adharma)? If so, listen to my reply on that matter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 34. Do you consider her having been brought into this assembly (sabhā) wearing a single garment to be an act of unrighteousness (adharma)? If so, listen to my reply on that matter.

sanskrit: एको भर्ता स्त्रिया देवैर्विहितः कुरुनन्दन ।इयं त्वनेकवशगा बन्धकीति विनिश्चिता ॥३५॥
transliteration: eko bhartā striyā devairvihitaḥ kurunandana ,iyaṁ tvanekavaśagā bandhakīti viniścitā.
english: O Kuru prince (kurunandana), the gods have ordained only one husband for a woman. But this woman (Draupadi) has become subservient to many, and is therefore deemed an unchaste woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 35. O Kuru prince (kurunandana), the gods have ordained only one husband for a woman. But this woman (Draupadi) has become subservient to many, and is therefore deemed an unchaste woman.

sanskrit: अस्याः सभामानयनं न चित्रमिति मे मतिः ।एकाम्बरधरत्वं वाप्यथ वापि विवस्त्रता ॥३६॥
transliteration: asyāḥ sabhāmānayanaṁ na citramiti me matiḥ ,ekāmbaradharatvaṁ vāpyatha vāpi vivastratā.
english: It is not surprising, in my opinion, that she was brought into this assembly (sabhā); nor is the state of her wearing a single garment surprising, nor even her complete nakedness.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 36. It is not surprising, in my opinion, that she was brought into this assembly (sabhā); nor is the state of her wearing a single garment surprising, nor even her complete nakedness.

sanskrit: यच्चैषां द्रविणं किंचिद्या चैषा ये च पाण्डवाः ।सौबलेनेह तत्सर्वं धर्मेण विजितं वसु ॥३७॥
transliteration: yaccaiṣāṁ draviṇaṁ kiṁcidyā caiṣā ye ca pāṇḍavāḥ ,saubaleneha tatsarvaṁ dharmeṇa vijitaṁ vasu.
english: Whatever wealth (dravina) belongs to them, and this woman (Draupadi), and the Pāṇḍavas themselves - all that has been legitimately (dharma) won here by Subala's son (Śakuni).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 37. Whatever wealth (dravina) belongs to them, and this woman (Draupadi), and the Pāṇḍavas themselves - all that has been legitimately (dharma) won here by Subala's son (Śakuni).

sanskrit: दुःशासन सुबालोऽयं विकर्णः प्राज्ञवादिकः ।पाण्डवानां च वासांसि द्रौपद्याश्चाप्युपाहर ॥३८॥
transliteration: duḥśāsana subālo'yaṁ vikarṇaḥ prājñavādikaḥ ,pāṇḍavānāṁ ca vāsāṁsi draupadyāścāpyupāhara.
english: O Duḥśāsana, bring the clothes of the Pāṇḍavas and also those of Draupadī; this Vikarṇa is merely a young boy speaking wise words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 38. O Duḥśāsana, bring the clothes of the Pāṇḍavas and also those of Draupadī; this Vikarṇa is merely a young boy speaking wise words.

sanskrit: तच्छ्रुत्वा पाण्डवाः सर्वे स्वानि वासांसि भारत ।अवकीर्योत्तरीयाणि सभायां समुपाविशन् ॥३९॥
transliteration: tacchrutvā pāṇḍavāḥ sarve svāni vāsāṁsi bhārata ,avakīryottarīyāṇi sabhāyāṁ samupāviśan.
english: Having heard that, all the Pāṇḍavas, O Bhārata, threw off their own upper garments and sat down in the assembly hall.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 39. Having heard that, all the Pāṇḍavas, O Bhārata, threw off their own upper garments and sat down in the assembly hall.

sanskrit: ततो दुःशासनो राजन्द्रौपद्या वसनं बलात् ।सभामध्ये समाक्षिप्य व्यपक्रष्टुं प्रचक्रमे ॥४०॥
transliteration: tato duḥśāsano rājandraupadyā vasanaṁ balāt ,sabhāmadhye samākṣipya vyapakraṣṭuṁ pracakrame.
english: Then, O King, Duḥśāsana, having grasped Draupadī's garment by force in the middle of the assembly, began to drag it off.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 40. Then, O King, Duḥśāsana, having grasped Draupadī's garment by force in the middle of the assembly, began to drag it off.

sanskrit: आकृष्यमाणे वसने द्रौपद्यास्तु विशां पते ।तद्रूपमपरं वस्त्रं प्रादुरासीदनेकशः ॥४१॥
transliteration: ākṛṣyamāṇe vasane draupadyāstu viśāṁ pate ,tadrūpamaparaṁ vastraṁ prādurāsīdanekaśaḥ.
english: But, O lord of the people, while Draupadī's garment was being pulled off, another garment of the same form appeared countless times.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 41. But, O lord of the people, while Draupadī's garment was being pulled off, another garment of the same form appeared countless times.

sanskrit: ततो हलहलाशब्दस्तत्रासीद्घोरनिस्वनः ।तदद्भुततमं लोके वीक्ष्य सर्वमहीक्षिताम् ॥४२॥
transliteration: tato halahalāśabdastatrāsīdghoranisvanaḥ ,tadadbhutatamaṁ loke vīkṣya sarvamahīkṣitām.
english: Then there was a tumultuous, dreadful roar (halahala-śabda). All the kings of the world observed that most astonishing event.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 42. Then there was a tumultuous, dreadful roar (halahala-śabda). All the kings of the world observed that most astonishing event.

sanskrit: शशाप तत्र भीमस्तु राजमध्ये महास्वनः ।क्रोधाद्विस्फुरमाणोष्ठो विनिष्पिष्य करे करम् ॥४३॥
transliteration: śaśāpa tatra bhīmastu rājamadhye mahāsvanaḥ ,krodhādvisphuramāṇoṣṭho viniṣpiṣya kare karam.
english: Then Bhīma, with a loud roar (mahāsvana), swore amidst the kings, his lips trembling with anger, as he pressed his hand against his other hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 43. Then Bhīma, with a loud roar (mahāsvana), swore amidst the kings, his lips trembling with anger, as he pressed his hand against his other hand.

sanskrit: इदं मे वाक्यमादद्ध्वं क्षत्रिया लोकवासिनः ।नोक्तपूर्वं नरैरन्यैर्न चान्यो यद्वदिष्यति ॥४४॥
transliteration: idaṁ me vākyamādaddhvaṁ kṣatriyā lokavāsinaḥ ,noktapūrvaṁ narairanyairna cānyo yadvadiṣyati.
english: O warriors (kṣatriya) who dwell in the world, listen to these words of mine! They have never been spoken before by other men, nor will any other person ever speak what I am about to say.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 44. O warriors (kṣatriya) who dwell in the world, listen to these words of mine! They have never been spoken before by other men, nor will any other person ever speak what I am about to say.

sanskrit: यद्येतदेवमुक्त्वा तु न कुर्यां पृथिवीश्वराः ।पितामहानां सर्वेषां नाहं गतिमवाप्नुयाम् ॥४५॥
transliteration: yadyetadevamuktvā tu na kuryāṁ pṛthivīśvarāḥ ,pitāmahānāṁ sarveṣāṁ nāhaṁ gatimavāpnuyām.
english: O lords of the earth, if, having declared this, I do not then fulfill it, may I not attain the destination (gati) of all my ancestors!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 45. O lords of the earth, if, having declared this, I do not then fulfill it, may I not attain the destination (gati) of all my ancestors!

sanskrit: अस्य पापस्य दुर्जातेर्भारतापसदस्य च ।न पिबेयं बलाद्वक्षो भित्त्वा चेद्रुधिरं युधि ॥४६॥
transliteration: asya pāpasya durjāterbhāratāpasadasya ca ,na pibeyaṁ balādvakṣo bhittvā cedrudhiraṁ yudhi.
english: If I do not tear open the chest of this sinful, ill-born disgrace among the Bhāratas and forcibly drink his blood in battle, then I would not drink.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 46. If I do not tear open the chest of this sinful, ill-born disgrace among the Bhāratas and forcibly drink his blood in battle, then I would not drink.

sanskrit: तस्य ते वचनं श्रुत्वा सर्वलोकप्रहर्षणम् ।प्रचक्रुर्बहुलां पूजां कुत्सन्तो धृतराष्ट्रजम् ॥४७॥
transliteration: tasya te vacanaṁ śrutvā sarvalokapraharṣaṇam ,pracakrurbahulāṁ pūjāṁ kutsanto dhṛtarāṣṭrajam.
english: Having heard that speech of yours, which delighted all people, they performed extensive honor (pūjā) while censuring the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 47. Having heard that speech of yours, which delighted all people, they performed extensive honor (pūjā) while censuring the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: यदा तु वाससां राशिः सभामध्ये समाचितः ।ततो दुःशासनः श्रान्तो व्रीडितः समुपाविशत् ॥४८॥
transliteration: yadā tu vāsasāṁ rāśiḥ sabhāmadhye samācitaḥ ,tato duḥśāsanaḥ śrānto vrīḍitaḥ samupāviśat.
english: But when a heap of garments had accumulated in the midst of the assembly, then Duḥśāsana, exhausted and ashamed, sat down.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 48. But when a heap of garments had accumulated in the midst of the assembly, then Duḥśāsana, exhausted and ashamed, sat down.

sanskrit: धिक्शब्दस्तु ततस्तत्र समभूल्लोमहर्षणः ।सभ्यानां नरदेवानां दृष्ट्वा कुन्तीसुतांस्तदा ॥४९॥
transliteration: dhikśabdastu tatastatra samabhūllomaharṣaṇaḥ ,sabhyānāṁ naradevānāṁ dṛṣṭvā kuntīsutāṁstadā.
english: But then, a hair-raising cry of 'Shame!' arose there among the assembled royal personages, when they saw the sons of Kuntī at that time.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 49. But then, a hair-raising cry of 'Shame!' arose there among the assembled royal personages, when they saw the sons of Kuntī at that time.

sanskrit: न विब्रुवन्ति कौरव्याः प्रश्नमेतमिति स्म ह ।स जनः क्रोशति स्मात्र धृतराष्ट्रं विगर्हयन् ॥५०॥
transliteration: na vibruvanti kauravyāḥ praśnametamiti sma ha ,sa janaḥ krośati smātra dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ vigarhayan.
english: The Kauravas, it is said, did not answer this question. The people there cried out, denouncing Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 50. The Kauravas, it is said, did not answer this question. The people there cried out, denouncing Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: ततो बाहू समुच्छ्रित्य निवार्य च सभासदः ।विदुरः सर्वधर्मज्ञ इदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥५१॥
transliteration: tato bāhū samucchritya nivārya ca sabhāsadaḥ ,viduraḥ sarvadharmajña idaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: Then, raising his arms and restraining the members of the assembly, Vidura, who knew all principles of (dharma) natural law, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 51. Then, raising his arms and restraining the members of the assembly, Vidura, who knew all principles of (dharma) natural law, spoke these words.

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।द्रौपदी प्रश्नमुक्त्वैवं रोरवीति ह्यनाथवत् ।न च विब्रूत तं प्रश्नं सभ्या धर्मोऽत्र पीड्यते ॥५२॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,draupadī praśnamuktvaivaṁ roravīti hyanāthavat ,na ca vibrūta taṁ praśnaṁ sabhyā dharmo'tra pīḍyate.
english: Vidura said: Draupadī, having posed the question in this manner, cries out as if she were without a protector. Yet you, O members of the assembly, do not answer that question; indeed, (dharma) natural law is being violated here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 52. Vidura said: Draupadī, having posed the question in this manner, cries out as if she were without a protector. Yet you, O members of the assembly, do not answer that question; indeed, (dharma) natural law is being violated here.

sanskrit: सभां प्रपद्यते ह्यार्तः प्रज्वलन्निव हव्यवाट् ।तं वै सत्येन धर्मेण सभ्याः प्रशमयन्त्युत ॥५३॥
transliteration: sabhāṁ prapadyate hyārtaḥ prajvalanniva havyavāṭ ,taṁ vai satyena dharmeṇa sabhyāḥ praśamayantyuta.
english: Indeed, an afflicted person enters the assembly, burning brightly like a fire. The members of the assembly should pacify him with truth and (dharma) natural law.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 53. Indeed, an afflicted person enters the assembly, burning brightly like a fire. The members of the assembly should pacify him with truth and (dharma) natural law.

sanskrit: धर्मप्रश्नमथो ब्रूयादार्तः सभ्येषु मानवः ।विब्रूयुस्तत्र ते प्रश्नं कामक्रोधवशातिगाः ॥५४॥
transliteration: dharmapraśnamatho brūyādārtaḥ sabhyeṣu mānavaḥ ,vibrūyustatra te praśnaṁ kāmakrodhavaśātigāḥ.
english: A distressed person should then present a question concerning natural law (dharma) before the members of the assembly. There, those (judges) who are free from the sway of desire and anger should explain the answer to that question.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 54. A distressed person should then present a question concerning natural law (dharma) before the members of the assembly. There, those (judges) who are free from the sway of desire and anger should explain the answer to that question.

sanskrit: विकर्णेन यथाप्रज्ञमुक्तः प्रश्नो नराधिपाः ।भवन्तोऽपि हि तं प्रश्नं विब्रुवन्तु यथामति ॥५५॥
transliteration: vikarṇena yathāprajñamuktaḥ praśno narādhipāḥ ,bhavanto'pi hi taṁ praśnaṁ vibruvantu yathāmati.
english: O rulers (nara-adhipa), Vikarna has put forth a question according to his understanding. Indeed, may all of you also explain that question according to your intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 55. O rulers (nara-adhipa), Vikarna has put forth a question according to his understanding. Indeed, may all of you also explain that question according to your intellect.

sanskrit: यो हि प्रश्नं न विब्रूयाद्धर्मदर्शी सभां गतः ।अनृते या फलावाप्तिस्तस्याः सोऽर्धं समश्नुते ॥५६॥
transliteration: yo hi praśnaṁ na vibrūyāddharmadarśī sabhāṁ gataḥ ,anṛte yā phalāvāptistasyāḥ so'rdhaṁ samaśnute.
english: Whoever, having attended an assembly as one who discerns natural law (dharma), indeed fails to explain a question, that person partakes of half the consequence that results from falsehood.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 56. Whoever, having attended an assembly as one who discerns natural law (dharma), indeed fails to explain a question, that person partakes of half the consequence that results from falsehood.

sanskrit: यः पुनर्वितथं ब्रूयाद्धर्मदर्शी सभां गतः ।अनृतस्य फलं कृत्स्नं संप्राप्नोतीति निश्चयः ॥५७॥
transliteration: yaḥ punarvitathaṁ brūyāddharmadarśī sabhāṁ gataḥ ,anṛtasya phalaṁ kṛtsnaṁ saṁprāpnotīti niścayaḥ.
english: On the other hand, whoever, having attended an assembly as one who discerns natural law (dharma), declares a falsehood, that person obtains the entire consequence of untruth. This is a definite conclusion.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 57. On the other hand, whoever, having attended an assembly as one who discerns natural law (dharma), declares a falsehood, that person obtains the entire consequence of untruth. This is a definite conclusion.

sanskrit: अत्राप्युदाहरन्तीममितिहासं पुरातनम् ।प्रह्लादस्य च संवादं मुनेराङ्गिरसस्य च ॥५८॥
transliteration: atrāpyudāharantīmamitihāsaṁ purātanam ,prahlādasya ca saṁvādaṁ munerāṅgirasasya ca.
english: In this regard, they also narrate this ancient story: the dialogue between Prahlāda and the sage Āṅgirasa.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 58. In this regard, they also narrate this ancient story: the dialogue between Prahlāda and the sage Āṅgirasa.

sanskrit: प्रह्लादो नाम दैत्येन्द्रस्तस्य पुत्रो विरोचनः ।कन्याहेतोराङ्गिरसं सुधन्वानमुपाद्रवत् ॥५९॥
transliteration: prahlādo nāma daityendrastasya putro virocanaḥ ,kanyāhetorāṅgirasaṁ sudhanvānamupādravat.
english: There was a king of the daityas (demons) named Prahlāda, and his son was Virocana. Virocana approached Sudhanvan, a descendant of Āṅgirasa, for the sake of a maiden.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 59. There was a king of the daityas (demons) named Prahlāda, and his son was Virocana. Virocana approached Sudhanvan, a descendant of Āṅgirasa, for the sake of a maiden.

sanskrit: अहं ज्यायानहं ज्यायानिति कन्येप्सया तदा ।तयोर्देवनमत्रासीत्प्राणयोरिति नः श्रुतम् ॥६०॥
transliteration: ahaṁ jyāyānahaṁ jyāyāniti kanyepsayā tadā ,tayordevanamatrāsītprāṇayoriti naḥ śrutam.
english: Then, with the desire for the maiden, saying 'I am superior! I am superior!', a contest regarding their very lives occurred between the two of them. This is what we have heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 60. Then, with the desire for the maiden, saying 'I am superior! I am superior!', a contest regarding their very lives occurred between the two of them. This is what we have heard.

sanskrit: तयोः प्रश्नविवादोऽभूत्प्रह्लादं तावपृच्छताम् ।ज्यायान्क आवयोरेकः प्रश्नं प्रब्रूहि मा मृषा ॥६१॥
transliteration: tayoḥ praśnavivādo'bhūtprahlādaṁ tāvapṛcchatām ,jyāyānka āvayorekaḥ praśnaṁ prabrūhi mā mṛṣā.
english: A dispute arose between them concerning this question. They both asked Prahlāda: 'Who is the superior one (jyāyān) among us two? Please answer this question truthfully, without deceit.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 61. A dispute arose between them concerning this question. They both asked Prahlāda: 'Who is the superior one (jyāyān) among us two? Please answer this question truthfully, without deceit.'

sanskrit: स वै विवदनाद्भीतः सुधन्वानं व्यलोकयत् ।तं सुधन्वाब्रवीत्क्रुद्धो ब्रह्मदण्ड इव ज्वलन् ॥६२॥
transliteration: sa vai vivadanādbhītaḥ sudhanvānaṁ vyalokayat ,taṁ sudhanvābravītkruddho brahmadaṇḍa iva jvalan.
english: Being afraid of the contention, he looked at Sudhanvan. Sudhanvan, blazing like Brahma's staff and enraged, spoke to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 62. Being afraid of the contention, he looked at Sudhanvan. Sudhanvan, blazing like Brahma's staff and enraged, spoke to him.

sanskrit: यदि वै वक्ष्यसि मृषा प्रह्लादाथ न वक्ष्यसि ।शतधा ते शिरो वज्री वज्रेण प्रहरिष्यति ॥६३॥
transliteration: yadi vai vakṣyasi mṛṣā prahlādātha na vakṣyasi ,śatadhā te śiro vajrī vajreṇa prahariṣyati.
english: Prahlāda, if you speak falsely or do not speak at all, then Indra, who wields the thunderbolt, will strike your head into a hundred pieces with his thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 63. Prahlāda, if you speak falsely or do not speak at all, then Indra, who wields the thunderbolt, will strike your head into a hundred pieces with his thunderbolt.

sanskrit: सुधन्वना तथोक्तः सन्व्यथितोऽश्वत्थपर्णवत् ।जगाम कश्यपं दैत्यः परिप्रष्टुं महौजसम् ॥६४॥
transliteration: sudhanvanā tathoktaḥ sanvyathito'śvatthaparṇavat ,jagāma kaśyapaṁ daityaḥ paripraṣṭuṁ mahaujasam.
english: Having been addressed thus by Sudhanvan, the demon (Prahlāda), agitated like an Asvattha leaf, went to the greatly powerful Kaśyapa to make an inquiry.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 64. Having been addressed thus by Sudhanvan, the demon (Prahlāda), agitated like an Asvattha leaf, went to the greatly powerful Kaśyapa to make an inquiry.

sanskrit: प्रह्लाद उवाच ।त्वं वै धर्मस्य विज्ञाता दैवस्येहासुरस्य च ।ब्राह्मणस्य महाप्राज्ञ धर्मकृच्छ्रमिदं शृणु ॥६५॥
transliteration: prahlāda uvāca ,tvaṁ vai dharmasya vijñātā daivasyehāsurasya ca ,brāhmaṇasya mahāprājña dharmakṛcchramidaṁ śṛṇu.
english: Prahlāda said: "O greatly wise one, you are indeed the knower of the natural law (dharma) concerning both the divine and the asuras here, as well as the Brahmins. Please listen to this difficult matter regarding natural law (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 65. Prahlāda said: "O greatly wise one, you are indeed the knower of the natural law (dharma) concerning both the divine and the asuras here, as well as the Brahmins. Please listen to this difficult matter regarding natural law (dharma)."

sanskrit: यो वै प्रश्नं न विब्रूयाद्वितथं वापि निर्दिशेत् ।के वै तस्य परे लोकास्तन्ममाचक्ष्व पृच्छतः ॥६६॥
transliteration: yo vai praśnaṁ na vibrūyādvitathaṁ vāpi nirdiśet ,ke vai tasya pare lokāstanmamācakṣva pṛcchataḥ.
english: He who indeed does not answer a question, or even gives a false answer, what higher realms (lokas) await him? Please tell me this, as I inquire.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 66. He who indeed does not answer a question, or even gives a false answer, what higher realms (lokas) await him? Please tell me this, as I inquire.

sanskrit: कश्यप उवाच ।जानन्न विब्रुवन्प्रश्नं कामात्क्रोधात्तथा भयात् ।सहस्रं वारुणान्पाशानात्मनि प्रतिमुञ्चति ॥६७॥
transliteration: kaśyapa uvāca ,jānanna vibruvanpraśnaṁ kāmātkrodhāttathā bhayāt ,sahasraṁ vāruṇānpāśānātmani pratimuñcati.
english: Kaśyapa said: The one who, knowing (the answer), fails to explain a question due to desire, anger, or fear, binds a thousand of Varuṇa's nooses upon his own being (ātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 67. Kaśyapa said: The one who, knowing (the answer), fails to explain a question due to desire, anger, or fear, binds a thousand of Varuṇa's nooses upon his own being (ātman).

sanskrit: तस्य संवत्सरे पूर्णे पाश एकः प्रमुच्यते ।तस्मात्सत्यं तु वक्तव्यं जानता सत्यमञ्जसा ॥६८॥
transliteration: tasya saṁvatsare pūrṇe pāśa ekaḥ pramucyate ,tasmātsatyaṁ tu vaktavyaṁ jānatā satyamañjasā.
english: When a full year is complete, one noose is released from him. Therefore, truth should certainly be spoken straightforwardly by one who knows the truth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 68. When a full year is complete, one noose is released from him. Therefore, truth should certainly be spoken straightforwardly by one who knows the truth.

sanskrit: विद्धो धर्मो ह्यधर्मेण सभां यत्र प्रपद्यते ।न चास्य शल्यं कृन्तन्ति विद्धास्तत्र सभासदः ॥६९॥
transliteration: viddho dharmo hyadharmeṇa sabhāṁ yatra prapadyate ,na cāsya śalyaṁ kṛntanti viddhāstatra sabhāsadaḥ.
english: Where natural law (dharma), wounded by unrighteousness (adharma), enters an assembly, and the assembly members, themselves afflicted, do not extract its dart.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 69. Where natural law (dharma), wounded by unrighteousness (adharma), enters an assembly, and the assembly members, themselves afflicted, do not extract its dart.

sanskrit: अर्धं हरति वै श्रेष्ठः पादो भवति कर्तृषु ।पादश्चैव सभासत्सु ये न निन्दन्ति निन्दितम् ॥७०॥
transliteration: ardhaṁ harati vai śreṣṭhaḥ pādo bhavati kartṛṣu ,pādaścaiva sabhāsatsu ye na nindanti ninditam.
english: When assembly members fail to condemn that which deserves to be condemned, the chief (judge) indeed incurs half of the sin. A quarter of the sin falls upon the perpetrators, and indeed a quarter falls upon those very assembly members.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 70. When assembly members fail to condemn that which deserves to be condemned, the chief (judge) indeed incurs half of the sin. A quarter of the sin falls upon the perpetrators, and indeed a quarter falls upon those very assembly members.

sanskrit: अनेना भवति श्रेष्ठो मुच्यन्ते च सभासदः ।एनो गच्छति कर्तारं निन्दार्हो यत्र निन्द्यते ॥७१॥
transliteration: anenā bhavati śreṣṭho mucyante ca sabhāsadaḥ ,eno gacchati kartāraṁ nindārho yatra nindyate.
english: When the condemnable one is condemned, the chief (judge) becomes faultless, and the assembly members are released (from any fault). The sin then goes to the perpetrator (of the original wrongdoing).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 71. When the condemnable one is condemned, the chief (judge) becomes faultless, and the assembly members are released (from any fault). The sin then goes to the perpetrator (of the original wrongdoing).

sanskrit: वितथं तु वदेयुर्ये धर्मं प्रह्लाद पृच्छते ।इष्टापूर्तं च ते घ्नन्ति सप्त चैव परावरान् ॥७२॥
transliteration: vitathaṁ tu vadeyurye dharmaṁ prahlāda pṛcchate ,iṣṭāpūrtaṁ ca te ghnanti sapta caiva parāvarān.
english: O Prahlāda, those who speak falsely to one who asks about "dharma" (natural law), they indeed destroy their "iṣṭāpūrta" (sacrifices and charitable works) and also (the merit of) their seven generations, both ancestors and descendants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 72. O Prahlāda, those who speak falsely to one who asks about "dharma" (natural law), they indeed destroy their "iṣṭāpūrta" (sacrifices and charitable works) and also (the merit of) their seven generations, both ancestors and descendants.

sanskrit: हृतस्वस्य हि यद्दुःखं हतपुत्रस्य चापि यत् ।ऋणिनं प्रति यच्चैव राज्ञा ग्रस्तस्य चापि यत् ॥७३॥
transliteration: hṛtasvasya hi yadduḥkhaṁ hataputrasya cāpi yat ,ṛṇinaṁ prati yaccaiva rājñā grastasya cāpi yat.
english: Indeed, whatever sorrow is experienced by one whose wealth has been stolen, and whatever (sorrow) by one whose son has been killed; and indeed whatever (sorrow is experienced) regarding a debtor, and whatever (sorrow) by one seized by the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 73. Indeed, whatever sorrow is experienced by one whose wealth has been stolen, and whatever (sorrow) by one whose son has been killed; and indeed whatever (sorrow is experienced) regarding a debtor, and whatever (sorrow) by one seized by the king.

sanskrit: स्त्रियाः पत्या विहीनायाः सार्थाद्भ्रष्टस्य चैव यत् ।अध्यूढायाश्च यद्दुःखं साक्षिभिर्विहतस्य च ॥७४॥
transliteration: striyāḥ patyā vihīnāyāḥ sārthādbhraṣṭasya caiva yat ,adhyūḍhāyāśca yadduḥkhaṁ sākṣibhirvihatasya ca.
english: The distress experienced by a woman bereft of her husband, by one separated from his caravan, by a woman whose co-wife has been unjustly installed, and by one defeated by witnesses – these are all considered misfortunes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 74. The distress experienced by a woman bereft of her husband, by one separated from his caravan, by a woman whose co-wife has been unjustly installed, and by one defeated by witnesses – these are all considered misfortunes.

sanskrit: एतानि वै समान्याहुर्दुःखानि त्रिदशेश्वराः ।तानि सर्वाणि दुःखानि प्राप्नोति वितथं ब्रुवन् ॥७५॥
transliteration: etāni vai samānyāhurduḥkhāni tridaśeśvarāḥ ,tāni sarvāṇi duḥkhāni prāpnoti vitathaṁ bruvan.
english: The gods (tridaśeśvara) declare these to be common misfortunes. One who speaks falsely incurs all those misfortunes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 75. The gods (tridaśeśvara) declare these to be common misfortunes. One who speaks falsely incurs all those misfortunes.

sanskrit: समक्षदर्शनात्साक्ष्यं श्रवणाच्चेति धारणात् ।तस्मात्सत्यं ब्रुवन्साक्षी धर्मार्थाभ्यां न हीयते ॥७६॥
transliteration: samakṣadarśanātsākṣyaṁ śravaṇācceti dhāraṇāt ,tasmātsatyaṁ bruvansākṣī dharmārthābhyāṁ na hīyate.
english: Witnessing (sākṣya) is based on seeing directly and also on hearing and remembering. Therefore, a witness (sākṣin) speaking the truth does not forfeit (dharma) or material gain (artha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 76. Witnessing (sākṣya) is based on seeing directly and also on hearing and remembering. Therefore, a witness (sākṣin) speaking the truth does not forfeit (dharma) or material gain (artha).

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।कश्यपस्य वचः श्रुत्वा प्रह्लादः पुत्रमब्रवीत् ।श्रेयान्सुधन्वा त्वत्तो वै मत्तः श्रेयांस्तथाङ्गिराः ॥७७॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,kaśyapasya vacaḥ śrutvā prahlādaḥ putramabravīt ,śreyānsudhanvā tvatto vai mattaḥ śreyāṁstathāṅgirāḥ.
english: Vidura said: Having heard Kaśyapa's words, Prahlāda said to his son: "Sudhanvan is superior to you, and similarly, Aṅgirās is superior to me."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 77. Vidura said: Having heard Kaśyapa's words, Prahlāda said to his son: "Sudhanvan is superior to you, and similarly, Aṅgirās is superior to me."

sanskrit: माता सुधन्वनश्चापि श्रेयसी मातृतस्तव ।विरोचन सुधन्वायं प्राणानामीश्वरस्तव ॥७८॥
transliteration: mātā sudhanvanaścāpi śreyasī mātṛtastava ,virocana sudhanvāyaṁ prāṇānāmīśvarastava.
english: O Virocana, Sudhanvan's mother is superior even to your own mother. This Sudhanvan is indeed the lord of your life (prāṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 78. O Virocana, Sudhanvan's mother is superior even to your own mother. This Sudhanvan is indeed the lord of your life (prāṇa).

sanskrit: सुधन्वोवाच ।पुत्रस्नेहं परित्यज्य यस्त्वं धर्मे प्रतिष्ठितः ।अनुजानामि ते पुत्रं जीवत्वेष शतं समाः ॥७९॥
transliteration: sudhanvovāca ,putrasnehaṁ parityajya yastvaṁ dharme pratiṣṭhitaḥ ,anujānāmi te putraṁ jīvatveṣa śataṁ samāḥ.
english: Sudhanvan said: 'Since you, having abandoned attachment to your son, are steadfast in your moral duty (dharma), I grant permission for your son to live. May he live for a hundred years.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 79. Sudhanvan said: 'Since you, having abandoned attachment to your son, are steadfast in your moral duty (dharma), I grant permission for your son to live. May he live for a hundred years.'

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।एवं वै परमं धर्मं श्रुत्वा सर्वे सभासदः ।यथाप्रश्नं तु कृष्णाया मन्यध्वं तत्र किं परम् ॥८०॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,evaṁ vai paramaṁ dharmaṁ śrutvā sarve sabhāsadaḥ ,yathāpraśnaṁ tu kṛṣṇāyā manyadhvaṁ tatra kiṁ param.
english: Vidura said: 'Indeed, having heard this supreme natural law (dharma), all you assembly members, what more could you possibly consider regarding Kṛṣṇā's question?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 80. Vidura said: 'Indeed, having heard this supreme natural law (dharma), all you assembly members, what more could you possibly consider regarding Kṛṣṇā's question?'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।विदुरस्य वचः श्रुत्वा नोचुः किंचन पार्थिवाः ।कर्णो दुःशासनं त्वाह कृष्णां दासीं गृहान्नय ॥८१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vidurasya vacaḥ śrutvā nocuḥ kiṁcana pārthivāḥ ,karṇo duḥśāsanaṁ tvāha kṛṣṇāṁ dāsīṁ gṛhānnaya.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Having heard Vidura's words, the kings said nothing at all. But Karṇa spoke to Duḥśāsana, saying, "Take Kṛṣṇā, the slave, to the residence."'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 81. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: 'Having heard Vidura's words, the kings said nothing at all. But Karṇa spoke to Duḥśāsana, saying, "Take Kṛṣṇā, the slave, to the residence."'

sanskrit: तां वेपमानां सव्रीडां प्रलपन्तीं स्म पाण्डवान् ।दुःशासनः सभामध्ये विचकर्ष तपस्विनीम् ॥८२॥
transliteration: tāṁ vepamānāṁ savrīḍāṁ pralapantīṁ sma pāṇḍavān ,duḥśāsanaḥ sabhāmadhye vicakarṣa tapasvinīm.
english: Duḥśāsana dragged that trembling, ashamed, and lamenting woman, who was calling out to the Pandavas, in the midst of the assembly. She was truly a distressed woman.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 61 Verse 82. Duḥśāsana dragged that trembling, ashamed, and lamenting woman, who was calling out to the Pandavas, in the midst of the assembly. She was truly a distressed woman.

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।पुरस्तात्करणीयं मे न कृतं कार्यमुत्तरम् ।विह्वलास्मि कृतानेन कर्षता बलिना बलात् ॥१॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,purastātkaraṇīyaṁ me na kṛtaṁ kāryamuttaram ,vihvalāsmi kṛtānena karṣatā balinā balāt.
english: Draupadi said, "The important duty, which was to be performed by me first, has not been accomplished. I have been made distraught by this powerful man who is dragging me by force."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 1. Draupadi said, "The important duty, which was to be performed by me first, has not been accomplished. I have been made distraught by this powerful man who is dragging me by force."

sanskrit: अभिवादं करोम्येषां गुरूणां कुरुसंसदि ।न मे स्यादपराधोऽयं यदिदं न कृतं मया ॥२॥
transliteration: abhivādaṁ karomyeṣāṁ gurūṇāṁ kurusaṁsadi ,na me syādaparādho'yaṁ yadidaṁ na kṛtaṁ mayā.
english: I would perform obeisance to these elders (guru) in the Kuru assembly. May this not be considered an offense (aparādha) on my part, if this (duty) is not performed by me.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 2. I would perform obeisance to these elders (guru) in the Kuru assembly. May this not be considered an offense (aparādha) on my part, if this (duty) is not performed by me.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।सा तेन च समुद्धूता दुःखेन च तपस्विनी ।पतिता विललापेदं सभायामतथोचिता ॥३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,sā tena ca samuddhūtā duḥkhena ca tapasvinī ,patitā vilalāpedaṁ sabhāyāmatathocitā.
english: Vaiśampāyana said, "And she, the distressed woman, having been agitated by him and by sorrow, fell in the assembly, and lamented in a manner unbefitting her station."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 3. Vaiśampāyana said, "And she, the distressed woman, having been agitated by him and by sorrow, fell in the assembly, and lamented in a manner unbefitting her station."

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।स्वयंवरे यास्मि नृपैर्दृष्टा रङ्गे समागतैः ।न दृष्टपूर्वा चान्यत्र साहमद्य सभां गता ॥४॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,svayaṁvare yāsmi nṛpairdṛṣṭā raṅge samāgataiḥ ,na dṛṣṭapūrvā cānyatra sāhamadya sabhāṁ gatā.
english: Draupadī said: "I, who was seen by kings assembled in the arena during my self-choice marriage (svayaṃvara), and who was never seen elsewhere before, have today come to this assembly."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 4. Draupadī said: "I, who was seen by kings assembled in the arena during my self-choice marriage (svayaṃvara), and who was never seen elsewhere before, have today come to this assembly."

sanskrit: यां न वायुर्न चादित्यो दृष्टवन्तौ पुरा गृहे ।साहमद्य सभामध्ये दृश्यामि कुरुसंसदि ॥५॥
transliteration: yāṁ na vāyurna cādityo dṛṣṭavantau purā gṛhe ,sāhamadya sabhāmadhye dṛśyāmi kurusaṁsadi.
english: "I, whom neither the wind (vāyu) nor the sun (āditya) had seen before in my home, am today being seen in the midst of this assembly of Kurus."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 5. "I, whom neither the wind (vāyu) nor the sun (āditya) had seen before in my home, am today being seen in the midst of this assembly of Kurus."

sanskrit: यां न मृष्यन्ति वातेन स्पृश्यमानां पुरा गृहे ।स्पृश्यमानां सहन्तेऽद्य पाण्डवास्तां दुरात्मना ॥६॥
transliteration: yāṁ na mṛṣyanti vātena spṛśyamānāṁ purā gṛhe ,spṛśyamānāṁ sahante'dya pāṇḍavāstāṁ durātmanā.
english: "I, whom they would not tolerate even if touched by the wind (vāyu) in my home before, am today endured by the Pāṇḍavas while being touched by this wicked person."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 6. "I, whom they would not tolerate even if touched by the wind (vāyu) in my home before, am today endured by the Pāṇḍavas while being touched by this wicked person."

sanskrit: मृष्यन्ते कुरवश्चेमे मन्ये कालस्य पर्ययम् ।स्नुषां दुहितरं चैव क्लिश्यमानामनर्हतीम् ॥७॥
transliteration: mṛṣyante kuravaśceme manye kālasya paryayam ,snuṣāṁ duhitaraṁ caiva kliśyamānāmanarhatīm.
english: "And these Kurus, I believe, are enduring the reversal of time (kāla), as they see their daughter-in-law, their own daughter, being tormented, though she is undeserving of such treatment."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 7. "And these Kurus, I believe, are enduring the reversal of time (kāla), as they see their daughter-in-law, their own daughter, being tormented, though she is undeserving of such treatment."

sanskrit: किं त्वतः कृपणं भूयो यदहं स्त्री सती शुभा ।सभामध्यं विगाहेऽद्य क्व नु धर्मो महीक्षिताम् ॥८॥
transliteration: kiṁ tvataḥ kṛpaṇaṁ bhūyo yadahaṁ strī satī śubhā ,sabhāmadhyaṁ vigāhe'dya kva nu dharmo mahīkṣitām.
english: But what could be more pitiable than this, that I, a pure woman, am made to enter the midst of this assembly today? Where then is the natural law (dharma) of kings?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 8. But what could be more pitiable than this, that I, a pure woman, am made to enter the midst of this assembly today? Where then is the natural law (dharma) of kings?

sanskrit: धर्म्याः स्त्रियः सभां पूर्वं न नयन्तीति नः श्रुतम् ।स नष्टः कौरवेयेषु पूर्वो धर्मः सनातनः ॥९॥
transliteration: dharmyāḥ striyaḥ sabhāṁ pūrvaṁ na nayantīti naḥ śrutam ,sa naṣṭaḥ kauraveyeṣu pūrvo dharmaḥ sanātanaḥ.
english: We have heard that, in the past, righteous women were not brought into the assembly. That ancient and eternal natural law (dharma) has been lost among the Kauravas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 9. We have heard that, in the past, righteous women were not brought into the assembly. That ancient and eternal natural law (dharma) has been lost among the Kauravas.

sanskrit: कथं हि भार्या पाण्डूनां पार्षतस्य स्वसा सती ।वासुदेवस्य च सखी पार्थिवानां सभामियाम् ॥१०॥
transliteration: kathaṁ hi bhāryā pāṇḍūnāṁ pārṣatasya svasā satī ,vāsudevasya ca sakhī pārthivānāṁ sabhāmiyām.
english: How, indeed, could she, the wife of the Pāṇḍavas, the sister of Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), and a friend of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), a virtuous woman, come to the assembly of kings?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 10. How, indeed, could she, the wife of the Pāṇḍavas, the sister of Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), and a friend of Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), a virtuous woman, come to the assembly of kings?

sanskrit: तामिमां धर्मराजस्य भार्यां सदृशवर्णजाम् ।ब्रूत दासीमदासीं वा तत्करिष्यामि कौरवाः ॥११॥
transliteration: tāmimāṁ dharmarājasya bhāryāṁ sadṛśavarṇajām ,brūta dāsīmadāsīṁ vā tatkariṣyāmi kauravāḥ.
english: O Kauravas, tell me whether this wife of the King of natural law (dharma), born of an equal social standing, is a slave or not a slave. Whatever you say, I will do.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 11. O Kauravas, tell me whether this wife of the King of natural law (dharma), born of an equal social standing, is a slave or not a slave. Whatever you say, I will do.

sanskrit: अयं हि मां दृढं क्षुद्रः कौरवाणां यशोहरः ।क्लिश्नाति नाहं तत्सोढुं चिरं शक्ष्यामि कौरवाः ॥१२॥
transliteration: ayaṁ hi māṁ dṛḍhaṁ kṣudraḥ kauravāṇāṁ yaśoharaḥ ,kliśnāti nāhaṁ tatsoḍhuṁ ciraṁ śakṣyāmi kauravāḥ.
english: This contemptible fellow, who indeed destroys the fame of the Kauravas, intensely torments me. O Kauravas, I will not be able to endure that for long.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 12. This contemptible fellow, who indeed destroys the fame of the Kauravas, intensely torments me. O Kauravas, I will not be able to endure that for long.

sanskrit: जितां वाप्यजितां वापि मन्यध्वं वा यथा नृपाः ।तथा प्रत्युक्तमिच्छामि तत्करिष्यामि कौरवाः ॥१३॥
transliteration: jitāṁ vāpyajitāṁ vāpi manyadhvaṁ vā yathā nṛpāḥ ,tathā pratyuktamicchāmi tatkariṣyāmi kauravāḥ.
english: O kings, whether you consider it a victory or a defeat, or whatever you deem it to be, I desire a reply. O Kauravas, I will then act accordingly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 13. O kings, whether you consider it a victory or a defeat, or whatever you deem it to be, I desire a reply. O Kauravas, I will then act accordingly.

sanskrit: भीष्म उवाच ।उक्तवानस्मि कल्याणि धर्मस्य तु परां गतिम् ।लोके न शक्यते गन्तुमपि विप्रैर्महात्मभिः ॥१४॥
transliteration: bhīṣma uvāca ,uktavānasmi kalyāṇi dharmasya tu parāṁ gatim ,loke na śakyate gantumapi viprairmahātmabhiḥ.
english: Bhishma said: 'O blessed one, I have indeed explained the supreme understanding (gati) of natural law (dharma). In this world, it is not possible to reach that (understanding), even for great souls (mahātman) who are Brahmins.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 14. Bhishma said: 'O blessed one, I have indeed explained the supreme understanding (gati) of natural law (dharma). In this world, it is not possible to reach that (understanding), even for great souls (mahātman) who are Brahmins.'

sanskrit: बलवांस्तु यथा धर्मं लोके पश्यति पूरुषः ।स धर्मो धर्मवेलायां भवत्यभिहितः परैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: balavāṁstu yathā dharmaṁ loke paśyati pūruṣaḥ ,sa dharmo dharmavelāyāṁ bhavatyabhihitaḥ paraiḥ.
english: But, how a powerful person (puruṣa) in the world perceives natural law (dharma) - that very natural law (dharma) becomes declared by others at the crucial time for natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 15. But, how a powerful person (puruṣa) in the world perceives natural law (dharma) - that very natural law (dharma) becomes declared by others at the crucial time for natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: न विवेक्तुं च ते प्रश्नमेतं शक्नोमि निश्चयात् ।सूक्ष्मत्वाद्गहनत्वाच्च कार्यस्यास्य च गौरवात् ॥१६॥
transliteration: na vivektuṁ ca te praśnametaṁ śaknomi niścayāt ,sūkṣmatvādgahanatvācca kāryasyāsya ca gauravāt.
english: I am certainly unable to definitively answer this question of yours, due to the subtle and profound nature of this matter (kārya) and its gravity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 16. I am certainly unable to definitively answer this question of yours, due to the subtle and profound nature of this matter (kārya) and its gravity.

sanskrit: नूनमन्तः कुलस्यास्य भविता नचिरादिव ।तथा हि कुरवः सर्वे लोभमोहपरायणाः ॥१७॥
transliteration: nūnamantaḥ kulasyāsya bhavitā nacirādiva ,tathā hi kuravaḥ sarve lobhamohaparāyaṇāḥ.
english: Surely, the end of this family will come soon, because all the Kurus are completely engrossed in greed and delusion (moha).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 17. Surely, the end of this family will come soon, because all the Kurus are completely engrossed in greed and delusion (moha).

sanskrit: कुलेषु जाताः कल्याणि व्यसनाभ्याहता भृशम् ।धर्म्यान्मार्गान्न च्यवन्ते यथा नस्त्वं वधूः स्थिता ॥१८॥
transliteration: kuleṣu jātāḥ kalyāṇi vyasanābhyāhatā bhṛśam ,dharmyānmārgānna cyavante yathā nastvaṁ vadhūḥ sthitā.
english: O auspicious one, those born in (noble) families, even when greatly afflicted by misfortune, do not deviate from the path of righteousness (dharma), just as you, our daughter-in-law, remain firm.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 18. O auspicious one, those born in (noble) families, even when greatly afflicted by misfortune, do not deviate from the path of righteousness (dharma), just as you, our daughter-in-law, remain firm.

sanskrit: उपपन्नं च पाञ्चालि तवेदं वृत्तमीदृशम् ।यत्कृच्छ्रमपि संप्राप्ता धर्ममेवान्ववेक्षसे ॥१९॥
transliteration: upapannaṁ ca pāñcāli tavedaṁ vṛttamīdṛśam ,yatkṛcchramapi saṁprāptā dharmamevānvavekṣase.
english: O Pañcāli, this kind of conduct from you is indeed fitting, because even having encountered such great distress, you uphold only righteousness (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 19. O Pañcāli, this kind of conduct from you is indeed fitting, because even having encountered such great distress, you uphold only righteousness (dharma).

sanskrit: एते द्रोणादयश्चैव वृद्धा धर्मविदो जनाः ।शून्यैः शरीरैस्तिष्ठन्ति गतासव इवानताः ॥२०॥
transliteration: ete droṇādayaścaiva vṛddhā dharmavido janāḥ ,śūnyaiḥ śarīraistiṣṭhanti gatāsava ivānatāḥ.
english: These Drona and others, venerable people who understand natural law (dharma), stand here with bodies devoid of spirit, as if lifeless and bowed down.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 20. These Drona and others, venerable people who understand natural law (dharma), stand here with bodies devoid of spirit, as if lifeless and bowed down.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तु प्रश्नेऽस्मिन्प्रमाणमिति मे मतिः ।अजितां वा जितां वापि स्वयं व्याहर्तुमर्हति ॥२१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastu praśne'sminpramāṇamiti me matiḥ ,ajitāṁ vā jitāṁ vāpi svayaṁ vyāhartumarhati.
english: Indeed, Yudhisthira is the ultimate authority in this matter, that is my opinion. He himself ought to declare whether it is a lost (ajitām) or won (jitām) situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 21. Indeed, Yudhisthira is the ultimate authority in this matter, that is my opinion. He himself ought to declare whether it is a lost (ajitām) or won (jitām) situation.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तथा तु दृष्ट्वा बहु तत्तदेवं रोरूयमाणां कुररीमिवार्ताम् ।नोचुर्वचः साध्वथ वाप्यसाधु महीक्षितो धार्तराष्ट्रस्य भीताः ॥२२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tathā tu dṛṣṭvā bahu tattadevaṁ; rorūyamāṇāṁ kurarīmivārtām ,nocurvacaḥ sādhvatha vāpyasādhu; mahīkṣito dhārtarāṣṭrasya bhītāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: But having thus seen that person lamenting greatly, like a distressed osprey, the kings, afraid of Dhritarashtra, spoke neither good nor bad words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 22. Vaiśampāyana said: But having thus seen that person lamenting greatly, like a distressed osprey, the kings, afraid of Dhritarashtra, spoke neither good nor bad words.

sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु तान्पार्थिवपुत्रपौत्रांस्तूष्णींभूतान्धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रः ।स्मयन्निवेदं वचनं बभाषे पाञ्चालराजस्य सुतां तदानीम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: dṛṣṭvā tu tānpārthivaputrapautrāṁ;stūṣṇīṁbhūtāndhṛtarāṣṭrasya putraḥ ,smayannivedaṁ vacanaṁ babhāṣe; pāñcālarājasya sutāṁ tadānīm.
english: Having seen those princes and grandsons of kings who had become silent, Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana), as if smiling, then spoke these words to the daughter of the Pañcāla king (Draupadī).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 23. Having seen those princes and grandsons of kings who had become silent, Dhritarashtra's son (Duryodhana), as if smiling, then spoke these words to the daughter of the Pañcāla king (Draupadī).

sanskrit: तिष्ठत्वयं प्रश्न उदारसत्त्वे भीमेऽर्जुने सहदेवे तथैव ।पत्यौ च ते नकुले याज्ञसेनि वदन्त्वेते वचनं त्वत्प्रसूतम् ॥२४॥
transliteration: tiṣṭhatvayaṁ praśna udārasattve; bhīme'rjune sahadeve tathaiva ,patyau ca te nakule yājñaseni; vadantvete vacanaṁ tvatprasūtam.
english: Yājñaseṇī, let this question be put to Bhīma, Arjuna, and Sahadeva, who possess noble strength, and likewise to your husband Nakula. Let them declare the statement born from you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 24. Yājñaseṇī, let this question be put to Bhīma, Arjuna, and Sahadeva, who possess noble strength, and likewise to your husband Nakula. Let them declare the statement born from you.

sanskrit: अनीश्वरं विब्रुवन्त्वार्यमध्ये युधिष्ठिरं तव पाञ्चालि हेतोः ।कुर्वन्तु सर्वे चानृतं धर्मराजं पाञ्चालि त्वं मोक्ष्यसे दासभावात् ॥२५॥
transliteration: anīśvaraṁ vibruvantvāryamadhye; yudhiṣṭhiraṁ tava pāñcāli hetoḥ ,kurvantu sarve cānṛtaṁ dharmarājaṁ; pāñcāli tvaṁ mokṣyase dāsabhāvāt.
english: Pañcāli, for your sake, let them declare Yudhiṣṭhira to be without power among the noble ones. And let all declare the king of natural law (dharma-rāja) to be false; then, Pañcāli, you will be freed from the state of servitude.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 25. Pañcāli, for your sake, let them declare Yudhiṣṭhira to be without power among the noble ones. And let all declare the king of natural law (dharma-rāja) to be false; then, Pañcāli, you will be freed from the state of servitude.

sanskrit: धर्मे स्थितो धर्मराजो महात्मा स्वयं चेदं कथयत्विन्द्रकल्पः ।ईशो वा ते यद्यनीशोऽथ वैष वाक्यादस्य क्षिप्रमेकं भजस्व ॥२६॥
transliteration: dharme sthito dharmarājo mahātmā; svayaṁ cedaṁ kathayatvindrakalpaḥ ,īśo vā te yadyanīśo'tha vaiṣa; vākyādasya kṣipramekaṁ bhajasva.
english: Let the great-souled king of natural law (dharma-rāja), who is established in natural law (dharma) and comparable to Indra, himself declare this. If he is your master, or if he is not, then quickly accept one [of these options] based on his statement.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 26. Let the great-souled king of natural law (dharma-rāja), who is established in natural law (dharma) and comparable to Indra, himself declare this. If he is your master, or if he is not, then quickly accept one [of these options] based on his statement.

sanskrit: सर्वे हीमे कौरवेयाः सभायां दुःखान्तरे वर्तमानास्तवैव ।न विब्रुवन्त्यार्यसत्त्वा यथावत्पतींश्च ते समवेक्ष्याल्पभाग्यान् ॥२७॥
transliteration: sarve hīme kauraveyāḥ sabhāyāṁ; duḥkhāntare vartamānāstavaiva ,na vibruvantyāryasattvā yathāva;tpatīṁśca te samavekṣyālpabhāgyān.
english: Indeed, all these Kauravas in the assembly are currently experiencing your suffering. Yet, even the noble-minded ones (the elders) do not speak what is right, though they observe your husbands who are of noble character but possess little fortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 27. Indeed, all these Kauravas in the assembly are currently experiencing your suffering. Yet, even the noble-minded ones (the elders) do not speak what is right, though they observe your husbands who are of noble character but possess little fortune.

sanskrit: ततः सभ्याः कुरुराजस्य तत्र वाक्यं सर्वे प्रशशंसुस्तदोच्चैः ।चेलावेधांश्चापि चक्रुर्नदन्तो हा हेत्यासीदपि चैवात्र नादः ।सर्वे चासन्पार्थिवाः प्रीतिमन्तः कुरुश्रेष्ठं धार्मिकं पूजयन्तः ॥२८॥
transliteration: tataḥ sabhyāḥ kururājasya tatra; vākyaṁ sarve praśaśaṁsustadoccaiḥ ,celāvedhāṁścāpi cakrurnadanto; hā hetyāsīdapi caivātra nādaḥ ,sarve cāsanpārthivāḥ prītimantaḥ; kuruśreṣṭhaṁ dhārmikaṁ pūjayantaḥ.
english: Then, all the courtiers present loudly praised the Kuru king's statement. They also made triumphant gestures with their arms while shouting, and a resounding cry of "hā hā" arose there. All the kings were pleased, honoring the best of the Kurus, who was adhering to dharma (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 28. Then, all the courtiers present loudly praised the Kuru king's statement. They also made triumphant gestures with their arms while shouting, and a resounding cry of "hā hā" arose there. All the kings were pleased, honoring the best of the Kurus, who was adhering to dharma (dharma).

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरं च ते सर्वे समुदैक्षन्त पार्थिवाः ।किं नु वक्ष्यति धर्मज्ञ इति साचीकृताननाः ॥२९॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhiraṁ ca te sarve samudaikṣanta pārthivāḥ ,kiṁ nu vakṣyati dharmajña iti sācīkṛtānanāḥ.
english: And all those kings looked at Yudhishthira, wondering, "What will the knower of dharma (dharma) indeed say?" – with their faces turned sideways in anticipation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 29. And all those kings looked at Yudhishthira, wondering, "What will the knower of dharma (dharma) indeed say?" – with their faces turned sideways in anticipation.

sanskrit: किं नु वक्ष्यति बीभत्सुरजितो युधि पाण्डवः ।भीमसेनो यमौ चेति भृशं कौतूहलान्विताः ॥३०॥
transliteration: kiṁ nu vakṣyati bībhatsurajito yudhi pāṇḍavaḥ ,bhīmaseno yamau ceti bhṛśaṁ kautūhalānvitāḥ.
english: They wondered, "What will Bibhatsu (Arjuna), the unconquered Pandava (pāṇḍava) in battle, say? And Bhimasena? And the twins?" Thus, they were greatly filled with curiosity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 30. They wondered, "What will Bibhatsu (Arjuna), the unconquered Pandava (pāṇḍava) in battle, say? And Bhimasena? And the twins?" Thus, they were greatly filled with curiosity.

sanskrit: तस्मिन्नुपरते शब्दे भीमसेनोऽब्रवीदिदम् ।प्रगृह्य विपुलं वृत्तं भुजं चन्दनरूषितम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: tasminnuparate śabde bhīmaseno'bravīdidam ,pragṛhya vipulaṁ vṛttaṁ bhujaṁ candanarūṣitam.
english: When that sound ceased, Bhimasena spoke this, raising his massive, well-formed arm, which was smeared with sandalwood paste.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 31. When that sound ceased, Bhimasena spoke this, raising his massive, well-formed arm, which was smeared with sandalwood paste.

sanskrit: यद्येष गुरुरस्माकं धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।न प्रभुः स्यात्कुलस्यास्य न वयं मर्षयेमहि ॥३२॥
transliteration: yadyeṣa gururasmākaṁ dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,na prabhuḥ syātkulasyāsya na vayaṁ marṣayemahi.
english: If Yudhishthira, our teacher and the king of righteousness (dharma), were not the master of this family, we would not tolerate this.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 32. If Yudhishthira, our teacher and the king of righteousness (dharma), were not the master of this family, we would not tolerate this.

sanskrit: ईशो नः पुण्यतपसां प्राणानामपि चेश्वरः ।मन्यते जितमात्मानं यद्येष विजिता वयम् ॥३३॥
transliteration: īśo naḥ puṇyatapasāṁ prāṇānāmapi ceśvaraḥ ,manyate jitamātmānaṁ yadyeṣa vijitā vayam.
english: He is the lord of our meritorious austerities (tapas), and indeed, the lord of our very lives. If he considers his own self (ātman) conquered, then we are also conquered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 33. He is the lord of our meritorious austerities (tapas), and indeed, the lord of our very lives. If he considers his own self (ātman) conquered, then we are also conquered.

sanskrit: न हि मुच्येत जीवन्मे पदा भूमिमुपस्पृशन् ।मर्त्यधर्मा परामृश्य पाञ्चाल्या मूर्धजानिमान् ॥३४॥
transliteration: na hi mucyeta jīvanme padā bhūmimupaspṛśan ,martyadharmā parāmṛśya pāñcālyā mūrdhajānimān.
english: Indeed, no one with a mortal nature (martyadharman) who is alive and treads upon the earth with his foot shall escape me, after having seized these locks of hair of Panchali.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 34. Indeed, no one with a mortal nature (martyadharman) who is alive and treads upon the earth with his foot shall escape me, after having seized these locks of hair of Panchali.

sanskrit: पश्यध्वमायतौ वृत्तौ भुजौ मे परिघाविव ।नैतयोरन्तरं प्राप्य मुच्येतापि शतक्रतुः ॥३५॥
transliteration: paśyadhvamāyatau vṛttau bhujau me parighāviva ,naitayorantaraṁ prāpya mucyetāpi śatakratuḥ.
english: Behold my two long and rounded arms, like iron bars. Not even Indra (Śatakratu) would escape after getting between them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 35. Behold my two long and rounded arms, like iron bars. Not even Indra (Śatakratu) would escape after getting between them.

sanskrit: धर्मपाशसितस्त्वेवं नाधिगच्छामि संकटम् ।गौरवेण निरुद्धश्च निग्रहादर्जुनस्य च ॥३६॥
transliteration: dharmapāśasitastvevaṁ nādhigacchāmi saṁkaṭam ,gauraveṇa niruddhaśca nigrahādarjunasya ca.
english: Thus, even though I am bound by the bonds of duty (dharma), I do not perceive this as a calamity. I am restrained by reverence and also by the control exerted by Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 36. Thus, even though I am bound by the bonds of duty (dharma), I do not perceive this as a calamity. I am restrained by reverence and also by the control exerted by Arjuna.

sanskrit: धर्मराजनिसृष्टस्तु सिंहः क्षुद्रमृगानिव ।धार्तराष्ट्रानिमान्पापान्निष्पिषेयं तलासिभिः ॥३७॥
transliteration: dharmarājanisṛṣṭastu siṁhaḥ kṣudramṛgāniva ,dhārtarāṣṭrānimānpāpānniṣpiṣeyaṁ talāsibhiḥ.
english: But like a lion unleashed by the king of duty (dharma), I would crush these sinful sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, these wicked ones, like small deer, with mighty hand-strikes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 37. But like a lion unleashed by the king of duty (dharma), I would crush these sinful sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, these wicked ones, like small deer, with mighty hand-strikes.

sanskrit: तमुवाच तदा भीष्मो द्रोणो विदुर एव च ।क्षम्यतामेवमित्येवं सर्वं संभवति त्वयि ॥३८॥
transliteration: tamuvāca tadā bhīṣmo droṇo vidura eva ca ,kṣamyatāmevamityevaṁ sarvaṁ saṁbhavati tvayi.
english: Then Bhishma, Drona, and Vidura also said to him: 'Please forgive them, for everything is possible for you.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 62 Verse 38. Then Bhishma, Drona, and Vidura also said to him: 'Please forgive them, for everything is possible for you.'

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।त्रयः किलेमे अधना भवन्ति दासः शिष्यश्चास्वतन्त्रा च नारी ।दासस्य पत्नी त्वं धनमस्य भद्रे हीनेश्वरा दासधनं च दासी ॥१॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,trayaḥ kileme adhanā bhavanti; dāsaḥ śiṣyaścāsvatantrā ca nārī ,dāsasya patnī tvaṁ dhanamasya bhadre; hīneśvarā dāsadhanaṁ ca dāsī.
english: Karna said: 'Indeed, these three are without property: a slave, a disciple, and a woman who is not independent. You are the wife of a slave; you are his property, O virtuous lady. A woman whose master is lowly, and a female slave, are both considered a slave's property.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 1. Karna said: 'Indeed, these three are without property: a slave, a disciple, and a woman who is not independent. You are the wife of a slave; you are his property, O virtuous lady. A woman whose master is lowly, and a female slave, are both considered a slave's property.'

sanskrit: प्रविश्य सा नः परिचारैर्भजस्व तत्ते कार्यं शिष्टमावेश्य वेश्म ।ईशाः स्म सर्वे तव राजपुत्रि भवन्ति ते धार्तराष्ट्रा न पार्थाः ॥२॥
transliteration: praviśya sā naḥ paricārairbhajasva; tatte kāryaṁ śiṣṭamāveśya veśma ,īśāḥ sma sarve tava rājaputri; bhavanti te dhārtarāṣṭrā na pārthāḥ.
english: O princess, having entered our home, you should serve us with your service; that is your remaining duty upon entering the house. We are all your masters. The sons of Dhritarashtra are now yours, not the sons of Pritha.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 2. O princess, having entered our home, you should serve us with your service; that is your remaining duty upon entering the house. We are all your masters. The sons of Dhritarashtra are now yours, not the sons of Pritha.

sanskrit: अन्यं वृणीष्व पतिमाशु भामिनि यस्माद्दास्यं न लभसे देवनेन ।अनवद्या वै पतिषु कामवृत्तिर्नित्यं दास्ये विदितं वै तवास्तु ॥३॥
transliteration: anyaṁ vṛṇīṣva patimāśu bhāmini; yasmāddāsyaṁ na labhase devanena ,anavadyā vai patiṣu kāmavṛtti;rnityaṁ dāsye viditaṁ vai tavāstu.
english: O passionate woman, quickly choose another husband, one from whom you will not incur slavery through gambling. Indeed, a choice made according to one's desire regarding husbands is blameless for those in slavery; let this always be known to you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 3. O passionate woman, quickly choose another husband, one from whom you will not incur slavery through gambling. Indeed, a choice made according to one's desire regarding husbands is blameless for those in slavery; let this always be known to you.

sanskrit: पराजितो नकुलो भीमसेनो युधिष्ठिरः सहदेवोऽर्जुनश्च ।दासीभूता प्रविश याज्ञसेनि पराजितास्ते पतयो न सन्ति ॥४॥
transliteration: parājito nakulo bhīmaseno; yudhiṣṭhiraḥ sahadevo'rjunaśca ,dāsībhūtā praviśa yājñaseni; parājitāste patayo na santi.
english: Nakula, Bhimasena, Yudhishthira, Sahadeva, and Arjuna are defeated. O daughter of Yajnasena, having become a slave, enter [our house]! Your husbands are defeated and are no longer [your true] masters.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 4. Nakula, Bhimasena, Yudhishthira, Sahadeva, and Arjuna are defeated. O daughter of Yajnasena, having become a slave, enter [our house]! Your husbands are defeated and are no longer [your true] masters.

sanskrit: प्रयोजनं चात्मनि किं नु मन्यते पराक्रमं पौरुषं चेह पार्थः ।पाञ्चाल्यस्य द्रुपदस्यात्मजामिमां सभामध्ये योऽतिदेवीद्ग्लहेषु ॥५॥
transliteration: prayojanaṁ cātmani kiṁ nu manyate; parākramaṁ pauruṣaṁ ceha pārthaḥ ,pāñcālyasya drupadasyātmajāmimāṁ; sabhāmadhye yo'tidevīdglaheṣu.
english: What purpose, indeed, does Arjuna (pārtha) now consider in himself (ātman) for his valor and manliness here - he who gambled away this daughter of Drupada, the king of Pañcāla, in the midst of the assembly, in the dice games?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 5. What purpose, indeed, does Arjuna (pārtha) now consider in himself (ātman) for his valor and manliness here - he who gambled away this daughter of Drupada, the king of Pañcāla, in the midst of the assembly, in the dice games?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तद्वै श्रुत्वा भीमसेनोऽत्यमर्षी भृशं निशश्वास तदार्तरूपः ।राजानुगो धर्मपाशानुबद्धो दहन्निवैनं कोपविरक्तदृष्टिः ॥६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tadvai śrutvā bhīmaseno'tyamarṣī; bhṛśaṁ niśaśvāsa tadārtarūpaḥ ,rājānugo dharmapāśānubaddho; dahannivainaṁ kopaviraktadṛṣṭiḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing that, Bhīmasena, exceedingly wrathful, sighed heavily, appearing distressed. Bound by the noose of duty (dharma) and following the king, his gaze, reddened by anger, seemed to burn him (Karṇa) as if.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 6. Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing that, Bhīmasena, exceedingly wrathful, sighed heavily, appearing distressed. Bound by the noose of duty (dharma) and following the king, his gaze, reddened by anger, seemed to burn him (Karṇa) as if.

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।नाहं कुप्ये सूतपुत्रस्य राजन्नेष सत्यं दासधर्मः प्रविष्टः ।किं विद्विषो वाद्य मां धारयेयुर्नादेवीस्त्वं यद्यनया नरेन्द्र ॥७॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,nāhaṁ kupye sūtaputrasya rāja;nneṣa satyaṁ dāsadharmaḥ praviṣṭaḥ ,kiṁ vidviṣo vādya māṁ dhārayeyu;rnādevīstvaṁ yadyanayā narendra.
english: Bhīma said: O King, I do not get angry at the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra). Indeed, this servile conduct (dāsadharma) has entered (me). Why would enemies restrain me today, O King, if you had not, by this (act), made these revered ladies (Draupadī) 'not goddesses' (i.e., disgraced them)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 7. Bhīma said: O King, I do not get angry at the son of the charioteer (Sūtaputra). Indeed, this servile conduct (dāsadharma) has entered (me). Why would enemies restrain me today, O King, if you had not, by this (act), made these revered ladies (Draupadī) 'not goddesses' (i.e., disgraced them)?

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।राधेयस्य वचः श्रुत्वा राजा दुर्योधनस्तदा ।युधिष्ठिरमुवाचेदं तूष्णींभूतमचेतसम् ॥८॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,rādheyasya vacaḥ śrutvā rājā duryodhanastadā ,yudhiṣṭhiramuvācedaṁ tūṣṇīṁbhūtamacetasam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having then heard the words of the son of Rādhā (Rādheya / Karṇa), King Duryodhana then spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, who had become silent and was senseless.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 8. Vaiśampāyana said: Having then heard the words of the son of Rādhā (Rādheya / Karṇa), King Duryodhana then spoke these words to Yudhiṣṭhira, who had become silent and was senseless.

sanskrit: भीमार्जुनौ यमौ चैव स्थितौ ते नृप शासने ।प्रश्नं प्रब्रूहि कृष्णां त्वमजितां यदि मन्यसे ॥९॥
transliteration: bhīmārjunau yamau caiva sthitau te nṛpa śāsane ,praśnaṁ prabrūhi kṛṣṇāṁ tvamajitāṁ yadi manyase.
english: O King, Bhīma and Arjuna, and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) are indeed subject to your command. If you consider Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) unconquered, then speak your question (regarding her).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 9. O King, Bhīma and Arjuna, and the twins (Nakula and Sahadeva) are indeed subject to your command. If you consider Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī) unconquered, then speak your question (regarding her).

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा स कौन्तेयमपोह्य वसनं स्वकम् ।स्मयन्निवैक्षत्पाञ्चालीमैश्वर्यमदमोहितः ॥१०॥
transliteration: evamuktvā sa kaunteyamapohya vasanaṁ svakam ,smayannivaikṣatpāñcālīmaiśvaryamadamohitaḥ.
english: Having thus spoken to the son of Kunti, he, deluded by the intoxication of power, removed his own garment and, as if smirking, looked at Draupadi.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 10. Having thus spoken to the son of Kunti, he, deluded by the intoxication of power, removed his own garment and, as if smirking, looked at Draupadi.

sanskrit: कदलीदण्डसदृशं सर्वलक्षणपूजितम् ।गजहस्तप्रतीकाशं वज्रप्रतिमगौरवम् ॥११॥
transliteration: kadalīdaṇḍasadṛśaṁ sarvalakṣaṇapūjitam ,gajahastapratīkāśaṁ vajrapratimagauravam.
english: Resembling a banana stalk, endowed with all auspicious marks, similar to an elephant's trunk, and possessing the majesty of a thunderbolt.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 11. Resembling a banana stalk, endowed with all auspicious marks, similar to an elephant's trunk, and possessing the majesty of a thunderbolt.

sanskrit: अभ्युत्स्मयित्वा राधेयं भीममाधर्षयन्निव ।द्रौपद्याः प्रेक्षमाणायाः सव्यमूरुमदर्शयत् ॥१२॥
transliteration: abhyutsmayitvā rādheyaṁ bhīmamādharṣayanniva ,draupadyāḥ prekṣamāṇāyāḥ savyamūrumadarśayat.
