english: Smiling scornfully towards Karna, and as if challenging Bhima, he displayed his left thigh to Draupadi, who was watching.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 12. Smiling scornfully towards Karna, and as if challenging Bhima, he displayed his left thigh to Draupadi, who was watching.

sanskrit: वृकोदरस्तदालोक्य नेत्रे उत्फाल्य लोहिते ।प्रोवाच राजमध्ये तं सभां विश्रावयन्निव ॥१३॥
transliteration: vṛkodarastadālokya netre utphālya lohite ,provāca rājamadhye taṁ sabhāṁ viśrāvayanniva.
english: Vrikodara (Bhima), having seen that, and with his reddened eyes wide open, spoke to Duryodhana in the midst of the kings, as if proclaiming to the entire assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 13. Vrikodara (Bhima), having seen that, and with his reddened eyes wide open, spoke to Duryodhana in the midst of the kings, as if proclaiming to the entire assembly.

sanskrit: पितृभिः सह सालोक्यं मा स्म गच्छेद्वृकोदरः ।यद्येतमूरुं गदया न भिन्द्यां ते महाहवे ॥१४॥
transliteration: pitṛbhiḥ saha sālokyaṁ mā sma gacchedvṛkodaraḥ ,yadyetamūruṁ gadayā na bhindyāṁ te mahāhave.
english: May Vṛkodara (Bhīma) not attain the same world (sālokya) as his ancestors, if I do not break this thigh with my mace in the great battle.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 14. May Vṛkodara (Bhīma) not attain the same world (sālokya) as his ancestors, if I do not break this thigh with my mace in the great battle.

sanskrit: क्रुद्धस्य तस्य स्रोतोभ्यः सर्वेभ्यः पावकार्चिषः ।वृक्षस्येव विनिश्चेरुः कोटरेभ्यः प्रदह्यतः ॥१५॥
transliteration: kruddhasya tasya srotobhyaḥ sarvebhyaḥ pāvakārciṣaḥ ,vṛkṣasyeva viniśceruḥ koṭarebhyaḥ pradahyataḥ.
english: From all the pores of that enraged (Bhīma), flames of fire (pāvakārcis) burst forth, just as they emerge from the hollows of a burning tree.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 15. From all the pores of that enraged (Bhīma), flames of fire (pāvakārcis) burst forth, just as they emerge from the hollows of a burning tree.

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।परं भयं पश्यत भीमसेनाद्बुध्यध्वं राज्ञो वरुणस्येव पाशात् ।दैवेरितो नूनमयं पुरस्तात्परोऽनयो भरतेषूदपादि ॥१६॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,paraṁ bhayaṁ paśyata bhīmasenā;dbudhyadhvaṁ rājño varuṇasyeva pāśāt ,daiverito nūnamayaṁ purastā;tparo'nayo bharateṣūdapādi.
english: Vidura said: 'Behold the supreme danger from Bhīmasena! Be vigilant, as it is like the noose of King Varuṇa. Surely, this great calamity (anaya), instigated by divine will, has arisen among the Bhāratas from ancient times.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 16. Vidura said: 'Behold the supreme danger from Bhīmasena! Be vigilant, as it is like the noose of King Varuṇa. Surely, this great calamity (anaya), instigated by divine will, has arisen among the Bhāratas from ancient times.'

sanskrit: अतिद्यूतं कृतमिदं धार्तराष्ट्रा येऽस्यां स्त्रियं विवदध्वं सभायाम् ।योगक्षेमो दृश्यते वो महाभयः पापान्मन्त्रान्कुरवो मन्त्रयन्ति ॥१७॥
transliteration: atidyūtaṁ kṛtamidaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrā; ye'syāṁ striyaṁ vivadadhvaṁ sabhāyām ,yogakṣemo dṛśyate vo mahābhayaḥ; pāpānmantrānkuravo mantrayanti.
english: This excessive gambling has been perpetrated, you Dhārtarāṣṭras, who are disputing over this woman (Draupadī) in this assembly. Your well-being (yogakṣema) appears to be fraught with great peril. The Kurus are deliberating wicked counsels.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 17. This excessive gambling has been perpetrated, you Dhārtarāṣṭras, who are disputing over this woman (Draupadī) in this assembly. Your well-being (yogakṣema) appears to be fraught with great peril. The Kurus are deliberating wicked counsels.

sanskrit: इमं धर्मं कुरवो जानताशु दुर्दृष्टेऽस्मिन्परिषत्संप्रदुष्येत् ।इमां चेत्पूर्वं कितवोऽग्लहीष्यदीशोऽभविष्यदपराजितात्मा ॥१८॥
transliteration: imaṁ dharmaṁ kuravo jānatāśu; durdṛṣṭe'sminpariṣatsaṁpraduṣyet ,imāṁ cetpūrvaṁ kitavo'glahīṣya;dīśo'bhaviṣyadaparājitātmā.
english: O Kurus, quickly understand this natural law (dharma): if it is wrongly observed, this assembly will become utterly corrupt. If the gambler had previously staked this [person], while he still possessed an unconquered spirit (ātman), then he would indeed have been her master.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 18. O Kurus, quickly understand this natural law (dharma): if it is wrongly observed, this assembly will become utterly corrupt. If the gambler had previously staked this [person], while he still possessed an unconquered spirit (ātman), then he would indeed have been her master.

sanskrit: स्वप्ने यथैतद्धि धनं जितं स्यात्तदेवं मन्ये यस्य दीव्यत्यनीशः ।गान्धारिपुत्रस्य वचो निशम्य धर्मादस्मात्कुरवो मापयात ॥१९॥
transliteration: svapne yathaitaddhi dhanaṁ jitaṁ syā;ttadevaṁ manye yasya dīvyatyanīśaḥ ,gāndhāriputrasya vaco niśamya; dharmādasmātkuravo māpayāta.
english: I consider that if someone who is not his own master (īśa) gambles, any wealth won is like wealth won in a dream. O Kurus, having heard the words of Gāndhārī's son (Duryodhana), do not deviate from this natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 19. I consider that if someone who is not his own master (īśa) gambles, any wealth won is like wealth won in a dream. O Kurus, having heard the words of Gāndhārī's son (Duryodhana), do not deviate from this natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।भीमस्य वाक्ये तद्वदेवार्जुनस्य स्थितोऽहं वै यमयोश्चैवमेव ।युधिष्ठिरं चेत्प्रवदन्त्यनीशमथो दास्यान्मोक्ष्यसे याज्ञसेनि ॥२०॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,bhīmasya vākye tadvadevārjunasya; sthito'haṁ vai yamayoścaivameva ,yudhiṣṭhiraṁ cetpravadantyanīśa;matho dāsyānmokṣyase yājñaseni.
english: Duryodhana said: 'I stand firm regarding Bhīma's words, just as I do regarding Arjuna's, and the twins' in the very same way. O Yājñasenī, if they declare Yudhiṣṭhira not to be his own master (īśa), then you will be freed from slavery.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 20. Duryodhana said: 'I stand firm regarding Bhīma's words, just as I do regarding Arjuna's, and the twins' in the very same way. O Yājñasenī, if they declare Yudhiṣṭhira not to be his own master (īśa), then you will be freed from slavery.'

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।ईशो राजा पूर्वमासीद्ग्लहे नः कुन्तीपुत्रो धर्मराजो महात्मा ।ईशस्त्वयं कस्य पराजितात्मा तज्जानीध्वं कुरवः सर्व एव ॥२१॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,īśo rājā pūrvamāsīdglahe naḥ; kuntīputro dharmarājo mahātmā ,īśastvayaṁ kasya parājitātmā; tajjānīdhvaṁ kuravaḥ sarva eva.
english: Arjuna said: 'Previously, our great-souled (mahātman) King of natural law (dharma), the son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), was indeed his own master (īśa) regarding the stakes. But now, with a defeated spirit (ātman), of whom is he the master? O Kurus, all of you should know this!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 21. Arjuna said: 'Previously, our great-souled (mahātman) King of natural law (dharma), the son of Kuntī (Yudhiṣṭhira), was indeed his own master (īśa) regarding the stakes. But now, with a defeated spirit (ātman), of whom is he the master? O Kurus, all of you should know this!'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रस्य गेहे गोमायुरुच्चैर्व्याहरदग्निहोत्रे ।तं रासभाः प्रत्यभाषन्त राजन्समन्ततः पक्षिणश्चैव रौद्राः ॥२२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato rājño dhṛtarāṣṭrasya gehe; gomāyuruccairvyāharadagnihotre ,taṁ rāsabhāḥ pratyabhāṣanta rāja;nsamantataḥ pakṣiṇaścaiva raudrāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Then, in the house of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, a jackal howled loudly at the Agnihotra (fire sacrifice) altar. O King, donkeys and fierce birds responded to it from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 22. Vaiśampāyana said: Then, in the house of King Dhṛtarāṣṭra, a jackal howled loudly at the Agnihotra (fire sacrifice) altar. O King, donkeys and fierce birds responded to it from all directions.

sanskrit: तं च शब्दं विदुरस्तत्त्ववेदी शुश्राव घोरं सुबलात्मजा च ।भीष्मद्रोणौ गौतमश्चापि विद्वान्स्वस्ति स्वस्तीत्यपि चैवाहुरुच्चैः ॥२३॥
transliteration: taṁ ca śabdaṁ vidurastattvavedī; śuśrāva ghoraṁ subalātmajā ca ,bhīṣmadroṇau gautamaścāpi vidvā;nsvasti svastītyapi caivāhuruccaiḥ.
english: Vidura, who was a knower of reality (tattva), and the daughter of Subala (Gāndhārī), heard that dreadful sound. Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and the learned Gautama also loudly exclaimed, "May there be welfare! May there be welfare!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 23. Vidura, who was a knower of reality (tattva), and the daughter of Subala (Gāndhārī), heard that dreadful sound. Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and the learned Gautama also loudly exclaimed, "May there be welfare! May there be welfare!"

sanskrit: ततो गान्धारी विदुरश्चैव विद्वांस्तमुत्पातं घोरमालक्ष्य राज्ञे ।निवेदयामासतुरार्तवत्तदा ततो राजा वाक्यमिदं बभाषे ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato gāndhārī viduraścaiva vidvāṁ;stamutpātaṁ ghoramālakṣya rājñe ,nivedayāmāsaturārtavattadā; tato rājā vākyamidaṁ babhāṣe.
english: Then Gāndhārī and the wise Vidura, perceiving that dreadful omen, reported it to the King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) as if in distress. Then the King spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 24. Then Gāndhārī and the wise Vidura, perceiving that dreadful omen, reported it to the King (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) as if in distress. Then the King spoke these words.

sanskrit: हतोऽसि दुर्योधन मन्दबुद्धे यस्त्वं सभायां कुरुपुंगवानाम् ।स्त्रियं समाभाषसि दुर्विनीत विशेषतो द्रौपदीं धर्मपत्नीम् ॥२५॥
transliteration: hato'si duryodhana mandabuddhe; yastvaṁ sabhāyāṁ kurupuṁgavānām ,striyaṁ samābhāṣasi durvinīta; viśeṣato draupadīṁ dharmapatnīm.
english: "You are ruined (hata), Duryodhana, you foolish one (mandabuddhe)! You, who in the assembly of the best of the Kurus, address a woman (strī) so disrespectfully, especially Draupadī, your lawful wife (dharmapatnī)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 25. "You are ruined (hata), Duryodhana, you foolish one (mandabuddhe)! You, who in the assembly of the best of the Kurus, address a woman (strī) so disrespectfully, especially Draupadī, your lawful wife (dharmapatnī)."

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वा धृतराष्ट्रो मनीषी हितान्वेषी बान्धवानामपायात् ।कृष्णां पाञ्चालीमब्रवीत्सान्त्वपूर्वं विमृश्यैतत्प्रज्ञया तत्त्वबुद्धिः ॥२६॥
transliteration: evamuktvā dhṛtarāṣṭro manīṣī; hitānveṣī bāndhavānāmapāyāt ,kṛṣṇāṁ pāñcālīmabravītsāntvapūrvaṁ; vimṛśyaitatprajñayā tattvabuddhiḥ.
english: Having spoken thus, Dhritarashtra, who was a sagacious man, seeking the welfare of his kinsmen from danger, reflected on this matter with his intellect (prajñā) that discerned truth. Then, he spoke consolingly to Krishnaa Panchali.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 26. Having spoken thus, Dhritarashtra, who was a sagacious man, seeking the welfare of his kinsmen from danger, reflected on this matter with his intellect (prajñā) that discerned truth. Then, he spoke consolingly to Krishnaa Panchali.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।वरं वृणीष्व पाञ्चालि मत्तो यदभिकाङ्क्षसि ।वधूनां हि विशिष्टा मे त्वं धर्मपरमा सती ॥२७॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,varaṁ vṛṇīṣva pāñcāli matto yadabhikāṅkṣasi ,vadhūnāṁ hi viśiṣṭā me tvaṁ dharmaparamā satī.
english: Dhritarashtra said, "O Panchali, choose any boon you desire from me. For among my daughters-in-law, you are indeed the most distinguished, a supremely devoted woman (satī) to (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 27. Dhritarashtra said, "O Panchali, choose any boon you desire from me. For among my daughters-in-law, you are indeed the most distinguished, a supremely devoted woman (satī) to (dharma)."

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।ददासि चेद्वरं मह्यं वृणोमि भरतर्षभ ।सर्वधर्मानुगः श्रीमानदासोऽस्तु युधिष्ठिरः ॥२८॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,dadāsi cedvaraṁ mahyaṁ vṛṇomi bharatarṣabha ,sarvadharmānugaḥ śrīmānadāso'stu yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Draupadi said, "O chief among the Bharatas! If you grant me a boon, I choose this: let Yudhishthira, who is glorious and follows all the principles of (dharma), be free from servitude."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 28. Draupadi said, "O chief among the Bharatas! If you grant me a boon, I choose this: let Yudhishthira, who is glorious and follows all the principles of (dharma), be free from servitude."

sanskrit: मनस्विनमजानन्तो मा वै ब्रूयुः कुमारकाः ।एष वै दासपुत्रेति प्रतिविन्ध्यं तमागतम् ॥२९॥
transliteration: manasvinamajānanto mā vai brūyuḥ kumārakāḥ ,eṣa vai dāsaputreti prativindhyaṁ tamāgatam.
english: Let the boys, not knowing the high-minded (Yudhishthira), not say about Prativindhya, who has arrived, 'This one is the son of a slave.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 29. Let the boys, not knowing the high-minded (Yudhishthira), not say about Prativindhya, who has arrived, 'This one is the son of a slave.'

sanskrit: राजपुत्रः पुरा भूत्वा यथा नान्यः पुमान्क्वचित् ।लालितो दासपुत्रत्वं पश्यन्नश्येद्धि भारत ॥३०॥
transliteration: rājaputraḥ purā bhūtvā yathā nānyaḥ pumānkvacit ,lālito dāsaputratvaṁ paśyannaśyeddhi bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, a prince who was once cherished and had no equal among men, would surely perish upon seeing himself reduced to the state of a slave's son.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 30. O Bhārata, a prince who was once cherished and had no equal among men, would surely perish upon seeing himself reduced to the state of a slave's son.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।द्वितीयं ते वरं भद्रे ददामि वरयस्व माम् ।मनो हि मे वितरति नैकं त्वं वरमर्हसि ॥३१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,dvitīyaṁ te varaṁ bhadre dadāmi varayasva mām ,mano hi me vitarati naikaṁ tvaṁ varamarhasi.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'O gentle lady, I grant you a second boon; ask it of me. My mind is indeed inclined to give, for you are worthy of more than a single boon.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 31. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'O gentle lady, I grant you a second boon; ask it of me. My mind is indeed inclined to give, for you are worthy of more than a single boon.'

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।सरथौ सधनुष्कौ च भीमसेनधनंजयौ ।नकुलं सहदेवं च द्वितीयं वरये वरम् ॥३२॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,sarathau sadhanuṣkau ca bhīmasenadhanaṁjayau ,nakulaṁ sahadevaṁ ca dvitīyaṁ varaye varam.
english: Draupadī said: 'I choose as my second boon Bhīmasena and Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), along with their chariots and bows, and also Nakula and Sahadeva.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 32. Draupadī said: 'I choose as my second boon Bhīmasena and Dhanaṃjaya (Arjuna), along with their chariots and bows, and also Nakula and Sahadeva.'

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।तृतीयं वरयास्मत्तो नासि द्वाभ्यां सुसत्कृता ।त्वं हि सर्वस्नुषाणां मे श्रेयसी धर्मचारिणी ॥३३॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,tṛtīyaṁ varayāsmatto nāsi dvābhyāṁ susatkṛtā ,tvaṁ hi sarvasnuṣāṇāṁ me śreyasī dharmacāriṇī.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Choose a third boon from us. You have not been sufficiently honored with just two boons. Indeed, among all my daughters-in-law, you are the most excellent, a woman who truly upholds righteousness (dharma).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 33. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: 'Choose a third boon from us. You have not been sufficiently honored with just two boons. Indeed, among all my daughters-in-law, you are the most excellent, a woman who truly upholds righteousness (dharma).'

sanskrit: द्रौपद्युवाच ।लोभो धर्मस्य नाशाय भगवन्नाहमुत्सहे ।अनर्हा वरमादातुं तृतीयं राजसत्तम ॥३४॥
transliteration: draupadyuvāca ,lobho dharmasya nāśāya bhagavannāhamutsahe ,anarhā varamādātuṁ tṛtīyaṁ rājasattama.
english: Draupadi said: "O venerable one, O best of kings, I do not wish for greed to lead to the destruction of righteous conduct (dharma). I am unworthy to accept a third boon."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 34. Draupadi said: "O venerable one, O best of kings, I do not wish for greed to lead to the destruction of righteous conduct (dharma). I am unworthy to accept a third boon."

sanskrit: एकमाहुर्वैश्यवरं द्वौ तु क्षत्रस्त्रिया वरौ ।त्रयस्तु राज्ञो राजेन्द्र ब्राह्मणस्य शतं वराः ॥३५॥
transliteration: ekamāhurvaiśyavaraṁ dvau tu kṣatrastriyā varau ,trayastu rājño rājendra brāhmaṇasya śataṁ varāḥ.
english: They say that one boon is for a Vaiśya. However, for a Kṣatriya woman, two boons are appropriate. For a king, O best of kings, three boons (vara) are stated, and for a Brāhmaṇa, a hundred boons (vara).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 35. They say that one boon is for a Vaiśya. However, for a Kṣatriya woman, two boons are appropriate. For a king, O best of kings, three boons (vara) are stated, and for a Brāhmaṇa, a hundred boons (vara).

sanskrit: पापीयांस इमे भूत्वा संतीर्णाः पतयो मम ।वेत्स्यन्ति चैव भद्राणि राजन्पुण्येन कर्मणा ॥३६॥
transliteration: pāpīyāṁsa ime bhūtvā saṁtīrṇāḥ patayo mama ,vetsyanti caiva bhadrāṇi rājanpuṇyena karmaṇā.
english: Even though these husbands of mine were very sinful, now, having been delivered (saved), they will, O King, certainly experience good fortunes through meritorious actions (karma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 63 Verse 36. Even though these husbands of mine were very sinful, now, having been delivered (saved), they will, O King, certainly experience good fortunes through meritorious actions (karma).

sanskrit: कर्ण उवाच ।या नः श्रुता मनुष्येषु स्त्रियो रूपेण संमताः ।तासामेतादृशं कर्म न कस्यांचन शुश्रुमः ॥१॥
transliteration: karṇa uvāca ,yā naḥ śrutā manuṣyeṣu striyo rūpeṇa saṁmatāḥ ,tāsāmetādṛśaṁ karma na kasyāṁcana śuśrumaḥ.
english: Karna said: 'Among humanity, we have heard of women who are renowned for their beauty. But such a deed as this, we have not heard of from any of them.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 1. Karna said: 'Among humanity, we have heard of women who are renowned for their beauty. But such a deed as this, we have not heard of from any of them.'

sanskrit: क्रोधाविष्टेषु पार्थेषु धार्तराष्ट्रेषु चाप्यति ।द्रौपदी पाण्डुपुत्राणां कृष्णा शान्तिरिहाभवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: krodhāviṣṭeṣu pārtheṣu dhārtarāṣṭreṣu cāpyati ,draupadī pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ kṛṣṇā śāntirihābhavat.
english: When both the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā) and the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra) were exceedingly filled with anger, Draupadī, also known as Kṛṣṇā, became the peace for the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas) in this situation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 2. When both the Pārthas (sons of Pṛthā) and the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra) were exceedingly filled with anger, Draupadī, also known as Kṛṣṇā, became the peace for the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas) in this situation.

sanskrit: अप्लवेऽम्भसि मग्नानामप्रतिष्ठे निमज्जताम् ।पाञ्चाली पाण्डुपुत्राणां नौरेषा पारगाभवत् ॥३॥
transliteration: aplave'mbhasi magnānāmapratiṣṭhe nimajjatām ,pāñcālī pāṇḍuputrāṇāṁ naureṣā pāragābhavat.
english: For the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas), who were submerged and sinking in unstable (apratiṣṭha), supportless (aplave) waters, this Pāñcālī (Draupadī) became the boat that carried them to the other shore.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 3. For the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas), who were submerged and sinking in unstable (apratiṣṭha), supportless (aplave) waters, this Pāñcālī (Draupadī) became the boat that carried them to the other shore.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तद्वै श्रुत्वा भीमसेनः कुरुमध्येऽत्यमर्षणः ।स्त्री गतिः पाण्डुपुत्राणामित्युवाच सुदुर्मनाः ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tadvai śrutvā bhīmasenaḥ kurumadhye'tyamarṣaṇaḥ ,strī gatiḥ pāṇḍuputrāṇāmityuvāca sudurmanāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Having indeed heard that, Bhīmasena, who was exceedingly enraged (atyamarṣaṇa) in the assembly of the Kurus and deeply dejected, said, "A woman is the only refuge (gati) for the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas)!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: Having indeed heard that, Bhīmasena, who was exceedingly enraged (atyamarṣaṇa) in the assembly of the Kurus and deeply dejected, said, "A woman is the only refuge (gati) for the sons of Pāṇḍu (Pandavas)!"

sanskrit: त्रीणि ज्योतींषि पुरुष इति वै देवलोऽब्रवीत् ।अपत्यं कर्म विद्या च यतः सृष्टाः प्रजास्ततः ॥५॥
transliteration: trīṇi jyotīṁṣi puruṣa iti vai devalo'bravīt ,apatyaṁ karma vidyā ca yataḥ sṛṣṭāḥ prajāstataḥ.
english: Devala indeed said that a man (puruṣa) has three lights: progeny, action (karma), and knowledge (vidyā). It is from these that beings are created.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 5. Devala indeed said that a man (puruṣa) has three lights: progeny, action (karma), and knowledge (vidyā). It is from these that beings are created.

sanskrit: अमेध्ये वै गतप्राणे शून्ये ज्ञातिभिरुज्झिते ।देहे त्रितयमेवैतत्पुरुषस्योपजायते ॥६॥
transliteration: amedhye vai gataprāṇe śūnye jñātibhirujjhite ,dehe tritayamevaitatpuruṣasyopajāyate.
english: In a body that is impure, indeed lifeless, empty, and abandoned by relatives, these three qualities (of impurity, lifelessness, and emptiness) become evident concerning the individual (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 6. In a body that is impure, indeed lifeless, empty, and abandoned by relatives, these three qualities (of impurity, lifelessness, and emptiness) become evident concerning the individual (puruṣa).

sanskrit: तन्नो ज्योतिरभिहतं दाराणामभिमर्शनात् ।धनंजय कथं स्वित्स्यादपत्यमभिमृष्टजम् ॥७॥
transliteration: tanno jyotirabhihataṁ dārāṇāmabhimarśanāt ,dhanaṁjaya kathaṁ svitsyādapatyamabhimṛṣṭajam.
english: That luster of our lineage has been extinguished due to the violation of wives. O Dhanañjaya, how then could offspring born from such a violation possibly exist (purely)?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 7. That luster of our lineage has been extinguished due to the violation of wives. O Dhanañjaya, how then could offspring born from such a violation possibly exist (purely)?

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।न चैवोक्ता न चानुक्ता हीनतः परुषा गिरः ।भारताः प्रतिजल्पन्ति सदा तूत्तमपूरुषाः ॥८॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,na caivoktā na cānuktā hīnataḥ paruṣā giraḥ ,bhāratāḥ pratijalpanti sadā tūttamapūruṣāḥ.
english: Arjuna said: 'Noble individuals (uttamapuruṣa) among the Bhāratas never respond with harsh words - neither directly spoken nor merely implied - even when in a disadvantaged position. Indeed, they always (act nobly).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 8. Arjuna said: 'Noble individuals (uttamapuruṣa) among the Bhāratas never respond with harsh words - neither directly spoken nor merely implied - even when in a disadvantaged position. Indeed, they always (act nobly).'

sanskrit: स्मरन्ति सुकृतान्येव न वैराणि कृतानि च ।सन्तः प्रतिविजानन्तो लब्ध्वा प्रत्ययमात्मनः ॥९॥
transliteration: smaranti sukṛtānyeva na vairāṇi kṛtāni ca ,santaḥ prativijānanto labdhvā pratyayamātmanaḥ.
english: The virtuous (santas), having gained a firm conviction about the true self (ātman), remember only good deeds and not the enmities that have occurred.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 9. The virtuous (santas), having gained a firm conviction about the true self (ātman), remember only good deeds and not the enmities that have occurred.

sanskrit: भीम उवाच ।इहैवैतांस्तुरा सर्वान्हन्मि शत्रून्समागतान् ।अथ निष्क्रम्य राजेन्द्र समूलान्कृन्धि भारत ॥१०॥
transliteration: bhīma uvāca ,ihaivaitāṁsturā sarvānhanmi śatrūnsamāgatān ,atha niṣkramya rājendra samūlānkṛndhi bhārata.
english: Bhima said: "I will quickly kill all these assembled enemies right here. Afterward, O King, O Bharata, you should sally forth and utterly destroy them."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 10. Bhima said: "I will quickly kill all these assembled enemies right here. Afterward, O King, O Bharata, you should sally forth and utterly destroy them."

sanskrit: किं नो विवदितेनेह किं नः क्लेशेन भारत ।अद्यैवैतान्निहन्मीह प्रशाधि वसुधामिमाम् ॥११॥
transliteration: kiṁ no vivaditeneha kiṁ naḥ kleśena bhārata ,adyaivaitānnihanmīha praśādhi vasudhāmimām.
english: O Bharata, what is the use of disputing here? Why should we undergo hardship? I will slay these enemies right here, today itself. Then you govern this earth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 11. O Bharata, what is the use of disputing here? Why should we undergo hardship? I will slay these enemies right here, today itself. Then you govern this earth.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्त्वा भीमसेनस्तु कनिष्ठैर्भ्रातृभिर्वृतः ।मृगमध्ये यथा सिंहो मुहुः परिघमैक्षत ॥१२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktvā bhīmasenastu kaniṣṭhairbhrātṛbhirvṛtaḥ ,mṛgamadhye yathā siṁho muhuḥ parighamaikṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "Having spoken thus, Bhimasena, surrounded by his younger brothers, repeatedly gazed at his mace, like a lion amidst deer."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 12. Vaiśampāyana said: "Having spoken thus, Bhimasena, surrounded by his younger brothers, repeatedly gazed at his mace, like a lion amidst deer."

sanskrit: सान्त्व्यमानो वीज्यमानः पार्थेनाक्लिष्टकर्मणा ।स्विद्यते च महाबाहुरन्तर्दाहेन वीर्यवान् ॥१३॥
transliteration: sāntvyamāno vījyamānaḥ pārthenākliṣṭakarmaṇā ,svidyate ca mahābāhurantardāhena vīryavān.
english: Though being consoled and fanned by Arjuna, the one whose actions are unwearied, the mighty-armed and valiant Bhima sweats due to an intense inner burning.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 13. Though being consoled and fanned by Arjuna, the one whose actions are unwearied, the mighty-armed and valiant Bhima sweats due to an intense inner burning.

sanskrit: क्रुद्धस्य तस्य स्रोतोभ्यः कर्णादिभ्यो नराधिप ।सधूमः सस्फुलिङ्गार्चिः पावकः समजायत ॥१४॥
transliteration: kruddhasya tasya srotobhyaḥ karṇādibhyo narādhipa ,sadhūmaḥ sasphuliṅgārciḥ pāvakaḥ samajāyata.
english: O king of men, from the openings of that enraged one, such as his ears, fire arose with smoke, sparks, and flames.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 14. O king of men, from the openings of that enraged one, such as his ears, fire arose with smoke, sparks, and flames.

sanskrit: भ्रुकुटीपुटदुष्प्रेक्ष्यमभवत्तस्य तन्मुखम् ।युगान्तकाले संप्राप्ते कृतान्तस्येव रूपिणः ॥१५॥
transliteration: bhrukuṭīpuṭaduṣprekṣyamabhavattasya tanmukham ,yugāntakāle saṁprāpte kṛtāntasyeva rūpiṇaḥ.
english: His face became difficult to look at because of the furrowed brow, just like the embodied Kr̥tānta (god of death) at the time when the dissolution of the age (yugānta) had arrived.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 15. His face became difficult to look at because of the furrowed brow, just like the embodied Kr̥tānta (god of death) at the time when the dissolution of the age (yugānta) had arrived.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिरस्तमावार्य बाहुना बाहुशालिनम् ।मैवमित्यब्रवीच्चैनं जोषमास्स्वेति भारत ॥१६॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhirastamāvārya bāhunā bāhuśālinam ,maivamityabravīccainaṁ joṣamāssveti bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, Yudhiṣṭhira, restraining that mighty-armed one with his arm, said to him, "Do not do this! Remain silent!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 16. O Bhārata, Yudhiṣṭhira, restraining that mighty-armed one with his arm, said to him, "Do not do this! Remain silent!"

sanskrit: निवार्य तं महाबाहुं कोपसंरक्तलोचनम् ।पितरं समुपातिष्ठद्धृतराष्ट्रं कृताञ्जलिः ॥१७॥
transliteration: nivārya taṁ mahābāhuṁ kopasaṁraktalocanam ,pitaraṁ samupātiṣṭhaddhṛtarāṣṭraṁ kṛtāñjaliḥ.
english: Having restrained that mighty-armed one, whose eyes were reddened with anger, he (Yudhiṣṭhira) approached his father Dhṛtarāṣṭra with joined hands.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 64 Verse 17. Having restrained that mighty-armed one, whose eyes were reddened with anger, he (Yudhiṣṭhira) approached his father Dhṛtarāṣṭra with joined hands.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।राजन्किं करवामस्ते प्रशाध्यस्मांस्त्वमीश्वरः ।नित्यं हि स्थातुमिच्छामस्तव भारत शासने ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,rājankiṁ karavāmaste praśādhyasmāṁstvamīśvaraḥ ,nityaṁ hi sthātumicchāmastava bhārata śāsane.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said, "O King, what service can we render to you? Please govern us, for you are our lord. Indeed, O Bhārata, we always desire to remain under your guidance."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 1. Yudhiṣṭhira said, "O King, what service can we render to you? Please govern us, for you are our lord. Indeed, O Bhārata, we always desire to remain under your guidance."

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अजातशत्रो भद्रं ते अरिष्टं स्वस्ति गच्छत ।अनुज्ञाताः सहधनाः स्वराज्यमनुशासत ॥२॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,ajātaśatro bhadraṁ te ariṣṭaṁ svasti gacchata ,anujñātāḥ sahadhanāḥ svarājyamanuśāsata.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said, "O Ajātaśatru, may you have well-being. Depart safely and without harm. Having been permitted, along with your wealth, go and rule your own kingdom."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 2. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said, "O Ajātaśatru, may you have well-being. Depart safely and without harm. Having been permitted, along with your wealth, go and rule your own kingdom."

sanskrit: इदं त्वेवावबोद्धव्यं वृद्धस्य मम शासनम् ।धिया निगदितं कृत्स्नं पथ्यं निःश्रेयसं परम् ॥३॥
transliteration: idaṁ tvevāvaboddhavyaṁ vṛddhasya mama śāsanam ,dhiyā nigaditaṁ kṛtsnaṁ pathyaṁ niḥśreyasaṁ param.
english: Indeed, this instruction from me, an old man, must be fully understood. It has been entirely imparted with wisdom, and it is wholesome, leading to the ultimate well-being (niḥśreyasa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 3. Indeed, this instruction from me, an old man, must be fully understood. It has been entirely imparted with wisdom, and it is wholesome, leading to the ultimate well-being (niḥśreyasa).

sanskrit: वेत्थ त्वं तात धर्माणां गतिं सूक्ष्मां युधिष्ठिर ।विनीतोऽसि महाप्राज्ञ वृद्धानां पर्युपासिता ॥४॥
transliteration: vettha tvaṁ tāta dharmāṇāṁ gatiṁ sūkṣmāṁ yudhiṣṭhira ,vinīto'si mahāprājña vṛddhānāṁ paryupāsitā.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, my dear son, you understand the subtle path of natural law (dharma). You are modest, O greatly wise one, and you have diligently attended upon the elders.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 4. O Yudhiṣṭhira, my dear son, you understand the subtle path of natural law (dharma). You are modest, O greatly wise one, and you have diligently attended upon the elders.

sanskrit: यतो बुद्धिस्ततः शान्तिः प्रशमं गच्छ भारत ।नादारौ क्रमते शस्त्रं दारौ शस्त्रं निपात्यते ॥५॥
transliteration: yato buddhistataḥ śāntiḥ praśamaṁ gaccha bhārata ,nādārau kramate śastraṁ dārau śastraṁ nipātyate.
english: Where there is wisdom (buddhi), there is peace. Therefore, attain tranquility, O Bhārata. A weapon is not employed against that which is not a proper target; rather, a weapon is struck down upon a proper target.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 5. Where there is wisdom (buddhi), there is peace. Therefore, attain tranquility, O Bhārata. A weapon is not employed against that which is not a proper target; rather, a weapon is struck down upon a proper target.

sanskrit: न वैराण्यभिजानन्ति गुणान्पश्यन्ति नागुणान् ।विरोधं नाधिगच्छन्ति ये त उत्तमपूरुषाः ॥६॥
transliteration: na vairāṇyabhijānanti guṇānpaśyanti nāguṇān ,virodhaṁ nādhigacchanti ye ta uttamapūruṣāḥ.
english: The excellent persons (puruṣa) do not acknowledge enmities. They perceive qualities and not faults, and they do not recognize conflict. Such are the excellent persons (puruṣa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 6. The excellent persons (puruṣa) do not acknowledge enmities. They perceive qualities and not faults, and they do not recognize conflict. Such are the excellent persons (puruṣa).

sanskrit: संवादे परुषाण्याहुर्युधिष्ठिर नराधमाः ।प्रत्याहुर्मध्यमास्त्वेतानुक्ताः परुषमुत्तरम् ॥७॥
transliteration: saṁvāde paruṣāṇyāhuryudhiṣṭhira narādhamāḥ ,pratyāhurmadhyamāstvetānuktāḥ paruṣamuttaram.
english: O Yudhiṣṭhira, the worst of men (narādhama) speak harsh words in conversation. However, the mediocre ones, when addressed harshly, retort with harsh replies to them.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 7. O Yudhiṣṭhira, the worst of men (narādhama) speak harsh words in conversation. However, the mediocre ones, when addressed harshly, retort with harsh replies to them.

sanskrit: नैवोक्ता नैव चानुक्ता अहिताः परुषा गिरः ।प्रतिजल्पन्ति वै धीराः सदा उत्तमपूरुषाः ॥८॥
transliteration: naivoktā naiva cānuktā ahitāḥ paruṣā giraḥ ,pratijalpanti vai dhīrāḥ sadā uttamapūruṣāḥ.
english: Harmful, harsh words are neither uttered nor kept unsaid by the wise (dhīra). Indeed, excellent persons (puruṣa) always respond.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 8. Harmful, harsh words are neither uttered nor kept unsaid by the wise (dhīra). Indeed, excellent persons (puruṣa) always respond.

sanskrit: स्मरन्ति सुकृतान्येव न वैराणि कृतान्यपि ।सन्तः प्रतिविजानन्तो लब्ध्वा प्रत्ययमात्मनः ॥९॥
transliteration: smaranti sukṛtānyeva na vairāṇi kṛtānyapi ,santaḥ prativijānanto labdhvā pratyayamātmanaḥ.
english: Good people (santaḥ), having obtained a clear understanding of their inner self (ātman), remember only the good deeds that were performed, and not even the acts of hostility that have been committed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 9. Good people (santaḥ), having obtained a clear understanding of their inner self (ātman), remember only the good deeds that were performed, and not even the acts of hostility that have been committed.

sanskrit: तथाचरितमार्येण त्वयास्मिन्सत्समागमे ।दुर्योधनस्य पारुष्यं तत्तात हृदि मा कृथाः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tathācaritamāryeṇa tvayāsminsatsamāgame ,duryodhanasya pāruṣyaṁ tattāta hṛdi mā kṛthāḥ.
english: My dear son (tāta), you have behaved thus nobly in this assembly of virtuous people (satsamāgama). Therefore, do not harbor Duryodhana's harshness in your heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 10. My dear son (tāta), you have behaved thus nobly in this assembly of virtuous people (satsamāgama). Therefore, do not harbor Duryodhana's harshness in your heart.

sanskrit: मातरं चैव गान्धारीं मां च त्वद्गुणकाङ्क्षिणम् ।उपस्थितं वृद्धमन्धं पितरं पश्य भारत ॥११॥
transliteration: mātaraṁ caiva gāndhārīṁ māṁ ca tvadguṇakāṅkṣiṇam ,upasthitaṁ vṛddhamandhaṁ pitaraṁ paśya bhārata.
english: O Bhārata, behold your mother Gandhari, and me, your aged, blind father, who stands here desiring your virtues.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 11. O Bhārata, behold your mother Gandhari, and me, your aged, blind father, who stands here desiring your virtues.

sanskrit: प्रेक्षापूर्वं मया द्यूतमिदमासीदुपेक्षितम् ।मित्राणि द्रष्टुकामेन पुत्राणां च बलाबलम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: prekṣāpūrvaṁ mayā dyūtamidamāsīdupekṣitam ,mitrāṇi draṣṭukāmena putrāṇāṁ ca balābalam.
english: This game of dice was intentionally permitted by me, as I desired to discern the strengths and weaknesses of both my friends and my sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 12. This game of dice was intentionally permitted by me, as I desired to discern the strengths and weaknesses of both my friends and my sons.

sanskrit: अशोच्याः कुरवो राजन्येषां त्वमनुशासिता ।मन्त्री च विदुरो धीमान्सर्वशास्त्रविशारदः ॥१३॥
transliteration: aśocyāḥ kuravo rājanyeṣāṁ tvamanuśāsitā ,mantrī ca viduro dhīmānsarvaśāstraviśāradaḥ.
english: O King, the Kurus are not to be grieved for, as you are their ruler and wise Vidura, an expert in all scriptures, is their minister.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 13. O King, the Kurus are not to be grieved for, as you are their ruler and wise Vidura, an expert in all scriptures, is their minister.

sanskrit: त्वयि धर्मोऽर्जुने वीर्यं भीमसेने पराक्रमः ।श्रद्धा च गुरुशुश्रूषा यमयोः पुरुषाग्र्ययोः ॥१४॥
transliteration: tvayi dharmo'rjune vīryaṁ bhīmasene parākramaḥ ,śraddhā ca guruśuśrūṣā yamayoḥ puruṣāgryayoḥ.
english: In you (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) resides adherence to natural law (dharma), in Arjuna, valor; in Bhimasena, prowess. And in the foremost among men, the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), there is faith (śraddhā) and service to their preceptor (guruśuśrūṣā).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 14. In you (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) resides adherence to natural law (dharma), in Arjuna, valor; in Bhimasena, prowess. And in the foremost among men, the two Yamas (Nakula and Sahadeva), there is faith (śraddhā) and service to their preceptor (guruśuśrūṣā).

sanskrit: अजातशत्रो भद्रं ते खाण्डवप्रस्थमाविश ।भ्रातृभिस्तेऽस्तु सौभ्रात्रं धर्मे ते धीयतां मनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ajātaśatro bhadraṁ te khāṇḍavaprasthamāviśa ,bhrātṛbhiste'stu saubhrātraṁ dharme te dhīyatāṁ manaḥ.
english: O Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), may good fortune be upon you. Enter Khaṇḍavaprastha. Let there be good brotherhood (saubhrātram) with your brothers, and let your mind be placed in natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 15. O Ajātaśatru (Yudhiṣṭhira), may good fortune be upon you. Enter Khaṇḍavaprastha. Let there be good brotherhood (saubhrātram) with your brothers, and let your mind be placed in natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तो भरतश्रेष्ठो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।कृत्वार्यसमयं सर्वं प्रतस्थे भ्रातृभिः सह ॥१६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityukto bharataśreṣṭho dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,kṛtvāryasamayaṁ sarvaṁ pratasthe bhrātṛbhiḥ saha.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost of the Bhāratas and the King of natural law (dharma), having fulfilled the entire agreement of the noble ones, set out with his brothers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 16. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the foremost of the Bhāratas and the King of natural law (dharma), having fulfilled the entire agreement of the noble ones, set out with his brothers.

sanskrit: ते रथान्मेघसंकाशानास्थाय सह कृष्णया ।प्रययुर्हृष्टमनस इन्द्रप्रस्थं पुरोत्तमम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: te rathānmeghasaṁkāśānāsthāya saha kṛṣṇayā ,prayayurhṛṣṭamanasa indraprasthaṁ purottamam.
english: With cheerful hearts, they mounted chariots that resembled clouds and, accompanied by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), set forth for Indraprastha, the finest of cities.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 65 Verse 17. With cheerful hearts, they mounted chariots that resembled clouds and, accompanied by Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), set forth for Indraprastha, the finest of cities.

sanskrit: जनमेजय उवाच ।अनुज्ञातांस्तान्विदित्वा सरत्नधनसंचयान् ।पाण्डवान्धार्तराष्ट्राणां कथमासीन्मनस्तदा ॥१॥
transliteration: janamejaya uvāca ,anujñātāṁstānviditvā saratnadhanasaṁcayān ,pāṇḍavāndhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṁ kathamāsīnmanastadā.
english: Janamejaya asked: "When they learned that the Pāṇḍavas had been granted permission by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their accumulated jewels and wealth, how then were the minds of the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra)?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 1. Janamejaya asked: "When they learned that the Pāṇḍavas had been granted permission by Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their accumulated jewels and wealth, how then were the minds of the Dhārtarāṣṭras (sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra)?"

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।अनुज्ञातांस्तान्विदित्वा धृतराष्ट्रेण धीमता ।राजन्दुःशासनः क्षिप्रं जगाम भ्रातरं प्रति ॥२॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,anujñātāṁstānviditvā dhṛtarāṣṭreṇa dhīmatā ,rājanduḥśāsanaḥ kṣipraṁ jagāma bhrātaraṁ prati.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, upon learning that they (the Pāṇḍavas) had been granted permission by the wise Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Duḥśāsana swiftly went to his brother."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 2. Vaiśampāyana said: "O King, upon learning that they (the Pāṇḍavas) had been granted permission by the wise Dhṛtarāṣṭra, Duḥśāsana swiftly went to his brother."

sanskrit: दुर्योधनं समासाद्य सामात्यं भरतर्षभ ।दुःखार्तो भरतश्रेष्ठ इदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥३॥
transliteration: duryodhanaṁ samāsādya sāmātyaṁ bharatarṣabha ,duḥkhārto bharataśreṣṭha idaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: O bull among the Bhāratas, O best of the Bhāratas, having approached Duryodhana, who was accompanied by his ministers, Duḥśāsana, distressed by sorrow, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 3. O bull among the Bhāratas, O best of the Bhāratas, having approached Duryodhana, who was accompanied by his ministers, Duḥśāsana, distressed by sorrow, spoke these words.

sanskrit: दुःखेनैतत्समानीतं स्थविरो नाशयत्यसौ ।शत्रुसाद्गमयद्द्रव्यं तद्बुध्यध्वं महारथाः ॥४॥
transliteration: duḥkhenaitatsamānītaṁ sthaviro nāśayatyasau ,śatrusādgamayaddravyaṁ tadbudhyadhvaṁ mahārathāḥ.
english: This wealth, acquired with great difficulty, that old man is now destroying, letting it go to our enemies. O great warriors, understand this!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 4. This wealth, acquired with great difficulty, that old man is now destroying, letting it go to our enemies. O great warriors, understand this!

sanskrit: अथ दुर्योधनः कर्णः शकुनिश्चापि सौबलः ।मिथः संगम्य सहिताः पाण्डवान्प्रति मानिनः ॥५॥
transliteration: atha duryodhanaḥ karṇaḥ śakuniścāpi saubalaḥ ,mithaḥ saṁgamya sahitāḥ pāṇḍavānprati māninaḥ.
english: Then Duryodhana, Karṇa, and Shakuni, the son of Subala, being proud, gathered together among themselves concerning the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 5. Then Duryodhana, Karṇa, and Shakuni, the son of Subala, being proud, gathered together among themselves concerning the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: वैचित्रवीर्यं राजानं धृतराष्ट्रं मनीषिणम् ।अभिगम्य त्वरायुक्ताः श्लक्ष्णं वचनमब्रुवन् ॥६॥
transliteration: vaicitravīryaṁ rājānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ manīṣiṇam ,abhigamya tvarāyuktāḥ ślakṣṇaṁ vacanamabruvan.
english: Filled with urgency, they approached King Dhritarāṣṭra, the wise son of Vichitravīrya, and spoke gentle words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 6. Filled with urgency, they approached King Dhritarāṣṭra, the wise son of Vichitravīrya, and spoke gentle words.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन उवाच ।न त्वयेदं श्रुतं राजन्यज्जगाद बृहस्पतिः ।शक्रस्य नीतिं प्रवदन्विद्वान्देवपुरोहितः ॥७॥
transliteration: duryodhana uvāca ,na tvayedaṁ śrutaṁ rājanyajjagāda bṛhaspatiḥ ,śakrasya nītiṁ pravadanvidvāndevapurohitaḥ.
english: Duryodhana said: "O King, have you not heard what Bṛhaspati, the wise priest of the gods, declared as Śakra's policy?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 7. Duryodhana said: "O King, have you not heard what Bṛhaspati, the wise priest of the gods, declared as Śakra's policy?"

sanskrit: सर्वोपायैर्निहन्तव्याः शत्रवः शत्रुकर्षण ।पुरा युद्धाद्बलाद्वापि प्रकुर्वन्ति तवाहितम् ॥८॥
transliteration: sarvopāyairnihantavyāḥ śatravaḥ śatrukarṣaṇa ,purā yuddhādbalādvāpi prakurvanti tavāhitam.
english: O chastiser of enemies, foes should be eliminated by all means before they cause you harm, whether through war or by force.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 8. O chastiser of enemies, foes should be eliminated by all means before they cause you harm, whether through war or by force.

sanskrit: ते वयं पाण्डवधनैः सर्वान्संपूज्य पार्थिवान् ।यदि तान्योधयिष्यामः किं वा नः परिहास्यति ॥९॥
transliteration: te vayaṁ pāṇḍavadhanaiḥ sarvānsaṁpūjya pārthivān ,yadi tānyodhayiṣyāmaḥ kiṁ vā naḥ parihāsyati.
english: If we, having honored all the kings with the wealth of the Pāṇḍavas, then fight against them, what will be left for us?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 9. If we, having honored all the kings with the wealth of the Pāṇḍavas, then fight against them, what will be left for us?

sanskrit: अहीनाशीविषान्क्रुद्धान्दंशाय समुपस्थितान् ।कृत्वा कण्ठे च पृष्ठे च कः समुत्स्रष्टुमर्हति ॥१०॥
transliteration: ahīnāśīviṣānkruddhāndaṁśāya samupasthitān ,kṛtvā kaṇṭhe ca pṛṣṭhe ca kaḥ samutsraṣṭumarhati.
english: Who would be willing to release angry, venomous snakes, poised to bite, after having placed them around his neck and on his back?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 10. Who would be willing to release angry, venomous snakes, poised to bite, after having placed them around his neck and on his back?

sanskrit: आत्तशस्त्रा रथगताः कुपितास्तात पाण्डवाः ।निःशेषं नः करिष्यन्ति क्रुद्धा ह्याशीविषा यथा ॥११॥
transliteration: āttaśastrā rathagatāḥ kupitāstāta pāṇḍavāḥ ,niḥśeṣaṁ naḥ kariṣyanti kruddhā hyāśīviṣā yathā.
english: O father, the Pāṇḍavas, with weapons drawn, mounted on their chariots, and infuriated, will completely annihilate us, just as angry venomous snakes would.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 11. O father, the Pāṇḍavas, with weapons drawn, mounted on their chariots, and infuriated, will completely annihilate us, just as angry venomous snakes would.

sanskrit: संनद्धो ह्यर्जुनो याति विवृत्य परमेषुधी ।गाण्डीवं मुहुरादत्ते निःश्वसंश्च निरीक्षते ॥१२॥
transliteration: saṁnaddho hyarjuno yāti vivṛtya parameṣudhī ,gāṇḍīvaṁ muhurādatte niḥśvasaṁśca nirīkṣate.
english: Indeed, Arjuna, fully armed, advances, opening his excellent quivers. He repeatedly draws his Gāṇḍīva bow, and sighing, he observes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 12. Indeed, Arjuna, fully armed, advances, opening his excellent quivers. He repeatedly draws his Gāṇḍīva bow, and sighing, he observes.

sanskrit: गदां गुर्वीं समुद्यम्य त्वरितश्च वृकोदरः ।स्वरथं योजयित्वाशु निर्यात इति नः श्रुतम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: gadāṁ gurvīṁ samudyamya tvaritaśca vṛkodaraḥ ,svarathaṁ yojayitvāśu niryāta iti naḥ śrutam.
english: Bhīma (Vṛkodara), having quickly raised his heavy mace and swiftly yoked his own chariot, set out; this is what we have heard.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 13. Bhīma (Vṛkodara), having quickly raised his heavy mace and swiftly yoked his own chariot, set out; this is what we have heard.

sanskrit: नकुलः खड्गमादाय चर्म चाप्यष्टचन्द्रकम् ।सहदेवश्च राजा च चक्रुराकारमिङ्गितैः ॥१४॥
transliteration: nakulaḥ khaḍgamādāya carma cāpyaṣṭacandrakam ,sahadevaśca rājā ca cakrurākāramiṅgitaiḥ.
english: Nakula, taking his sword and a shield adorned with eight moons, and Sahadeva and King Yudhiṣṭhira conveyed their intentions through gestures.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 14. Nakula, taking his sword and a shield adorned with eight moons, and Sahadeva and King Yudhiṣṭhira conveyed their intentions through gestures.

sanskrit: ते त्वास्थाय रथान्सर्वे बहुशस्त्रपरिच्छदान् ।अभिघ्नन्तो रथव्रातान्सेनायोगाय निर्ययुः ॥१५॥
transliteration: te tvāsthāya rathānsarve bahuśastraparicchadān ,abhighnanto rathavrātānsenāyogāya niryayuḥ.
english: And all of them, having mounted their chariots, which were equipped with many weapons, charged against the throngs of chariots and set out for the battle formation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 15. And all of them, having mounted their chariots, which were equipped with many weapons, charged against the throngs of chariots and set out for the battle formation.

sanskrit: न क्षंस्यन्ते तथास्माभिर्जातु विप्रकृता हि ते ।द्रौपद्याश्च परिक्लेशं कस्तेषां क्षन्तुमर्हति ॥१६॥
transliteration: na kṣaṁsyante tathāsmābhirjātu viprakṛtā hi te ,draupadyāśca parikleśaṁ kasteṣāṁ kṣantumarhati.
english: Indeed, they will never forgive us for the way we ill-treated them. And who among them could possibly forgive Draupadi's suffering?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 16. Indeed, they will never forgive us for the way we ill-treated them. And who among them could possibly forgive Draupadi's suffering?

sanskrit: पुनर्दीव्याम भद्रं ते वनवासाय पाण्डवैः ।एवमेतान्वशे कर्तुं शक्ष्यामो भरतर्षभ ॥१७॥
transliteration: punardīvyāma bhadraṁ te vanavāsāya pāṇḍavaiḥ ,evametānvaśe kartuṁ śakṣyāmo bharatarṣabha.
english: Good fortune to you! Let us play dice again with the Pāṇḍavas, with forest dwelling as the stake. In this way, O best of Bharatas, we will be able to bring them under our control.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 17. Good fortune to you! Let us play dice again with the Pāṇḍavas, with forest dwelling as the stake. In this way, O best of Bharatas, we will be able to bring them under our control.

sanskrit: ते वा द्वादश वर्षाणि वयं वा द्यूतनिर्जिताः ।प्रविशेम महारण्यमजिनैः प्रतिवासिताः ॥१८॥
transliteration: te vā dvādaśa varṣāṇi vayaṁ vā dyūtanirjitāḥ ,praviśema mahāraṇyamajinaiḥ prativāsitāḥ.
english: Either they or we, having been defeated in gambling, should enter the great forest for twelve years, clothed in deer skins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 18. Either they or we, having been defeated in gambling, should enter the great forest for twelve years, clothed in deer skins.

sanskrit: त्रयोदशं च सजने अज्ञाताः परिवत्सरम् ।ज्ञाताश्च पुनरन्यानि वने वर्षाणि द्वादश ॥१९॥
transliteration: trayodaśaṁ ca sajane ajñātāḥ parivatsaram ,jñātāśca punaranyāni vane varṣāṇi dvādaśa.
english: And the thirteenth year (must be spent) unrecognized in populated areas. If (they are) recognized, then again another twelve years in the forest (must be spent).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 19. And the thirteenth year (must be spent) unrecognized in populated areas. If (they are) recognized, then again another twelve years in the forest (must be spent).

sanskrit: निवसेम वयं ते वा तथा द्यूतं प्रवर्तताम् ।अक्षानुप्त्वा पुनर्द्यूतमिदं दीव्यन्तु पाण्डवाः ॥२०॥
transliteration: nivasema vayaṁ te vā tathā dyūtaṁ pravartatām ,akṣānuptvā punardyūtamidaṁ dīvyantu pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Let us (Kauravas) live (in peace), or let the dice game proceed in this manner: the Pāṇḍavas, having cast the dice, should play this game again.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 20. Let us (Kauravas) live (in peace), or let the dice game proceed in this manner: the Pāṇḍavas, having cast the dice, should play this game again.

sanskrit: एतत्कृत्यतमं राजन्नस्माकं भरतर्षभ ।अयं हि शकुनिर्वेद सविद्यामक्षसंपदम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: etatkṛtyatamaṁ rājannasmākaṁ bharatarṣabha ,ayaṁ hi śakunirveda savidyāmakṣasaṁpadam.
english: O King, O best among the Bhāratas, this (rematch) is most advisable for us. Indeed, this Śakuni knows the entire skill of dice-play.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 21. O King, O best among the Bhāratas, this (rematch) is most advisable for us. Indeed, this Śakuni knows the entire skill of dice-play.

sanskrit: दृढमूला वयं राज्ये मित्राणि परिगृह्य च ।सारवद्विपुलं सैन्यं सत्कृत्य च दुरासदम् ॥२२॥
transliteration: dṛḍhamūlā vayaṁ rājye mitrāṇi parigṛhya ca ,sāravadvipulaṁ sainyaṁ satkṛtya ca durāsadam.
english: We are firmly established in the kingdom, having secured our allies, and also having strengthened a substantial, vast, and unassailable army.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 22. We are firmly established in the kingdom, having secured our allies, and also having strengthened a substantial, vast, and unassailable army.

sanskrit: ते च त्रयोदशे वर्षे पारयिष्यन्ति चेद्व्रतम् ।जेष्यामस्तान्वयं राजन्रोचतां ते परंतप ॥२३॥
transliteration: te ca trayodaśe varṣe pārayiṣyanti cedvratam ,jeṣyāmastānvayaṁ rājanrocatāṁ te paraṁtapa.
english: And if they (the Pāṇḍavas) complete their vow in the thirteenth year, we will (still) conquer them, O King. Let this be agreeable to you, O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 23. And if they (the Pāṇḍavas) complete their vow in the thirteenth year, we will (still) conquer them, O King. Let this be agreeable to you, O tormentor of foes (paraṃtapa).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।तूर्णं प्रत्यानयस्वैतान्कामं व्यध्वगतानपि ।आगच्छन्तु पुनर्द्यूतमिदं कुर्वन्तु पाण्डवाः ॥२४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,tūrṇaṁ pratyānayasvaitānkāmaṁ vyadhvagatānapi ,āgacchantu punardyūtamidaṁ kurvantu pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "Quickly bring them back, even if they have gone far on their journey. Let the Pandavas come again and play this game of dice."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 24. Dhritarashtra said: "Quickly bring them back, even if they have gone far on their journey. Let the Pandavas come again and play this game of dice."

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो द्रोणः सोमदत्तो बाह्लीकश्च महारथः ।विदुरो द्रोणपुत्रश्च वैश्यापुत्रश्च वीर्यवान् ॥२५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato droṇaḥ somadatto bāhlīkaśca mahārathaḥ ,viduro droṇaputraśca vaiśyāputraśca vīryavān.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Then Drona, Somadatta, and the great charioteer (mahāratha) Bahlika, as well as Vidura, Drona's son (Aśvatthāman), and the powerful son of the Vaishya woman (Yuyutsu)"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 25. Vaishampayana said: "Then Drona, Somadatta, and the great charioteer (mahāratha) Bahlika, as well as Vidura, Drona's son (Aśvatthāman), and the powerful son of the Vaishya woman (Yuyutsu)"

sanskrit: भूरिश्रवाः शांतनवो विकर्णश्च महारथः ।मा द्यूतमित्यभाषन्त शमोऽस्त्विति च सर्वशः ॥२६॥
transliteration: bhūriśravāḥ śāṁtanavo vikarṇaśca mahārathaḥ ,mā dyūtamityabhāṣanta śamo'stviti ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Bhurishravas, the son of Shantanu (Bhishma), and Vikarna, the great charioteer (mahāratha), all of them said, "No more gambling (dyūta)! Let there be peace!"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 26. Bhurishravas, the son of Shantanu (Bhishma), and Vikarna, the great charioteer (mahāratha), all of them said, "No more gambling (dyūta)! Let there be peace!"

sanskrit: अकामानां च सर्वेषां सुहृदामर्थदर्शिनाम् ।अकरोत्पाण्डवाह्वानं धृतराष्ट्रः सुतप्रियः ॥२७॥
transliteration: akāmānāṁ ca sarveṣāṁ suhṛdāmarthadarśinām ,akarotpāṇḍavāhvānaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ sutapriyaḥ.
english: Despite the wishes of all his well-wishing friends, who could see the consequences, Dhritarashtra, who was fond of his sons, arranged for the summons of the Pandavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 27. Despite the wishes of all his well-wishing friends, who could see the consequences, Dhritarashtra, who was fond of his sons, arranged for the summons of the Pandavas.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीन्महाराज धृतराष्ट्रं जनेश्वरम् ।पुत्रहार्दाद्धर्मयुक्तं गान्धारी शोककर्शिता ॥२८॥
transliteration: athābravīnmahārāja dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janeśvaram ,putrahārdāddharmayuktaṁ gāndhārī śokakarśitā.
english: Then, O great king, Gandhari, emaciated by grief and moved by affection for her son, spoke to Dhritarashtra, the lord of the people, who was endowed with a sense of natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 28. Then, O great king, Gandhari, emaciated by grief and moved by affection for her son, spoke to Dhritarashtra, the lord of the people, who was endowed with a sense of natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: जाते दुर्योधने क्षत्ता महामतिरभाषत ।नीयतां परलोकाय साध्वयं कुलपांसनः ॥२९॥
transliteration: jāte duryodhane kṣattā mahāmatirabhāṣata ,nīyatāṁ paralokāya sādhvayaṁ kulapāṁsanaḥ.
english: When Duryodhana was born, Vidura, the greatly intelligent minister, spoke: 'Indeed, let this disgrace of the family be sent to the other world (i.e., be killed).'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 29. When Duryodhana was born, Vidura, the greatly intelligent minister, spoke: 'Indeed, let this disgrace of the family be sent to the other world (i.e., be killed).'

sanskrit: व्यनदज्जातमात्रो हि गोमायुरिव भारत ।अन्तो नूनं कुलस्यास्य कुरवस्तन्निबोधत ॥३०॥
transliteration: vyanadajjātamātro hi gomāyuriva bhārata ,anto nūnaṁ kulasyāsya kuravastannibodhata.
english: Indeed, as soon as he was born, he roared like a jackal, O descendant of Bharata! O Kurus, understand this well: certainly, this portends the end of this family.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 30. Indeed, as soon as he was born, he roared like a jackal, O descendant of Bharata! O Kurus, understand this well: certainly, this portends the end of this family.

sanskrit: मा बालानामशिष्टानामभिमंस्था मतिं प्रभो ।मा कुलस्य क्षये घोरे कारणं त्वं भविष्यसि ॥३१॥
transliteration: mā bālānāmaśiṣṭānāmabhimaṁsthā matiṁ prabho ,mā kulasya kṣaye ghore kāraṇaṁ tvaṁ bhaviṣyasi.
english: O lord, do not give credence to the advice of these immature and ill-behaved ones. May you not become the cause of this terrible destruction of the family.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 31. O lord, do not give credence to the advice of these immature and ill-behaved ones. May you not become the cause of this terrible destruction of the family.

sanskrit: बद्धं सेतुं को नु भिन्द्याद्धमेच्छान्तं च पावकम् ।शमे धृतान्पुनः पार्थान्कोपयेत्को नु भारत ॥३२॥
transliteration: baddhaṁ setuṁ ko nu bhindyāddhamecchāntaṁ ca pāvakam ,śame dhṛtānpunaḥ pārthānkopayetko nu bhārata.
english: Who, indeed, would break a built bridge? And who would fan a pacified fire? O Bhārata, who would again provoke the sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas) who are kept in peace?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 32. Who, indeed, would break a built bridge? And who would fan a pacified fire? O Bhārata, who would again provoke the sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas) who are kept in peace?

sanskrit: स्मरन्तं त्वामाजमीढ स्मारयिष्याम्यहं पुनः ।शास्त्रं न शास्ति दुर्बुद्धिं श्रेयसे वेतराय वा ॥३३॥
transliteration: smarantaṁ tvāmājamīḍha smārayiṣyāmyahaṁ punaḥ ,śāstraṁ na śāsti durbuddhiṁ śreyase vetarāya vā.
english: O Ājamīḍha, though you are remembering (what I said), I will remind you again. Scripture (śāstra) does not instruct a person with a foolish mind for their well-being (śreyas) or for anything else (its opposite).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 33. O Ājamīḍha, though you are remembering (what I said), I will remind you again. Scripture (śāstra) does not instruct a person with a foolish mind for their well-being (śreyas) or for anything else (its opposite).

sanskrit: न वै वृद्धो बालमतिर्भवेद्राजन्कथंचन ।त्वन्नेत्राः सन्तु ते पुत्रा मा त्वां दीर्णाः प्रहासिषुः ॥३४॥
transliteration: na vai vṛddho bālamatirbhavedrājankathaṁcana ,tvannetrāḥ santu te putrā mā tvāṁ dīrṇāḥ prahāsiṣuḥ.
english: O King (rājan), an old man should never, by any means, possess a childish mind. May your sons be guided by you, and may they not abandon you, torn apart by conflict.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 34. O King (rājan), an old man should never, by any means, possess a childish mind. May your sons be guided by you, and may they not abandon you, torn apart by conflict.

sanskrit: शमेन धर्मेण परस्य बुद्ध्या जाता बुद्धिः सास्तु ते मा प्रतीपा ।प्रध्वंसिनी क्रूरसमाहिता श्रीर्मृदुप्रौढा गच्छति पुत्रपौत्रान् ॥३५॥
transliteration: śamena dharmeṇa parasya buddhyā; jātā buddhiḥ sāstu te mā pratīpā ,pradhvaṁsinī krūrasamāhitā śrī;rmṛduprauḍhā gacchati putrapautrān.
english: May that understanding (buddhi) which arises from peace, righteousness (dharma), and the intellect of others be yours, and may it not be contrary (to good counsel). Prosperity (śrī) attained through cruel means is destructive, whereas prosperity (śrī) that is achieved with gentleness and firmness extends to one's sons and grandsons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 35. May that understanding (buddhi) which arises from peace, righteousness (dharma), and the intellect of others be yours, and may it not be contrary (to good counsel). Prosperity (śrī) attained through cruel means is destructive, whereas prosperity (śrī) that is achieved with gentleness and firmness extends to one's sons and grandsons.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीन्महाराजो गान्धारीं धर्मदर्शिनीम् ।अन्तः कामं कुलस्यास्तु न शक्ष्यामि निवारितुम् ॥३६॥
transliteration: athābravīnmahārājo gāndhārīṁ dharmadarśinīm ,antaḥ kāmaṁ kulasyāstu na śakṣyāmi nivāritum.
english: Then the great king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) spoke to Gāndhārī, who was discerning in righteousness (dharma): "Let the family's ruin certainly come to pass. I am unable to prevent it."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 36. Then the great king (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) spoke to Gāndhārī, who was discerning in righteousness (dharma): "Let the family's ruin certainly come to pass. I am unable to prevent it."

sanskrit: यथेच्छन्ति तथैवास्तु प्रत्यागच्छन्तु पाण्डवाः ।पुनर्द्यूतं प्रकुर्वन्तु मामकाः पाण्डवैः सह ॥३७॥
transliteration: yathecchanti tathaivāstu pratyāgacchantu pāṇḍavāḥ ,punardyūtaṁ prakurvantu māmakāḥ pāṇḍavaiḥ saha.
english: Let it be just as they desire. Let the Pāṇḍavas return, and let my sons play dice again with the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 66 Verse 37. Let it be just as they desire. Let the Pāṇḍavas return, and let my sons play dice again with the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।ततो व्यध्वगतं पार्थं प्रातिकामी युधिष्ठिरम् ।उवाच वचनाद्राज्ञो धृतराष्ट्रस्य धीमतः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tato vyadhvagataṁ pārthaṁ prātikāmī yudhiṣṭhiram ,uvāca vacanādrājño dhṛtarāṣṭrasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then Prātikāmī spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), who was on his journey, relaying the words of the wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Then Prātikāmī spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Pṛthā (Pārtha), who was on his journey, relaying the words of the wise King Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: उपस्तीर्णा सभा राजन्नक्षानुप्त्वा युधिष्ठिर ।एहि पाण्डव दीव्येति पिता त्वामाह भारत ॥२॥
transliteration: upastīrṇā sabhā rājannakṣānuptvā yudhiṣṭhira ,ehi pāṇḍava dīvyeti pitā tvāmāha bhārata.
english: O King Yudhiṣṭhira, the assembly hall is prepared, and the dice have been laid out. 'Come, O Pāṇḍava, play!' Thus your father (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) tells you, O Bhārata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 2. O King Yudhiṣṭhira, the assembly hall is prepared, and the dice have been laid out. 'Come, O Pāṇḍava, play!' Thus your father (Dhṛtarāṣṭra) tells you, O Bhārata.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।धातुर्नियोगाद्भूतानि प्राप्नुवन्ति शुभाशुभम् ।न निवृत्तिस्तयोरस्ति देवितव्यं पुनर्यदि ॥३॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,dhāturniyogādbhūtāni prāpnuvanti śubhāśubham ,na nivṛttistayorasti devitavyaṁ punaryadi.
english: Yudhishthira said: "By the ordinance of the Creator (dhātṛ), beings attain both auspicious and inauspicious outcomes. There is no cessation from these two if one must gamble again."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 3. Yudhishthira said: "By the ordinance of the Creator (dhātṛ), beings attain both auspicious and inauspicious outcomes. There is no cessation from these two if one must gamble again."

sanskrit: अक्षद्यूते समाह्वानं नियोगात्स्थविरस्य च ।जानन्नपि क्षयकरं नातिक्रमितुमुत्सहे ॥४॥
transliteration: akṣadyūte samāhvānaṁ niyogātsthavirasya ca ,jānannapi kṣayakaraṁ nātikramitumutsahe.
english: Even though I know it to be destructive, I am unable to transgress this summons to the dice game (akṣadyūta) and the command of the elder.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 4. Even though I know it to be destructive, I am unable to transgress this summons to the dice game (akṣadyūta) and the command of the elder.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इति ब्रुवन्निववृते भ्रातृभिः सह पाण्डवः ।जानंश्च शकुनेर्मायां पार्थो द्यूतमियात्पुनः ॥५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,iti bruvannivavṛte bhrātṛbhiḥ saha pāṇḍavaḥ ,jānaṁśca śakunermāyāṁ pārtho dyūtamiyātpunaḥ.
english: Vaishampayana said: "Saying this, the Pāṇḍava returned with his brothers. And knowing Shakuni's illusion (māyā), Pārtha again approached the dice game."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 5. Vaishampayana said: "Saying this, the Pāṇḍava returned with his brothers. And knowing Shakuni's illusion (māyā), Pārtha again approached the dice game."

sanskrit: विविशुस्ते सभां तां तु पुनरेव महारथाः ।व्यथयन्ति स्म चेतांसि सुहृदां भरतर्षभाः ॥६॥
transliteration: viviśuste sabhāṁ tāṁ tu punareva mahārathāḥ ,vyathayanti sma cetāṁsi suhṛdāṁ bharatarṣabhāḥ.
english: Those great warriors (mahāratha) again entered that assembly hall. And these foremost among the Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha) distressed the hearts of their well-wishers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 6. Those great warriors (mahāratha) again entered that assembly hall. And these foremost among the Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha) distressed the hearts of their well-wishers.

sanskrit: यथोपजोषमासीनाः पुनर्द्यूतप्रवृत्तये ।सर्वलोकविनाशाय दैवेनोपनिपीडिताः ॥७॥
transliteration: yathopajoṣamāsīnāḥ punardyūtapravṛttaye ,sarvalokavināśāya daivenopanipīḍitāḥ.
english: Comfortably seated, intent on gambling again, they were afflicted by destiny for the destruction of all people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 7. Comfortably seated, intent on gambling again, they were afflicted by destiny for the destruction of all people.

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।अमुञ्चत्स्थविरो यद्वो धनं पूजितमेव तत् ।महाधनं ग्लहं त्वेकं शृणु मे भरतर्षभ ॥८॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,amuñcatsthaviro yadvo dhanaṁ pūjitameva tat ,mahādhanaṁ glahaṁ tvekaṁ śṛṇu me bharatarṣabha.
english: Shakuni said, "The elder did not release your wealth; that was indeed honored. But listen to me, O bull among the Bharatas, about one great stake (glaha)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 8. Shakuni said, "The elder did not release your wealth; that was indeed honored. But listen to me, O bull among the Bharatas, about one great stake (glaha)."

sanskrit: वयं द्वादश वर्षाणि युष्माभिर्द्यूतनिर्जिताः ।प्रविशेम महारण्यं रौरवाजिनवाससः ॥९॥
transliteration: vayaṁ dvādaśa varṣāṇi yuṣmābhirdyūtanirjitāḥ ,praviśema mahāraṇyaṁ rauravājinavāsasaḥ.
english: Defeated by you in gambling, we should enter a great forest (mahāraṇya) for twelve years, wearing garments made of Ruru deer skin.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 9. Defeated by you in gambling, we should enter a great forest (mahāraṇya) for twelve years, wearing garments made of Ruru deer skin.

sanskrit: त्रयोदशं च सजने अज्ञाताः परिवत्सरम् ।ज्ञाताश्च पुनरन्यानि वने वर्षाणि द्वादश ॥१०॥
transliteration: trayodaśaṁ ca sajane ajñātāḥ parivatsaram ,jñātāśca punaranyāni vane varṣāṇi dvādaśa.
english: And for the thirteenth year, we shall live unrecognized among people for one year. If we are recognized, then we must spend another twelve years in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 10. And for the thirteenth year, we shall live unrecognized among people for one year. If we are recognized, then we must spend another twelve years in the forest.

sanskrit: अस्माभिर्वा जिता यूयं वने वर्षाणि द्वादश ।वसध्वं कृष्णया सार्धमजिनैः प्रतिवासिताः ॥११॥
transliteration: asmābhirvā jitā yūyaṁ vane varṣāṇi dvādaśa ,vasadhvaṁ kṛṣṇayā sārdhamajinaiḥ prativāsitāḥ.
english: Having been defeated by us, you must dwell in the forest for twelve years along with Krishnaa (Draupadi), clothed in deerskins.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 11. Having been defeated by us, you must dwell in the forest for twelve years along with Krishnaa (Draupadi), clothed in deerskins.

sanskrit: त्रयोदशे च निर्वृत्ते पुनरेव यथोचितम् ।स्वराज्यं प्रतिपत्तव्यमितरैरथ वेतरैः ॥१२॥
transliteration: trayodaśe ca nirvṛtte punareva yathocitam ,svarājyaṁ pratipattavyamitarairatha vetaraiḥ.
english: And when the thirteenth year is completed, your own kingdom should indeed be regained as is proper, either by you (Pandavas) or by us (Kauravas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 12. And when the thirteenth year is completed, your own kingdom should indeed be regained as is proper, either by you (Pandavas) or by us (Kauravas).

sanskrit: अनेन व्यवसायेन सहास्माभिर्युधिष्ठिर ।अक्षानुप्त्वा पुनर्द्यूतमेहि दीव्यस्व भारत ॥१३॥
transliteration: anena vyavasāyena sahāsmābhiryudhiṣṭhira ,akṣānuptvā punardyūtamehi dīvyasva bhārata.
english: O Yudhishthira, O Bharata, come, play the dice game again with us with this resolve, having thrown the dice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 13. O Yudhishthira, O Bharata, come, play the dice game again with us with this resolve, having thrown the dice.

sanskrit: सभासद ऊचुः ।अहो धिग्बान्धवा नैनं बोधयन्ति महद्भयम् ।बुद्ध्या बोध्यं न बुध्यन्ते स्वयं च भरतर्षभाः ॥१४॥
transliteration: sabhāsada ūcuḥ ,aho dhigbāndhavā nainaṁ bodhayanti mahadbhayam ,buddhyā bodhyaṁ na budhyante svayaṁ ca bharatarṣabhāḥ.
english: The members of the assembly said: 'Alas, shame! His relatives do not make him (Duryodhana) understand the great danger. And these chiefs among the Bharatas themselves do not comprehend through intelligence what ought to be understood.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 14. The members of the assembly said: 'Alas, shame! His relatives do not make him (Duryodhana) understand the great danger. And these chiefs among the Bharatas themselves do not comprehend through intelligence what ought to be understood.'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।जनप्रवादान्सुबहूनिति शृण्वन्नराधिपः ।ह्रिया च धर्मसङ्गाच्च पार्थो द्यूतमियात्पुनः ॥१५॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,janapravādānsubahūniti śṛṇvannarādhipaḥ ,hriyā ca dharmasaṅgācca pārtho dyūtamiyātpunaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing many such public rumors, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), out of shame and his adherence to his moral duty (dharma), would again go to the game of dice.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 15. Vaiśampāyana said: Hearing many such public rumors, the king (Yudhiṣṭhira), out of shame and his adherence to his moral duty (dharma), would again go to the game of dice.

sanskrit: जानन्नपि महाबुद्धिः पुनर्द्यूतमवर्तयत् ।अप्ययं न विनाशः स्यात्कुरूणामिति चिन्तयन् ॥१६॥
transliteration: jānannapi mahābuddhiḥ punardyūtamavartayat ,apyayaṁ na vināśaḥ syātkurūṇāmiti cintayan.
english: Even though he was greatly intelligent, he again engaged in the game of dice, thinking, "May this not be the destruction of the Kurus."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 16. Even though he was greatly intelligent, he again engaged in the game of dice, thinking, "May this not be the destruction of the Kurus."

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।कथं वै मद्विधो राजा स्वधर्ममनुपालयन् ।आहूतो विनिवर्तेत दीव्यामि शकुने त्वया ॥१७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,kathaṁ vai madvidho rājā svadharmamanupālayan ,āhūto vinivarteta dīvyāmi śakune tvayā.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira said: "Indeed, how could a king like me, upholding his own moral duty (dharma), refuse after being challenged? I will play dice with you, O Śakuni."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 17. Yudhiṣṭhira said: "Indeed, how could a king like me, upholding his own moral duty (dharma), refuse after being challenged? I will play dice with you, O Śakuni."

sanskrit: शकुनिरुवाच ।गवाश्वं बहुधेनूकमपर्यन्तमजाविकम् ।गजाः कोशो हिरण्यं च दासीदासं च सर्वशः ॥१८॥
transliteration: śakuniruvāca ,gavāśvaṁ bahudhenūkamaparyantamajāvikam ,gajāḥ kośo hiraṇyaṁ ca dāsīdāsaṁ ca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Śakuni said: "Cows and horses, many milch-cows, endless goats and sheep, elephants, a treasury, gold, and all types of male and female slaves."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 18. Śakuni said: "Cows and horses, many milch-cows, endless goats and sheep, elephants, a treasury, gold, and all types of male and female slaves."

sanskrit: एष नो ग्लह एवैको वनवासाय पाण्डवाः ।यूयं वयं वा विजिता वसेम वनमाश्रिताः ॥१९॥
transliteration: eṣa no glaha evaiko vanavāsāya pāṇḍavāḥ ,yūyaṁ vayaṁ vā vijitā vasema vanamāśritāḥ.
english: O Pāṇḍavas, this is our sole wager: for dwelling in the forest (vanavāsa). Whichever side, you or we, is defeated, that side shall reside, taking refuge in the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 19. O Pāṇḍavas, this is our sole wager: for dwelling in the forest (vanavāsa). Whichever side, you or we, is defeated, that side shall reside, taking refuge in the forest.

sanskrit: अनेन व्यवसायेन दीव्याम भरतर्षभ ।समुत्क्षेपेण चैकेन वनवासाय भारत ॥२०॥
transliteration: anena vyavasāyena dīvyāma bharatarṣabha ,samutkṣepeṇa caikena vanavāsāya bhārata.
english: O best of Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), let us gamble with this resolve, and, O Bhārata, for dwelling in the forest (vanavāsa) with just one throw (of the dice).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 20. O best of Bhāratas (bharatarṣabha), let us gamble with this resolve, and, O Bhārata, for dwelling in the forest (vanavāsa) with just one throw (of the dice).

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।प्रतिजग्राह तं पार्थो ग्लहं जग्राह सौबलः ।जितमित्येव शकुनिर्युधिष्ठिरमभाषत ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,pratijagrāha taṁ pārtho glahaṁ jagrāha saubalaḥ ,jitamityeva śakuniryudhiṣṭhiramabhāṣata.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira) accepted that wager. Saubala (Śakuni) then took the stake (glaha), and Śakuni indeed spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, 'It is won!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 67 Verse 21. Vaiśampāyana said: Pārtha (Yudhiṣṭhira) accepted that wager. Saubala (Śakuni) then took the stake (glaha), and Śakuni indeed spoke to Yudhiṣṭhira, saying, 'It is won!'

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।वनवासाय चक्रुस्ते मतिं पार्थाः पराजिताः ।अजिनान्युत्तरीयाणि जगृहुश्च यथाक्रमम् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vanavāsāya cakruste matiṁ pārthāḥ parājitāḥ ,ajinānyuttarīyāṇi jagṛhuśca yathākramam.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Those Pārthas, having been defeated, made the decision for dwelling in the forest (vanavāsa). And they took up their deer skins and upper garments in due order.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: Those Pārthas, having been defeated, made the decision for dwelling in the forest (vanavāsa). And they took up their deer skins and upper garments in due order.

sanskrit: अजिनैः संवृतान्दृष्ट्वा हृतराज्यानरिंदमान् ।प्रस्थितान्वनवासाय ततो दुःशासनोऽब्रवीत् ॥२॥
transliteration: ajinaiḥ saṁvṛtāndṛṣṭvā hṛtarājyānariṁdamān ,prasthitānvanavāsāya tato duḥśāsano'bravīt.
english: Then, Duḥśāsana spoke, seeing those subduers of enemies, whose kingdoms had been seized, clad in deerskins, and departed for forest exile.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 2. Then, Duḥśāsana spoke, seeing those subduers of enemies, whose kingdoms had been seized, clad in deerskins, and departed for forest exile.

sanskrit: प्रवृत्तं धार्तराष्ट्रस्य चक्रं राज्ञो महात्मनः ।पराभूताः पाण्डुपुत्रा विपत्तिं परमां गताः ॥३॥
transliteration: pravṛttaṁ dhārtarāṣṭrasya cakraṁ rājño mahātmanaḥ ,parābhūtāḥ pāṇḍuputrā vipattiṁ paramāṁ gatāḥ.
english: The power (cakra) of the great-souled King, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), has been set in motion. The sons of Pāṇḍu have been defeated and have fallen into the greatest misfortune.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 3. The power (cakra) of the great-souled King, the son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (Duryodhana), has been set in motion. The sons of Pāṇḍu have been defeated and have fallen into the greatest misfortune.

sanskrit: अद्य देवाः संप्रयाताः समैर्वर्त्मभिरस्थलैः ।गुणज्येष्ठास्तथा ज्येष्ठा भूयांसो यद्वयं परैः ॥४॥
transliteration: adya devāḥ saṁprayātāḥ samairvartmabhirasthalaiḥ ,guṇajyeṣṭhāstathā jyeṣṭhā bhūyāṁso yadvayaṁ paraiḥ.
english: Today, the gods have proceeded on even and unobstructed paths. This is because we are superior in qualities, indeed the most prominent, and more numerous than our adversaries.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 4. Today, the gods have proceeded on even and unobstructed paths. This is because we are superior in qualities, indeed the most prominent, and more numerous than our adversaries.

sanskrit: नरकं पातिताः पार्था दीर्घकालमनन्तकम् ।सुखाच्च हीना राज्याच्च विनष्टाः शाश्वतीः समाः ॥५॥
transliteration: narakaṁ pātitāḥ pārthā dīrghakālamanantakam ,sukhācca hīnā rājyācca vinaṣṭāḥ śāśvatīḥ samāḥ.
english: The Pāṇḍavas have been cast into an endless hell (naraka) for a long time. They are deprived of both happiness and kingdom, and are ruined for all eternity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 5. The Pāṇḍavas have been cast into an endless hell (naraka) for a long time. They are deprived of both happiness and kingdom, and are ruined for all eternity.

sanskrit: बलेन मत्ता ये ते स्म धार्तराष्ट्रान्प्रहासिषुः ।ते निर्जिता हृतधना वनमेष्यन्ति पाण्डवाः ॥६॥
transliteration: balena mattā ye te sma dhārtarāṣṭrānprahāsiṣuḥ ,te nirjitā hṛtadhanā vanameṣyanti pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: Those Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavāḥ) who were arrogant due to their strength and used to ridicule the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (dhārtarāṣṭrān) - they, having been defeated and deprived of their wealth, will now go to the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 6. Those Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavāḥ) who were arrogant due to their strength and used to ridicule the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra (dhārtarāṣṭrān) - they, having been defeated and deprived of their wealth, will now go to the forest.

sanskrit: चित्रान्संनाहानवमुञ्चन्तु चैषां वासांसि दिव्यानि च भानुमन्ति ।निवास्यन्तां रुरुचर्माणि सर्वे यथा ग्लहं सौबलस्याभ्युपेताः ॥७॥
transliteration: citrānsaṁnāhānavamuñcantu caiṣāṁ; vāsāṁsi divyāni ca bhānumanti ,nivāsyantāṁ rurucarmāṇi sarve; yathā glahaṁ saubalasyābhyupetāḥ.
english: Let them cast off their colorful armors and their splendid, shining garments. Let all of them be made to wear deer skins, just as they agreed to Śakuni's (saubalasya) wager.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 7. Let them cast off their colorful armors and their splendid, shining garments. Let all of them be made to wear deer skins, just as they agreed to Śakuni's (saubalasya) wager.

sanskrit: न सन्ति लोकेषु पुमांस ईदृशा इत्येव ये भावितबुद्धयः सदा ।ज्ञास्यन्ति तेऽऽत्मानमिमेऽद्य पाण्डवा विपर्यये षण्ढतिला इवाफलाः ॥८॥
transliteration: na santi lokeṣu pumāṁsa īdṛśā; ityeva ye bhāvitabuddhayaḥ sadā ,jñāsyanti te''tmānamime'dya pāṇḍavā; viparyaye ṣaṇḍhatilā ivāphalāḥ.
english: Those whose minds were always convinced, thinking, 'There are no such men in the world (loka),' these Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavāḥ) will now realize their true self (ātman) to be fruitless, like barren sesame seeds, in this adversity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 8. Those whose minds were always convinced, thinking, 'There are no such men in the world (loka),' these Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavāḥ) will now realize their true self (ātman) to be fruitless, like barren sesame seeds, in this adversity.

sanskrit: अयं हि वासोदय ईदृशानां मनस्विनां कौरव मा भवेद्वः ।अदीक्षितानामजिनानि यद्वद्बलीयसां पश्यत पाण्डवानाम् ॥९॥
transliteration: ayaṁ hi vāsodaya īdṛśānāṁ; manasvināṁ kaurava mā bhavedvaḥ ,adīkṣitānāmajināni yadva;dbalīyasāṁ paśyata pāṇḍavānām.
english: Indeed, O Kaurava (kaurava), may this situation - the wearing of such (simple) garments by high-minded men (manasvinām) - not befall you! Behold the powerful Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavānām), who are now (wearing) animal skins (ajināni) just like the uninitiated (adīkṣitānām)!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 9. Indeed, O Kaurava (kaurava), may this situation - the wearing of such (simple) garments by high-minded men (manasvinām) - not befall you! Behold the powerful Pāṇḍavas (pāṇḍavānām), who are now (wearing) animal skins (ajināni) just like the uninitiated (adīkṣitānām)!

sanskrit: महाप्राज्ञः सोमको यज्ञसेनः कन्यां पाञ्चालीं पाण्डवेभ्यः प्रदाय ।अकार्षीद्वै दुष्कृतं नेह सन्ति क्लीबाः पार्थाः पतयो याज्ञसेन्याः ॥१०॥
transliteration: mahāprājñaḥ somako yajñasenaḥ; kanyāṁ pāñcālīṁ pāṇḍavebhyaḥ pradāya ,akārṣīdvai duṣkṛtaṁ neha santi; klībāḥ pārthāḥ patayo yājñasenyāḥ.
english: The highly intelligent Somaka, Yajñasena, committed a wrong deed by giving his daughter Pāñcālī to the Pāṇḍavas. For Draupadī's (Yājñasenī's) husbands, the Pāṇḍavas, are certainly not impotent here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 10. The highly intelligent Somaka, Yajñasena, committed a wrong deed by giving his daughter Pāñcālī to the Pāṇḍavas. For Draupadī's (Yājñasenī's) husbands, the Pāṇḍavas, are certainly not impotent here.

sanskrit: सूक्ष्मान्प्रावारानजिनानि चोदितान्दृष्ट्वारण्ये निर्धनानप्रतिष्ठान् ।कां त्वं प्रीतिं लप्स्यसे याज्ञसेनि पतिं वृणीष्व यमिहान्यमिच्छसि ॥११॥
