transliteration: sūkṣmānprāvārānajināni coditā;ndṛṣṭvāraṇye nirdhanānapratiṣṭhān ,kāṁ tvaṁ prītiṁ lapsyase yājñaseni; patiṁ vṛṇīṣva yamihānyamicchasi.
english: O Draupadī (Yājñasenī), having seen the destitute and unsupported (Pāṇḍavas) in the forest, [who wear] fine garments and deerskins [as] prescribed, what satisfaction will you gain? Choose another husband, one whom you desire here.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 11. O Draupadī (Yājñasenī), having seen the destitute and unsupported (Pāṇḍavas) in the forest, [who wear] fine garments and deerskins [as] prescribed, what satisfaction will you gain? Choose another husband, one whom you desire here.

sanskrit: एते हि सर्वे कुरवः समेताः क्षान्ता दान्ताः सुद्रविणोपपन्नाः ।एषां वृणीष्वैकतमं पतित्वे न त्वां तपेत्कालविपर्ययोऽयम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: ete hi sarve kuravaḥ sametāḥ; kṣāntā dāntāḥ sudraviṇopapannāḥ ,eṣāṁ vṛṇīṣvaikatamaṁ patitve; na tvāṁ tapetkālaviparyayo'yam.
english: Indeed, all these assembled Kurus are patient, self-controlled, and endowed with great wealth. Choose any one of them as a husband. Then, this adverse turn of events will not torment you.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 12. Indeed, all these assembled Kurus are patient, self-controlled, and endowed with great wealth. Choose any one of them as a husband. Then, this adverse turn of events will not torment you.

sanskrit: यथाफलाः षण्ढतिला यथा चर्ममया मृगाः ।तथैव पाण्डवाः सर्वे यथा काकयवा अपि ॥१३॥
transliteration: yathāphalāḥ ṣaṇḍhatilā yathā carmamayā mṛgāḥ ,tathaiva pāṇḍavāḥ sarve yathā kākayavā api.
english: Just as barren sesame seeds are fruitless, and just as deer made of hide are worthless, so too are all the Pāṇḍavas, even like crow's barley.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 13. Just as barren sesame seeds are fruitless, and just as deer made of hide are worthless, so too are all the Pāṇḍavas, even like crow's barley.

sanskrit: किं पाण्डवांस्त्वं पतितानुपास्से मोघः श्रमः षण्ढतिलानुपास्य ।एवं नृशंसः परुषाणि पार्थानश्रावयद्धृतराष्ट्रस्य पुत्रः ॥१४॥
transliteration: kiṁ pāṇḍavāṁstvaṁ patitānupāsse; moghaḥ śramaḥ ṣaṇḍhatilānupāsya ,evaṁ nṛśaṁsaḥ paruṣāṇi pārthā;naśrāvayaddhṛtarāṣṭrasya putraḥ.
english: "Why do you attend to the fallen Pāṇḍavas? Your effort is in vain, like cultivating barren sesame seeds." Thus, the cruel son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra made the Pāṇḍavas listen to such harsh words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 14. "Why do you attend to the fallen Pāṇḍavas? Your effort is in vain, like cultivating barren sesame seeds." Thus, the cruel son of Dhṛtarāṣṭra made the Pāṇḍavas listen to such harsh words.

sanskrit: तद्वै श्रुत्वा भीमसेनोऽत्यमर्षी निर्भर्त्स्योच्चैस्तं निगृह्यैव रोषात् ।उवाचेदं सहसैवोपगम्य सिंहो यथा हैमवतः शृगालम् ॥१५॥
transliteration: tadvai śrutvā bhīmaseno'tyamarṣī; nirbhartsyoccaistaṁ nigṛhyaiva roṣāt ,uvācedaṁ sahasaivopagamya; siṁho yathā haimavataḥ śṛgālam.
english: Upon hearing that, the exceedingly wrathful Bhīmasena, having loudly rebuked and angrily seized him, quickly approached and spoke these words, just as a Himalayan lion (would speak to) a jackal.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 15. Upon hearing that, the exceedingly wrathful Bhīmasena, having loudly rebuked and angrily seized him, quickly approached and spoke these words, just as a Himalayan lion (would speak to) a jackal.

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।क्रूर पापजनैर्जुष्टमकृतार्थं प्रभाषसे ।गान्धारविद्यया हि त्वं राजमध्ये विकत्थसे ॥१६॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,krūra pāpajanairjuṣṭamakṛtārthaṁ prabhāṣase ,gāndhāravidyayā hi tvaṁ rājamadhye vikatthase.
english: Bhīmasena said: "O cruel one, you utter words that are meaningless and associated with wicked people. Indeed, you boast among kings by virtue of Gāndhārī's art."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 16. Bhīmasena said: "O cruel one, you utter words that are meaningless and associated with wicked people. Indeed, you boast among kings by virtue of Gāndhārī's art."

sanskrit: यथा तुदसि मर्माणि वाक्शरैरिह नो भृशम् ।तथा स्मारयिता तेऽहं कृन्तन्मर्माणि संयुगे ॥१७॥
transliteration: yathā tudasi marmāṇi vākśarairiha no bhṛśam ,tathā smārayitā te'haṁ kṛntanmarmāṇi saṁyuge.
english: "Just as you intensely pierce our vital parts here with your word-arrows, so I will make you remember (this) by cutting your vital parts in battle."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 17. "Just as you intensely pierce our vital parts here with your word-arrows, so I will make you remember (this) by cutting your vital parts in battle."

sanskrit: ये च त्वामनुवर्तन्ते कामलोभवशानुगाः ।गोप्तारः सानुबन्धांस्तान्नेष्यामि यमसादनम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ye ca tvāmanuvartante kāmalobhavaśānugāḥ ,goptāraḥ sānubandhāṁstānneṣyāmi yamasādanam.
english: And those who follow you, submitting to the sway of desire and greed, those protectors along with their kinsmen, I will lead them to the abode of Yama.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 18. And those who follow you, submitting to the sway of desire and greed, those protectors along with their kinsmen, I will lead them to the abode of Yama.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवं ब्रुवाणमजिनैर्विवासितं दुःखाभिभूतं परिनृत्यति स्म ।मध्ये कुरूणां धर्मनिबद्धमार्गं गौर्गौरिति स्माह्वयन्मुक्तलज्जः ॥१९॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evaṁ bruvāṇamajinairvivāsitaṁ; duḥkhābhibhūtaṁ parinṛtyati sma ,madhye kurūṇāṁ dharmanibaddhamārgaṁ; gaurgauriti smāhvayanmuktalajjaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: In the midst of the Kurus, one who was shameless, dancing around, called out 'Cow! Cow!' to him who was speaking thus, who had been stripped of his deer-skins, was overwhelmed by sorrow, and whose conduct was bound by natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 19. Vaiśampāyana said: In the midst of the Kurus, one who was shameless, dancing around, called out 'Cow! Cow!' to him who was speaking thus, who had been stripped of his deer-skins, was overwhelmed by sorrow, and whose conduct was bound by natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: भीमसेन उवाच ।नृशंसं परुषं क्रूरं शक्यं दुःशासन त्वया ।निकृत्या हि धनं लब्ध्वा को विकत्थितुमर्हति ॥२०॥
transliteration: bhīmasena uvāca ,nṛśaṁsaṁ paruṣaṁ krūraṁ śakyaṁ duḥśāsana tvayā ,nikṛtyā hi dhanaṁ labdhvā ko vikatthitumarhati.
english: Bhīmasena said: 'Such a cruel, harsh, and savage act is indeed possible for you, Duḥśāsana. For who, having obtained wealth through deceit, is worthy of boasting?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 20. Bhīmasena said: 'Such a cruel, harsh, and savage act is indeed possible for you, Duḥśāsana. For who, having obtained wealth through deceit, is worthy of boasting?'

sanskrit: मा ह स्म सुकृताँल्लोकान्गच्छेत्पार्थो वृकोदरः ।यदि वक्षसि भित्त्वा ते न पिबेच्छोणितं रणे ॥२१॥
transliteration: mā ha sma sukṛtāँllokāngacchetpārtho vṛkodaraḥ ,yadi vakṣasi bhittvā te na pibecchoṇitaṁ raṇe.
english: May I, Pārtha Vṛkodara (Bhīmasena), certainly not attain the worlds of good deeds, if I do not drink your blood in battle after tearing open your chest!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 21. May I, Pārtha Vṛkodara (Bhīmasena), certainly not attain the worlds of good deeds, if I do not drink your blood in battle after tearing open your chest!

sanskrit: धार्तराष्ट्रान्रणे हत्वा मिषतां सर्वधन्विनाम् ।शमं गन्तास्मि नचिरात्सत्यमेतद्ब्रवीमि वः ॥२२॥
transliteration: dhārtarāṣṭrānraṇe hatvā miṣatāṁ sarvadhanvinām ,śamaṁ gantāsmi nacirātsatyametadbravīmi vaḥ.
english: Having killed the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra in battle, while all the archers are watching, I shall very soon attain peace (śama). I speak this truth to you all.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 22. Having killed the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra in battle, while all the archers are watching, I shall very soon attain peace (śama). I speak this truth to you all.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्य राजा सिंहगतेः सखेलं दुर्योधनो भीमसेनस्य हर्षात् ।गतिं स्वगत्यानुचकार मन्दो निर्गच्छतां पाण्डवानां सभायाः ॥२३॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasya rājā siṁhagateḥ sakhelaṁ; duryodhano bhīmasenasya harṣāt ,gatiṁ svagatyānucakāra mando; nirgacchatāṁ pāṇḍavānāṁ sabhāyāḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: King Duryodhana, being dull-witted, joyfully and playfully imitated the lion-like gait of Bhīmasena with his own walk, as the Pāṇḍavas were departing from the assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 23. Vaiśampāyana said: King Duryodhana, being dull-witted, joyfully and playfully imitated the lion-like gait of Bhīmasena with his own walk, as the Pāṇḍavas were departing from the assembly.

sanskrit: नैतावता कृतमित्यब्रवीत्तं वृकोदरः संनिवृत्तार्धकायः ।शीघ्रं हि त्वा निहतं सानुबन्धं संस्मार्याहं प्रतिवक्ष्यामि मूढ ॥२४॥
transliteration: naitāvatā kṛtamityabravīttaṁ; vṛkodaraḥ saṁnivṛttārdhakāyaḥ ,śīghraṁ hi tvā nihataṁ sānubandhaṁ; saṁsmāryāhaṁ prativakṣyāmi mūḍha.
english: "This is not enough accomplished!" thus spoke Vṛkodara (Bhīma), with half his body turned back. "Indeed, O fool, I shall quickly respond to you after I have ensured that you and your kinsmen are slain."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 24. "This is not enough accomplished!" thus spoke Vṛkodara (Bhīma), with half his body turned back. "Indeed, O fool, I shall quickly respond to you after I have ensured that you and your kinsmen are slain."

sanskrit: एतत्समीक्ष्यात्मनि चावमानं नियम्य मन्युं बलवान्स मानी ।राजानुगः संसदि कौरवाणां विनिष्क्रमन्वाक्यमुवाच भीमः ॥२५॥
transliteration: etatsamīkṣyātmani cāvamānaṁ; niyamya manyuṁ balavānsa mānī ,rājānugaḥ saṁsadi kauravāṇāṁ; viniṣkramanvākyamuvāca bhīmaḥ.
english: Perceiving this insult to himself (ātman), the powerful and proud Bhīma, restraining his anger (manyu), then spoke words as he was departing from the assembly of the Kauravas, following the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 25. Perceiving this insult to himself (ātman), the powerful and proud Bhīma, restraining his anger (manyu), then spoke words as he was departing from the assembly of the Kauravas, following the king.

sanskrit: अहं दुर्योधनं हन्ता कर्णं हन्ता धनंजयः ।शकुनिं चाक्षकितवं सहदेवो हनिष्यति ॥२६॥
transliteration: ahaṁ duryodhanaṁ hantā karṇaṁ hantā dhanaṁjayaḥ ,śakuniṁ cākṣakitavaṁ sahadevo haniṣyati.
english: I will kill Duryodhana, Dhananjaya will kill Karna, and Sahadeva will kill Shakuni, the expert dice-player.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 26. I will kill Duryodhana, Dhananjaya will kill Karna, and Sahadeva will kill Shakuni, the expert dice-player.

sanskrit: इदं च भूयो वक्ष्यामि सभामध्ये बृहद्वचः ।सत्यं देवाः करिष्यन्ति यन्नो युद्धं भविष्यति ॥२७॥
transliteration: idaṁ ca bhūyo vakṣyāmi sabhāmadhye bṛhadvacaḥ ,satyaṁ devāḥ kariṣyanti yanno yuddhaṁ bhaviṣyati.
english: And I will declare this mighty statement again in this assembly: the gods will surely make true that our war will take place.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 27. And I will declare this mighty statement again in this assembly: the gods will surely make true that our war will take place.

sanskrit: सुयोधनमिमं पापं हन्तास्मि गदया युधि ।शिरः पादेन चास्याहमधिष्ठास्यामि भूतले ॥२८॥
transliteration: suyodhanamimaṁ pāpaṁ hantāsmi gadayā yudhi ,śiraḥ pādena cāsyāhamadhiṣṭhāsyāmi bhūtale.
english: I will kill this sinful Suyodhana in battle with my mace. And I will place my foot upon his head on the ground.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 28. I will kill this sinful Suyodhana in battle with my mace. And I will place my foot upon his head on the ground.

sanskrit: वाक्यशूरस्य चैवास्य परुषस्य दुरात्मनः ।दुःशासनस्य रुधिरं पातास्मि मृगराडिव ॥२९॥
transliteration: vākyaśūrasya caivāsya paruṣasya durātmanaḥ ,duḥśāsanasya rudhiraṁ pātāsmi mṛgarāḍiva.
english: Indeed, I will drink the blood of this Duhshasana - who is valorous only in words, harsh, and evil-minded - just like a king of beasts (mṛgarāṭ).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 29. Indeed, I will drink the blood of this Duhshasana - who is valorous only in words, harsh, and evil-minded - just like a king of beasts (mṛgarāṭ).

sanskrit: अर्जुन उवाच ।नैव वाचा व्यवसितं भीम विज्ञायते सताम् ।इतश्चतुर्दशे वर्षे द्रष्टारो यद्भविष्यति ॥३०॥
transliteration: arjuna uvāca ,naiva vācā vyavasitaṁ bhīma vijñāyate satām ,itaścaturdaśe varṣe draṣṭāro yadbhaviṣyati.
english: Arjuna said: "Bhima, the settled determination of virtuous people is not understood merely by their words. From now, in the fourteenth year, they will directly observe what will come to pass."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 30. Arjuna said: "Bhima, the settled determination of virtuous people is not understood merely by their words. From now, in the fourteenth year, they will directly observe what will come to pass."

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्य कर्णस्य शकुनेश्च दुरात्मनः ।दुःशासनचतुर्थानां भूमिः पास्यति शोणितम् ॥३१॥
transliteration: duryodhanasya karṇasya śakuneśca durātmanaḥ ,duḥśāsanacaturthānāṁ bhūmiḥ pāsyati śoṇitam.
english: The earth will drink the blood of Duryodhana, Karna, and the wicked (durātman) Śakuni, as well as of those for whom Duḥśāsana is the fourth.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 31. The earth will drink the blood of Duryodhana, Karna, and the wicked (durātman) Śakuni, as well as of those for whom Duḥśāsana is the fourth.

sanskrit: असूयितारं वक्तारं प्रस्रष्टारं दुरात्मनाम् ।भीमसेन नियोगात्ते हन्ताहं कर्णमाहवे ॥३२॥
transliteration: asūyitāraṁ vaktāraṁ prasraṣṭāraṁ durātmanām ,bhīmasena niyogātte hantāhaṁ karṇamāhave.
english: Bhimasena, by your command, I will slay Karna in battle  -  him who is envious, speaks harshly, and is an instigator of the wicked (durātman).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 32. Bhimasena, by your command, I will slay Karna in battle  -  him who is envious, speaks harshly, and is an instigator of the wicked (durātman).

sanskrit: अर्जुनः प्रतिजानीते भीमस्य प्रियकाम्यया ।कर्णं कर्णानुगांश्चैव रणे हन्तास्मि पत्रिभिः ॥३३॥
transliteration: arjunaḥ pratijānīte bhīmasya priyakāmyayā ,karṇaṁ karṇānugāṁścaiva raṇe hantāsmi patribhiḥ.
english: Arjuna pledges, desiring to please Bhima: "Indeed, I will slay Karna and his followers in battle with my arrows."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 33. Arjuna pledges, desiring to please Bhima: "Indeed, I will slay Karna and his followers in battle with my arrows."

sanskrit: ये चान्ये प्रतियोत्स्यन्ति बुद्धिमोहेन मां नृपाः ।तांश्च सर्वाञ्शितैर्बाणैर्नेतास्मि यमसादनम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: ye cānye pratiyotsyanti buddhimohena māṁ nṛpāḥ ,tāṁśca sarvāñśitairbāṇairnetāsmi yamasādanam.
english: And those other kings who will fight against me due to the delusion of their intellect, I will lead all of them to the abode of Yama with sharp arrows.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 34. And those other kings who will fight against me due to the delusion of their intellect, I will lead all of them to the abode of Yama with sharp arrows.

sanskrit: चलेद्धि हिमवान्स्थानान्निष्प्रभः स्याद्दिवाकरः ।शैत्यं सोमात्प्रणश्येत मत्सत्यं विचलेद्यदि ॥३५॥
transliteration: caleddhi himavānsthānānniṣprabhaḥ syāddivākaraḥ ,śaityaṁ somātpraṇaśyeta matsatyaṁ vicaledyadi.
english: Indeed, even if the Himalayas were to move from their place, the sun were to lose its radiance, and coldness were to vanish from the moon, my word (satyam) would still not waver.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 35. Indeed, even if the Himalayas were to move from their place, the sun were to lose its radiance, and coldness were to vanish from the moon, my word (satyam) would still not waver.

sanskrit: न प्रदास्यति चेद्राज्यमितो वर्षे चतुर्दशे ।दुर्योधनो हि सत्कृत्य सत्यमेतद्भविष्यति ॥३६॥
transliteration: na pradāsyati cedrājyamito varṣe caturdaśe ,duryodhano hi satkṛtya satyametadbhaviṣyati.
english: If Duryodhana does not yield the kingdom even after these fourteen years, then indeed, after (we have) acted honorably, this (vow/prediction) will come true.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 36. If Duryodhana does not yield the kingdom even after these fourteen years, then indeed, after (we have) acted honorably, this (vow/prediction) will come true.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।इत्युक्तवति पार्थे तु श्रीमान्माद्रवतीसुतः ।प्रगृह्य विपुलं बाहुं सहदेवः प्रतापवान् ॥३७॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,ityuktavati pārthe tu śrīmānmādravatīsutaḥ ,pragṛhya vipulaṁ bāhuṁ sahadevaḥ pratāpavān.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When Pārtha (Arjuna) had thus spoken, the glorious son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), full of valor, grasped his mighty arm.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 37. Vaiśampāyana said: When Pārtha (Arjuna) had thus spoken, the glorious son of Mādrī (Sahadeva), full of valor, grasped his mighty arm.

sanskrit: सौबलस्य वधं प्रेप्सुरिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ।क्रोधसंरक्तनयनो निःश्वसन्निव पन्नगः ॥३८॥
transliteration: saubalasya vadhaṁ prepsuridaṁ vacanamabravīt ,krodhasaṁraktanayano niḥśvasanniva pannagaḥ.
english: Desiring Saubala's death, he spoke these words, his eyes red with rage, hissing like a serpent.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 38. Desiring Saubala's death, he spoke these words, his eyes red with rage, hissing like a serpent.

sanskrit: अक्षान्यान्मन्यसे मूढ गान्धाराणां यशोहर ।नैतेऽक्षा निशिता बाणास्त्वयैते समरे वृताः ॥३९॥
transliteration: akṣānyānmanyase mūḍha gāndhārāṇāṁ yaśohara ,naite'kṣā niśitā bāṇāstvayaite samare vṛtāḥ.
english: O foolish one, who steals the glory of the Gāndhāras, you consider these to be mere dice. These are not dice; rather, they are sharpened arrows, chosen by you for battle!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 39. O foolish one, who steals the glory of the Gāndhāras, you consider these to be mere dice. These are not dice; rather, they are sharpened arrows, chosen by you for battle!

sanskrit: यथा चैवोक्तवान्भीमस्त्वामुद्दिश्य सबान्धवम् ।कर्ताहं कर्मणस्तस्य कुरु कार्याणि सर्वशः ॥४०॥
transliteration: yathā caivoktavānbhīmastvāmuddiśya sabāndhavam ,kartāhaṁ karmaṇastasya kuru kāryāṇi sarvaśaḥ.
english: Just as Bhīma spoke, addressing you and your kinsmen, saying, "I will be the doer of that (terrible) deed (karma)," now you should perform all your tasks completely (sarvaśaḥ).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 40. Just as Bhīma spoke, addressing you and your kinsmen, saying, "I will be the doer of that (terrible) deed (karma)," now you should perform all your tasks completely (sarvaśaḥ).

sanskrit: हन्तास्मि तरसा युद्धे त्वां विक्रम्य सबान्धवम् ।यदि स्थास्यसि संग्रामे क्षत्रधर्मेण सौबल ॥४१॥
transliteration: hantāsmi tarasā yuddhe tvāṁ vikramya sabāndhavam ,yadi sthāsyasi saṁgrāme kṣatradharmeṇa saubala.
english: O Saubala, I will surely kill you and your kinsmen with force in battle, after having shown valor, if you dare to stand in combat according to the code of a warrior (kṣatra-dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 41. O Saubala, I will surely kill you and your kinsmen with force in battle, after having shown valor, if you dare to stand in combat according to the code of a warrior (kṣatra-dharma).

sanskrit: सहदेववचः श्रुत्वा नकुलोऽपि विशां पते ।दर्शनीयतमो नॄणामिदं वचनमब्रवीत् ॥४२॥
transliteration: sahadevavacaḥ śrutvā nakulo'pi viśāṁ pate ,darśanīyatamo nṝṇāmidaṁ vacanamabravīt.
english: O lord of the people, having heard Sahadeva's words, Nakula, the most handsome among men, also spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 42. O lord of the people, having heard Sahadeva's words, Nakula, the most handsome among men, also spoke these words.

sanskrit: सुतेयं यज्ञसेनस्य द्यूतेऽस्मिन्धृतराष्ट्रजैः ।यैर्वाचः श्राविता रूक्षाः स्थितैर्दुर्योधनप्रिये ॥४३॥
transliteration: suteyaṁ yajñasenasya dyūte'smindhṛtarāṣṭrajaiḥ ,yairvācaḥ śrāvitā rūkṣāḥ sthitairduryodhanapriye.
english: In this game of dice, which was dear to Duryodhana, this daughter of Drupada (Yajñasena) was made to hear harsh words by the sons of Dhritarashtra, who stood there (uttering them).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 43. In this game of dice, which was dear to Duryodhana, this daughter of Drupada (Yajñasena) was made to hear harsh words by the sons of Dhritarashtra, who stood there (uttering them).

sanskrit: तान्धार्तराष्ट्रान्दुर्वृत्तान्मुमूर्षून्कालचोदितान् ।दर्शयिष्यामि भूयिष्ठमहं वैवस्वतक्षयम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: tāndhārtarāṣṭrāndurvṛttānmumūrṣūnkālacoditān ,darśayiṣyāmi bhūyiṣṭhamahaṁ vaivasvatakṣayam.
english: I will show those wicked sons of Dhritarashtra, who are doomed to die and impelled by time (fate), the ultimate realm of Yama (Vaivasvata).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 44. I will show those wicked sons of Dhritarashtra, who are doomed to die and impelled by time (fate), the ultimate realm of Yama (Vaivasvata).

sanskrit: निदेशाद्धर्मराजस्य द्रौपद्याः पदवीं चरन् ।निर्धार्तराष्ट्रां पृथिवीं कर्तास्मि नचिरादिव ॥४५॥
transliteration: nideśāddharmarājasya draupadyāḥ padavīṁ caran ,nirdhārtarāṣṭrāṁ pṛthivīṁ kartāsmi nacirādiva.
english: Following Draupadi's path (seeking vengeance for her), by the command of the king of natural law (dharma), Yudhishthira, I will very soon make the earth devoid of Dhritarashtra's sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 45. Following Draupadi's path (seeking vengeance for her), by the command of the king of natural law (dharma), Yudhishthira, I will very soon make the earth devoid of Dhritarashtra's sons.

sanskrit: एवं ते पुरुषव्याघ्राः सर्वे व्यायतबाहवः ।प्रतिज्ञा बहुलाः कृत्वा धृतराष्ट्रमुपागमन् ॥४६॥
transliteration: evaṁ te puruṣavyāghrāḥ sarve vyāyatabāhavaḥ ,pratijñā bahulāḥ kṛtvā dhṛtarāṣṭramupāgaman.
english: Thus, all those best of men (puruṣavyāghra), with mighty arms, having made many vows, approached Dhritarashtra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 68 Verse 46. Thus, all those best of men (puruṣavyāghra), with mighty arms, having made many vows, approached Dhritarashtra.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर उवाच ।आमन्त्रयामि भरतांस्तथा वृद्धं पितामहम् ।राजानं सोमदत्तं च महाराजं च बाह्लिकम् ॥१॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira uvāca ,āmantrayāmi bharatāṁstathā vṛddhaṁ pitāmaham ,rājānaṁ somadattaṁ ca mahārājaṁ ca bāhlikam.
english: Yudhishthira said: 'I am inviting the Bharatas, as well as the aged grandfather, King Somadatta, and the great king Bahlika.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 1. Yudhishthira said: 'I am inviting the Bharatas, as well as the aged grandfather, King Somadatta, and the great king Bahlika.'

sanskrit: द्रोणं कृपं नृपांश्चान्यानश्वत्थामानमेव च ।विदुरं धृतराष्ट्रं च धार्तराष्ट्रांश्च सर्वशः ॥२॥
transliteration: droṇaṁ kṛpaṁ nṛpāṁścānyānaśvatthāmānameva ca ,viduraṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ ca dhārtarāṣṭrāṁśca sarvaśaḥ.
english: Drona, Kripa, and other kings, and indeed Ashwatthama, Vidura, Dhritarashtra, and all the Dhritarashtras (sons of Dhritarashtra).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 2. Drona, Kripa, and other kings, and indeed Ashwatthama, Vidura, Dhritarashtra, and all the Dhritarashtras (sons of Dhritarashtra).

sanskrit: युयुत्सुं संजयं चैव तथैवान्यान्सभासदः ।सर्वानामन्त्र्य गच्छामि द्रष्टास्मि पुनरेत्य वः ॥३॥
transliteration: yuyutsuṁ saṁjayaṁ caiva tathaivānyānsabhāsadaḥ ,sarvānāmantrya gacchāmi draṣṭāsmi punaretya vaḥ.
english: Yuyutsu, Sanjaya, and similarly, all other members of the assembly. Having addressed them all, I depart; I shall see you again upon my return.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 3. Yuyutsu, Sanjaya, and similarly, all other members of the assembly. Having addressed them all, I depart; I shall see you again upon my return.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।न च किंचित्तदोचुस्ते ह्रिया सन्तो युधिष्ठिरम् ।मनोभिरेव कल्याणं दध्युस्ते तस्य धीमतः ॥४॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,na ca kiṁcittadocuste hriyā santo yudhiṣṭhiram ,manobhireva kalyāṇaṁ dadhyuste tasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Ashamed, they said nothing to Yudhiṣṭhira then. They only thought of the well-being of that wise one in their minds.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 4. Vaiśampāyana said: Ashamed, they said nothing to Yudhiṣṭhira then. They only thought of the well-being of that wise one in their minds.

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।आर्या पृथा राजपुत्री नारण्यं गन्तुमर्हति ।सुकुमारी च वृद्धा च नित्यं चैव सुखोचिता ॥५॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,āryā pṛthā rājaputrī nāraṇyaṁ gantumarhati ,sukumārī ca vṛddhā ca nityaṁ caiva sukhocitā.
english: Vidura said: The venerable Pṛthā, the princess, should not go to the forest. She is delicate and aged, and always accustomed to comfort.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 5. Vidura said: The venerable Pṛthā, the princess, should not go to the forest. She is delicate and aged, and always accustomed to comfort.

sanskrit: इह वत्स्यति कल्याणी सत्कृता मम वेश्मनि ।इति पार्था विजानीध्वमगदं वोऽस्तु सर्वशः ॥६॥
transliteration: iha vatsyati kalyāṇī satkṛtā mama veśmani ,iti pārthā vijānīdhvamagadaṁ vo'stu sarvaśaḥ.
english: The auspicious one will dwell here, honored in my house. O sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas), know this. May you all be completely free from harm.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 6. The auspicious one will dwell here, honored in my house. O sons of Pṛthā (Pārthas), know this. May you all be completely free from harm.

sanskrit: युधिष्ठिर विजानीहि ममेदं भरतर्षभ ।नाधर्मेण जितः कश्चिद्व्यथते वै पराजयात् ॥७॥
transliteration: yudhiṣṭhira vijānīhi mamedaṁ bharatarṣabha ,nādharmeṇa jitaḥ kaścidvyathate vai parājayāt.
english: Yudhiṣṭhira, O best of Bharatas, understand this, which is my view: no one who has been conquered by unrighteousness (adharma) is troubled by such a defeat.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 7. Yudhiṣṭhira, O best of Bharatas, understand this, which is my view: no one who has been conquered by unrighteousness (adharma) is troubled by such a defeat.

sanskrit: त्वं वै धर्मान्विजानीषे युधां वेत्ता धनंजयः ।हन्तारीणां भीमसेनो नकुलस्त्वर्थसंग्रही ॥८॥
transliteration: tvaṁ vai dharmānvijānīṣe yudhāṁ vettā dhanaṁjayaḥ ,hantārīṇāṁ bhīmaseno nakulastvarthasaṁgrahī.
english: You indeed understand the principles (dharma). Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) is a master of warfare. Bhīmasena is the destroyer of enemies, while Nakula is an accumulator of resources.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 8. You indeed understand the principles (dharma). Dhanañjaya (Arjuna) is a master of warfare. Bhīmasena is the destroyer of enemies, while Nakula is an accumulator of resources.

sanskrit: संयन्ता सहदेवस्तु धौम्यो ब्रह्मविदुत्तमः ।धर्मार्थकुशला चैव द्रौपदी धर्मचारिणी ॥९॥
transliteration: saṁyantā sahadevastu dhaumyo brahmaviduttamaḥ ,dharmārthakuśalā caiva draupadī dharmacāriṇī.
english: Sahadeva, however, is a master of self-control. Dhaumya is the foremost knower of the ultimate reality (brahman). And Draupadī, who observes her (dharma), is skilled in both righteousness (dharma) and material prosperity.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 9. Sahadeva, however, is a master of self-control. Dhaumya is the foremost knower of the ultimate reality (brahman). And Draupadī, who observes her (dharma), is skilled in both righteousness (dharma) and material prosperity.

sanskrit: अन्योन्यस्य प्रियाः सर्वे तथैव प्रियवादिनः ।परैरभेद्याः संतुष्टाः को वो न स्पृहयेदिह ॥१०॥
transliteration: anyonyasya priyāḥ sarve tathaiva priyavādinaḥ ,parairabhedyāḥ saṁtuṣṭāḥ ko vo na spṛhayediha.
english: All of them are dear to one another, and likewise, they speak kindly. They are impenetrable to others and are content. Who here would not envy them?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 10. All of them are dear to one another, and likewise, they speak kindly. They are impenetrable to others and are content. Who here would not envy them?

sanskrit: एष वै सर्वकल्याणः समाधिस्तव भारत ।नैनं शत्रुर्विषहते शक्रेणापि समोऽच्युत ॥११॥
transliteration: eṣa vai sarvakalyāṇaḥ samādhistava bhārata ,nainaṁ śatrurviṣahate śakreṇāpi samo'cyuta.
english: O Bhārata (Yudhiṣṭhira), this entire situation is indeed auspicious, as is your profound composure (samādhi). No enemy can overpower it, not even one equal to Indra, O Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 11. O Bhārata (Yudhiṣṭhira), this entire situation is indeed auspicious, as is your profound composure (samādhi). No enemy can overpower it, not even one equal to Indra, O Acyuta (Kṛṣṇa).

sanskrit: हिमवत्यनुशिष्टोऽसि मेरुसावर्णिना पुरा ।द्वैपायनेन कृष्णेन नगरे वारणावते ॥१२॥
transliteration: himavatyanuśiṣṭo'si merusāvarṇinā purā ,dvaipāyanena kṛṣṇena nagare vāraṇāvate.
english: You were instructed in the Himalayas by Merusāvarṇin in the past, and by Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana in the city of Vāraṇāvata.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 12. You were instructed in the Himalayas by Merusāvarṇin in the past, and by Kṛṣṇa Dvaipāyana in the city of Vāraṇāvata.

sanskrit: भृगुतुङ्गे च रामेण दृषद्वत्यां च शंभुना ।अश्रौषीरसितस्यापि महर्षेरञ्जनं प्रति ॥१३॥
transliteration: bhṛgutuṅge ca rāmeṇa dṛṣadvatyāṁ ca śaṁbhunā ,aśrauṣīrasitasyāpi maharṣerañjanaṁ prati.
english: You were also instructed at Bhṛgutuṅga by Rāma and at Dṛṣadvatī by Śambhu. Moreover, you heard the teaching of the great sage Asita concerning Añjana.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 13. You were also instructed at Bhṛgutuṅga by Rāma and at Dṛṣadvatī by Śambhu. Moreover, you heard the teaching of the great sage Asita concerning Añjana.

sanskrit: द्रष्टा सदा नारदस्य धौम्यस्तेऽयं पुरोहितः ।मा हार्षीः सांपराये त्वं बुद्धिं तामृषिपूजिताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: draṣṭā sadā nāradasya dhaumyaste'yaṁ purohitaḥ ,mā hārṣīḥ sāṁparāye tvaṁ buddhiṁ tāmṛṣipūjitām.
english: This Dhaumya, your family priest (purohita), always communes with Nārada. Do not lose that intellect (buddhi), revered by sages, in this critical juncture (sāmparāya).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 14. This Dhaumya, your family priest (purohita), always communes with Nārada. Do not lose that intellect (buddhi), revered by sages, in this critical juncture (sāmparāya).

sanskrit: पुरूरवसमैलं त्वं बुद्ध्या जयसि पाण्डव ।शक्त्या जयसि राज्ञोऽन्यानृषीन्धर्मोपसेवया ॥१५॥
transliteration: purūravasamailaṁ tvaṁ buddhyā jayasi pāṇḍava ,śaktyā jayasi rājño'nyānṛṣīndharmopasevayā.
english: O Pāṇḍava, you surpass Purūravas, the son of Ilā, through your intellect (buddhi). You overcome other kings by your might (śakti) and sages through your adherence to natural law (dharma).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 15. O Pāṇḍava, you surpass Purūravas, the son of Ilā, through your intellect (buddhi). You overcome other kings by your might (śakti) and sages through your adherence to natural law (dharma).

sanskrit: ऐन्द्रे जये धृतमना याम्ये कोपविधारणे ।विसर्गे चैव कौबेरे वारुणे चैव संयमे ॥१६॥
transliteration: aindre jaye dhṛtamanā yāmye kopavidhāraṇe ,visarge caiva kaubere vāruṇe caiva saṁyame.
english: One's mind should be fixed on victory (jaya) in the manner of Indra, on restraining anger in the manner of Yama, on generosity in the manner of Kubera, and also on self-control (saṃyama) in the manner of Varuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 16. One's mind should be fixed on victory (jaya) in the manner of Indra, on restraining anger in the manner of Yama, on generosity in the manner of Kubera, and also on self-control (saṃyama) in the manner of Varuna.

sanskrit: आत्मप्रदानं सौम्यत्वमद्भ्यश्चैवोपजीवनम् ।भूमेः क्षमा च तेजश्च समग्रं सूर्यमण्डलात् ॥१७॥
transliteration: ātmapradānaṁ saumyatvamadbhyaścaivopajīvanam ,bhūmeḥ kṣamā ca tejaśca samagraṁ sūryamaṇḍalāt.
english: Self-giving (ātmapradāna), gentleness (saumyatva), and sustenance (upajīvana) [should be derived] from the waters (adbhyaḥ); patience (kṣamā) from the earth (bhūmi); and complete splendor (tejas) from the sun's disk (sūryamaṇḍala).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 17. Self-giving (ātmapradāna), gentleness (saumyatva), and sustenance (upajīvana) [should be derived] from the waters (adbhyaḥ); patience (kṣamā) from the earth (bhūmi); and complete splendor (tejas) from the sun's disk (sūryamaṇḍala).

sanskrit: वायोर्बलं विद्धि स त्वं भूतेभ्यश्चात्मसंभवम् ।अगदं वोऽस्तु भद्रं वो द्रक्ष्यामि पुनरागतान् ॥१८॥
transliteration: vāyorbalaṁ viddhi sa tvaṁ bhūtebhyaścātmasaṁbhavam ,agadaṁ vo'stu bhadraṁ vo drakṣyāmi punarāgatān.
english: Know, O you, the strength (bala) of the wind (vāyu) and self-origination (ātmasaṃbhava) from all beings (bhūta). May you be free from illness; may good fortune be yours; I shall see you again upon your return.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 18. Know, O you, the strength (bala) of the wind (vāyu) and self-origination (ātmasaṃbhava) from all beings (bhūta). May you be free from illness; may good fortune be yours; I shall see you again upon your return.

sanskrit: आपद्धर्मार्थकृच्छ्रेषु सर्वकार्येषु वा पुनः ।यथावत्प्रतिपद्येथाः काले काले युधिष्ठिर ॥१९॥
transliteration: āpaddharmārthakṛcchreṣu sarvakāryeṣu vā punaḥ ,yathāvatpratipadyethāḥ kāle kāle yudhiṣṭhira.
english: O Yudhishthira, in times of calamity, in difficulties concerning natural law (dharma) and wealth (artha), or indeed in all matters, you should always act appropriately.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 19. O Yudhishthira, in times of calamity, in difficulties concerning natural law (dharma) and wealth (artha), or indeed in all matters, you should always act appropriately.

sanskrit: आपृष्टोऽसीह कौन्तेय स्वस्ति प्राप्नुहि भारत ।कृतार्थं स्वस्तिमन्तं त्वां द्रक्ष्यामः पुनरागतम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: āpṛṣṭo'sīha kaunteya svasti prāpnuhi bhārata ,kṛtārthaṁ svastimantaṁ tvāṁ drakṣyāmaḥ punarāgatam.
english: You have been questioned here, O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata); may you attain well-being. We shall see you again when you return, having achieved your objective and being prosperous.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 20. You have been questioned here, O son of Kunti (Kaunteya), O descendant of Bharata (Bhārata); may you attain well-being. We shall see you again when you return, having achieved your objective and being prosperous.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।एवमुक्तस्तथेत्युक्त्वा पाण्डवः सत्यविक्रमः ।भीष्मद्रोणौ नमस्कृत्य प्रातिष्ठत युधिष्ठिरः ॥२१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,evamuktastathetyuktvā pāṇḍavaḥ satyavikramaḥ ,bhīṣmadroṇau namaskṛtya prātiṣṭhata yudhiṣṭhiraḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the Pāṇḍava known for his true valor, saying 'so be it,' bowed to Bhīṣma and Droṇa and then departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 69 Verse 21. Vaiśampāyana said: Thus addressed, Yudhiṣṭhira, the Pāṇḍava known for his true valor, saying 'so be it,' bowed to Bhīṣma and Droṇa and then departed.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।तस्मिन्संप्रस्थिते कृष्णा पृथां प्राप्य यशस्विनीम् ।आपृच्छद्भृशदुःखार्ता याश्चान्यास्तत्र योषितः ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,tasminsaṁprasthite kṛṣṇā pṛthāṁ prāpya yaśasvinīm ,āpṛcchadbhṛśaduḥkhārtā yāścānyāstatra yoṣitaḥ.
english: Vaiśampāyana said: When he (Yudhiṣṭhira) had departed, Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā), deeply afflicted by sorrow, approached the glorious Pṛthā (Kuntī) and bid farewell to her and to the other women who were present there.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 1. Vaiśampāyana said: When he (Yudhiṣṭhira) had departed, Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā), deeply afflicted by sorrow, approached the glorious Pṛthā (Kuntī) and bid farewell to her and to the other women who were present there.

sanskrit: यथार्हं वन्दनाश्लेषान्कृत्वा गन्तुमियेष सा ।ततो निनादः सुमहान्पाण्डवान्तःपुरेऽभवत् ॥२॥
transliteration: yathārhaṁ vandanāśleṣānkṛtvā gantumiyeṣa sā ,tato ninādaḥ sumahānpāṇḍavāntaḥpure'bhavat.
english: After offering appropriate salutations and embraces, she (Draupadī) wished to depart. Then, a very great lamentation arose within the Pāṇḍavas' inner chambers (antaḥpura).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 2. After offering appropriate salutations and embraces, she (Draupadī) wished to depart. Then, a very great lamentation arose within the Pāṇḍavas' inner chambers (antaḥpura).

sanskrit: कुन्ती च भृशसंतप्ता द्रौपदीं प्रेक्ष्य गच्छतीम् ।शोकविह्वलया वाचा कृच्छ्राद्वचनमब्रवीत् ॥३॥
transliteration: kuntī ca bhṛśasaṁtaptā draupadīṁ prekṣya gacchatīm ,śokavihvalayā vācā kṛcchrādvacanamabravīt.
english: Greatly distressed, Kunti, seeing Draupadi departing, spoke with a voice choked by sorrow, uttering words with great difficulty.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 3. Greatly distressed, Kunti, seeing Draupadi departing, spoke with a voice choked by sorrow, uttering words with great difficulty.

sanskrit: वत्से शोको न ते कार्यः प्राप्येदं व्यसनं महत् ।स्त्रीधर्माणामभिज्ञासि शीलाचारवती तथा ॥४॥
transliteration: vatse śoko na te kāryaḥ prāpyedaṁ vyasanaṁ mahat ,strīdharmāṇāmabhijñāsi śīlācāravatī tathā.
english: O dear daughter, you should not give in to sorrow (śoka) despite having encountered this great calamity. You are indeed knowledgeable of the natural laws (dharma) of women and possess excellent conduct and character.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 4. O dear daughter, you should not give in to sorrow (śoka) despite having encountered this great calamity. You are indeed knowledgeable of the natural laws (dharma) of women and possess excellent conduct and character.

sanskrit: न त्वां संदेष्टुमर्हामि भर्तॄन्प्रति शुचिस्मिते ।साध्वीगुणसमाधानैर्भूषितं ते कुलद्वयम् ॥५॥
transliteration: na tvāṁ saṁdeṣṭumarhāmi bhartṝnprati śucismite ,sādhvīguṇasamādhānairbhūṣitaṁ te kuladvayam.
english: O pure-smiling one, I am not fit to instruct you regarding your husbands. Indeed, your two families (both paternal and marital) are adorned by your possession of the virtues of a chaste and devoted wife.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 5. O pure-smiling one, I am not fit to instruct you regarding your husbands. Indeed, your two families (both paternal and marital) are adorned by your possession of the virtues of a chaste and devoted wife.

sanskrit: सभाग्याः कुरवश्चेमे ये न दग्धास्त्वयानघे ।अरिष्टं व्रज पन्थानं मदनुध्यानबृंहिता ॥६॥
transliteration: sabhāgyāḥ kuravaśceme ye na dagdhāstvayānaghe ,ariṣṭaṁ vraja panthānaṁ madanudhyānabṛṁhitā.
english: O sinless one, fortunate are these Kurus, who were not consumed (burnt) by your wrath. Go forth on your journey unharmed, strengthened by my constant contemplation (dhyāna) and good wishes.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 6. O sinless one, fortunate are these Kurus, who were not consumed (burnt) by your wrath. Go forth on your journey unharmed, strengthened by my constant contemplation (dhyāna) and good wishes.

sanskrit: भाविन्यर्थे हि सत्स्त्रीणां वैक्लव्यं नोपजायते ।गुरुधर्माभिगुप्ता च श्रेयः क्षिप्रमवाप्स्यसि ॥७॥
transliteration: bhāvinyarthe hi satstrīṇāṁ vaiklavyaṁ nopajāyate ,gurudharmābhiguptā ca śreyaḥ kṣipramavāpsyasi.
english: Indeed, virtuous women do not experience agitation regarding future matters. And you, protected by weighty natural law (dharma), will swiftly attain ultimate good.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 7. Indeed, virtuous women do not experience agitation regarding future matters. And you, protected by weighty natural law (dharma), will swiftly attain ultimate good.

sanskrit: सहदेवश्च मे पुत्रः सदावेक्ष्यो वने वसन् ।यथेदं व्यसनं प्राप्य नास्य सीदेन्महन्मनः ॥८॥
transliteration: sahadevaśca me putraḥ sadāvekṣyo vane vasan ,yathedaṁ vyasanaṁ prāpya nāsya sīdenmahanmanaḥ.
english: And my son Sahadeva, who is living in the forest, should always be watched over, so that having encountered this distress, his great mind may not become dejected.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 8. And my son Sahadeva, who is living in the forest, should always be watched over, so that having encountered this distress, his great mind may not become dejected.

sanskrit: तथेत्युक्त्वा तु सा देवी स्रवन्नेत्रजलाविला ।शोणिताक्तैकवसना मुक्तकेश्यभिनिर्ययौ ॥९॥
transliteration: tathetyuktvā tu sā devī sravannetrajalāvilā ,śoṇitāktaikavasanā muktakeśyabhiniryayau.
english: Having said, "So be it," that queen, distressed by her flowing tears, wearing only a single blood-soaked garment, and with her hair disheveled, then departed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 9. Having said, "So be it," that queen, distressed by her flowing tears, wearing only a single blood-soaked garment, and with her hair disheveled, then departed.

sanskrit: तां क्रोशन्तीं पृथा दुःखादनुवव्राज गच्छतीम् ।अथापश्यत्सुतान्सर्वान्हृताभरणवाससः ॥१०॥
transliteration: tāṁ krośantīṁ pṛthā duḥkhādanuvavrāja gacchatīm ,athāpaśyatsutānsarvānhṛtābharaṇavāsasaḥ.
english: From sorrow, Pritha followed her as she cried and departed. Then she saw all her sons, whose ornaments and clothes had been taken away.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 10. From sorrow, Pritha followed her as she cried and departed. Then she saw all her sons, whose ornaments and clothes had been taken away.

sanskrit: रुरुचर्मावृततनून्ह्रिया किंचिदवाङ्मुखान् ।परैः परीतान्संहृष्टैः सुहृद्भिश्चानुशोचितान् ॥११॥
transliteration: rurucarmāvṛtatanūnhriyā kiṁcidavāṅmukhān ,paraiḥ parītānsaṁhṛṣṭaiḥ suhṛdbhiścānuśocitān.
english: Their bodies were covered with deer skins, and their faces were somewhat downcast due to shame. They were surrounded by greatly delighted enemies, and mourned by their friends.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 11. Their bodies were covered with deer skins, and their faces were somewhat downcast due to shame. They were surrounded by greatly delighted enemies, and mourned by their friends.

sanskrit: तदवस्थान्सुतान्सर्वानुपसृत्यातिवत्सला ।सस्वजानावदच्छोकात्तत्तद्विलपती बहु ॥१२॥
transliteration: tadavasthānsutānsarvānupasṛtyātivatsalā ,sasvajānāvadacchokāttattadvilapatī bahu.
english: Being extremely affectionate, she approached all her sons who were in that state. She embraced them and, weeping profusely from grief, uttered many lamentations.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 12. Being extremely affectionate, she approached all her sons who were in that state. She embraced them and, weeping profusely from grief, uttered many lamentations.

sanskrit: कथं सद्धर्मचारित्रवृत्तस्थितिविभूषितान् ।अक्षुद्रान्दृढभक्तांश्च दैवतेज्यापरान्सदा ॥१३॥
transliteration: kathaṁ saddharmacāritravṛttasthitivibhūṣitān ,akṣudrāndṛḍhabhaktāṁśca daivatejyāparānsadā.
english: How is it that those who are adorned with virtuous conduct and steadfastness in their intrinsic nature (dharma), who are noble-minded, firm in their devotion (bhakti), and always dedicated to the worship of gods [have come to such a plight]?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 13. How is it that those who are adorned with virtuous conduct and steadfastness in their intrinsic nature (dharma), who are noble-minded, firm in their devotion (bhakti), and always dedicated to the worship of gods [have come to such a plight]?

sanskrit: व्यसनं वः समभ्यागात्कोऽयं विधिविपर्ययः ।कस्यापध्यानजं चेदमागः पश्यामि वो धिया ॥१४॥
transliteration: vyasanaṁ vaḥ samabhyāgātko'yaṁ vidhiviparyayaḥ ,kasyāpadhyānajaṁ cedamāgaḥ paśyāmi vo dhiyā.
english: A calamity has certainly befallen you. What is this reversal of fate? Whose evil intention or offense has caused this? I perceive this for you through my intellect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 14. A calamity has certainly befallen you. What is this reversal of fate? Whose evil intention or offense has caused this? I perceive this for you through my intellect.

sanskrit: स्यात्तु मद्भाग्यदोषोऽयं याहं युष्मानजीजनम् ।दुःखायासभुजोऽत्यर्थं युक्तानप्युत्तमैर्गुणैः ॥१५॥
transliteration: syāttu madbhāgyadoṣo'yaṁ yāhaṁ yuṣmānajījanam ,duḥkhāyāsabhujo'tyarthaṁ yuktānapyuttamairguṇaiḥ.
english: Perhaps this is indeed the fault of my destiny, that I gave birth to you, who, despite being endowed with excellent qualities, must exceedingly endure sorrow and hardship.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 15. Perhaps this is indeed the fault of my destiny, that I gave birth to you, who, despite being endowed with excellent qualities, must exceedingly endure sorrow and hardship.

sanskrit: कथं वत्स्यथ दुर्गेषु वनेष्वृद्धिविनाकृताः ।वीर्यसत्त्वबलोत्साहतेजोभिरकृशाः कृशाः ॥१६॥
transliteration: kathaṁ vatsyatha durgeṣu vaneṣvṛddhivinākṛtāḥ ,vīryasattvabalotsāhatejobhirakṛśāḥ kṛśāḥ.
english: How will you live in these difficult forests, deprived of all prosperity, you who, though physically weakened, are intrinsically not diminished in valor, courage, strength, enthusiasm, and prowess?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 16. How will you live in these difficult forests, deprived of all prosperity, you who, though physically weakened, are intrinsically not diminished in valor, courage, strength, enthusiasm, and prowess?

sanskrit: यद्येतदहमज्ञास्यं वनवासो हि वो ध्रुवम् ।शतशृङ्गान्मृते पाण्डौ नागमिष्यं गजाह्वयम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: yadyetadahamajñāsyaṁ vanavāso hi vo dhruvam ,śataśṛṅgānmṛte pāṇḍau nāgamiṣyaṁ gajāhvayam.
english: If I had known that your exile to the forest was indeed inevitable, I would not have come to Gajāhvaya (Hastināpura) from Mount Śataśṛṅga after Pāṇḍu's death.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 17. If I had known that your exile to the forest was indeed inevitable, I would not have come to Gajāhvaya (Hastināpura) from Mount Śataśṛṅga after Pāṇḍu's death.

sanskrit: धन्यं वः पितरं मन्ये तपोमेधान्वितं तथा ।यः पुत्राधिमसंप्राप्य स्वर्गेच्छामकरोत्प्रियाम् ॥१८॥
transliteration: dhanyaṁ vaḥ pitaraṁ manye tapomedhānvitaṁ tathā ,yaḥ putrādhimasaṁprāpya svargecchāmakarotpriyām.
english: I consider your father blessed, who was endowed with asceticism (tapas) and intelligence. He did not experience the anguish concerning his sons, and thus cherished his desire for heaven.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 18. I consider your father blessed, who was endowed with asceticism (tapas) and intelligence. He did not experience the anguish concerning his sons, and thus cherished his desire for heaven.

sanskrit: धन्यां चातीन्द्रियज्ञानामिमां प्राप्तां परां गतिम् ।मन्येऽद्य माद्रीं धर्मज्ञां कल्याणीं सर्वथैव हि ॥१९॥
transliteration: dhanyāṁ cātīndriyajñānāmimāṁ prāptāṁ parāṁ gatim ,manye'dya mādrīṁ dharmajñāṁ kalyāṇīṁ sarvathaiva hi.
english: Today, I consider this blessed Madri, who possessed supra-sensory knowledge and attained the supreme state, as one who understood righteousness (dharma) and was auspicious in every respect.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 19. Today, I consider this blessed Madri, who possessed supra-sensory knowledge and attained the supreme state, as one who understood righteousness (dharma) and was auspicious in every respect.

sanskrit: रत्या मत्या च गत्या च ययाहमभिसंधिता ।जीवितप्रियतां मह्यं धिगिमां क्लेशभागिनीम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: ratyā matyā ca gatyā ca yayāhamabhisaṁdhitā ,jīvitapriyatāṁ mahyaṁ dhigimāṁ kleśabhāginīm.
english: By her affection, her wisdom, and her fate, by which I have been afflicted. Alas for this partaker of sorrow, who was dearer than life to me!
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 20. By her affection, her wisdom, and her fate, by which I have been afflicted. Alas for this partaker of sorrow, who was dearer than life to me!

sanskrit: एवं विलपतीं कुन्तीमभिसान्त्व्य प्रणम्य च ।पाण्डवा विगतानन्दा वनायैव प्रवव्रजुः ॥२१॥
transliteration: evaṁ vilapatīṁ kuntīmabhisāntvya praṇamya ca ,pāṇḍavā vigatānandā vanāyaiva pravavrajuḥ.
english: Having thus consoled Kunti, who was lamenting, and having bowed to her, the Pāṇḍavas, their joy gone, departed for the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 21. Having thus consoled Kunti, who was lamenting, and having bowed to her, the Pāṇḍavas, their joy gone, departed for the forest.

sanskrit: विदुरादयश्च तामार्तां कुन्तीमाश्वास्य हेतुभिः ।प्रावेशयन्गृहं क्षत्तुः स्वयमार्ततराः शनैः ॥२२॥
transliteration: vidurādayaśca tāmārtāṁ kuntīmāśvāsya hetubhiḥ ,prāveśayangṛhaṁ kṣattuḥ svayamārtatarāḥ śanaiḥ.
english: And Vidura and the others, having consoled that distressed Kunti with various arguments, slowly led her into the house of Kṣattṛ, they themselves being even more distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 22. And Vidura and the others, having consoled that distressed Kunti with various arguments, slowly led her into the house of Kṣattṛ, they themselves being even more distressed.

sanskrit: राजा च धृतराष्ट्रः स शोकाकुलितचेतनः ।क्षत्तुः संप्रेषयामास शीघ्रमागम्यतामिति ॥२३॥
transliteration: rājā ca dhṛtarāṣṭraḥ sa śokākulitacetanaḥ ,kṣattuḥ saṁpreṣayāmāsa śīghramāgamyatāmiti.
english: And King Dhritarashtra, his mind agitated by grief, sent a message to the charioteer (Vidura), saying, 'Come quickly!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 23. And King Dhritarashtra, his mind agitated by grief, sent a message to the charioteer (Vidura), saying, 'Come quickly!'

sanskrit: ततो जगाम विदुरो धृतराष्ट्रनिवेशनम् ।तं पर्यपृच्छत्संविग्नो धृतराष्ट्रो नराधिपः ॥२४॥
transliteration: tato jagāma viduro dhṛtarāṣṭraniveśanam ,taṁ paryapṛcchatsaṁvigno dhṛtarāṣṭro narādhipaḥ.
english: Then Vidura went to Dhritarashtra's palace. The distressed King Dhritarashtra then questioned him (Vidura).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 70 Verse 24. Then Vidura went to Dhritarashtra's palace. The distressed King Dhritarashtra then questioned him (Vidura).

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।कथं गच्छति कौन्तेयो धर्मराजो युधिष्ठिरः ।भीमसेनः सव्यसाची माद्रीपुत्रौ च तावुभौ ॥१॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,kathaṁ gacchati kaunteyo dharmarājo yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bhīmasenaḥ savyasācī mādrīputrau ca tāvubhau.
english: Dhritarashtra said: 'How fares the son of Kunti, King of righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira? And Bhimasena, Savyasaci (Arjuna), and both those sons of Madri?'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 1. Dhritarashtra said: 'How fares the son of Kunti, King of righteousness (dharma), Yudhishthira? And Bhimasena, Savyasaci (Arjuna), and both those sons of Madri?'

sanskrit: धौम्यश्चैव कथं क्षत्तर्द्रौपदी वा तपस्विनी ।श्रोतुमिच्छाम्यहं सर्वं तेषामङ्गविचेष्टितम् ॥२॥
transliteration: dhaumyaścaiva kathaṁ kṣattardraupadī vā tapasvinī ,śrotumicchāmyahaṁ sarvaṁ teṣāmaṅgaviceṣṭitam.
english: And how fares Dhaumya, O charioteer (kṣattṛ)? And Draupadi, that ascetic (tapasvinī) lady? I wish to hear all their activities.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 2. And how fares Dhaumya, O charioteer (kṣattṛ)? And Draupadi, that ascetic (tapasvinī) lady? I wish to hear all their activities.

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।वस्त्रेण संवृत्य मुखं कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः ।बाहू विशालौ कृत्वा तु भीमो गच्छति पाण्डवः ॥३॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,vastreṇa saṁvṛtya mukhaṁ kuntīputro yudhiṣṭhiraḥ ,bāhū viśālau kṛtvā tu bhīmo gacchati pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: Vidura said: Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, goes covering his face with a cloth. And Bhīma, the Pāṇḍava, goes with his powerful arms (bāhu) spread wide.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 3. Vidura said: Yudhiṣṭhira, the son of Kuntī, goes covering his face with a cloth. And Bhīma, the Pāṇḍava, goes with his powerful arms (bāhu) spread wide.

sanskrit: सिकता वपन्सव्यसाची राजानमनुगच्छति ।माद्रीपुत्रः सहदेवो मुखमालिप्य गच्छति ॥४॥
transliteration: sikatā vapansavyasācī rājānamanugacchati ,mādrīputraḥ sahadevo mukhamālipya gacchati.
english: Savyasācī (Arjuna) follows the king, scattering sand. Sahadeva, the son of Mādrī, goes with his face smeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 4. Savyasācī (Arjuna) follows the king, scattering sand. Sahadeva, the son of Mādrī, goes with his face smeared.

sanskrit: पांसूपलिप्तसर्वाङ्गो नकुलश्चित्तविह्वलः ।दर्शनीयतमो लोके राजानमनुगच्छति ॥५॥
transliteration: pāṁsūpaliptasarvāṅgo nakulaścittavihvalaḥ ,darśanīyatamo loke rājānamanugacchati.
english: Nakula, whose entire body is smeared with dust and whose mind is bewildered, the most handsome in the world, follows the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 5. Nakula, whose entire body is smeared with dust and whose mind is bewildered, the most handsome in the world, follows the king.

sanskrit: कृष्णा केशैः प्रतिच्छाद्य मुखमायतलोचना ।दर्शनीया प्ररुदती राजानमनुगच्छति ॥६॥
transliteration: kṛṣṇā keśaiḥ praticchādya mukhamāyatalocanā ,darśanīyā prarudatī rājānamanugacchati.
english: Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), with wide eyes, goes covering her face with her hair. Beautiful to behold, and weeping loudly, she follows the king.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 6. Kṛṣṇā (Draupadī), with wide eyes, goes covering her face with her hair. Beautiful to behold, and weeping loudly, she follows the king.

sanskrit: धौम्यो याम्यानि सामानि रौद्राणि च विशां पते ।गायन्गच्छति मार्गेषु कुशानादाय पाणिना ॥७॥
transliteration: dhaumyo yāmyāni sāmāni raudrāṇi ca viśāṁ pate ,gāyangacchati mārgeṣu kuśānādāya pāṇinā.
english: O lord of the people, Dhaumya walks along the paths, singing the Sāman melodies associated with Yama (yāmya) and Rudra (raudra), holding kusha grass in his hand.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 7. O lord of the people, Dhaumya walks along the paths, singing the Sāman melodies associated with Yama (yāmya) and Rudra (raudra), holding kusha grass in his hand.

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।विविधानीह रूपाणि कृत्वा गच्छन्ति पाण्डवाः ।तन्ममाचक्ष्व विदुर कस्मादेवं व्रजन्ति ते ॥८॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,vividhānīha rūpāṇi kṛtvā gacchanti pāṇḍavāḥ ,tanmamācakṣva vidura kasmādevaṁ vrajanti te.
english: Dhritarashtra said: "The Pandavas are going forth having assumed various forms here. Therefore, tell me, Vidura, why do they proceed in this manner?"
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 8. Dhritarashtra said: "The Pandavas are going forth having assumed various forms here. Therefore, tell me, Vidura, why do they proceed in this manner?"

sanskrit: विदुर उवाच ।निकृतस्यापि ते पुत्रैर्हृते राज्ये धनेषु च ।न धर्माच्चलते बुद्धिर्धर्मराजस्य धीमतः ॥९॥
transliteration: vidura uvāca ,nikṛtasyāpi te putrairhṛte rājye dhaneṣu ca ,na dharmāccalate buddhirdharmarājasya dhīmataḥ.
english: Vidura said: "Even though he has been cheated by your sons and his kingdom and wealth have been taken away, the mind (buddhi) of the wise King Yudhishthira (dharmarāja) does not deviate from his righteousness (dharma)."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 9. Vidura said: "Even though he has been cheated by your sons and his kingdom and wealth have been taken away, the mind (buddhi) of the wise King Yudhishthira (dharmarāja) does not deviate from his righteousness (dharma)."

sanskrit: योऽसौ राजा घृणी नित्यं धार्तराष्ट्रेषु भारत ।निकृत्या क्रोधसंतप्तो नोन्मीलयति लोचने ॥१०॥
transliteration: yo'sau rājā ghṛṇī nityaṁ dhārtarāṣṭreṣu bhārata ,nikṛtyā krodhasaṁtapto nonmīlayati locane.
english: O Bharata, that King, who is always compassionate towards the sons of Dhritarashtra (dhārtarāṣṭreṣu), does not open his two eyes (in fury) even though he is consumed by anger (krodhasaṃtapta) due to their deception.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 10. O Bharata, that King, who is always compassionate towards the sons of Dhritarashtra (dhārtarāṣṭreṣu), does not open his two eyes (in fury) even though he is consumed by anger (krodhasaṃtapta) due to their deception.

sanskrit: नाहं जनं निर्दहेयं दृष्ट्वा घोरेण चक्षुषा ।स पिधाय मुखं राजा तस्माद्गच्छति पाण्डवः ॥११॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ janaṁ nirdaheyaṁ dṛṣṭvā ghoreṇa cakṣuṣā ,sa pidhāya mukhaṁ rājā tasmādgacchati pāṇḍavaḥ.
english: I would not consume people merely by looking at them with a terrible eye. Therefore, that king, the Pandava (Pāṇḍava) prince, goes with his face covered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 11. I would not consume people merely by looking at them with a terrible eye. Therefore, that king, the Pandava (Pāṇḍava) prince, goes with his face covered.

sanskrit: यथा च भीमो व्रजति तन्मे निगदतः शृणु ।बाह्वोर्बले नास्ति समो ममेति भरतर्षभ ॥१२॥
transliteration: yathā ca bhīmo vrajati tanme nigadataḥ śṛṇu ,bāhvorbale nāsti samo mameti bharatarṣabha.
english: O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), listen as I narrate to you how Bhīma proceeds, (thinking), 'No one is equal to me in the strength of my arms.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 12. O best of Bharatas (Bharatarṣabha), listen as I narrate to you how Bhīma proceeds, (thinking), 'No one is equal to me in the strength of my arms.'

sanskrit: बाहू विशालौ कृत्वा तु तेन भीमोऽपि गच्छति ।बाहू दर्शयमानो हि बाहुद्रविणदर्पितः ।चिकीर्षन्कर्म शत्रुभ्यो बाहुद्रव्यानुरूपतः ॥१३॥
transliteration: bāhū viśālau kṛtvā tu tena bhīmo'pi gacchati ,bāhū darśayamāno hi bāhudraviṇadarpitaḥ ,cikīrṣankarma śatrubhyo bāhudravyānurūpataḥ.
english: Indeed, Bhīma proceeds, spreading wide his mighty arms, displaying them, proud of the strength of his arms, and wishing to perform deeds against his enemies in a manner befitting the power of his arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 13. Indeed, Bhīma proceeds, spreading wide his mighty arms, displaying them, proud of the strength of his arms, and wishing to perform deeds against his enemies in a manner befitting the power of his arms.

sanskrit: प्रदिशञ्शरसंपातान्कुन्तीपुत्रोऽर्जुनस्तदा ।सिकता वपन्सव्यसाची राजानमनुगच्छति ॥१४॥
transliteration: pradiśañśarasaṁpātānkuntīputro'rjunastadā ,sikatā vapansavyasācī rājānamanugacchati.
english: Then, Arjuna (Kuntīputra), the ambidextrous one (Savyasācī), follows the king, demonstrating volleys of arrows and scattering sand.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 14. Then, Arjuna (Kuntīputra), the ambidextrous one (Savyasācī), follows the king, demonstrating volleys of arrows and scattering sand.

sanskrit: असक्ताः सिकतास्तस्य यथा संप्रति भारत ।असक्तं शरवर्षाणि तथा मोक्ष्यति शत्रुषु ॥१५॥
transliteration: asaktāḥ sikatāstasya yathā saṁprati bhārata ,asaktaṁ śaravarṣāṇi tathā mokṣyati śatruṣu.
english: Just as grains of sand do not cling to him, O Bhārata, similarly, he will discharge showers of arrows upon his enemies without attachment (asakta).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 15. Just as grains of sand do not cling to him, O Bhārata, similarly, he will discharge showers of arrows upon his enemies without attachment (asakta).

sanskrit: न मे कश्चिद्विजानीयान्मुखमद्येति भारत ।मुखमालिप्य तेनासौ सहदेवोऽपि गच्छति ॥१६॥
transliteration: na me kaścidvijānīyānmukhamadyeti bhārata ,mukhamālipya tenāsau sahadevo'pi gacchati.
english: "Let no one recognize my face today, O Bhārata." Having smeared his face with that, even Sahadeva goes (forth).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 16. "Let no one recognize my face today, O Bhārata." Having smeared his face with that, even Sahadeva goes (forth).

sanskrit: नाहं मनांस्याददेयं मार्गे स्त्रीणामिति प्रभो ।पांसूपचितसर्वाङ्गो नकुलस्तेन गच्छति ॥१७॥
transliteration: nāhaṁ manāṁsyādadeyaṁ mārge strīṇāmiti prabho ,pāṁsūpacitasarvāṅgo nakulastena gacchati.
english: "I would not win the hearts of women on the path, O Lord!" - with his entire body covered in dust, Nakula goes (forth) in that (manner).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 17. "I would not win the hearts of women on the path, O Lord!" - with his entire body covered in dust, Nakula goes (forth) in that (manner).

sanskrit: एकवस्त्रा तु रुदती मुक्तकेशी रजस्वला ।शोणिताक्तार्द्रवसना द्रौपदी वाक्यमब्रवीत् ॥१८॥
transliteration: ekavastrā tu rudatī muktakeśī rajasvalā ,śoṇitāktārdravasanā draupadī vākyamabravīt.
english: But Draupadi, clad in a single garment, weeping, with disheveled hair, menstruating, and with her clothes wet and stained with blood, spoke these words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 18. But Draupadi, clad in a single garment, weeping, with disheveled hair, menstruating, and with her clothes wet and stained with blood, spoke these words.

sanskrit: यत्कृतेऽहमिमां प्राप्ता तेषां वर्षे चतुर्दशे ।हतपत्यो हतसुता हतबन्धुजनप्रियाः ॥१९॥
transliteration: yatkṛte'hamimāṁ prāptā teṣāṁ varṣe caturdaśe ,hatapatyo hatasutā hatabandhujanapriyāḥ.
english: For whose sake I have reached this state (of distress), in their fourteenth year, those women whose husbands are slain, whose sons are slain, and whose dear kinsmen are slain,
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 19. For whose sake I have reached this state (of distress), in their fourteenth year, those women whose husbands are slain, whose sons are slain, and whose dear kinsmen are slain,

sanskrit: बन्धुशोणितदिग्धाङ्ग्यो मुक्तकेश्यो रजस्वलाः ।एवं कृतोदका नार्यः प्रवेक्ष्यन्ति गजाह्वयम् ॥२०॥
transliteration: bandhuśoṇitadigdhāṅgyo muktakeśyo rajasvalāḥ ,evaṁ kṛtodakā nāryaḥ pravekṣyanti gajāhvayam.
english: Thus, the women, whose bodies are smeared with the blood of their kinsmen, with unbound hair, and appearing as if in a state of ritual impurity, after performing the water oblation for the dead, will enter Hastinapura.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 20. Thus, the women, whose bodies are smeared with the blood of their kinsmen, with unbound hair, and appearing as if in a state of ritual impurity, after performing the water oblation for the dead, will enter Hastinapura.

sanskrit: कृत्वा तु नैरृतान्दर्भान्धीरो धौम्यः पुरोहितः ।सामानि गायन्याम्यानि पुरतो याति भारत ॥२१॥
transliteration: kṛtvā tu nairṛtāndarbhāndhīro dhaumyaḥ purohitaḥ ,sāmāni gāyanyāmyāni purato yāti bhārata.
english: But, O Bhārata, the resolute priest Dhaumya, having arranged the kusha grass towards the direction of the demons (nirṛta), proceeds in front, singing the mournful (yāmya) Sāman chants.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 21. But, O Bhārata, the resolute priest Dhaumya, having arranged the kusha grass towards the direction of the demons (nirṛta), proceeds in front, singing the mournful (yāmya) Sāman chants.

sanskrit: हतेषु भारतेष्वाजौ कुरूणां गुरवस्तदा ।एवं सामानि गास्यन्तीत्युक्त्वा धौम्योऽपि गच्छति ॥२२॥
transliteration: hateṣu bhārateṣvājau kurūṇāṁ guravastadā ,evaṁ sāmāni gāsyantītyuktvā dhaumyo'pi gacchati.
english: 'Then, when the Bhāratas are slain in battle, the elders (guru) of the Kurus will thus chant the Sāman hymns.' Having spoken thus, Dhaumya also departs.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 22. 'Then, when the Bhāratas are slain in battle, the elders (guru) of the Kurus will thus chant the Sāman hymns.' Having spoken thus, Dhaumya also departs.

sanskrit: हा हा गच्छन्ति नो नाथाः समवेक्षध्वमीदृशम् ।इति पौराः सुदुःखार्ताः क्रोशन्ति स्म समन्ततः ॥२३॥
transliteration: hā hā gacchanti no nāthāḥ samavekṣadhvamīdṛśam ,iti paurāḥ suduḥkhārtāḥ krośanti sma samantataḥ.
english: "Oh no, our protectors are departing! Witness this tragic situation!" Thus, the citizens, deeply distressed, cried out from all sides.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 23. "Oh no, our protectors are departing! Witness this tragic situation!" Thus, the citizens, deeply distressed, cried out from all sides.

sanskrit: एवमाकारलिङ्गैस्ते व्यवसायं मनोगतम् ।कथयन्तः स्म कौन्तेया वनं जग्मुर्मनस्विनः ॥२४॥
transliteration: evamākāraliṅgaiste vyavasāyaṁ manogatam ,kathayantaḥ sma kaunteyā vanaṁ jagmurmanasvinaḥ.
english: The resolute (manasvin) sons of Kunti (Kaunteyā), indicating their inner resolve through such gestures and signs, went to the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 24. The resolute (manasvin) sons of Kunti (Kaunteyā), indicating their inner resolve through such gestures and signs, went to the forest.

sanskrit: एवं तेषु नराग्र्येषु निर्यत्सु गजसाह्वयात् ।अनभ्रे विद्युतश्चासन्भूमिश्च समकम्पत ॥२५॥
transliteration: evaṁ teṣu narāgryeṣu niryatsu gajasāhvayāt ,anabhre vidyutaścāsanbhūmiśca samakampata.
english: Thus, as those foremost among men departed from Hastinapura, lightning flashed in a cloudless sky, and the earth trembled.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 25. Thus, as those foremost among men departed from Hastinapura, lightning flashed in a cloudless sky, and the earth trembled.

sanskrit: राहुरग्रसदादित्यमपर्वणि विशां पते ।उल्का चाप्यपसव्यं तु पुरं कृत्वा व्यशीर्यत ॥२६॥
transliteration: rāhuragrasadādityamaparvaṇi viśāṁ pate ,ulkā cāpyapasavyaṁ tu puraṁ kṛtvā vyaśīryata.
english: O lord of the people, Rahu eclipsed the sun at an unpropitious time. And a meteor, having circled the city ominously (apasavya) from the right, then shattered.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 26. O lord of the people, Rahu eclipsed the sun at an unpropitious time. And a meteor, having circled the city ominously (apasavya) from the right, then shattered.

sanskrit: प्रव्याहरन्ति क्रव्यादा गृध्रगोमायुवायसाः ।देवायतनचैत्येषु प्राकाराट्टालकेषु च ॥२७॥
transliteration: pravyāharanti kravyādā gṛdhragomāyuvāyasāḥ ,devāyatanacaityeṣu prākārāṭṭālakeṣu ca.
english: Flesh-eating vultures, jackals, and crows shriek loudly in temples, sacred enclosures, ramparts, and watchtowers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 27. Flesh-eating vultures, jackals, and crows shriek loudly in temples, sacred enclosures, ramparts, and watchtowers.

sanskrit: एवमेते महोत्पाता वनं गच्छति पाण्डवे ।भारतानामभावाय राजन्दुर्मन्त्रिते तव ॥२८॥
transliteration: evamete mahotpātā vanaṁ gacchati pāṇḍave ,bhāratānāmabhāvāya rājandurmantrite tava.
english: O King, these great omens (utpāta) manifest in this way for the destruction of the Bharatas, due to your evil counsel, as the Pandava (Yudhishthira) goes to the forest.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 28. O King, these great omens (utpāta) manifest in this way for the destruction of the Bharatas, due to your evil counsel, as the Pandava (Yudhishthira) goes to the forest.

sanskrit: नारदश्च सभामध्ये कुरूणामग्रतः स्थितः ।महर्षिभिः परिवृतो रौद्रं वाक्यमुवाच ह ॥२९॥
transliteration: nāradaśca sabhāmadhye kurūṇāmagrataḥ sthitaḥ ,maharṣibhiḥ parivṛto raudraṁ vākyamuvāca ha.
english: And Narada, surrounded by great sages (ṛṣi), stood before the Kurus in the midst of the assembly and indeed spoke terrible words.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 29. And Narada, surrounded by great sages (ṛṣi), stood before the Kurus in the midst of the assembly and indeed spoke terrible words.

sanskrit: इतश्चतुर्दशे वर्षे विनङ्क्ष्यन्तीह कौरवाः ।दुर्योधनापराधेन भीमार्जुनबलेन च ॥३०॥
transliteration: itaścaturdaśe varṣe vinaṅkṣyantīha kauravāḥ ,duryodhanāparādhena bhīmārjunabalena ca.
english: From now, in the fourteenth year, the Kauravas will perish in this world due to Duryodhana's offense and the might of Bhima and Arjuna.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 30. From now, in the fourteenth year, the Kauravas will perish in this world due to Duryodhana's offense and the might of Bhima and Arjuna.

sanskrit: इत्युक्त्वा दिवमाक्रम्य क्षिप्रमन्तरधीयत ।ब्राह्मीं श्रियं सुविपुलां बिभ्रद्देवर्षिसत्तमः ॥३१॥
transliteration: ityuktvā divamākramya kṣipramantaradhīyata ,brāhmīṁ śriyaṁ suvipulāṁ bibhraddevarṣisattamaḥ.
english: Having thus spoken, the foremost among the divine sages (devarṣi), bearing immense divine (brāhmī) splendor, quickly ascended to the sky and disappeared.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 31. Having thus spoken, the foremost among the divine sages (devarṣi), bearing immense divine (brāhmī) splendor, quickly ascended to the sky and disappeared.

sanskrit: ततो दुर्योधनः कर्णः शकुनिश्चापि सौबलः ।द्रोणं द्वीपममन्यन्त राज्यं चास्मै न्यवेदयन् ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato duryodhanaḥ karṇaḥ śakuniścāpi saubalaḥ ,droṇaṁ dvīpamamanyanta rājyaṁ cāsmai nyavedayan.
english: Then Duryodhana, Karṇa, and also Śakuni, the son of Subala, regarded Droṇa as their refuge (dvīpa) and offered the kingdom to him.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 32. Then Duryodhana, Karṇa, and also Śakuni, the son of Subala, regarded Droṇa as their refuge (dvīpa) and offered the kingdom to him.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीत्ततो द्रोणो दुर्योधनममर्षणम् ।दुःशासनं च कर्णं च सर्वानेव च भारतान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: athābravīttato droṇo duryodhanamamarṣaṇam ,duḥśāsanaṁ ca karṇaṁ ca sarvāneva ca bhāratān.
english: Then Droṇa spoke to the impatient Duryodhana, and to Duḥśāsana, and to Karṇa, and indeed to all the Bhāratas (Kauravas).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 33. Then Droṇa spoke to the impatient Duryodhana, and to Duḥśāsana, and to Karṇa, and indeed to all the Bhāratas (Kauravas).

sanskrit: अवध्यान्पाण्डवानाहुर्देवपुत्रान्द्विजातयः ।अहं तु शरणं प्राप्तान्वर्तमानो यथाबलम् ॥३४॥
transliteration: avadhyānpāṇḍavānāhurdevaputrāndvijātayaḥ ,ahaṁ tu śaraṇaṁ prāptānvartamāno yathābalam.
english: The twice-born ones (dvijāti) declare the Pāṇḍavas, as sons of gods, to be inviolable. But I, acting to the best of my ability, will protect those who seek refuge (śaraṇa).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 34. The twice-born ones (dvijāti) declare the Pāṇḍavas, as sons of gods, to be inviolable. But I, acting to the best of my ability, will protect those who seek refuge (śaraṇa).

sanskrit: गतान्सर्वात्मना भक्त्या धार्तराष्ट्रान्सराजकान् ।नोत्सहे समभित्यक्तुं दैवमूलमतः परम् ॥३५॥
transliteration: gatānsarvātmanā bhaktyā dhārtarāṣṭrānsarājakān ,notsahe samabhityaktuṁ daivamūlamataḥ param.
english: I cannot bring myself to completely abandon the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their rulers, who have approached (me) with complete devotion (bhakti). What lies beyond this is rooted in divine will.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 35. I cannot bring myself to completely abandon the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, along with their rulers, who have approached (me) with complete devotion (bhakti). What lies beyond this is rooted in divine will.

sanskrit: धर्मतः पाण्डुपुत्रा वै वनं गच्छन्ति निर्जिताः ।ते च द्वादश वर्षाणि वने वत्स्यन्ति कौरवाः ॥३६॥
transliteration: dharmataḥ pāṇḍuputrā vai vanaṁ gacchanti nirjitāḥ ,te ca dvādaśa varṣāṇi vane vatsyanti kauravāḥ.
english: Indeed, by natural law (dharma), the sons of Pāṇḍu, though defeated, are going to the forest. And they, the Kauravas (Pāṇḍavas), will dwell in the forest for twelve years.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 36. Indeed, by natural law (dharma), the sons of Pāṇḍu, though defeated, are going to the forest. And they, the Kauravas (Pāṇḍavas), will dwell in the forest for twelve years.

sanskrit: चरितब्रह्मचर्याश्च क्रोधामर्षवशानुगाः ।वैरं प्रत्यानयिष्यन्ति मम दुःखाय पाण्डवाः ॥३७॥
transliteration: caritabrahmacaryāśca krodhāmarṣavaśānugāḥ ,vairaṁ pratyānayiṣyanti mama duḥkhāya pāṇḍavāḥ.
english: And the Pāṇḍavas, having observed their spiritual discipline (brahmacarya) and now governed by anger and resentment, will retaliate (lit. bring back enmity), which will cause me sorrow.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 37. And the Pāṇḍavas, having observed their spiritual discipline (brahmacarya) and now governed by anger and resentment, will retaliate (lit. bring back enmity), which will cause me sorrow.

sanskrit: मया तु भ्रंशितो राज्याद्द्रुपदः सखिविग्रहे ।पुत्रार्थमयजत्क्रोधाद्वधाय मम भारत ॥३८॥
transliteration: mayā tu bhraṁśito rājyāddrupadaḥ sakhivigrahe ,putrārthamayajatkrodhādvadhāya mama bhārata.
english: But, O Bhārata, Drupada, whom I had deprived of his kingdom due to a quarrel between friends, then out of anger performed a Vedic ritual (yajña) to obtain a son for my destruction.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 38. But, O Bhārata, Drupada, whom I had deprived of his kingdom due to a quarrel between friends, then out of anger performed a Vedic ritual (yajña) to obtain a son for my destruction.

sanskrit: याजोपयाजतपसा पुत्रं लेभे स पावकात् ।धृष्टद्युम्नं द्रौपदीं च वेदीमध्यात्सुमध्यमाम् ॥३९॥
transliteration: yājopayājatapasā putraṁ lebhe sa pāvakāt ,dhṛṣṭadyumnaṁ draupadīṁ ca vedīmadhyātsumadhyamām.
english: He obtained a son, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, and the slender-waisted Draupadī from the sacrificial fire, emerging from the middle of the altar, due to the austerities (tapas) performed by Yaja and Upayaja.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 39. He obtained a son, Dhṛṣṭadyumna, and the slender-waisted Draupadī from the sacrificial fire, emerging from the middle of the altar, due to the austerities (tapas) performed by Yaja and Upayaja.

sanskrit: ज्वालावर्णो देवदत्तो धनुष्मान्कवची शरी ।मर्त्यधर्मतया तस्मादिति मां भयमाविशत् ॥४०॥
transliteration: jvālāvarṇo devadatto dhanuṣmānkavacī śarī ,martyadharmatayā tasmāditi māṁ bhayamāviśat.
english: That divinely-endowed (devadatta) warrior, with a flame-like radiance, armed with a bow, clad in armor, and equipped with arrows - from him, fear (bhaya) thus assailed me, owing to the inherent nature (dharma) of mortals.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 40. That divinely-endowed (devadatta) warrior, with a flame-like radiance, armed with a bow, clad in armor, and equipped with arrows - from him, fear (bhaya) thus assailed me, owing to the inherent nature (dharma) of mortals.

sanskrit: गतो हि पक्षतां तेषां पार्षतः पुरुषर्षभः ।सृष्टप्राणो भृशतरं तस्माद्योत्स्ये तवारिभिः ॥४१॥
transliteration: gato hi pakṣatāṁ teṣāṁ pārṣataḥ puruṣarṣabhaḥ ,sṛṣṭaprāṇo bhṛśataraṁ tasmādyotsye tavāribhiḥ.
english: Indeed, that Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the best among men (puruṣarṣabha), has sided with them, created with an abundance of life. Therefore, I shall fight your enemies.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 41. Indeed, that Pārṣata (Dhṛṣṭadyumna), the best among men (puruṣarṣabha), has sided with them, created with an abundance of life. Therefore, I shall fight your enemies.

sanskrit: मद्वधाय श्रुतो ह्येष लोके चाप्यतिविश्रुतः ।नूनं सोऽयमनुप्राप्तस्त्वत्कृते कालपर्ययः ॥४२॥
transliteration: madvadhāya śruto hyeṣa loke cāpyativiśrutaḥ ,nūnaṁ so'yamanuprāptastvatkṛte kālaparyayaḥ.
english: Indeed, this (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) is renowned and very well-known in the world for my destruction. Surely, this destined turn of time (kālaparyaya) has now arrived on your behalf.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 42. Indeed, this (Dhṛṣṭadyumna) is renowned and very well-known in the world for my destruction. Surely, this destined turn of time (kālaparyaya) has now arrived on your behalf.

sanskrit: त्वरिताः कुरुत श्रेयो नैतदेतावता कृतम् ।मुहूर्तं सुखमेवैतत्तालच्छायेव हैमनी ॥४३॥
transliteration: tvaritāḥ kuruta śreyo naitadetāvatā kṛtam ,muhūrtaṁ sukhamevaitattālacchāyeva haimanī.
english: Quickly strive for the ultimate good (śreyaḥ)! This much is not enough. This happiness is merely momentary, like the shade of a palm tree in winter.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 43. Quickly strive for the ultimate good (śreyaḥ)! This much is not enough. This happiness is merely momentary, like the shade of a palm tree in winter.

sanskrit: यजध्वं च महायज्ञैर्भोगानश्नीत दत्त च ।इतश्चतुर्दशे वर्षे महत्प्राप्स्यथ वैशसम् ॥४४॥
transliteration: yajadhvaṁ ca mahāyajñairbhogānaśnīta datta ca ,itaścaturdaśe varṣe mahatprāpsyatha vaiśasam.
english: And perform great sacrifices (yajñas), enjoy pleasures, and give [charity]! From now, in the fourteenth year, you will experience a great catastrophe.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 44. And perform great sacrifices (yajñas), enjoy pleasures, and give [charity]! From now, in the fourteenth year, you will experience a great catastrophe.

sanskrit: दुर्योधन निशम्यैतत्प्रतिपद्य यथेच्छसि ।साम वा पाण्डवेयेषु प्रयुङ्क्ष्व यदि मन्यसे ॥४५॥
transliteration: duryodhana niśamyaitatpratipadya yathecchasi ,sāma vā pāṇḍaveyeṣu prayuṅkṣva yadi manyase.
english: O Duryodhana, having heard this, you may act as you wish. Or, if you deem it proper, employ conciliation (sāma) towards the Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 45. O Duryodhana, having heard this, you may act as you wish. Or, if you deem it proper, employ conciliation (sāma) towards the Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।द्रोणस्य वचनं श्रुत्वा धृतराष्ट्रोऽब्रवीदिदम् ।सम्यगाह गुरुः क्षत्तरुपावर्तय पाण्डवान् ॥४६॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,droṇasya vacanaṁ śrutvā dhṛtarāṣṭro'bravīdidam ,samyagāha guruḥ kṣattarupāvartaya pāṇḍavān.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard Droṇa's words, Dhṛtarāṣṭra then said this: 'The preceptor (guru) has spoken well, O charioteer (kṣatta)! Bring back the Pāṇḍavas.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 46. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: Having heard Droṇa's words, Dhṛtarāṣṭra then said this: 'The preceptor (guru) has spoken well, O charioteer (kṣatta)! Bring back the Pāṇḍavas.'

sanskrit: यदि वा न निवर्तन्ते सत्कृता यान्तु पाण्डवाः ।सशस्त्ररथपादाता भोगवन्तश्च पुत्रकाः ॥४७॥
transliteration: yadi vā na nivartante satkṛtā yāntu pāṇḍavāḥ ,saśastrarathapādātā bhogavantaśca putrakāḥ.
english: If, however, the Pāṇḍavas do not return after being honored, then let them go [their own way]. And your sons (the Kauravas) are prosperous, possessing weapons, chariots, and foot soldiers.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 71 Verse 47. If, however, the Pāṇḍavas do not return after being honored, then let them go [their own way]. And your sons (the Kauravas) are prosperous, possessing weapons, chariots, and foot soldiers.

sanskrit: वैशंपायन उवाच ।वनं गतेषु पार्थेषु निर्जितेषु दुरोदरे ।धृतराष्ट्रं महाराज तदा चिन्ता समाविशत् ॥१॥
transliteration: vaiśaṁpāyana uvāca ,vanaṁ gateṣu pārtheṣu nirjiteṣu durodare ,dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ mahārāja tadā cintā samāviśat.
english: Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O great king, when the Pāṇḍavas had gone to the forest (vana), having been defeated in the dice game, then worry (cintā) overcame Dhṛtarāṣṭra.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 1. Vaiśaṃpāyana said: O great king, when the Pāṇḍavas had gone to the forest (vana), having been defeated in the dice game, then worry (cintā) overcame Dhṛtarāṣṭra.

sanskrit: तं चिन्तयानमासीनं धृतराष्ट्रं जनेश्वरम् ।निःश्वसन्तमनेकाग्रमिति होवाच संजयः ॥२॥
transliteration: taṁ cintayānamāsīnaṁ dhṛtarāṣṭraṁ janeśvaram ,niḥśvasantamanekāgramiti hovāca saṁjayaḥ.
english: Saṃjaya said thus to that Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of people (janeśvara), who was seated, worrying, sighing, and distracted.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 2. Saṃjaya said thus to that Dhṛtarāṣṭra, the lord of people (janeśvara), who was seated, worrying, sighing, and distracted.

sanskrit: अवाप्य वसुसंपूर्णां वसुधां वसुधाधिप ।प्रव्राज्य पाण्डवान्राज्याद्राजन्किमनुशोचसि ॥३॥
transliteration: avāpya vasusaṁpūrṇāṁ vasudhāṁ vasudhādhipa ,pravrājya pāṇḍavānrājyādrājankimanuśocasi.
english: O king, O lord of the earth (vasudhā-adhipa), having obtained an earth (vasudhā) completely filled with wealth (vasu) and having exiled the Pāṇḍavas from the kingdom, why do you grieve?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 3. O king, O lord of the earth (vasudhā-adhipa), having obtained an earth (vasudhā) completely filled with wealth (vasu) and having exiled the Pāṇḍavas from the kingdom, why do you grieve?

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।अशोच्यं तु कुतस्तेषां येषां वैरं भविष्यति ।पाण्डवैर्युद्धशौण्डैर्हि मित्रवद्भिर्महारथैः ॥४॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,aśocyaṁ tu kutasteṣāṁ yeṣāṁ vairaṁ bhaviṣyati ,pāṇḍavairyuddhaśauṇḍairhi mitravadbhirmahārathaiḥ.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "But how can those not be considered pitiable, with whom the Pāṇḍavas - who are mighty warriors, skilled in battle, and possess great charioteers as allies - will have enmity?"
