search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 4. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: "But how can those not be considered pitiable, with whom the Pāṇḍavas - who are mighty warriors, skilled in battle, and possess great charioteers as allies - will have enmity?"

sanskrit: संजय उवाच ।तवेदं सुकृतं राजन्महद्वैरं भविष्यति ।विनाशः सर्वलोकस्य सानुबन्धो भविष्यति ॥५॥
transliteration: saṁjaya uvāca ,tavedaṁ sukṛtaṁ rājanmahadvairaṁ bhaviṣyati ,vināśaḥ sarvalokasya sānubandho bhaviṣyati.
english: Saṃjaya said: "O King, this act of yours will result in a great enmity. It will lead to the destruction of the entire world along with its connections."
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 5. Saṃjaya said: "O King, this act of yours will result in a great enmity. It will lead to the destruction of the entire world along with its connections."

sanskrit: वार्यमाणोऽपि भीष्मेण द्रोणेन विदुरेण च ।पाण्डवानां प्रियां भार्यां द्रौपदीं धर्मचारिणीम् ॥६॥
transliteration: vāryamāṇo'pi bhīṣmeṇa droṇena vidureṇa ca ,pāṇḍavānāṁ priyāṁ bhāryāṁ draupadīṁ dharmacāriṇīm.
english: Even though he was being restrained by Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and Vidura, (he acted upon) Draupadī - the dear wife of the Pāṇḍavas, who adheres to (dharma) righteous conduct.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 6. Even though he was being restrained by Bhīṣma, Droṇa, and Vidura, (he acted upon) Draupadī - the dear wife of the Pāṇḍavas, who adheres to (dharma) righteous conduct.

sanskrit: प्राहिणोदानयेहेति पुत्रो दुर्योधनस्तव ।सूतपुत्रं सुमन्दात्मा निर्लज्जः प्रातिकामिनम् ॥७॥
transliteration: prāhiṇodānayeheti putro duryodhanastava ,sūtaputraṁ sumandātmā nirlajjaḥ prātikāminam.
english: Your son Duryodhana, who was extremely dull-witted and shameless, dispatched the charioteer Prātikāmin, saying, 'Bring her here!'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 7. Your son Duryodhana, who was extremely dull-witted and shameless, dispatched the charioteer Prātikāmin, saying, 'Bring her here!'

sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच ।यस्मै देवाः प्रयच्छन्ति पुरुषाय पराभवम् ।बुद्धिं तस्यापकर्षन्ति सोऽपाचीनानि पश्यति ॥८॥
transliteration: dhṛtarāṣṭra uvāca ,yasmai devāḥ prayacchanti puruṣāya parābhavam ,buddhiṁ tasyāpakarṣanti so'pācīnāni paśyati.
english: Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: The gods, when they grant defeat (parābhava) to a man (puruṣa), first diminish his intellect. Consequently, he perceives things in a distorted manner.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 8. Dhṛtarāṣṭra said: The gods, when they grant defeat (parābhava) to a man (puruṣa), first diminish his intellect. Consequently, he perceives things in a distorted manner.

sanskrit: बुद्धौ कलुषभूतायां विनाशे प्रत्युपस्थिते ।अनयो नयसंकाशो हृदयान्नापसर्पति ॥९॥
transliteration: buddhau kaluṣabhūtāyāṁ vināśe pratyupasthite ,anayo nayasaṁkāśo hṛdayānnāpasarpati.
english: When the intellect (buddhi) becomes tainted and destruction (vināśa) is impending, then unsound counsel, which appears to be proper policy, does not leave the heart.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 9. When the intellect (buddhi) becomes tainted and destruction (vināśa) is impending, then unsound counsel, which appears to be proper policy, does not leave the heart.

sanskrit: अनर्थाश्चार्थरूपेण अर्थाश्चानर्थरूपिणः ।उत्तिष्ठन्ति विनाशान्ते नरं तच्चास्य रोचते ॥१०॥
transliteration: anarthāścārtharūpeṇa arthāścānartharūpiṇaḥ ,uttiṣṭhanti vināśānte naraṁ taccāsya rocate.
english: Misfortunes (anartha) appear in the guise of advantages, and advantages (artha) take on the form of misfortunes. These phenomena occur when destruction (vināśa) is at its end, and surprisingly, that very state of affairs is pleasing to the man.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 10. Misfortunes (anartha) appear in the guise of advantages, and advantages (artha) take on the form of misfortunes. These phenomena occur when destruction (vināśa) is at its end, and surprisingly, that very state of affairs is pleasing to the man.

sanskrit: न कालो दण्डमुद्यम्य शिरः कृन्तति कस्यचित् ।कालस्य बलमेतावद्विपरीतार्थदर्शनम् ॥११॥
transliteration: na kālo daṇḍamudyamya śiraḥ kṛntati kasyacit ,kālasya balametāvadviparītārthadarśanam.
english: Time (Kāla) does not pick up a stick and cut off anyone's head. The power of Time (Kāla) is only this: it causes the perception of things in an inverted or perverse way.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 11. Time (Kāla) does not pick up a stick and cut off anyone's head. The power of Time (Kāla) is only this: it causes the perception of things in an inverted or perverse way.

sanskrit: आसादितमिदं घोरं तुमुलं लोमहर्षणम् ।पाञ्चालीमपकर्षद्भिः सभामध्ये तपस्विनीम् ॥१२॥
transliteration: āsāditamidaṁ ghoraṁ tumulaṁ lomaharṣaṇam ,pāñcālīmapakarṣadbhiḥ sabhāmadhye tapasvinīm.
english: This dreadful, tumultuous, and hair-raising event was brought about by those who dragged the wretched Pañcālī into the assembly hall.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 12. This dreadful, tumultuous, and hair-raising event was brought about by those who dragged the wretched Pañcālī into the assembly hall.

sanskrit: अयोनिजां रूपवतीं कुले जातां विभावरीम् ।को नु तां सर्वधर्मज्ञां परिभूय यशस्विनीम् ॥१३॥
transliteration: ayonijāṁ rūpavatīṁ kule jātāṁ vibhāvarīm ,ko nu tāṁ sarvadharmajñāṁ paribhūya yaśasvinīm.
english: Who, indeed, would humiliate her – the one not born from a womb, beautiful, born into a noble family, radiant, knowing all aspects of natural law (dharma), and glorious?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 13. Who, indeed, would humiliate her – the one not born from a womb, beautiful, born into a noble family, radiant, knowing all aspects of natural law (dharma), and glorious?

sanskrit: पर्यानयेत्सभामध्यमृते दुर्द्यूतदेविनम् ।स्त्रीधर्मिणीं वरारोहां शोणितेन समुक्षिताम् ॥१४॥
transliteration: paryānayetsabhāmadhyamṛte durdyūtadevinam ,strīdharmiṇīṁ varārohāṁ śoṇitena samukṣitām.
english: Who, indeed, would bring her into the assembly hall - except for the wicked gambler - her, who was observing her feminine duties (dharma), beautiful, and stained with blood?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 14. Who, indeed, would bring her into the assembly hall - except for the wicked gambler - her, who was observing her feminine duties (dharma), beautiful, and stained with blood?

sanskrit: एकवस्त्रां च पाञ्चालीं पाण्डवानभ्यवेक्षतीम् ।हृतस्वान्भ्रष्टचित्तांस्तान्हृतदारान्हृतश्रियः ॥१५॥
transliteration: ekavastrāṁ ca pāñcālīṁ pāṇḍavānabhyavekṣatīm ,hṛtasvānbhraṣṭacittāṁstānhṛtadārānhṛtaśriyaḥ.
english: And Pañcālī, clad in a single garment, was looking at the Pāṇḍavas – those whose wealth had been stolen, whose minds were bewildered, whose wives had been seized, and whose glory had been lost.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 15. And Pañcālī, clad in a single garment, was looking at the Pāṇḍavas – those whose wealth had been stolen, whose minds were bewildered, whose wives had been seized, and whose glory had been lost.

sanskrit: विहीनान्सर्वकामेभ्यो दासभाववशं गतान् ।धर्मपाशपरिक्षिप्तानशक्तानिव विक्रमे ॥१६॥
transliteration: vihīnānsarvakāmebhyo dāsabhāvavaśaṁ gatān ,dharmapāśaparikṣiptānaśaktāniva vikrame.
english: Those who are deprived of all their desires, who have fallen under the sway of servitude, who are bound by the fetters of natural law (dharma), and who are, as it were, powerless in their valor.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 16. Those who are deprived of all their desires, who have fallen under the sway of servitude, who are bound by the fetters of natural law (dharma), and who are, as it were, powerless in their valor.

sanskrit: क्रुद्धाममर्षितां कृष्णां दुःखितां कुरुसंसदि ।दुर्योधनश्च कर्णश्च कटुकान्यभ्यभाषताम् ॥१७॥
transliteration: kruddhāmamarṣitāṁ kṛṣṇāṁ duḥkhitāṁ kurusaṁsadi ,duryodhanaśca karṇaśca kaṭukānyabhyabhāṣatām.
english: Duryodhana and Karṇa spoke harsh words in the Kuru assembly to Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā), who was angry, indignant, and distressed.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 17. Duryodhana and Karṇa spoke harsh words in the Kuru assembly to Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā), who was angry, indignant, and distressed.

sanskrit: तस्याः कृपणचक्षुर्भ्यां प्रदह्येतापि मेदिनी ।अपि शेषं भवेदद्य पुत्राणां मम संजय ॥१८॥
transliteration: tasyāḥ kṛpaṇacakṣurbhyāṁ pradahyetāpi medinī ,api śeṣaṁ bhavedadya putrāṇāṁ mama saṁjaya.
english: Saṃjaya, the earth itself might be consumed by her pitiful eyes. Will there be any trace left of my sons today?
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 18. Saṃjaya, the earth itself might be consumed by her pitiful eyes. Will there be any trace left of my sons today?

sanskrit: भारतानां स्त्रियः सर्वा गान्धार्या सह संगताः ।प्राक्रोशन्भैरवं तत्र दृष्ट्वा कृष्णां सभागताम् ॥१९॥
transliteration: bhāratānāṁ striyaḥ sarvā gāndhāryā saha saṁgatāḥ ,prākrośanbhairavaṁ tatra dṛṣṭvā kṛṣṇāṁ sabhāgatām.
english: All the women of the Bhārata lineage, assembled with Gāndhārī, cried out terribly there upon seeing Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā) who had been brought to the assembly.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 19. All the women of the Bhārata lineage, assembled with Gāndhārī, cried out terribly there upon seeing Draupadī (Kṛṣṇā) who had been brought to the assembly.

sanskrit: अग्निहोत्राणि सायाह्ने न चाहूयन्त सर्वशः ।ब्राह्मणाः कुपिताश्चासन्द्रौपद्याः परिकर्षणे ॥२०॥
transliteration: agnihotrāṇi sāyāhne na cāhūyanta sarvaśaḥ ,brāhmaṇāḥ kupitāścāsandraupadyāḥ parikarṣaṇe.
english: In the evening, the daily fire Vedic rituals (yajña) were not offered at all. The Brahmins, too, were enraged by the dragging and humiliation of Draupadi.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 20. In the evening, the daily fire Vedic rituals (yajña) were not offered at all. The Brahmins, too, were enraged by the dragging and humiliation of Draupadi.

sanskrit: आसीन्निष्टानको घोरो निर्घातश्च महानभूत् ।दिवोल्काश्चापतन्घोरा राहुश्चार्कमुपाग्रसत् ।अपर्वणि महाघोरं प्रजानां जनयन्भयम् ॥२१॥
transliteration: āsīnniṣṭānako ghoro nirghātaśca mahānabhūt ,divolkāścāpatanghorā rāhuścārkamupāgrasat ,aparvaṇi mahāghoraṁ prajānāṁ janayanbhayam.
english: There was a dreadful roar, and a great thunderclap occurred. Furthermore, terrible meteors fell from the sky, and Rahu eclipsed the sun at an inauspicious time, thereby generating extremely dreadful fear among the people.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 21. There was a dreadful roar, and a great thunderclap occurred. Furthermore, terrible meteors fell from the sky, and Rahu eclipsed the sun at an inauspicious time, thereby generating extremely dreadful fear among the people.

sanskrit: तथैव रथशालासु प्रादुरासीद्धुताशनः ।ध्वजाश्च व्यवशीर्यन्त भरतानामभूतये ॥२२॥
transliteration: tathaiva rathaśālāsu prādurāsīddhutāśanaḥ ,dhvajāśca vyavaśīryanta bharatānāmabhūtaye.
english: Similarly, fire manifested in the chariot-stables, and the banners of the Bharatas crumbled, signaling their undoing.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 22. Similarly, fire manifested in the chariot-stables, and the banners of the Bharatas crumbled, signaling their undoing.

sanskrit: दुर्योधनस्याग्निहोत्रे प्राक्रोशन्भैरवं शिवाः ।तास्तदा प्रत्यभाषन्त रासभाः सर्वतोदिशम् ॥२३॥
transliteration: duryodhanasyāgnihotre prākrośanbhairavaṁ śivāḥ ,tāstadā pratyabhāṣanta rāsabhāḥ sarvatodiśam.
english: In Duryodhana's daily fire ritual (yajña), jackals wailed dreadfully. Then, in response, donkeys brayed from all directions.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 23. In Duryodhana's daily fire ritual (yajña), jackals wailed dreadfully. Then, in response, donkeys brayed from all directions.

sanskrit: प्रातिष्ठत ततो भीष्मो द्रोणेन सह संजय ।कृपश्च सोमदत्तश्च बाह्लीकश्च महारथः ॥२४॥
transliteration: prātiṣṭhata tato bhīṣmo droṇena saha saṁjaya ,kṛpaśca somadattaśca bāhlīkaśca mahārathaḥ.
english: Then, Sanjaya, Bhishma, accompanied by Drona, Kripa, Somadatta, and the great warrior Bahlik, set out.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 24. Then, Sanjaya, Bhishma, accompanied by Drona, Kripa, Somadatta, and the great warrior Bahlik, set out.

sanskrit: ततोऽहमब्रुवं तत्र विदुरेण प्रचोदितः ।वरं ददानि कृष्णायै काङ्क्षितं यद्यदिच्छति ॥२५॥
transliteration: tato'hamabruvaṁ tatra vidureṇa pracoditaḥ ,varaṁ dadāni kṛṣṇāyai kāṅkṣitaṁ yadyadicchati.
english: Then, urged by Vidura, I said there, 'I will grant Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā) whatever boon she desires.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 25. Then, urged by Vidura, I said there, 'I will grant Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā) whatever boon she desires.'

sanskrit: अवृणोत्तत्र पाञ्चाली पाण्डवानमितौजसः ।सरथान्सधनुष्कांश्चाप्यनुज्ञासिषमप्यहम् ॥२६॥
transliteration: avṛṇottatra pāñcālī pāṇḍavānamitaujasaḥ ,sarathānsadhanuṣkāṁścāpyanujñāsiṣamapyaham.
english: There, Draupadi (Pāñcālī) chose the Pandavas of immeasurable strength, along with their chariots and bows; and I too granted permission.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 26. There, Draupadi (Pāñcālī) chose the Pandavas of immeasurable strength, along with their chariots and bows; and I too granted permission.

sanskrit: अथाब्रवीन्महाप्राज्ञो विदुरः सर्वधर्मवित् ।एतदन्ताः स्थ भरता यद्वः कृष्णा सभां गता ॥२७॥
transliteration: athābravīnmahāprājño viduraḥ sarvadharmavit ,etadantāḥ stha bharatā yadvaḥ kṛṣṇā sabhāṁ gatā.
english: Then Vidura, the greatly wise and knower of all natural law (dharma), said: 'O Bharatas, your end has come, for Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā) has entered your assembly.'
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 27. Then Vidura, the greatly wise and knower of all natural law (dharma), said: 'O Bharatas, your end has come, for Draupadi (Kṛṣṇā) has entered your assembly.'

sanskrit: एषा पाञ्चालराजस्य सुतैषा श्रीरनुत्तमा ।पाञ्चाली पाण्डवानेतान्दैवसृष्टोपसर्पति ॥२८॥
transliteration: eṣā pāñcālarājasya sutaiṣā śrīranuttamā ,pāñcālī pāṇḍavānetāndaivasṛṣṭopasarpati.
english: This is the daughter of the king of Pañcāla, this unsurpassed beauty (śrī). Pañcālī, created by the gods, is approaching these Pāṇḍavas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 28. This is the daughter of the king of Pañcāla, this unsurpassed beauty (śrī). Pañcālī, created by the gods, is approaching these Pāṇḍavas.

sanskrit: तस्याः पार्थाः परिक्लेशं न क्षंस्यन्तेऽत्यमर्षणाः ।वृष्णयो वा महेष्वासाः पाञ्चाला वा महौजसः ॥२९॥
transliteration: tasyāḥ pārthāḥ parikleśaṁ na kṣaṁsyante'tyamarṣaṇāḥ ,vṛṣṇayo vā maheṣvāsāḥ pāñcālā vā mahaujasaḥ.
english: The Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), being exceedingly indignant, will not tolerate her great distress. Neither will the Vṛṣṇis, who are great archers, nor the Pañcālas, who are of great energy.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 29. The Pārthas (Pāṇḍavas), being exceedingly indignant, will not tolerate her great distress. Neither will the Vṛṣṇis, who are great archers, nor the Pañcālas, who are of great energy.

sanskrit: तेन सत्याभिसंधेन वासुदेवेन रक्षिताः ।आगमिष्यति बीभत्सुः पाञ्चालैरभिरक्षितः ॥३०॥
transliteration: tena satyābhisaṁdhena vāsudevena rakṣitāḥ ,āgamiṣyati bībhatsuḥ pāñcālairabhirakṣitaḥ.
english: Protected by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), whose resolution is firm, Bībhatsu (Arjuna) will arrive, well-guarded by the Pañcālas.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 30. Protected by Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa), whose resolution is firm, Bībhatsu (Arjuna) will arrive, well-guarded by the Pañcālas.

sanskrit: तेषां मध्ये महेष्वासो भीमसेनो महाबलः ।आगमिष्यति धुन्वानो गदां दण्डमिवान्तकः ॥३१॥
transliteration: teṣāṁ madhye maheṣvāso bhīmaseno mahābalaḥ ,āgamiṣyati dhunvāno gadāṁ daṇḍamivāntakaḥ.
english: Among them, Bhīmasena, a mighty warrior and great archer, will come, wielding his mace like the staff of Antaka (Yama).
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 31. Among them, Bhīmasena, a mighty warrior and great archer, will come, wielding his mace like the staff of Antaka (Yama).

sanskrit: ततो गाण्डीवनिर्घोषं श्रुत्वा पार्थस्य धीमतः ।गदावेगं च भीमस्य नालं सोढुं नराधिपाः ॥३२॥
transliteration: tato gāṇḍīvanirghoṣaṁ śrutvā pārthasya dhīmataḥ ,gadāvegaṁ ca bhīmasya nālaṁ soḍhuṁ narādhipāḥ.
english: Then, O kings, having heard the roar of the intelligent Pārtha's Gāṇḍīva bow and the swift impact of Bhīma's mace, they will not be able to endure it.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 32. Then, O kings, having heard the roar of the intelligent Pārtha's Gāṇḍīva bow and the swift impact of Bhīma's mace, they will not be able to endure it.

sanskrit: तत्र मे रोचते नित्यं पार्थैः सार्धं न विग्रहः ।कुरुभ्यो हि सदा मन्ये पाण्डवाञ्शक्तिमत्तरान् ॥३३॥
transliteration: tatra me rocate nityaṁ pārthaiḥ sārdhaṁ na vigrahaḥ ,kurubhyo hi sadā manye pāṇḍavāñśaktimattarān.
english: In this matter, conflict with the Pāṇḍavas is never pleasing to me. Indeed, I always consider the Pāṇḍavas to be more powerful than the Kurus.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 33. In this matter, conflict with the Pāṇḍavas is never pleasing to me. Indeed, I always consider the Pāṇḍavas to be more powerful than the Kurus.

sanskrit: तथा हि बलवान्राजा जरासंधो महाद्युतिः ।बाहुप्रहरणेनैव भीमेन निहतो युधि ॥३४॥
transliteration: tathā hi balavānrājā jarāsaṁdho mahādyutiḥ ,bāhupraharaṇenaiva bhīmena nihato yudhi.
english: Indeed, in this way, the mighty and glorious King Jarāsandha was killed in battle by Bhīma with merely the strike of his arms.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 34. Indeed, in this way, the mighty and glorious King Jarāsandha was killed in battle by Bhīma with merely the strike of his arms.

sanskrit: तस्य ते शम एवास्तु पाण्डवैर्भरतर्षभ ।उभयोः पक्षयोर्युक्तं क्रियतामविशङ्कया ॥३५॥
transliteration: tasya te śama evāstu pāṇḍavairbharatarṣabha ,ubhayoḥ pakṣayoryuktaṁ kriyatāmaviśaṅkayā.
english: O best of Bharatas, let there be peace for you with the Pāṇḍavas. Let what is proper for both sides be done without hesitation.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 35. O best of Bharatas, let there be peace for you with the Pāṇḍavas. Let what is proper for both sides be done without hesitation.

sanskrit: एवं गावल्गणे क्षत्ता धर्मार्थसहितं वचः ।उक्तवान्न गृहीतं च मया पुत्रहितेप्सया ॥३६॥
transliteration: evaṁ gāvalgaṇe kṣattā dharmārthasahitaṁ vacaḥ ,uktavānna gṛhītaṁ ca mayā putrahitepsayā.
english: Thus, O Sañjaya (son of Gavalgala), the charioteer (Vidura) spoke words consistent with righteousness (dharma) and purpose, but they were not accepted by me due to my strong desire for the welfare of my sons.
search_text: Mahabharata Sabha Parva Chapter 72 Verse 36. Thus, O Sañjaya (son of Gavalgala), the charioteer (Vidura) spoke words consistent with righteousness (dharma) and purpose, but they were not accepted by me due to my strong desire for the welfare of my sons.
